title: Log retention exception  Northstar Bank (disable prompt/completion logging)
body:
Issue summary:
Northstar Bank (Dedicated) requests a retention exception: do not store prompts/completions (payload) in request logs.

Impact:
Contractual blocker for go-live.

Requested behavior:
- Payload retention: 0 days (do not persist prompt/completion text)
- Metadata retention: reduce from 90 days default to 30 days (customer prefers shorter)

Environment:
- Dedicated cluster: `ded-northstar-prod-us-east`
- Traffic via standard API gateway.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Prompt/completion fields are not written to persistent log storage for this customer.
2) Metadata-only logs retained for 30 days.
3) Implementation validated with a test request.

Links:
- Policy: confluence://security-and-compliance/data-residency-and-retention/inference-request-log-retention-policy
- Register: confluence://security-and-compliance/risk-and-exceptions/inference-log-retention-exception-register

comments.author: Aisha Rahman (2025-02-10)
comments.body: Customer security team requires no payload logging. They are ok with metadata-only for 30 days. Need Security sign-off for exception + guidance for implementation.

comments.author: Priya Natarajan (2025-02-11)
comments.body: Approved. Payload retention = 0 days (disabled). Metadata retention = 30 days. Scope: Northstar Bank only; Dedicated us-east. Ensure access controls remain least privilege.

comments.author: Alex Martinez (2025-02-12)
comments.body: Implementation plan: add customer override in `obs-logs-prod` pipeline to drop `prompt`, `completion`, and `messages` fields before sink. Separate TTL for `request_metadata` index = 30d. Will roll out behind config flag `log_payload_enabled=false`.

comments.author: Alex Martinez (2025-02-14)
comments.body: Rolled out to `ded-northstar-prod-us-east`. Verified via sample request: payload fields absent in persisted record; metadata present.

comments.author: Hannah Schmitt (2025-02-18)
comments.body: Confirmed customer acceptance recorded via email (Aisha mailbox). Please update exception register row.

comments.author: Alex Martinez (2025-02-18)
comments.body: Register updated. Closing.

title: Streaming responses freeze mid-generation for Arcadia Health (SSE stalls, request never completes)
body:
Issue summary:
- Customer reports streaming chat responses intermittently stop mid-generation. Client keeps the HTTP connection open; server stops emitting SSE tokens and never sends [DONE].

Impact:
- Production incident for Arcadia Health’s support-agent. End users see partial completions and stuck spinners.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool in us-east.
- Client uses OpenAI-compatible streaming endpoint.

Steps to reproduce (customer-provided):
1) Start streaming completion with max_tokens ~800.
2) After ~10–40 seconds, stream stalls mid-sentence.
3) No further bytes, no close, no error.

Investigation notes:
- Correlates with longer generations + high concurrent streams.
- Gateway shows upstream still open; runtime logs show request context cancelled late or not at all.
- Suspected backpressure interaction between token streamer + gzip + chunked transfer.

Workaround:
- Customer can s...

comments.author: Alex Martinez
comments.body: Seeing similar symptoms in perf-canary when gzip enabled. I can reproduce in staging with 200 concurrent streams.

comments.author: Dylan Brooks
comments.body: Gateway fix in progress; will ship a config toggle for SSE gzip off by default.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Confirmed with customer: after change, no stalls observed over 2 hours of load test. Closing with workaround note still recommended on client side.

title: Access request: AWS Marketplace + GCP Marketplace partner portals (roles, MFA) and shared mailbox setup
body:
Requesting access to AWS and GCP marketplace partner portals needed for the Cloud Marketplace Listing Refresh project.

Business justification:
- We are refreshing AWS/GCP marketplace listings and submitting updated SKUs and security artifacts. Multiple teams (Partnerships, PMM, Docs, Security) need portal access for submission workflows, review iterations, and compliance attachments.

Systems / portals:
1) AWS Marketplace Management Portal (seller / AMMP) and AWS Partner Central (as needed for offer workflows)
2) GCP Partner Advantage + Producer Portal (Cloud Marketplace)

Access requested (users):
- Camila Reyes (Marketing lead) — listing content/asset updates
- Soojin Lee (PMM lead) — positioning/copy approvals + SKU alignment
- Chris Osei (Program lead) — submission tracking and coordination
- Naomi Feldman (Security/compliance PO) — security packet attachments and claims review
-...

comments.author: Isabelle Laurent ()
comments.body: Filing per marketplace refresh kickoff. We need consistent portal access across teams + a shared mailbox so partner review threads don’t disappear when someone is OOO.

comments.author: Alex Martinez ()
comments.body: Acknowledged. Please confirm which 1–2 users should be portal admins for AWS and GCP. We should keep admin count minimal.

comments.author: Chris Osei
comments.body: Recommend admins: Chris Osei (program) + Soojin Lee (PMM). Everyone else editor/reviewer. We also need to ensure Naomi can upload security docs / attestations.

comments.author: Alex Martinez
comments.body: Copy. For the shared mailbox: do you want a true shared inbox (delegated Gmail) or group alias? Group alias is quicker and auditable.

comments.author: Naomi Feldman
comments.body: Group alias is fine as long as we can receive external emails and retain an archive for audit. Please ensure membership changes are restricted.

comments.author: Alex Martinez
comments.body: Created Google Group marketplace-partners@redwoodinference.com, restricted posting to members, allowed external senders, enabled archive. Sent test email from external account; received successfully. Added requested members.

comments.author: Alex Martinez
comments.body: AWS portal: invitations sent to Camila, Soojin, Chris, Naomi. Waiting on users to accept + complete MFA enrollment. Note: AWS portal requires each user to have an AWS account tied to their corporate email.

comments.author: Soojin Lee
comments.body: Accepted invite; MFA enabled (Authenticator). Confirmed can edit listing draft fields; cannot publish (expected).

comments.author: Camila Reyes
comments.body: Accepted invite and can upload images. Got blocked on one screen due to missing permission to “Change public offer details.” Is that restricted to admin?

comments.author: Alex Martinez
comments.body: Yes, that’s admin-only. Kept as-is per least privilege. If you need a one-off change, route through Chris/Soojin as admins.

comments.author: Alex Martinez
comments.body: GCP Producer Portal: invited Camila, Soojin, Chris, Naomi, Alex. Some users may see a delay until Partner Advantage provisioning completes (up to 24h).

comments.author: Chris Osei
comments.body: Confirmed GCP access active. Can view draft listing and required artifacts section. Naomi can access the security documentation upload area.

comments.author: Alex Martinez
comments.body: Closing out. All requested users confirmed access on both portals. Shared alias created and in use for partner review threads.

title: Helio Bank security review: audit log schema v1 fields, retention/residency, and SIEM export options (Hosted/Dedicated/Private)
body:
Issue summary
Helio Bank (Private, EU) requested written answers for their security questionnaire and procurement exhibit focused on audit logging. They specifically asked for: canonical audit event schema fields, delivery semantics (duplicates), retention and data residency behavior, and supported SIEM exports (Splunk/Sentinel). They also requested clarification that Redwood does not log prompt/completion bodies in audit logs.

Impact
Blocking security review sign-off for Private deployment expansion to additional business unit. Customer requested response by EOW.

Customer request / questions (verbatim-ish)
1) Provide audit event schema: required fields, actor/target semantics, timestamps, correlation IDs.
2) Do audit events include any customer data content (prompts/completions) or secrets?
3) Where are audit logs stored (data residency)? Can audit logs remain in-region for EU tena...

Impact

3) Where are audit logs stored (data residency)? Can audit logs remain in-region for EU tena...

comments.body: 12 PT — Dev Patel (Support): Opened per Helio Bank security questionnaire. They want written answers + schema attachments by EOW. Looping in Naomi (Product Sec/Compliance) + Kevin (exporter) + Vivek (Private).

48 PT — Naomi Feldman (Product): Please avoid hardcoding retention numbers in writing unless it’s in their contract. OK to describe Private as customer-controlled retention and Hosted as plan/contract-defined. We can link to the schema v1 + redaction policy pages.

06 PT — Kevin Osei (Security Eng): Splunk HEC + Sentinel export targets are supported. Exporter includes schema_version and preserves event_id across retries. We should explicitly mention at-least-once semantics and dedupe on event_id to address their concern about duplicates.

22 PT — Vivek Kulkarni (Product, Private): For Private, we should state events are generated within customer environment; operator identity during install/upgrade is “where available” (e.g., interactive operator, SSO user in Console, or service identity). Do not promise every installer action has perfect operator attribution yet; say coverage is being standardized.

10 PT — Hanae Suzuki (Security Eng): Reviewed draft response. Ensure wording: ‘Audit logs do not include LLM prompt/completion bodies’ and ‘never log secrets; key material is redacted.’ Also mention correlation IDs (request_id/trace_id) for investigations.

02 PT — Dev Patel (Support): Sent response to customer with approved wording + links to Confluence schema/redaction/versioning pages. Awaiting confirmation of SIEM endpoints and field mapping preferences.

title: Brightline Insurance: Audit log export schema + retention controls questions (regulated security review)
body:
Issue summary:
Brightline Insurance Security team requested detailed clarification on (a) the audit log export format/schema and (b) retention controls for Redwood Dedicated / Redwood Private. They want an example payload and a clear statement of what is/is not included (e.g., prompts, generated text, keys, PHI).

Impact:
Blocking completion of their security questionnaire and internal auditability review. This is a gating item for advancing from security review to procurement.

Customer questions (verbatim-ish):
1) “Do you provide an audit log export? What format (JSON/CSV/SIEM) and can it be streamed?”
2) “Please provide the schema/fields and a sample event.”
3) “What is the default retention period? Can we configure retention (e.g., 7/30/90/365 days) and do we control where logs are stored?”
4) “Do logs include prompt / completion content or customer data?”
5) “Can we export to our...

Impact:

Customer questions (verbatim-ish):

3) “What is the default retention period? Can we configure retention (e.g., 7/30/90/365 days) and do we control where logs are stored?”
4) “Do logs include prompt / completion content or customer data?”
5) “Can we export to our...

comments.author: Naomi Feldman
comments.body: Creating ticket to track Brightline’s audit log export + retention questions. They asked for schema + sample event and whether logs contain prompt/completion content. Need response by early next week to keep them on track for June procurement window.

comments.author: Olga Petrov
comments.body: Acknowledged. I’ll draft a customer-safe response for Naomi/AE and validate retention + export capabilities with SE + Security. Looping in Docs for a durable artifact we can reuse across regulated accounts.

comments.author: Markus Klein, Solutions
comments.body: From prior regulated deep dives: audit logs are available for Dedicated + Private. For Private deployments, customers can configure export destination (e.g., customer-owned object storage / SIEM forwarder depending on deployment). We should be explicit that content logging (prompts/completions) is NOT part of platform audit logs. Audit logs focus on access/admin/config + request metadata. Recommend referencing the audit log export example once the snippet is merged.

comments.author: Dr. Aisha Rahman
comments.body: Please avoid hard-coding “default retention = X days” unless we confirm per offering. We can state: retention is configurable and can be set per tenant/policy; options may vary by deployment mode and contract. Also confirm language: Redwood does not include prompt/completion bodies in audit logs by default. We should say that customers can choose what to log in their own application layer, but Redwood platform audit logs are separate.

comments.author: Olga Petrov
comments.body:
Draft response (for review):
- Export formats: JSON Lines (newline-delimited JSON) available; CSV can be provided for limited event types on request; integrations for SIEM via scheduled export or forwarding agent (Private) depending on environment.
- Delivery: batch export on a schedule (hourly/daily) and on-demand export supported; streaming/real-time forwarding is available in Private deployments where the customer manages the log forwarder.
- Scope: events include authentication events, user/admin actions, policy/config changes, key management configuration changes, and request metadata (request_id/trace_id, model identifier, timestamp,...

comments.author: Evan Scott, Solutions
comments.body: Sanity check: include these common fields we’ve shown in deep dives: event_time, event_type, actor (user/service), actor_ip, auth_method, tenant_id, resource_type, resource_id, action, outcome, request_id, trace_id, region, user_agent (where applicable). For request metadata events: route, model, token_counts (prompt_tokens, completion_tokens), latency_ms, status_code. We should be careful: token counts/latency are present for API requests; admin events won’t have them.

comments.author: Kira Thompson, Docs
comments.body: I can add a short doc section “Audit logs: export + schema (high level)” and link from the regulated workloads landing page + security FAQ. Need final approved language from Security (Aisha/Naomi) and a sample payload. If we can reference the examples repo snippet, that’s best. Target: end of week.

comments.author: Olga Petrov
comments.body:
Sent customer-facing response to Naomi/Avery for delivery to Brightline (pending final sign-off from Security). Included:
- Audit logs do not contain prompts/completions by default.
- Export available (JSONL) with example event.
- Retention is configurable; for long-term retention export to customer-controlled storage.
Also noted: If Brightline needs a specific Splunk HEC format, we can provide guidance but they may implement transform in their forwarder.

comments.author: Naomi Feldman
comments.body: Delivered response to Brightline security contact. They asked one follow-up: “Can you confirm whether request metadata includes end-user identifiers?” I responded that Redwood logs identify the calling API key/service principal and tenant; end-user identifiers are customer-defined and not inherently present unless the customer includes them in headers/metadata and chooses to log them on their side.

comments.author: Daniel Carter, Compliance
comments.body: Evidence pack update suggestion: add a one-page appendix listing audit log event categories + sample JSON (redacted). Also add a note on retention being policy-driven and export for extended retention. I can update the evidence pack index once Docs publishes the canonical page.

comments.author: Olga Petrov
comments.body: Marking resolved. Brightline confirmed this satisfies their questionnaire section. Remaining items are general (SOC 2 request handled separately). Follow-ups created for Docs + evidence pack index.

title: Enable shadow traffic feature flags for internal dogfood tenant (Shadow Experiments + quality scoring)
body:
Request
- Please enable Shadow Traffic (shadow experiments) feature flags for the internal dogfood tenant so Console & Rollouts can run end-to-end testing before Dedicated beta.

Tenant
- Tenant name: redwood-internal
- Tenant ID: tnt_internal_01HMWK2J7D7VQ8A9B2K4 (per Console)
- Org/Project: Internal / DevRel + Platform (shared)

Requested flags (control plane + console)
1) shadow_traffic_policies_enabled = true
2) shadow_experiments_console_enabled = true
3) shadow_scoring_pipeline_enabled = true
4) shadow_streaming_mirroring_enabled = false (keep off for initial dogfood)

Scope / guardrails
- Sampling: max 1% on selected routes only (per runbook); no global defaults.
- Fail-open required: any shadow routing/scoring failure must not affect primary response.
- Retention: default 24h for prompts/responses; metrics aggregates retained per standard.
- RBAC: limit experiment create/edit ...

comments.body: 14 PT — Talia Benson (Reporter): Filing internal request per dogfood plan. Internal routes selected by Dylan/Chloe; sampling will be 0.5–1% max. Please keep streaming mirroring off for first pass.
32 PT — Jordan Lee (Assignee): Acknowledged. Need explicit approvals recorded here before toggling prod flags. Also confirming: do we want this tenant only, not all internal orgs?
44 PT — Dylan Brooks: Yes, tenant only (redwood-internal). We’ll start with one route: /v1/chat/completions (non-streaming).
05 PT — Marissa Cole: Approved for internal dogfood under stated guardrails (<=1% sampling; fail-open; 24h retention).
18 PT — Ethan Park: Approved. Please ensure schema migration already applied in prod before enabling shadow_traffic_policies_enabled.

30 PT — Sean Gallagher: SRE approval with conditions: (1) enable only in us-east, (2) scoring queue lag dashboard watched during first 2 hours, (3) paging disabled for dogfood unless Sev1 impact on user path, (4) confirm circuit breaker thresholds are in place.

41 PT — Marcus Lin: Security approval for internal dogfood. Reminder: prompts/responses must be redacted/truncated per default policy; no raw PII in long-term storage; keep retention at 24h until formal beta.
10 PT — Jordan Lee: Confirmed migrations present in prod. Proceeding to enable flags for tenant tnt_internal_01HMWK2J7D7VQ8A9B2K4 in us-east only. Keeping shadow_streaming_mirroring_enabled=false.
22 PT — Jordan Lee: Change applied. Console feature flag propagation ~5–10 min. Please validate UI + API. Audit event captured (see notes).

41 PT — Chloe Martin: Validated. Console shows Shadow Experiments. API list/create works. Created experiment “dogfood-routing-mirror-v1” on /v1/chat/completions with 0.5% sampling; shadow target = candidate model variant. Seeing telemetry events in staging dashboards.
05 PT — Nadia Rahman: Observed expected ingestion volume; no cardinality warnings. Scoring queue stable (<2m lag).
12 PT — Talia Benson: Closing out. Dogfood completed for week 1; no user-path latency deltas observed. Keeping streaming mirroring disabled until ENG-5414 completes rollout.

title: Permissions request: Support/CS access to internal-only model changelog + audit views (Console Admin + API)
body:
Requester(s): Support + Customer Success
- Dev Patel (Support escalation/workflow)
- Miguel Santos (Enterprise CS)

Business need:
Support and CS need access to INTERNAL-ONLY model changelog and audit history to resolve escalations where customers report output drift / unexpected behavior and we must answer: what changed, when, and who/what promoted it.

Current problem:
- Console customer-facing release notes intentionally redact internal fields.
- Support currently relies on ad-hoc Eng lookups or screenshots.
- Recent incidents/tickets (e.g., SUP-28429 output regression; SUP-28411 missing release notes) require faster, self-serve visibility.

Requested access scope (internal-only):
1) Console Admin UI: per-model change history with internal notes, event payload details, and audit event IDs.
2) Internal API endpoints: query changelog/audit by model_id, model_version, deployment mode,...

1) Console Admin UI: per-model change history with internal notes, event payload details, and audit event IDs.
2) Internal API endpoints: query changelog/audit by model_id, model_version, deployment mode,...

comments.body: 14 Dev Patel (Reporter): Creating ticket after SUP-28429 escalation. We spent ~6 hours to identify the exact model version promotion + internal notes. Need a small set of Support/CS folks to see internal changelog/audit views so we can answer customers quickly and stop pulling Eng into every case.

03 Miguel Santos (CS): +1. For enterprise accounts we often need to provide an evidence trail (what changed, when, rollback path). Public release notes are necessary but not sufficient. We need internal view + audit event IDs for our internal case files.

22 Chloe Martin (Console Backend EM): Agree on read-only access. Please ensure we don't conflate with the customer-visible release notes feed. We need explicit RBAC checks server-side + UI guards (we already have tests after PR-18463).

05 Marcus Lin (Security Eng Reviewer): Approved in principle with conditions: (1) least-privilege role separated from existing admin roles, (2) mandatory audit logs on read access (query-level), (3) time-bound access for CS unless explicitly justified, (4) no access to internal notes export endpoints without redaction guardrails.

40 Naomi Feldman (Security/Compliance): Approval granted provided retention for access logs meets enterprise expectations and we document access request/revocation. Also ensure this does not change what is published publicly (no new fields).

18 Svetlana Petrenko (Assignee): Plan: create Okta group + RBAC binding; implement permission checks on internal changelog endpoints; add audit log events for "admin_changelog_read" including actor, filters, and result count (no payload content). Will coordinate with Jada on endpoint enforcement and Leila on regression tests.

07 Jada Williams (Staff Console): Implemented server-side gates for internal fields: if caller lacks model_changelog:read_internal, response omits internal_notes, raw_event_payload, and audit_event_ids. UI only renders internal tab when permission present. Added test coverage for role matrix.

55 Connor O'Brien (Release Eng): Please add this role to our release-note preview workflow docs. Reviewers sometimes ask Support to validate notes; Support should be able to see internal deltas without needing repo access.

30 Dev Patel: Confirmed access works for Support Engineering group. We can now pull change history for SUP-28411 and attach internal evidence to the case without pinging Eng. Requesting CS access for Liam O'Rourke (TAM) and Miguel Santos (CS lead) as time-bound (90 days).

12 Svetlana Petrenko: Okta group "support-model-changelog-admin" created and mapped. Added time-bound memberships for Miguel Santos + Liam O'Rourke (expires 2026-01-16). Access logging enabled; dashboard query created for Security to review monthly. Closing ticket.

title: Private: Audit logs missing RBAC change events (role/assignment updates not exported)
body:
Issue summary
- Customer reports that RBAC changes made in their Private deployment are not present in exported audit logs.
- Non-RBAC audit events (e.g., API key lifecycle) are present, so pipeline is partially working.

Customer impact
- Helio Bank security team cannot meet internal monitoring requirements for privileged access changes (who changed roles / who granted access).
- Blocks completion of security review gate for production go-live (customer states this is a “must have” control).

What the customer expected
- Audit events for RBAC admin actions, including at least:
 - role created/updated/deleted
 - role binding / membership added/removed
 - service account role assignment changes
- Events should include actor identity + source IP where available and be exported to Splunk (HEC) from their Private cluster.

What the customer observed
- No events for RBAC workflows in Conso...

- No events for RBAC workflows in Conso...

comments.body: 12 Dev Patel (Support): Opened by Helio Bank (Private) via support portal. They report missing RBAC change events in Splunk. They provided timeframe and confirmed other audit events export correctly.
03 Liam O'Rourke (Customer coordination): Customer flagged as go-live blocker for security sign-off. Requested ETA for patch and whether we can provide interim mitigation for compliance evidence.
20 Hanae Suzuki (Security Eng): Acknowledged. Likely Private parity gap with control-plane RBAC instrumentation. Looping in Ethan Park and Kevin Osei to confirm producer/exporter behavior in 1.18.x.

04 Kevin Osei (Audit pipeline/SIEM): Asked for exporter metrics: dropped_events_total, validation_failures_total, and whether they enabled schema validation. If validation enabled and RBAC emits legacy fields/unregistered event_type, exporter may be dropping rather than forwarding.

47 Dev Patel (Support): Customer replied: strict validation IS enabled (they turned it on during initial setup). Exporter dashboard shows validation_failures_total increasing, but they didn’t have labels to break down by event_type. They can share logs under NDA if we send exact grep patterns.

10 Ethan Park (Control plane): Confirmed RBAC audit events were added in hosted/dedicated first; Private 1.18.2 likely missing the newer audit library + registry. In Private, RBAC endpoints may still emit legacy “admin_action” events with inconsistent fields (or none). Will verify against branch used for 1.18.2.

32 Hanae Suzuki (Security Eng): Proposed immediate mitigation: enable exporter normalization mode for legacy events (if available in their version) OR temporarily relax strict validation while we ship Private patch. Need Kevin to confirm correct config keys and risk.

05 Kevin Osei (Audit pipeline/SIEM): Exporter PR 842 includes schema_version tagging + normalization improvements; customer’s exporter build predates that. If they can upgrade exporter component only (without full Private upgrade), that may reduce drops and surface RBAC events if they exist. Still need to confirm whether RBAC events are emitted at all in 1.18.2.
18 Dev Patel (Support): Customer can do a rolling upgrade of exporter only (Helm release separate). They asked for a tested version matrix and whether upgrading exporter alone can break ingestion.

41 Hanae Suzuki (Security Eng): Internal repro in lab Private 1.18.2: RBAC change endpoints do not emit v1 audit events. Only some admin actions are audited (API keys). Confirms parity gap rather than only exporter drops. Next step: patch control-plane RBAC service to emit v1 events and register event types.

06 Ethan Park (Control plane): Plan: backport RBAC audit instrumentation from PR 22005 into Private release branch; ensure actor attribution works in Private auth context; include request_id/trace_id per PR 22047 patterns. Estimate: code complete by Feb 5, QA by Feb 7, customer patch release next week if no blockers.

22 Dev Patel (Support): Updated customer: confirmed product gap; we’re preparing a Private patch release to add RBAC audit events. Offered interim workaround: export Console admin activity logs (non-audit) is NOT sufficient for their control, so they prefer patch. Asked for earliest available candidate build for validation in their staging cluster.

title: Request: export experiment audit events (route experiments) with required fields for SIEM/compliance
body:
Customer: Helio Financial (enterprise) is in security review for Route-level A/B testing ("route experiments") and requires an exportable audit trail for experiment lifecycle/config changes suitable for ingestion into their SIEM.

Request context
- Helio’s security/compliance team is asking for evidence that experiment configuration changes are fully auditable, exportable, and include before/after details.
- They specifically called out that screenshots from Console audit history are not acceptable for their controls; they need structured export (API or downloadable JSON/CSV).

What customer needs (minimum fields)
1) Actor and authentication context
- actor_type (human/service)
- actor_id (user id) + actor_email
- auth_method (SSO/SAML, API token, break-glass)
- rbac_role at time of change

2) Event metadata
- event_type: experiment.create | experiment.update | experiment.start | expe...

Request context
- Helio’s security/compliance team is asking for evidence that experiment configuration changes are fully auditable, exportable, and include before/after details.
- They specifically called out that screenshots from Console audit history are not acceptable for their controls; they need structured export (API or downloadable JSON/CSV).

What customer needs (minimum fields)
1) Actor and authentication context
- actor_type (human/service)
- actor_id (user id) + actor_email
- auth_method (SSO/SAML, API token, break-glass)
- rbac_role at time of change

2) Event metadata
- event_type: experiment.create | experiment.update | experiment.start | expe...

comments.author: Marissa Cole
comments.body: Logging this from Helio’s security review. They’re aligned on our control plane model but need an exportable audit trail. They listed required fields (actor, event type, timestamps, before/after, config diff). Requesting Compliance/Security + Platform confirm plan + ETA.

comments.author: Connor O’Brien
comments.body: Assigned to Kevin for audit export format. Adding Marcus Lin for security requirements confirmation. Helio asked for a sample payload by end of week to keep review moving.

comments.author: Marcus Lin
comments.body: Confirmed enterprise requirement is consistent with our Confluence spec. Key missing pieces historically: change diff + variant identifiers and stable event_id/request_id. Also confirm redaction rules: do not include prompt content; route_id should be normalized, not raw path with identifiers.

comments.author: Kevin Osei, Audit Log Export/SIEM
comments.body: Current export pipeline supports rollout policy events, but experiment events are only partially represented. Plan: extend export format to include experiment_id/variant details and include change_diff summary. Targeting patch in next release train; can provide a sample JSON payload to unblock security review before GA.

comments.author: Customer via
comments.body: Helio asking specifically: can we pull audit events via API (preferred) vs manual download? Also need retention confirmation (12 months) and whether exports include who disabled auto-stop kill switch if changed.

comments.author: Kevin Osei
comments.body:
API is preferred; interim option is a CSV export via Console admin action limited to org admins. For Helio, we can also provide an evidence package: (1) schema doc, (2) sample create/update/start/stop JSON, (3) retention statement for enterprise audit logs.

Proposed sample (high level):
- event_type: experiment.update
- actor_email
- experiment_id
- route_id
- config_version_before/after
- change_diff: allocations + variants + guardrails + stickiness key

Will follow up with exact field names once PR is ready; aligning naming with OTel conventions and our existing audit export keys.

comments.author: Kevin Osei
comments.body:
Implemented additional export fields in PR (internal) and confirmed redaction. Also added change_summary and machine-readable diff entries for: allocations, variants list, guardrails, and stickiness.

Next: coordinate with Support to send Helio a sample payload + doc link. Retention for enterprise: 400 days (meets 1 year).

comments.author: Rafael Mendes
comments.body: Note: Helio asked whether stop events capture stop semantics (immediate vs drain). Please ensure we include stop_mode and effective_timestamp if drain is used, since they treat routing config changes as controlled changes.

comments.author: Kevin Osei
comments.body: Added stop_mode + effective_timestamp fields to stop events. Confirmed event_id uniqueness and request_id linking for API mutations. Waiting on merge + deploy window to prod.
Sent Helio evidence package draft: retention statement + sample JSON payloads + list of fields. Helio confirmed this is sufficient to proceed while they wait for API export endpoint availability.

comments.author: Kevin Osei
comments.body: Deployed export field extensions. Support can tell Helio: audit export now includes experiment lifecycle events with actor + diff + variant ids. Docs update in progress (owner: DevEx). Marking ticket resolved; leaving open follow-up for explicit customer confirmation.

title: Enable Usage Insights (beta) feature flag for AcmeAI enterprise eval workspace
body:
Requester (SE): Evan Scott (Solutions Engineering)
Account: AcmeAI (enterprise eval)
Workspace: acmeai-enterprise-eval
Workspace ID: ws_7K2Q9M3P
Org ID: org_2J8D4R
Primary contact: Avery Johnson (AE)
Region: us-east (hosted)

Request
Please enable the new Console feature: Usage/Cost → “Usage Insights (Beta)” for the AcmeAI eval workspace.

Why / customer context
AcmeAI is evaluating Redwood primarily for cost transparency. They requested route + model breakdown and CSV export for finance workflows. They are OK with beta limitations as long as totals reconcile with billing within tolerance and exports are permission-gated.

Requested scope
- Enable feature flag / allowlist for workspace: ws_7K2Q9M3P
- Ensure cost views + export are only available to workspace users with appropriate role (Owner/Admin + Cost Viewer if applicable)
- Confirm audit events are emitted for export generation +...

comments.body: 18 Evan Scott (Reporter): Requesting enablement for AcmeAI eval (ws_7K2Q9M3P). Avery has customer on a call next week and wants to demo route+model breakdown + export. Customer understands this is beta. Please confirm approvals needed and proceed.

42 Connor O'Brien (Assignee): Acknowledged. Looping in Product + Security for explicit OK since this includes exports. @Marissa Cole / @Paula Mendes can you confirm AcmeAI is on the beta allowlist? @Marcus Lin / @Kevin Osei please confirm export audit logging fields are in place for this enablement.
05 Paula Mendes: Product OK to enable for AcmeAI. They’re listed as a priority eval in the beta customer list. Please ensure they’re on latest build with freshness banner and reconciliation checks.

22 Kevin Osei: Security OK provided that (1) cost visibility is role-gated, (2) export generation + download emit audit events with workspace_id, actor_id, export_id, and signed_url expiry, and (3) restricted workspaces are not enabled. AcmeAI is not restricted; proceed.

03 Connor O'Brien: Confirmed RBAC settings: workspace setting “Hide cost” is OFF; roles reviewed with SE. Proceeding with enablement under feature flag `console.usage_insights.beta` (workspace allowlist).

27 Connor O'Brien: Enabled `console.usage_insights.beta` for ws_7K2Q9M3P in prod. Verified in Console (admin account) that Usage Insights page loads and breakdown table returns data for last 7 days. 30-day range is slower but completes; advised SE to start with 7-day for live demos.

41 Connor O'Brien: Export verification: as Owner role, export job completes and signed download URL generated. As Member role without cost permission, page shows permission message and export button is hidden. Audit log events observed: `usage_insights_export_created` and `usage_insights_export_downloaded` (fields present: workspace_id, actor_id, export_id).

10 Dev Patel: Support readiness note—known issues include (a) possible invoice mismatch due to late events and rounding over short windows, and (b) timeouts on 30-day interactive queries; recommend export for larger windows. If AcmeAI reports mismatch, open SUP ticket and link back here.
02 Evan Scott: Confirmed enabled on our side; we can see the page and export. Thanks. We’ll monitor for reconciliation drift after customer traffic ramps.

20 Connor O'Brien: Closing as resolved. Monitoring for 7 days: freshness lag banner, query timeouts, and reconciliation drift. If drift > 1% persists, we’ll disable flag and escalate to Console Backend + Billing hooks owners.

title: Request: add additional audit log fields for Usage Insights CSV export downloads
body:
Requester: Security Engineering (Kevin Osei)
Service/Area: Usage Insights export (CSV) + signed download URLs

Background
Security review for Usage Insights beta flagged that our existing audit events for downloads are missing required context to support:
- Customer requests for “who exported what, when, from where”
- Restricted workspace controls (cost visibility + exports)
- Incident response / forensic workflows when a share link or signed URL is used

Problem
The current audit log entry for export downloads only records a generic “download” action and user identifier. We need additional fields and consistent event naming so that exported cost data can be traced and reconciled.

Request
Add/extend audit log events for Usage Insights exports with additional fields.

Proposed event names
1) usage_insights.export.created
2) usage_insights.export.downloaded
3) usage_insights.export.fai...

normalized.description:
Requester: Security Engineering (Kevin Osei)
Service/Area: Usage Insights export (CSV) + signed download URLs

Background
Security review for Usage Insights beta flagged that our existing audit events for downloads are missing required context to support:
- Customer requests for “who exported what, when, from where”
- Restricted workspace controls (cost visibility + exports)
- Incident response / forensic workflows when a share link or signed URL is used

Problem
The current audit log entry for export downloads only records a generic “download” action and user identifier. We need additional fields and consistent event naming so that exported cost data can be traced and reconciled.

Request
Add/extend audit log events for Usage Insights exports with additional fields.

Proposed event names
1) usage_insights.export.created
2) usage_insights.export.downloaded
3) usage_insights.export.fai...

title: Enterprise request: Runtime LTS channel availability + support window and patch SLA (Dedicated/Private readiness)
body:
Customer (Northstar Bank) asked for formal confirmation of a Long Term Support (LTS) channel for the Redwood serving runtime as a blocker for their change-control and procurement process.

Request details (from customer):
- Need a stable runtime baseline supported for 12 months (minimum 6 months acceptable only with clear renewal/overlap). They explicitly do NOT want to be forced onto feature releases to receive security fixes.
- Patch cadence: monthly patch releases on the LTS line. They want to know if patches are optional vs mandatory, and how we communicate that.
- Security patch SLA: customer requested written commitment for critical vulnerabilities (customer phrasing: “high/critical CVEs”) with an out-of-band patch within 72 hours of confirmation, plus disclosure / comms process.
- Backport policy: eligibility criteria and approval process (who signs off, risk scoring, testing g...

- Backport policy: eligibility criteria and approval process (who signs off, risk scoring, testing g...

comments.author: Jasmine Liu
comments.body: Created per Northstar platform team request. They want an LTS commitment in writing for change-control. Adding Dev Patel to drive the support response and coordinate internally.

comments.author: Dev Patel
comments.body: Acknowledged. Routing to Release Eng / Runtime for authoritative answer. Looping in Connor O’Brien (Release Eng), Mateo Alvarez (VP Eng Runtime), and Dr. Aisha Rahman (security policy sponsor). Will propose customer-facing language we can stand behind.

comments.author: Connor O’Brien
comments.body: We’re actively standing up Runtime LTS. Not ready to promise dates publicly until ADR is approved, but direction is: runtime-1.18-lts branch with monthly patch cadence + out-of-band security fixes. Support window target is 12 months with 3-month overlap between LTS lines. Backport policy: security + Sev1 + select Sev2 only; minimal diffs; perf gates required. I’ll provide draft language for customer.

comments.author: Mateo Alvarez
comments.body: Confirmed directionally yes. For commitments: we can state intent + process now; specific SLA numbers need Security sign-off. For backports/perf: LTS will have stricter gating than mainline (scheduler smoke + perf canary).

comments.author: Dr. Aisha Rahman
comments.body: For security SLAs: we can offer tiered response language (e.g., critical remote code execution or credential exposure gets expedited out-of-band). Exact timelines should be aligned with Hanae Suzuki (vuln response coordination) and existing enterprise security commitments. Suggest wording: “as soon as practicable, target within X business days” unless contractually committed. If Northstar needs 72 hours, that likely needs to be a contracted term for Private/Dedicated.

comments.author: Dev Patel
comments.body: Drafted response for customer (internal review) covering: (1) LTS support window target 12 months, (2) monthly patch cadence + out-of-band security, (3) backport eligibility and approvals, (4) reproducible artifacts w/ checksums (SBOM in progress). Sending to Connor + Rachel Kim for CS alignment and Avery Johnson for sales/procurement language.

comments.author: Rachel Kim
comments.body: From CS perspective: Northstar will likely accept “12 months support” + clear comms, but will press on security patch SLA. Recommend we position 72-hour as a contractual SLA option for Private/Dedicated with premium support. Also highlight rollback guidance and “mandatory patch” criteria to reduce operational surprises.

comments.author: Avery Johnson
comments.body: Northstar procurement wants a one-pager. Please keep language crisp and avoid “best effort” wording if possible. If we can’t commit to 72h universally, we should offer a clear escalation path and an option to contract the timeline.

comments.author: Connor O’Brien
comments.body: Updated: ADR for branching/versioning is in approval. We can state branch naming convention and support window, and that release artifacts will include checksums/provenance. Artifact signing/SBOM is planned (phased). Provided customer-facing draft language and pointed to docs PR in progress.

comments.author: Dev Patel
comments.body:
Sent customer response (via CSM/AEs) with:
- Runtime LTS channel planned with a stable branch (runtime-1.18-lts) and monthly patch releases.
- Backport scope: security + critical correctness fixes only; features generally not backported.
- Testing: CI + targeted scheduler regression suite + perf canary gates; releases include known issues and rollback guidance.
- Artifact verification: checksums/provenance metadata; SBOM support is planned.
- Security patch handling: out-of-band security patches; exact SLA to be addressed in contract language for enterprise tier.
Awaiting confirmation from Northstar that this meets procurement needs; they ...

comments.author: Dev Patel
comments.body: Customer confirmed this is sufficient to proceed to the next procurement step, with an open item for contractual security patch SLA. Marking ticket Resolved; follow-up tracked with Sales/CS. If they request an explicit 72-hour SLA, route through Security + Legal for contract addendum.

title: Audit log export missing Optimization Config rollback event (Console/API rollback succeeds, no event in SIEM feed)
body:
Issue summary
Northbridge Analytics reports that Optimization Config rollback actions are not appearing in their audit log export / SIEM pipeline. Rollback itself succeeds (traffic returns to baseline), and they can see other Optimization Config lifecycle events (create/apply/promote) in the audit feed.

Impact
- Customer compliance/change-management requirement: all production rollbacks must be auditable with actor + scope + before/after.
- Procurement blocker for enabling Safe Apply + Rollback broadly across their org.
- No production outage, but compliance risk + inability to prove rollback action occurred.

Customer report
- They rolled back Optimization Config via Console (Rollback button) on 2026-02-11 ~18:40 UTC.
- They expected an audit event within ~1–2 minutes in their exported audit stream.
- They can correlate the rollback in Redwood Console history and via performance rec...

title: Break-glass access request audit — time-bound elevation for Private control plane role binding fix (Northbridge POC)
body:
Request type: Break-glass (emergency admin elevation)

Requester: Colin O'Donnell (Solutions Eng, Private customer pilot lead)
Requested by (ticket reporter): Fiona McCarthy (SecOps)
System(s): Redwood Private Control Plane (customer VPC), RBAC v2 enabled (pilot)
Customer context: Northbridge Healthcare (Private POC)

Reason / business justification:
- During a scheduled POC validation window, Northbridge admins were blocked from exporting audit logs and rotating an API key due to a mis-scoped role binding created during initial bootstrap.
- Customer impact: POC validation blocked; risk of missing security sign-off milestone.
- Standard path (customer admin fixes via Console) not possible because the affected binding controlled Console admin access.

Scope of requested access:
- Minimal actions required:
 1) Read RBAC bindings for org "northbridge-healthcare" in the private control pl...

Scope of requested access:
- Minimal actions required:
 1) Read RBAC bindings for org "northbridge-healthcare" in the private control pl...

comments.body: 12 PT — Fiona McCarthy (SecOps): Creating INT ticket for audit trail. Colin reports Northbridge POC blocked; Console admin role binding appears incorrect post-bootstrap. Requesting time-bound break-glass to adjust RBAC bindings at org scope only. Proposed duration 90 mins.

18 PT — David Kim (IT/Identity): Confirmed customer IdP group mapping is correct on their side (SAML assertion includes group "nbhc-redwood-admins"). Issue is likely our role template / binding mismatch, not IdP.

22 PT — Marcus Lin (Security Eng): Approved break-glass request BGW-2025-02-11-1830-PT-0427 with org-only scope. Reminder: do not use break-glass to bypass customer data controls; restrict actions to RBAC binding reads/writes + verification calls. Ensure authz decision logs captured.

24 PT — Fiona McCarthy (SecOps): Approved as second approver. Alerting configured: page SecOps if break-glass exceeds TTL or if actions outside approved permission set are observed. Please paste event IDs here once complete.

26 PT — Dr. Aisha Rahman: Acknowledged (FYI). Please ensure we capture evidence-friendly notes (who/what/why, time bounds, and validation) since this is a regulated POC and may be requested in the security review.

35 PT — Marcus Lin: Break-glass elevation started. Verified target org_id=org_7Qp1N and cluster=nbhc-private-us-west-1. Read current bindings: customer-admin group bound to role template missing audit.export and key.rotate permissions. Proceeding to add corrected binding(s) only.

02 PT — Marcus Lin: Changes applied: added org-scoped bindings for group "nbhc-redwood-admins" to roles "Security Admin" + "Read-only Auditor" (existing incorrect binding left in place for now to avoid surprises; will be cleaned up via standard process with customer). Confirmed cache invalidation occurred (policy-evaluator metrics show invalidate event for org_7Qp1N).
18 PT — Colin O'Donnell: Customer verified audit export now succeeds via Console; also verified API key rotation succeeds. They are continuing the checklist. Thanks.

41 PT — Marcus Lin: Captured audit event IDs for evidence: evt_aud_01JMB3H0KZ2R9S6R, evt_aud_01JMB3N4F1QX2D8W, evt_aud_01JMB3Q9Q0G8C3VJ. Authz decision context present (decision=allow; policy_version=rbcv2-bundle-2025.02.10).

01 PT — Fiona McCarthy: Confirming break-glass access expired on schedule. No alerts triggered for out-of-scope actions. Please schedule follow-up to remove the incorrect binding via non-break-glass path and document the root cause for bootstrap templates (ENG-18410).

10 PT — Marcus Lin: Post-event review (48-hour quick check): 1) Access used only within requested window; 2) Actions limited to RBAC binding read/write + verification; 3) Audit trail complete (ticket + BGW record + control plane audit events); 4) Follow-up: Colin to coordinate customer change window to remove obsolete binding; David to validate group mapping doc; Security Eng to add guardrail in installer bootstrap to prevent missing audit.export for Private admin templates.

title: Audit log exporter missing quant_profile_id + route identifiers for quantization profile change events
body:
Request (Compliance/Security): Update the audit log export pipeline so quantization profile change events include the required identifiers for downstream compliance reporting and customer-facing audit log exports.

Context:
- Redwood recently added route-level quantization profile enablement (opt-in) with structured audit events.
- Console change history UI is showing the right details, but the exported audit log feed (S3 -> SIEM) appears to be missing key fields (quant_profile_id + route identifiers).

Why this matters:
- Enterprise customers (and internal audits) require proof of who changed performance/cost controls and what exactly changed.
- Quantization profiles can change output quality; changes must be attributable to route + profile and searchable in the SIEM.

Observed gap:
- In exported audit events for quantization changes, we see actor + timestamp + generic event_type, bu...

Observed gap:
- In exported audit events for quantization changes, we see actor + timestamp + generic event_type, bu...

comments.author: Naomi Feldman
comments.body: Pulled a sample from the exported audit log feed (Splunk index redwood_audit_prod). For event_type=route.quantization_profile.updated the payload is missing route_id and quant_profile_id. Console UI shows the route name and profile selection, so I think the event has it but exporter is dropping it.

comments.author: Kevin Osei
comments.body: Acknowledged. Exporter uses a per-event allowlist (flatten + select) before shipping to S3/SIEM. Likely new fields weren’t added when PR-18503 landed. I’ll confirm what the event payload contains and whether we need an exporter-only change vs upstream event schema change.

comments.author: Jada Williams
comments.body: The audit event payload emitted by control plane includes route_id, route_display_name, quant_profile_ref (string), and quant_profile_id (immutable) in the event ‘details’ blob. Console reads it via the change history endpoint. If SIEM export is missing those, it’s almost certainly the exporter mapping.

comments.author: Marcus Lin
comments.body: +1 compliance need. For FalconBank we also need actor principal and request_id/correlation_id preserved so we can tie config change to API calls during reviews. Actor is required; correlation_id is strongly preferred.

comments.author: Kevin Osei
comments.body: Found root cause: exporter v2 schema flattener explicitly drops nested ‘details.*’ fields unless mapped. Quantization profile fields live under details.quantization.* and details.route.* and were never added. Proposed fix: add explicit mappings for route_id, route_name, quant_profile_id, quant_profile_ref, and diff(before/after). Keep existing top-level fields unchanged. Also adding correlation_id when present.

comments.author: Kevin Osei
comments.body: Patch merged + deployed to prod export worker. Validated by triggering a test change in staging and verifying export JSONL includes route_id + quant_profile_id. Starting a short prod verification window (next export batch) before marking resolved.

comments.author: Naomi Feldman
comments.body: Verified in Splunk: fields route_id, route_name, quant_profile_id, quant_profile_ref now present for route.quantization_profile.updated events. Actor + source preserved. This unblocks the audit log export field checklist for the security review. Thanks.

title: Private deployments: request audit log export to customer-owned object storage (S3/GCS)
body:
Issue summary
- Multiple Private prospects/customers are blocked in security review until we can export audit logs to customer-managed storage.

Impact
- Deals at risk: at least 2 (see SUP-2194 for one active account).
- Current Private audit logs are only accessible via in-cluster tooling and do not meet customer retention requirements.

Requested outcome
- Support export of audit logs to customer-owned object storage.
- Minimum: AWS S3 and/or GCS.
- Requirements noted by customers: encryption at rest, least-privilege auth, predictable prefixes, delivery gap detection.

Environment
- Private (customer-managed Kubernetes), generally EKS and GKE.

Notes
- Solutions requests Azure Blob support from one customer; not required for initial unblock but may come up.

Comments
2025-01-21 Ava Chen (reporter): Customer security team asked for "ship audit logs to our S3 bucket" + proof we don't ...

title: Console: route-level Quantization Profile toggle missing for eligible users (RBAC + feature-flag mismatch)
body:
Issue summary:
- Multiple internal users (Support + CS + Solutions) report the "Quantization profile" toggle is not visible under Console → Routes → Settings for routes where the feature should be available.
- This is blocking Support from assisting beta design partners with enable/disable and verifying applied/fallback behavior.

Impact:
- Internal teams cannot view/modify route-level quantization settings in Console, even when tenant/route is allowlisted.
- Workaround is to use internal control plane tooling / direct API calls (higher risk, no guardrails UI), or have an engineer with broader permissions perform the change.

Affected users:
- Users in roles that include read/write access to Routes but do NOT include the newly introduced quantization-specific permission.
- Most frequently observed for: Support Escalation, Customer Success, and Solutions roles.

Environment:
- Prod (us...

- Most frequently observed for: Support Escalation, Customer Success, and Solutions roles.

Environment:
- Prod (us...

comments.body: 12 Naomi Feldman (Reporter): Flagging internal helpdesk issue. Support team cannot see Quantization Profile toggle for allowlisted tenants. Engineer Admin can see it. Suspect RBAC mismatch. Can Console team confirm expected permission set + whether UI should hide vs show disabled state?

28 Chloe Martin (Assignee): Confirmed on my end. With role 'CS-Standard' I get no Quantization section. Network tab shows GET /console-backend/route-settings/quantization returns 403. UI currently suppresses the whole section when endpoint errors. This looks like capability check needs to be separated from settings read.

05 Jada Williams: +1. Hiding on 403 is confusing for audit/change-control. At minimum should show a message: 'You don't have permission to view/modify quantization settings'. We also need to ensure audit log events are still visible even if toggle isn't.

42 Naomi Feldman: RBAC mapping note: new permission appears to be 'route.quantization_settings:write' (name from internal doc). Existing roles have 'routes:write' but not the quant-specific scope. If that's by design, we need explicit UX for insufficient permission and a role update plan.

10 Chloe Martin: Root cause analysis:
- Console backend endpoint checks two things:
 (a) feature flag allowlist (tenant-level)
 (b) RBAC permission (new scope)
- On missing RBAC scope, backend returns 403.
- Console UI treats any non-200 on the 'capability' request as 'feature not enabled' and hides the entire section.
- Additionally, some tenants are allowlisted via 'quant_profiles_library_beta' flag, but Console is still checking 'optimize.quant_profiles.enabled'. That makes the capability endpoint return 404/false in those tenants.
Action: fix backend to return a structured capability response (enabled=true/false, authorized=true/false)...

03 Dylan Brooks: Aligning on expected behavior: if tenant allowlisted but user not authorized, show section with locked state + tooltip. If tenant not allowlisted, hide section entirely. Also add an internal-only banner explaining that feature may be gated during beta.

25 Chloe Martin: Implemented fix in Console backend (separate allowlist vs authorization; return 200 with capability flags instead of 403) + updated UI gating logic. Also updated feature flag key to the canonical beta flag. Pushed to staging for verification; requesting Naomi to validate with CS role.

40 Naomi Feldman: Validated in staging:
- With CS role: section now shows with lock icon + 'Insufficient permissions' copy (good).
- With Admin role: toggle and profile picker visible.
- With non-allowlisted tenant: section hidden (as intended).
Request: please ensure role docs include new permission name and add it to 'Support Escalation' role (not broad CS role) per least privilege.

15 Chloe Martin: Prod rollout plan:
1) Deploy Console backend + UI changes in next release train.
2) Update RBAC role 'Support Escalation' to include read capability for quant settings (write remains restricted).
3) Add monitoring: track 403 rate on /route-settings/quantization (should drop to near-zero) and add log annotation for authz failures.
4) Post internal note in #support and #customer-success with expected behavior and escalation path.
55 Connor O'Brien: Release train: changes included in 2025.03.06-rt1. Verified smoke tests on prod canary. No increase in error budget consumption. Proceeding to full rollout.

08 Chloe Martin: Rollout complete in prod. Validation:
- Confirmed CS role sees locked section (not hidden).
- Confirmed Support Escalation role can view (read) and Engineering Admin can edit.
- Confirmed allowlisted tenant shows section; non-allowlisted does not.
- Confirmed audit/change history page continues to render.
Closing ticket.

If users still report missing toggle, first checks:
- Tenant allowlist flag
- Console build version
- User RBAC role includes quant settings read scope
- Browser cache (feature flag caching ~5 min)

title: High attach latency and throughput collapse from small-chunk inference stream CQ saturation
body:
Issue summary: BrightWrite (dedicated pool) reported a mid-morning collapse in steady-state throughput for multiple batched routes. Impact: p50/p95 token throughput drop ~40-70% for routed models and p99 attach latency spiking to 1.2s from baseline ~80ms. Customer-facing inference requests saw elevated 5xx attach timeouts and increased fallbacks to smaller model variants. Environment: prod us-west dedicated pool, heterogeneous GPU hosts (A100 80GB + A10 mixed), customer traffic characterized by many short inference streams (~12-32 tokens) at high qps.

We observed a pattern inconsistent with normal autoscaler signals: autoscaler did not trigger additional hosts despite attach latency rising. Support triaged as potential noisy-tenant + kernel-level IO backpressure (PCIe completion queue saturation) affecting attach path and the serving scheduler fairness.

Impact details: Customer repo...

title: SIEM audit export: customer asks for audit category taxonomy + filter examples (allowlist/denylist, actor/resource predicates)
body:
Issue summary:
Quantiva Security Ops team is evaluating the SIEM-ready audit export beta (Splunk/S3) and asked for (a) the definitive category taxonomy for Audit Event Schema v2, and (b) concrete filter examples they can use to limit exported events to what their SOC needs (and minimize PII).

Customer questions (verbatim-ish from call + follow-up email):
1) “What are the exact category values, and are they stable?”
2) “Can we filter to only admin/console actions and exclude model inference traffic?”
3) “Can we do an allowlist for a few actions (e.g., workspace.user.invite, api_key.create) and denylist everything else?”
4) “Can we filter out fields like source_ip / user_agent, or at least exclude events where those are present?”
5) “Do you support filtering by actor email / resource name / prefix match?”

Impact:
Blocking customer from finalizing filter configuration for beta rollout....

Issue summary:
Quantiva Security Ops team is evaluating the SIEM-ready audit export beta (Splunk/S3) and asked for (a) the definitive category taxonomy for Audit Event Schema v2, and (b) concrete filter examples they can use to limit exported events to what their SOC needs (and minimize PII).

Customer questions (verbatim-ish from call + follow-up email):
1) “What are the exact category values, and are they stable?”
2) “Can we filter to only admin/console actions and exclude model inference traffic?”
3) “Can we do an allowlist for a few actions (e.g., workspace.user.invite, api_key.create) and denylist everything else?”
4) “Can we filter out fields like source_ip / user_agent, or at least exclude events where those are present?”
5) “Do you support filtering by actor email / resource name / prefix match?”

Impact:
Blocking customer from finalizing filter configuration for beta rollout....

comments.author: Liam O'Rourke
comments.body: Created ticket from Quantiva thread. Assigning to Colin for SE-style guidance; looping Security/PM for canonical taxonomy + doc link we can share externally.

comments.author: Colin O'Donnell, Solutions Engineer
comments.body: Acknowledged. I can provide practical examples, but we need Marcus/Kevin to confirm the canonical category list + stability guarantees. Also need to be careful: we don’t currently support filtering that inspects presence of specific fields (e.g., “exclude events with source_ip present”).

comments.author: Kevin Osei
comments.body:
Category values are intended to be stable (additive changes only during beta, no renames). Source of truth is Audit Event Schema v2 + taxonomy mapping. High-level buckets we’ve been using: auth, access, admin, data, model, billing, security, system. Some actions are also grouped under these.
PII: source_ip and user_agent are only populated for certain event types; we do not support filtering by presence of those fields today. If they need strict minimization, recommended approach is to filter out entire categories/actions that tend to include network context.
Also confirm: filtering is evaluated on event metadata (category/action + selecte...

comments.author: Naomi Feldman
comments.body:
+1. We should publish a customer-shareable “Category taxonomy + example filters” section as part of beta docs/FAQ. Important clarifications:
- Filters are about what is exported, not what is retained in Redwood.
- During beta, categories/actions may expand but we’ll avoid breaking changes.
- Non-scope: regex / prefix match on arbitrary strings; no filtering on request bodies/prompt content.
I’ll track doc update separately; for now, Support can send a short list + examples with beta disclaimers.

comments.author: Colin O'Donnell, Solutions Engineer
comments.body:
Draft response to customer (for Support to send):

1) Category taxonomy (Audit Schema v2)
We group audit events into stable categories used for filtering + SIEM mapping:
- auth: login/logout, SSO/SAML events, token/session lifecycle
- access: permission checks, membership changes (may overlap with admin depending on action)
- admin: workspace/org administration (settings, invites, role changes)
- security: key/secret lifecycle, export config changes, policy changes
- model: model access/config changes, routing policy changes (NOT per-request inference content)
- data: dataset/connectors (if applicable), artifacts metadata operations
- bill...

comments.author: Liam O'Rourke
comments.body: Sent customer response using Colin’s draft + added disclaimer: “category list is authoritative for beta but may expand; no renames planned.” Customer asked one follow-up: can they export ONLY “audit_export.*” events to monitor changes to export config itself. I told them yes via action allowlist (exact match), pending confirmation from Eng on action strings.

comments.author: Jordan Lee, Policy
comments.body:
Confirmed: action filtering is exact match on action string. For export config audit events, action names are:
- audit_export.create
- audit_export.update
- audit_export.delete
- audit_export.rotate_credentials
- audit_export.test_connection
(Names are in schema v2; ensure customer understands rotate_credentials may be admin-only).

comments.author: Colin O'Donnell, Solutions Engineer
comments.body: Quantiva confirmed this meets their needs. They will start with allowlist of [audit_export.* actions + workspace role changes]. Reminder: they still want to minimize PII. We reiterated we can’t redact per-destination fields via filtering; redaction is applied at event emission per policy. If they need stricter controls, we should discuss category/action selection and confirm which events include IP/UA.

comments.author: Liam O'Rourke
comments.body: Closing as Resolved. Outcome: provided taxonomy + examples; PM will incorporate into docs/FAQ. Next step is INT enablement ticket once they finalize destination settings.

title: Dedicated (A100 80GB, TP=4) intermittent tensor-parallel hangs causing streaming timeouts (Acme AI)
body:
Issue summary
- Customer (Acme AI, Dedicated) reports intermittent hangs on multi-GPU tensor-parallel replicas (A100 80GB, TP=4) that present as streaming requests timing out. When the hang occurs, affected replicas stop making forward progress; queued requests back up; downstream app sees elevated p99 and repeated client-side timeouts.

Impact
- Customer-facing: streaming responses stall mid-generation; client disconnects at ~60–180s depending on their gateway timeout.
- Platform: p99 latency spikes, retry storms, autoscaling churn (adds capacity but stuck replicas remain in rotation unless drained).
- Incident classification: Sev1 (enterprise Dedicated), started 2025-02-17.

Customer symptoms (from Acme)
- “Some requests never finish. They stream a few tokens and then hang until our 120s timeout.”
- Happens more during peak traffic and mixed workload (short prompts + long prompts, c...

comments.body: 58 UTC — Owen Phillips (Support) — Created ticket from Sev1 intake. Customer reports intermittent streaming hangs; attached sanitized request IDs + timestamps. Paging SRE + runtime on-call via #incidents.

07 UTC — Rafael Mendes (SRE) — Acknowledged. Seeing elevated request_timeout_rate on Acme dedicated route starting ~19:40 UTC. Manually drained 2 replicas with anomalous GPU util drop. Short-term improvement observed. Tracking in incident thread #incidents/1740165551.

19 UTC — Liam O'Rourke (TAM/Support) — Sent customer interim mitigation: cap concurrency to 24 streams/replica (from 48), reduce max_batch_tokens by 25%, and disable cuda_graphs on TP replicas for now. Asked for 3 stuck request IDs + client timestamps for correlation.

44 UTC — Noah Patel (Eng, Runtime TL) — This matches known TP stall signatures under mixed prefill/decode. Likely collective ordering/scheduler starvation interaction (collectives blocked behind prefill-heavy work). We’re working ENG-4821 initiative; near-term: try scheduler fairness guardrail flag + reduce prefill batch aggressiveness. We’ll share exact flag names once confirmed for 1.18.x.

12 UTC — Aisha Bello (SRE) — Added temporary dashboard panels for Acme: per-replica request timeout correlation + GPU util by rank. Alert draft: tp_heartbeat_gap_seconds > 30 for 2m (notify, not page) during mitigation window.

05 UTC — Rachel Kim (CS Lead) — Customer says mitigation reduced frequency but not eliminated; still saw 2 hangs during peak. They can accept reduced throughput temporarily but need a real fix/ETA. Requested weekly updates and canary plan details.
31 UTC — Hiro Tanaka (Eng, Kernels) — Recommend enabling extra NCCL debug logging for a short canary window (need helpdesk to flip safely). Opening INT-2219 to coordinate logging + rollback.
22 UTC — Tomáš Novák (SRE) — Updated internal runbook with explicit drain steps + what logs to capture (NCCL opCount, per-rank heartbeat). Shared link in incident channel and pinned to oncall notes.

47 UTC — Liam O'Rourke (TAM/Support) — Customer provided additional data: hangs correlate with long-context prompts (12k–16k) plus concurrent short requests. Notably occurs when a client cancels mid-stream (browser disconnect). Forwarded to runtime team; may match cancellation-path deadlock bug.

03 UTC — Sofia Ivanova (Eng, Scheduling) — We have a candidate fix: prevent collective starvation by enforcing ordering/fairness between prefill-heavy batches and decode steps. Targeting runtime 1.19; can provide backportable flag to reduce risk for pinned customers (lower perf).

28 UTC — Rafael Mendes (SRE) — Enabled automated drain on sustained TP heartbeat gaps for Acme dedicated cluster (guarded, rate-limited). This is a mitigation only; should reduce hang duration impact from ~120s to <60s in most cases.

40 UTC — Liam O'Rourke (TAM/Support) — Shared rollout plan with customer: Dedicated-first canary for runtime 1.19 TP stability fixes, starting with non-critical route, then expanding. Customer agreed to provide a 2-hour peak window for observation. Linked Fireflies deep-dive notes in CRM (internal).

05 UTC — Marissa Cole (Product) — Go/no-go approved for 1.19 canary for TP stability package (watchdog + scheduling + NCCL sync hardening, feature-flagged). Dedicated sequencing: Acme first (opt-in), then broader dedicated fleet.

12 UTC — Liam O'Rourke (TAM/Support) — Customer confirmed mitigations + auto-drain reduced user-visible impact; proceeding with 1.19 canary next. Leaving ticket in Resolved with workaround; will reopen if canary shows regression or continued hangs > agreed threshold.

title: Cascading client retries causing apparent inference latency for FluxHealth production traffic
body:
Issue summary:
FluxHealth reports large increase in perceived inference latency and frequent client-side timeouts for their production routes starting 2026-03-10 11:12 UTC. Customer sees 5–20x slower tail latency and many requests fail client-side before any server error is returned.

Impact:
- All FluxHealth production routes in us-east show user-visible slow responses for ~45 minutes.
- Business impact: downstream patient-facing feature degraded; customer flagged as Sev1.
- Traffic volume for this tenant is ~2.5x normal during window; Spike correlated with batch job from FluxHealth but their team says job is within expected parameters.

Environment:
- Redwood Hosted API (prod) in us-east
- Customer account: fluxhealth-prod (enterprise)

title: Variant redispatch to secondary model during staged canary due to orchestrator snapshot lag
body:
Issue summary: During a staged canary rollout for redwood-llama-7b-v2 in us-east, ~3% of BrightHealth AI traffic was unexpectedly routed to the secondary variant (quantized fallback) despite an explicit tenant model pin. Impact: customer reported mixed responses in production affecting 12 tenant sessions (chat flows). Symptoms observed at 09:21 UTC: inconsistent model outputs from the same session-id, and increased token latency (p50 -> p95).

High-level hypothesis: orchestrator snapshot application lag caused a partial control-plane commit where edge proxies had stale routing snapshots while the central router had already committed a canary weight update. This led to a redispatch path where requests that should have be pinned were re-evaluated by a reconciliation step and dispatched to the secondary variant.

Requested outcome: restore full pin coherence for affected tenant(s), ident...

comments.body: 03Z]: Customer observed mixed responses starting at ~09:20 UTC. Collecting session IDs and example payloads. Escalating to SRE and Orchestratorowners.

comments.author: Priya Nair
comments.body: 17Z]: Early look suggests snapshot apply on orchestrator leader took 6.4s due to compaction GC. Edge nodes pulled older snapshot concurrently causing transient routing divergence. Repro steps drafted.

02Z]: Running replay of affected session traffic on staging mirrored cluster to reproduce redispatch. Noted that reconciliation path triggers when commit_idx mismatch and session lease renewal coincides with healthprobe backoff.

26Z]: We saw 12 sessions with garbled summaries; thanks for the quick response. We need fix and mitigation — customer requests rollback to previous canary immediately if pin coherence cannot be guaranteed.

40Z]: Mitigation performed: paused the canary rollout, increased snapshot propagate timeout, and added a temporary edge-side check to prefer control-plane pin header until snapshot commit confirmed. Monitoring for re-occurrence.
12Z]: Patch landed to add a short-term reconciliation guard (PR linked). Next: follow-up to remove guard after we verify orchestration GC tuning and snapshot apply times are within threshold.
