title: Optimize what-if ROI estimates show unrealistically high savings (cache warm-rate assumed ~100%)
body:
Customer reports that Optimize "Enable prefix caching" recommendation shows 65–80% cost savings for their chat workload, which they believe is not achievable given their prompt variability and low cache hit rate.

Issue summary:
- Optimize UI what-if ROI for caching shows very large savings.
- Customer believes actual cache hit rate is low (~10–25%) and that estimates should reflect warm-up / low reuse.

Impact:
- Customer questions credibility of Optimize ROI and Redwood cost methodology.
- Risk: sales cycle slowdown and additional CS time.

Customer context:
- Arcadia LegalTech is evaluating Optimize for a regulated workload with heavy system prompts + per-user context. They report high prompt diversity (low expected prefix reuse).

Observed behavior:
- Optimize ROI card shows large delta in "cache savings" with little/no downside.
- When customer runs A/B manually, realized savings...

normalized.description:
Customer reports that Optimize "Enable prefix caching" recommendation shows 65–80% cost savings for their chat workload, which they believe is not achievable given their prompt variability and low cache hit rate.

Issue summary:
- Optimize UI what-if ROI for caching shows very large savings.
- Customer believes actual cache hit rate is low (~10–25%) and that estimates should reflect warm-up / low reuse.

Impact:
- Customer questions credibility of Optimize ROI and Redwood cost methodology.
- Risk: sales cycle slowdown and additional CS time.

Customer context:
- Arcadia LegalTech is evaluating Optimize for a regulated workload with heavy system prompts + per-user context. They report high prompt diversity (low expected prefix reuse).

Observed behavior:
- Optimize ROI card shows large delta in "cache savings" with little/no downside.
- When customer runs A/B manually, realized savings...

comments.body: 14 PT — Marissa Cole (Reporter): Customer (Arcadia) says Optimize caching recommendation claims 70% savings. They expect ~10–25% cache hit rate due to varied user context. They’re asking if our what-if assumes a warm cache / perfect reuse. Requesting Eng/Optimize to confirm assumptions + provide revised estimate we can share.

02 PT — Aditya Rao (Assignee): Acknowledged. Looping in Hassan (caching ROI) + Ethan (cost-model API). We recently switched ROI calcs to the standard cost model outputs; might be a default value issue in the what-if payload. Will reproduce with the same model + workload profile.

26 PT — Hassan Mirza: Repro’d in staging with similar workload summary: if cache warmness is missing, projection uses optimistic warm cache assumption (effectively warm_rate=1.0 and stable prefix reuse). That is not appropriate for first-time/low-reuse workloads. We need to either (a) force the caller to provide a warm-rate/hit-rate or (b) default to a conservative range and show CI/uncertainty.

05 PT — Ethan Park: Confirmed Cost Model API v1 what-if has an optional field for cache warmness but semantics are not explicit in schema docs. When omitted, the service currently treats it as 'steady-state warm' (historical reason: Optimize prototypes used steady-state comparisons). That’s likely the source of inflated ROI.
Action: propose changing default to 'unknown' => returns a bounded range + requires Optimize to pass a workload-derived estimate when available. We also need to avoid breaking Console callers (Console shouldn’t be using what-if today).

40 PT — Jada Williams: +1 on default behavior being dangerous. From an API standards perspective, what-if should be deterministic, but returning a confidence interval (min/max/expected) is acceptable as long as bounded and documented. If the field is optional, omitting it should not silently become best-case.

08 PT — Nadia Rahman: From telemetry, Arcadia’s prefix cache hit rate last 7d looks like ~0.18 (18%) with high variance by route. If we plug 0.15–0.25 warm/hit into the model, savings are closer to 12–22% (ballpark), not 70%. We can provide this range to Support for customer comms.

22 PT — Aditya Rao: Proposed customer message: "Earlier ROI assumed steady-state warm cache; using your measured hit rate, estimated savings are ~15–25% depending on route" + note that Optimize will be updated to ask for/workload-estimate cache warmness and display a range.
Also filing internal follow-up to update schema docs + UI defaults.

47 PT — Marissa Cole: Shared preliminary explanation with customer (no numbers yet) that our projection assumed warm cache steady-state and we’re recomputing using their hit-rate. Customer asked for written clarification we can share with finance stakeholders (what counts, what assumptions).

31 PT — Ethan Park: Engineering plan:
1) Cost Model API what-if: change omission behavior from best-case to conservative-default (e.g., warm_rate defaults to 0.2 unless caller supplies, or returns an error/warning requiring explicit input). Prefer returning 'warnings' in response + range.
2) Update schema docs to explicitly define 'cache_warm_rate' and whether it represents (a) probability of warm cache at request start vs (b) expected prefix reuse/hit probability.
3) Add validation guardrails to reject warm_rate > 0.95 unless workload explicitly claims stable prefixes.

Need signoff from Optimize + PM for behavior change (may change numbe...

18 PT — Jonas Weber: For what-if methodology, recommend response includes:
- assumptions_used: list (as a string list in our logs; for API can return warnings)
- confidence: low/med/high
- savings_range: [min,max] (we can encode as string fields if needed)
Even if v1 doesn’t support full CIs, we should at least surface 'assumed_warm_cache=true' in the response so UI can communicate it.

02 PT — Aditya Rao: Optimize change: UI currently sends warm_rate=1.0 by default when user doesn’t edit the advanced field (historical default). That’s the smoking gun. We should default to 'unset' and let API decide conservative behavior, or compute from telemetry when available.
I’ll coordinate a hotfix to set default to null/unset and show "Using measured cache hit rate" when available.

50 PT — Marissa Cole: Customer update sent with revised range using telemetry-derived hit rate (18% avg; 10–25% range). Customer response: "This is much more believable." They asked for ETA on product change so future recs don’t show best-case. They’re OK if UI says "steady-state" explicitly.

10 PT — Aditya Rao: Resolution:
- Optimize hotfix merged to stop defaulting warm_rate to 1.0; now omitted unless user explicitly sets it, and when telemetry-derived estimate exists we pass that value.
- Cost Model API team to clarify schema semantics (doc update) and add a response warning when warm-rate is omitted (tracked separately).
Closing this support ticket since customer is unblocked and understands the corrected estimate.
12 PT — Marissa Cole: Closing as resolved/closed. Customer satisfied with corrected projection + explanation. Follow-ups captured below.

title: Activation funnel dashboard discrepancy (EU-West drop + mismatch vs warehouse) after lifecycle growth instrumentation changes
body:
Issue summary
- Growth/Marketing and Product flagged a sudden drop and cross-source mismatch in the activation funnel metrics (signup → API key created → first request) used for the Lifecycle Growth Experiments sprint.
- Primary symptom: EU-West conversion from signup → api_key_created appeared ~12–18% lower than US-East starting 2026-01-21 18:00 UTC, and overall “new signups” appeared higher than expected vs auth/user tables.
- Impact: Week 1–2 experiment readouts risked being invalid/noisy; we paused one planned expansion of the email A/B until numbers were reconciled.

Impact
- Affected dashboards: Activation Funnel (Observability Pack) + internal Looker view used by Growth.
- Impacted decisions: stop/go thresholds and lift calculations for onboarding email v2 and console nudge experiments.
- Customer impact: none direct (reporting/measurement issue only), but could cause us to mak...

Dashboard shows eu-west signup→api_key_created down ~15% vs yesterday; warehouse counts don’t match. Linking Slack thread from #help where Growth asked who can debug regional missing events.

05 UTC — Logan Wright (Assignee): Acknowledged. Pulling collector + warehouse comparisons for eu-west vs us-east. Initial glance: no obvious collector ingestion lag; going to compare dedupe rates by region for signup_created.

40 UTC — Mei Lin: In Snowflake, api_key_created counts by region look stable; signup_created seems inflated in us-east starting 1/21 evening. Suspect duplicate emission rather than eu-west loss. Can share query if needed.

10 UTC — Nadia Rahman: I see two signup events per user for a subset with the same user_id but different request_id. Likely from console onboarding + auth verification both firing. Repro attempt ongoing; will coordinate with Dylan/Chloe.

30 UTC — Logan Wright: Confirmed: funnel visualization is using inconsistent dedupe keys across steps and an extra join that drops some eu-west events around UTC day boundary. Short-term mitigation: pause region comparison in readouts; use warehouse deduped table for activation rate until we patch dashboards.

55 UTC — Dylan Brooks: We can patch duplicate event emission quickly. There’s a known console/auth edge case where the signup event fires twice after verification redirect. Will ship fix behind a small canary first.

12 UTC — Logan Wright: PR-28521 opened to fix duplicate signup emission; requesting fast review from platform + console owners. Once merged, we’ll update dashboard query to use event_id-first dedupe and remove the problematic join.

20 UTC — Logan Wright: PR-28521 merged + deployed. Duplicate rate on signup_created dropped from ~7–9% to <0.5% in us-east within 2 hours. Updating Observability Pack funnel query now; will annotate dashboard for the impacted window.

05 UTC — Sergio Costa: Dashboard panels updated; verified against Mei’s warehouse query. EU-West conversion no longer shows the step-change. Added a note on the dashboard for 1/19–1/22 data interpretation.
30 UTC — Allison Grant: Closing. Please add follow-up to create an automated alert on duplicate event rates by region; this should be caught before Growth sees it in readouts.

title: Multiplexed streaming 502s during mid-morning tenant burst linked to ephemeral-worker shed
body:
Issue summary: Between 09:20 and 10:05 PDT on 2026-03-13 several customers observed intermittent 502 responses on multiplexed streaming endpoints. The errors coincided with a mid-morning tenant burst from a few heavy tenants on Dedicated pools. Impact: streaming sessions saw partial responses followed by 502s; reconnect loops caused visible degradation for ScribeAI (dedicated tenant) and a small set of other tenants. Not a global outage but high error-rate window for affected tenants.

Observed behavior: API gateway returned 502s with upstream read timeout/connection reset. Upstream runtime pods reported short-lived OOM restarts and voluntary eviction of ephemeral worker threads. Autoscaler logs show a rapid shed of ephemeral workers while persistent workers remained. Traffic pattern: long-lived multiplexed HTTP/2 streams with periodic large token bursts (embedding + generation mix).

title: Intermittent stuck stream (no [DONE]) during peak load
body:
Issue summary: Customer sees intermittent streaming requests that stop emitting tokens and never terminate with [DONE]. They report this clusters around peak traffic windows.

Impact: About 1–3% of streaming requests hang; they must cancel client-side and retry.

Environment: Dedicated in us-east, high QPS, stream=true for most calls.

Investigation hypothesis: backend worker may be wedged while holding a stream open; gateway keeps connection alive due to heartbeat but no new tokens are generated.

title: Streaming sometimes stops mid-generation (no final chunk) on Hosted API
body:
Customer reports that streaming responses occasionally stop without sending a final chunk / [DONE]. They do not always see a network error, but their SDK callback stops firing and they end up with a truncated completion. Happens sporadically, more noticeable during peak hours.

Customer wording (from CSM): "stream just ends halfway through a sentence".

Initial hypothesis: client-side read timeout OR server-side stream closed due to upstream cancellation/backpressure.

Initial hypothesis: client-side read timeout OR server-side stream closed due to upstream cancellation/backpressure.

title: Streaming chat requests time out at 120s for Pinecone Labs (eu-west)
body:
Issue summary: Customer using streaming chat endpoint reports responses cut off at ~120 seconds for long generations. Client receives EOF; they retry and sometimes succeed.

Impact: Affects long-running customer support agent flows; 'partial answers' and retries increasing cost.

Environment: Dedicated cluster behind ALB (eu-west).

Observed: ALB idle timeout 120s. Streaming chunks are not emitted frequently enough during long tool execution.

Ask: Can Redwood send keepalive chunks / heartbeat events during tool execution and/or update recommended ALB settings?

Observed: ALB idle timeout 120s. Streaming chunks are not emitted frequently enough during long tool execution.

comments.author: Naomi Feldman (CSM)
comments.body: Customer says 'streaming starts then pauses for 2+ minutes when tool call runs' -> ALB closes.

comments.author: Hiro Tanaka (Platform)
comments.body: Confirmed ALB idle_timeout=120s on their dedicated ingress. Current behavior: during tool execution we don't emit stream chunks.

comments.author: Hiro Tanaka
comments.body: Workaround: raise idle timeout to 300s and enable TCP keepalive. Shared doc snippet with customer.

comments.author: Elliot Price (Runtime)
comments.body: Longer-term: add optional heartbeat event 'event: ping' every 15s when generation is blocked on tool. Tracking in internal issue INT-812.
Customer updated ALB idle timeout to 300s; confirmed no more mid-stream disconnects. Ticket closed.

title: token accounting discrepancy between streaming usage and invoice export
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports a token accounting discrepancy: the Usage page (streaming chat route) shows ~18% more billable tokens than what they compute from request/response logs. Their invoice export CSV matches the higher number.

Impact:
Customer is pausing rollout until they can reconcile token counts for February.

Steps to reproduce (from customer):
1) Call /v1/chat/completions with streaming enabled
2) Sum prompt_tokens + completion_tokens from the final chunk they receive
3) Compare to billable tokens shown in Console > Usage and invoice export

Notes:
- Customer claims some requests show completion_tokens=0 in their logs while they still received text
- They suspect retransmits / reconnects

Requested outcome:
Explain what counts as billable tokens for streaming and whether retries can double-count.

comments.author: Ava Chen ()
comments.body: Looping in runtime + billing. Customer explicitly says: 'token accounting discrepancy' and wants a definitive explanation. Any known behavior with streaming reconnect?

comments.author: Dev Patel ()
comments.body: Looking at metrics pipeline. Initial guess: client reconnect causes a second stream; we count both if we send tokens twice. Need to confirm whether they are reusing the same request id.

comments.author: Priya Natarajan ()
comments.body: Found duplicate request_ids from their integration. They retry on HTTP 499 but keep the same external correlation id, different Redwood request id. So their log reconciliation is missing retries.

comments.author: Ava Chen ()
comments.body: Sharing guidance with customer: billable tokens are counted per successful token emitted; retries that receive tokens will be billed. Also sending link to SDK example for idempotency keys.

comments.author: Dev Patel ()
comments.body: Added console tooltip + docs patch queued: clarify streaming reconnect/retry semantics + recommend idempotency-key header to prevent double work.

title: Hosted API: intermittent SSE streaming disconnects (client sees connection closed)
body:
Issue summary:
Acme Analytics reports intermittent disconnects during streaming responses on the Hosted API. Their client receives "connection closed" / EOF while the model is still generating. Retries usually succeed.

Impact:
Customer chat feature drops partial completions; end-users see truncated responses ~1-3% of requests.

Environment:
Hosted API (OpenAI-compatible), streaming=true, us-east.

Steps to reproduce (customer):
1) Start a chat completion with streaming enabled (SSE).
2) After ~10-40 seconds, stream terminates unexpectedly.

Logs / notes:
Customer shared: "unexpected EOF reading response body" and occasional "http2: stream closed".
Support observed brief 499/connection resets at the edge around the reported times.

Support observed brief 499/connection resets at the edge around the reported times.

comments.author: Naomi Feldman () 2026-03-08
comments.body: Escalating to Eng; seeing multiple similar reports this week. Slack thread: #support https://slack/redwood/archives/support/p1772951300

comments.author: Ethan Park (Platform) 2026-03-09
comments.body: We might be hitting idle timeout / proxy buffering edge case on long streams. Pulling envoy/nginx timeouts for streaming routes.

comments.author: Aisha Rahman (SRE) 2026-03-09
comments.body: Noticed a small spike in upstream "downstream_cx_destroy" in us-east around 03/08 22:00-23:00 UTC. Correlates with edge deploy rwd-edge-1.42.0.

comments.author: Ethan Park 2026-03-10
comments.body: Reproduced in staging with forced long generations; the edge was closing connections when response headers were delayed after a brief backpressure window.

comments.author: Naomi Feldman 2026-03-11
comments.body: Customer asks for workaround; they can increase client retry but want root cause.

comments.author: Ethan Park 2026-03-12
comments.body: Mitigation shipped: increased streaming route timeouts and disabled response buffering for SSE on rwd-edge-1.42.1 in us-east. Monitoring 24h.

title: TypeScript SDK: streaming chat drops/misses chunks under high throughput
body: Customer reports TS SDK streaming chat sometimes yields incomplete output (missing words/segments) while server logs show full token stream. Observed in Node 20 and Bun. Using redwood-sdk-typescript 2.4.0. They concatenate chunk deltas; output sometimes skips a delta event. Suspected cause: stream parser treats multi-line SSE events incorrectly when proxy coalesces packets.

normalized.description: Customer reports TS SDK streaming chat sometimes yields incomplete output (missing words/segments) while server logs show full token stream. Observed in Node 20 and Bun. Using redwood-sdk-typescript 2.4.0. They concatenate chunk deltas; output sometimes skips a delta event. Suspected cause: stream parser treats multi-line SSE events incorrectly when proxy coalesces packets.

comments.author: (Aisha)
comments.body: Asked for raw event payloads; customer attached a dump showing events that include multiple 'data:' lines.

comments.author: (Dylan)
comments.body: Reproduced with a local proxy that merges frames; our parser only reads first data line and ignores the rest. Will fix to accumulate all data lines until blank line terminator.

comments.author: (Dylan)
comments.body: Fix in progress; adding regression test with multiline SSE frames.

title: Private deployment: permission denied when exporting audit logs after RBAC v2 rollout
body:
Customer (Northbridge Healthcare) reports that audit log export started failing with 403 PermissionDenied immediately after enabling RBAC v2 on their Private deployment. They are attempting to export audit logs to their SIEM integration for daily compliance review.

Impact:
- Security team cannot export audit logs from Redwood Console or via API.
- Blocks compliance evidence collection for internal audit window (customer stated deadline: end of week).

Notes:
- Customer indicates exports worked the prior week.
- They recently upgraded their Private deployment and enabled RBAC v2 feature flag per rollout guidance.
- Customer uses SSO group mappings -> built-in role templates; no custom roles yet.

normalized.description:
Customer (Northbridge Healthcare) reports that audit log export started failing with 403 PermissionDenied immediately after enabling RBAC v2 on their Private deployment. They are attempting to export audit logs to their SIEM integration for daily compliance review.

Impact:
- Security team cannot export audit logs from Redwood Console or via API.
- Blocks compliance evidence collection for internal audit window (customer stated deadline: end of week).

Notes:
- Customer indicates exports worked the prior week.
- They recently upgraded their Private deployment and enabled RBAC v2 feature flag per rollout guidance.
- Customer uses SSO group mappings -> built-in role templates; no custom roles yet.

comments.author: Colin O'Donnell
comments.body: Opened on behalf of Northbridge after POC admin reports they cannot export audit logs. Customer suspects RBAC v2 change; started immediately after upgrade/flag enablement. Asking for quick workaround due to audit deadline.

comments.author: Kevin Osei
comments.body: Acknowledged. Requesting: policy bundle version, Private platform version, whether export is Console-only or also API, and one request_id for correlation. Also asking customer to confirm which IdP group/role mapping is in use.

comments.author: Colin O'Donnell
comments.body: Customer provided request_id req_9c7f2b2c0c4c and confirmed it fails both in Console and direct API call. Policy version shown in debug header: rbac-bundle-2025.02.21. Group mapped to "Read-only Auditor" template.

comments.author: Kevin Osei
comments.body: Found deny log in audit-exporter with reason SUBJECT_MISSING_PERMISSION for action audit.export. This looks like expected enforcement + missing permission in role template. Coordinating with Security Eng to confirm intended role coverage.

comments.author: Marcus Lin
comments.body: Confirmed audit.export is intentionally separate from audit.read for least privilege; however the built-in auditor template should include export for most enterprise setups. We should update role templates and document recommended split (Auditor vs Auditor-NoExport).

comments.author: Kevin Osei
comments.body: Provided workaround to customer: create custom role with audit.export at org scope and bind to their IdP group. Customer will apply immediately while we prep updated policy bundle.

comments.author: Kevin Osei
comments.body: Role template change landed in policy bundle; advising customer to update to new bundle artifact and resync. Noting cache TTL up to 60s; may need to re-save binding to trigger invalidation. (Related: cache TTL discussion in #eng-platform thread 1739808400.)

comments.author: Colin O'Donnell
comments.body: Customer upgraded bundle and confirmed exports are now succeeding; SIEM push also successful. Requesting confirmation that audit log entries include authz context for their SOC evidence.

comments.author: Kevin Osei
comments.body: Confirmed exporter emits authz context fields (decision, action, policy_version, reason codes) aligned with the authz decision schema. Closing ticket.

title: Request: add/update approved Enterprise Security RFP snippets in Generic Response Tool (GRT)
body:
Request to add/update standardized RFP response snippets in the Generic Response Tool (GRT) used by AEs/SEs.

Background:
We are refreshing the Enterprise Security Enablement Kit and need GRT to reflect the same approved language. Field is currently copy/pasting from older docs, which is causing version drift (esp. ISO wording, data residency nuance, and audit log export claims).

Request scope:
1) Create a new snippet pack: "Enterprise Security (2026)" with curated blocks by category.
2) Deprecate/redirect older snippets tagged "Security FAQ 2024/2025" to reduce accidental use.
3) Add guardrails: a short header on each snippet reminding users to not modify compliance claims without review + link to evidence request process.

Categories + required snippet titles (exact):
A. Security Program
- "Security program overview (SOC 2)"
- "Vulnerability management + penetration testing (approv...

normalized.description:
Request to add/update standardized RFP response snippets in the Generic Response Tool (GRT) used by AEs/SEs.

Background:
We are refreshing the Enterprise Security Enablement Kit and need GRT to reflect the same approved language. Field is currently copy/pasting from older docs, which is causing version drift (esp. ISO wording, data residency nuance, and audit log export claims).

Request scope:
1) Create a new snippet pack: "Enterprise Security (2026)" with curated blocks by category.
2) Deprecate/redirect older snippets tagged "Security FAQ 2024/2025" to reduce accidental use.
3) Add guardrails: a short header on each snippet reminding users to not modify compliance claims without review + link to evidence request process.

Categories + required snippet titles (exact):
A. Security Program
- "Security program overview (SOC 2)"
- "Vulnerability management + penetration testing (approv...

comments.body: 14 PT — Isabelle Laurent (Reporter): Creating this ticket to operationalize the kit refresh. We keep seeing custom edits in questionnaires. Please prioritize ISO + audit log export + data residency snippets; those are the highest-risk for misstatement.

02 PT — Dev Patel (Assignee): Acknowledged. Quick questions: (1) Do we want the snippets to be editable in GRT by anyone or locked to admins only? (2) For deprecating old snippets, do we hide them or keep searchable with a warning banner?
18 PT — Isabelle Laurent: Admin-only edits preferred. For old snippets: ideally hidden from default search; if not possible, keep searchable but prepend "DEPRECATED" + link to the Confluence index.

41 PT — Dev Patel: GRT supports admin-only packs. For deprecation we can (a) remove from default pack list and (b) add a deprecation note in body, but cannot fully prevent search. Proposed workaround: rename old pack to "[DEPRECATED] Security FAQ (2024/2025)" and add a first line warning.

55 PT — Dev Patel: Imported draft snippets from rfp-response-library-master-draft. Need Security/GRC to confirm final language for: ISO alignment paragraph, audit log export limitations, and "no training" definition. Tagging approvers per ticket.

08 PT — Marcus Lin: For audit log export: ensure wording says exports are supported "via configured destinations" and that event coverage depends on enabled products/deployment mode. Do not claim "all admin actions" unless we enumerate them. Use the Confluence audit logging overview as reference.

22 PT — Olga Petrov: ISO wording must be "aligned with many ISO 27001 control themes" and must NOT state or imply certification. Also add a sentence: "We can share SOC 2 Type II report under NDA via the evidence request process."

03 PT — Camila Reyes: Please keep snippet intros consistent: short, factual, and avoid "best-in-class". Add the guardrail line: "If a questionnaire requires commitments beyond this text, escalate via Security Review Escalation + Handoff."

27 PT — Dev Patel: Updates applied. Added "Last reviewed: 2026-02-14 | Owners: Security/GRC" line to each snippet. Renamed old pack to "[DEPRECATED] Security FAQ (2024/2025)" and inserted top-of-snippet warnings + link to Confluence index.

06 PT — Isabelle Laurent: Tested in GRT. Formatting preserved, categories filter works. One nit: "Private deployment" snippet should mention customer-managed networking boundary but avoid implying we manage customer IAM in on-prem. Otherwise good.
40 PT — Dev Patel: Adjusted Private snippet to clarify shared responsibility + customer-managed networking/IAM boundaries. Ready to close; please confirm.
12 PT — Isabelle Laurent: Confirmed. Closing ticket. Will reference the "Enterprise Security (2026)" pack in the sales training notes and HubSpot snippets next.

title: Enable Dedicated staged rollouts feature flag for beta design partners (account/pool scoped)
body:
Request: enable the Dedicated staged rollouts beta feature flag for specific Dedicated accounts/pools so CS/TAM + SRE can run staged upgrades with gate evaluation and rollback semantics.

Context:
- Project: "Dedicated staged rollouts for reserved pools"
- We are moving from internal dogfood to limited beta (1–2 design partners) and need scoped enablement by account + pool (+ region where applicable).
- This is NOT general customer self-serve; only for beta accounts with operator involvement.

comments.body: 14 (Liam O'Rourke, Reporter): Customer success wants to schedule the first staged upgrade dry-run with Lattice AI this week. Requesting enablement ASAP, scoped to their Dedicated pool only. EmberSearch is next week if Lattice goes smoothly.

02 (Rafael Mendes): Picking this up. Before toggling prod flags: need explicit signoff on (a) automated gates on/off for beta, (b) audit event visibility/retention for force actions, and (c) confirmation that pool sizes meet partitioning minimums.

25 (Lauren Bishop): Approved for beta enablement for Lattice AI + EmberSearch, pool-scoped only. For week 1, prefer manual gates (operators verify dashboards) unless SRE is comfortable with the automated gate behavior under telemetry lag.

10 (Priya Natarajan): Engineering signoff. Please ensure the control plane deploy in prod is >= build redwood-controlplane@2025.03.02. Also confirm idempotency keys are being used by Console backend for promote/abort.

04 (Kaitlyn Nguyen): Security/RBAC check: OK to enable as long as force-promote/hold remains admin-only and emits audit events with actor + reason. Please confirm ff scope is restricted to the account_ids listed and not org-wide for the tenant.

32 (Vanessa Ortiz): Infra note: Lattice pool_rsrv_7c1b2a has 32x H100 across 2 regions; OK for 1%/10% stages. EmberSearch pool_rsrv_5a88e1 is smaller (8x H100 single region). Recommend stage plan uses replica partitions not 1% traffic split; otherwise canary may starve capacity.

05 (Rafael Mendes): Plan: enable staged rollouts + UI for both accounts today. Automated gates will remain OFF (manual gates only) for week 1. We can toggle automated gates after we confirm telemetry freshness and no cardinality regressions.

40 (Rafael Mendes): Executed enablement in prod:
- Enabled ff.dedicated_rollouts.staged.enabled scoped to acct_9f2d3c1b/pool_rsrv_7c1b2a and acct_21a6b0fe/pool_rsrv_5a88e1.
- Enabled ff.console.dedicated_rollouts.staged_ui scoped to acct_9f2d3c1b and acct_21a6b0fe.
- Confirmed ff.dedicated_rollouts.gates_automated_v1 remains disabled.
Verification:
- Console timeline visible for both orgs.
- Gate evidence panel populates (shows "Manual verification required" state).
- Stage-tagged metrics present; no immediate cardinality anomalies.
Posted details to #support and #eng-oncall with rollback steps.

22 (Logan Wright): Telemetry spot-check: stage_id tag is present on request_latency_ms, request_errors_total, gpu_utilization, and oom_events. Label allowlist looks correct. If you see evidence panel empty, it’s usually the stage_id vs rollout_stage_id mismatch (fixed in latest Console backend).

10 (Liam O'Rourke): Lattice AI can see the staged rollout UI now. Scheduling the first plan creation with SRE tomorrow. EmberSearch acknowledged the beta limitations and agrees to operator-led rollout.

48 (Rafael Mendes): Closing. Enablement complete and validated. Follow-up: after first Lattice run, revisit whether to enable automated gates for these accounts (new request/ticket) and update the beta checklist with the EmberSearch small-pool stage guidance.

title: Acme Banking: Splunk SIEM integration request for Redwood audit logs (HEC export) + timeline pressure for security audit
body:
Issue summary:
Acme Banking is requesting a supported integration to export Redwood audit logs into Splunk (preferably Splunk HEC) as part of their security monitoring + compliance program.

Customer impact / urgency:
- Customer has an internal security review and external audit window starting ~2025-03-15.
- They need a concrete plan for SIEM ingestion (what events, delivery guarantees, and an ETA) to unblock a broader rollout of Redwood.
- They are explicitly asking for "near real-time" export of admin and access events (config changes, key/credential changes, RBAC changes, login events, API key usage, etc.).

What customer is asking for:
1) A supported way to stream/forward audit events to Splunk (HEC preferred).
2) Guidance on field mapping (CIM-ish naming conventions) and deduplication expectations.
3) Clarification on delivery semantics (exactly-once vs at-least-once), retention...

3) Clarification on delivery semantics (exactly-once vs at-least-once), retention...

comments.body:
2025-02-21 | Liam O'Rourke (Reporter) | Customer (Acme) request received via email
"We need Redwood audit logs in Splunk (Splunk Cloud). Our audit window begins mid-March. Console visibility isn't sufficient—we need automated ingestion with reliability semantics documented. Is HEC supported? If not, can we get S3 delivery that Splunk can pull from?"

2025-02-21 | Colin O'Donnell (Assignee) | Initial triage
Thanks—looping in Security and Product. I believe Splunk HEC is part of the upcoming SIEM-ready audit export beta (feature-flagged). Need to confirm eligibility and target timeline for a beta tenant allowlist enable.

Actions:
- Ask customer for Splunk HEC endpoint format (event collector URL), index/source/sourcetype preferences, and whether they require client certs.
- Confirm their outbound allowlist requirements (domain/IP ranges) so we can validate exporter egress controls.

2025-02-22 | Marcus Lin (Security) | Security considerations
We can support at-least-once delivery with event_id for downstream dedup. Must be explicit that duplicates can occur and Splunk should dedup on event_id.
PII: IP/user-agent are "where applicable" and may be omitted depending on event type; we should not promise full IP coverage for all actions. Also ensure we do not include request payloads or prompt content.

Please use the beta security FAQ language for customer-facing statements.

2025-02-24 | Naomi Feldman (Product) | Beta access guidance
Acme is a strong candidate for the limited beta, but we need to confirm (a) volume expectations and (b) whether they can accept beta limitations (no Private/on-prem, limited backfill window, at-least-once semantics).
If Acme agrees, file internal enablement request with tenant ID and target destination (Splunk HEC).

2025-02-25 | Colin O'Donnell (Assignee) | Follow-up questions sent to customer
Sent questions:
1) Confirm Splunk HEC URL and whether you will use raw endpoint vs services/collector/event.
2) Confirm index/source/sourcetype and whether you want gzip compression.
3) Confirm expected steady-state events/sec and peak bursts.
4) Confirm whether you can accept at-least-once delivery (possible duplicates) with event_id-based dedup.
5) Confirm minimum acceptable backfill (e.g., 24h/72h) and whether you need replays.

Also shared preliminary workaround: S3 delivery (JSONL partitions) + Splunk S3 input, if HEC is delayed.

comments.author:
| Liam O'Rourke () | Customer responses
Customer confirms
comments.body:
- Splunk Cloud HEC endpoint: https://http-inputs-<redacted>.splunkcloud.com/services/collector/event
- Wants index=redwood_audit, sourcetype=redwood:audit
- Can accept gzip.
- Peak bursts: up to ~2,000 events/sec during deploys; steady ~200–400 events/sec.
- At-least-once is acceptable if event_id is stable.
- Backfill: minimum 72 hours preferred; 24 hours acceptable for beta if clearly stated.
- Outbound allowlist: they will allowlist Splunk HEC endpoint only; no inbound requirements.

2025-02-28 | Colin O'Donnell (Assignee) | Internal enablement request
Filed INT-9012 to enable SIEM audit export beta for Acme tenant allowlist once approved. Requested Splunk HEC destination with:
- index=redwood_audit
- sourcetype=redwood:audit
- source=redwood
- gzip enabled

Also asked Platform team to confirm egress allowlist behavior and destination host validation requirements.

2025-03-03 | Rafael Mendes (SRE) | Operational expectations for beta
Please ensure the customer is aligned on:
- Delivery is at-least-once; duplicates possible.
- Export lag SLO is "minutes" under normal conditions; can increase under downstream 429s.
- If Splunk throttles (429), exporter backs off and retries; customer should monitor for lag rather than expecting strict real-time.
- We can pause exports as mitigation during extended downstream issues.

Support should set expectations that this is beta and subject to tuning.

2025-03-04 | Colin O'Donnell (Assignee) | Customer-facing update drafted
Draft sent to AM/CSM for review:
- Redwood will support Splunk HEC export via SIEM-ready audit export beta.
- At-least-once delivery with stable event_id; customers should dedup on event_id.
- Backfill window for beta: up to 24 hours (working to extend; GA target larger). Customer can request manual replay assistance if needed.
- Fields: actor/action/resource/tenant/workspace/timestamps, plus source IP/user-agent where applicable; PII minimized.
- TLS in transit; tokens stored securely; RBAC required for export configuration.

Pending: confirm exact beta enablement date.

2025-03-05 | Liam O'Rourke (Reporter) | Customer accepted beta limitations
Customer confirmed acceptance of beta limitations (at-least-once, 24h backfill for beta) and wants enablement by week of 2025-03-10 if possible.

Closing this support request as "Resolved" once enablement is tracked under INT-9012; will continue comms on INT ticket.

title: Audit log export missing event types (customer evidence export incomplete)
body:
Issue summary:
Northbridge Bank reports that audit log exports (CSV/JSON evidence package) are missing certain event types that are visible in the Console audit log viewer / internal query results. This is blocking their security review because the exported evidence does not show expected access/admin actions.

Customer impact:
- Compliance evidence export appears incomplete (missing key admin/RBAC-related actions), raising concerns about audit log coverage.
- Procurement/security review is time-sensitive; they requested an updated export for the last 30 days.

Environment:
- Product: Redwood Dedicated
- Region: us-east
- Export method: Audit Log Exporter (report template-based export)
- Export formats: CSV + JSON bundle

Reported missing event types (examples from customer):
- rbac.role_assignment.created
- rbac.role_assignment.updated
- api_key.created
- api_key.revoked
(They also e...

(They also e...

comments.body: 12 PT — Rachel Kim (CS): Customer escalated during security review call. They can see RBAC/API key changes in Console, but the exported evidence package does not include those events. Requested: last 30 days, all admin/security-relevant actions, delivered as CSV + JSON. Looping in Kevin Osei for exporter investigation.

03 PT — Kevin Osei (Eng): Acknowledged. I pulled the export template used by the exporter. It’s applying a hardcoded event_type IN (...) filter. RBAC + api_key events are not in the list. Next: confirm taxonomy ownership + whether those events should be included in the "Customer Evidence - Admin Actions" template. If yes, patch template/filtering and ship.

24 PT — Olga Petrov (GRC): From an evidence perspective, RBAC role assignment and API key lifecycle events must be in the baseline pack. If those are emitted in audit logs, exports should include them. Console login events are nice-to-have but we should be careful about scope/definition; prioritize RBAC + credential lifecycle.

05 PT — Kevin Osei (Eng): Confirmed via control-plane query: events exist for NBK-*** in the requested window (rbac.role_assignment.updated, api_key.revoked). Exporter template filter is the reason they’re missing. Root cause: exporter’s template allowlist wasn’t updated when new event types were added; also the exporter UI/CLI doesn’t clearly indicate it’s filtering by allowlist vs "all events".

22 PT — Rachel Kim (CS): Replying to customer: we found a filtering issue in the export tool. We’ll provide a corrected export and follow up with a permanent fix. Requested confirmation: time window 2025-01-20 through 2025-02-19 UTC, and include RBAC + credential lifecycle categories.

18 PT — Kevin Osei (Eng): Fix is being rolled into audit-log-exporter improvements to support time-windowed exports and explicit filters (PR-612). As part of that, we’re updating the default templates and adding an "All event types" mode that does not apply the stale allowlist unless explicitly requested.

41 PT — Kevin Osei (Eng): Workaround for today: I ran a direct export using an internal query path (no allowlist) and validated event_type distribution includes RBAC + api_key events. Shared package with Rachel for customer delivery (internal link in ticket: /secure/attachments/SUP-3917/northbridge_audit_export_fixed_2025-02-20.zip).

07 PT — Rachel Kim (CS): Customer confirmed receipt and that RBAC + API key events are now present. They asked for a brief written explanation of why the initial export was incomplete and what changed. I’ll send: “template filter allowlist was outdated; exporter fix in progress; we validated completeness against source audit log store.”

33 PT — Kevin Osei (Eng): PR-612 merged. Includes: (1) time-windowed exports, (2) explicit event-type filters, (3) template refresh to include RBAC + api_key categories, and (4) clearer metadata in the export manifest indicating applied filters. Deployment scheduled for next exporter release train.

55 PT — Kevin Osei (Eng): Deployed audit-log-exporter release containing PR-612 to prod. Re-ran the standard "Customer Evidence - Admin Actions" template for NBK-*** and confirmed RBAC + api_key events are included. Marking Resolved.

02 PT — Rachel Kim (CS): Closing loop with customer: exporter fix deployed; future exports should include the missing categories. Also pointed them to the updated evidence template naming and asked them to re-run from their side if needed. Moving ticket to Resolved.

title: LDAP referral chasing disabled in private deployment causing intermittent missing AD group membership and RBAC failures
body:
Issue summary:
Customers using Redwood Private with Active Directory that exposes LDAP referrals intermittently lose group membership for users who are resolved via referred directory servers. Affected users are unable to perform RBAC-protected actions (console admin routes and model pinning) until a manual remap or connector restart is performed.

Impact:
- Several users at Acme Retail reported inability to access the Redwood Console role 'org-admin' and received 403 on admin API calls.
- Incidents are intermittent (approx 5-10% of auth flows) and correlate with time windows when referrals are returned by AD DS domain controllers.
- Affects only customers on private deployments using LDAP over the internal network where referrals are expected (multi-domain forest).

Environment:
- Redwood Private (v2026.02.1) deployed in customer VPC (us-east).
- LDAP connector configured with primar...

comments.author: Mateo Alvarez ()
comments.body: Received initial report from Acme Retail CS. Confirmed symptoms and requested LDAP logs + sample ldapsearch. Asked for time windows when issue occurred.

comments.author: Acme Engineer (Customer)
comments.body: Provided connector logs and ldapsearch output showing referral response pointing to dc2.acme.local and noted that referrals are expected in their multi-domain AD topology.

comments.author: Clara Ruiz (SRE)
comments.body: Reproduced locally in staging by spinning up multi-domain AD. Confirmed referralChasing=false causes missing groups. Verified setting referralChasing=true fixes group lookup.

comments.author: Gabe Thompson (, )
comments.body: Applied temporary config change to customer's private control plane to enable referralChasing=true after getting customer admin approval. Restarted connector; customer confirmed immediate improvement.

comments.author: Emily Zhang
comments.body: Flagged the security considerations. Requested design for follow-limited mode and an allowlist for referral hosts before enabling by default. Opened INT-4421 for the security review.

comments.author: Nina Patel (CS)
comments.body: Customer accepted workaround and requested timeline for permanent fix. Asked that we prioritize KB and a patch for the follow-limited mode.

comments.author: Gabe Thompson ()
comments.body: Documented remediation plan and target release v2026.03.3. Assigned follow-ups to Engineering and Security. Marking ticket Triage until patch lands.

title: Private install: Grafana loads but no Redwood dashboards (minimum observability pack)
body:
Issue summary
- Customer completed Redwood Private install (including the “observability minimum pack”). Grafana UI is reachable and Prometheus is scraping targets, but there are no Redwood dashboards visible under Browse/Dashboards.

Customer impact
- Customer platform team cannot validate runtime health post-install and is blocked on their internal acceptance checklist for go-live.
- No production outage, but reduces operational visibility and delays launch.

Customer environment (as provided)
- Deployment type: On-prem / air-gapped
- Installer version: redwood-private-installer v1.12.3 (offline bundle)
- Helm charts: redwood-helm-charts 1.12.3
- Observability pack: included in offline bundle (customer unsure which pack version)
- Namespace(s): redwood-system (control plane), redwood-runtime (data plane), monitoring (Grafana/Prometheus)
- Grafana: 10.4.x (from our bundled chart)
- P...

normalized.description:
Issue summary
- Customer completed Redwood Private install (including the “observability minimum pack”). Grafana UI is reachable and Prometheus is scraping targets, but there are no Redwood dashboards visible under Browse/Dashboards.

Customer impact
- Customer platform team cannot validate runtime health post-install and is blocked on their internal acceptance checklist for go-live.
- No production outage, but reduces operational visibility and delays launch.

Customer environment (as provided)
- Deployment type: On-prem / air-gapped
- Installer version: redwood-private-installer v1.12.3 (offline bundle)
- Helm charts: redwood-helm-charts 1.12.3
- Observability pack: included in offline bundle (customer unsure which pack version)
- Namespace(s): redwood-system (control plane), redwood-runtime (data plane), monitoring (Grafana/Prometheus)
- Grafana: 10.4.x (from our bundled chart)
- P...

comments.body:
12 (Dev Patel - Support)
Created ticket from customer email + #support handoff. Requested environment details and installer/Helm versions. Asked for: `kubectl get cm -A | grep grafana` and Grafana pod logs around provisioning start.

03 (Customer - Northbridge Health Analytics)
Install finished successfully and health checks passed. We can log into Grafana, Prometheus datasource works, but there are no Redwood dashboards. `kubectl get cm -n monitoring | grep -i redwood` returns nothing. We are air-gapped and used the offline bundle from Redwood. NetworkPolicies are enabled cluster-wide.

26 (Bruno Silva - PS / Private Deployments)
Likely either (1) dashboards not applied because the pack values were off, or (2) dashboards are created in a different namespace than Grafana’s sidecar is watching. Can you run `kubectl get cm -A | grep -i grafana` and `kubectl describe deploy grafana -n monitoring` (look for sidecar args)?

02 (Customer - Northbridge Health Analytics)
`kubectl get cm -A | grep -i grafana` shows:
- redwood-system/redwood-private-observability-dashboards-1
- redwood-runtime/redwood-runtime-dashboards-1
Both have labels:
- app.kubernetes.io/managed-by=Helm
- redwood.ai/observability-pack=true
No label like grafana_dashboard=1.
`kubectl describe deploy grafana -n monitoring` shows sidecar enabled but label selector is `grafana_dashboard=1` and namespace is monitoring only.

20 (Dylan Brooks - Dashboards/tooling)
That explains it: our dashboard ConfigMaps in 1.12.3 are labeled `redwood.ai/observability-pack=true` (not `grafana_dashboard=1`). Either update sidecar label selector to match our label, or (safer) add `grafana_dashboard=1` label to the existing ConfigMaps. Also, sidecar is only monitoring namespace; if ConfigMaps live in redwood-system/redwood-runtime you need searchNamespace=ALL or copy the ConfigMaps into monitoring.
Proposed immediate workaround:
1) Label ConfigMaps: `kubectl label cm -n redwood-system redwood-private-observability-dashboards-1 grafana_dashboard=1 --overwrite` and same for runtim...

05 (Bruno Silva - PS / Private Deployments)
Customer prefers not to run sidecar across all namespaces due to security review. Suggested option: copy dashboards ConfigMaps into monitoring namespace.
Commands:
- `kubectl get cm -n redwood-system redwood-private-observability-dashboards-1 -o yaml | sed 's/namespace: redwood-system/namespace: monitoring/' | kubectl apply -n monitoring -f -`
- Repeat for `redwood-runtime/redwood-runtime-dashboards-1`
- Label the new CMs: `kubectl label cm -n monitoring <cm-name> grafana_dashboard=1 --overwrite`
Then restart Grafana.
Also asked customer to confirm Grafana SA has list/watch on ConfigMaps in monit...

18 (Customer - Northbridge Health Analytics)
We copied both ConfigMaps into monitoring and labeled `grafana_dashboard=1`. After restarting Grafana, we now see a “Redwood Private (Minimum Pack)” folder and dashboards are present. Panels were initially empty; after ~2 minutes they started populating. This unblocks us.
Question: is there an official supported fix so we don’t have to maintain copies after upgrades?

01 (Dev Patel - Support)
Confirmed the workaround is acceptable short-term but we want a supported path. Looping in SRE/Telemetry to confirm recommended values for private minimum pack. Also linking runbook: private observability day-2 operations (Grafana provisioning section).

42 (Rafael Mendes - SRE)
Recommendation for supported installs: either (A) keep dashboards in monitoring namespace to avoid cross-namespace watch, or (B) explicitly enable cross-namespace watch and add RBAC for grafana sidecar. For Private minimum pack we should default to (A) in air-gapped bundles to reduce security friction.
Also noted we’ve had a similar CI failure in INT-11804 where dashboards weren’t discovered due to label mismatch.

10 (Connor O'Brien - Release/packaging)
Checked offline bundle manifest for 1.12.3: dashboards are included, but labels differ from kube-prometheus-stack defaults. This is tracked for backport in the private installer/helm wiring. Suggest we point customer to upgrading to 1.12.4 once released (will include corrected labels and/or chart values).

30 (Dev Patel - Support)
Customer confirmed stable after 48h. No further action needed on their side until next upgrade. We provided guidance for keeping the copy step as part of their upgrade runbook if they remain on 1.12.3.
Closing ticket as resolved with workaround + upcoming product fix.
Internal follow-up: ensure minimum pack dashboards ship with `grafana_dashboard=1` (or chart default label is updated) and install places dashboards in monitoring namespace for air-gapped installs.

title: Acme Bank: request for KMS key rotation cutover procedure + evidence artifacts (audit/SOC2) for Private deployment
body:
Customer request (Acme Bank) received via Sales/SE channel and routed to Support.

Context:
- Acme Bank is in a security review for Redwood Private deployment and needs written operational procedure + evidence artifacts for KMS-backed envelope encryption key rotation.
- Customer specifically asked how cutover works (dual-read/dual-decrypt window), how rollback is handled, and what evidence Redwood can provide for audits.

Customer questions (verbatim-ish):
1) "Please provide the rotation procedure you recommend (quarterly/annual), including operator steps and expected system behavior."
2) "How do you avoid downtime during cutover? Is there a dual-read / dual-decrypt period?"
3) "What audit evidence do you provide? We need a checklist and examples (change ticket IDs, who approved, timestamps, log extracts)."
4) "If KMS is degraded (timeouts/throttling), what is the failure mode? Is tra...

comments.body: 12 PT — Kira Thompson (Reporter): Created ticket from #support escalation. Customer wants: (a) written rotation/cutover steps, (b) evidence template, (c) audit events + SIEM export confirmation. Assigning to Daniel Carter for evidence artifacts; looping in Naomi Feldman for customer-facing expectations.

25 PT — Daniel Carter (Assignee): Acknowledged. I can provide the draft evidence template today and map each evidence line item to where it comes from (audit log export vs change ticket vs approvals). Will confirm what is available in current Private release vs next patch.

41 PT — Naomi Feldman: For enterprise reviews we should reference the standard + ADR and keep promises tight: what exists now vs what ships in upcoming maintenance release. Please include outage behavior: circuit breaker + retry budget + explicit error taxonomy (AccessDenied, Throttling, Timeout, InvalidCiphertext).

10 PT — Sean Gallagher (SRE, via internal note): For KMS degradation messaging: we do NOT recommend unlimited retries; we cap retries and can trip CB to avoid storms. Customer docs should say "requests requiring decrypt may fail" rather than implying continued operation without KMS. Point them to decrypt failures runbook for symptoms + mitigations.

05 PT — Daniel Carter → Customer (email summarized): Shared draft evidence template + explained what to capture: (1) rotation change request/ticket ID, (2) approval names/timestamps, (3) preflight validation report from installer, (4) audit log export entries for cutover/rotation events, (5) post-rotation verification results (sample decrypt checks + dashboards). Noted that final doc page is publishing shortly (docs PR in review).

32 PT — Kira Thompson: Provided customer-friendly link list (draft): Key Management Standard (KMS integrations), ADR-008 summary (dual-read/cutover/rollback), and KMS decrypt failures runbook. Also clarified that rotation is supported without algorithm changes; it is operational key versioning with staged cutover.

02 PT — Customer (Acme Bank) response (via SE, summarized): Thanks. Two follow-ups: (1) confirm dual-decrypt window duration and how it’s configured; (2) confirm audit events are exportable to SIEM and include “who changed config”.

18 PT — Daniel Carter: Responded: Dual-read/dual-decrypt window is configurable (recommended 7–14 days for enterprise change windows); exact duration is customer-controlled via rotation plan and cutover flag sequencing. SIEM export: audit log exporter includes rotation/cutover events; actor identity is recorded as the control-plane principal (service account/user) who executed change, plus change ticket reference when provided (where applicable).

44 PT — Kira Thompson: Confirmed docs publication status: rotation procedure + evidence checklist is now live in docs (PR-1294 merged). Sent final link to customer along with note to use the Drive template for their internal change record; runbook remains in Confluence for operator use.
15 PT — Customer (Acme Bank) response (summarized): Confirmed receipt of procedure + template. No further questions at this time; will reach out if auditor requests additional artifacts.

20 PT — Daniel Carter: Marking Resolved. Follow-up action (non-blocking): ensure installer preflight validation report format matches evidence template fields; track via existing hardening initiative PR-392.

title: Persistent 502/503 plateau from proxy TLS-handshake queueing and slow-starting upstream runtimes
body:
Issue summary:
ScribeAI reported a persistent elevated 502/503 error rate starting 2026-03-12 02:10 UTC across multiple endpoints. Errors present as short-lived waves that plateau rather than sharp spikes — API gateway returning 502/503 while upstream runtimes report long TLS handshake queues and delayed worker start.

Impact:
- Customer-facing requests to ScribeAI (dedicated) experienced 10-20% error rate for ~3 hours during peak traffic windows.
- Streaming sessions intermittently failed to establish with 502s.
- Latency for successful requests increased by ~2x during plateau periods.

Environment:
- prod/us-east (dedicated pool for ScribeAI)
- Models: gptx-13b-v2 pinned variant
- Redwood stack: api-gateway -> ephemeral-proxy group -> serving-runtime (dedicated GPU pool)

Steps to reproduce (observed):
1. Generate steady small-payload traffic simulating ScribeAI client (60 rps).
2. ...

comments.body: 27 UTC - Support (Ava Chen): Opened ticket after ScribeAI reported elevated 502/503; collected initial logs and traffic windows.
45 UTC - SRE (Miguel Ortega): Observed handshake_queue_depth spike on ephemeral-proxy. Applying temporary increase to proxy thread pool; requesting autoscaler logs for runtime spin-up delays.
12 UTC - Customer (ScribeAI, CS rep): Confirmed error rate decreased slightly after mitigation but plateau persists during bursts. Asked for ETA on permanent fix.
40 UTC - Infra (Connor Blake): Kernel ACCEPT backlog tuning applied on a subset of nodes; monitoring for regressions.

50 UTC - Eng (Samir Gupta): Identified correlation between recent autoscaler change (PR #981) and increased worker_init_latency on cold starts. Kicking off a rollback of the aggressive cold-start optimization.
10 UTC - Support (Ava Chen): Notified customer of planned rollback and next steps; scheduled post-incident follow-up meeting.
00 UTC - Engineering (Samir Gupta): Rollback completed on a canary subset. Metrics show handshake_queue reduced and 502/503 plateau abating; rolling to remaining fleet.
22 UTC - SRE (Miguel Ortega): Confirmed sustained recovery; recommending a postmortem to cover root causes and long-term mitigations.

title: Audit log export to SIEM missing Dedicated rollout promote/abort event types (staged rollouts beta)
body:
Issue summary
- The audit log export pipeline (used for compliance export/SIEM forwarding) is not emitting the new Dedicated staged rollout action event types (promote/abort/hold/force-promote) introduced for the “Dedicated staged rollouts for reserved pools” project.

Context
- Dedicated staged rollouts beta is being enabled behind feature flags for a small set of accounts/pools (see INT-1933).
- The control plane emits audit events for actions (create/update/promote/abort, plus admin overrides). Console and internal tooling show these events in-line, but our export feed does not include them.
- This was raised during the enterprise security review expectations: customers (esp. regulated) require end-to-end auditability (Console + export/SIEM) for change-management controls.

Current behavior
- Events visible in Console audit trail / internal admin views.
- SIEM/exported audit stream...

- The control plane emits audit events for actions (create/update/promote/abort, plus admin overrides). Console and internal tooling show these events in-line, but our export feed does not include them.
- This was raised during the enterprise security review expectations: customers (esp. regulated) require end-to-end auditability (Console + export/SIEM) for change-management controls.

Current behavior
- Events visible in Console audit trail / internal admin views.
- SIEM/exported audit stream...

comments.body:
12 PT — Kaitlyn Nguyen (reporter)
Flagging a compliance gap for the staged rollouts beta. Promote/abort actions show in Console audit trail but do not show up in the SIEM export stream. We need this fixed before we broaden beta enablement.

Refs:
- Confluence audit event spec: audit-events-for-dedicated-rollout-actions
- Related implementation PRs: PR-28458 (promote/abort events), PR-28592 (force-promote/hold)

01 PT — Kevin Osei (assignee)
Confirmed. I pulled 3 sample promote request_ids from admin audit API and none exist in the exported stream. Create/update rollout plan events *do* export.

Initial suspicion: exporter’s event_type allowlist missing:
- dedicated_pool_rollout.stage_promote
- dedicated_pool_rollout.abort
- dedicated_pool_rollout.hold
- dedicated_pool_rollout.force_promote

Looping in Logan for where the drop occurs.

18 PT — Logan Wright
Checked the producer side: events are being emitted to the internal audit bus with expected event_type + payload. So this looks downstream (exporter). I also see “unknown_event_type” counters incrementing on the exporter service after a promote.

Action: update allowlist + add explicit mapping for rollout action payload fields.

42 PT — Kevin Osei
Thanks. Please make sure we include override flags + reason field in the export payload. For regulated customers, the “why” for force-promote/hold is important.

Also request: add a guardrail so new audit event types fail loudly (page/ticket) rather than silently dropped.

30 PT — Logan Wright
Patch is in review (internal audit-exporter repo). Changes:
- Added rollout action event types to allowlist.
- Updated schema to permit stage_from/stage_to and override metadata.
- Added unit test that validates exporter rejects “unknown event_type” by incrementing a metric + logs at ERROR.

Plan: deploy to staging tomorrow, prod Thursday during low traffic.

07 PT — Kevin Osei
Validated in staging: promoted stage 0->1 and abort; both present in staging export stream within ~2 minutes. Payload includes actor, account_id, pool_id, rollout_plan_id, stage_from/to, reason.

Request before prod: can we add an alert for unknown_event_type_rate > 0 for 5m? (ticket is fine; paging optional).

55 PT — Logan Wright
Deployed to prod. Added alerting (ticket-level) to #eng-sre via existing audit-export alerts policy. Unknown event type counter back to 0 after rollout.

20 PT — Kaitlyn Nguyen
Confirmed with Kevin: promote/abort/hold/force-promote now present in prod export. Marking resolved. Please ensure this is referenced in beta enablement checklist (INT-1933) so we don’t expand without export validation.

title: Dashboards missing failover annotations during controlled failover test (add automation-driven markers)
body:
Requester: SRE/On-call

Context:
During the 2026-01-15 controlled regional failover (us-east → eu-west), the failover signal dashboards did not show timeline annotations for key automation actions (policy flip recommended/executed, rollback, hold-down timer activation, dedicated drain start/stop). This made it hard to align metric changes (5xx burn, tail latency, reachability probes, capacity headroom) with what the controller actually did.

Problem:
- Grafana/Datadog panels lacked vertical markers for:
 - failover_controller: decision computed (dry-run) vs executed
 - routing policy flips (primary/secondary region change)
 - rollback events and reasons (anti-flap / hold-down)
 - dedicated drain workflow milestones
- In the live #incidents thread, we relied on manual timestamp callouts, which were inconsistent and not searchable.

Impact:
- Slower triage during the game day (multiple ...

comments.body: 04 PT — Aisha Bello (Reporter): Noticed during the live run that the dashboards didn’t get any annotations when the controller executed the policy flip. We had to cross-check controller logs + Slack timestamps. Can we get automation-driven markers before next rehearsal?

22 PT — Sergio Costa (Assignee): Acknowledged. We currently have alert annotations but not event annotations. Need to decide source of truth: failover audit log events vs incident-bot posts vs routing config change stream.

10 PT — Devon Price (Incident-bot/tooling): incident-bot PR-412 posts summaries to Slack, but those don’t backfill dashboards. We *do* emit structured audit events from controller (PR-19824). If we can route those into the observability pipeline, we can annotate reliably.

03 PT — Allison Grant (Observability lead): +1 to using audit events as source of truth. Requirements: normalized event types, stable reason strings, and a run_id so we can filter to the game day blast radius. Also need a clear spec for which dashboards get annotations and what text format looks like.

18 PT — Sergio Costa (Assignee): Plan proposal:
1) Create a small "failover_events" timeseries/event feed in observability-pack sourced from controller audit events.
2) Add a dashboard annotation query (filter by env, region, event_type).
3) Add automatic tags for event_type + region_to.
Question: do we need separate annotations for Dedicated drain milestones or bundle into same stream?
41 PT — Irene Choi (Infra): Please include Dedicated drain start/complete. During the run we couldn’t correlate eu-west capacity headroom dip with the drain steps.
27 PT — Aisha Bello (Reporter): Agree; single stream ok, but display text should be concise: "EXECUTE failover us-east→eu-west (5xx_burn + reachability)" and "ROLLBACK to us-east (hold_down_timer)".

02 PT — Sergio Costa (Assignee): Drafted annotation text format + query. Blocker: some audit events currently emit a free-form "reason"; we need normalization to avoid 50+ variants. Will coordinate with routing/controller (Jordan Lee / Benji Okafor).

30 PT — Benji Okafor (Safe rollout/guardrails): We can normalize reasons to a small enum aligned to ADR-042/043 (reachability_failed, error_burn, latency_burn, capacity_exhaustion, manual_operator, anti_flap, hold_down). I’ll file an eng change if needed; for now we can map common strings in the dashboard query as a stopgap.
20 PT — Sergio Costa (Assignee): Implementing in observability-pack bundle next. Targeting to have annotations in staging by 2026-01-22 rehearsal. Will update with links when PR is ready.

title: Private upgrade blocked: preflight check fails due to insufficient storage headroom (cluster PV/PVC)
body:
Issue summary:
Customer attempted to upgrade Redwood Private in their AWS VPC environment using the new installer preflight checks. Upgrade is blocked with a hard failure: insufficient storage headroom on one of the required volumes (PVC backing Postgres + platform state). Customer expected the upgrade to proceed; they were not aware of the headroom requirement.

Impact:
- Upgrade window paused; customer cannot proceed with planned maintenance.
- No production outage, but customer is blocked from security/feature release.

Customer request:
- Clarify why the check is blocking vs warning.
- Provide concrete remediation steps and thresholds (how much free space is required) and how to verify.

Environment:
- Deployment mode: Private (VPC)
- Region: us-east-1
- K8s: EKS
- Storage: EBS CSI
- Installer: redwood-private-installer v0.31.0 (as provided in 1.19.0 upgrade bundle)
- Attempted up...

comments.body: 12 (Dev Patel, Support): Customer (Cascade Financial Group) reports upgrade blocked by "StorageHeadroomCheck". They are in a 4-hour change window and need guidance ASAP. Added logs/output in description. Requesting Infra/SRE guidance on remediation + confirm threshold behavior.

26 (Irene Choi, Infra): The check is intended to be blocking. During upgrade we can (a) take a snapshot/backup depending on config and (b) run DB migrations that may create temporary files + WAL growth. We’ve seen failures mid-migration when disk is <10% free. Current threshold in the precheck spec is max(15% of PVC capacity, 75Gi). The customer has ~6% free (32Gi on 500Gi) so this is a correct block.

Recommended remediation for EKS + EBS CSI:
1) Expand the PVC by increasing requested storage (e.g., 750Gi or 1Ti) and ensure the StorageClass supports allowVolumeExpansion.
2) Verify filesystem resize completes on the postgres pod.
3) Re-run...

41 (Rafael Mendes, SRE): +1 blocking. We should not advise overriding; rollback reliability depends on having enough headroom too. Customer-facing explanation: this prevents partial upgrades / DB corruption risk.

Verification commands:
- kubectl -n redwood-system get pvc redwood-postgres-data
- kubectl -n redwood-system exec deploy/redwood-postgres -- df -h /var/lib/postgresql/data

If they do PVC expansion, confirm EBS volume modification completes (AWS console/CLI) and the node filesystem reflects the new size.

05 (Dev Patel, Support): Sent customer remediation steps: increase PVC size (recommended) and provided verification commands + rationale why check is blocking. Asked for their StorageClass name and whether allowVolumeExpansion is enabled.

22 (Customer via Support): StorageClass is gp3, allowVolumeExpansion is true. They can expand during window. Asking what target size to set to be safe, and whether the installer backup step increases usage substantially.

37 (Irene Choi, Infra): For 500Gi PVC, required is 75Gi free (15% is 75Gi). They currently have 32Gi free. If they expand to 750Gi, 15% is 112.5Gi; required becomes 112.5Gi. If their used remains ~468Gi, free would become ~282Gi which passes comfortably. 700Gi would also be fine. Installer backup: depends on their configuration; snapshots are not stored on the PVC, but migrations can create temporary tables/WAL churn. Recommend 750Gi to avoid flirting with the threshold.

10 (Dev Patel, Support): Relayed recommendation: expand PVC to ~750Gi (or 700Gi minimum) to satisfy precheck and reduce risk. Clarified snapshots are not stored on the PVC; headroom is for DB operations + safety.
54 (Dev Patel, Support): Customer expanded PVC to 750Gi, filesystem resized successfully. Prechecks now PASS. They are proceeding with upgrade plan execution.
33 (Dev Patel, Support): Customer completed upgrade to 1.19.0 successfully. No rollback required. They asked for a documentation link to ensure storage headroom is validated ahead of future windows.
45 (Rafael Mendes, SRE): Please point them to the Private Upgrade Runbook section "Prechecks -> Storage headroom". Also recommend adding a recurring check/alert for PVC usage >80% on postgres PVCs.
18 (Dev Patel, Support): Provided runbook link and suggested monitoring: alert when postgres PVC >80% used. Customer confirmed receipt and closed the loop.

title: Latency regression suspected after runtime upgrade (p99 TTFT + p99 end-to-end) on dedicated deployment
body:
Issue summary
- Customer reports a noticeable tail-latency regression after a runtime upgrade on their Dedicated deployment.
- Correlates with internal Q1 baseline sweep signals: a similar p99 TTFT regression signature appeared in perf-canary for long-prefill + mixed chat workloads, but was initially classified as canary-only/no-customer-impact until this report.

Customer impact
- HelioBank reports increased time-to-first-token (TTFT) and overall response time on streaming chat endpoints.
- Impact: degraded UX (customer-facing assistant), increased client-side timeouts, and higher retry volume.
- Customer states this started “within the last ~24 hours” following their scheduled runtime upgrade window.

Timeline (customer-provided)
- 2026-01-18 21:00–23:00 UTC: Dedicated runtime upgrade applied (customer acknowledged maintenance window).
- 2026-01-19 07:30 UTC: First internal alert fr...

normalized.description:
Issue summary
- Customer reports a noticeable tail-latency regression after a runtime upgrade on their Dedicated deployment.
- Correlates with internal Q1 baseline sweep signals: a similar p99 TTFT regression signature appeared in perf-canary for long-prefill + mixed chat workloads, but was initially classified as canary-only/no-customer-impact until this report.

Customer impact
- HelioBank reports increased time-to-first-token (TTFT) and overall response time on streaming chat endpoints.
- Impact: degraded UX (customer-facing assistant), increased client-side timeouts, and higher retry volume.
- Customer states this started “within the last ~24 hours” following their scheduled runtime upgrade window.

Timeline (customer-provided)
- 2026-01-18 21:00–23:00 UTC: Dedicated runtime upgrade applied (customer acknowledged maintenance window).
- 2026-01-19 07:30 UTC: First internal alert fr...

comments.body: 18 UTC (Kira Thompson - Support): Created ticket from HelioBank email + attached screenshots. Customer reports regression started right after runtime upgrade window. Asking SRE/runtime to correlate with baseline canary signals.

36 UTC (Sean Gallagher - SRE): Acknowledged. Seeing matching p99 TTFT bump in us-east on dedicated dashboards; queue_wait_ms is elevated. Pulling per-route breakdown and confirming runtime tag rollout time on their cluster.

05 UTC (Aisha Bello - SRE/Obs): Confirmed SLO view shows p99 degradation for streaming cohort only; non-streaming completion endpoints stable. Suggest focusing on TTFT measurement path + scheduler/prefill overlap changes.

22 UTC (Sofia Ivanova - Runtime Scheduling): The symptom matches the long-prefill backpressure tuning that landed recently. If their traffic mix includes more long-prefill than average, the new defaults could be too aggressive. Recommending temporary override: lower prefill concurrency cap and adjust backpressure thresholds to protect chat TTFT.

41 UTC (Hiro Tanaka - Kernels): Also worth checking kernel fallback. Recent changes add optional dumps for perf runs; for customer cluster we can enable sampled kernel selection debug for 30 minutes to confirm whether we’re falling back on prefill.

10 UTC (Kira Thompson - Support -> Customer): We’re investigating with SRE/runtime. Can you share 10 request IDs (5 before / 5 after) and confirm region(s) impacted? Also confirm whether any client-side streaming behavior changed (buffering/reading cadence).
02 UTC (Customer - HelioBank): Provided 14 request IDs, all us-east. No client changes; same streaming reader. Noted increased timeouts at peak (their 20s client timeout).

24 UTC (Sean Gallagher - SRE): Correlated request IDs to elevated queue_wait_ms spikes. Decode tokens/sec stable. This is consistent with scheduler/backpressure or prefill kernel path rather than general GPU starvation.

15 UTC (Connor O'Brien - Release/Gating): Noted this aligns with perf-canary thread from 2026-01-18 where long-prefill p99 regressed. Suggest rollback for customer now; we can patch-forward after root cause. Logging this for gating exception review.

04 UTC (Sean Gallagher - SRE): Executing mitigation: pin HelioBank us-east dedicated cluster back to redwood-runtime 2026.01.11.3. ETA 30–45 minutes including drain + warmup. Will monitor TTFT p95/p99 and error rate.
02 UTC (Sean Gallagher - SRE): Rollback completed. Early signals: p99 TTFT back within ~5–10% of pre-upgrade baseline. Queue_wait_ms down materially. Monitoring for another peak window.
20 UTC (Kira Thompson - Support -> Customer): We rolled back the runtime in us-east and are seeing TTFT improving. Please confirm whether you see improvement on your side over the next hour.
07 UTC (Customer - HelioBank): Confirmed improvement. Their synthetic checks back to normal. Requesting RCA and guidance on when to safely re-upgrade.

45 UTC (Sofia Ivanova - Runtime Scheduling): Root cause is likely interaction between new backpressure defaults and bursty mixed workload; fix is to adjust defaults and add guardrail for mixed chat + long-prefill. Points to PR: pr-21541 and follow-up config changes. Also recommending we report cold vs warm cache separately in baseline dashboards for this cohort.

30 UTC (Allison Grant - Observability): Added note: ensure TTFT definition is consistent (first-token-on-wire, server-side). Customer measurement includes client network; internal metric is server-side. However, regression magnitude seen server-side is sufficient to explain customer pain. No further action required on definitions for this ticket.

40 UTC (Sean Gallagher - SRE): Patch-forward plan: apply updated scheduler config on newer runtime to HelioBank in staging first; monitor representative suite metrics; then schedule prod upgrade with customer.
12 UTC (Kira Thompson - Support): Customer satisfied with rollback + plan. Leaving ticket in Resolved pending scheduled re-upgrade coordination under separate change request.

title: Per-tenant prefetch & quantization policy mismatch causing intermittent tail latency; request for staged degrade + safe rollback plan
body:
Issue summary:
Customer MedLedger reported a sudden increase in 95th/99th percentile latency for long conversation sessions after applying a tenant-scoped optimizer profile that enables aggressive KV-prefetch and an experimental 8-bit quantization profile. The change apparently only affected a subset of requests (longer prompt prefixes / streaming chat sessions), resulting in tail latency spikes and occasional streaming disconnects.

Impact:
- Intermittent 95p/99p latency spikes for MedLedger dedicated pool, observed since 2026-03-10 21:00 UTC.
- User-facing: chat sessions with long context occasionally time out or experience partial streaming responses. No data loss reported but poor UX.
- Affects ~12% of their active sessions across two app endpoints.

Environment:
- Dedicated capacity (tenant-level GPU pool) in prod-us-east (us-east-1).
- Optimizer profile applied via console: tena...

title: Add RBAC permissions for rollout auto-rollback suppression/override (admin-only)
body:
Requester: Felix Schneider (Console views / auditability)

Context
As part of the Auto-rollback on SLO breach integration, we are adding:
- Admin-only suppression windows ("do not auto-rollback for X")
- Admin-only manual override actions (pause/rollback) when auto-actions are enabled

We need RBAC updates so these controls are not available to general Console admins or service owners by default, and all usage is auditable.

Current behavior
- In staging, suppression/override endpoints are behind a feature flag but currently rely on a coarse "console_admin" check.
- This is too broad for production; Security requested explicit permissions and audit trail requirements before enabling for production services.

Request
1) Create/define new RBAC permissions (names below are suggested; fine to adjust to match existing RBAC conventions):
 - rollouts:auto_rollback:suppress (create/update/del...
