Request
1) Create/define new RBAC permissions (names below are suggested; fine to adjust to match existing RBAC conventions):
 - rollouts:auto_rollback:suppress (create/update/del...

comments.author: Felix Schneider
comments.body: Filing per Security feedback from last review. We need explicit RBAC permissions before we flip the prod flag for admin override/suppression. Suggested permission names included in ticket; open to better naming if RBAC has a standard pattern.

comments.author: Ethan Park
comments.body: Acknowledged. I’ll coordinate with Benji/Jordan on policy-engine enforcement points and with Hannah on Console UI gating. We already have an authz hook in the control plane; should be straightforward to add two permissions + a read permission for audit details.

comments.author: Kevin Osei
comments.body: Security requirement: justification must be required and stored with the audit event. Also need a clear distinction between 'override' vs 'suppress' because suppression can hide regressions. Prefer: override allowed for SRE Oncall; suppress only allowed for Rollouts Admin group. Please ensure denied attempts are logged (at least to security audit stream) to detect misuse.

comments.author: Sean Gallagher
comments.body: From SRE perspective: override is needed for oncall mitigation (esp. when auto-rollback is suppressed due to known incident or monitor staleness). Agree with Kevin: suppression should be tighter than override, and must have a short TTL by default (e.g., max 2h) unless Rollouts Admin extends with explicit justification.

comments.author: Ethan Park
comments.body: Draft implementation plan: (1) add new permissions in RBAC service; (2) wire enforcement in Console API endpoints for suppression/override; (3) ensure control plane also checks permission server-side (defense in depth); (4) emit audit events per ADR-0128. Targeting end of week for merge + staging verification.

comments.author: Felix Schneider
comments.body: Quick UX note: UI will show disabled action with tooltip 'Requires Rollouts Admin permission' and link to internal access request. Also adding field validation for justification length + required.

comments.author: Ethan Park
comments.body: Implemented permission checks in control plane + console API. Added new RBAC group mapping: Rollouts Admin -> suppress + override + read_audit; SRE Oncall -> override + read_audit. Waiting on Security sign-off after staging verification. Related PRs: pr-28511, pr-28435.

comments.author: Kevin Osei
comments.body: Reviewed staging events: audit payload includes actor, permission, target, justification, request_id. Looks good. Please ensure max suppression TTL enforced at policy layer (not just UI). Approving from Security side contingent on TTL guardrail being present.

comments.author: Ethan Park
comments.body: Added server-side guardrail: suppression TTL max 2h unless actor has suppress_extended permission (granted only to Rollouts Admin; not to SRE Oncall). Staging re-verified. Marking resolved; will follow release train enablement checklist with Connor.

title: Nightingale Health — sustained 5xx burst and streaming truncation during EU maintenance window; customer escalation and action plan
body:
Issue summary:
During the scheduled EU control-plane maintenance window (2026-03-10 11:45–12:30 UTC), Nightingale Health observed a sustained burst of 5xx errors and multiple streaming response truncations. Customer reported increased tail latency and truncated streams beginning ~11:50 UTC which persisted intermittently until mitigation at ~12:22 UTC. Impact: degraded user-facing inference (chat/streaming) and increased token billing for retries.

Impact:
- Customer-facing streaming truncation (partial transcripts) for ~32 minutes with ~18% request error rate for affected routes.
- Elevated 5xx (502/504) and gateway timeouts; some requests retried by client, amplifying load.
- Billing exposure due to client retries and duplicate partial completions.

Environment:
- Prod / eu-west-1 serving pool (dedicated nodes for Nightingale enterprise account).
- Models in use: open-llama-13b-v2 fo...

- Models in use: open-llama-13b-v2 fo...

comments.author: (Aisha)
comments.body: Logged initial report and created incident ticket. Opened #support-nightingale and invited SRE and Eng. Notified customer of investigation and ETA for first update.

comments.author: SRE (Liam O'Connor)
comments.body: Observed 502/504 spike tied to routing fallback during coordinator restart. Hypothesis: routing decision component marked dedicated pool degraded prematurely due to heartbeat lag; created INT-43120 to track root-cause in coordinator. Applied temporary damping on fallback decisions at 12:15 UTC.

comments.author: (Diego)
comments.body: Confirmed routing policy logic includes a conservative \"fail fast\" threshold that can be tripped by delayed heartbeats during rolling restarts. Preparing PR to add hysteresis and cross-check with KV cache staleness. PR posted: https://github.com/redwood-inference/serving-runtime/pull/4821

comments.author: Nightingale Ops, Maya R.
comments.body: Appreciated rapid updates. Reported fewer truncated streams after 12:20 UTC; asked for formal post-incident notes and billing credit request steps.

comments.author: (Aisha)
comments.body: Provided next steps and preliminary action items; escalated billing request to finance (ticket FIN-2026-045). Scheduled follow-up call for 2026-03-13.

comments.author: SRE (Liam)
comments.body: Added monitoring alert to detect rapid fallback-amplification patterns and created a dashboard panel: /dashboards/fallback-amplification-eu-west. Linked in runbook.

title: Dedicated: Admin user cannot edit routing policy after RBAC v2 enablement (403 permission denied)
body:
Issue summary:
- Customer on Dedicated reports that users with their “Dedicated Admin” role can no longer edit routing policies in Console after RBAC v2 was enabled.
- Save action fails with a 403 Permission Denied.

Impact:
- Customer cannot update routing rules (fallback/model pinning behavior) for production traffic.
- Blocks planned rollout of routing changes tied to a model update.

Customer context:
- Company: Aegis Insurance Group
- Deployment: Dedicated
- Auth: SSO group mapping -> built-in role template “Dedicated Admin”

Environment:
- prod / us-east
- Console + Control Plane API

Steps to reproduce (customer):
1) Console -> Routing -> Policies
2) Open existing policy
3) Change target model or traffic split
4) Click Save
5) Error banner: “Permission denied”

Observed behavior:
- Console request returns 403
- Customer reports this workflow worked prior to RBAC v2 migration/en...

2) Open existing policy
3) Change target model or traffic split
4) Click Save
5) Error banner: “Permission denied”

Observed behavior:
- Console request returns 403
- Customer reports this workflow worked prior to RBAC v2 migration/en...

comments.body:
41 UTC — Colin O'Donnell (Reporter)
Customer (Aegis) escalated during Dedicated pilot: admin cannot edit routing policy. They are blocked from changing fallback behavior ahead of a model version pin change. They confirmed they’re in the “Dedicated Admin” group in their IdP. Attaching request id: rwreq_9f3c0b1a1b2a.

05 UTC — Benji Okafor (Assignee)
Pulled logs for rwreq_9f3c0b1a1b2a from Control Plane. Response 403 with structured denial. Denial reason code: RBAC_DENY_MISSING_PERMISSION. Required permission (from endpoint annotation): controlplane.routing_policies.update. Subject had roles: [template/dedicated_admin] scoped to org: aegis-prod. No matching permission found in evaluated policy bundle.

22 UTC — Marcus Lin
This looks like a template gap, not a runtime bug: endpoint was recently annotated and now enforced deny-by-default. Action item should be to add controlplane.routing_policies.update to the dedicated_admin role template (and any operator template that previously had access). Also confirm read vs write split (list/get vs create/update/delete).

40 UTC — Naomi Feldman
+1 template gap. For least-privilege, Dedicated Admin should include routing policy write. We should not broaden other roles by default. Please also add an integration test that validates dedicated_admin can update routing policies (to avoid regressions when we add new sensitive permissions).

10 UTC — Benji Okafor
Workaround for customer: created a temporary custom role “RoutingPolicyEditor” with permission controlplane.routing_policies.update and bound it to their admin group at org scope. Customer confirmed they can now save policy edits in Console. Next: patch built-in template and roll out updated policy bundle.

Notes:
- This is consistent with deny-by-default behavior working as intended.
- The permission exists in the registry; it’s missing from the role template YAML used to generate built-ins.

15 UTC — Connor O'Brien
Release coordination: once policy bundle is updated, we can roll out to Dedicated tenants via standard policy bundle sync. Please confirm expected bundle version and whether any cache invalidation is needed (role template changes should take effect after policy bundle refresh; no code deploy required).

05 UTC — Benji Okafor
Template patch prepared and merged in policy bundle repo (internal). Deployed policy bundle refresh to Aegis Dedicated org at 2025-03-12 13:52 UTC. Removed temporary custom role binding after verifying built-in role now includes permission. Customer retested successfully.

30 UTC — Colin O'Donnell
Customer confirmation: routing policy edits are working again; no additional 403s observed. They proceeded with planned routing change. Closing ticket.

title: Reserved pool throughput drop from rapid tenant scatter causing slot thrash and GPU allocation shortfalls
body: Issue summary: During the customer's recurring midday window we observed a sustained p95/p99 throughput degradation for NovaDocs' reserved pool that coincided with a large number of short-lived tenant connections spreading across many slots (tenant scatter). Impact: customer requests experience 3x p99 increase and burst 429/RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED for batched routes, causing downstream timeouts for user-facing documents ingestion pipelines. Environment: production reserved pool (dedicated) in us-east. Observed starting 2026-03-10 11:40 UTC. Steps to reproduce: 1) Replay customer traffic (small, high-concurrency short-streams) into reserved pool using synthetic tenant generator 2) Observe slot churn and repeated small allocations 3) Watch autoscaler not react quickly due to many ephemeral allocations and scheduler fragmentation Observed behavior: rapid slot attach/detach, high scheduler RPC...

comments.author: Ava Chen ()
comments.body: Initial triage completed; capturing metrics and requested sample request IDs from customer.

comments.author: Diego Morales (SRE)
comments.body: Observed repeating attach-timeout pattern on pool 'reserved-us-east-42' correlated with scheduler heartbeat spikes. Attaching top-of-stack logs to ticket.

comments.author: Customer (NovaDocs)
comments.body: Confirmed we saw increased 429s between 11:40-12:05 UTC; sent anonymized request traces and traffic profile.

comments.author: Diego Morales (SRE)
comments.body: Applied short-term mitigation: enabled customer fallback routing to shared low-latency pool and temporarily raised pool burst limit for 15 minutes; customer reports partial improvement.

comments.author: Priya Rao
comments.body: Noted KV-cache growth not implicated; but scheduler log shows many tiny allocation batches (avg <300 tokens) leading to slot thrash. Adding hypothesis to investigation notes.

comments.author: Diego Morales (SRE)
comments.body: Scaling policy change proposed: increase attach-grace-period and add aggregation window for ephemeral short streams. Awaiting review from autoscaler owners.

title: Request: add/assign 'Quality Gate Override Approver' role for Optimize time-bound overrides (Console RBAC)
body:
Request type: Access / RBAC change

Background:
Optimize Quality Gates are now blocking Apply when a gate fails. We have a time-bound override flow (admin-controlled) for documented exceptions (see exception policy). During limited production rollout, we need a designated oncall/PM to be able to approve overrides in Console without using a shared admin account.

What is needed:
1) Create (or confirm) a dedicated RBAC role/group for this capability:
 - Role name (proposal): quality_gate_override_approver
 - Group name (proposal): optimize-quality-gate-override-approvers
2) Permissions required (least privilege):
 - Approve/deny time-bound override requests for Optimize config changes (batching/caching/quant profile)
 - View gate run details + artifacts links sufficient to make decision
 - View audit log entries related to overrides (read-only)
 - No ability to edit gating thresholds, d...

 - View audit log entries related to overrides (read-only)
 - No ability to edit gating thresholds, d...

comments.body: 18 PT — Aditya Rao (Reporter): Creating INT ticket per leadership request. We need real approvers (not console-admin) for time-bound overrides before we widen limited prod rollout. Requested initial approvers: Paula Mendes + Sean Gallagher.

02 PT — Marcus Lin (Assignee): Acknowledged. Please confirm whether these users already have any elevated Console roles. Also confirm if the override approval endpoint is already enforcing 'override_approver' vs 'admin' (PR-19537 mentions admin-only guardrails).

21 PT — Aditya Rao: Both currently have standard Console access only (viewer/editor depending on team). PR-19537 in our stack currently checks for 'console_admin' OR 'quality_gate_override_approver' flag (feature-flagged). We need the role/group created and wired to SSO (preferred) or assigned manually for beta.

05 PT — Marcus Lin: Proposed approach: create a dedicated Console RBAC role with only the override approval permissions + audit read for override events. No access to policy/threshold admin. Will coordinate with Platform (Jordan Lee / Benji Okafor) to ensure permission boundary is correct.

47 PT — Marcus Lin: Platform confirmed permission set exists behind feature flag: PERM_OPTIMIZE_OVERRIDE_APPROVE + PERM_OPTIMIZE_GATE_READ + PERM_AUDITLOG_READ(override.*). Proceeding with SSO group mapping request to IT (internal). For beta, we can also assign directly in Console until SSO sync is in place.

32 PT — Sean Gallagher: Adding note from SRE: please ensure the role can view gate failure reasons/artifacts, otherwise oncall can't make an informed approval decision. Also please ensure approvals are searchable in audit logs by route/account.

10 PT — Marcus Lin: Completed: created group optimize-quality-gate-override-approvers and mapped to Console role quality_gate_override_approver. Added Paula Mendes + Sean Gallagher. Verified they can approve/deny override requests and cannot access admin policy pages. Audit events present (override.requested / override.approved / override.denied) with actor + expiration.
06 PT — Aditya Rao: Confirmed in Console: both users can approve overrides; cannot change gating policy; approval emits audit log event with expiration + linked gate_run_id. Closing.

title: AuroraMed production surge: multipath fallback + autoscaler deadtime caused session resets and high-tail latency — customer restore plan & post-incident actions
body:
Issue summary: On 2024-11-15 03:12 UTC AuroraMed began seeing elevated tail latency and session resets for multi-turn chat flows. Impact: clients experienced truncated streaming responses, duplicate completions for retrying clients, and an increased 5xx rate (service errors) for approx. 43 minutes. Multiple integrations reported out-of-order tokens and session resumption failures.

Timeline (high level):
- 03:10 UTC: first anomaly flagged by synthetic monitors (latency > 99th pct).
- 03:12 UTC: AuroraMed engineering reported customer-facing errors via support channel and opened an escalation.
- 03:15–03:32 UTC: automatic fallback routing generated multipath requests to variant models; autoscaler entered a deadtime window when scaling signals peaked and could not spin new workers fast enough.
- 03:32 UTC: mitigation step applied (temporary routing throttle and manual scale-up of dedica...

title: Midday throughput erosion in dedicated pool correlating with background optimizer I/O bursts
body:
Issue summary:
Customer Quillify reports an unexplained 20-35% sustained throughput reduction in their dedicated pool during midday peak windows despite autoscaler headroom. P90 latency increased modestly and P99 spike frequency increased; many short-stream requests experienced attach delays.

Impact:
- Dedicated reserved pool dc-805 serving Quillify saw reduced throughput and elevated latency for ~45 minutes across the US-East window.
- Customer-facing traffic experienced degraded tail latency and 5xx spikes tied to delayed GPU attach/slot allocation.

Environment:
- prod-us-east dedicated capacity (dc-805)
- Node type: A100-80GB mixed cluster
- Tenants: multiple short-stream, high-fanout tenants including Quillify (dedicated slice)

Observed behavior vs expected:
- Observed: throughput drop with autoscaler still showing available capacity; allocator logs show attach delays and slot ...

comments.author: Ava Chen ()
comments.body: Notified Quillify that we are investigating. Asked for spike timing and sample request IDs. Customer provided sample req IDs: rq_7812,rq_7819.

comments.author: Diego Morales (SRE)
comments.body: Correlated allocator attach_time with prefetch-opt compaction windows. Initial hypothesis: background optimizer is producing short NVMe bursts that cascade into kernel scheduling pressure and GPU attach latency.

comments.author: Runtime
comments.body: Looked at kernel traces. Found preempt_count spikes and nvme latency peaking at same timestamps. Suggested lowering prefetch-opt IO priority and limiting compaction concurrency as a quick mitigation.

comments.author: Quillify (Customer)
comments.body: Applied fallback routing per Support's recommendation; reported partial relief for priority endpoints but general degradation persisted for bulk traffic. Asked for ETA on root cause and mitigation.

comments.author: Diego Morales (SRE)
comments.body: Deployed temporary autoscaler weight change to decouple NVMe IO metric from immediate shrink decisions. Observed reduction in attach time spikes during next window.

comments.author: Runtime (Priya)
comments.body: Opened PR to add I/O niceness to prefetch-opt and to add a soft backoff to compaction when allocator attach latency rises (see related PR).

title: Private deployment: SSO token aud check fails when customer ingress preserves Host header via identity-proxy, causing 403 to admin APIs
body:
Issue summary: Customer reports sudden 403 authorization failures for admin/operator endpoints in their private VPC deployment after a weekend ingress upgrade. Symptoms align with SSO-authenticated sessions failing authorization checks despite tokens appearing valid at the IdP side. Impact: Admin console and API calls that require elevated RBAC roles are blocked for multiple operators; day-to-day ops impaired. 

Observed behavior: Calls authenticated via Azure AD SSO succeed at token issuance but are rejected by Redwood private control-plane with an explicit audience mismatch message during token validation. Error started after customer applied an ingress change which enabled "preserveHost: true" on their identity-proxy service.

Why this matters: For private deployments we validate token audience (aud) strictly against expected runtime client IDs. Changes in the perceived host/origin...

comments.body: Customer reported that operators started seeing 403s after ingress change. Collected tokens and logs; attached in ticket. Marking P1 since admin ops blocked.

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: Confirmed aud mismatch in logs. Reproduced on staging by toggling preserveHost. Adding workaround to accepted_audiences to restore access. Assigning to SRE/Infra to coordinate hotfix.

comments.author: Priya Singh (Customer)
comments.body: Thank you — adding the audience list unblocked our team. We prefer a code fix so we don't need long-lived tenant config. Can you share ETA?

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: PR created to add audience_aliases feature to auth-proxy and a controlled rollout plan. Targeting hotfix release 2026-03-15 with canary in staging then prod-private rollouts. Adding link to PR in ticket.

title: Catalog sync drift caused wrong model routing, quality regression, and enterprise customer escalation
body:
Issue summary:
On 2026-03-10 at ~09:23 UTC we observed a large increase in customer-reported hallucinations and unrelated completions for Fresco Retail. Root cause traced to catalog-sync drift: the model-catalog service had an out-of-sync cache window and continued advertising an older (incompatible) variant for the 'product-description' route, causing requests to be routed to gptx-13b-v2-quant instead of the intended gptx-13b-v2 (non-quantized). This introduced repeated generation artifacts and a measurable quality regression for Fresco's production traffic.

Impact:
- Fresco Retail (enterprise) experienced elevated hallucination rate and semantic errors across catalog-enrichment requests for ~2 hours.
- API response quality SLO degraded (precision drop) and several customers opened escalations with support and account team.
- Billing exposure: some requests were served by a higher-c...

comments.author: Jamie Holt
comments.body: We are seeing unrelated product descriptions being injected; some SKUs have hallucinated attributes. This started ~09:20 UTC. Please advise.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Acknowledged and escalated to SRE. Opening investigation and capturing traces. We'll keep you updated every 30 mins.

comments.author: Marcus Ortega
comments.body: Checked GPU pools and serving runtime; no exhaustion. Elevated to Eng to look at model-router and catalog-sync. Initiating deeper telemetry pull.

comments.author: Priya Nair
comments.body: Found evidence of stale alias mapping in catalog-sync after a 503 from metadata-store at 09:17 UTC. Recommending forced refresh to restore correct mapping.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Forced catalog refresh and rolling restart completed; routing corrected. Initial quality metrics moving back to baseline. Notifying customer with mitigation and offered credit.

comments.author: Daniel Ruiz
comments.body: Fresco is grateful for the quick fix; they request a technical postmortem and assurance this won't recur during peak season.

comments.author: Priya Nair
comments.body: Patch to TTL-backoff logic landed in branch and under review (PR references in metadata). Canary tests scheduled for 2026-03-14.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Billing team confirmed invoice delta and applied credit; customer notified and accepted the credit. Incident marked resolved pending postmortem.

comments.author: Marcus Ortega
comments.body: Post-incident report prepared and shared on Confluence. Action items assigned to Eng and Observability teams.

- Fresco Retail (enterprise) experienced elevated hallucination rate and semantic errors across catalog-enrichment requests for ~2 hours.
- API response quality SLO degraded (precision drop) and several customers opened escalations with support and account team.
- Billing exposure: some requests were served by a higher-c...

title: Emergency safe drain and reallocate: quantized batching + KV cache tuning causing per-tenant tail latency
body: Issue summary: After applying a tenant-scoped optimizer profile that enabled aggressive int8 quantization + async batching + KV prefetch for gptj-8b-int8, LumeHealth is seeing intermittent 95/99p tail latency spikes (1.2s -> 5–7s) on a subset (~12%) of inference requests. Impact: Customer-facing API timeouts and degraded user experience for multi-turn chat flows. No error 5xx spike, but significant latency augmentation for many sessions. Environment: Production (dedicated pool), us-east region, tenant_id=lh-prod-7. Recent change: Optimizer profile rollout on 2026-03-09 23:40 UTC via console staged rollout (20% -> 100% over 2h).

Environment: Production (dedicated pool), us-east region, tenant_id=lh-prod-7. Recent change: Optimizer profile rollout on 2026-03-09 23:40 UTC via console staged rollout (20% -> 100% over 2h).

comments.body: 05 UTC - Diego Martinez (Support): Acknowledged. Initial hypothesis: async batching bump + KV prefetch is creating uneven scheduling pressure for variable-length prompts. Opening SRE runbook and paging scheduler-oncall.

20 UTC - SRE (Priya Rao): Observed elevated queue depth on a subset of workers pinned to tenant lh-prod-7. No GPU OOMs. Suggest draining affected workers, disabling async batching for the tenant, and falling back to sync batching + conservative quant profile as a safe rollback.

47 UTC - Support (Diego Martinez): Action plan sent to customer: perform controlled drain, apply fallback profile (opt-tenant-lh-prod-7-safe) which sets int8->fp16 mixed mode and async_batch_size=1, disable kv-prefetch. Awaiting customer approval to proceed at 02:00 UTC.
14 UTC - LumeHealth (Sara Patel): Approved. Please proceed and confirm when finished.

title: Final invoice shows hosted charges not reflected in Console estimator after multi-model batch run
body: Issue summary: Customer ran a scheduled multi-model batch job (rerank + embedding pipeline) on 2026-03-08. Console estimator showed an expected hosted+dedicated blended estimate of ~$1,300 for the run; final invoice shows an additional $2,700 hosted charge. BrightLens claims they should have only consumed reserved dedicated capacity for the run, and that the Console estimator did not indicate potential hosted fallback costs. Impact: Unexpected $2,700 hosted charge on final invoice, customer requests explanation and credit if charge is not justified. Environment: Dedicated pool org-id=org-bltl, reserved-capacity plan (monthly commit), job submitted from orchestration service in us-east-1 at 2026-03-08T03:12:00Z. Attachments: customer-provided CSV of usage lines, sample batch job manifest, and Console estimator screenshot.

comments.author: Aisha Rahman ()
comments.body: Opened ticket after receiving customer's invoice CSV and estimator screenshot. Asked customer for job manifest and sample request IDs.

comments.author: Elsa Martinez (Billing)
comments.body: Pulled invoice lines and router logs; confirmed hosted-tagged lines overlap job time window. Noted router logged fallback events. Escalated to SRE for autoscaler timeline.

comments.author: Jon Park
comments.body: Investigated autoscaler. Found a rapid scale-down at 03:36 caused by an aggressive cooldown misconfiguration on pool 'dedic-pool-bltl-1' combined with transient GPU pressure from a neighbor job. This led to ~8 minute window where new requests were routed to hosted variants. Logged as INT-4421.

comments.author: Elsa Martinez
comments.body: Shared preliminary findings with customer and proposed a partial invoice credit for hosted fallback charges while we perform permanent mitigations to autoscaler config. Asked customer if they accept a one-time credit and whether they want us to pull extended logs for reconciliation.

comments.author: BrightLens - Taylor Ng
comments.body: Requested a full refund for the $2,700, stating the job was scheduled with guaranteed capacity and they were not warned. Asked for mitigation so the incident does not recur.

title: Conservative opt-tuning safety net: tenant sees intermittent 5xx and elevated tail latency after enabling kv-prefetch + q4 quant profile
body:
Issue summary: After enabling a tenant-level optimizer profile that enables aggressive KV prefetch (window=32) and a 4-bit quant profile (q4_mixed) the customer reports intermittent 5xx errors and a noticeable P99 tail latency increase for interactive chat routes.

Impact: Intermittent 5xx responses (approx 0.6% of requests during peak) and P95->P99 jump from ~450ms to ~1.2s for the tenant's primary chat route. Customer reports visible user complaints and warned they may revert to a competitor if unresolved.

Environment: Dedicated pool in prod-us-east serving BrightPost Health tenant. Traffic is primarily short-turn chat (avg seq len 80 tokens). The change was applied during a low-traffic maintenance window two days ago and rolled to 100% tenant traffic 90 minutes after initial canary.

Observed behavior: 1) Spikes of 502/503 with upstream serving-runtime logs showing 'KV_CACHE_MISS'...

Observed behavior: 1) Spikes of 502/503 with upstream serving-runtime logs showing 'KV_CACHE_MISS'...

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Raised by customer at 09:12 UTC. Created ticket and escalated to SRE. First-pass suggested rollback options and asked for logs. (Assigned to Diego)

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: Performed quick gating: rolled traffic back to snapshot-2026-0309-09 for tenant and temporarily set kv_prefetch.window=8. Observed immediate drop in 5xx. Requesting confirmation from customer on user-visible impact.

comments.author: BrightPost SE - Maya Patel
comments.body: Confirmed reduced errors after kv_prefetch window change and quant pin to q8. Reports slightly higher per-token cost but acceptable for now. Asks for recommended long-term safe rollout pattern so they can re-enable q4 in the future.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Proposed conservative staged rollout: (1) q8 -> shadow q4 traffic with 5% sampled traffic; (2) incremental kv_prefetch window increases 8->16->24 with 1-hour observation windows and automatic rollback on P99 or 5xx thresholds; (3) enable async_batching only after prefetched KV working set is stable. Suggested adding a circuit breaker that auto-demotes quant when per-node GPU mem_used > 90%.

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: Implemented a tenant gating rule and created a safe rollback runbook (link in related_confluence_pages). Scheduling a staged replay in staging on 2026-03-13 to verify thresholds and collect exact inflection points for kv_prefetch window size.

title: Dedicated pool: sudden GPU starvation after autoscaler telemetry drop; noisy sidecar heartbeat implicated
body:
Issue summary:
Customer (LuminaHealth) reports a sudden drop in throughput and elevated request queueing beginning ~2026-03-11 09:30 PDT in their dedicated GPU pool (us-west). P50/P95 latency rose and many requests hit 429/503 fallback. Impact: production features serving clinical note summarization degraded across multiple endpoints.

Impact:
- Customer reports ~60% reduction in sustained throughput for pinned routes.
- P99 latency saw >5x increase for batched routes.
- Some requests failed with 503 and others were routed to lower-tier model variants.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: pool-id dcp-west-7 (reserved GPUs).
- Region: us-west-2.
- Models serving: redwood-7b-instruct (primary) with redwood-13b-quant fallback.
- Config: autoscaler with predictive window enabled, heartbeat-based slot accounting (sidecar heartbeats to scheduler).

Steps to reproduce / observed behavior:
1) Cust...

1) Cust...

comments.author: (Ava)
comments.body: Acknowledged at 09:38 PDT. Escalating to SRE and Dedicated Ops. Notified customer of initial triage and that we are pausing non-essential maintenance windows.

comments.author: SRE (Diego)
comments.body: Initial telemetry shows sidecar heartbeat ingestion collapse at 09:30. Hypothesis: sidecar process CPU pressure led to missed heartbeats and autoscaler inferred node emptiness, triggering shrink. Collecting /var/log/sidecar logs and kernel traces from nodes.

comments.author: SRE (Priya - networking)
comments.body: Observed short-lived PCIe latency bumps on 3 nodes. Correlating with sidecar restarts triggered by oom-killer. Investigating background process (checkpoint writer) on those nodes.

comments.author: Customer (LuminaHealth - ops)
comments.body: Confirmed they did not push any traffic changes. Appreciated quick update. Asked if we can fail traffic over to their reserved fallback dedicated pool in another AZ while we investigate.

comments.author: (Ava)
comments.body: Provided temporary routing to reserved standby pool in us-west-2a at 10:05 PDT per customer's request; documented change and monitored for restoration of throughput.

comments.author: SRE (Diego)
comments.body: After sidecar restarts and temporary routing, throughput returned to expected baseline by 10:42 PDT. Marking incident mitigated. Will continue root cause analysis and a patch rollout.

comments.author: Customer
comments.body: Requested a postmortem and confirmation of patch schedule before end of week.

title: Enable SIEM-ready audit export beta via tenant allowlist + validate RBAC and destination config (Helio Health)
body:
Request
- Please enable the SIEM-ready audit export beta feature flag for Helio Health and confirm they can create destinations in Console.

Requester / approvals
- Requester: Liam O'Rourke (TAM)
- PM approval: Naomi Feldman (per email + weekly beta check-in)
- Security aware: Marcus Lin (no additional approval needed for allowlist; follow standard PII constraints)

Tenant details
- Customer: Helio Health
- Deployment/tier: enterprise (Hosted + Dedicated mix; beta is hosted/dedicated only)
- Region: us-east
- Tenant: helio-health-prod
- Tenant ID: tnt_7c2f1f9d (from customer record / admin console)
- Primary workspace: ws_01HSHK9G4Y9KZ7

Enablement scope
1) Add tenant to SIEM Audit Export beta allowlist (kill switch remains off).
2) Validate RBAC:
 - Enterprise Admins can: create/update/delete export configs, rotate credentials refs, pause/resume exports.
 - Auditors can: view deliver...

comments.author: Liam O'Rourke (TAM)
comments.body: Filing internal enablement request for Helio Health. Naomi confirmed they’re in the initial 3–5 beta cohort. Tenant ID tnt_7c2f1f9d. Please enable allowlist and let me know once Console shows Audit Export settings page + they can create Datadog/S3 destinations. They have a security checkpoint on Monday.

comments.author: Benji Okafor (Platform)
comments.body: Acknowledged. Will add to allowlist using admin tooling and validate RBAC via impersonation (enterprise_admin vs auditor). Will also sanity-check status endpoint output for the tenant.

comments.author: Benji Okafor (Platform)
comments.body:
Allowlist update completed for tenant tnt_7c2f1f9d in prod/us-east. Confirmed flag value: siem_audit_export_beta=enabled (tenant-scoped). Kill switch remains disabled globally.

Next: RBAC + Console checks.

comments.author: Kaitlyn Nguyen (Auth/RBAC)
comments.body: For validation: enterprise_admin should have permissions audit_export.config:write and audit_export.status:read. auditor role should only have audit_export.status:read. If you see auditors able to edit configs, please capture the role assignment + permission set.

comments.author: Benji Okafor (Platform)
comments.body:
RBAC validation results:
- Impersonated role: enterprise_admin -> can access Console > Settings > Audit Export; can create destination + filters; can pause/resume config.
- Impersonated role: security_auditor -> can view status/health and last error; cannot edit destinations (UI controls disabled; API returns 403 on PATCH/POST).

Looks correct.

comments.author: Liam O'Rourke (TAM)
comments.body: Customer created Datadog destination but is seeing intermittent 403s from Datadog intake. Might be key scoping (matches SUP-18497). For now they will proceed with S3 validation. Can we confirm exporter isn’t stuck/backpressured for their tenant?

comments.author: Benji Okafor (Platform)
comments.body:
Checked exporter status endpoint for tnt_7c2f1f9d: exporter running; Datadog adapter showing auth_error (403) and circuit breaker opening intermittently; S3 adapter healthy.
Action taken: advised customer (via TAM) to pause Datadog destination config until key scope fixed; keep S3 enabled to validate flow.
No system-wide backpressure observed; tenant lag stayed < 2m on S3 path.

comments.author: Liam O'Rourke (TAM)
comments.body: Confirmed with customer: S3 delivery is working; they see partitions created and events flowing. Datadog key rotation scheduled separately. Please close this enablement ticket; I’ll track Datadog fix under SUP-18497 and credential work under INT- follow-up if needed.

title: Enable Hosted data residency enforcement feature flag in staging + seed test orgs/API keys for verification
body:
Background
We are preparing end-to-end staging verification for Hosted data residency enforcement (region pinning + deterministic blocks + standardized errors). This internal support request is to enable the staging feature flags and seed a few test orgs/projects/API keys so Engineering + SRE + Security can validate routing, fallback behavior, logging boundaries, and the public error contract.

Request
1) Enable residency enforcement feature flags in staging
- Enable residency policy evaluation + enforcement in gateway + routing.
- Ensure cross-region fallback is disabled by default when residency constraints are present.

2) Seed staging test entities
Create the following in staging control plane (do not use real customer data):
A) Org: "residency-e2e-eu-only" (owner: platform-test@redwood.example)
- Project: "hosted-chat"
- Residency policy: allowed_regions=[EU], primary_region=EU (...

comments.body: Acknowledged. Will coordinate flag enablement after confirming current staging deploy contains gateway PR-21533 build. Will also confirm routing has PR-21507 in staging before flipping enforcement on.

comments.author: API Gateway
comments.body: Gateway staging is on build from main @ 7c31d2a; includes residency error contract changes behind flag. Streaming path fix for SUP-28577 is included. OK to proceed once routing is confirmed.

comments.author: Mina Haddad (Routing)
comments.body: Routing staging includes residency evaluation integration (PR-21507) behind the same flag. Cross-region fallback is disabled by default when residency policy present. Only exception is explicitly configured allow_cross_region_fallback (not set for these test orgs).

Approved staging enablement with constraints: do not log prompts/outputs; ensure policy_id is OK to expose internally; confirm audit events are present for config changes. Please capture screenshots/links for evidence pack once validated.

comments.author: Sean Gallagher (SRE)
comments.body: Flags enabled in staging at 13:02 UTC. Seed requests in progress. Created 1Password item and shared with #eng-platform + #eng-sre. (No keys pasted here.)

comments.author: Chloe Martin (Console)
comments.body: Confirmed Console shows all three orgs and policy history entries. Blocked requests view shows entries after first test run; request_id links to logs in staging Kibana.

comments.author: API error contract
comments.body: Verified block response: HTTP 409, error_code=RESIDENCY_POLICY_VIOLATION, includes request_id and help_url. Streaming path now returns same structured error (previously generic 500).

comments.author: SRE Manager
comments.body: Marking resolved. Next: add staging monitor to catch unexpected spike in block rate during dogfood (tracked separately by ENG-4846 dashboards).

title: Dedicated pool throughput collapse from connection-reservoir leak causing batched-slot starvation
body:
Issue summary:
DataScribe reported a sudden drop in dedicated pool throughput in us-west-2 starting 2026-03-10 14:10 UTC. Customer traffic experienced elevated p50/p95/p99 for batched routes; many short requests were queued and not scheduled to GPUs.

Impact:
- Customer-facing latency p99 increased from ~220ms to >2.8s for batched generation routes.
- Throughput dropped ~40% for the reserved pool attached to DataScribe's dedicated capacity.
- Automatic fallback to lower-tier variants triggered repeatedly, causing billing and quality regressions.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: ds-prod-reserved-17 (us-west-2)
- Serving runtime: 2026.02.21-release
- Autoscaler: adaptive-v3
- Affected models: redwood-v1-13b (pinned), redwood-v2-7b (fallback)

Steps to reproduce (from customer):
1) Run high concurrency burst of many short chatlet streams (avg 120 tokens, 50ms client think-time) pinned to ...

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Acknowledged customer, collected initial traces and asked for traffic sample. Escalated to SRE with high priority.

comments.author: Diego Martinez
comments.body: Started reproducer against staging. Observed reservoir holding behavior. Added temporary alert on scheduler.connection_reservoir_occupied > 80%.

comments.author: Priya Kapoor
comments.body: Confirmed recent handshake change in runtime PR #4821 could affect idle detection; requested runtime logs for connection state transitions.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Customer accepted a short maintenance window to apply a configuration rollback to restore 15s idle timeout on their pool.

comments.author: Diego Martinez
comments.body: Rolled back the timeout config for ds-prod-reserved-17 as an emergency mitigation — reservoir occupancy fell from 96% to 28% and p99 returned to ~240ms. Noted this is a temporary mitigation.

comments.author: Marcela Ruiz
comments.body: Proposed code fix to fold duplicate reservations and added an integration test for multi-part streams. Patch target: autoscaler v3 + scheduler bookkeeping. Linked PR: github.com/redwood-inference/autoscaler/pull/1299.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Customer report: latency stabilized after mitigation; however they saw a single recurrence during a midnight burst — investigating follow-up automation to prevent regressions.

comments.author: Diego Martinez
comments.body: Hotfix deployed to scheduler that ensures X milliseconds idle threshold is respected and merges duplicate reservoir entries on reconnect. Monitoring shows stable reservoir occupancy across pools.

title: Template: Private upgrade change request (required approvals/evidence)
body:
Goal: Standardize the change record for enterprise Private upgrades. We currently have inconsistent checklists across teams.

Deliverable: Create an internal-support Jira template issue (cloneable) called "Private Upgrade Change Request" with required fields and checkboxes for: change owner, customer approver, window, rollback plan, preflight evidence, SRE review, Security sign-off (when required), and customer comms.

Acceptance criteria:
- Template enforces required links to relevant Confluence policies
- Template includes explicit GO/NO-GO at window start
- Template includes post-window verification window and all-clear comms

References:
- Confluence: Production Change Management Policy
- Confluence: Private Deployments: Upgrade Go/No-Go Checklist (Enterprise)
- Confluence: Planned Maintenance Communications SOP (Enterprise)
- Confluence: Planned Change: SLO / Error Budget Impact ...

title: traffic-diversion-to-quantized-fallback-enters-warmup-rack-causing-burst-503s
body:
Issue summary:
Customer NovaSearch reported a sudden spike in 5xx (503) responses and p99 inference latency starting 2026-03-11 21:10 UTC. Traffic was diverted to a quantized GPU fallback variant that appears to be in a warmup/initializing state on a single rack, causing serial cold-starts and temporary capacity exhaustion.

Impact:
- Intermittent 503s and p99 latency > 12s for search reranking calls between 21:10–21:48 UTC.
- Around 18% of requests for particular routes returned 503 during the peak window.
- Customer-facing application experienced degraded search relevance and increased error rates across user sessions.

Environment:
- Redwood hosted dedicated pool (NovaSearch reserved cluster), prod-us-east-1, model routing policy uses auto-fallback: primary -> quantized-gpu-fallback -> cpu-fallback.
- Autoscaler set: min_replicas=2, max_replicas=16, scale_window=30s.

Steps to repr...

12 UTC — Engineering Manager (Diego Fernández): 'Targeting fast-track merge and hotfix deploy on 2026-03-15; will update timeline after canary metrics.'

title: Request: Support/admin override tools for onboarding checklist (mark steps complete/reset + audit trail)
body:
Requesting internal Support tooling to override onboarding checklist state for a tenant during troubleshooting.

Background / why this is needed:
- The new Guided Onboarding + “First production launch” checklist is being rolled out in Console.
- Support is starting to use checklist step state to triage onboarding issues (auth, streaming disconnects, 429 surprises, usage dashboard lag).
- During troubleshooting, we frequently need to:
 1) re-run a step after changing configuration (e.g., customer rotates API key, changes model, fixes env vars), or
 2) mark a step complete when the customer performed the action outside the instrumented path (e.g., made first request via OpenAI-compat layer or a custom gateway path that didn’t emit the event during early rollout), or
 3) unstick accounts when instrumentation gaps/race conditions cause a step to remain “Not started” even though the custom...

 3) unstick accounts when instrumentation gaps/race conditions cause a step to remain “Not started” even though the custom...

comments.author: Dev Patel
comments.body: Logging this as an internal helpdesk request for the Console/Platform teams. Without an override we’re going to be blocked on using the checklist as a reliable triage tool during rollout. We already have 3+ cases where a customer did the action but the step didn’t flip due to instrumentation gaps.

comments.author: Chloe Martin
comments.body: Acknowledged. We can add an admin override layer in the checklist progress service. We’ll need to align on permission model (Support role vs Console admin) and audit log format. Looping in Dylan Brooks + Jada Williams for API shape.

comments.author: Dylan Brooks
comments.body:
Agree on need. Proposal: internal-only endpoints in checklist progress service:
- POST /internal/tenants/{tenant_id}/checklist/{checklist_id}/steps/{step_id}:override (state + reason)
- DELETE /internal/tenants/{tenant_id}/checklist/{checklist_id}/steps/{step_id}:override (clear)
Console will gate behind an internal feature flag + RBAC.
We’ll store overrides separately and compute final state = max(computed, override) with monotonic rules (reset allowed only via explicit reset override).

comments.author: Priya Shah
comments.body: From support perspective: must support both ‘mark complete’ and ‘reset’. Reset is critical for streaming troubleshooting and for “first successful request” step when the customer wants to validate with a clean run after fixing auth. Also strongly prefer required reason + field for SUP ticket ID.

comments.author: Marcus Lin
comments.body: Security note: ensure audit log contains operator identifiers but no customer PII beyond tenant/org ID. Retention should match existing admin action logging. Please avoid freeform fields that could accidentally capture secrets; add UI copy warning not to paste API keys/logs into reason.

comments.author: Chloe Martin
comments.body:
Implementing under ENG-5852. Will include:
- RBAC check: internal_support or console_admin
- Required reason (min length) + optional ‘support_case_key’ field
- Immutable audit table + emit internal audit event
Targeting in time for cohort rollout support readiness.

comments.author: Chloe Martin
comments.body: Shipped in PR-28522 (admin override endpoints + audit logging). Dylan to add Console surface behind internal flag. Support playbook update in progress with Dev + Priya.

comments.author: Dev Patel
comments.body: Validated in prod with test tenant: can mark ‘API key created’ and reset ‘Streaming established’. Audit entries visible; reason required. Closing.

title: SIEM export: audit events not normalized (inconsistent field names/shape across event types)
body:
Issue summary
Vectorgrid reports that audit events exported to Splunk HEC contain inconsistent field names and shapes depending on event type. Their SIEM correlation rules expect stable fields for actor, target, tenant, request/correlation IDs, and event taxonomy. Current inconsistency is blocking their rollout of detection rules and creating noise in dashboards.

Impact
- SIEM parsing rules break for a subset of events.
- Detection queries cannot reliably join events by actor/tenant/request.
- Customer perceives audit logging as incomplete/unreliable for compliance monitoring.

Customer expectation
- All exported events conform to the canonical v1 schema with consistent top-level fields.
- Exporter should tag schema_version and normalize legacy/variant producers into v1 shape.

Environment
- Dedicated deployment (Vectorgrid)
- Export target: Splunk HEC
- Region: us-east

Examples (cu...

Impact

- Detection queries cannot reliably join events by actor/tenant/request.
- Customer perceives audit logging as incomplete/unreliable for compliance monitoring.

Customer expectation
- All exported events conform to the canonical v1 schema with consistent top-level fields.
- Exporter should tag schema_version and normalize legacy/variant producers into v1 shape.

Environment
- Dedicated deployment (Vectorgrid)
- Export target: Splunk HEC
- Region: us-east

Examples (cu...

comments.body: 18 Dev Patel (Support): Opened ticket from Vectorgrid (SE escalation via #support). They sent ~200 sample events showing mixed field names. Attaching sanitized examples in description. Requesting Security Eng / Exporter owner to confirm whether this is expected prior to schema v1 rollout or a bug.

02 Kevin Osei (Security Eng): Confirmed this is a bug/known gap. We have multiple producers emitting "legacy" shapes (pre v1) and exporter currently forwards mostly as-is. Plan is exporter-side normalization + schema_version tagging so customers can depend on stable v1 fields. Tracking in exporter PR: pr-842-add-schema-version-tagging-and-normalization. I’ll post a specific mapping list once I finish diffing the customer samples vs expected v1.

26 Hanae Suzuki (Security Eng): Quick triage on samples: API key events appear to originate from console-backend older codepath (uses type/id/timestamp/workspace). Policy events appear to originate from policy-engine service that hasn’t adopted event_type naming convention yet. We should ensure lints reject non-registered event types going forward (ref: redwood/pr-21991).

41 Kevin Osei (Security Eng): Proposed normalization rules (exporter):
- Map legacy keys: type|eventType -> event_type; timestamp|time -> occurred_at; id -> event_id
- Tenancy: workspace|org_id -> tenant_id
- Actor: actor string -> actor_id + actor_type=user (if prefix usr_) else service; actor object -> actor_type/actor_id
- Network: ip -> source_ip
- Correlation: traceId -> trace_id
- Outcome: status|outcome -> result (success|failure) + error_code/error_message when present
- Unknown/producer-specific fields go under extensions.*

Ask: Support confirm customer is OK with actor_email being optional (not always available for service actors).

12 Dev Patel (Support): Confirmed with customer: actor_email optional is fine; they mostly need actor_id/actor_type/source_ip/tenant_id/event_type/occurred_at/event_id and request/trace IDs when available.

55 Kevin Osei (Security Eng): Patch ready in exporter branch; adds schema_version=1.0 and normalization middleware. Also validates payload against schema for known event_types (best-effort: emits metric and forwards original if validation fails to avoid dropping events). Pending review from Observability (Allison) on metric naming and from SRE (Sean) on retry behavior. Linked: sources/github/audit-log-exporter/pr-842-add-schema-version-tagging-and-normalization.json

30 Sean Gallagher (SRE): Reviewed exporter behavior. Recommend: do not block export on schema validation failure; emit counter audit_export.validation_failed and include producer_service tag (low-cardinality allow-list). Also ensure we’re not increasing payload size above Splunk HEC limits. OK to proceed if extensions payload is capped (e.g., 8KB) and truncation is explicit (extensions_truncated=true).

07 Kevin Osei (Security Eng): Incorporated SRE feedback: extension payload cap + explicit truncation flag (extensions_truncated). Added metrics and dashboard link request for observability-pack follow-up. Preparing staged rollout to us-east Dedicated first.

22 Dev Patel (Support): Sent customer interim workaround: update Splunk props/transforms to coalesce fields (event_type = coalesce(event_type, type, eventType), occurred_at = coalesce(occurred_at, timestamp, time), tenant_id = coalesce(tenant_id, workspace, org_id), source_ip = coalesce(source_ip, ip)). Also clarified duplicates may occur due to retry semantics; use event_id for de-dupe (linked SUP-18510).

40 Kevin Osei (Security Eng): PR pr-842 merged. Rollout plan: enable normalization in exporter behind flag export.normalization.v1=true per environment. Targeting Vectorgrid Dedicated exporter instances in us-east on 2026-02-10 during low-traffic window.

15 Kevin Osei (Security Eng): Flag enabled for Vectorgrid exporter. Initial metrics: validation_failed <0.3% (mostly unknown legacy eventType strings). No increased retry rate. Sending Support the list of remaining producer gaps (policy-engine + one console path) that will be fixed via producer-side adoption + lints.

18 Dev Patel (Support): Customer confirms new events look consistent in Splunk (schema_version present; stable top-level fields). They will monitor for 48 hours before closing. Asked for written guarantee on deprecation window; pointed them to versioning/backcompat policy Confluence page.

56 Dev Patel (Support): Customer confirmed success and unblocked correlation rules. Closing ticket. Follow-ups remain tracked internally for producer-side enforcement and reducing validation_failed to ~0.

title: Ephemeral driver IO attach lag causing reserved-pool throughput sag
body:
Issue summary:
An intermittent surge of host-level IO activity (short-lived bursts from a background checkpoint writer) is causing large GPU attach/handshake latencies in a reserved pool. The attach delays lead to the autoscaler and scheduler seeing transient capacity unavailability, which triggers rapid slot reassignments and an observable throughput sag for the customer's reserved capacity window.

Impact:
- Streamline AI reports elevated p95/p99 latency for batched requests during a 30-90s window several times per hour.
- Throughput drop: observed 20-40% drop in steady-state token throughput for the affected partition during bursts.
- Application-level errors: a small percentage (~0.2%) of requests return 5xx attach-timeouts before autoscaler fallback kicks in.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: reserved-cluster-14 (prod-us-west)
- Host type: 8x A100 (mainly homogeneous) with local NV...

comments.author: Ava Chen ()
comments.body: Customer reported intermittent p99 spikes and a handful of 5xx attach timeouts. Gathered logs and asked for traffic profile and whether local checkpointing is on.

comments.author: Streamline AI (Customer)
comments.body: Confirmed they run an internal checkpoint writer on hosts (default every 10m). Attached their writer config and sample CPU/io profile.

comments.author: Luis Martinez (SRE)
comments.body: Correlated Grafana attach-latency and host IO spike panels. Reproduced locally with synthetic writer. Draft mitigation: throttle checkpoint writer and adjust driver-manager attach backoff in next patch.

comments.author: Ava Chen ()
comments.body: Applied conservative throttle on Streamline AI reserved nodes and enabled prewarm persistence for their pool as a short-term mitigation. Customer reports immediate reduction in p99 spikes.

comments.author: Luis Martinez (SRE)
comments.body: Preparing PR for driver-manager backoff change; scheduling NVMe QoS experiment in staging on 2026-03-15.

title: Request for custom burst/refill policy to support occasional long-tail embedding spikes without impacting low-latency chat
body: BrightMaps is running a mixed workload on a Redwood Dedicated pool: low-latency chat for end-users and periodic large embedding backfills (long-tail documents). They are seeing unexpected 429s on embedding jobs during scheduled spikes which are degrading their bulk import SLA. They are requesting a nonstandard burst/refill policy (occasional high-capacity bursts without permanently increasing cost or harming chat latency) and guidance on configuration and migration steps.

They are seeing unexpected 429s on embedding jobs during scheduled spikes which are degrading their bulk import SLA. They are requesting a nonstandard burst/refill policy (occasional high-capacity bursts without permanently increasing cost or harming chat latency) and guidance on configuration and migration steps.

comments.body: 20 UTC - Ava Chen (Support) - Initial intake: Customer requests a burst model that allows 3x sustained throughput for a 30 minute window once per day without changing long-term quotas. Wants to avoid separate pool due to cost. Asked for logs and exact schedule.

15 UTC - Customer (BrightMaps - Ops) - Provided scheduling cron: 02:00 UTC daily for 4 hours, median document size 6KB, target 10k docs. Prefers not to maintain separate cluster if a config tweak can work.

02 UTC - Liam O'Connor (SRE) - Triage: Reproduced token-window exhaustion in testenv when simulating 24 concurrent workers. Hypothesis: tenant-level burst credits exhausted quickly due to mixed realtime and bulk accounting sharing refill window. Suggested options: separate route quotas, create short-lived burst pool, or increase tenant burst credits temporarily.

45 UTC - Priya Patel (Eng - Rate Limiter) - Added: We can support a scheduled burst policy via route_tags and scheduled_quota_refill; this requires config change and a short rollout. Need customer's approval for a temporary increase of refill rate during their window.

title: Elevated P95 latency and 504s on dedicated pool — node-level interference + autoscale gap
body: Issue summary: Starting 2026-03-10 08:22 UTC, Ledgerly reported intermittent p95 latency spikes and an increase in 504/timeout responses for requests routed to their dedicated GPU pool in eu-west-1. Surface metrics show sustained p50 ~150ms but p95 jumping from ~220ms baseline to 1.2-1.6s during spikes, and a burst of 504s coincident with those spikes. Impact: customer-facing feature degraded; higher error rate and user-visible latency. Hypothesis: localized GPU contention (noisy neighbor) on one or two nodes combined with autoscaler not reacting quickly enough (min_replicas/config mismatch), leading to request queueing and timeouts.

Hypothesis: localized GPU contention (noisy neighbor) on one or two nodes combined with autoscaler not reacting quickly enough (min_replicas/config mismatch), leading to request queueing and timeouts.

comments.body: 38 UTC - Marcus Ortiz (SRE): Identified node-12 as primary offender; cordoning now. Initial cordon shows immediate p95 improvement. Will keep monitoring.
10 UTC - Ava Chen (CS): Informed Ledgerly that we're investigating and performed a temporary mitigation (cordon). Customer reports partial relief; they requested timeline for permanent fix.

20 UTC - SRE Lead (Priya Nair): Autoscaler cooldown of 300s is too conservative for bursty traffic. Proposal: reduce to 60s for pools with label bursty_workloads; need config change and rollout. Linking config PR.
50 UTC - Marcus Ortiz: Increased min_replicas to 4 and reduced cooldown to 60s in staging; running soak tests. No regressions observed so far.
05 UTC - Ledgerly (customer comment): Thank you for the quick workaround. We'll monitor and provide feedback. Please keep us updated on scheduler placement changes.

title: Unexpected p99 latency and 504s when continuous batcher scales down during active streaming sessions
body:
Issue summary:
LexiDocs reported intermittent p99 latency spikes and a small number of 504 timeouts on streaming completions that correlate with the continuous batcher performing scale-down events on their dedicated pool. The pattern appears during periods when active streaming sessions remain open while background autoscaler removes a GPU worker.

Impact:
- Customer is seeing ~1-3 504s/hour and p99 latency increase from ~450ms -> 3s for streaming end-of-response in us-east-1.
- Affects production traffic for LexiDocs (dedicated), causing degraded UX for end-users using real-time document summarization.

Environment:
- Customer tier: dedicated
- Pool: lexidocs-prod-pool (reserved 2 GPUs, burst to 3)
- Region: us-east-1
- Model: redwood-7b-v1, streaming completion API

Steps to reproduce (observed):
1) Customer holds ~15 concurrent open streaming connections with low per-connection QPS...

title: Internal IT/admin users receiving 403 in Console (missing permissions) during RBAC v2 dogfood
body:
Issue summary:
Internal IT/admin users (Okta group: "IT-Admins" and "Corp-Helpdesk") are getting blocked in the Redwood Console during RBAC v2 dogfood with "You don’t have permission" / 403 errors. They cannot access org settings pages needed for provisioning and support.

Impact:
- IT cannot perform basic admin workflows (user provisioning checks, role assignments, org-level settings) during dogfood.
- Support/IT escalations increasing because admins cannot self-serve.
- Risk: perceived instability of RBAC v2 rollout; potential to slow down internal dogfood readiness.

Environment:
- Console: prod (internal dogfood)
- Authz: RBAC v2 feature flag enabled for Redwood internal org
- Identity: Okta SSO with group->role mapping

Who is impacted:
- David Kim (IT)
- Fiona McCarthy (SecOps)
- On-call internal admins attempting to triage permission-denied reports

Symptoms / error:
- Console ...

Who is impacted:
- David Kim (IT)
- Fiona McCarthy (SecOps)
- On-call internal admins attempting to triage permission-denied reports

Symptoms / error:
- Console ...

comments.body: 12 PT - David Kim (Reporter): Noticed since yesterday afternoon: IT-Admins can log in but cannot open Settings -> Users / Roles. Console shows permission denied and page is blank. This blocks provisioning troubleshooting for new hires. Please advise on correct role binding under RBAC v2 dogfood.

34 PT - Kaitlyn Nguyen (Assignee): Acknowledged. Please paste a request ID from the failed call (from DevTools) and confirm which Okta groups the user is in. Also confirm whether you’re in the Redwood internal org or a test org.
02 PT - David Kim: Request ID: req_7d3b1c1f1a. User in Okta groups: IT-Admins, Corp-Helpdesk. Org: Redwood Internal (dogfood).

25 PT - Felix Schneider (Console): Pulled logs for req_7d3b1c1f1a. Denied on action permission: "console.org.users.list" (exact string in log) at scope org=redwood-internal. Denial reason code in log: MISSING_PERMISSION. User had bindings only for legacy role "admin" (v1) which in v2 mapping does not include Console permissions (only a subset for API key mgmt).

41 PT - Kaitlyn Nguyen: Confirmed: This is a migration gap in our default mapping for internal admin groups. In v2, Console backend endpoints require explicit console.* permissions; the legacy mapping grants control-plane.* but not console.*. Proposing immediate workaround: bind IT-Admins to built-in template "Org Admin" in Redwood Internal org until we finalize an "Internal Admin" template.

05 PT - David Kim: Workaround sounds ok. We need IT to be able to manage users/roles + view audit logs. We do not want IT to have billing admin by default if avoidable, but can accept temporarily for dogfood.

22 PT - Jordan Lee (Policy engine): +1 on short-term fix. Longer-term: add a first-party role template "IT Support Admin" (org-scoped) with users/roles read-write, audit log read/export, no billing, no secret rotation. We should generate it in the policy bundle and document recommended internal group mapping.

10 PT - Kaitlyn Nguyen: Implemented immediate fix in Redwood Internal org: created role binding: Okta group "IT-Admins" -> role template "Org Admin" at org scope. Also added "Corp-Helpdesk" -> "Read-only Auditor" temporarily so Helpdesk can at least view user lists and audit logs. Please re-login and confirm.

37 PT - David Kim: Confirmed after logout/login: Settings -> Users loads, can invite/remove users and edit role assignments. Audit log page loads as well. Thanks. Requesting follow-up to reduce scope (remove billing permissions) before broad dogfood.

18 PT - Naomi Feldman (PM): Tracking follow-up action: define internal-facing role template (IT Support Admin) and update internal Okta mapping guidance. Please link any proposed permission set to the Role Templates & Matrix page.

05 PT - Chloe Martin (Console backend): Adding note: we should ensure Console pages have clearer “why denied” UX for internal dogfood. Current experience is blank page + toast. We should at least show required permission + escalation path for admins (ties to denial reason codes + debug header).

28 PT - Kaitlyn Nguyen: Follow-ups captured below. Closing this internal support ticket since access is restored via role binding change; remaining work will be tracked in engineering/product tickets.

Resolution:
- Added org-scoped role bindings for Okta groups IT-Admins and Corp-Helpdesk in Redwood Internal org.
- Verified IT-Admins can access Console org admin workflows.

Root cause:
- Legacy/v1 role mapping did not include newly required console.* permissions under deny-by-default middleware.

Follow-up actions:
1) Create/ship "IT Support Admin" role template (no billing) in policy bundle; update internal mapping docs (Owner: Jordan Le...

title: Request access for Security + GRC to Audit Log Coverage Dashboard (least-privilege roles)
body:
Requesting access for Security and GRC to the new “Audit Log Coverage (Sensitive Actions)” dashboard used for evidence collection and ongoing monitoring.

Business need
- SOC 2 / ISO-aligned evidence: demonstrate monitoring + coverage tracking for sensitive actions audit events.
- Security incident response: ability to quickly validate whether expected audit events are being emitted for specific event_types/services.

Requested access (least privilege)
1) Read-only access to dashboard(s) + saved views.
2) No ability to edit dashboards, create alerts, or query raw log payloads.
3) Access should allow viewing:
 - expected event types vs observed
 - per-service coverage breakdown
 - time window filters (last 24h / 7d / 30d)
 - links out to runbook for regressions
4) Access should NOT allow:
 - exporting raw event bodies containing target identifiers or other potentially sensitive context...

title: Consult: rate-window and quota split for mixed low-latency chat + bulk embedding pipeline
body: Issue summary: Customer runs a mixed workload in a dedicated pool: low-latency chat (sub-200ms SLO) interleaved with large bulk embedding backfills. After a recent change to their batching client, they see frequent 429s on chat routes during concurrent backfills. Impact: chat latency degrades as client-side retries amplify throttles; customer reports significant UX regressions on production traffic. Environment: Dedicated pool (dedicated tier) backed by a 4-GPU nodeset, multiple routes defined (chat-priority, embeddings-bulk).

normalized.description: Issue summary: Customer runs a mixed workload in a dedicated pool: low-latency chat (sub-200ms SLO) interleaved with large bulk embedding backfills. After a recent change to their batching client, they see frequent 429s on chat routes during concurrent backfills. Impact: chat latency degrades as client-side retries amplify throttles; customer reports significant UX regressions on production traffic. Environment: Dedicated pool (dedicated tier) backed by a 4-GPU nodeset, multiple routes defined (chat-priority, embeddings-bulk).

comments.author: Aisha Rahman ()
comments.body: Customer says this started after they upgraded their embedding uploader to aggressive parallelism and switched to larger batch sizes. They want guidance to avoid 429s for chat while maintaining bulk throughput.

comments.author: Miguel Torres (SRE)
comments.body: Initial triage — looks like token-window exhaustion at the route layer. Possible causes: shared tenant token accounting between routes, burst credits being consumed by embeddings workers, and refill smoothing lag after refill. Pulling relevant metrics now.

comments.author: Miguel Torres (SRE)
comments.body: Found correlated spikes in embeddings token consumption and a sawtooth pattern on the tenant-level refill. Slack thread: #support -> https://slack.redwood.com/archives/C05RATE/xyZ12345

comments.author: Customer (AtlasSearch)
comments.body: Can we get a config recommendation? We need chat preserved at 200ms p95 and still finish the 10k job within 2 hours if possible.

comments.author: Miguel Torres (SRE)
comments.body: Suggested workaround posted in console and sent via email: 1) create route-level quota separation for chat vs embeddings, 2) apply small priority weights giving chat higher priority, 3) schedule bulk with jitter and reduce parallel workers from 20 -> 6. Customer will test tonight.

comments.author: Customer (AtlasSearch)
comments.body: After reducing workers to 6 and applying route quota split, chat 429s dropped ~95% and p95 latency returned to expected. Bulk runtime increased but remained within acceptable window. They asked for permanent config guidance and whether we can automate fallback to a cheaper embedding variant when bulk consumes burst credits.

Impact: chat latency degrades as client-side retries amplify throttles; customer reports significant UX regressions on production traffic. Environment: Dedicated pool (dedicated tier) backed by a 4-GPU nodeset, multiple routes defined (chat-priority, embeddings-bulk).

title: cache-index-mismatch triggers cold KV lookups on repeated prompts causing timeouts
body: Issue summary: BrightWrite Labs reports repeat prompts (identical user messages sent sequentially) are experiencing significantly higher latencies and occasional 504 timeouts starting 2026-03-12 08:30 UTC. Impact: production traffic for a dedicated tenant; customer reports degraded UX with repeat prompt cases in their chat product, estimated 18% of requests hitting the slow path for recent hour. Environment: Dedicated pool (dedicated-nyc-1), prod, us-east-1 region. Observed: p50 increased from ~60ms to ~320ms, p95 from ~220ms to ~1.8s for repeat prompts; error spikes of 504/timeouts at ~0.6% during peak. Immediate customer impact: user-facing failures and latency spikes for repeated-turn interactions (e.g., quick clarifications).

comments.author: (Emily)
comments.body: Customer reported degradation at 08:30 UTC after overnight traffic increase. Collected traces and initial logs, escalated to SRE.

comments.author: SRE (Mateo)
comments.body: Confirmed correlation between scheduler rebalance and latency spikes. Created reproduction against dedicated-nyc-1. Temporary mitigation applied (raise retention TTL on shard set).

comments.author: BrightWrite PE
comments.body: Confirmed immediate improvement after mitigation. They still want a permanent guarantee to avoid regressions during rebalances.

comments.author: Priya Rao - runtime
comments.body: Looking into fallback path. Suspect synchronous index rebuild on request path is the primary contributor. Proposing async warm/rebuild + rate-limiting for rebuilds. Will open a patch.

Immediate customer impact: user-facing failures and latency spikes for repeated-turn interactions (e.g., quick clarifications).

comments.author: Talia Benson
comments.body: Filing this as the formal prod-change approval request for the controlled failover test. ENG-2421 has the run-of-show; this ticket is for the enablement + rollback signoffs.

comments.author: Tomáš Novák
comments.body: Acknowledged. Need explicit: (1) exact flags + scopes, (2) who can approve execution in-prod, (3) rollback steps that do not require code deploys, (4) monitoring links. Please add those before we route for approvals.

comments.author: Rafael Mendes
comments.body: Added to body: approval-required mode, allowlist only, rollback is flag-based + policy snapshot restore. Approval in-prod: on-call SRE (myself) with routing on-call (Ravi Menon) present in channel.

comments.author: Allison Grant
comments.body: Observability readiness: OK if pr-907 dashboards are deployed to prod by EOD 01/13 and alert routing is tested in staging. Please ensure burn-rate alerts are in "ticket" mode during the test (no paging) unless error burn exceeds Sev2 thresholds.

comments.author: Victor Alvarez
comments.body: Networking: confirm no DNS TTL changes during the window. We can support. Please add check: validate resolver caches in us-east and eu-west before induced impairment; provider status page open in parallel.

comments.author: Mina Haddad
comments.body: Routing signoff: OK. Ensure capacity gate is hard-blocking reroute if eu-west headroom < threshold. Also require hold-down timers enabled for the allowlist to prevent flip-flop.

comments.author: Sean Gallagher
comments.body: Approved with conditions: (1) go/no-go checklist completed in ENG-2421 thread, (2) approval-required mode enforced, (3) support liaison in channel, (4) stop conditions reiterated at start of window.

comments.author: Tomáš Novák
comments.body: Change window started. Confirmed: dry-run on globally, execution on allowlist only. Monitoring active. Status page not updated (planned internal test); comms plan ready if unexpected impact occurs.

comments.author: Rafael Mendes
comments.body: Test executed and rolled back successfully. No broad customer impact observed; brief elevated latency for 2 allowlisted tenants during induced impairment. Follow-ups logged in ENG-2426. Closing approval record.

comments.author: Tomáš Novák
comments.body: Closing as completed. Please ensure final artifacts (results + action items) are linked from ENG-2426 and the game day results page once published.

title: Latency regression (p95) on model-xyz after runtime release (us-east Dedicated)
body:
Issue summary
- Customer (Tensorlytics) reports increased p95 latency on Dedicated traffic for model-xyz immediately following Redwood runtime release rollout in us-east.
- Report references internal canary alert firing around the same time.

Customer impact
- Elevated p95 end-to-end latency on generation requests; intermittent client-side timeouts (customer timeout set to 3.0s).
- Customer reports downstream job SLA impact on their real-time pipeline.

Scope / environment
- Environment: prod / us-east / Dedicated (Tensorlytics)
- Model: model-xyz (customer pinned; no model-version change reported by customer)
- Start time (customer-reported): ~2026-02-07 08:40 UTC
- Runtime change window: runtime rollout started ~2026-02-07 08:15 UTC (us-east dedicated pools)

Observed metrics (Support + SRE verification)
- Baseline (prior 24h): p95 e2e latency ~1.25s; tokens/sec steady (within norma...

comments.body: 02 UTC — Rafael Mendes (Support/SRE): Customer (Tensorlytics) reports p95 jumped from ~1.2s to ~2.0s on model-xyz in us-east Dedicated. They’re seeing timeouts at 3s client timeout. Asking for request IDs + exact start time + whether model version changed on their side.

11 UTC — Tensorlytics (Customer): Started noticing around 08:40 UTC. No application changes. Model pin unchanged. Example request IDs: req_7c1f… req_8a22… (more in attachment shared via secure channel).

18 UTC — Aisha Bello (SRE): Confirmed perf-canary fired for model-xyz us-east about ~09:14 UTC (warning, then sustained). Dashboard shows runtime-only latency shift; queueing-time panel looks flat. Suggest checking kernel tag + runtime build tag for the pool.

26 UTC — Omar El-Sayed (Runtime Instrumentation): Pulled telemetry for model-xyz: runtime_build changed to runtime-2026.02.07.1; kernel_selection_id changed on prefill path at same time. Decode looks stable; prefill p95 up ~40% in the long-prompt bucket.

40 UTC — Sean Gallagher (SRE, assignee): Treating as Sev2 customer-impacting performance regression. Coordinating with runtime on rollback vs kernel pin. If rollback resolves quickly, we’ll keep investigation scoped to kernel change window.

05 UTC — Vanessa Ortiz (Fleet/Rollout): Initiated rollback for Tensorlytics us-east dedicated pool to runtime-2026.01.30.2. Also applied temporary kernel pin for model-xyz to previous kernel set (per Hiro’s recommendation). ETA ~15–20 min for full drain/replace.
28 UTC — Caleb Johnson (Telemetry): Post-rollback metrics show p95 trending down; tokens/sec recovering. perf-canary no longer above threshold in last 10m, awaiting sustained confirmation window.
44 UTC — Rafael Mendes (Support/SRE): Sent customer update: rollback in progress and early indications latency recovering. Asked them to confirm from their side once stable.
02 UTC — Tensorlytics (Customer): Confirmed p95 back to normal range (~1.2–1.3s). Timeouts stopped. Thanks.

15 UTC — Hiro Tanaka (Kernel): Early suspicion: new prefill kernel path regression for seq_len ~2k–3k on H100 in this build; reproducing in perf suite. Will provide RCA details once confirmed. Recommend keeping kernel pin for model-xyz until fix is merged and canary-green in us-east.

30 UTC — Sean Gallagher (SRE): Marking Resolved. Mitigation: rollback + kernel pin. Action items tracked in runtime/release workflows (outside Jira Support). Will share RCA summary with customer when available.

title: Private: scheduled-worker service tokens receive 403 after STS realm claim contains slash
body:
Issue summary:
NovaAnalytics reports that scheduled background workers began failing with 403s when calling Redwood Private admin APIs after their internal STS rotation. Impact: dozens of scheduled jobs failing across environments, customer unable to run nightly ingestion.

Impact:
- Customer production scheduled workers returning 403 on admin/model endpoints.
- Jobs retried, causing backlog and higher cost.
- Affects all workers that use service tokens issued by customer's STS.

Environment:
- Redwood Private (on-prem bundle v1.6.2) deployed in NovaAnalytics datacenter (prod-us-east).
- Identity stack: internal STS -> ingress-proxy -> redwood identity-proxy -> private control plane.
- Auth scheme: bearer token with JWT including custom "realm" claim used by customer's RBAC mapping.

Steps to reproduce:
1) Obtain service token from customer's STS for job-runner service account (token ...
