comments.body: 12 Priya Nair (CS): Customer reported sudden 403s on scheduled ingestion jobs after STS rotation. Attached sample token and logs. Triage P1 due to production impact.
30 Ava Chen (Support): Initial triage - looks like claim value contains '/'. Asking for exact token (redacted) and ingress-proxy config. Requested earliest window for test change.
05 NovaAnalytics (Customer): Provided sample JWT (redacted) and said new STS release added hierarchical realm claims like 'edge/analytics' for multi-tenant separation.

20 Diego Martinez (SRE): Reproduced locally using customer token. Identity-proxy normalizes claim by splitting on non-alphanumeric characters; confirmed behavior. Filed INT-4421 to track internal parsing tech debt.
00 Ava Chen (Support): Provided workaround - configure ingress-proxy to URL-encode '/' in realm claim. Customer applied and validated jobs resumed. Marked as temporary mitigation.

35 Marta Kovac (Eng): Created PR to remove slash-based splitting in claim parser and add unit tests for UTF-8 characters. PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/runtime/pull/8123. Planning rollout in private v1.6.4.

10 Ava Chen (Support): Patch merged and staged for 1.6.4. Coordinating with NovaAnalytics for scheduled upgrade. Customer validated workaround downstream; no further action required now. Closing support ticket after post-upgrade validation window.
00 NovaAnalytics (Customer): Confirming they are happy to wait for scheduled upgrade; noted workaround is acceptable for now. Thank you for fast support.

title: Standardize ephemeral workspace creation and autosweeper for feature pods
body:
Issue summary:
We need a standardized, auditable automation that provisions ephemeral Slack channels, Confluence draft spaces/pages, and temporary Jira groups when a feature pod requests a sandbox workspace. The automation should attach an owner, enforce RBAC, and schedule an automatic review/archival (autosweeper) after a configurable TTL (default 30 days).

Background / why: 
Feature teams frequently create ad-hoc channels and Confluence pages for short-lived experiments. Lack of standardization causes orphaned channels, inconsistent permissions, and audit gaps. Security and Platform would like a predictable lifecycle that reduces noise and improves cost/retention hygiene.

Impact: 
- Cross-pod clutter in Slack and Confluence; difficulty finding active workspaces.
- Manual cleanup burden on Platform and SRE.
- Potential permission drift and audit holes (unknown owners).

Request: 
1...

title: Suggestions beta: Risk level label is confusing to non-technical stakeholders (needs clearer explanation + shareable language)
body:
Issue summary:
Arkadia Finance (Optimize Suggestions beta) reports that the “Risk: Low/Medium/High” label in the Console Suggestions view is being interpreted by internal stakeholders as security/compliance risk (or “chance Redwood is wrong”), rather than “likelihood of latency/reliability regression if you apply the recommended batching/caching change.” They requested clearer, shareable language for governance review and change-management tickets.

Impact:
- Slows adoption of Suggestions recommendations (internal approvals blocked)
- Increases back-and-forth with platform governance/security teams
- Risk of miscommunication: stakeholders assume “High risk” implies security risk

Where this shows up:
- Suggestions list table risk badge tooltip
- Details drawer “Risk” section copy
- Beta docs page (glossary / definitions)

Customer request:
1) Rename/clarify what “risk” means (performa...

normalized.description:
Issue summary:
Arkadia Finance (Optimize Suggestions beta) reports that the “Risk: Low/Medium/High” label in the Console Suggestions view is being interpreted by internal stakeholders as security/compliance risk (or “chance Redwood is wrong”), rather than “likelihood of latency/reliability regression if you apply the recommended batching/caching change.” They requested clearer, shareable language for governance review and change-management tickets.

Impact:

- Risk of miscommunication: stakeholders assume “High risk” implies security risk

Where this shows up:

- Details drawer “Risk” section copy
- Beta docs page (glossary / definitions)

Customer request:
1) Rename/clarify what “risk” means (performa...

comments.body: 18 (Dev Patel, Support) — Ticket created from CS escalation. Customer quote: “Our security review board flagged ‘High risk’ as a compliance concern. We need wording that clearly states this is performance risk only, and what we should monitor.”

02 (Miguel Santos, Customer Success) — Confirmed on call with Arkadia: They’re screenshotting the Suggestions page into internal approval docs. They want a one-liner definition + a second line with concrete guardrails (e.g., canary rollout, watch p95 + error rate). Asked if we can add a doc link directly in the tooltip/drawer.

10 (Marissa Cole, Product - Console) — Agree this is a priority for beta trust. Proposed copy direction: explicitly say “Performance & reliability risk” and add a tooltip line: “Not a security/compliance risk.” Also add “How we calculate risk” deep-link to docs. Looping in Design + Docs.

41 (Kenji Nakamura, Design) — Reviewed current tooltip. It only says “Likelihood of negative impact” which is too vague. Suggest: 1) Rename section header in drawer to “Performance risk” (if feasible without re-layout), 2) Tooltip: “Estimates likelihood of latency or error-rate regression when applying this config change. Not a security risk.” 3) Add examples in the drawer: “Why this is Medium: traffic is bursty; batching could increase p95.”

06 (Sana Farid, Risk/Confidence DS) — +1. Also recommend we avoid implying quality risk since beta scope is batching/caching. We can mention “reliability (timeouts/queueing)” and “latency (p95)” but not “model output quality.” If we mention quality at all, it should be phrased as “No expected output-quality change for batching/caching; monitor as usual.”

32 (Elliot Price, Docs) — Drafted docs additions: a short glossary entry and a ‘How to roll out safely’ section with concrete steps (canary, compare baseline, watch p95/p99 + 5xx/429 + timeouts). Will also add a note: “Risk labels are conservative and based on observed traffic patterns; not guarantees.”

20 (Nora Ahmed, Console UX Eng) — Implemented copy updates behind the existing Suggestions beta flag. Proposed UI changes: 
- Tooltip title: “Performance & reliability risk”
- Tooltip body: “How likely applying this suggestion is to increase latency or error rate for this route. Not a security/compliance risk.”
- Drawer: added “What to monitor” bullets (p95 latency, timeouts/5xx, queue depth if available).
Pending PM/Design final approval.

05 (Miguel Santos, Customer Success) — Shared the updated wording with Arkadia for review (screenshot + text). Customer responded that this resolves their governance confusion and asked for the docs link they can reference in change tickets.
48 (Dev Patel, Support) — Closing loop: Doc updates merged and Console copy queued for next beta patch rollout. Provided customer with a pasteable definition + rollout checklist (see Resolution).

title: Unexpected 5xx spike driven by tenant throttle, accept-queue growth and runtime worker flood
body:
Issue summary:
Customers observed a sharp rise in 5xx responses across multiple endpoints for a subset of tenants in us-east-1 starting ~2026-03-10T20:05Z. The pattern included high API Gateway accept-queue depth, connection keepalive churn, and repeated runtime worker restarts leading to upstream 502/503 errors.

Impact:
- Lumina Analytics (dedicated) reported increased API failures affecting interactive chat and short-streaming sessions (~30% failure rate for a 5-minute window).
- Broader, low-level 5xx floor visible for ~12 tenants sharing the same routing shard.

Environment:
- prod us-east-1
- Affected routing shard: shard-e-12
- Serving runtime version: 2026.02.11-hotfix

Observed timeline (UTC):
- 2026-03-10T20:03: service-level alarms start triggering on apigw accept-queue (>1500).
- 2026-03-10T20:05: Lumina support case created; we see elevated 5xx percentages at api layer.
-...

title: Contractual rate override not honored during private -> hosted fallback resulting in unexpected invoice line
body:
Issue summary:
Customer Kestrel Health reports that a contractual rate override (enterprise addendum ID: KH-2025-A) is not being applied when their private deployment falls back to hosted in eu-west. They were charged at standard hosted per-token rates for ~3 hours on 2026-03-09 during a private->hosted fallback window.

Impact:
- Unexpected invoice line of ~€18,564 for fallback-hosted usage on the March invoice.
- Affects revenue recognition and customer expectations (enterprise contract explicitly specifies a lowered hosted fallback rate for private customers).
- Customer-facing SLA and CSM escalation opened; finance has paused autopay pending resolution.

Environment:
- Customer: Kestrel Health (private deployment, enterprise tier)
- Private control plane: vpc-kestrel-eu (managed by Redwood Private)
- Fallback path: private -> hosted (eu-west) automatic routing when private pool un...

comments.author: Aisha Patel ()

title: Console: Rollback button not visible for on-call operator (RBAC permission denied / role mapping misconfigured)
body:
Issue summary
- On-call operator cannot see the "Rollback" button in the Console for Optimization Config versions.
- This blocks rollback during gameday / incident response and is confusing because the operator is in the expected on-call group.

Impact
- Internal: on-call cannot execute one-click rollback via Console (must escalate to Platform team or use break-glass).
- Risk: delayed mitigation if a bad optimization config causes latency/5xx regression.

Who is affected
- Internal users in Okta/SSO group: sre-oncall (expected to have Optimization Change Operator privileges).

Observed behavior
- UI: No "Rollback" CTA shown on version detail page.
- API: direct call to rollback endpoint returns 403.

Expected behavior
- Members of the on-call operator group should have permission to rollback optimization configs within approved scopes.

Error details
- Console network call response:
 ...

comments.body: 12 Sean Gallagher (reporter): During rollback gameday, on-call couldn’t find the Rollback button in Console. We expected sre-oncall to have operator permissions. Can someone from RBAC take a look? This is blocking the drill.

26 Chloe Martin: Confirmed rollback feature flag is ON for internal beta. UI hides CTA if console-backend says user lacks optimization_config.rollback. Can someone capture the failing API response headers? We can add extra logging if needed.

41 Aisha Bello: Repro’d on my account. POST to rollback endpoint returns 403 permission_denied missing optimization_config.rollback. I’m in sre-oncall group. Screenshot and request-id attached in internal thread (#eng-oncall).

05 Jordan Lee (assignee): Looking at RBAC bindings in prod now. Suspect the group mapping didn’t get applied with the last policy bundle publish. Will verify policy_version + effective role for Aisha.

32 Jordan Lee: Found missing binding: sre-oncall mapped only to optimization_config.viewer in prod. Also seeing policy sync drift in us-east (some pods on older policy). Working with SRE to force a sync and invalidate caches.
14 Jordan Lee: Applied binding update and forced RBAC sync in us-east. Restarted console-backend to clear cache. Aisha—can you re-test?
27 Aisha Bello: Re-test successful. Rollback button now appears and rollback request returns 200. Thanks. Suggest Console shows a warning instead of hiding CTA; was confusing during the drill.
40 Sean Gallagher: Closing. Please add the drift alert + runbook note; this is a sharp edge for Sev response.

title: Delayed initial token and sporadic request timeouts after runtime ABI + model variant handoff
body:
Issue summary:
Customer IteraChat reports elevated time-to-first-token (initial token emission stalls of ~1.2s median, spike to 6-8s p95) and intermittent request timeouts (client sees 504) for chat completions after our 2026-03-11 runtime v1.9 rollout and associated model variant swap. Problem appears during session warmup and immediately after model handoff to quantized kernel variants.

Impact:
- Affected: IteraChat's dedicated pool (capacity-reserved) serving ~120 qps per cluster of rw-instruct-7b-v2-quant.
- Customer-visible: delayed message appearance and occasional 504 for chat requests; user complaints and degraded UX for active enterprise users.
- Business: Dedicated-tier SLA risk and support escalation from account team.

Environment:
- Deployment: Dedicated clusters in us-east-1 using runtime v1.9 (rolled 2026-03-11 02:00 UTC).
- Models in use: rw-instruct-7b-v2-quant pinne...

title: Tenant-induced pipeline IO spill causing autoscaler latency and p99 throughput degradation in reserved pool
body: Issue summary: Multiple LexiHealth tenants on reserved capacity experienced sudden p99 throughput regressions and elevated tail latency beginning 2026-03-10 14:12 UTC. Symptoms include stalled batch queues, autoscaler taking 30-90s to react, and error bursts of 502/504 on long streaming requests. Impact: LexiHealth reported degraded user-facing response times for inference flows tied to clinical note summarization, causing timeouts and retries in their app. Timeline: 14:12 UTC spike in IO wait and NVMe device queue depth observed; autoscaler attempted scale-up but new allocations delayed due to slot fragmentation; 14:27 UTC temporary mitigation applied (tenant soft-rate-limit). Hypothesis: A noisy tenant generated high small-request fanout (many short streams) that induced storage-io pressure on the node local NVMe, which cascaded into serving runtime stalls and autoscaler misjudging ...

comments.author: Diego Martinez (SRE) 2026-03-10
comments.body: Observed NVMe queue spike on nodes hosting LexiHealth shards. Kicking off node-level io captures and sampling container io stats.

comments.author: Infra
comments.body: Identified a LexiHealth sidecar that writes frequent small checkpoints. Requesting customer to temporarily disable sidecar while we test scheduler affinity change.

comments.author: LexiHealth-SRE (customer) 2026-03-11
comments.body: Can disable sidecar for a 30-min window at 15:00 UTC. Asking for recommended mitigation until permanent fix.

comments.body: Applied temporary tenant soft-rate-limit (50% of baseline) and bumped reserved pool by +1 GPU as short-term mitigation. Monitoring.

comments.author: Ava Chen () 2026-03-13
comments.body: Customer confirmed reduced 5xx and p99 latency returned closer to baseline after mitigation. Planning follow-up for permanent remediation.

title: Request: documented rollback procedure for Redwood Private (on-prem / air-gapped) upgrades
body:
Customer (Northstar Health Systems) is planning an on-prem / air-gapped upgrade of Redwood Private and is requesting a clear, operator-friendly rollback procedure that can be shared internally for change control.

Request details (from customer):
- They need step-by-step rollback instructions suitable for an IT ops runbook, including what is and is not supported (e.g., Helm rollback vs restore-from-backup).
- They require clarity on expected downtime/RTO and what artifacts must be preserved (offline bundle, chart versions, container images, DB backups, PV snapshots).
- They have strict constraints:
 - Air-gapped environment (offline bundle only, no outbound registry/chart repo access).
 - Change windows are short (2 hours max) with a hard stop where they must revert.
 - Audit/compliance requires evidence of backups taken and a deterministic rollback path.

What they are upgrading:
- R...

normalized.description:
Customer (Northstar Health Systems) is planning an on-prem / air-gapped upgrade of Redwood Private and is requesting a clear, operator-friendly rollback procedure that can be shared internally for change control.

Request details (from customer):
- They need step-by-step rollback instructions suitable for an IT ops runbook, including what is and is not supported (e.g., Helm rollback vs restore-from-backup).
- They require clarity on expected downtime/RTO and what artifacts must be preserved (offline bundle, chart versions, container images, DB backups, PV snapshots).
- They have strict constraints:
 - Air-gapped environment (offline bundle only, no outbound registry/chart repo access).
 - Change windows are short (2 hours max) with a hard stop where they must revert.
 - Audit/compliance requires evidence of backups taken and a deterministic rollback path.

What they are upgrading:
- R...

comments.body: 14 PT — Dev Patel (Support) — Ticket created after customer asked for rollback guarantees and a written procedure for on-prem upgrades. Customer explicitly asked for something they can attach to their CAB/change request.

02 PT — Owen Phillips (Support Escalation SME) — Acknowledged. Key point to set expectations: today, rollback is supported via restore-from-backup taken immediately before upgrade (DB + config + PV snapshots), not via Helm rollback. Will draft customer-facing steps and validate with SRE/Release Eng.

21 PT — Rafael Mendes (SRE) — Confirming: Helm rollback is not considered safe once DB migrations run. If any migration step has executed, the only supported rollback is full restore (DB restore + PV snapshot restore as applicable) to the pre-upgrade point-in-time. Also recommend pausing data plane rollouts first to reduce blast radius.

37 PT — Connor O'Brien (Release Eng) — For air-gapped: ensure they retain the full pre-upgrade offline bundle (charts + images + manifests) and checksums. If they overwrite their mirror with new tags, rollback becomes hard. Recommend immutable, versioned directories in their artifact mirror (e.g., /redwood-private/2.9.3 and /redwood-private/2.10.0).

05 PT — Michael Nguyen (Migrations) — Add guidance: if upgrade fails during migration, do not attempt a "downgrade" by running older pods against a migrated DB. Restore the DB from the pre-upgrade backup. For Postgres, prefer pg_dump/pg_restore for logical backup (or a storage snapshot if their storage backend supports consistent snapshots).

18 PT — Owen Phillips (Support) — Sent customer a draft rollback procedure (below) and asked them to confirm their current versions (installer + chart + platform) and storage backend.

Draft rollback procedure (customer-facing, current GA):
A. Before upgrade (required to have a rollback path)
1) Ensure you have the full pre-upgrade artifact set stored offline:
 - The offline bundle for the currently running version (charts + images + manifests).
 - Checksums/signature file if provided.
2) Take backups immediately before running the upgrade:
 - Control plane database backup (Postgres logical dump) and verify you can restore it.
 - Snapshot ...

44 PT — Dev Patel (Support) — Customer replied: they use CSI-backed storage with snapshot support, and want a single PDF-like doc. They also asked: "Is there a way to do a rollback without restoring PVs, just DB?"

27 PT — Irene Choi (Infra) — Answer: depends on which PVs are used and whether any stateful components store upgrade-time state outside DB. Conservative guidance: assume PV restore is required unless we can confirm components are stateless or only cache. For model cache PVs: usually can be cleared/rebuilt, but we should not present that as universal without confirming their deployment topology.

11 PT — Owen Phillips (Support) — Responded to customer: DB-only rollback may be possible if PVs contain only rebuildable caches, but safest supported rollback remains DB restore + PV restore for stateful services. Asked them to provide which PVCs they have and what components are stateful in their install (control-plane DB PVC, object store, model cache).

03 PT — Kenji Nakamura (UX) — FYI: upcoming installer work will include a first-class rollback command that consumes stored upgrade state and runs deterministic hooks. Not GA yet; do not promise timelines to customer. Point them to the current runbook excerpt and we can update once GA.

41 PT — Owen Phillips (Support) — Final: Provided a cleaned-up rollback procedure (same content as draft, reworded as a runbook) and linked the relevant Redwood internal/external-aligned docs section for upgrades + troubleshooting. Marking as Resolved; follow-up task: convert to customer-facing doc snippet once docs PR lands.

title: Sustained mini-burst shadowing causing gradual GPU credit leakage and p99 throughput spikes
body: Issue summary: Customer LexiDocs on a dedicated pool is observing gradual throughput degradation at p99 over ~6 hour windows coincident with many short-lived inference streams. Impact: intermittent request spikes, elevated p99 latency (3-10x), occasional 429s and autoscaler failing to add capacity fast enough. Environment: production dedicated cluster in us-west-2 serving the redwood/gpt-4o-instruct-2.7b-quant4x variant. Observed since 2026-03-09 23:30 UTC and recurring daily during customer batch windows.

Impact: intermittent request spikes, elevated p99 latency (3-10x), occasional 429s and autoscaler failing to add capacity fast enough. Environment: production dedicated cluster in us-west-2 serving the redwood/gpt-4o-instruct-2.7b-quant4x variant. Observed since 2026-03-09 23:30 UTC and recurring daily during customer batch windows.

comments.author: Riley Nguyen (SRE)
comments.body: Acknowledged. Initial triage shows synchronized short-stream fanout from LexiDocs region causing prefetcher to queue; autoscaler saw rapid GPU credit usage and stayed in cooldown. Collecting allocator and prefetcher traces.

comments.author: Customer Success
comments.body: Customer confirms they have a new analytics job that spawns thousands of ephemeral streams during the midnight window; they can throttle but would prefer platform-side mitigation.

comments.author: Riley Nguyen (SRE)
comments.body: Applied temporary reserved burst (added 2x small GPU units) to LexiDocs dedicated pool to absorb immediate load spike. Observed p99 drop back toward baseline within 2 minutes.

comments.author: (serving-runtime)
comments.body: We have a follow-up PR to alter prefetch coordination and expose a per-tenant prefetch cap. Planning rollout to staging this week.

comments.author: LexiDocs Ops
comments.body: Appreciative of temporary reserve; asks for guidance on client-side pacing and whether Redwood can implement graceful retry rate-limiting for short streams.

title: Repeat-prompt latency spike (2-3x) after KV shard-mapping rollout; increased cold reads for prefix lookups
body: Customer reports 2-3x higher tail latencies for repeated prompt requests in long-running chat sessions starting after 2026-03-09 04:00 UTC. Symptoms match cold KV/prefix lookups: prefix cache hit-rate dropped sharply leading to synchronous remote reads and request timeouts (504) for a subset of requests. Impact: Several hundred requests/minute for the customer's production org experienced >1s extra latency and intermittent timeouts; affecting their chat product's UX. The customer is on an enterprise Dedicated plan and is running production traffic through our us-east cluster where a KV shard-mapping change was rolled out in the morning of 2026-03-09.

The customer is on an enterprise Dedicated plan and is running production traffic through our us-east cluster where a KV shard-mapping change was rolled out in the morning of 2026-03-09.

comments.author: Elena Park ()
comments.body: Initial triage done. Confirmed customer-facing latency and collected logs. Opening ticket and escalating to SRE/Serving.

comments.author: Mateo Alvarez (SRE)
comments.body: Observed 'collision=true' tag in kv-router for several keys. Rolling back PR-487 on canary reproduced baseline hit-rate. Investigating whether the mapping function is stable under rolling update.

comments.author: NimbleDocs contact, Priya Sharma
comments.body: Customer reporting intermittent 504s for agents in production; they want ETA for mitigation. Provided temporary mitigation: toggle to older shard map via flag 'kv-router.mapping=stable-v4'.

comments.author: Mateo Alvarez (SRE)
comments.body: Performed emergency rollback of kv-router mapping change in us-east at 2026-03-10 23:50 UTC. Early telemetry shows prefix hit-rate recovering to ~91% and remote-read QPS decreasing. Need to verify full roll-forward plan with fixes and tests.

comments.author: Lina Chen
comments.body: Confirmed race where write-ack path could be dropped during rolling updates (see INT-4021). Preparing a patch to make assignments idempotent and to add write-sync handshake before draining shards.

normalized.cleanBody: Customer reports 2-3x higher tail latencies for repeated prompt requests in long-running chat sessions starting after 2026-03-09 04:00 UTC. Symptoms match cold KV/prefix lookups: prefix cache hit-rate dropped sharply leading to synchronous remote reads and request timeouts (504) for a subset of requests. Impact: Several hundred requests/minute for the customer's production org experienced >1s extra latency and intermittent timeouts; affecting their chat product's UX. The customer is on an enterprise Dedicated plan and is running production traffic through our us-east cluster where a KV shard-mapping change was rolled out in the morning of 2026-03-09.

title: Reserved pool throughput degradation caused by GPU power-capping + fragmented micro-batching
body: Issue summary: Customer BeaconAI observed sustained p95/p99 latency regressions and intermittent 504 timeouts during peak windows on their dedicated reserved pool. Impact: Customer reports request timeouts and model responses taking 2x-4x expected time; production product is degrading for end users. Timeline: problem first reported 2026-03-10 08:10 UTC, support triaged and opened this ticket. Initial hypothesis included noisy-tenant and autoscaler undershoot. This ticket captures investigation showing a combination of node-level GPU power capping (thermal/clock throttling) combined with workload micro-batching fragmentation and a conservative autoscaler min_replicas setting.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Customer reports p99 > 1.8s and intermittent 504s during morning peak. Asked for request ids and sample traces.

comments.author: Rafael Ortega
comments.body: Pulled node telemetry and found sustained clock throttling on node-gpu-07. Suspect thermal throttling plus poor batching. Investigating autoscaler policy.

comments.author: Lan Nguyen - BeaconAI
comments.body: Confirmed a long-running streaming ingestion job started at 07:50 UTC. Can move it if helpful; prefer a fix that lets it coexist.

comments.author: Priya Desai
comments.body: Verified serving-runtime traces show batch fragmentation due to many small concurrent requests. Suggest enabling adaptive coalescing and increasing min_replicas for this pool as a stopgap.

comments.author: Rafael Ortega
comments.body: Applied emergency workarounds: node isolation label added, large stream job drained off node-gpu-07, min_replicas set to 3 for BeaconAI reserved pool. Monitoring metrics show p95 returned to within baseline after draining.

comments.author: Lan Nguyen
comments.body: Verified product SLA is back to expected performance. Thanks — awaiting permanent fixes.

comments.author: Priya Desai
comments.body: Created follow-up engineering task INT-4321 to address autoscaler tuning and scheduler improvements to avoid colocating long-seq streaming with latency-sensitive short-seq workloads.

title: Synchronous schema ping from function proxy adds blocking validation hop — p99 end-to-end latency spikes and 10s timeouts for dedicated customer
body:
Issue summary: Customer ScribeAI reports sudden increase in end-to-end request latency (p95/p99) for short chat completions after a recent rollout of a validation change in the function-proxy. Impact: Customer-facing streaming sessions experience long tails and periodic 10s request timeouts. This has escalated to degraded UX for interactive flows.

Observed behavior: During short prompts (<= 64 tokens) we observe p99 latency rising from ~220ms to ~2.1s and occasional 10s timeouts. Traces show an extra synchronous RPC from function-proxy to the schema-validator service during the tool/function call orchestration. The RPC performs a schema/contract validation and an indirect K/V metadata lookup which adds blocking I/O on the request path.

Expected behavior: Schema validation should be async or performed off the hot path (prefetch/validate-on-register) so that per-request latency remain...

comments.body: 45Z): Noted related incident INT-4112 — rolling restart of schema-validator in us-east coincided with increased DNS latencies. Investigating resolver config.

comments.author: (2026-03-13T18
comments.body: 42Z): Rollback applied for ScribeAI; monitoring metrics. Need follow-up EPIC to make validation non-blocking across all clusters.

title: Sustained APAC inference p99 spike: geo-proxy sticky-session fallback causing request queueing
body:
Issue summary:
Customer Brushwell Analytics reports sustained p99 inference latency increase (from ~350ms to 2.5–4s) and occasional request timeouts for requests routed to apac-east.

Impact:
- Production inference for multiple endpoints degraded for Brushwell (dedicated account).
- Client sees increased API latency and intermittent 504s; affects real-time scoring pipelines.

Environment:
- Redwood hosted, dedicated capacity for Brushwell in apac-east region.
- Observed across multiple model variants pinned to redwood-v2-medium.

Steps to reproduce (from customer):
1) Send streaming chat completion requests to customer key scoped to apac-east.
2) Observe p95/p99 token latencies and occasional streaming disconnects after 2–4s.
3) Repeatedly repros ~20% of requests during window between 2026-03-12T02:10Z and ongoing.

Observed logs / telemetry snippets:
- API gateway traces show request...

comments.body: 05 UTC — Ava Chen: Ticket created after customer report. Initial triage: looks like routing fallback in APAC; escalating to SRE and Edge team.

12 UTC — Diego Morales (SRE): Captured sample traces. Noticed sticky session cookie expiry causing immediate fallback to alternate proxy; observed TCP teardown leading to request retransmit and queueing on target GPU host.

30 UTC — Edge Team (comment): We deployed a config rollback of recent sticky-affinity change (PR 712) to a subset of apac proxies; early telemetry shows reduced immediate fallbacks but queue depth still elevated on gpu-pool-03.
20 UTC — Diego Morales: Investigating KV cache miss patterns; hypothesis: proxy fallback mixes origin headers leading to cache key partitioning and cold KV state, increasing per-request setup time.
10 UTC — Customer CS: Brushwell reports degradation mostly mitigated for live endpoints after temporary workaround; requesting postmortem and permanent remediation timeline.

title: Dedicated customer request: clearer cost reporting ($/1M output tokens) by model/region for Dedicated capacity
body:
Issue summary
AcmeAssist (Dedicated tier) is asking for clearer cost reporting for their Dedicated deployment. They want a breakdown that looks like: blended $/1M output tokens with drilldowns by model, model version/quantization profile (if applicable), and region. Their current view (monthly invoice + high-level usage exports) doesn’t help them attribute cost changes to model mix, output length, caching/batching efficiency, or region.

Customer impact
- Customer is trying to justify Dedicated expansion internally and needs a defensible breakdown of unit costs for QBR/finance review.
- Without a breakdown, they are attributing cost spikes to “platform inefficiency” and are asking for manual ad-hoc reports.
- Risk: renewal friction and increased support load from repeated billing/cost questions.

Customer request (what they asked for)
1) Dedicated cost metric aligned to tokens: blende...

Customer request (what they asked for)
1) Dedicated cost metric aligned to tokens: blende...

comments.body: 14 PT — Allison Grant (Reporter): Logged per CSM escalation. AcmeAssist asking for per-model + per-region cost reporting for Dedicated. They specifically asked for $/1M output tokens and monthly export. Routing to Product (Console) + Finance methodology for guidance on what we can share safely.

02 PT — Marissa Cole (Assignee): Acknowledged. This aligns with internal Unit Economics dashboard goals, but we are not ready to externalize the UI. Suggest we provide a curated export for this customer as a stopgap after confirming (a) blended vs marginal preference and (b) timezone/month boundaries. Please include caveats: blended includes idle allocation and uses finance rate card versioned for the period.

27 PT — Rishi Malhotra (FP&A): For any shared number: must cite rate card version (e.g., 2025Q4) and allocation policy (idle included; overhead treatment). Recommend we label the metric “Redwood blended cost” and avoid implying it matches invoice line items exactly. Reconciliation typically within a tolerance band depending on timing windows.

40 PT — Allison Grant: Replied to customer: we can provide a monthly export with blended unit cost and breakdown by model + region for their Dedicated usage, with definitions and caveats. Asked customer to confirm whether they want blended vs marginal and whether UTC month boundaries are acceptable.

18 PT — Customer (AcmeAssist): Thanks. We prefer blended (all-in) for internal planning. UTC month boundaries are fine. Need a QTD view and last full month, with model breakdown and us-east vs eu-west split. CSV export works.

06 PT — Allison Grant: Internal note: We can produce a CSV from internal aggregates for Feb QTD + Jan full month; include columns: date (day), model_id (normalized), region (canonical), output_tokens, blended_cost_usd, blended_usd_per_1m_output_tokens. Exclude any customer-identifying top-N beyond their own account. Ensure we do not disclose internal GPU rate-card line items; provide only the derived metric + methodology summary.

30 PT — Marissa Cole: Please add explicit disclaimer to the export: (1) metric is for cost transparency/diagnostics, (2) not a billing statement, (3) subject to small adjustments due to late-arriving telemetry/backfills, (4) rate card version pinned for the report period. Also note that quantization profile drilldown is not reliably available for all models yet; keep it out unless coverage is >99%.

05 PT — Allison Grant: Sent AcmeAssist the Jan + Feb QTD CSV export via CSM along with a 1-page methodology note and definitions (blended $/1M output tokens, included costs, idle treatment). Offered to repeat monthly for next 2 cycles while we evaluate a productized solution.
22 PT — Customer (AcmeAssist): Received, this is what we needed. The blended vs invoice note is helpful. Please plan to send this monthly; we’ll revisit if/when it becomes available in Console.

title: Intermittent 403s after group membership updates due to entitlement propagation lag in Private VPC
body:
Issue summary: Customer reports intermittent 403 Forbidden responses from Redwood Private endpoints for certain users immediately after SCIM-driven group membership changes. The failures resolve after ~30–90 seconds.

Impact: Affects ~5% of authentication attempts for affected users immediately after admin changes; blocks customer admins from testing role changes and causes automated workflows to fail briefly.

Environment: Private deployment in customer's VPC (prod-us-east). IdP: Okta (SAML), SCIM provisioning enabled from Okta -> Redwood SCIM endpoint. RBAC model uses group->role bindings.

Steps to reproduce:
1) In Okta, add a user to the 'data-scientists' group which maps to 'model-infer' role in Redwood.
2) Wait until SCIM push completes (Okta shows success).
3) Immediately have user call the hosted API via Private endpoint. Observe 403 for first few calls.
4) Repeat: after ~30–9...

comments.author: Jay Patel
comments.body: 'We see users denied immediately after we add them to groups. Waiting a minute always fixes it.'

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: 'Thanks — can you confirm whether users refresh tokens or reuse existing sessions when testing? Also, can you provide the Okta SCIM event ids for a failed attempt?'

comments.author: Jay Patel
comments.body: 'We re-used existing sessions. Here's one SCIM event id: 8a7f3b.'

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: 'We observed entitlement-index lag spikes correlated with bursty SCIM writes. Reproduced locally with load test hitting SCIM 200r/s — indexer queued events and processed in batch with delay.'

comments.author: Priya Raman
comments.body: 'Dashboard shows indexer CPU saturation during customer's daily user provisioning window. Queue depth reached 18 for ~3 minutes.'

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: 'We will schedule a maintenance window to deploy a hotfix that reduces index batching window. ETA for rollout: 2026-03-02.'

title: OnyxBank: multipath retry amplification causing duplicate completions, latency spike, and invoice exposure — post-incident action plan
body:
Issue summary:
On 2026-03-10 14:12 UTC a subset of OnyxBank traffic routed through the realtime-router experienced a multipath retry amplification. Requests that normally fan out to 1-2 serving backends were duplicated across 6-10 backends due to stale routing metadata and an aggressive retry policy. Symptoms included elevated tail latency (p95 -> 18s), a surge of duplicate completions returned to the customer, streaming disconnects for long-running sessions, and a notable invoice delta reported by OnyxBank.

Impact:
- Customer-facing failures: intermittent streaming truncation and duplicate completions for 12% of OnyxBank requests between 14:12–15:25 UTC.
- Latency: sustained p95 increase from 400ms to 18s during peak amplification.
- Billing: OnyxBank reported a 3.7x unexpected token/billing delta for the affected window.
- Business: Enterprise SLA exposure and escalated executive c...

16 UTC — Priya Kapoor (Support): Acknowledged — triaging now, opening PagerDuty and tagging routing and billing teams.

22 UTC — Marco Ruiz (Routing ENG): Observed duplicated request IDs being emitted from router nodes; suspect control-plane metadata race. Rolling mitigation: soft-rate-limit and disable retry reset for this route.
40 UTC — Samira Patel (Router ENG): PR #2834 rollback queued and validated in canary. Hotfix for cache TTL prepared for staging.
45 UTC — Priya Kapoor (Support): Customer call completed; OnyxBank accepted provisional credit and asked for timeline on permanent fixes. Scheduled follow-up with ENG owners.
12 UTC — Kevin Osei (SRE): Added canary traffic simulation task to backlog and created INT-1578 to track automation work.
00 UTC — Marco Ruiz (Routing ENG): Post-deploy verification complete. No reoccurrence in 72h. Closing incident and tracking follow-ups in engineering tickets.

title: Horizon Health: long-streaming-hangs and retry storm during quantized model rollout causing 5xx and billing spike
body: Issue summary: On 2026-03-10 02:12 UTC Horizon Health reported repeated client-side streaming hangs and timeouts for long-completion requests (>2k tokens) to their dedicated Redwood pool in us-east. Symptoms included stalled streaming frames, abrupt client disconnects, and an influx of retries that produced elevated 5xx rates and an unexpected billing surge. Impact: Affected production ML features for Horizon Health's triage assistant during a peak processing window; multiple customer endpoints returned partial responses or duplicate completions. Environment: dedicated prod-us-east pool, tenant horizon-health, rollout of quantized variant 'gptq-llama-70b-q4_0-v2' to reduce cost. title: Dedicated: burst capacity not activating during traffic spikes (Northstar Payments, us-east)
body:
Issue summary
- Customer reports Dedicated burst capacity did not activate during predictable traffic spikes. Throughput capped at baseline replicas, causing queueing and elevated p95/p99 latency.
- Customer expectation: burst should add temporary replicas within 2–5 minutes when utilization/queue depth crosses threshold.

Impact
- 2026-02-12 17:05–18:20 UTC: sustained elevated latency and intermittent 5xx from upstream timeouts due to request queue buildup.
- Customer-visible symptoms:
 - Latency increase: p95 from ~850ms to ~4.8s (chat), p99 up to ~9–12s.
 - RPS capped at baseline; queue depth increased and stayed elevated.
 - No visible scale-out events in their Console view.

Customer / Tier
- Northstar Payments (Dedicated)

Environment
- prod, us-east
- Dedicated pool: northstar-prod-gpu-pool (A100-80GB)

What we expected
- Burst capacity should temporarily scale above reserved b...

comments.author: Owen Phillips
comments.body: Customer escalation via CSM. Burst expected to kick in for top-of-hour spike; replicas stayed at 12 and queue depth climbed. Creating bridge per playbook; paging SRE on-call with request IDs + pool name.

comments.author: Rafael Mendes, SRE
comments.body: Looking at autoscaler events now. I see triggers met but scale-out blocked; suspect max cap. Pulling current policy + scheduler logs. Will update in 30m.

comments.author: Rafael Mendes, SRE
comments.body: Confirmed: burst_max_replicas set to 20 but pool max is still effectively 12 due to legacy schema path. Applying override to raise cap and manually scaling to 16 (capacity constraints in cell).

comments.author: Priya Natarajan, Platform
comments.body: +1 on schema mismatch. This tenant created before burst policy v2 rollout; we need validation/upgrade path. Please link to ENG-4829 for guardrail + clear UI/console messaging.

comments.author: Rachel Kim, CS
comments.body: Customer asked whether this counts as SLA breach. I responded that we’re actively mitigating and will follow up after stability; please keep timeline + comms in ticket for potential credits review if requested.

comments.author: Rafael Mendes, SRE
comments.body: Capacity note: us-east A100 saturation is real blocker for full burst. We reserved additional headroom in adjacent cell and adjusted placement. Watching next scheduled spike to confirm burst reaches 18.

comments.author: Owen Phillips
comments.body: Customer confirms improved behavior at peak; replicas scaled to 17 within ~4 minutes and latency stayed <2s p95. Leaving ticket open for 1 more spike window before resolving.

comments.author: Rafael Mendes, SRE
comments.body: Observed two additional spikes; burst activation successful and no 'scale_out_blocked' events. Marking resolved. Follow-up remains with ENG-4829 + capacity planning action.

title: SDK exponential backoff jitter misconfiguration causing serialized retries and apparent inference timeouts for bulk requests
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports a spike in end-to-end inference latency and client-side timeouts when sending large batched inference requests (bulk job of ~25 concurrent requests per client). The SDK appears to serialize retries and produces long head-of-line delays rather than distributing retry attempts across healthy connections.

Impact:
- Several customers on Dedicated capacity experienced perceived timeouts and increased median latency for batched/parallel inference.
- Client-side metrics show heavy retry activity and a single active TCP connection per SDK process during peak bursts.
- Affects production workloads for PromptCraft (dedicated allocation).

Environment:
- Redwood Dedicated (prod us-east-1)
- Customer SDK: redwood-sdk-js v2.3.0 (custom config)
- Load pattern: ~25 parallel connections from a single process batching 16 prompts per request

Observed behavior:
- Burst ...

title: Sidecar connection thundering causing reserved-pool slot latch & transient throughput collapse
body:
Issue summary: Between 2026-03-10 12:07 and 12:35 UTC, a customer (DataMuse - dedicated pool) observed a sharp drop in sustained throughput and elevated p99 latency on batched generation routes. Impact: multiple short-lived requests from many clients produced a sidecar connection storm which led to transient GPU slot reservations ('slot latch') and prefetch starvation for batched streams; customer reported 40-60% reduction in tokens/sec for 20 minutes. Environment: production reserved pool in us-east; Dedicated capacity cluster: rp-us-east-07; Serving runtime v2.8.1, sidecar-proxy v1.5.3, scheduler v0.9.4.

Steps to reproduce (observed):
1) Customer traffic pattern: thousands of very short streams (median duration ~150ms) originating from a fanout of ~200 client endpoints hitting the same model route.
2) Sidecar proxies opened and closed connections rapidly; many connections reset mid...

comments.body: 27 UTC - Support (Aisha): Customer submitted case with screenshots of Console p99 spike and sample logs. Marked P1 due to dedicated pool impact.

45 UTC - SRE (Mateo): Initial triage — confirmed sidecar connection churn correlated with slot_hold_count rise. Hypothesis: sidecar connection resets left slots in a transient HELD_BY_SIDECAR state until lease expiry, preventing prefetch attach and reducing batching efficiency.
05 UTC - Customer (DataMuse): Confirmed they had recently deployed a client change that multiplexes many tiny requests rather than batching. Willing to roll back if necessary.
22 UTC - SRE (Mateo): Applied mitigation #1: reduced sidecar connection timeout and increased socket backlog to reduce reset storms. Observed partial recovery in throughput (tokens/sec +25%).

48 UTC - SRE (Mateo): Applied mitigation #2: temporarily lowered autoscaler grow threshold for this reserved pool (allow faster reacts to queue spikes). Throughput returned to baseline by 12:55-13:00 UTC.
15 UTC - Engineering (Priya): Patch merged to sidecar to coalesce rapid connects into a short windowed-attach operation (PR #412). Backport scheduled to v1.5.x.
30 UTC - Customer (DataMuse): Confirmed good recovery and accepted compensating configuration changes; requested post-incident notes and preventive recommendations.

title: Request: guidance on break-glass override for offline bundle signature verification (air-gapped Redwood Private install)
body:
Issue summary
GovLab Research is preparing an air-gapped Redwood Private upgrade. Their internal security team requested a formal, step-by-step process for using the installer “break-glass” override if signature verification blocks the install, including:
- Who at Redwood must approve (and what evidence we provide)
- What the customer must record locally (commands/logs/audit events)
- How to remediate after the emergency install to return to normal verification

Context
- Customer is in a strict air-gapped environment with controlled media transfer and change-management gates.
- They anticipate possible verification failure due to: time skew, trust store not yet updated after key rotation, or media transfer corruption.
- They do NOT want a generic ‘use --insecure’ instruction; they need a documented, auditable exception path.

Customer request
1) Provide the official Redwood approval ...

comments.body: 14 PT — Dev Patel (Reporter): GovLab Research asking for documented break-glass process for air-gapped upgrades. They’re not currently failing verification; they want the procedure approved by security/GRC before their change window. Assigning to Owen for response + aligning with Security (Marcus) and GRC (Olga).

02 PT — Owen Phillips (Assignee): Acknowledged. I’ll draft customer-facing steps + internal approval matrix. Will confirm minimum installer version that supports auditable break-glass and what audit events we expect to see locally.

26 PT — Marcus Lin (Security Eng): For guidance: emphasize we fail closed by default; break-glass is only for time-bound exception with explicit audit marker. Require customer to capture verify output and reason codes. If failure reason is “unknown key”, they must update trust store ASAP; break-glass should not be used as a long-term workaround for key rotation.

10 PT — Olga Petrov (GRC): Add: customer should retain local logs for their standard change-management retention period; Redwood will provide a short exception memo stating scope (bundle build id/version/hash), reason, and follow-up actions. For repeated usage, route to Aisha for exec-level review.

18 PT — Owen Phillips: Sent GovLab the ‘Break-glass override procedure’ draft via email and attached it to the ticket. Included (1) decision tree before break-glass, (2) Redwood approval contacts and required sign-offs, (3) exact artifacts/logs to collect, (4) follow-up steps to restore normal verification. Also included note that they must be on the minimum installer version that emits the break-glass audit event and includes verification status in the support bundle.

44 PT — Colin O'Donnell (Solutions Eng): Adding that GovLab uses offline Windows jump hosts for media prep; we should remind them to avoid extraction tools that can alter file contents/line endings. Referenced our prior guidance from SUP-31892 (hash mismatch after transfer).

07 PT — Owen Phillips: Customer confirmed receipt and that the approval matrix/log requirements meet their internal security review checklist. No break-glass executed yet. Closing as resolved; if they hit an actual verification failure during the window, we’ll open a new incident-style ticket with logs attached.

title: Unexpected throughput degradation during burst scale-up in dedicated pool; suspected noisy-tenant interference
body:
Issue summary: Customer reports a sharp and repeatable drop in throughput for a dedicated GPU pool during short burst traffic. Impact: ~60% throughput reduction and P95 token latency increases from ~140ms to ~1.4s for their production routes. Environment: dedicated pool (customer-dedicated-pool-45) in prod us-east-1 serving pinned models orca-1.3-large and llama-2-13b-instruct.

Observed behavior: During an autoscale event triggered by a traffic spike, new GPU workers are launched but many requests experience long queuing and reduced batching; some requests are failing with 502/timeout during model load. We see GPU allocation churn (rapid attach/detach), and a small subset of tenants in the pool show sustained >90% single-GPU kernel utilization while other workers sit underutilized. Expected behavior: scale-up should increase available capacity; per-request latency should remain near ...

comments.body: 'We see the spike exactly after your pool scales up. Prior to the spike the p95 is fine. This started after we increased concurrent sessions last week.'

comments.author: Ava Chen ()
comments.body: 'Thanks Marcus — we are triaging. Can you provide a handful of request IDs that experienced 502s and the exact window (UTC)? Also confirming this is dedicated pool customer-dedicated-pool-45.'

comments.author: Diego Martinez (SRE)
comments.body: 'Attached scheduler logs and initial metric snapshots to the issue. Noting repeated gpu_attach_timeout events during the window. Kicking off synthetic reproduction in staging.'

comments.author: Runtime
comments.body: 'We see heavy KV cache misses during model loads. Suggest staggered pod startup (10s jitter) and look at pre-warm hooks for shared layers.'

comments.author: Diego Martinez (SRE)
comments.body: 'Applied autoscaler guardrail to limit parallel launches for this pool (infra PR 2231). Monitoring shows improvement but not full regression to baseline.'

comments.author: Marcus Li (SaaSCo)
comments.body: 'Improvement noted — fewer user errors today. Please keep us posted on long-term fix.'

comments.author: Autoscaler
comments.body: 'Root-cause work accepted. Plan: implement launch pacing and smarter packer heuristic to avoid fragmentation; target patch ETA 2026-03-20.'

title: Transient throughput degradation from scheduler 'slice shift' during peaky rebalance causing short-lived GPU shortages
body:
Issue summary:
During a short peak window on 2026-03-12 (11:03–11:12 UTC) NexaAnalytics observed periodic P95/P99 throughput regressions and request queueing on their dedicated pool. Root cause appears correlated with a scheduler 'slice shift' event: the scheduler rebalanced service slices across GPU hosts to accommodate ephemeral capacity signals, and during the handoff a set of short-lived allocation hold times caused transient GPU shortages for the customer's slices.

Impact:
- Customer-facing latency increased: p95 increased ~2.7x, p99 spiked up to 8s for batched routes.
- ~18% of requests experienced queuing / delayed start; no 5xx errors were reported.
- Affected a single dedicated cluster for NexaAnalytics in us-west-2; we estimate ~6 minutes of visible customer impact across repeated micro-windows.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: nexaanalytics-prod-dedicated-3
- Region: us-wes...

36 UTC - Marco Li (SRE): Observed slice-rebalance events coincident with p99 spikes. Initial repro attempt successful in staging when injecting NVMe I/O. Marking as Sev1 and escalating to infra team.
10 UTC - Priya Natarajan (Infra): Identified snapshotter schedule overlap on gpu-78; will throttle and coordinate changes. Planning follow-up tests in canary cluster tonight.
22 UTC - NexaAnalytics SRE (customer): Confirmed temporary mitigation reduced visible spikes in their readiness tests. Asked for ETA on permanent guardrail.
12 UTC - Marco Li (SRE): Guardrail PR opened: github.com/redwood/realtime-scheduler/pull/842; requesting review from infra and scheduler owners.

normalized.cleanBody:
Issue summary:
During a short peak window on 2026-03-12 (11:03–11:12 UTC) NexaAnalytics observed periodic P95/P99 throughput regressions and request queueing on their dedicated pool. Root cause appears correlated with a scheduler 'slice shift' event: the scheduler rebalanced service slices across GPU hosts to accommodate ephemeral capacity signals, and during the handoff a set of short-lived allocation hold times caused transient GPU shortages for the customer's slices.

Impact:
- Customer-facing latency increased: p95 increased ~2.7x, p99 spiked up to 8s for batched routes.
- ~18% of requests experienced queuing / delayed start; no 5xx errors were reported.
- Affected a single dedicated cluster for NexaAnalytics in us-west-2; we estimate ~6 minutes of visible customer impact across repeated micro-windows.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: nexaanalytics-prod-dedicated-3
- Region: us-wes...

title: Ephemeral allocator latency from sparse tenant spinups causing transient P99 throughput regression
body:
Issue summary:
Customer Acme Cloud observed a sudden and repeatable p99 throughput regression on their dedicated pool during midday windows. The dips correlate with waves of short-lived tenant spinups (many 5–30s streams) after a marketing campaign. Impact: sustained ~30% drop in batch throughput and elevated queueing for ~3-6 minutes per wave.

Impact:
- Customer-facing inference latency spikes and degraded SLAs for batch-heavy endpoints
- Autoscaler temporarily failed to instrument capacity (delayed upscales) leading to request queueing and budgeted fallback model routing

Environment:
- Dedicated pool (Acme Cloud reserve), prod, us-west-2
- Mix of models (13B, 7B) with batching enabled and prefix-KV cache warmups

Observation summary:
Metrics show short bursts of many short streams that cause the allocator to take ~200-700ms stalls when assigning slots; these stalls accumulate, cau...

comments.author: Aisha Patel () - 2026-03-13
comments.body: Customer reported first occurrence at 11:30 PDT after email campaign. They are seeing p99 increases and multiple model fallbacks. Asking for urgent triage.

comments.author: Riley Chen (SRE) - 2026-03-13
comments.body: Triage started. Running synthetic load to reproduce; initial signs point to allocator RPC queuing. Putting alloc pods into debug mode to capture stacks.

comments.author: Infra
comments.body: Confirmed high mutex wait times in allocator at peak. Recommending temporary scale of allocator replicas as mitigation while we prepare a patch.

comments.author: Riley Chen (SRE) - 2026-03-13
comments.body: Scaled allocator replicas 3->6 in prod dedicated pool for Acme (change id: infra/scale/ACL-881). Observed throughput recovery within two waves. Customer notified of workaround.

comments.author: Allocator Owner
comments.body: Root cause narrowed to prefetch cancel path holding global slot-index lock. Preparing PR to replace coarse lock with per-shard lock; will run canary on staging.

comments.author: Aisha Patel () - 2026-03-14
comments.body: Customer acknowledges temporary improvement but requests ETA on permanent fix and asks for retro on how to avoid future recurrence during campaigns.

title: Streaming chat connection goes silent mid-response during live model swap on Dedicated pool
body: Issue summary: Customer reports SSE chat sessions receiving an initial stream of tokens and then entering a prolonged silent period (~30-90s) without socket close or error. Client eventually times out. Impact: High — production traffic for Nimbus Finance experienced multiple user-facing interruptions for multi-turn assistant sessions during peak business hours. Environment: Dedicated pool (reserved capacity) for Nimbus Finance in us-west-2 running quantized llama-2-13b model variant. Observed at ~2026-03-12 15:04 PDT during a staged live model swap triggered by platform rollout automation.

34 PDT - Ava Chen (SRE): Confirmed replication on our staging dedicated cluster by triggering a canary swap. Observed identical silence window during handoff. Creating hotfix for routing to avoid handoff mid-stream.

47 PDT - Diego Morales (Support): Temp mitigation applied, notified customer of rollback and asked for verification. Customer acknowledged partial recovery for new sessions; some users still saw timeouts for sessions that started during the event.

10 PDT - Nimbus Support Contact (Elena K.): Thanks — recovery visible for new chats, but multi-turn threads started during the window were impacted and a small percentage of messages lost. Requesting SLA credit assessment if full session loss confirmed.
02 PDT - Ava Chen (SRE): Filing INT-5567 to track scheduler/preload changes and PR linked. Will schedule postmortem; ETA for permanent fix 2 weeks pending prioritization.

title: Staged pin handshake timeout exposes legacy variant to subset of tenant traffic
body:
Issue summary:
Customer AtlasFintech reported that during a staged model version pin rollout for redwood-open-7b-v2 the control plane handshake timeout occasionally allowed the older (legacy) model variant to serve a fraction of requests for a pinned cohort.

Impact:
- Approximately 0.4% of requests from the pinned tenant cohort were routed to the legacy variant for ~30s windows during the staged rollout on 2026-03-10.
- Reports include subtle semantic changes in output that break downstream QA gates for the customer.
- No increased error rate or 5xxs observed; this is a correctness/consistency regression.

Environment:
- Prod (us-east)
- Hosted API (customer using Redwood hosted dedicated-like pin via tenant-level config)
- Model: redwood-open-7b-v2 pinned to cohort A (pin strategy: staged 10% -> 50% -> 100%)

Steps to reproduce (observed pattern):
1) Start a staged pin rollout targe...

comments.author: Aisha Patel ()
comments.body: Customer reported mixed outputs during 10% staged pin; attached sample traces and timestamps. Urgent — impacts production QA for customer flows.

comments.author: Riley Chen
comments.body: Acknowledged. Reproducing in lab with similar staged pin config. Escalating to SRE to check control-plane handshake latencies.

comments.author: Mateo Alvarez
comments.body: Initial findings: control-plane had a brief GC spike at 10:09 UTC; observed handshake latency spikes ~1.1-1.4s. Edge timeout currently set to 1.0s. Pushing a temporary increase to 2.0s in us-east for monitoring.

comments.author: Priya Rao
comments.body: Small change proposed to make fallback selection prefer last-known pinned variant when handshake fails. PR opened: https://github.com/redwood-inference/serving-runtime/pull/1789. Adding feature flag for gradual rollout.

comments.author: Riley Chen
comments.body: Applied tenant-level short-circuit fallback for AtlasFintech to prefer pin-preserving behavior while patch is staged. Customer confirmed reduction in mixed outputs after the change.

comments.author: Priya Rao
comments.body: Runtime patch merged to canary. Instrumentation added to edge to log fallback_reason and token_namespace on every routing decision. Deploying canary to 5% of edge hosts in us-east.

comments.author: Mateo Alvarez
comments.body: Monitoring shows handshake latency back within SLO on increased timeout; no new mixed-variant reports from the customer during the canary window so far. Will keep ticket open until 72h verification complete.

comments.author: Riley Chen
comments.body: Customer notified of ongoing canary and mitigation; scheduling follow-up on 2026-03-15 to confirm closure if verification passes.

title: Hosted API: unexpected 403 Forbidden on API key rotation endpoint after RBAC v2 rollout (deny-by-default)
body:
Customer reports that rotating API keys via Hosted API returns 403 Forbidden. This started after their org was enabled for RBAC v2 (feature flag) and coincides with new gateway authz middleware behavior (deny-by-default).

Impact:
- Customer security team cannot rotate keys as part of their credential hygiene checks.
- Blocks their pilot readiness checklist and internal security review timeline.

Customer report (Ironbank Financial):
- Endpoint: POST /v1/api-keys/{key_id}/rotate
- Response: 403 Forbidden
- Behavior: Happens consistently for their Admin user/token; creating keys still works via Console, but rotation fails via API.

Initial hypothesis:
- Route missing/mis-mapped permission annotation OR legacy RBAC v1 -> v2 mapping does not grant rotate permission for their existing built-in role binding.
- Gateway is enforcing deny-by-default and returning 403 where previously service ...

normalized.description:
Customer reports that rotating API keys via Hosted API returns 403 Forbidden. This started after their org was enabled for RBAC v2 (feature flag) and coincides with new gateway authz middleware behavior (deny-by-default).

Impact:
- Customer security team cannot rotate keys as part of their credential hygiene checks.
- Blocks their pilot readiness checklist and internal security review timeline.

Customer report (Ironbank Financial):
- Endpoint: POST /v1/api-keys/{key_id}/rotate
- Response: 403 Forbidden
- Behavior: Happens consistently for their Admin user/token; creating keys still works via Console, but rotation fails via API.

Initial hypothesis:
- Route missing/mis-mapped permission annotation OR legacy RBAC v1 -> v2 mapping does not grant rotate permission for their existing built-in role binding.
- Gateway is enforcing deny-by-default and returning 403 where previously service ...

comments.body: 18 PT (Colin O'Donnell): Customer reports 403 on key rotation endpoint. They are in Hosted API prod (us-east). They can create/list keys but rotation is blocked. Asking for request IDs + exact endpoint/path.

02 PT (Customer via Colin): Repro is stable. Example curl:
POST https://api.redwoodinference.com/v1/api-keys/ak_01HRR.../rotate
Authorization: Bearer <admin token>
Returns 403 {"error":{"type":"permission_denied","message":"Not authorized"}}.
Request IDs: rwreq_7f3c2a9b8c, rwreq_7f3c2aa11d (around 2025-03-21 09:54 PT).

25 PT (Amara Diallo): Pulled gateway logs for rwreq_7f3c2a9b8c. Denial is coming from gateway middleware (not upstream service). Denial reason code: MISSING_PERMISSION. Required permission recorded as rbac.api_keys.rotate (exact string in log). User has org role binding that includes rbac.api_keys.read + rbac.api_keys.create but not rotate.

31 PT (Kaitlyn Nguyen): This looks like a gap in the default role template / v1->v2 mapping for legacy “Org Admin” for Hosted. Rotate permission was added later in the permission registry and didn’t get included in the legacy mapping table. Suggest: patch policy bundle mapping + confirm endpoint annotation is correct per API standards.

05 PT (Amara Diallo): Confirmed endpoint annotation is correct and matches authorization-check-patterns (gateway enforced). The rotate route was recently added to sensitive coverage and is now enforced by deny-by-default. The missing piece is default permissions for role template + migration mapping.

26 PT (Naomi Feldman): For enterprise pilots we should treat key rotation as critical. Please prioritize a safe fix that does not broaden permissions unintentionally. Also ensure denial reason is visible for org admins via debug header if they enable it.

10 PT (Amara Diallo): Workaround for customer while fix rolls out: create a new API key via Console (or POST /v1/api-keys create) and revoke old key; rotation is blocked until permissions updated. If they require rotation semantics, we can temporarily grant rbac.api_keys.rotate via a custom role binding (Support-assisted) for their org.

42 PT (Amara Diallo): Fix prepared: update role template for Hosted “Org Admin” to include rbac.api_keys.rotate and update v1->v2 migration mapping table so existing org_admin bindings inherit rotate. Added regression test to ensure rotate permission is included for org_admin. Rolling out behind RBAC policy bundle version bump. Tracking via RBAC v2 rollout dashboard deny spikes.

05 PT (Colin O'Donnell): Informed customer of workaround + ETA for fix (next business day rollout). Customer requested confirmation once effective; they are running compliance tests for their SOC-aligned checklist.

30 PT (Amara Diallo): Deployed policy bundle v2025.03.24-1 to Hosted control plane + gateway (us-east). Verified with internal test org in prod: org_admin can now rotate keys; read-only role still denied. Deny-by-default preserved for other sensitive routes.
15 PT (Colin O'Donnell): Customer confirmed rotation now works (same endpoint) and 403 is resolved. Closing ticket.

title: Long-tail prompt length burst causes KV-cache fragmentation, runtime OOMs and elevated 5xx (apigw 503)
body:
Issue summary:
Customer CurrentsAI reported a sudden spike of 503/5xx responses starting 2026-03-10 02:10 UTC for a subset of their requests. Error rate peaked ~18% for the tenant for ~45 minutes.

Impact:
- Tenant-facing latency increased and many streaming sessions were terminated with 503.
- Several serving-runtime pods experienced OOM kills and restarted; autoscaler reacted by adding nodes which increased churn.
- Customer reports multiple failed inference calls and degraded UX for production users.

Timeline (high-level):
- 02:08 UTC: tenant traffic shows a shift: 90th/99th prompt length jumped from ~400 tokens to ~6k tokens (long-tail burst from batch import job).
- 02:10 UTC: API Gateway backlog increases, 503s observed and routed to support alerts.
- 02:15 - 02:40 UTC: multiple runtime pods report GPU memory fragmentation and OOM; KV cache evictions spike.
- 02:45 UTC: mitigat...

comments.body: 25 UTC - Maya (Support): Notified customer of mitigation (concurrency cap). Asked for replay artifacts and timestamps.
33 UTC - SRE (Liam O'Neill): Observed rapid eviction pattern in KV cache and multiple pods reporting small alloc failures then full OOM. Opened a tracing span to capture per-request token lengths.

38 UTC - Runtime Eng (Ravi Patel): Hypothesis: prefetch_buffer * max_seq_len combination with long-tail prompts triggers memory fragmentation. Suggest reducing prefetch multiplier and adding min-eviction-age to KV cache.
50 UTC - Customer (CurrentsAI - ops@currents.ai): Confirmed we started a bulk import at 02:05 UTC; can provide manifests. Asking for whether we should retry imports now or wait for fix.
12 UTC - Ravi Patel: Pushed a hotfix (canary) to cap per-request prefetch to 2048 tokens and reduce background compaction frequency. Monitoring telemetry.
00 UTC - Maya (Support): Customer confirmed retry succeeded with capped settings and no 503s observed post-fix.

title: Provision break-glass, time-bound audit access for KMS rotation evidence collection (Security/GRC)
body:
Request type: Break-glass audit access (time-bound)

Requester: Daniel Carter (Security & Compliance / Evidence)
Business justification:
- Need to collect/verify rotation evidence for upcoming audits and customer security reviews.
- Evidence requires exporting and validating rotation-related audit events (key version updates, cutover toggles), plus confirming the exporter schema includes new KMS rotation event types.

Scope of access requested (read-only):
1) Audit log export admin console (read-only) to run/export queries for a defined window.
2) Production audit-log-exporter job logs (read-only) for validation/troubleshooting.
3) Control-plane audit events index/search (read-only) for rotation event verification.

Out of scope / explicitly NOT requested:
- No ability to modify KMS keys, key policies, or rotation flags.
- No write permissions to audit event streams or exporter config...

comments.body: 18 PT — Daniel Carter (Reporter): Creating this as an internal helpdesk request. We need temporary audit access to validate/export the new KMS rotation audit events for evidence packs (SOC2 narrative + enterprise review). Access must be read-only and time-bound. See the approvals section in the description; I can attach screenshots of the email approvals if needed.

02 PT — Olga Petrov (Compliance/evidence): Confirming this is required for the evidence artifact. We need to show (a) rotation events exist, (b) exporter can include them, (c) we can produce a time-scoped export with operator identity. Read-only is sufficient.

26 PT — Marcus Lin (Security Eng): Approved, provided: (1) separate break-glass role, (2) expires automatically within 7 days, (3) audit logging enabled for role assumption, (4) no write privileges anywhere. Please add a note in the ticket once provisioned with the exact policy scopes.

48 PT — Dr. Aisha Rahman (Security exec sponsor): Approved for a 1-week window only. Ensure the role is not reusable without re-approval. If additional time is needed, open a new ticket with justification.

14 PT — Jordan Lee (Assignee): Working on it. Plan: create role "breakglass-audit-evidence-readonly" with least privilege to audit event search + exporter UI read endpoints + log read for audit-log-exporter in prod. Will require MFA + session duration 1h max. Will assign to Daniel only. Target completion today.

37 PT — Jordan Lee (Assignee): Provisioned.
- Role: breakglass-audit-evidence-readonly
- Principal: Daniel Carter
- Permissions: read-only to (1) audit-events search index (rotation event types included), (2) audit-log-export UI export endpoints, (3) audit-log-exporter service logs (prod) for validation.
- Controls: MFA required, max session duration 1h, CloudTrail/central audit logging enabled for AssumeRole events.
- Expiry: automated deprovision scheduled for 2025-11-12 17:00 PT.
Please confirm you can access and that the scopes are sufficient; if anything is missing, specify the exact endpoint/service and we’ll evaluate.

22 PT — Daniel Carter (Reporter): Confirmed access works. I can query/export rotation events and validate exporter schema output. No write access observed (as expected). Thanks. Will update once evidence export is complete.
41 PT — Daniel Carter (Reporter): Evidence export complete and attached to the audit packet. No extension needed. OK to let access expire per schedule.
06 PT — Jordan Lee (Assignee): Expiry executed. Role removed from Daniel’s assignments and break-glass policy detached. Verified no remaining bindings. Closing ticket.

title: Log retention exception  QuantaGov (extended retention for audit)
body:
Issue summary:
QuantaGov (Private VPC deployment) requests longer retention for inference request logs for audit purposes.

Requested retention:
- Payload: 180 days
- Metadata: 365 days

Notes:
This is a Private deployment. Customer uses Redwood-managed telemetry exporter into their VPC-managed log store, but Redwood is still responsible for the default TTL config in the exporter.

Acceptance criteria:
1) Exporter config updated to 180d payload and 365d metadata for QuantaGov only.
2) Security approval documented.
3) Customer acceptance recorded (security questionnaire / email).

References:
- Policy: confluence://security-and-compliance/data-residency-and-retention/inference-request-log-retention-policy
- Exception register: confluence://security-and-compliance/risk-and-exceptions/inference-log-retention-exception-register

comments.body: Validation: config applied and retention policies visible in exporter status endpoint. Customer confirmed.

comments.body: Added to exception register. Closing.

title: Console cost breakdown totals do not match invoice totals (Northbridge Security Labs)
body:
Issue summary
- Customer reports that the Redwood Console "Cost breakdown" totals for Feb do not match their Feb invoice totals. They are treating Console as the authoritative cost number for internal allocation and are seeing a ~6–9% delta depending on workspace.

Customer / account context
- Customer: Northbridge Security Labs (enterprise)
- Deployment: Hosted API + some Dedicated capacity add-on (per CSM notes)
- Stakeholders: Engineering + Finance (invoice reconciliation)
- Customer expectation: Console breakdown totals should reconcile to invoice for the same period when filtered to the same org/workspaces.

Impact
- High trust risk with finance stakeholder; customer is escalating internally.
- Customer blocked on monthly internal chargeback using Console exports.

What customer is seeing
- Console (Cost Breakdown view) totals: lower than invoice.
- Invoice includes line items fo...

What customer is seeing
- Console (Cost Breakdown view) totals: lower than invoice.
- Invoice includes line items fo...

comments.body: 18 PT — Marissa Cole (Reporter / Console Product): Opened on behalf of CSM escalation. Customer finance team expects Console to reconcile 1:1 with invoice for the month. Please prioritize explanation + any fix needed.

02 PT — Chloe Martin (Assignee / Console Integration): Confirming Console cost breakdown for this org is behind feature flag "console_cost_breakdown_use_cost_model_api"; rollout began last week for ~10% enterprise orgs. I’ll verify flag state for Northbridge and pull a sample response from Cost Model API for the same window.

34 PT — Logan Wright (Telemetry Lead): Noted drift alert on 2026-02-19 correlated with telemetry lag from ingestion backlog (see incident thread). Lag window was ~2–4 hours; late events backfilled. If Console materializes aggregates before late events arrive, you can see temporary undercount until backfill completes.

10 PT — Laura Bennett (Finance): Reminder: invoice includes non-usage line items (platform fee, commit true-ups, credits/adjustments, taxes). Console should be presented as usage + (optional) overhead allocation, not contract artifacts. We need an explicit reconciliation note + a stable mapping for enterprise customers.

26 PT — Chloe Martin: Verified flag ON for Northbridge. Console is showing Cost Model v1 breakdown. Console "Total" currently sums: request_attributable_total + overhead_allocated_total. Invoice grand total includes additional items. For this customer, the delta seems to match their reserved capacity commit true-up + enterprise platform fee.

41 PT — Jada Williams (Console Backend): Also found a UI bug: in the export CSV, the header says "Total (USD)" without clarifying it’s usage-only (plus allocated overhead) and excludes credits/commit/platform fees. Suggest we rename column and add tooltip + docs link. This isn’t a cost computation bug, it’s a semantics/UX mismatch.

15 PT — Ethan Park (Service/API Lead): Confirming Cost Model API taxonomy is per ADR-022: request-attributable costs + explicitly modeled overhead allocation. It intentionally does not model contract-level items (fees, discounts, credits, taxes). Billing is aligned to primitives, but invoice assembly is separate. Recommend we add a Console banner/learn-more for enterprise invoices.

27 PT — Marissa Cole: Action items:
1) Support response to customer with reconciliation steps and explicit exclusions.
2) Console UX: change label "Total" to "Usage total" and add invoice reconciliation note.
3) Confirm telemetry lag did not permanently affect Feb totals (only transient).

03 PT — Chloe Martin: Ran backtest for Feb 1–29 UTC (org_id redacted). Cost Model API aggregates match billing usage subtotal within 0.4% after late-arrival backfill; remaining difference is explained by (a) commit true-up and (b) platform fee not represented in Console breakdown. No evidence of ongoing undercount.

32 PT — Support (via Marissa): Sent customer reconciliation note: compare invoice "Usage" line item subtotal to Console "Usage total" for matching UTC calendar month; exclude platform fee/commit true-ups/credits/taxes. Provided screenshot and filter settings (UTC, Feb 1 00:00 to Mar 1 00:00). Customer acknowledged and will update their internal process.
20 PT — Chloe Martin: Closing as Resolved. Follow-up product task opened for Console labeling and docs link (tracked in Console backlog; not a support ticket).

title: Midday throughput cliff on dedicated capacity traced to silent-tenant heartbeat suppression causing allocator queue blocking and autoscaler stall
body:
Issue summary:
During a midday traffic window (approx 12:45–13:15 UTC) a FinNova dedicated capacity cluster experienced a sharp drop in successful throughput for batched routes serving redwood-rlhf-13b. P50 remained acceptable but p95/p99 spiked and many requests were queued >10s. Customers reported timeouts and increased 5xx rate.

Impact:
- FinNova observed ~40% throughput loss for critical batching routes for ~24 minutes.
- Several requests hit route-level timeouts and triggered client retries.
- No billing or data-loss impact observed.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: finnova-prod-1 (reserved)
- Region: us-east-1
- Models: redwood-rlhf-13b (pinned v2026-02-28), fallback to open-llama-13b-v2 enabled

Steps to reproduce (as observed):
1) Targeted load pattern: many small interactive streams (~200–300 simultaneous short streams) started after a quiet period.
2) Autoscaler did not prov...

comments.author: Priya Shah ()
comments.body: A customer reported timeouts and high p99 — triage started. Assigned to Mateo. Opened allocator trace and initial timeline.

comments.author: Mateo Alvarez (SRE)
comments.body: Observed allocator queue growth and several ATTACH operations in 'waiting' state for >9s. Noted suppressed heartbeats on tenant T-1849 sockets. Added observability hooks to capture sidecar heartbeat metrics.

comments.author: on-call (Ibrahim)
comments.body: Confirmed autoscaler saw low moving-average due to delayed attach completions; filed a short-term mitigation to lower autoscale evaluation window to 10s for the pool as an emergency step.

comments.author: Mateo Alvarez (SRE)
comments.body: Temporary mitigation applied: reduced allocator lease timeout to 6s for pool finnova-prod-1 and restarted allocator workers on 3 nodes. Throughput recovered to baseline at ~13:10 UTC.

comments.author: Zoe Chen
comments.body: Post-incident follow-up: customer notified, incident summary prepared, planned follow-ups added to backlog.
