title: S3 audit export: customer reports "missing" audit events during downstream throttling; exporter lag/backfill semantics and dedup guidance
body:
Issue summary
- Helio Health reports that S3 audit export appears to be missing events for a ~25–40 minute window on 2025-04-28 (approx 18:10–18:50 UTC). They observed a gap when querying S3 objects by ingestion time in their downstream pipeline.

Impact
- Customer perceived loss of audit evidence (compliance concern). No Redwood control-plane/service disruption reported. Primary risk is trust in export correctness and ability to reconstruct audit trail.

Customer context / destination
- Destination: Amazon S3 (cross-account), SSE-KMS enabled (customer-managed key)
- Delivery format: newline-delimited JSON batches written to date/hour partitions
- Customer ingestion: internal pipeline that expects near-real-time object creation, then forwards into their SIEM.

What the customer observed
- Gaps in their SIEM view when filtered by event timestamps.
- S3 bucket showed reduced object crea...

Issue summary
- Helio Health reports that S3 audit export appears to be missing events for a ~25–40 minute window on 2025-04-28 (approx 18:10–18:50 UTC). They observed a gap when querying S3 objects by ingestion time in their downstream pipeline.

Impact
- Customer perceived loss of audit evidence (compliance concern). No Redwood control-plane/service disruption reported. Primary risk is trust in export correctness and ability to reconstruct audit trail.

Customer context / destination
- Destination: Amazon S3 (cross-account), SSE-KMS enabled (customer-managed key)
- Delivery format: newline-delimited JSON batches written to date/hour partitions
- Customer ingestion: internal pipeline that expects near-real-time object creation, then forwards into their SIEM.

What the customer observed
- Gaps in their SIEM view when filtered by event timestamps.
- S3 bucket showed reduced object crea...

comments.body:
12 UTC — Liam O'Rourke (Reporter, TAM)
Customer report from Helio Health (security ops) that they have a gap in audit events in their SIEM view and suspect S3 export dropped records. They cite a ~30 minute window yesterday where their S3 ingest saw no new objects. Requesting urgent confirmation whether Redwood dropped events or can backfill.

26 UTC — Miguel Santos (Enterprise CSM)
Adding: customer is in beta cohort. They are concerned about audit evidence completeness for compliance. They are currently querying by object creation time; may be discarding late data in their pipeline. They asked whether we guarantee exactly-once (they assumed yes).

41 UTC — Aisha Bello (SRE, Assignee)
Looking at exporter dashboards for tenant in us-east. I see a lag spike around 18:10–19:00 UTC with increased s3 adapter errors. Pattern looks like S3 SlowDown + retries. Checkpoint did not advance during that window; it resumed afterward and caught up. No indication of drops so far.

03 UTC — Colin O'Donnell (Solutions Engineer)
Can someone confirm customer bucket settings? Cross-account + SSE-KMS can be sensitive if KMS throttles. Also ask if they have lifecycle rules or prefix hot-spot issues. For now, we should align on semantics: at-least-once, dedup via event_id.

18 UTC — Aisha Bello (SRE)
Pulled exporter logs (sanitized). Repeated PutObject failures:
- error_code=SlowDown
- http_status=503
- retry_backoff_ms increasing
Retries eventually succeed. No DLQ writes during the reported window. This is consistent with delivery delay, not loss.

33 UTC — Liam O'Rourke (TAM)
Relayed to customer: we see downstream throttling responses from S3, exporter applied backoff, and delivery lagged but resumed and backfilled. Asked them to re-run ingestion for the window and dedup on event_id. Customer asked for written explanation they can attach to internal incident ticket.

05 UTC — Kevin Osei (Security Engineering)
Please be explicit: beta uses at-least-once; not exactly-once. Dedup is expected downstream using event_id. Also confirm they understand Redwood retention vs export responsibilities (Confluence policy page). Avoid implying we retain indefinitely; stick to documented backfill limits.

02 UTC — Aisha Bello (SRE)
Follow-up: export lag peaked ~35 min, then returned to baseline. Current health: last_success within 2 min, error rate normal. I recommend we ask customer to adjust their pipeline to accept late-arriving objects and not treat "no objects created" as data loss.

17 UTC — Liam O'Rourke (TAM)
Customer re-ran ingestion over last 24h and confirmed the previously "missing" events appeared, but their pipeline had filtered them out as late. They will update their logic to use event timestamp and dedup by event_id. They requested a short set of best practices; I pointed them at our current beta guidance (delivery semantics + dedup).

40 UTC — Aisha Bello (SRE)
Closing notes: no evidence of data loss; behavior explained by S3 throttling and exporter backoff. Suggest adding a product/docs action item to emphasize that object creation time is not a reliable proxy for audit event time and that lag/backfill is expected during downstream throttling.

title: Cleanup and reconcile SLA orchestration: template variables, routing keys, and reporting parity
body:
Issue summary:
Recent deploys to sla-enforcer-v2 and the rule-engine refactor created several small divergences between SLA configuration, routing key normalization, and the downstream reporting transforms. The net effect: daily SLA breach counts in the support dashboard are ~7% lower than expected and some internal routing macros do not expand, causing tickets to be auto-assigned to the default queue instead of tiered on-call.

Impact:
- Incorrect auto-assignment for ~120 internal support tickets last week (internal queues).
- Daily SLA breach totals for internal dashboards are undercounted (~7% gap) which affects SRE on-call capacity planning.
- Risk of misrouted P1/P2 escalations if macros are not fixed before next release window.

Observed behavior / Evidence:
- Audit log from sla-enforcer (2026-03-08 03:14 - 03:46 UTC) shows macro expansion failures: variable \"priority_level\" n...

title: Client-facing timeouts for extremely long prompts combined with very large max_tokens during extended-output generation
body:
Issue summary:
Customers are observing client-side timeouts (HTTP 504 or streaming disconnect) when requests include a very large prompt (long conversation history + documents) and a large max_tokens target for generation (~50k+ output tokens). The problem manifests intermittently but is reproducible with the same inputs and appears correlated with the combined token footprint (prompt tokens + max_tokens) that causes prolonged per-request serving time or backend stalls.

Impact:
- DocuMind (dedicated) reports 60% of extended-generation requests failing with timeouts in nightly batch runs, blocking their indexing pipeline.
- Users see streaming disconnects and truncated outputs; retries frequently hit rate-limits or resource contention.
- Business impact: critical data ingestion delayed; customer requests escalation to enterprise success.

Environment:
- Redwood Dedicated cluster: prod...

- DocuMind (dedicated) reports 60% of extended-generation requests failing with timeouts in nightly batch runs, blocking their indexing pipeline.
- Users see streaming disconnects and truncated outputs; retries frequently hit rate-limits or resource contention.
- Business impact: critical data ingestion delayed; customer requests escalation to enterprise success.

Environment:
- Redwood Dedicated cluster: prod...

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Acknowledged. Opened ticket and escalated to SRE + Runtime. Provided request id and asked for any recent deploys.

comments.author: SRE (Liam O'Connor)
comments.body: Investigating GPU memory and scheduler backpressure. Initial checks show node-12 had elevated OOM kills earlier this morning; collecting per-request KV cache metrics.

comments.author: Maya Patel, DocuMind
comments.body: We reproduced reliably in our nightly run. Can we get a mitigation quickly? This blocks our SLAs with customers.

comments.author: Priya Rao
comments.body: Found a path where extremely long max_tokens keeps the request in a hot execution slot preventing KV cache eviction; scheduler's preemption threshold not triggering due to inaccurate duration estimate. Raising hypothesis in investigation notes.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Temporary mitigation provided: reduce max_tokens to 8k or split generation into windowed chunks. Customer testing now. Follow-up action: implement preemption guard and update scheduler heuristics.

comments.author: Maya Patel, DocuMind
comments.body: Workaround successful for nightly job when we window output to 8k tokens per call. Requesting eta for permanent fix and note on any billing impact.

title: Streaming request timeouts during peak load correlated with TP replicas (Dedicated / Acme AI)
body:
Issue summary
- Acme AI reports intermittent streaming timeouts during peak load. Failures present as:
 - client-side disconnects / timeouts while stream is active
 - increased 504s from gateway for streaming routes
 - p99 latency spikes and elevated cancellations
- Correlation: failures concentrate on multi-GPU tensor-parallel (TP) replicas (TP=4) and are much less frequent on single-GPU replicas.

Customer impact
- Customer-facing chat streams terminate mid-response during traffic bursts.
- Acme AI reports ~2–6% of streaming requests timing out between 30–90 seconds during peak.
- Downstream: retries amplify load; user-visible degradation; occasional backlog buildup in their app.

Environment
- Deployment: Dedicated (Acme AI)
- Region: us-east
- GPU: A100 80GB (multi-GPU replicas)
- Runtime: 1.18.x (exact build to confirm from /health/version)
- Mode: streaming chat completions, mix...

Environment

- Region: us-east
- GPU: A100 80GB (multi-GPU replicas)
- Runtime: 1.18.x (exact build to confirm from /health/version)
- Mode: streaming chat completions, mix...

comments.body: 12 PST — Owen Phillips (Support) — Ticket created from customer report in #support-escalations. Customer seeing streaming timeouts during peak; suspected correlation with TP replicas. Looping in Liam (TAM) + Rachel (CS) for comms, and tagging runtime/SRE for triage.

03 PST — Liam O'Rourke (Support/TAM) — Spoke with Acme AI (via Slack Connect). They report 30–90s stream timeouts, mainly during 9–11am PT peak. They can provide request IDs + timestamps. Asked them to share client timeout settings and whether any upstream ALB idle timeout is 60s (common culprit).

36 PST — Aisha Bello (SRE) — Checked tp-stall-and-timeout dashboard for Acme us-east. There’s a clear correlation between request_timeout spikes and tp_rank_heartbeat gaps on 2 replicas in the pool. No matching NCCL error spikes; looks like silent stall. Suggest applying concurrency cap immediately while eng continues fixes.

05 PST — Rafael Mendes (SRE) — +1 on mitigation. We can also temporarily reduce max_batch_tokens and avoid long-context in same pool during peak. If customer approves, we can run a controlled logging window (NCCL_DEBUG=INFO + limited scope) per INT-2219, but we should do it after applying caps to avoid compounding latency.

22 PST — Liam O'Rourke (Support/TAM) — Customer confirmed their client timeout is 75s and upstream LB idle timeout is 120s, so this doesn’t look like a simple idle timeout. They shared 15 request IDs (attached in internal note) that timed out; timestamps align with heartbeat gaps SRE flagged.

41 PST — Liam O'Rourke (Support/TAM) — Coordinated with SRE to apply interim workaround: reduced max concurrent streaming requests per TP replica from 24 -> 12 and lowered max_batch_tokens during peak window. Customer notified this is temporary pending runtime fixes; asked them to monitor %timeouts and report back.

10 PST — Jacob Stein (SRE) — After caps, timeout rate down from ~4% peak to ~0.7% peak (still above baseline). Heartbeat gaps reduced but not eliminated. Suggest keeping caps + proceed with eng instrumentation rollout so we can classify stall reason (compute vs comms vs scheduler).

18 PST — Noah Patel (Eng, Runtime) — This matches the TP stall signature we’re prioritizing in ENG-4821. We have PRs in flight for heartbeat + stall reason codes (21834) and scheduler guardrail for collective starvation (21841). Once 21834 lands, we should be able to tag these stalls more precisely and decide whether to flip watchdog (21860) for Dedicated canary.

02 PST — Rachel Kim (CS) — Customer update: caps helped materially; they’re okay with reduced throughput during peak as long as streaming remains stable. They want an ETA for a permanent fix and would like to be first canary for 1.19 in Dedicated if risk is acceptable.

27 PST — Liam O'Rourke (Support/TAM) — Shared tentative plan with Acme AI: Dedicated-first canary of runtime 1.19.x once gating checks pass. For now, we’re leaving concurrency caps in place. Keeping ticket open until we complete canary + confirm timeouts return to baseline without caps.

title: Intermittent 5xx wave from TLS-resume-ticket exhaustion combined with HTTP/2 negotiation mismatch between API gateway and runtime
body: Issue summary: Starting 2026-03-10 09:12 UTC NimbusHealth observed repeated 5xx errors for short periods (~2-8 minutes) across multiple chat and embedding routes. The API gateway returned 502/503 and upstream serving runtimes reported rapid connection resets and ephemeral worker evictions. Impact: customer-facing requests intermittently failed (up to 18% error rate during the peak window) causing degraded application experience for their enterprise workflows. Environment: production (us-east), mixed-hosted and dedicated tenant; incident coincided with a tenant priority reconfiguration from NimbusHealth account team.

comments.author: (Ava)
comments.body: Customer reported error spikes and shared initial request ids and timestamps. Asked for logs and whether they had changed client-side keepalive settings.

comments.author: SRE (Diego)
comments.body: Observed TLS ticket rotation metrics matching spike; created temporary alert to track ticket cache saturation. Started investigating apigw HTTP/2 settings pushed in the last config roll.

comments.author: (Priya S.)
comments.body: Confirmed apigw config change that nudges SETTINGS initial_window_size and max_concurrent_streams during tenant priority reshuffle. Reverted the SETTINGS tweak in Canary, seeing immediate reduction in GOAWAYs.

comments.author: (Ava)
comments.body: Updated NimbusHealth that we are rolling a targeted mitigation and requested they test. Customer reported partial improvement but still small blips for heavy short-lived streams.

comments.author: SRE (Diego)
comments.body: Deployed a two-part mitigation: 1) temporarily increase TLS session-ticket cache, 2) pin HTTP/2 SETTINGS for apigw-to-runtime connections to stable values during tenant reshuffles. Monitoring shows 5xx rate dropped below 1%.

title: Parallel model shard downloads fail with 403s due to CGNAT ephemeral port collisions on customer's egress path
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports intermittent 403s and corrupted ranged downloads when their private deployment attempts to fetch model shards in parallel (10-20 concurrent range GETs). Failures only occur when the customer's egress path transits a carrier-grade NAT (CGNAT) device provided by their ISP.

Impact:
- Production model loads failing ~15-40% of parallel shard fetch attempts, causing increased model startup latency and occasional inference timeouts for user requests.
- Errors are presigned-url 403s and occasional HTTP range truncation requiring retry.

Environment:
- Customer: DataQube Analytics (dedicated private deploy, VPC peered to redwood control plane)
- Region: us-east
- Serving nodes: rwinf-dedicated-ssd-gpu-32 (pool of 6)
- Model: gpt-large-v2 (sharded objects, parallel range GETs)

comments.body: 05Z: Customer ran agent with --parallel=16 and returned 403s repeatedly. Attached NAT logs from their edge showing 1 external port mapping for multiple internal sockets.

comments.author: Mateo Rivera (Networking) - 2026-03-10T16
comments.body: 30Z: Reproduced locally by injecting a CGNAT emulator; observed same behavior. Proposed short-term: add concurrency cap for presigned fetches in agent config. Long-term: discuss origin signature relaxation or a handshake to allow multi-stream downloads behind NAT.

comments.author: Customer (DataQube) - 2026-03-10T18
comments.body: 02Z: We can switch to an alternate egress IP but this will require change window. Please advise best temporary setting for our deployment to restore throughput with minimal impact.

comments.author: (Ava) - 2026-03-11T08
comments.body: 00Z: Recommended agent flag set: RWINF_MAX_SHARD_DOWNLOAD_CONCURRENCY=4 and confirmed successful model pull in customer test run.

title: SomaHealth: semantic accuracy drop after enabling adaptive async batching + 4-bit quant + KV TTL eviction
body: Issue opened by SomaHealth CS: after enabling the new adaptive async batching policy (per-request grouping) together with the new 4-bit quantization profile and the kv-cache TTL eviction (LRU-timeout=30s) for tenant somahealth-prod, several endpoints show increased semantic errors and hallucinations for a critical clinical extraction prompt set. Customer observed sudden mismatch between human-labeled expected extraction values and model outputs starting 2026-03-11 22:00 UTC after a config rollout. They want guidance for immediate safe rollback and root-cause investigation.

Customer observed sudden mismatch between human-labeled expected extraction values and model outputs starting 2026-03-11 22:00 UTC after a config rollout. They want guidance for immediate safe rollback and root-cause investigation.

comments.author: SomaHealth CS
comments.body: 'We validated on a mirror and see divergence only when q4 + adaptive async batching is on.'

comments.author: (Ava)
comments.body: 'Collecting failing request IDs and creating snapshot. We'll validate rollback steps; assigning to runtime team.'

comments.author: (Liam)
comments.body: 'I will reproduce on staging, capture kernel-level traces, and prepare a safe rollback plan. Suspect packing heuristics + quantization interplay.'

comments.author: SRE (Mira)
comments.body: 'Recommend immediate traffic-splitting to fallback model variant and disable kv-eviction for somahealth-prod while investigation continues.'

title: Sudden autoscale stutter causing reserved-pool throughput dips from ephemeral prober flood
body: Issue summary: Starting 2026-03-12 09:17 PDT, Nimbus AI reported recurring p95/p99 latency spikes and 429s on a set of batched routes served from their Dedicated reserved pool. Impact: customer-facing throughput dropped ~35% for 12-18 minute windows, causing downstream timeouts and >5% request failure rate for their primary chat endpoint. Environment: Dedicated reserved pool (customer: Nimbus AI, prod-us-west). Initial symptom pattern looks like autoscaler briefly shrinking capacity or delaying attachments, followed by rapid attach/fallback churn and transient GPU shortages.

Impact: customer-facing throughput dropped ~35% for 12-18 minute windows, causing downstream timeouts and >5% request failure rate for their primary chat endpoint. Environment: Dedicated reserved pool (customer: Nimbus AI, prod-us-west). Initial symptom pattern looks like autoscaler briefly shrinking capacity or delaying attachments, followed by rapid attach/fallback churn and transient GPU shortages.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: 32: Sent initial triage to SRE channel #incidents with sample logs and customer timeline. Asked Nimbus for traffic dump and confirmed this was not a usage spike from their app.

comments.author: Marcus Lee
comments.body: 45: Noted a clustered burst of 0.5s-frequency health probes from a Nimbus sidecar process coinciding with autoscaler shrink decisions. Hypothesis: prober flood masquerading as legitimate small streams causing allocator to mark heartbeats and reduce perceived demand during scale_down cooldowns.

comments.author: Nimbus Ops, Priya Shah
comments.body: 02: Confirmed a recent rollout of an internal monitoring agent that increased health-check frequency for internal containers. We will temporarily disable it on canary nodes.

comments.author: Marcus Lee
comments.body: 15: Applied temporary mitigation: enforce per-tenant sidecar probe rate-limit at the edge and increased reserved-pool headroom by +2 replicas. Monitoring for stability.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: 20: Noted throughput recovered to baseline after mitigation; customer still sees occasional p95 blips during midday. Keeping ticket open to validate root cause and implement durable fix.

title: Tenant migration between Dedicated pools causes delayed pin reconciliation, silent fallback to safe-variant and token spike
body:
Issue summary: During an automated tenant migration from one Dedicated GPU pool to another in us-east-1, several customer sessions experienced a delayed model-version pin reconciliation. As a consequence the routing layer silently diverted a subset of traffic to the safe fallback variant (rwd-pegasus-13b-v2->rwd-pegasus-13b-quant) for ~18 minutes, which produced higher token counts and a billing spike for the customer.

Impact: NimbleHealth reported a 2.6x increase in token consumption for ~27% of their active sessions during the window. Customer-facing latency remained within SLOs but model outputs changed (quantized variant had higher repetition on longer prompts). Customer flagged potential data-quality regression and unexpected invoice increase.

Environment: Production Dedicated pool migration (us-east-1). Migration triggered via Reserved Capacity reassignment job: rc-migrate-202...

comments.author: Connor Lee
comments.body: Customer reported sudden increase in token usage and subtle quality change. Provided initial logs and sample request IDs.

comments.author: Priya Nair
comments.body: Confirmed migration job rc-migrate-2026-03-12-08 executed; observed multiple orchestrator snapshot versions in controller logs.

comments.author: Mateo Alvarez
comments.body: Found batching commit window in reconciler code (orchestrator/service/pin_reconciler.go) where writes would be delayed up to 20s under heavy snapshot churn. Raising suspicion this was extended further under dedicated pool migration due to a retry timer bug.

comments.author: Connor Lee
comments.body: Customer shared their request traces and example request IDs: rh-req-20260312-01902, rh-req-20260312-01937. Support noticed outputs coming from quantized variant artifacts (repetition on long completions).

comments.author: Priya Nair
comments.body: Edge cache TTL logs show suppressed refresh events for tenant nimble-tenant-42 between 08:14:02 and 08:32:19.

comments.author: NimbleHealth - Sarah Chen
comments.body: Asks for immediate mitigation and visibility on how billing will be adjusted. Requests temporary rollback of migration if needed.

comments.author: Mateo Alvarez
comments.body: Created hotfix branch to remove batching suppression during pool-reassignment window and added a cache-warmup handshake to force edge pin refresh. PR referenced above.

comments.author: Priya Nair
comments.body: Applied emergency configuration: set orchestrator.pin_reconciler.max_batch_delay=0 for ongoing migrations in us-east-1 to prevent further delayed commits. Monitoring in progress.

title: Streaming chat stalls mid-generation when Dedicated GPU worker triggers kernel memory compaction
body:
Issue summary:
Customer BrightForms reports that SSE-based chat streams stop producing tokens mid-response (~after 200-600 tokens) but the HTTP connection remains open. The client sees no final event and ultimately times out after ~30s of silence. This is observed only on their Dedicated pool in us-west-2 and primarily with redwood-neo-13B-quant; some occurrences with redwood-14B-v1 were reported after model fallback.

Impact:
- Multiple end users see partial/paused responses in production web chat widgets.
- Reported across three customer sessions in the last 24 hours; affects important SLAs for BrightForms (enterprise contract).
- Workaround (client reconnect) recovers response but may produce duplicated content or truncated context.

Environment:
- Dedicated capacity (reserved GPU pool) for BrightForms Inc. in us-west-2
- Serving runtime version: v1.18.3
- Autoscaler: v0.9.1 (scale...

comments.body: Notified BrightForms that we have a temporary flag to disable compaction and asked for permission to apply in prod. Customer approved immediate change.

comments.author: SRE Bot
comments.body: Flag applied to pool 'brightforms-prod-usw2', compaction.enabled=false. Confirmed no new reports in next 3 hours.

comments.author: Serving Runtime
comments.body: Proposed patch to make compaction cooperative and non-blocking (PR linked). Will run test harness overnight.

comments.author: Observability (automated)
comments.body: Added sse_token_emit_pause metric to staging dashboards. Alert rule draft created (severity=P1 if >2s for >3 streams).

Issue summary:
Customer BrightForms reports that SSE-based chat streams stop producing tokens mid-response (~after 200-600 tokens) but the HTTP connection remains open. The client sees no final event and ultimately times out after ~30s of silence. This is observed only on their Dedicated pool in us-west-2 and primarily with redwood-neo-13B-quant; some occurrences with redwood-14B-v1 were reported after model fallback.

Impact:
- Multiple end users see partial/paused responses in production web chat widgets.
- Reported across three customer sessions in the last 24 hours; affects important SLAs for BrightForms (enterprise contract).
- Workaround (client reconnect) recovers response but may produce duplicated content or truncated context.

Environment:
- Dedicated capacity (reserved GPU pool) for BrightForms Inc. in us-west-2
- Serving runtime version: v1.18.3
- Autoscaler: v0.9.1 (scale...

title: Console widget load latency / 504s when trace-hook request headers exceed 16KB
body:
Issue summary: Several LuminaHealth users report Dashboard widgets failing to render or timing out (504) intermittently when the underlying traces include very large trace-hook header payloads (base64-encoded span attributes).

Impact: Affects multiple dashboard panels that include trace links. Charts either hang in 'loading' state for 15-30s then 504, or render partial data with missing trace links. This prevents on-call and SRE from drilling into traces during incidents and has impacted two production incident investigations in the last 48h.

Environment: Production, us-east-1. Issue started after deployment of trace-hook header expansion change (round-robin agent enrichment) rolled on 2026-03-09. Customer on enterprise plan with Route policy pinned to us-east ingestion.

Observed behavior: When trace metadata headers attached to trace-hook callbacks exceed ~16KB, the console widget...

comments.author: (Ava)
comments.body: Customer reported multiple dashboards returning 504s during incident. Captured sample trace IDs and headers. SLA flagged P1.

comments.author: SRE (Diego)
comments.body: Confirmed reproduction with synthetic header. Opening INT ticket to track proxy rollback decision. Assigned to Diego for deeper debug.

comments.author: Customer (LuminaHealth)
comments.body: Additional context - our agents started attaching larger debug blobs for correlating spans after recent rollout; can revert if needed but would prefer a platform fix.

comments.author: SRE (Diego)
comments.body: Found proxy disk-buffer handshake path does not propagate Transfer-Encoding: chunked header; created minimal patch to set content-length or chunked flag depending on buffer mode. Draft PR: github.com/redwood-inference/proxy/pull/1892

comments.author: (Ava)
comments.body: Notified customer of temporary workaround (reduce trace-hook payload size or enable header-trim at agent side). Customer accepted workaround while engineering prepares patch.

title: Rapid elevation of 5xx rate during large embedding upload + priority-shed misclassification causing upstream OOMs
body:
Issue summary: Customers reported a sharp increase in 5xx responses (primarily 502/503) starting ~2026-03-13 11:45 UTC. Several dedicated customers (notably NimbleStack) saw higher error rates on embedding and streaming routes during a large bulk embedding upload. Errors correlated with upstream runtime restarts and OOM-like preemption logs.

Impact: Multiple production customers experienced request failures and increased latency for ~45 minutes. Error rate peaked at ~18% on affected routes (baseline <0.5%). Business impact for NimbleStack: failed job completions and API retries causing billing anomalies.

Environment: prod cluster in us-east (dedicated pool A, customer tenant: nimblestack-ded-1). Affects streaming and /v1/embeddings endpoints.

Observed: 5xx increase, apigw return codes 502/503, runtime processes killed and restarted with GPU OOM flag in driver logs, kvcache eviction...

comments.body: 05Z - Evelyn Park (Support): Received the alert from monitoring. Engaging SRE and customer liaison. Asking customer to pause large uploads temporarily.

12Z - Priya Nair (SRE): Observed correlated kvcache eviction spikes and runtime restarts. Hypothesis: prefetch/pinning combined with priority-shed rule allowed too many low-priority connections to enter runtime acceptance window.
30Z - Diego Morales (On-call Runtime): Pushed temporary rate-limit tweak on nimblestack-ded-1 to throttle concurrent embedding batches. Error rate fell to ~2% after throttling initial bursts.
10Z - Tom Baker (NimbleStack): Confirming we paused the job and resume slowly per Support instruction. Appreciate quick mitigation. Observed job completions after throttle applied.

45Z - Priya Nair (SRE): Follow-up: reproduced a similar error pattern in staging with synthetic load. Submitted fix PR to autoscaler backoff logic to avoid immediate ephemeral worker spin-up when kvcache eviction > threshold. See linked PRs.

title: Audit-bridge mini-dump access request with two-person control for payment reconciliation probe
body:
Issue summary: Request from Payments Ops to provision a minimal, credentialed 'mini-dump' of request/response metadata and token-hash records for a small set of transactions (approx 30 sessions) to reconcile a reported billing discrepancy. Requestor requests expedited access under two-person control (operator + compliance reviewer).

Impact: Business-facing payment reconciliation blocked until we validate a suspected duplicate charge path. Data sensitivity: payloads may include customer identifiers but we only need hashed IDs, timestamps, route metadata and truncated output snippets (first 64 chars hashed). No full plaintext customer messages should be in scope.

Environment: prod us-east (inference cluster behind audit-bridge capture).

Requested scope and retention: minimal micro-sample (30 sessions), only columns: request_id, hashed_customer_id, model_version, route, timestamp, req...

comments.author: Aisha Rahman
comments.body: Opening request — attached PAY-2026-03 ticket from Finance. Requesting scoped mini-dump for 30 sessions and two-person control. Marking priority P1 due to payment hold.

comments.author: Elena Park
comments.body: Acknowledged. Need explicit schema and list of fields; require retention <= 7 days and KMS envelope. Also require justification memo signed by Payments Ops lead. Put request in pending until memo received.

comments.author: Marcus Li
comments.body: Uploaded justification memo (PAY-MEMO-491827). Confirms target time window 2026-03-07 14:20-14:35 UTC and affected routing key 'billing-proxy-v2'.

comments.author: Damien Cole
comments.body: Can run micro-query but need exact selector and approval to write to s3://redwood-internal/audit-bridge/mini-dumps/INT-491827/ (will wrap with KMS key arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456:key/xxxx). Requesting Compliance to confirm key and two approvers list.

comments.author: Elena Park
comments.body: Approved scope and KMS key. Approvers for two-person control: Damien Cole (SRE) and Elena Park (Compliance). Approval conditions: retention 72 hours, no plaintext output exported outside secured VM, redaction step mandatory. Move to In Progress.

comments.author: Aisha Rahman
comments.body: Noted, scheduling extraction window 2026-03-13 01:00 UTC when cluster load is low. Will update when export performed.

title: Private installer upgrade stuck at migrations (Helix Health) — control plane reports 'running migrations' > 60m
body:
## Issue summary
Helix Health (Private) attempted an in-place upgrade of the Redwood Private control plane using the Private installer. Upgrade appears to hang during the "migrations" step. Installer UI/CLI reports "running migrations" continuously (>60 minutes) with no visible progress. Customer is blocked from completing the upgrade window.

## Impact
- Customer cannot complete planned upgrade during maintenance window.
- Risk: partial upgrade state / inconsistent schema versions.
- Customer is compliance-heavy; prefers minimal manual DB intervention.

## Customer environment
- Deployment mode: Redwood Private (customer-managed VPC cluster)
- Control plane: customer-managed
- Data store: customer-managed Postgres (managed service)
- Observability: limited access; Support relies on customer-provided logs + diagnostics bundle.

## What changed
- Customer initiated upgrade from previou...

- Customer is compliance-heavy; prefers minimal manual DB intervention.

## Customer environment

- Data store: customer-managed Postgres (managed service)
- Observability: limited access; Support relies on customer-provided logs + diagnostics bundle.

## What changed
- Customer initiated upgrade from previou...

comments.body: 12 UTC — Priya Shah (Support): Opened incident ticket from Helix escalation. Customer reports upgrade stuck at "running migrations" for ~70 minutes. Requested installer logs + pod logs + versions. Created #support bridge thread and pulled in Platform per escalation playbook.

26 UTC — Ethan Park (Platform): Agree likely migration lock contention or runner waiting on DB connection. Please ask for: (1) migration pod logs, (2) Postgres max connections / pooler settings (high-level), (3) whether app traffic continued during maintenance window. Avoid advising manual DB changes until we see output.

43 UTC — Owen Phillips (Support Incidents): Marking P1/Sev1 due to maintenance window blocker + risk of partial state. Starting a small bridge in #support; will move to #incidents if we confirm broader impact (currently single customer).

05 UTC — Customer (Helix, via Support email): Provided installer version + target version; confirmed Postgres reachable and no maintenance today. They can pull pod logs and will share within 30 minutes. They asked if they should cancel/retry the installer now.
10 UTC — Priya Shah (Support): Advised customer to hold off on cancel/retry until logs are collected. Shared a short checklist for safe artifact collection (no data).
02 UTC — Customer: Shared kubectl describe + logs snippet indicating migration step repeatedly printing "waiting for advisory lock" and no progress. No crash-looping observed.

18 UTC — Priya Natarajan (Platform Eng sponsor): "waiting for advisory lock" suggests another session holds lock or previous attempt didn’t release. Ask customer DBA for lock snapshot and any long-lived sessions from prior deployment tooling. If they can safely terminate the locking session, migrations should proceed. If they cannot, we should plan rollback and reschedule upgrade.

40 UTC — Owen Phillips (Support): Drafted rollback decision tree for customer (pending Platform sign-off). Noted we need to confirm whether any schema changes applied before lock wait began; request migration runner output from start of step.

12 UTC — Customer: DBA reviewed and found one long-running session holding advisory lock from previous failed attempt. They terminated that session. Migrations progressed and upgrade completed successfully ~10 minutes later. Control plane health checks green.

30 UTC — Priya Shah (Support): Confirmed resolution with customer; requested they keep logs for 7 days in case of follow-up. Proposed turning this into a Known Issue article section: symptoms + "waiting for advisory lock" explanation + customer-safe lock-check instructions.

05 UTC — Kira Thompson (Docs): Added clearer "When to escalate" + "Do not run manual DB downgrades" language to workaround draft. Request Platform to review lock-contention guidance for customer safety.

22 UTC — Ethan Park (Platform): Signed off on KB wording with minor edits. Asked that we fast-track ENG-4828 so future installs produce a diagnostics bundle automatically (locks + migration phase + last successful step).

title: Embed dimension negotiation: proxy strips metadata header causing rerank CPU fallback and intermittent empty results
body:
Issue summary: Since 2026-03-11 22:00 UTC, CortexSearch reports intermittent empty candidate sets and p95 rerank latency spikes for their dedicated pool. The symptoms started shortly after they flipped the proxy compression/fast-path toggle during a traffic spike.

Impact: Client-facing search experiences occasional blank results and multi-second tail latency for rerank calls affecting approx 2-3% of queries; revenue impact unknown but noticeable on monitoring dashboards.

Environment: Dedicated cluster in us-west, vpc-connected private control plane, embed endpoint pinned to embed-small-v2, rerank endpoint pinned to rerank-lite-v1.

Steps to reproduce (customer):
1) Send embed request with Accept: application/x-embed+proto and X-Embed-Dims: 128 to /v1/embeddings
2) Immediately call rerank with the returned embedding payload to /v1/rerank
3) Observe sometimes an empty candidate list a...

normalized.description:

Environment: Dedicated cluster in us-west, vpc-connected private control plane, embed endpoint pinned to embed-small-v2, rerank endpoint pinned to rerank-lite-v1.

Steps to reproduce (customer):
1) Send embed request with Accept: application/x-embed+proto and X-Embed-Dims: 128 to /v1/embeddings
2) Immediately call rerank with the returned embedding payload to /v1/rerank
3) Observe sometimes an empty candidate list a...

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Thanks for the report, CortexSearch. We're triaging. Can you confirm whether you saw any proxy config changes around 22:00 UTC? Also requested a HAR and minimal repro payload.

comments.author: Arjun Patel
comments.body: Confirmed — we toggled proxy compression fast-path to true at ~21:58 UTC to workaround some small spike. Attached HAR and repro script. Repro rate is ~1 in 40 requests locally against your dedicated endpoint.

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: Observed matching symptom on dedicated ingress: Nginx sidecar combined with compressor module is dropping non-standard headers when the fast-path is enabled. Captured tcpdump showing header absent post-proxy before hitting rerank. Queued for infra patch.

comments.author: Priya Rao
comments.body: Hypothesis: proxy compression optimization strips custom headers with Accept-like names when using streaming chunking. Workaround: add X-Embed-Dims to preserved_headers list or switch to embedding payload embedding-dim in proto body. We'll prepare a small patch and a canary rollout to a single node pool.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Customer applied workaround to disable fast-path; reported immediate reduction in empty-results and improved p95. Still investigating root cause and patch schedule.

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: Deployed infra PR to add X-Embed-Dims and other embed headers to preserved_headers list in the compressor. Canary scheduled on 2026-03-14 15:00 UTC for one node in us-west dedicated pool.

comments.author: Arjun Patel
comments.body: Canary looks good so far; no empty results for last 10k queries. Please advise ETA to roll to full pool and recommended long-term mitigation.

comments.author: Priya Rao
comments.body: Plan: merge infra PR, release serving-runtime change to treat missing header as reject (fail-fast) to avoid silent CPU fallback while we rollout. Full rollout planned 2026-03-15 if canary remains stable.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Noted. Customer asked for retro/postmortem once complete and for a runbook entry. Assigned follow-ups to ENG and SRE teams.

title: Sustained 5xx spike during large embedding upload causing accept-queue collapse and runtime preemption
body:
Issue summary:
Customer QueryFlow reported a sustained elevation in 5xx responses (~8-12% error rate) beginning 2026-03-13 03:20 UTC during a large embedding ingestion job. Errors manifested as 502/503 from API gateway with upstream runtime disconnects and occasional runtime GPU preemptions.

Impact:
- QueryFlow dedicated tenant experienced failed embedding batches (10-20% of batches failing) and increased tail latency for chat calls.
- Customer reported retry storms and billing disputes for failed uploads.
- Incident observed primarily in us-west-2; spillover traces in us-east-1 for cross-region prefetch.

Environment:
- prod, dedicated capacity pool: dp-dedicated-42, node type: a100-80gb equivalent
- Ingress: api-gw-v3 (http2 multiplexing enabled), runtime: inference-runtime-v1.28
- Affected models: redwood-embed-v2 (batching for embedding), llama-2-7b-chat (mixed chat traffic)

Ste...

Customer QueryFlow reported a sustained elevation in 5xx responses (~8-12% error rate) beginning 2026-03-13 03:20 UTC during a large embedding ingestion job. Errors manifested as 502/503 from API gateway with upstream runtime disconnects and occasional runtime GPU preemptions.

Impact:
- QueryFlow dedicated tenant experienced failed embedding batches (10-20% of batches failing) and increased tail latency for chat calls.
- Customer reported retry storms and billing disputes for failed uploads.
- Incident observed primarily in us-west-2; spillover traces in us-east-1 for cross-region prefetch.

Environment:
- prod, dedicated capacity pool: dp-dedicated-42, node type: a100-80gb equivalent
- Ingress: api-gw-v3 (http2 multiplexing enabled), runtime: inference-runtime-v1.28
- Affected models: redwood-embed-v2 (batching for embedding), llama-2-7b-chat (mixed chat traffic)

Ste...

comments.body: 45 UTC - Ava Chen (Support): Initial triage and acknowledged to customer. Asked for request IDs, sample payload, and approximate start time. Escalated to SRE.

10 UTC - Miguel Alvarez (SRE): Noted accept_queue growth on api-gw in us-west-2 correlating with customer's start time. Socket backlog saturated; observed large number of half-open chunked uploads. Temporarily increased accept backlog on affected frontends to see immediate effect.

28 UTC - QueryFlow (Customer): Provided request IDs and a sample of the multipart upload payload (14MB average per bundle) and confirmed parallelism of 40 workers. Reported intermittent success after retries but overall high failure rate.
05 UTC - Support (Ava): Notified customer of temporary mitigation (reduced ingress concurrency and chunk size guidance). Opened linked issue with runtime team for GPU preempt investigation.

12 UTC - Runtime Eng (Leila Kaur): Found evidence of KV cache thrashing on dedicated nodes when embedding prefetch and chat KV warms co-occurred; eviction storms led to slower responses and higher OOM likelihood under heavy batching.

30 UTC - Miguel Alvarez (SRE): Implemented transient rate-limiting rule for chunked embedding route for this tenant and increased autoscaler headroom for dp-dedicated-42. Monitoring shows 5xx rate reduced to ~1.2% post-mitigation.
00 UTC - Ava Chen (Support): Updated customer with mitigation and next steps. Customer confirmed improved success rate and agreed to staged rollout of guidance and fixes.

- QueryFlow dedicated tenant experienced failed embedding batches (10-20% of batches failing) and increased tail latency for chat calls.

title: Dedicated cluster: GPU contention causing sustained throughput collapse and autoscale oscillation
body:
Issue summary:
Scanly Labs reports a sharp, sustained drop in throughput from their dedicated GPU pool in us-west starting 2026-03-11 09:12 UTC. Latency for streaming generation requests increases and many requests time out. The Redwood autoscaler began oscillating (rapid scale-up then scale-down) around the same time, making the throughput degradation persistent.

Impact:
- Production traffic for Scanly Labs' inference endpoints degraded: 60-80% drop in token throughput on pinned routes.
- Errors observed: tail latency spikes >10s, client-side timeouts, and a small increase in 5xx from serving-runtime.
- Customer-facing feature (real-time report generation) impacted for ~80% of requests routed to dedicated pool.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: scanly-prod-pool-usw (reserved 12 GPUs across 3 nodes)
- Region: us-west
- Models: gptj-compat-13b (primary), redwood-rlhf-7b (fallback)

Obse...

comments.author: Priya Natarajan
comments.body: Triage note: Customer reported at 09:12 UTC. Assigned to SRE on-call. Collected initial logs (prometheus, kube events) and requested a full gpu node health dump from the customer. Marking P1 / Sev1 due to production impact.

comments.author: Marco Ruiz (SRE)
comments.body: Observed ECC corrected increases on gpu-02-usw and repeated allocation timeouts. Temporarily cordoned gpu-02-usw and rebalanced pods; customer reports slight improvement but autoscaler still oscillating.

comments.author: Customer Success
comments.body: Customer confirms throughput improved from 20% to ~50% after cordon, but latency still elevated. They are running a load test to reproduce; shared logs in the ticket.

comments.author: Marco Ruiz
comments.body: Found node-level driver logs referencing NVIDIA UVM leak warnings and several kernel messages around PCIe link state. Hypothesis: driver leak causing fragmentation; raised INT-412 for infra to check driver/firmware state across pool.

comments.author: Luis Soto
comments.body: Ran kube-scheduler predicate trace. Scheduler is preferring nodes with 'gpu.available_memory' label; label values on some nodes stale after recent patch causing imbalanced placements. Created a small patch to update labels from telemetry on schedule (PR referenced).

comments.author: Marco Ruiz
comments.body: Applied mitigation: 1) Temporarily increased min replicas in autoscaler policy for scanly-prod-pool-usw to 6 to reduce oscillation sensitivity; 2) cordoned gpu-02-usw for deeper diagnostics; 3) added a short-lived admission hook to avoid scheduling to nodes with ECC errors. Customer reporting throughput ~85% of baseline now.

comments.author: Scanly Ops (customer)
comments.body: Thanks — throughput better. We see occasional tail latency spikes still during peak. Please advise on long-term fix and whether we need to migrate workload.

comments.author: Marco Ruiz
comments.body: Next steps: schedule maintenance window to repro with kernel/driver debug flags, plan firmware/driver upgrade for gpu-02-usw if hardware diagnostics confirm issues, and merge scheduler label sync fix. Open follow-up ticket to review autoscaler logic around allocation failures (INT-435).

title: Streaming requests with very large conversation history intermittently time out after heartbeat drop
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports streaming completions that begin successfully but fail mid-stream with a client-facing timeout after ~40-75s when the conversation history (system + user messages) exceeds ~120k tokens. Failures are observed only for dedicated tier customers on our us-east pool and primarily when max_tokens is set to a high value (>= 50k).

Impact:
- Affects Lexicon Labs production service: recurring failed streams for long-history sessions.
- Requests either close with a 504/timeout or a client-observed abrupt stream disconnect.
- Customer reports reduced completion reliability for long document synth jobs and increased error retries.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: dp-lex-01 in us-east-1
- API: /v1/streams.generate over websocket + HTTP streaming fallback
- Models: redwood-13b-std (primary), redwood-7b-quant (fallback)

Steps to reproduce (as performed by customer):

comments.author: Liam O'Connor (SRE)

comments.body: Confirmed observations — after the workaround we saw no streaming disconnects during repeated test runs. Thank you, please keep us posted on permanent fix timeline.

comments.author: Maya Chen ()

title: Intermittent generation requests with very large max_tokens time out near token-window boundary (KV-cache refill stall suspected)
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports intermittent 504 / request timeouts for single generation requests when using very large max_tokens (typically >= 120k token window). Failures occur late in the generation (after ~70-95% of requested tokens produced). Suspected stall during KV-cache refill / eviction cycle in serving runtime when prefill and decode phases overlap under heavy batching.

Impact:
- Affects multiple request attempts from NimbusAI production workloads on Dedicated pools in us-east; blocking their long-transcript generation features.
- Symptoms: spike in 5xx (504 gateway timeouts) and client-observed disconnects; partial outputs received before termination.

Environment:
- Dedicated customer pool (dedicated), prod:us-east; models: gptx-16b-fp16 primarily.
- Requests routed through API gateway with default 60s client timeout in their code, but server-side timeouts observed bey...

Impact:

Environment:
- Dedicated customer pool (dedicated), prod:us-east; models: gptx-16b-fp16 primarily.
- Requests routed through API gateway with default 60s client timeout in their code, but server-side timeouts observed bey...

comments.author: NimbusAI (customer)
comments.body: We're seeing ~5-10% of long-generation jobs fail with 504s. This started after we increased max_tokens for a transcript feature. Attaching traces and sample request IDs.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Acknowledged. Opening P1 incident and routing to SRE + Serving runtime team. Could you confirm whether you're using streaming or non-streaming requests and whether you can temporarily lower max_tokens as a mitigation?

comments.author: Marco Reyes
comments.body: Initial triage - found correlated spikes in 'kv_cache_refill_ms' metric and increased kernel execution stalls on host pool rp-dedicated-12. Traces show long blocking in 'prefill->decode' handoff. Reproduced locally with synthetic load using customer-like concurrency.

comments.author: Priya Nair
comments.body: Added deeper prints to the runtime; observed that when batch mixing includes both very long prefill and short requests, eviction/refill of KV shards triggers synchronous disk/host-memory copies for certain quantized kernels causing stalls. Patch proposed to make KV refill asynchronous and add backpressure on batches that exceed token-window thresholds.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Shared a temporary mitigation with customer: (1) Reduce max_tokens to <= 80k or (2) enable streaming=true to flush tokens incrementally, or (3) route large requests to a low-concurrency pool. Customer applying (2) streaming and (3) routing; reporting fewer failures but still seeing rare timeouts.

comments.author: Marco Reyes
comments.body: Deployed a canary patch to rp-dedicated-12 that makes KV refill async and limits batch coalescing of very long prefill requests. Early telemetry (12h) shows 85% reduction in kv_cache_refill_ms spikes and near-zero 504s for affected traces on canary hosts. Planning broader rollout after runbook and automated test coverage added.

comments.author: NimbusAI (customer)
comments.body: Thanks—canary seems promising. We'll keep streaming=true for now and report if we see new regressions. Please share ETA for full rollout.

comments.author: Priya Nair
comments.body: ETA for full rollout across dedicated pools: 2026-03-18 pending one more stability run and a regression test in CI that simulates heavy long-prefill coalescing. Follow-up action: create an alert for kv_cache_refill_ms > 5s per host and add a dashboard panel to per-pool view.

title: Staggered retry/warmup patterns from client fleet causing tenant-level 429 throttles
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports recurring tenant-level 429 responses during large fleet rollouts and worker restarts. Behavior appears concentrated during a ~2-6 minute window after a portion of their uploader fleet resumes activity (we see synchronized warmups).

Impact:
- NimbleDocs ingestion pipelines observe 20-40% increased 429s for ~3 minutes after batches of uploader processes restart (~100s of clients).
- Processing latency and backlog grow; customer sees failed items and manual retries.
- Affects production workload (dedicated pool, us-west-2).

Environment:
- Dedicated pool (customer-dedicated-42), prod us-west-2.
- Client SDK: nimble-uploader v1.3.2 (homegrown wrapper that performs exponential backoff with jitter).
- Observed on model routes: /v1/generate and /v1/embeddings (mixed).

Steps to reproduce (from customer repro script):
1) Stop 250 uploader workers (simulate rol...

comments.author: Priya Shah ()
comments.body: Ticket opened after NimbleDocs reached out via email. Requested immediate logs and sample timestamps. Asked for deployment schedule and SDK version.

comments.author: NimbleDocs (Customer)
comments.body: Provided restart schedule, sample request timestamps (2000+), and confirmed they use an internal wrapper that applies uniform jitter at the batch level.

comments.author: Miguel Torres (SRE)
comments.body: Collected gateway rate-limiter traces and token-bucket metrics for customer-dedicated-42; saw alignment windows at 00s of each minute when bursts peaked.

comments.author: Miguel Torres (SRE)
comments.body: Reproduced similar pattern in staging by orchestrating group restarts; confirmed that default burst allowance (50 tokens per window) is getting exhausted momentarily despite average QPS < committed QPS.

comments.author: (Priya)
comments.body: Shared immediate mitigation steps and recommended client config changes (stagger start and per-worker random delay) while we prepare a server-side soft-fix.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Implemented temporary rate-limiter tweak in control plane to increase per-tenant burst allowance and add smoothing on refill for the dedicated pool. Deployed behind feature flag for NimbleDocs only.

comments.author: NimbleDocs (Customer)
comments.body: Confirmed drop in transient 429 spikes after the flag was applied; reporting normalization during next rolling deploy. Customer requested a recommended long-term config and SDK guidance.

comments.author: Miguel Torres (SRE)
comments.body: Marked follow-ups for console UI to expose burst settings and to add a guidance doc for SDK backoff best-practices.

title: Clarify burst-credit accounting for transient signed-url multipart uploads mixed with priority-weighted streaming
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports recurring 429s and quota exhaustion during mixed workload: a fleet of short-lived pre-signed multipart upload workers (for vector ingestion) running concurrently with low-latency priority streaming (interactive sessions). They believe burst credits are being charged twice or applied in the wrong window.

Impact:
- Intermittent 429 responses on ingestion clients causing backfill stalls (throughput drop ~35%).
- Interactive users see elevated latency or request rejections during concurrent heavy multipart uploads.
- Customer on Dedicated pool; impacting production search indexing and user-facing queries.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool (LuminaSearch), region: us-east, prod
- Ingest workers use short-lived signed URLs to upload chunks to object store and then POST completion to /v1/embeddings/batch
- Concurrent low-latency streaming sessions (websocket) use...

title: Unexpected model pin reset during regional canary expansion due to health-probe header rewrite
body:
Issue summary:
During a staged canary expansion for Finlytics (enterprise tenant) traffic was unexpectedly routed to the degraded fallback variant (opt-7B-quant) despite the tenant having an explicit model pin set to open-7B-v2. Impact: user-facing quality regressions and a short billing spike due to heavier quantized variant usage.

Impact:
- Several hundred requests observed returning lower-quality responses for ~18 minutes for sessions that had previously been pinned.
- Customer reported multiple production incidents and opened a support call.

Environment:
- Prod, us-east primary; canary slice expanding to eu-west.
- Dedicated capacity pool (enterprise).

Steps to reproduce (observed):
1) Tenant has an active explicit pin to redwood/open-7B-v2 (cookie+X-Model-Pin header in Client).
2) Canary expansion triggered across regions; sidecar health probes roll out to new edge proxies.
3)...

comments.author: Maya Chen ()
comments.body: Customer alerted via phone about QPS shift and quality regression. Attached request ids and sample transcripts.

comments.author: Diego Alvarez (SRE)
comments.body: Initial triage — observed cohort-health anomaly coincident with canary expansion window. Raised hypothesis that health-probe normalization may be stripping custom pin header.

comments.author: Control Plane
comments.body: Confirmed router decision logs showing probe-unhealthy path. Added instrumentation flag to log incoming header sets for suspect probes.

comments.author: Diego Alvarez (SRE)
comments.body: Deployed temporary rule to preserve X-Model-Pin on probe flows and kicked a targeted reassert of pin for affected tenant. Customer confirmed improvement; quality metrics returned to baseline.

comments.author: Maya Chen ()
comments.body: Customer requested permanent mitigation and follow-up postmortem.

- Customer reported multiple production incidents and opened a support call.

Environment:
- Prod, us-east primary; canary slice expanding to eu-west.
- Dedicated capacity pool (enterprise).

Steps to reproduce (observed):
1) Tenant has an active explicit pin to redwood/open-7B-v2 (cookie+X-Model-Pin header in Client).
2) Canary expansion triggered across regions; sidecar health probes roll out to new edge proxies.
3)...

title: Scheduled alert digest emails strip tracehook query params with leading underscore after retention rollover
body:
Issue summary: Several customers (first reported by Nimbus Analytics) have reported that scheduled alert digest emails generated right after a retention rollover strip specific tracehook query parameters that begin with an underscore (e.g., _trace_id, _route).

Impact: Digests contain links that no longer include the required tracehook params. When users click the link from the digest the console loads but the trace context is missing, so the alert does not link to the originating trace. This breaks escalation workflows and increases investigation time for on-call engineers.

Environment: Production, runs during nightly retention rollovers and compact windows in us-east.

Steps to reproduce:
1) Have an org with scheduled alert digests configured to include tracehook links (example org: Nimbus Analytics).
2) Ensure retention compaction is scheduled to run within the same hour as the di...

title: incident-bot prod service identity missing scope/role for config diff fetcher (cards render empty)
body: Granted `config.diff:read` scope to prod slack-incident-bot service identity via RBAC binding; verified config-service diff endpoint authorization and redaction behavior in prod; confirmed audit events emitted and visible in audit stream. Config diff Slack card now renders successfully in prod.

comments.author: Miles Thornton
comments.body: Seeing this in two separate incident threads today. Staging works. Prod returns 403 from config-service. Can we confirm prod service identity scopes?

comments.body: Confirmed. Prod slack-incident-bot identity lacks `config.diff:read`. Staging has it via a newer role binding. Will add prod binding and ensure audit event wiring is enabled.

comments.body: +1 on least-privilege. Please ensure bot can only call the diff endpoint (not raw config) and that audit events include tenant + incident/thread context. Reference the incident-bot access audit doc for required fields/retention.

comments.author: Kaitlyn Nguyen
comments.body: Implemented RBAC change for prod identity + verified config-service rejects raw config reads. Coordinating with Kevin on SIEM pipeline visibility for new audit event type.

comments.body: Audit event type `incident_bot.config_diff_access` showing up in prod audit stream. Fields present: service_identity, tenant_id (hashed), incident_channel, thread_ts, endpoint, result=ALLOW/DENY. Retention policy unchanged.

comments.author: Miles Thornton
comments.body: Verified in #incidents with a known safe config change (flag toggled in test tenant): Config diff card now renders and shows redacted fields correctly. Marking resolved.

title: Ephemeral sidechain burst causing regional GPU squeeze and autoscaler undershoot
body:
Issue summary: Starting 2026-03-10 09:12 UTC we observed a sustained p99 latency spike and reduced reserved GPU attach rates in CobaltAI's dedicated pool in us-west-2. Impact: Customer reports 40-60% reduction in throughput for batched routes and 5xx spikes for streaming requests. Autoscaler did not provision additional GPUs during the event, and several tenants experienced throttling.

Environment: Dedicated pool (cust-reserved-44), production, us-west-2, customer tier=dedicated.

Steps to reproduce: 1) Customer runs multi-tenant inference with mixed short/long-streams using SDK v2. 2) Background analytics sidecar scheduled a high-frequency sample upload sweep. 3) Within minutes, batch queue lengths grew and attach latency to GPUs increased. 4) Autoscaler cooldown prevented new allocations for ~6 minutes.

Observed behavior: Sudden burst of small sidecar uploads coincided with a larg...

comments.author: | Aisha Patel ()
comments.body: Notified SRE after initial triage; provided customer request IDs and sample SDK traces. Asked for early signal if this maps to known allocator regressions.

comments.author: | Mateo Alvarez (SRE)
comments.body: Observed NVMe IO wait spike (hosted telemetry). Prefetcher and analytics sidecar generated a 3k/s file upload burst from the customer's infra. Autoscaler window is set to 60s smoothing; detected a short spike and suppressed scale-up due to hysteresis. Captured allocator timeline in /confluence/runbooks/dedicated-allocator-debug.

comments.author: | Jonas Reed ()
comments.body: Reproduced attach latency with a local loadgen simulating small-stream fanout + NVMe stress. Hypothesis: sidecar upload threads saturate host IO and induce GPU driver attach latency; autoscaler rules need an additional 'attach_latency' metric to trigger earlier scale-up.

comments.author: | CobaltAI (Customer)
comments.body: Confirmed they rolled a temporary throttling policy and saw immediate improvement in throughput. Asked for permanent mitigation and guidance to avoid future regressions.

comments.author: | Jonas Reed ()
comments.body: Deployed temporary autoscaler rule change to lower cooldown from 60s to 30s for this reserved pool; monitoring for noise amplification. Planning a hotfix to add attach_latency to autoscaler feature gating.

title: Intermittent 401s for service principals after ingress SNI host-rewrite causes tenant claim misrouting in private deployment
body:
Issue summary: Several service principals (machine-to-machine tokens) started receiving intermittent 401 responses when calling the Redwood Private ingestion endpoint behind the customer's edge proxy. The failures began after the customer's ops team rolled out a host-rewrite rule on their SNI-terminating proxy.

Impact: Authentication fails for automated jobs that call Redwood's admin/service APIs. Approximately 6% of requests from BlueWave's ingest workers fail with 401 over the past 8 hours, causing batch jobs to abort and delaying data pipelines. No user-facing impact reported, but multiple downstream workflows are impacted.

Environment: Private deployment (customer-managed control plane) in us-west region. Customer uses an external edge proxy that performs SNI termination and rewrites the Host header to route to internal tenants. Service principals authenticate using mutual TLS-b...

comments.author: Ava Chen (2026-03-10 08
comments.body: 02): Opened ticket after customer report. Gathered initial logs and asked for proxy config snippets and a pcap of TLS handshake if possible.

comments.author: BlueWave Ops (2026-03-10 09
comments.body: 20): Provided proxy rewrite rules and confirmed they began a staging-to-prod rollout at 06:45 UTC. Attached example NGINX config and clarified they use SNI position-based routing for tenant segmentation.

comments.author: Marcus Lee - SRE (2026-03-10 10
comments.body: 15): Reproduced a failing request in our lab by emulating host rewrite. Observed tenant resolution map choosing an internal tenant alias when Host != SNI. Added temporarily to in-progress.

comments.author: Priya Kapoor - Auth (2026-03-11 11
comments.body: 40): Investigating token binding codepath — found parsing order prefers forwarded host header for tenant scoping when X-Redwood-Tenant is present, but SNI-derived tenant mapping is derived earlier and can be overwritten by host header rewrite leading to incorrect resolved tenant.

comments.author: BlueWave Ops (2026-03-11 12
comments.body: 05): Rolled back host-header rewrite as interim mitigation; reported immediate reduction in 401s (observed 0.2% failures after rollback).

title: Geo cutover: variant affinity mismatch causes split request routing for dedicated tenant
body: Issue summary: During a scheduled geo cutover for a dedicated customer (Peregrine HealthTech) we observed a subset of requests from the tenant being routed to a legacy variant despite an explicit model version pin. Impact: ~12% of requests for the tenant in us-west were answered by redwood-chat-v2.1-legacy (legacy weights) resulting in higher latency and tokenization mismatch with customer post-processing, causing errors in their pipeline. Environment: Production dedicated cluster (us-west) on committed capacity. Canary weight adjustment and region handoff occurred as part of a planned cutover at 2026-03-10T03:00Z. Observed behavior: After the cutover we see session-level split routing: some streams maintain the pinned variant while others are diverted to an older variant. Expected behavior: Tenant-level explicit pin should ensure steady routing to pinned model across the cutover wind...

Expected behavior: Tenant-level explicit pin should ensure steady routing to pinned model across the cutover wind...

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: 12Z]: Customer reports ~12% of requests failing post-cutover. Collected logs and traces attached. Escalating to SRE.

comments.author: Diego Martinez
comments.body: 35Z]: Initial triage suggests epoch visibility lag. I pushed an immediate pin-refresh broadcast (control-plane API) to all edge nodes; customer reports a drop in legacy responses to <1%.

comments.author: Peregrine - Ravi Patel
comments.body: 00Z]: After pin-refresh the downstream pipeline still saw occasional schema mismatches, but the error rate dropped. We need assurance this won't recur on future cutovers.

comments.author: Maya Singh
comments.body: 20Z]: We're reproducing locally with staged snapshot latency. Adding a commit quorum check to ensure explicit pins land before weight adjustments. Will open PR for coordinated commit barrier.

comments.author: Diego Martinez
comments.body: 05Z]: Rolled temporary control-plane patch to hold canary weight changes until pin commit is observed by >=90% of edge nodes. Monitoring for regressions.

title: Phased offboarding for senior engineer: partial SSO deprovision delay, expiring bridge service-account, and corporate mobile return logistics
body:
Issue summary: Senior SWE (employee# 92014) is transitioning from full-time to a 6-week contractor bridge starting 2026-03-15. HR requests a phased offboarding rather than immediate deprovisioning because the engineer will complete two critical rollups: (1) a code-owner handoff for the inference scheduler repo and (2) a multi-region migration playbook.

Impact: Immediate full deprovisioning would block final rollouts and risk a regression in production. Delaying some access increases risk surface and requires controlled, auditable exceptions (temporary service account + expiring keys).

Request: 1) Keep SSO and repo write access for two weeks (until 2026-03-29) but remove access to billing consoles and dedicated GPU lab immediately. 2) Create a temporary bridge service-account scoped to the inference-scheduler repo and the release pipeline with keys that expire 2026-03-29. 3) Reassign...

title: Procure evaluation seats for ML Feature Store vendor with SSO, BYOK KMS, and compliance evidence
body:
Issue summary:
We need to procure a 30-day evaluation for an ML Feature Store product (vendor: FeatherStore) for the Feature Engineering and Data Platform teams. Evaluation requires 12 concurrent seats, SAML SSO + SCIM provisioning, BYOK integration with AWS CloudHSM, and EU data residency assurance for storage and logs. Security must provide SOC2 Type II report and completed security questionnaire (SIG). Procurement needs a PO estimate, billing cost center tag, and contract DPA addendum.

Impact:
- Enables Data Platform to validate feature storage, lineage, and online store latency before committing to Dedicated procurement.
- Blocking Data Science model rollouts that depend on low-latency feature lookups.
- Timeline: evaluation must be completed within 30 days to feed Q2 roadmap decision.

Environment / Scope:
- Staging account in eu-west region.
- 12 seats for engineers (data scien...

title: Intermittent VPN/DNS failures to private deployment test environment
body: Issue summary: Engineers intermittently cannot reach internal private deployment test hosts over VPN from remote office and home networks. Failures present as DNS resolution timeouts or long tail connection drops to internal staging endpoints (10.240.x.x) used for on-call tests. Impact: Blocking some end-to-end tests and manual QA for private deployment workflows; intermittent but reproducible during high-traffic windows. Environment: staging - us-east; private deployment cluster (on-prem lab) reachable via corporate VPN. Steps to reproduce: 1) Connect to corporate VPN from a remote laptop (home WiFi or office guest). 2) Attempt curl https://staging-private.internal.redwood: read timeout ~10s observed or "could not resolve host". 3) Retry; sometimes succeeds after 30-90s. Logs / observations: - Several clients show DNS NXDOMAIN or SERVFAIL at time of failure. - Local resolver on VPN g...

title: Console alert duplication and incorrect attribution after hourly retention compaction
body:
Issue summary:
Customer KappaHealth reported multiple duplicate alert notifications for the same console rule over a four-hour window after our hourly retention compaction job ran. Alerts were emitted with different attribution metadata (org/route/model) and caused duplicated paging to their on-call.

Impact:
- Several duplicate P1 alerts sent to KappaHealth on-call (SMS + PagerDuty) between 02:00–06:00 UTC on 2026-03-10.
- Noise led to escalation and temporary disruption of their incident response.
- Affects alert evaluation and attribution reports in Console for orgs with high-cardinality tags.

Environment:
- Production (us-east-1), console and alerting clusters behind ALB.
- Retention compaction job v2.3.1 (daily/hourly rollups) ran at 02:00 UTC.

Steps to reproduce (internal):
1) On a high-cardinality org with many tag permutations, enable a per-route 5m error-rate alert with 1m ...

comments.author: (Ava)
comments.body: Customer reported duplicate alerts. Collected org id and sample alert ids. Escalated to SRE for investigation.

comments.author: SRE (Diego)
comments.body: Reproduced locally with a synthetic high-cardinality load. Saw duplicate alert evals around compaction boundary. Suspect compaction produces transient overlapping timebuckets used by evaluation engine.

comments.author: (Priya, alerting)
comments.body: Confirmed compaction v2.3.1 writes compacted timebucket keys in place but does not update in-flight evaluation cache; causes duplicate eval paths on next evaluation cycle.

comments.author: (Ava) to customer
comments.body: Provided temporary mute guidance and request to postpone compaction during their peak hours. Customer accepted temporary mitigation while we work on a fix.

comments.author: (Diego)
comments.body: Implemented a hypotheses test: added a per-transaction guard to dedupe evaluations by alert fingerprint and timebucket. Observed reduced duplicates in staging. Preparing PR for hotfix.

comments.author: (Diego)
comments.body: Hotfix PR opened and running integration tests. Requesting on-call approval for a canary deploy to prod at low-traffic window (03:00 UTC).

title: Delegated access approval hierarchy blocking console elevation for engineers after Okta group merge
body:
Issue summary:
A delegated access request for console elevation is stuck in the approval workflow for multiple engineers after an Okta group merge. The approval UI shows 'Pending - waiting for approver' even though required approvers are listed and have approved. This prevents engineers from obtaining temporary elevated roles to access production dashboards and runbooks.

Impact:
- 6 engineers are blocked from elevating to a support role across multiple teams (SRE, Platform).
- On-call rotation had to manually escalate via Slack and temporary token sharing, increasing operational risk.
- No customer-facing outages, but internal response time for incidents increased.

Environment:
- Production (us-east)
- Affected service: approval-microservice v1.3.2, rbac-service v2.0.1, okta-scim-sync job (daily)

Steps to reproduce:
1) Create a delegated access request via Console -> Access -> Requ...

title: Main-thread GC starvation in session-sidecar causing cascading 503s and api-gateway retries
body:
Issue summary:
Customers observed a sudden rise in 5xx responses (primarily 503 / upstream unavailable) from the hosted API in us-west during high-concurrency streaming workloads. Error wave started 2026-03-13 02:12 UTC and lasted ~45 minutes with intermittent recovery windows.

Impact:
- LexiDocs (dedicated tenant) reported ~35% of streaming sessions failing with 503s and mid-request disconnects.
- Platform-wide p99 tail latency for text-generation increased from 110ms -> 1.8s for affected hosts.
- API gateway showed elevated retry rates causing localized threadpool exhaustion.

Environment:
prod/us-west, dedicated pool for LexiDocs. Session-sidecars (Python 3.11) present on each node to manage streaming sessions and multipart uploads. Serving runtime (Rust) remained mostly healthy on GPU hosts.

Timeline / Observed signals:
- 02:10 UTC: LexiDocs bulk streaming load begins (concurren...

title: Okta SAML session affinity breaks cross-account console switching
body: Issue summary: Several engineers and on-call have reported that when they use the console's 'Switch account' feature after authenticating via Okta SAML, they are immediately redirected back to the Okta login flow or see a 401 in the console even though Okta indicates an active session. This appears to be a session affinity / cookie routing problem that surfaces only when switching between org accounts (cross-account switch) or when requests are routed to different backends behind the auth-proxy.

This appears to be a session affinity / cookie routing problem that surfaces only when switching between org accounts (cross-account switch) or when requests are routed to different backends behind the auth-proxy.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Initial report from Jacob M. — multiple engineers hitting 401 when switching orgs. Attached console HAR and screenshots.

comments.author: Marcus Patel
comments.body: I can reproduce; adding traces and checking haproxy. Suspect affinity. Pinning as P1.

comments.author: Sofia Martinez
comments.body: Found auth-proxy change that touched SameSite. Creating a PR to add memcache fallback for RelayState. See PR #842.

comments.author: Marcus Patel
comments.body: Rolled back lb change that coincided with spike. Partial improvement, but still seeing intermittent failures in us-east. Starting canary sticky header.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Users on-call report much better behavior after canary; continuing monitoring.

comments.author: Ethan Rao
comments.body: Added automated test scenario to console E2E that simulates cross-account switch across multiple backends (test id: e2e-switch-104).

title: NCCL stall suspected in multi-GPU (TP) replica — possible collective mismatch / stream ordering issue
body:
Issue summary
Acme AI is seeing intermittent request hangs/timeouts on their Dedicated cluster when running multi-GPU tensor-parallel replicas (A100 80GB, TP=4). During the affected windows, we captured NCCL debug output indicating a suspected collective mismatch / out-of-order collective sequence across ranks. Symptoms align with “silent stall” scenarios: GPU utilization drops to near-idle on one or more ranks, in-flight requests stop making progress, and the API layer eventually returns timeouts.

Customer impact
- Streaming chat requests hang (no tokens emitted) and then time out.
- p99 latency spikes + elevated 504s from gateway for the affected route.
- Incident-level impact during peak hours; Acme AI has implemented a temporary concurrency cap but is still seeing sporadic stalls under burst.

Environment
- Deployment: Dedicated (customer VPC peering)
- Region: us-east
- Hardware...
