Issue summary
Acme AI is seeing intermittent request hangs/timeouts on their Dedicated cluster when running multi-GPU tensor-parallel replicas (A100 80GB, TP=4). During the affected windows, we captured NCCL debug output indicating a suspected collective mismatch / out-of-order collective sequence across ranks. Symptoms align with “silent stall” scenarios: GPU utilization drops to near-idle on one or more ranks, in-flight requests stop making progress, and the API layer eventually returns timeouts.

Customer impact
- Streaming chat requests hang (no tokens emitted) and then time out.
- p99 latency spikes + elevated 504s from gateway for the affected route.
- Incident-level impact during peak hours; Acme AI has implemented a temporary concurrency cap but is still seeing sporadic stalls under burst.

Environment
- Deployment: Dedicated (customer VPC peering)
- Region: us-east
- Hardware...

comments.body: 02 UTC — Owen Phillips (Support): Created from Acme escalation + logs received. Initial read suggests collective mismatch: rank 3 advances to AllGather while rank 0 still on AllReduce (opCount skew). Looping in runtime kernels + scheduling. Linked to INC-2025-02-17-DEDICATED-TP-HANG and related tickets SUP-48321/SUP-48344.

25 UTC — Liam O'Rourke (TAM/CS liaison): Confirmed with Acme: workload is bursty, streaming enabled, and they do see client disconnects at peak (front-end retries). They can keep concurrency cap at 24 for 48 hours, but want an ETA for a stability build. Requested we provide a recommended set of env flags (NCCL debug, async error handling) that won’t materially degrade throughput.

10 UTC — Rafael Mendes (SRE): We can enable extra NCCL/runtime debug logging for a bounded canary window (1–2h) but want a rollback plan + confirmation it won’t emit sensitive payloads. Please track under INT-2219. Also: during the Feb 17 window, GPU metrics showed one rank dropping to ~0% SM while others stayed moderate, consistent with a stall at a sync boundary.

03 UTC — Grace Kim (Kernel Eng): The opCount skew is suspicious. Two common paths: (1) missing stream sync before ncclGroupEnd when upstream work is on another stream, (2) conditional collective in code (e.g., different branch taken) caused by early-exit/cancellation. Can you confirm whether these logs correspond to a period with client cancellations? If yes, please correlate request ids around 18:31–18:33 UTC.

18 UTC — Owen Phillips (Support): Correlated timestamps: there were 17 client disconnects (HTTP/2 stream resets) in the 2-minute window prior to the first observed stall. Also saw a spike in "request_cancelled" events on the same route. Added correlation CSV to attachments. This aligns with cancellation path possibly leaving ranks out of sync.

40 UTC — Hiro Tanaka (Mgr Kernels): Acknowledged. We have a hardening PR in flight around stream/event sync at NCCL boundaries (PR-21853) and a separate cancellation deadlock fix (PR-21890). For this ticket, we need to verify whether cancellation can cause ranks to skip a collective (mismatch) vs just hang waiting. We’ll run the benchmark-lab repro with induced cancels once Rina posts the harness config.

12 UTC — Yuki Sato (Kernel Eng): Early repro in lab: with mixed prefill/decode + forced cancellations at ~1–2% rate, saw a stall once in ~90 minutes on A100 TP=4. Not yet a clean deterministic repro, but traces show a stream wait preceding the NCCL call on rank 0. This points toward missing event sync (matches PR-21853 intent).

55 UTC — Aisha Bello (SRE): Draft alerting plan: heartbeat gap per rank + in-flight collective counters. Once PR-21834 and dashboard panels merge, we can page on “heartbeat gap > 30s” correlated with request timeout rate. For Acme, we’ll route alerts to SRE oncall + tag runtime oncall. Added notes to incident follow-ups.

22 UTC — Liam O'Rourke (TAM/CS liaison): Shared interim guidance with Acme: keep concurrency cap at 24; disable CUDA graphs for TP routes; schedule a 1.19 canary once approved. Acme agreed to provide another 24h window for debug logs during canary if needed, but prefers a “fix-first” approach rather than more logging.

title: SEV-2: eu-west latency spike after runtime build 2026.03.18.0 (kernel selection)
body:
Issue summary: p95 latency regression for long-context traffic in eu-west after runtime build 2026.03.18.0.

Impact: SLO burn for chat long-context bucket; customers saw slower responses but low error rate.

Environment: prod eu-west, A100 pools.

Investigation notes: Suspected kernel selection heuristic change; perf-canary shows regression aligned with build.

Mitigation: Disable heuristic via feature flag; force kernel to prior stable variant.

title: Long-lived client connections causing API gateway accept-queue starvation and upstream runtime crashloop with elevated 5xx
body:
Issue summary:
Customer QueryWell reported a rapid increase in 5xx responses starting 2026-03-12 10:20 UTC for production traffic routed to their dedicated pool. Symptoms include API gateway 502/503 spikes coincident with long-lived streaming connections from a handful of client IPs. Upstream runtimes entered crashloop/eviction cycles and GPU memory fragmentation grew steadily during the window.

Impact:
- QueryWell observed ~18% of requests returning 5xx over a 40-minute period, primarily streaming endpoints.
- Latency increased for non-streaming requests by ~30%.
- Incident impacted a single dedicated pool (customer-dedicated) — no cross-customer outage reported.

Environment:
- prod/us-east (customer dedicated capacity)
- Affected runtimes: r10-r16 in dedicated pool qwe-us-east-02
- Models in use: redwood-alpha-13b-q4 (inference with KV cache and streaming enabled)

Steps to reprod...

title: Scoped production diagnostic slice export request — privacy-minimization review
body:
Issue summary: Request from Security to extract a tightly scoped set of production inference logs (a 'slice') to investigate a reported prompt redaction anomaly from a single enterprise tenant. The extraction must minimize customer-identifiable data and follow privacy minimization principles.

Impact: Security needs the slice to validate whether a model/serving runtime change correlated with increased exposure of masked fields. Customers are not currently impacted broadly, but the tenant reported potential PII in outputs. This is time-sensitive for the incident triage.

Request details: Export up to 10k inference events for tenant 'NovaPayments' spanning 2026-02-20 to 2026-02-25, filtered to routes: /v1/chat/stream and /v1/generate. The export should include: timestamp, request_id, model_version, route, truncated prompt (first 512 chars), truncated response (first 1024 chars), redacti...

comments.body: Opening request — marking as P1 due to security triage need. Adding Privacy, Security, and SRE as approvers.

comments.author: Priya Shah - Privacy (2026-03-02)
comments.body: Please provide justification for retention >7 days. Default is 7 days; Legal must sign off for longer. Also confirm exact fields required (avoid full prompts where possible).

comments.author: Daniel Kim - (2026-03-03)
comments.body: Noted. We need to restrict export to named engineers: Marco Hernandez, Lena Ortiz, and Samir Patel. Provide list of principals for ephemeral role. Also require video recording of access session.

comments.author: Marco Hernandez (2026-03-04)
comments.body: SRE can run export once approvals are in place. Drafted export runbook and access checklist. Will run dry run in staging first and share hash mapping for verification.

comments.author: Emma Liu
comments.body: Reviewed scope. Approve 7-day retention and ephemeral access. For retention beyond 7 days, require signed customer consent or legal subpoena.

title: Okta claim encoding collision prevents RBAC provisioning for internal ops console
body:
Issue summary:
Users are denied access to the Ops Console with a 403 RBAC error even though their Okta profiles include the expected groups. The denial appears tied to group claim encoding changes introduced by last week's Okta connector upgrade (v2.7.1).

Impact:
Multiple engineering and SRE users cannot access runbook/incident tooling. This blocks oncall rotations and delays incident response. Affected users are primarily in us-west and include contractors and full-time staff.

Observed behavior:
- Users report 403 when opening /console/runbooks (UI) and 403 from CLI tooling that hits the same token-exchange endpoint.
- Okta shows group membership correctly (group names include em-dash and accented characters for some internal teams).
- Our identity proxy logs show group claim payloads with mixed percent-encoding and UTF-8 bytes, which the RBAC service fails to normalize.

Expected ...

comments.body: Performed manual RBAC grant for three critical responders to restore immediate access while fix is prepared.

comments.author: (L. Gomez)
comments.body: Please ensure patch preserves auditability of original claims. Recommend logging decoded value alongside redacted original for debugging.

comments.author: Miguel Torres
comments.body: Will add redaction-safe logging and coordinate canary rollout. ETA hotfix deploy 2026-03-13.

title: Gradual queue entanglement in continuous batcher causing intermittent p95/p99 long-tail waves
body:
Issue summary:
Customers on a dedicated pool (NovaHealth) observed recurring p95/p99 latency waves and occasional 504s during sustained moderate traffic (20-40 qps) for chat endpoints. Spikes happen in a repeating pattern with ~7 minute periodicity and last ~30-90s. Impacts customer production workflows (document summarization pipelines) and triggers automated retries and billing anomalies.

Impact:
- Multiple requests for a single customer returning >2s p95 and >8s p99 during spikes; some connections eventually time out with 504.
- Affects NovaHealth's critical nightly ingest and real-time chat.
- Auto-retry from SDKs increased load and worsened tail during waves.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: dp-nova-01 (us-west-2)
- Serving runtime: v1.21.3
- Continuous batcher config: linger=10ms, max_batch_tokens=4096, tenant_weighting=enabled
- KV cache: prefix-invalidation=periodic, warmup=en...

- KV cache: prefix-invalidation=periodic, warmup=en...

comments.author: | Priya Nair ()
comments.body: Triage: Customer reported p99 waves and 504s starting 2026-03-07 22:00 UTC. Assigned to SRE for deeper investigation.

comments.author: | Rafael Ortiz (SRE)
comments.body: Initial checks: node-level CPU and queue metrics show correlated resource pressure on a small subset of pod replicas; no obvious OOMs. Added continuous-batcher debug logging. Asked customer for SDK log bundle.

comments.author: | NovaHealth (Customer)
comments.body: Provided SDK logs and reproduction script. Noted that spikes align with their ingest batch windows. Asked whether model pinning could cause hot-sharding.

comments.author: | Rafael Ortiz (SRE)
comments.body: Observed periodic lockstep flushes: several tenant queues appear to align their timers and cause simultaneous flushes, creating short-lived overload. Hypothesis: stale prefix kv-cache invalidation combined with tenant-weighted coalescing causes 'queue entanglement'.

comments.author: Platform
comments.body: Running canary of a mitigation that randomizes batcher flush offsets and increases per-tenant jitter. Will collect 24h of metrics before recommending rollout.

comments.author: | Priya Nair ()
comments.body: Notified customer of ongoing mitigation test and provided temporary workaround: lower client-side parallelism to reduce impact until fix is validated.

title: Request temporary H100 quota + benchmark-runner permissions for FP8/INT8 kernel benchmarking sprint (2-week window)
body:
Requester: Talia Benson (Serving Runtime sprint PM)

Request type: Temporary quota + permissions

Business context:
Serving Runtime is running a focused FP8/INT8 kernel benchmarking sprint. We need additional H100 capacity to run the full benchmark matrix (microbench + end-to-end) with pinned driver/CUDA and GPU clocks. Current shared capacity is insufficient to complete the matrix in the sprint window without contending with other jobs.

What we need (quota/capacity):
- H100 GPU quota (temporary) for benchmark clusters only, not customer-serving pools
- Regions/clusters:
 - us-east (primary): +32x H100 GPUs for 6-hour blocks daily
 - us-west (secondary / overflow): +16x H100 GPUs for 4-hour blocks as needed
 - eu-west: optional +8x H100 GPUs for 2 runs to validate regional variance (can be deferred if constrained)
- Time window:
 - Start: 2026-02-11 09:00 PT
 - End: 2026-02-24 18:00 ...

comments.body: 14 PT — Talia Benson (Reporter): Filing internal helpdesk request to unblock sprint. Neha has approved spend; we need quota + permissions by tomorrow morning to hit the harness calibration schedule. Also requesting confirmation that benchmark nodes can run with clocks pinned (per benchmark-lab runner changes).

02 PT — Irene Choi (Assignee): Acknowledged. I can allocate additional H100s in benchmark-lab us-east and update the allowlist. Need final concurrency cap + confirmation whether eu-west is hard requirement or best-effort. Also confirming with Vanessa that this will not touch Dedicated pools.

18 PT — Talia Benson: Concurrency cap proposal: max 8 nodes per user, max 24 nodes total for fp8-int8-sprint tag in us-east; us-west overflow max 8. eu-west is best-effort (nice-to-have) for one validation run; can drop if constrained.

09 PT — Vanessa Ortiz: Confirmed: benchmark-lab pool is isolated; will add an explicit reservation in us-east + us-west and set an alert if benchmark jobs attempt to schedule on customer-serving node groups. Will also publish the allowed scheduling windows.
37 PT — Neha Kapoor: Approved for the requested window and estimated hours. Condition: hard isolation from Dedicated pools and daily usage report during the sprint (high-level spend + hours).

22 PT — Irene Choi: Implementing quota increase now. Plan: us-east +32 H100 GPUs under benchmark-lab; us-west +16. eu-west remains pending capacity check; will follow up EOD. Adding allowlist entries for Grace / Rina / Isabella + svc-benchmark-lab-runner. Expiration set for 2026-02-24 18:00 PT (automatic revert).

08 PT — Irene Choi: Completed. Quota live in us-east + us-west. Allowlist updated. Clock pinning enabled on benchmark-lab nodes (validated on a canary node). Shared details + usage-tag requirement in #eng-infra and #eng-runtime. eu-west capacity limited this week; can revisit for 1 run next week if needed.
51 PT — Talia Benson: Confirmed runner jobs are scheduling successfully in us-east. Thanks. We will send daily usage summaries to Neha/Vanessa via the sprint channel.

26 PT — Irene Choi: Mid-sprint check: utilization within approved range; no scheduling attempts observed on customer pools. Reminder: quota expires automatically on 02/24 18:00 PT; open a follow-up if an extension becomes necessary.
40 PT — Talia Benson: All set. No extension expected. Closing ticket.

title: Control-plane pin swap during maintenance caused sporadic cross-region egress and auth-retry bursts for dedicated tenant
body:
Issue summary:
Customer observed a spike in end-to-end latency and an increase in auth retries immediately after a scheduled control-plane maintenance window that included a tenant residency pin swap.

Impact:
- Multiple requests from Acme Analytics (dedicated tenant) reported p95 increase from ~120ms to ~900ms and p99 bursts up to 3.2s for text-gen endpoints.
- Batch embedding jobs (off-peak) saw 4–6x token-cost increase due to repeated retries and multi-hop egress.
- Customer reported transient 401 and 502 errors correlated with the time window. No data loss reported.

Environment:
- Tenant: Acme Analytics (dedicated GPU pool)
- Primary residency: eu-west
- Control-plane maintenance window: 2026-03-10 03:00–03:20 UTC
- SDK: redwood-js v3.2.0
- Observed from multiple client regions (eu and APAC) via customer logs.

Steps to reproduce (customer-provided):
1) Send steady stream of smal...

comments.body: 25 - Samira Patel (Support): Acknowledged and asked customer for request IDs and token TTLs. Requested sample SDK logs and timestamps. Escalated to SRE and control-plane on-call.

10 - Diego Morales (SRE): Initial triage: observed pin update loop from control-plane services during maintenance window. Correlated with edges in us-east temporarily accepting requests for tid-ACME. Asked infra to pull edge TLS and auth logs for the 03:00-03:10 window.
30 - Customer (Acme Analytics): Provided request IDs, SDK debug logs, and noticed same behavior from two different VPCs. Attached p95/p99 charts from their telemetry.

00 - Diego Morales (SRE): Found ~18 control-plane writes for the tenant over a 90s window (expected 1 write). Hypothesis: control-plane maintenance task retried pin writes due to transient DB timeouts, which caused pin-ttl churn and edge affinity refreshes.

45 - Product Ops: Noted that maintenance run involved a config deploy that bumped the pin-touch interval. Rolling back that config fixed the immediate churn in a canary cluster. Running a controlled replay to validate fix.

30 - Samira Patel (Support): Shared temporary mitigation with customer: enable strict-residency-mode for tenant or schedule maintenance off their peak window. Customer applied strict-residency and reported p95 returned to baseline within 8 minutes.
00 - Diego Morales (SRE): Follow-up: creating INT ticket to add circuit-breaker to control-plane pin writes and add telemetry alert on >3 writes/min for a single tenant during maintenance.
20 - Customer (Acme Analytics): Requested timeline for permanent fix and asked for any configuration guidance to avoid similar issues in future.

title: Dashboard permissions: CS blocked from viewing Incident Taxonomy dashboards (incident_type/owner breakdown)
body:
Issue summary
- CS team members cannot access the new Incident Taxonomy dashboards (volume by incident_type/owner, MTTR/SLA breach panels).
- Errors observed:
 - Grafana: "Access denied" / "You do not have permission to view this dashboard"
 - Console (ops dashboards embed): dashboard panel fails to load; API returns 403 for incident taxonomy endpoints.

Impact
- CS cannot answer customer questions during escalations ("what kind of incident is this?", "which team owns it?", "are we within remediation SLA?") without asking SRE/Eng to screenshot or paste data.
- Blocks rollout of the comms workflow described in the incident taxonomy program (CS is supposed to use incident_type + owner to pick templates and escalation steps).
- Affects at least 8 users in Customer Success/Support (reported by Rachel K. + Owen P.).

Who is affected
- Customer Success / Support (CS) viewers who should have...

comments.author: Dev Patel
comments.body: Creating internal helpdesk bug. CS needs read-only access to incident taxonomy dashboards for escalations. Rachel K. reports she’s blocked; Owen P. sees same. This is blocking the new comms workflow. Can someone from Observability/Dashboards take a look?

comments.author: Sergio Costa
comments.body: Taking. Quick check: the dashboards live under the "SRE/Oncall" Grafana folder with folder-level restrictions to on-call groups. We also have a Console embed path that queries incident taxonomy endpoints; likely a separate permissions gate. Will coordinate with Allison (Obs standards) + Platform for scopes.

comments.author: Allison Grant
comments.body: Confirmed: folder permissions are too tight (only SRE and Eng-Oncall). For v1 we agreed CS can view incident_type/owner but not edit. Suggest adding Okta group "Customer-Success" as Viewer to the "Incident Taxonomy" folder only (not the entire ops folder). We should also ensure queries don’t expose any sensitive tenant identifiers.

comments.author: Sergio Costa
comments.body: Implemented Grafana folder split: moved taxonomy dashboards into a new folder "Incident Taxonomy (Read-only)" and granted Viewer access to groups: SRE, Eng-Oncall, Customer-Success. Left the rest of ops dashboards restricted. Need CS validation.

comments.author: Dylan Brooks
comments.body: Re: Console embed 403. The ops Console API endpoint for incident taxonomy is behind internal scope "ops:incidents.read". CS has "cs:incidents.read" only. Proposed: allow CS group to call ops endpoint but with filtered fields (no internal-only annotations). Alternatively expose a CS-safe endpoint. For speed, adding CS group to ops:incidents.read with field-level filtering seems safest.

comments.author: Sergio Costa
comments.body: Patched API permissions: CS can call incident taxonomy read endpoints; confirmed response excludes internal-only fields (incident commander notes, private links). Added audit log entry for scope grant. Waiting on CS to confirm dashboards load end-to-end (Grafana + Console embed).

comments.author: Rachel Kim
comments.body: Confirmed I can now open the "Incidents by Type / Owner (v1)" dashboard and view panels. Console embed also loads. Thanks. Please make sure new taxonomy dashboards land in the CS-readable folder going forward.

comments.author: Sergio Costa
comments.body: Closing out. Follow-up: document access model + add checklist item for new dashboards so we don’t repeat this. Also recommend adding a lightweight automated permission test for the CS role.

title: Dashboard CSV snapshot export strips row-level filters for long-range snapshots
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports that when they request a CSV snapshot export for a dashboard that includes row-level filters (RBAC-driven dataset visibility + cohort filters) over a time range greater than ~90 days, the exported CSV contains either zero rows or a dataset that ignores row-level filters (showing a global count).

Impact:
- A Tellus Health analytics team cannot reconcile exported CSVs with the dashboard numbers for monthly/quarterly reporting.
- Potential data leakage risk (exports that ignore row-level visibility) or incorrect zeroed exports for long-range snapshots.
- Blocks the customer's compliance reporting workflow; customer flagged as enterprise SLA-impacting.

Environment:
- Production account for Tellus Health (enterprise tenant) in us-east region.
- Dashboard uses nested cohort filters and organization-scoped row-level ACL via console RBAC.
- Export API called ...

comments.author: Ava Chen ()
comments.body: Created ticket after customer provided export logs and sample CSVs. Customer highly impacted; asked for immediate triage.

comments.author: Marco Diaz
comments.body: Acknowledged and requested request_id + sample CSV. Escalated to Console eng triage channel #incidents-console for hypothesis on export pipeline.

comments.author: Priya Nair
comments.body: Noted a potential predicate truncation behavior in scheduler when filter payload > 30KB for long windows. Requested job logs and full export payload. Assigned INT-4221 to investigate internal export worker behavior.

comments.author: SRE (Liam O'Connor)
comments.body: Replicated locally using a synthetic tenant with large combined cohort filters and 180-day window. Observed "predicate_truncation" in exporter and fallback to null filters for serialization performance. Opening PR to increase predicate buffer and add defensive checks.

comments.author: Marco Diaz
comments.body: Informed customer of progress and provided temporary split-range workaround. Customer accepted but requested target ETA for a proper fix and added this to their internal compliance timeline.

comments.author: Priya Nair
comments.body: PR #1185 opened that ensures filter predicates are stored as compressed blobs when threshold exceeded and preserves row-level filters. Running CI + staging rollout smoke tests.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Notified customer that code change is queued for hotfix; asked customer to hold on bulk exports until confirmation.

title: Dedicated H100 pool throughput drop during FP8/INT8 benchmark window (capacity contention)
body:
Issue summary:
- Customer on Dedicated (Summit AI) reported a sudden throughput drop (tokens/sec) and elevated queueing during a scheduled window.
- We correlated the drop with internal FP8/INT8 benchmark jobs that inadvertently consumed capacity in the same H100 node pool used for the customer’s Dedicated deployment.

Customer impact:
- Time window: 2026-02-16 02:10–03:05 UTC
- Symptoms: lower sustained throughput, increased p95 latency, intermittent request timeouts at peak concurrency.
- Scope: Summit AI Dedicated H100 pool in us-east.

Environment:
- Dedicated cluster/pool: dedicated-prod-use1-h100
- Region: us-east

Detection:
- Customer email to Support + Console screenshots showing throughput reduction.
- SRE saw increased scheduler queue depth for the dedicated namespace and elevated GPU utilization from non-dedicated workloads.

Initial hypothesis:
- Noisy-neighbor / capacity...

- Region: us-east

Detection:
- Customer email to Support + Console screenshots showing throughput reduction.
- SRE saw increased scheduler queue depth for the dedicated namespace and elevated GPU utilization from non-dedicated workloads.

Initial hypothesis:
- Noisy-neighbor / capacity...

comments.body: 24 UTC — Rafael Mendes (SRE): Created ticket after on-call page from Dedicated throughput alarm + customer report. Seeing queue depth rising in dedicated-prod-use1-h100 while GPU util ~98–100%. Error rate stable; this looks like contention rather than crash.

31 UTC — Vanessa Ortiz (Infra): Confirmed: benchmark-lab jobs are present in the dedicated-prod-use1-h100 cluster. These should have been pinned to bench-use1-h100. Suspect runner config regression / missing guardrail.

38 UTC — Irene Choi (Fleet): Capacity reservation for sprint was approved, but the isolation relied on correct cluster targeting. There’s no hard admission control preventing non-dedicated namespaces from landing on dedicated node pools right now.

42 UTC — Vanessa Ortiz (Infra): Immediate mitigation started: cordon + drain nodes running benchmark jobs; deleting benchmark-lab workloads in dedicated cluster; re-submitting to bench cluster. ETA 10–15 mins for perf to recover.
55 UTC — Rafael Mendes (SRE): Metrics improving. Queue depth returning to baseline; p95 latency trending down. No further timeouts in the last 5 mins.

07 UTC — Support (internal note, owner Rafael Mendes): Sent customer update: identified internal capacity contention, stopped conflicting jobs, performance recovering; offered to provide incident summary and discuss safeguards.

18 UTC — Talia Benson (Program Mgmt): Flagging as sprint-impacting: benchmark runners must not target Dedicated pools. Adding action item for infra scheduling guardrails + runner preflight validation before continuing full matrix runs.

04 UTC — Vanessa Ortiz (Infra): Proposed guardrails:
1) Dedicated node pools tainted + only dedicated namespaces have tolerations.
2) Admission controller policy: deny benchmark-lab namespace in dedicated-prod clusters.
3) Bench runner preflight: require explicit --cluster=bench-* and validate against allowlist.
4) Reservation calendar enforcement: automated check to avoid collisions with Dedicated autoscaling windows.
Owners: Infra + SRE. Target: within 48 hrs.

22 UTC — Rafael Mendes (SRE): Customer confirmed performance normalized. Marking incident resolved; keeping follow-ups tracked under internal infra tasking (see linked INT-9923 for access/quota + guardrail changes).

title: Console route experiment results appear stale / not updating for active experiment (aggregation + propagation lag suspected)
body:
Customer reports that the new Console "Route Experiments" results dashboard is not updating (appears stuck on the same deltas/graphs) even though traffic is flowing to both variants.

Issue summary:
- Console results view for an active route experiment shows stale charts/metrics (latency/cost/error deltas unchanged for ~45–90 minutes).
- Customer validated traffic is being split (they see variant headers in responses).

Impact:
- Customer cannot make ramp/stop decisions from Console due to stale guardrail readouts.
- Increased risk of running an experiment longer than intended / missing regression.

Customer context:
- Account: Acme AI (beta design partner)
- Experiment: route-level A/B test (A=baseline, B=model swap) on production route.
- Customer expectations: near-real-time results (they assumed <5–10 min lag).

Environment:
- Prod
- Regions observed: us-east and eu-west

What we ...

- Customer validated traffic is being split (they see variant headers in responses).

Impact:

- Increased risk of running an experiment longer than intended / missing regression.

Customer context:
- Account: Acme AI (beta design partner)
- Experiment: route-level A/B test (A=baseline, B=model swap) on production route.
- Customer expectations: near-real-time results (they assumed <5–10 min lag).

Environment:
- Prod
- Regions observed: us-east and eu-west

What we ...

comments.body: 18 PT — Marissa Cole (Reporter): Customer (Acme AI) says results dashboard hasn’t moved since ~07:30 PT. They’re seeing variant headers in their own logs. Asking if data is delayed or stuck.

33 PT — Sean Gallagher (SRE leadership): Please capture (a) experiment_id, (b) route_id normalization form, (c) regions with traffic, and (d) their selected Console time range. We’ve seen rollups lag when partitions don’t advance. Also check propagation lag metric for the experiment config service.

41 PT — Support (Connor O’Brien): Sent customer quick workaround: widen time range to last 6h, hard refresh, and verify variant headers. Asked for experiment_id/route_id and timestamps of start/ramp actions.

02 PT — Logan Wright (Telemetry): Looking at dashboards. Not seeing ingestion failure, but rollup job for experiment_variant_metrics is ~62 minutes behind for eu-west partitions. us-east is ~8 minutes behind. Could explain why customer sees “frozen” deltas if most of their traffic is eu-west.

08 PT — Caleb Johnson (Metrics ingestion): Confirmed backlog in the aggregation worker queue. No crashes, but throughput dropped after we added dimensions last week. CPU saturation on one worker pool. We can scale workers and reprocess the missing partitions.

17 PT — Rafael Mendes (Runbooks/alerts): Propagation lag metric looks okay (<90s p95) for config distribution in us-east; eu-west has occasional spikes (up to ~9m) but not enough to explain 60m staleness alone. This smells more like analytics delay than control-plane propagation.

25 PT — Chloe Martin (Console backend): Console results API caches responses for 2 minutes max, so this isn’t front-end caching. If rollups lag, results API will return older windows with no explicit “data freshness” today. We should add a freshness indicator banner for beta users.

46 PT — Logan Wright (Telemetry): Scaled aggregation workers + restarted one stuck consumer. Lag is dropping: eu-west now ~28 minutes behind and trending down. Expect caught up within ~20–30 minutes if traffic stays stable.

12 PT — Support (Connor O’Brien): Updated customer: identified analytics rollup delay (eu-west) causing stale results; mitigation in progress. Provided workaround: use per-request variant headers for immediate verification + check Grafana guardrail dashboard if they need near-real-time signals while Console catches up.

55 PT — Logan Wright (Telemetry): eu-west rollup lag now ~6 minutes behind. Console should start reflecting changes as next windows materialize. Also seeing slightly elevated attribution drop for streaming retries earlier in the morning; likely related to SUP-24877 path, but primary issue here was rollup backlog.
20 PT — Support (Connor O’Brien): Customer confirmed results started updating again ~12:10 PT yesterday. They requested an explicit freshness indicator + an “as of” timestamp in Console.

05 PT — Logan Wright (Telemetry): Follow-up actions filed internally: add alerting on rollup lag threshold for experiment datasets; add Console UI “Data freshness” indicator and surface rollup lag (read-only). Marking support ticket resolved.

title: Initial byte delay observed during chat handshake after SSL negotiation and serving runtime update
body:
Issue summary: Customers report elevated time-to-first-token (TTFB) for chat completions beginning immediately after a serving runtime update and concurrent SSL/TLS healthcheck change. Impact: Multiple enterprise customers (first report from HyperDocs Inc.) experienced 5–15s delays for the first token on new chat sessions; some requests hit client-side timeouts. Scope: observed across rw-3.5-chat and redwood-3b-quant variants in us-east-1.

Observed behavior: - New chat sessions show large gap between TCP/TLS handshake completion and the first byte emitted by upstream (server-side). - 95th percentile TTFB spiked from ~450ms to 6.2s starting at 2026-03-13T15:12Z. - No increase in token generation time once streaming begins.

Expected behavior: First token emitted within typical TTFB envelope (<1s p95) for pinned model/variant workloads.

comments.body: 00Z - Maya Patel (Support): Customer (HyperDocs) reported multiple 5–15s first-token delays; provided request IDs and timestamps. Escalated to SRE/Eng.
18Z - Aisha Rahman (SRE): Confirmed correlation with rollout. Implemented temporary routing change to pin HyperDocs to previous runtime variant. Observed immediate improvement.
05Z - Daniel Kim (HyperDocs, customer): Confirmed degraded sessions returned to normal after support's pinning work. Requesting root cause and ETA for permanent fix.
12Z - Luca Bianchi (Eng): Able to reproduce in local harness; identified blocking path in connection acquire. Preparing PR for non-blocking fallback and improved metrics.
40Z - Ethan Chen (Assignee): Coordinating controlled rollback of TLS healthcheck flag for remaining clusters to reduce blast radius while Eng finalizes patch.

title: Request: Append-only signed audit stream with sequence proofs plus SIEM field mapping and monthly retention attestations
body:
Issue summary:
Customer requests a machine-verifiable, append-only audit export for legal review and SIEM ingestion. They need sequence proofs and detached signatures per archive so auditors can verify immutability and ordering.

Impact:
Aurelia Health requires cryptographic proof of log immutability plus a field-level mapping for their SIEM. Delay blocks their SOC attestation and quarterly compliance milestone.

Environment:
- Redwood hosted API (prod, us-east)
- Customer tenant: aurelia-prod (enterprise)

Customer request (detailed):
1) A daily export (UTC) of all tenant-scoped audit events for March 2026 in JSONL, delivered to the customer's SFTP endpoint.
2) Each daily archive must be accompanied by:
 - A detached signature (PKCS7 or OpenPGP) OR an HMAC-SHA256 manifest signed with a per-customer KMS key.
 - A sequence manifest listing the start and end sequence numbers for that da...

comments.author: Monica Patel
comments.body: Thanks for the request, Priya — we've triaged this as P1 for Aurelia Health. Assigning to eng to validate current export capability and whether signing is supported.

comments.author: Ethan Rivera
comments.body: Investigating. I can confirm daily JSONL exports are available in the tenant's export bucket. Detached signatures are not currently produced by the export job. Need to check KMS signing workflow and whether we can attach prev_hash chaining. Marking as Blocked for security review.

comments.author: Lina Chen
comments.body: We have a KMS key for tenant-scoped signing, but export pipeline does not call Sign API today. Security requests design review before enabling key usage. I'll coordinate with Eng to scope impact.

comments.author: Aurelia SOC - Jessica Moreno
comments.body: Can you provide at least a sample gzipped file and a signed manifest for one day? Our external auditor starts next week and we need POC artifacts ASAP.

comments.author: Ethan Rivera
comments.body: Accepted. Will deliver a one-day gzipped JSONL sample and a manually-generated manifest signed by our security team's OpenPGP key by 2026-03-13 18:00 UTC as a temporary measure. Tracking full pipeline change as follow-up.

comments.author: Monica Patel
comments.body: Updated SLA date to 2026-03-12 per customer urgency and agreed POC timeline.

Customer requests a machine-verifiable, append-only audit export for legal review and SIEM ingestion. They need sequence proofs and detached signatures per archive so auditors can verify immutability and ordering.

Impact:
Aurelia Health requires cryptographic proof of log immutability plus a field-level mapping for their SIEM. Delay blocks their SOC attestation and quarterly compliance milestone.

Environment:
- Redwood hosted API (prod, us-east)
- Customer tenant: aurelia-prod (enterprise)

Customer request (detailed):
1) A daily export (UTC) of all tenant-scoped audit events for March 2026 in JSONL, delivered to the customer's SFTP endpoint.
2) Each daily archive must be accompanied by:
 - A detached signature (PKCS7 or OpenPGP) OR an HMAC-SHA256 manifest signed with a per-customer KMS key.
 - A sequence manifest listing the start and end sequence numbers for that da...

title: Reserved pool throughput collapse during low-traffic window caused by tenant background training job and autoscale mis-sizing
body:
Issue summary:
Customers observed a sudden drop in throughput and a spike in p95/p99 latency for requests routed to NimbusAI's reserved GPU pool during an off-peak window (approx 02:10 - 02:45 UTC). Requests returned 504s and streaming connections frequently reset.

Impact:
- NimbusAI (dedicated) reports 40-70% reduction in sustained token/sec for long-sequence inference (seq > 1024).
- Several production endpoints experienced request timeouts and retries, causing downstream rate-limiter escalations.

Environment:
- Dedicated reserved pool: prod-us-east (reservation id: rsv-78c2)
- Autoscaler policy: min_replicas=2, target_util=0.7, cooldown=300s (recently adjusted on 2026-03-08)
- Models: gptx-13b (quantized), gptx-7b

Steps to reproduce (as performed by Support/SRE):
1) Replayed customer traffic pattern (short burst followed by steady long-seq inference) against a staging copy of rs...

comments.author: Ava Chen (Customer)
comments.body: We're seeing a sudden drop in throughput and lots of 504s for our long-sequence generator around 02:15 UTC. This started without any traffic changes on our side.

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: Acknowledged. Can you confirm whether you had any background training/checkpointing jobs during that window? Also sharing first-response ETA ~20m.

comments.author: Ava Chen (Customer)
comments.body: We did trigger a scheduled checkpoint for a fine-tune job at 02:10 UTC on a separate project. Thought it would be isolated. Attaching job id and logs.

comments.author: Priya Nair
comments.body: Identified node n-usp-12 with repeated GPU page-faults and EVictions. Cordoning nodes and migrating inference pods; requesting forced scale to 6 replicas to restore capacity.

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: Forced scale completed, throughput recovered to baseline. Applying temporary mitigation while ENG reviews scheduler/taint config.

comments.author: Marco Alvarez
comments.body: PR open to include GPU memory metrics in autoscaler (see related PR #208). Planning lower cooldown for dedicated pools; will monitor for regressions.

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: Closing ticket after confirming long-term mitigations deployed and NimbusAI verified stable traffic for 72h. Customer accepted follow-up advisory.

comments.author: Ava Chen (Customer)
comments.body: Thanks, team — mitigations look good. We'll adjust our scheduled checkpoint windows and flag future training jobs to avoid reserved nodes.

title: Corporate firewall idle-probe policy terminates long-lived gRPC/control-plane sessions causing streaming interruptions in private deployment
body:
Issue summary:
Customer (FinLedger) reports intermittent user-facing streaming breaks for long-lived chat sessions in their private deployment. Breaks correlate with gRPC errors and immediate reconnect attempts from the SDK.

Impact:
- Several production chat sessions dropped every ~3 minutes for users in us-east-1.
- Affects real-time streaming responses and some control-plane heartbeats; customers see partial responses followed by reconnect.
- Customer SLA: private deployment with 24/7 support (enterprise).

Environment:
- Private deployment behind customer-managed Palo Alto NGFW (on-prem firewall + transit GW).
- Redwood control plane + model-serving endpoints over peered subnets (10.10.0.0/16).
- Observed in prod | us-east-1 only.

Steps to reproduce (observed):
1) Start long-running streaming chat session via Redwood SDK to vicuna-13b-stream pinned model.
2) Let session remain id...

comments.author: FinLedger NetOps (customer)
comments.body: Confirmed Palo Alto policy 'session-idle-timeout' set to 180s for application-inspection profile. Will test bypass rule in staging tonight.

comments.author: Diego Morales (SRE)
comments.body: Reviewed pcap. RST is sent by intermediate firewall IP 192.0.2.45. No server FIN. Hypothesis: firewall idle-probe/reset. Suggested keepalive on client or firewall exception.

comments.author: Priya Shah (CS)
comments.body: Coordinating maintenance window with FinLedger for firewall exception test on 2026-03-14 02:00 UTC.

comments.author: FinLedger NetOps (customer)
comments.body: After bypassing inspection for control-plane CIDR, streaming sessions no longer reset in the test environment. Will roll to prod later today.

comments.author: Diego Morales (SRE)
comments.body: Created INT-4382 to implement configurable TCP keepalive in private-deploy agent (link in related PR). Marking as engineering action item.

comments.author: Ava Chen ()
comments.body: Sent customer runbook excerpt recommending either keepalives or firewall exceptions. Customer acknowledged and requested final docs post-change.

title: Promotional waiver applied to next billing cycle instead of current after dedicated fallback
body:
Issue summary:
Customer BrightLearn Inc. reports a promotional waiver (30% overage credit, promo code: SPRINGENTERPRISE-30) intended to offset a disputed overage on the invoice for the current billing cycle (INV-2026-03-BLE-7301). Instead, the waiver line appeared as a provisional credit scheduled for the next invoice period, resulting in a short-term overcharge and an autopay failure for the current cycle.

Impact:
- Immediate financial impact: customer account temporarily charged $12,345.67 more than expected; autopay attempted and failed, triggering billing alerts.
- Business impact: customer procurement flagged the charge and paused integrations until billing is corrected.
- Affects BrightLearn production inference workloads in us-east and eu-west.

Environment:
- Tier: enterprise (dedicated GPU pool)
- Regions: us-east primary, eu-west fallback
- Components implicated: billing po...

comments.author: Harper Singh
comments.body: Found that dedicated-fallback window overlapped with a scheduled pricing migration job that ran at 03:00 UTC on 2026-03-02. Suspecting race between pricing-tag-update and postback assignment. Pulling logs from routing service.

comments.body: Reproduced postback entry showing promo acceptance event with future_period_id=2026-04 erroneously attached. Uploading related PR draft to fix idempotency key handling. See PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/billing-service/pull/2012

comments.author: Customer Success
comments.body: Informed BrightLearn that we have a temporary workaround: manual credit applied to the current invoice while engineering continues root-cause. Customer requested written confirmation and timeline.

comments.author: Diego Martinez (Billing)
comments.body: Applied manual invoice adjustment and triggered refund/autopay retry. Ticket remains open pending permanent fix and postmortem.

title: Interleaved 512/384 embedding payloads cause rerank score oscillation and streaming timeouts
body: Issue summary: BrightHealth AI reports production rerank results intermittently returning non-monotonic similarity rankings together with repeated streaming timeouts when using the /v1/rerank endpoint. Impact: Search relevance oscillations leading to incorrect top-k selection for user-facing recommendations and elevated request latency causing streaming client disconnects. Environment: Production (us-east) - traffic from BrightHealth's enterprise account is routed through dedicated Dedicated pool A. Observed starting ~2026-03-12 09:12 UTC after the customer's nightly batch embedding job. Hypothesis: Mixed embedding payloads (512-d and 384-d) are being sent in the same batch due to a client-side SDK regression that omits an embedding-dimension header for chunked uploads. The reranker attempts model compatibility fallback and, depending on which replica reads first, sometimes performs a...

comments.body: Acknowledged customer ticket; requested sample payloads and recent request_ids. Asked for a short window of logs and customer consent to triage in prod.

comments.author: BrightHealth - Maya Singh
comments.body: Uploaded sample payloads (s3://brighthealth-qa/batches/bh-5902) and granted access. Reported that the issue started after enabling new embedding config.

comments.author: Rajan Iyer
comments.body: Reproduced mixed-dim ingestion locally; confirmed SDK v0.9.7 bug. Applied temporary ingress validation rule for BrightHealth to reject mixed-dim in a single batch (rolled at 15:10 UTC).

comments.author: Linus Park
comments.body: PR created to add explicit 'embedding_dim' header to multipart chunking and a validation pass on client side. PR: github.com/redwood/embed/PR#1187

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Customer notified of temporary mitigation and the upcoming SDK patch. Open actions: coordinate SDK release, run post-deploy canary with BrightHealth, add server-side best-effort auto-resize path as engineering backlog item.

title: High-rate 429s for interactive chat after enabling SDK session_replay telemetry
body:
Issue summary:
Nimbus Analytics reports intermittent 429 throttles for low-latency chat sessions that started immediately after they enabled the SDK 'session_replay' telemetry feature (client-side keystroke/interaction capture). Throttles coincide with periods of apparently low interactive traffic (user idle windows) and occur across multiple user sessions.

Impact:
- Customer-facing latency and 429s on interactive chat endpoints for a subset (~3-5%) of active sessions during nightly hours and occasional daytime spikes.
- Affects multiple product users and prevents real-time typing UX for affected sessions.

Environment:
- Customer: Nimbus Analytics (enterprise) on Redwood Private (VPC) using a dedicated pool in eu-west.
- SDK: rust-sdk v1.6.2 with session_replay enabled and telemetry_endpoint configured to use same API key as production calls.
- Models in path: gpt-redwood-24k (prima...

comments.body: 42 UTC - Aisha Patel (Support): Opened case after Nimbus escalated via account manager. Collected initial SDK config and sample request ids. Customer reports issue started immediately after toggling session_replay in v1.6.2.

05 UTC - Sofia Chen (SRE): Confirmed tenant token-window exhaustion in eu-west around the reported timestamps. Correlated spikes with small telemetry POSTs from the customer's edge IP range. Rate-limiter logs show multiple short token drains from ephemeral short requests counted against the same tenant quota.

20 UTC - Tom Blake (Nimbus engineer): Provided orchestration details — they fan out SDK instances for multi-tab journeys and rely on SDK-level flush to reduce network chatter. By design telemetry uses same API key; assumed it wouldn’t be quota counted.

title: Audit log exporter retries causing duplicate events in customer SIEM (Splunk HEC)
body:
Issue summary
VectorGrid reports duplicate audit log events arriving in Splunk via Redwood audit-log-exporter. Duplicates correlate with exporter retries/timeouts. Customer is concerned about false positives in detections and increased ingest volume.

Impact
- Duplicate events in Splunk (same action appears 2–6 times)
- Detections/alerts fire multiple times for a single admin action
- Increased SIEM ingestion cost and investigation noise

Customer expectation
- At-most-once delivery OR deterministic de-duplication guidance
- Clear semantics for event_id / idempotency keys

Environment
- Customer: VectorGrid (Dedicated)
- SIEM: Splunk HEC
- Export path: Redwood audit-log-exporter -> Splunk endpoint
- Region: us-east

What we observed (initial)
- Exporter retries on 5xx and request timeout.
- Splunk HEC occasionally accepts payload but exporter times out before receiving HTTP response, ...

normalized.description:
Issue summary
VectorGrid reports duplicate audit log events arriving in Splunk via Redwood audit-log-exporter. Duplicates correlate with exporter retries/timeouts. Customer is concerned about false positives in detections and increased ingest volume.

Impact
- Duplicate events in Splunk (same action appears 2–6 times)
- Detections/alerts fire multiple times for a single admin action
- Increased SIEM ingestion cost and investigation noise

Customer expectation
- At-most-once delivery OR deterministic de-duplication guidance
- Clear semantics for event_id / idempotency keys

Environment
- Customer: VectorGrid (Dedicated)
- SIEM: Splunk HEC
- Export path: Redwood audit-log-exporter -> Splunk endpoint
- Region: us-east

What we observed (initial)
- Exporter retries on 5xx and request timeout.
- Splunk HEC occasionally accepts payload but exporter times out before receiving HTTP response, ...

comments.body: 08 PT — Dev Patel (Support): Opened ticket based on VectorGrid escalation. Customer sees duplicates in Splunk for RBAC and API key lifecycle events; started after enabling Splunk HEC export last week. Requested sample raw payloads and approximate time windows.

22 PT — Markus Klein (Solutions Eng): Adding context from call: VectorGrid has correlation rules in Splunk keyed on event_type + actor, but they do not currently dedupe on event_id. They asked if we can guarantee exactly-once. I told them delivery is likely at-least-once and we’ll confirm + provide best practice.

41 PT — Kevin Osei (Security Eng / Export): Reviewed sample events: duplicates have the same event_id, same occurred_at, same schema_version. This strongly suggests retry duplication (not double-produce). Exporter logs show repeated POST attempts due to client timeout while waiting for HEC response (no definitive 2xx observed).

05 PT — Sean Gallagher (SRE): Confirming current intended semantics: audit export is at-least-once. During transient sink issues/timeouts we retry; duplicates are possible. Consumers must dedupe using event_id. We should update customer guidance to explicitly call this out.

18 PT — Kevin Osei: Proposed immediate customer guidance: dedupe by event_id (primary). If needed, secondary key = (event_id, schema_version). event_id is globally unique per audit event emission; duplicates should share the same event_id. For Splunk, ensure event_id is extracted as a field and use it in correlation searches / suppression logic. We can provide a SPL snippet for de-dupe in saved searches: "... | dedup event_id".

30 PT — Dev Patel (Support): Sent customer interim response: delivery is at-least-once; duplicates possible; recommended to dedup on event_id; asked them to confirm event_id is consistently present in Splunk indexed fields (not only in raw JSON).

02 PT — Kevin Osei: Exporter improvement options: (a) tune HTTP timeout to reduce false timeouts for successful ingests, (b) enable/require sink acknowledgment where available (Splunk HEC ack) to reduce ambiguous outcomes, (c) add exporter metric + log tag for "retry_reason=timeout" to help customers correlate. Tracking in exporter backlog; not a hotfix commitment for this ticket.

17 PT — VectorGrid (via Support): Confirmed they see identical event_id across duplicates. They asked whether we can add an explicit idempotency_key field. They can implement de-dupe in SPL but prefer product guidance they can share with their security team.

26 PT — Kevin Osei: Response guidance: event_id is the idempotency key. We should not add a second field unless required by schema; instead we’ll document that event_id is stable across retries and should be used for de-dupe. For Splunk, de-dupe can be applied in searches/detections; index-time de-dupe is possible but customer-specific.

40 PT — Dev Patel (Support): Drafted customer-facing message (pending Kevin review): 1) confirm at-least-once semantics, 2) recommend de-dupe on event_id, 3) provide SPL example and note about retry timeouts, 4) note we are evaluating exporter-side improvements to reduce duplicates but de-dupe is still required for correctness.

12 PT — Kevin Osei: Approved message. Also confirmed exporter is preserving event_id and schema_version (per normalization work). Pointed to ADR on delivery guarantees and offered to share a short "SIEM De-dupe" blurb that we’re adding to our audit export guidance.

03 PT — Dev Patel (Support): Customer confirmed they implemented "dedup event_id" in the affected detection searches and alert noise is resolved. Closing ticket as resolved; leaving internal follow-up to product/docs for official SIEM export guidance update.

title: Burst of 5xx (mostly 503/502) traced to runtime threadpool starvation from hedged streaming/prefetch traffic
body: Issue summary: Starting ~2026-03-12 20:15 UTC we observed a sustained uptick in 5xx errors (predominantly 503 and 502) for a Dedicated customer (BrightWrite Inc.) in us-east. Errors correlate with a heavy number of long-lived streaming sessions that use hedged prefetch requests. Impact: API calls to generation/chat endpoints intermittently return 502/503; streaming sessions abruptly close; customers report degraded latency and dropped responses. Initial hypothesis: runtime threadpool exhaustion / blocking IO in prefetch path leading to upstream failures at the API gateway.

comments.author: BrightWrite (customer)
comments.body: 'We've been seeing dropped streams and 502s in production for the last 10 minutes. Can you investigate? Our engineering team is on standby.'

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: 'Thanks for the report — we've triaged and engaged runtime. We'll apply temporary mitigations and follow up.'

comments.author: Samir Gupta
comments.body: 'Disabled hedged prefetch for tenant and observed 70% reduction in blocked threads within 3 minutes. Proceeding to increase threadpool soft limits.'

comments.author: Priya Patel
comments.body: 'Patch ready to convert prefetch to async; PR opened at https://github.com/redwood/serving-runtime/pull/4271. Need QA on stress harness.'

comments.author: Miguel Torres
comments.body: 'Customer notified. We'll schedule canary and keep BrightWrite updated.'

title: Missing mid-cycle prorated credit for Dedicated committed-hours — hourly metering not reflecting credit
body:
Issue summary: Customer reports that a custom reserved commitment purchased mid-billing-cycle applied as a prorated credit at purchase time, but subsequent hourly metering continued to bill against raw usage without the committed-hour credit being applied.

Impact: ParcelLogic is seeing an unexpected spike in invoices for the current month estimated at ~$9k above expected after they purchased a 2k-hour dedicated commitment on 2026-03-05. They are mid-market, production workloads, reporting degraded confidence in billing accuracy.

Environment: Production (dedicated), us-east-1. Commitment purchased via Console by customer billing admin on 2026-03-05.

Steps to reproduce / provided by customer:
1) On 2026-03-05 customer purchased a 2000-hour dedicated commitment (custom pricing) with immediate prorated application for remainder of billing cycle.
2) Console shows a credit line applied o...

title: Post-migration edge proxy: intermittent 429s for mixed API keys and signed-url callbacks
body:
Issue summary:
After a scheduled edge proxy migration (blue->green) on 2026-03-09, the customer reports intermittent 429 responses for a subset of traffic that uses a mix of persistent API keys and signed URL callbacks. Impact: several ETL workers and webhook callbacks saw periodic failures for ~20 minutes during a heavy ingest window, causing backlog and retries downstream.

Impact:
- Acme Analytics batch ingest pipeline experienced ~18% failed request rate during the window, causing requeues and delayed downstream processing.
- Customer reported billing and operational disruption; they are on a Dedicated plan and requested immediate assistance.

Environment:
- Customer Dedicated pool in us-west-2, routed through new edge-proxy cluster (ep-usw-3).
- Mix of long-lived API keys (service accounts) and short-lived signed URLs generated by their ingestion service.
- HTTP/2 multiplexing be...

comments.author: Lina Chen ()

title: Request new analytics dataset/table for cache telemetry aggregates (prefix/KV cache) to support dashboards + recommendations MVP
body:
Requester: Mei Lin (Analytics datasets & batch job engineer)

Background
We’re instrumenting new prefix/KV cache telemetry in the serving runtime and building (a) cache dashboards/alerts and (b) a first-pass recommendations batch job ("increase hit-rate" levers). Both efforts need a stable, queryable analytics table of *aggregated* cache telemetry with strict privacy and cardinality controls.

Request
Please create a new curated dataset/table (or view) in the analytics warehouse that contains cache telemetry aggregates at controlled dimensions (no raw prompts, no high-cardinality identifiers). This table will be consumed by:
- Recommendations batch job (daily + on-demand backtests)
- SRE/Observability ad-hoc analysis and dashboard backfills
- Console stub/report validation (internal-only initially)

Proposed dataset/table
- Dataset (schema/namespace): analytics_prod.telemetry
- Table ...

- Console stub/report validation (internal-only initially)

Proposed dataset/table
- Dataset (schema/namespace): analytics_prod.telemetry
- Table ...

comments.author: Mei Lin
comments.body: Filing this as internal support because we need the dataset created + permissions handled centrally. We’re blocked on backtesting recommendation heuristics until we can query stable daily aggregates.

comments.author: Thomas Baird
comments.body: Acknowledged. Need two clarifications: (1) Do you need hourly granularity or is daily enough for MVP? (2) For route dimension, can we use the gateway’s allowlisted route_group rather than raw route_id to keep series bounded?

comments.author: Mei Lin
comments.body: Daily is enough for MVP (recommendations job runs daily). Hourly would be helpful later for incident response but not required. Route_group is acceptable as long as we can map it to the same identifiers SRE uses in dashboards (Hosted vs Dedicated routes differ).

comments.author: Allison Grant
comments.body: +1 on route_group. For SRE triage we mostly slice by region/model/prompt bucket, then drill into the top 20 routes only. If we can keep a small allowlist of high-volume routes and bucket the rest as 'other', that works.

comments.author: Thomas Baird
comments.body: Plan: create analytics_prod.telemetry.cache_efficiency_agg_daily partitioned by ds. Dimensions: deployment_mode, region, model_id, model_version_id, route_group (allowlisted), prompt_len_bucket, prompt_tokens_bucket. Miss reasons will be pivoted into columns. Will also add telemetry_sample_rate and a simple dq flag (rows_ingested) per partition.

comments.author: Mei Lin
comments.body: Sounds good. Please include wasted_prefill_tokens and prefill_tokens_total; the recommendations rely on 'wasted prefill %' + eviction pressure correlation.

comments.author: Thomas Baird
comments.body: Implemented table + scheduled job. Backfill for last 7 days completed. Granted read access to groups: eng-platform-telemetry, eng-sre-observability, prod-optimize-pm-readonly. Holding off on support/cs access pending PM approval; can add later via a view.

comments.author: Paula Mendes
comments.body: Confirmed PM read-only access is sufficient. Prefer CS consumes through dashboards rather than direct warehouse access. Thanks.

comments.author: Thomas Baird
comments.body: Marking resolved. Next steps: if SRE needs hourly granularity for incident response, open a follow-up INT ticket; we can add cache_efficiency_agg_hourly with tighter retention.

title: Conntrack hash rewrite by perimeter firewall causes ephemeral port collisions and OCSP/STS token stalls on private deployment
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports intermittent control-plane authentication failures and long model download latencies from private deployment behind their perimeter firewall. Failures correlate with bursts of outbound artifact fetches (nightly batch jobs). Impact: model pulls stall, control-plane heartbeats delay, and client token refreshes (STS/OAuth) see multiple retries and occasional 401s.

Impact:
- Multiple serving nodes in the customer's VPC observed KV cache heartbeats delayed (leading to increased cold starts).
- Control-plane auth attempts show OCSP lookup timeouts and STS token refresh retries, causing customer application errors and elevated latencies.
- Reproducible during high parallel shard downloads (batch jobs).

Environment:
- Redwood Private deployment (VPC + VPCE)
- Customer firewall / NGFW in egress path performing conntrack and port-preserving NAT (partner device ...

Impact:
- Multiple serving nodes in the customer's VPC observed KV cache heartbeats delayed (leading to increased cold starts).
- Control-plane auth attempts show OCSP lookup timeouts and STS token refresh retries, causing customer application errors and elevated latencies.
- Reproducible during high parallel shard downloads (batch jobs).

Environment:
- Redwood Private deployment (VPC + VPCE)
- Customer firewall / NGFW in egress path performing conntrack and port-preserving NAT (partner device ...

comments.author: Ava Chen ()
comments.body: Customer opened ticket. Included pcap and firewall conntrack dump. Marked P1 due to production impact. First response SLA set for 2026-03-11.

comments.author: Miguel Torres (SRE)
comments.body: Acknowledged. Requested firewall model/firmware and NAT config. Asked for sample trace IDs and time windows. Assigned to Networking on-call.

comments.author: DataScale SRE
comments.body: Provided firewall syslog excerpts and NAT pool config. Confirmed single active egress appliance and batch schedule (03:00–03:20 UTC). Attached pcap showing duplicated source ports across parallel flows.

comments.author: Priya Raman
comments.body: Reproduced symptom in lab by simulating 400 parallel ephemeral flows with a conntrack hash collision emulator. Observed port aliasing and mid-handshake TCP resets analogous to customer's capture. Hypothesis: conntrack hash normalization under high churn collapses multiple 5-tuple flows into same bucket and reassigns source ports.

comments.author: Miguel Torres
comments.body: Reviewed control-plane traces. OCSP lookups showed TCP fallback and 3x latency spike aligning with customer's event window. Token refresh retries were client-side visible as 401/timeout loops. Suggested immediate mitigation: reduce parallel shard concurrency and increase conntrack size on firewall.

comments.author: Customer
comments.body: Applied conntrack table increase and reduced parallelism; reported reduction in incidents but still occasional spikes at peak. Asked Redwood whether we can make artifact downloads more tolerant to transient port reuse.

comments.author: (Liam O'Neil)
comments.body: Investigating changes to artifact downloader to add short-lived retry jitter and enable TLS session resume quicker; tracking as infra PR (see related_github_prs). Also evaluating whether client-side STS refresh backoff can be hardened to tolerate transient network anomalies.

comments.author: Miguel Torres
comments.body: Plan: 1) Provide formal guidance to customer for firewall tuning (conntrack sizing and disable aggressive hash normalization), 2) Short-term support asks customer to cap parallel shard downloads to 200, 3) Engineering to deliver a hardened retry patch within 2 weeks. Scheduling follow-up call.

comments.author: Customer
comments.body: Agreed to cap parallel downloads and to schedule change window to test firewall firmware patch next week. Asked for runbook steps from Redwood for diagnosing when to escalate to WAN vendor.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Uploaded runbook excerpt and escalation checklist (links provided) and asked customer to file vendor case referencing conntrack bucket collisions if patching is required.

comments.author: Customer
comments.body: Confirmed ticket will remain open while engineering implements downloader resilience changes. Requesting RFC for long-term mitigations (e.g., presigned URL reuse patterns, built-in client-side multiplexing).

title: Pinned cohort unexpectedly coalesced onto legacy variant during weighted canary cutover, causing mixed outputs and cost spike
body:
Issue summary: During a planned weighted canary cutover for redwood-v2-medium -> redwood-v2-quantized at ~2026-03-13T18:22Z, approximately 7% of Finato Bank's tenant cohort (cohort id c-88ff) that should have been routed to the canary variant was instead routed to the legacy variant pinned from a previous rollout. Impact: mixed-model responses, higher than expected token costs, and a small number of failing structured outputs for validation prompts. Severity: customer-visible degradation (inconsistent outputs) and billing spike.

Observed behaviour: traffic that matched the pin cohort showed two routing fingerprints in edge logs; a subset of requests carried a routing cookie keyed to legacy-variant-id=rv2-2025-11 while orchestration snapshots indicated the cohort should map to rv2-q1. The routing mesh appears to have merged cohort metadata during a snapshot reconcile operation which c...

The routing mesh appears to have merged cohort metadata during a snapshot reconcile operation which c...

comments.author: (Ava)
comments.body: Acknowledged. Opening for SRE and Rollouts. Notified customer that we are investigating and temporarily paused weight increases.

comments.author: Customer (Finato - Priya R.)
comments.body: We are seeing inconsistent outputs on validation calls; please advise. We've put a hold on critical workflows.

comments.author: SRE (Diego)
comments.body: Observed snapshot merge in orchestrator logs that merged adjacent cohort keys; capturing dump of cohort table and rolling back weight to previous stable state. Temporary mitigation applied: reverted canary weight to 3% and forced cohort remap.

comments.author: (R&D - Jonas P.)
comments.body: Hypothesis: hash-bucket collision in snapshot-merge optimizer when commit contains contiguous cohort boundaries. Adding debug flags to snapshot commit to preserve separate entries. Preparing small patch to prevent coalescence on identical prefix hashes.

comments.author: (Ava)
comments.body: Customer accepted temporary rollback and confirmed reduced inconsistency. Scheduling follow-up to validate patch in staging with synthetic cohort adjacency tests.

comments.author: Customer (Priya)
comments.body: Thank you. Please keep us updated on root cause and fix ETA.

title: SCIM group displayName with emoji prevents RBAC mapping for internal console elevations
body:
Issue summary:
Several internal users cannot assume temporary elevated roles in the support console after being added to Okta groups whose displayName contains non-ASCII characters (emoji and mixed scripts). This surfaced after an HR group import that included emojis and slashes in group names.

Impact:
- Engineers could not escalate to runbook/incident responder roles for two oncall incidents; manual escalation caused ~10-20 minute delays.
- Affects any group where SCIM displayName includes surrogate pairs or combined unicode sequences. ASCII-only groups unaffected.

Environment:
- Okta -> SCIM sync -> internal identity-proxy (us-west).
- RBAC mapper resolves Okta group displayName to internal role grants during elevation.

Steps to reproduce:
1) Create Okta group with displayName: "Incident Responders 🚨" and externalId: "incident-responders".
2) Allow SCIM incremental sync.
3) Attem...

Several internal users cannot assume temporary elevated roles in the support console after being added to Okta groups whose displayName contains non-ASCII characters (emoji and mixed scripts). This surfaced after an HR group import that included emojis and slashes in group names.

title: Transparent midstream proxy insertion causing serialized streaming and long tail latency between APAC and EU
body:
Issue summary: Since 2026-03-12 03:20 UTC StreamlineAI sees high p95/p99 latency and streaming timeouts for inference requests originating in APAC and routed to eu-west. Symptoms point to an intermediate transparent proxy that is serializing HTTP/2 streams and introducing head-of-line delays; streaming responses are returned in full chunks rather than interleaved tokens, causing user-observed stalls and occasional 504s at the application layer. Impact: customer-facing production service degradation, active customer requests timing out or experiencing multi-second tail latency, increased error rates in their monitoring. Environment: production, apac-east region for client egress, destination region eu-west hosting model endpoints.

Expected behavior: streaming responses should arrive token-by-token (low TTFB, steady token stream). Observed behavior: initial handshake fine, then long pa...

title: Tenant-aware event index and verifiable archive evidence request for regulated audit
body:
Issue summary:
Customer (FinSecure Analytics, enterprise) requests cryptographically verifiable evidence that audit events delivered to their S3-compatible archive are tenant-scoped, immutable, and traceable back to the originating request IDs in Redwood’s audit pipeline.

Impact:
- Customer requires this evidence as part of an external SOC 2 + sector-specific audit; failure to provide suitable artifacts will delay attestation.
- They specifically need proofs that (1) raw events are stored with a content hash manifest, (2) each archived object contains metadata linking to the original Redwood request_id and tenant_id, and (3) the archive retention and object-immutability controls are enforced for the requested date range (90 days).

Environment:
- Redwood prod (us-east-1) -> customer S3-compatible endpoint (OCI-compatible object store supplied by customer).
- Export pipeline: audit-se...

comments.body: We can generate a signed manifest and extract sample objects, but need customer confirmation on acceptable PII redaction approach (tokenization vs field removal). comments.author: FinSecure - Compliance Lead, Maya Patel
comments.body: Prefers PGP-signed manifests using their public key. Also requests full object-key -> request_id mapping CSV and the SHA-256 values in hex. PII should be redacted with deterministic tokenization so auditors can verify mapping without seeing raw data.

comments.author: Luis Romero
comments.body: Acknowledged. Opened internal PR to export index and generate PGP-signed manifest. Linked PR: github.com/redwood-inference/internal/audit-export/pull/347. Asked Storage team for export window and ETA: 48 hours.

comments.author: Nina Gupta
comments.body: Export started. We will deliver: (a) PGP-signed manifest using customer's supplied public key, (b) CSV mapping of object_key, sha256_hex, request_id, tenant_id (redacted values tokenized), (c) S3 server-side metadata snapshot. Estimated delivery 2026-03-14 morning UTC.

title: Streaming completions time out (504) when client-side HTTP/2 WINDOW_UPDATE is not sent by corporate proxy
body:
Issue summary:
Customer AcmeFin reports repeated streaming completions from our hosted API returning 504s after long tails of silence. This appears only for some users behind a corporate network appliance.

Impact:
- ~30 requests/hr from AcmeFin experience 40-120s stalls then 504s when generating long completions (>512 tokens).
- End-user UX broken for interactive flows.
- Customer is enterprise tier; impact threatens a contract renewal conversation.

Environment:
- Prod edge (us-east) -> API Gateway (HTTP/2 enabled) -> NGINX sidecars -> serving-runtime GPU pool.
- Observed on models: gpt-open-13b, redwood-rl-7b.

Steps to reproduce (approx):
1) Customer client opens HTTP/2 connection to /v1/stream with streaming=true.
2) Server starts emitting many small DATA frames (KV-warmup + prefix output) over the stream for ~30-90s.
3) Corporate proxy between client and our edge silently fails ...

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Customer reports 'stream hangs for ~1m then errors out' on interactive flows. Provided request IDs and times. Routed to SRE for packet capture.

comments.author: Diego Martinez (SRE)
comments.body: Captured tcpdump on edge; initial look shows many server DATA frames with no WINDOW_UPDATE for failing flows. Suspect middlebox. Creating internal reproduction proxy to validate.

comments.author: Logan Park
comments.body: Confirmed NGINX send_timeout default (60s) is causing premature upstream timeouts when client window doesn't advance. Temporarily increased send_timeout on customer route to 120s as stopgap.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Applied HTTP/2 disable on route for AcmeFin; customer reports streaming restored. Coordinating a call with customer's network team tomorrow to share pcap.

comments.author: Diego Martinez
comments.body: Created PR for auto-fallback heuristic (detect 30s of send stall without WINDOW_UPDATE -> set connection-level header to force HTTP/1.1 next attempt). Linked PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/platform/pull/4432

title: Intermittent SSE half-closed stream with cross-session kv-cache leakage causing partial/duplicated choices
body: Issue summary: Parcelly calls our streaming SSE chat endpoint from a dedicated cluster. Over the last two days they observe intermittent sessions that end in a half-closed state: the client receives a final 'data: [DONE]' but the JSON choice is truncated or duplicated and subsequent requests from different browser tabs sometimes see fragments from the earlier session.

normalized.description: Issue summary: Parcelly calls our streaming SSE chat endpoint from a dedicated cluster. Over the last two days they observe intermittent sessions that end in a half-closed state: the client receives a final 'data: [DONE]' but the JSON choice is truncated or duplicated and subsequent requests from different browser tabs sometimes see fragments from the earlier session.

comments.author: Ava Chen (2026-03-10)
comments.body: Raised by Parcelly after they saw duplicated fragments across tabs. Provided sample client trace and session ids. Notified CS and attached customer logs.

comments.author: Priya Nair (SRE) (2026-03-10)
comments.body: ACKed. Requested full server trace ids and the customer's multiplexing header values. Suspect multiplex slot reuse under race on reconnect.

comments.author: Parcelly_Eng (2026-03-10)
comments.body: Provided client capture (EventSource timeline), observed final chunk trimmed and then appeared as leading fragment in new tab. Attached cURL/headers.

comments.author: Diego Ramirez (2026-03-11)
comments.body: Instrumented allocator to log multiplex-slot lifecycle. Found slots 21/22 showing rapid allocate/free with overlapping attach windows matching customer times. Escalating to serving-runtime team.

comments.author: Serving Runtime
comments.body: Hypothesis: kv-cache tombstone TTL too low combined with race in session detachment leads to stale KV context being re-attached to a different logical session id. This can produce partial/duplicated choices when the earlier generator emits buffered tokens after tombstone.

title: Browser DNS pre-resolve + HTTP/2 connection reuse causing requests to egress via us-east for eu-west tenant
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports sudden increase in median (p50) request latency for eu-west traffic starting 2026-03-09 06:00 UTC. The tenant is enterprise, pinned to eu-west. Traffic observed to egress to us-east for a subset of long-lived browser clients.

Impact:
- Customer FinSight Analytics sees ~120ms median increase for text-generation requests and periodic spikes to 500ms on some sessions.
- No 5xx errors, but increased client-side timeouts reported on mobile web app.

- Pin: region_residency=eu-west, strict residency enabled
- Client: Web SPA using connection pre-resolve and HTTP/2 keepalive (Chrome/Edge mobile)
- Models: redwood-m-1 (default), occasional gpt-neoX-20b for heavy prompts

1) Open web SPA and perform series of generation requests.
2) Observe requests initially hitting eu-we...

comments.body: Notified customer of proposed timeline for hotfix and confirmed temporary workaround steps with onboarding docs link: https://redwood.docs/edge/connection-policies#client-side-considerations

title: Saved dashboard query times out for >24h ranges and associated tracing links return 404
body:
Issue summary: Saved dashboard query for 'usage_per_model' consistently times out when run over windows larger than 24 hours. When the query is re-run, the console shows result rows but the 'view trace' links on individual rows return a 404. Impact: Customer cannot investigate usage spikes or link traces from console; blocking incident investigation and delaying billing reconciliations for their dedicated tenant. Environment: prod (us-west), dedicated tenant soma-prod-01, console v2.8.2.

Steps to reproduce:
1) Login as customer user on SomaHealth dedicated tenant.
2) Open Console -> Dashboards -> Saved Queries -> 'usage_per_model'.
3) Set time range to last 7 days and Run.
4) Observe spinner; query returns after ~60s with partial results.
5) Click 'View Trace' on any row -> 404 from tracing endpoint.

Expected behavior: Query completes within SLO (<30s) for 7d with aggregated rollups...

comments.body: Created ticket after customer reported. Attached console screenshot and request id 9c3f2ad1-console. Assigned to SRE for initial triage.

comments.author: Ethan Cole (SRE)
comments.body: Reproduced timeout on staging replica with prod dataset. Query tends to spin up large aggregation job hitting analytics-engine mem limit. Trace 404 seems related: trace id missing from link payload vs trace index TTL mismatch. Added labels and requested logs from analytics-engine.

comments.author: Customer (SomaHealth)
comments.body: Confirmed issue occurs during their end-of-day billing job, added urgency; requesting ETA.

comments.author: Aisha Rahman
comments.body: Notified customer we are investigating and provided workaround (split ranges). SLA due today EOD.

comments.author: Ethan Cole (SRE)
comments.body: Narrowed root cause to query planner change in analytics-engine v3.4.0 leading to full-scan fallback for >24h ranges; tracing payload occasionally truncates trace_id header when the backend returns partial rows. Preparing patch.
