title: Sustained 429 spikes after credit reconciliation during simultaneous bulk backfill and low-latency webhooks
body:
Issue summary:
Customer ScribeAI reports sustained 429 responses across a subset of API keys immediately following a tenant credit reconciliation event (automatic refill) that occurred 2026-03-10 00:05 UTC. The tenant was simultaneously running a long-running bulk embedding backfill and high-frequency webhook-driven chat inference for low-latency UI actions.

Impact:
- Customer sees 20-40% of requests returning 429 for ~3 hours after refill window.
- Real-time chat flows experienced user-visible failures; bulk backfill jobs retried and slowed throughput.
- Appears to affect only requests routed via the dedicated pool in us-east.

Requested outcome:
- Root cause analysis and mitigation to avoid recurrence during future credit refills and autosyncs.
- Guidance / recommended config to isolate bulk backfills from low-latency routes (rate classes, burst pools, or route separation).

comments.body: Received initial report from customer and asked for request_id samples and approximate timestamps. Customer provided sample ids and traces.

comments.author: Diego Martin
comments.body: Correlated provided request_ids to edge logs. Found that edge token windows were not refreshed for the connection pool that handled the bulk-worker fans after refill. Suspect refill race with health-check probes.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Sent mitigation steps to customer to stagger bulk ingest start times away from refill window; asked if they can pin bulk to 'bulk-ingest' route with lower priority.

comments.author: ScribeAI - Priya Rao
comments.body: Implemented staggered start and saw reduction of 429s to <2% during next refill. Customer requests formal config recommendations and whether platform can avoid future edge-origin window drift.

comments.author: Diego Martin
comments.body: Proposed short-term workaround: configure separate route id and priority class for bulk backfills, enable route isolation via Console (create dedicated low-priority route with its own burst pool). Long term: engineering to investigate edge refill sync logic for dedicated pools.

normalized.cleanBody:
Issue summary:
Customer ScribeAI reports sustained 429 responses across a subset of API keys immediately following a tenant credit reconciliation event (automatic refill) that occurred 2026-03-10 00:05 UTC. The tenant was simultaneously running a long-running bulk embedding backfill and high-frequency webhook-driven chat inference for low-latency UI actions.

Impact:
- Customer sees 20-40% of requests returning 429 for ~3 hours after refill window.
- Real-time chat flows experienced user-visible failures; bulk backfill jobs retried and slowed throughput.
- Appears to affect only requests routed via the dedicated pool in us-east.

Requested outcome:
- Root cause analysis and mitigation to avoid recurrence during future credit refills and autosyncs.
- Guidance / recommended config to isolate bulk backfills from low-latency routes (rate classes, burst pools, or route separation).

title: SAML assertions signed with RSA-PSS rejected by private auth proxy causing 403s for SSO logins
body:
Issue summary: Customer reports that users in their private deployment cannot complete SSO login. Requests to our auth proxy return 403 Authorization Denied.
Impact: All Aurelia Finance users in the eu-west private deployment are unable to sign in via SSO (affects web console and API token exchange for automation).
Environment: Private deployment (customer-managed control plane) running auth-proxy v1.8.1, auth-service v3.2.0. IdP: corporate SAML IdP configured to sign assertions with RSA-PSS (RSASSA-PSS with SHA256).
Observed behaviour: SAML assertion accepted by IdP and delivered to our endpoint, but auth-proxy logs show signature validation failure and downstream RBAC mapping is not performed, resulting in 403.
Expected behaviour: Our SAML verifier should validate IdP-signed assertions and proceed to role mapping; if algorithm unsupported, we should surface clear diagnostic and prov...

title: Consolidate Q3 SaaS seat forecast and run security + procurement evaluation for new annotation vendor
body:
Issue summary:
- Request: Procure and onboard a small-pilot bundle of seats for a vendor evaluation (annotation tool) and reconcile Q3 seat forecast for Data Labeling and Research teams.
- Business impact: Labeling work for two product pilots (Search tuning and Safety evals) is blocked without a vetted annotation tool; delays will push model evaluation and release timelines by ~6 weeks.
- Requested scope: 25 initial editor seats + 5 manager seats (30 total) for 12 months (pilot -> expansion). Renewal cadence: annual.
- Vendor: LabelWave (SME annotation platform; SOC2 self-attested) — initial commercial quote attached in Confluence.
- Licensing model: per-seat annual subscription with optional storage add-on. Initial quoted cost: $36,000/yr (USD) for seats + $3,000 one-time migration assistance.
- Budget owner: ML Ops (cost center: ML-PLAT).
- Timeline: Need pilot access by 2026-04-01 ...

title: Dedicated capacity pulse: sudden p50/p99 latency increases correlated with misaligned sidecar heartbeats
body: Issue summary: Customer Lumina Analytics reported a recurring, short-lived pulse in p50 and p99 latency across their dedicated pool starting 2026-03-09 14:00 UTC. Impact: user-facing API calls (generation and streaming) saw p50 increase from ~120ms to ~320ms and p99 spikes to 1.2s for 10-30s windows, causing degraded interactive experience. Pattern: pulses occur roughly every 5-12 minutes and last ~10-30s; throughput (tokens/s) drops by 20-40% during pulses. Initial hypothesis: interference from background sidecar telemetry/metrics emitters causing transient scheduler/attach delays and autoscaler sensing noise.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Ticket opened after customer alert. Attached flamegraphs and request traces. Asked SRE to triage potential host noise.

comments.author: Marcus Delgado
comments.body: Initial observation: short I/O bursts from metrics sidecar causing CPU/PCIe contention on subset of hosts. Spoke to infra to pull sidecar metrics cadence and check emitter rate-limiter.

comments.author: Lumina - Priya Rao
comments.body: Confirmed user reports line up with the windows in our UX tests; provided sample request IDs. We see degraded interactivity during pulses.

comments.author: Rui Patel
comments.body: Collected allocator and nvlink telemetry; noticed small but consistent queueing. Proposed mitigation: lower sidecar emit bursts (increase jitter and backoff) and add autoscaler smoothing patch.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Temporary mitigation rolled to subset of hosts: sidecar emit cadence changed from 5s fixed to randomized 5s-15s with token bucket rate limit. Monitoring for recurrence.

title: Streaming disconnect spikes in us-east not reflected on public status page (status remained green)
body:
Customer report (Northbridge AI Platform, enterprise):

Issue summary:
- Beginning ~2025-11-21 09:05 PT, customer observed elevated streaming disconnects for chat completions in us-east.
- Impacted traffic was primarily streaming (SSE). Non-streaming responses were reported as “mostly OK” with intermittent latency.
- Public status page showed all components operational during the impact window; customer is asking why the incident was not communicated and requesting per-capability visibility (Streaming vs non-streaming).

Customer impact:
- Customer’s end users experienced partial responses / dropped connections while waiting for streamed tokens.
- Customer’s app retries increased significantly; they estimate ~6–10% of sessions required a user refresh.

Environment:
- Prod
- Region: us-east

- “We see spike in `ERR_STREAM_CLOSED` / client disconnects between ...

title: KV cache serialization spike causes backpressure and client timeouts on deep prompts with high max_tokens
body:
Issue summary:
Customer LumaDocs reports repeated request timeouts (504 / client disconnects) when sending prompts with very large context + max_tokens=120000 via the Redwood hosted API. Occurs primarily for streams that use function-calling and chunked streaming.

Impact:
- Several production API calls for LumaDocs are timing out during long-document summarization jobs, causing failed jobs and user-facing errors.
- Error rate ~12% for affected routes over a 6-hour window; spikes align with heavy serialization activity on the serving nodes.

Environment:
- Hosted (Redwood managed), dedicated tenant for LumaDocs
- Region: us-east
- Models: redwood-alpha-1 (default), gptj-6b used for fallback testing

Steps to reproduce (customer):
1) Send a streaming generation request with a prompt consisting of concatenated OCRed pages (~90k tokens) plus system instructions (function-calls enabled).
...

comments.author: (Ava)
comments.body: Acknowledged with customer, collected request ids and sample payloads. Escalating to infra on-call.

comments.author: SRE (Diego)
comments.body: Reproduced on dedicated repro cluster. Observed large synchronous KV serialization spikes. Adding as investigation owner.

comments.author: (Ava)
comments.body: Provided customer with temporary mitigation: reduce max_tokens to 40k OR disable function-calls for long-document summarization. Customer confirms reduced error rate but requires long-term fix.

comments.author: Priya Rao
comments.body: Hypothesis - synchronous kv-cache flush runs on finalizer thread causing blocking; propose async background serialization and streaming of final tokens before flush.

comments.author: (Diego)
comments.body: Deployed config change to defer KV flush until post-response for a subset of traffic (canary). Observed drop in 504s for canary traffic from 11% to 1.5%.

comments.author: (Ava)
comments.body: Customer accepted the canary behavior as interim; requested ETA for full fix and follow-up on SLA impact.

title: Intermittent upstream process crashes and elevated 5xx during short-lived microbursts (keepalive fd leak hypothesis)
body: Issue summary: Starting 2026-03-10 09:12 UTC NovaSearch observed a pattern of intermittent 5xx errors returned to a small percentage (~3-5%) of requests during high-frequency short-lived request bursts from their dedicated pool. Impact: customer latency spikes and ~3% error rate increase over 15 minute windows; most affected endpoints are short chat completions and single-turn embed calls. Environment: Dedicated tenant on prod/us-east-1 using rw-infer-base-v2 pinned model. Symptoms: - API gateway returns 502/503 to client. - Upstream runtime processes (ephemeral worker) show sudden exits (SIGABRT or OOM-like) in kmsg. - Accept queue grows briefly, then recovers as autoscaler spawns extra ephemerals. - Errors correlate with tight bursts of small payloads from customer side (many connections <200ms).

normalized.description: Issue summary: Starting 2026-03-10 09:12 UTC NovaSearch observed a pattern of intermittent 5xx errors returned to a small percentage (~3-5%) of requests during high-frequency short-lived request bursts from their dedicated pool. Impact: customer latency spikes and ~3% error rate increase over 15 minute windows; most affected endpoints are short chat completions and single-turn embed calls. Environment: Dedicated tenant on prod/us-east-1 using rw-infer-base-v2 pinned model. Symptoms: - API gateway returns 502/503 to client. - Upstream runtime processes (ephemeral worker) show sudden exits (SIGABRT or OOM-like) in kmsg. - Accept queue grows briefly, then recovers as autoscaler spawns extra ephemerals. - Errors correlate with tight bursts of small payloads from customer side (many connections <200ms).

comments.body: 00Z - Support (Aisha): Received initial report from NovaSearch CS about ~3-5% 5xx increase during morning ingest. Customer attached request traces and sample timestamps.
30Z - SRE (Marcus): Initial triage shows short-lived bursts correlate with accept-queue growth and ephemeral worker churn. Marking as high priority and adding on-call infra.

10Z - SRE (Marcus): Found repeated SIGABRTs in ephemeral logs; kernel OOMs also recorded shortly after. Hypothesis: frequent connect/close pattern causing keepalive/proxy fd accumulation and causing downstream resource pressure on runtime leading to crashes.
45Z - Dev (Lina Cho): Added pprof snapshot for runtime; saw thread stacks stuck in accept() cleanup path and delayed socket close handling. Possible leak window ~1.5-2s under heavy connect churn.

00Z - Support (Aisha): Customer confirmed they can temporarily batch requests or reuse keepalive; doing that reduces observed error rate to baseline. NovaSearch requests a fix or a mitigation that doesn't require changes to their client.

20Z - SRE (Marcus): Deployed temporary proxy knob: extend keepalive idle timeout and increased kernel fd reclaim frequency on dedicated nodes for NovaSearch pool. Error rate dropped to <0.5% during replicated tests. Preparing patch for proxy close-path handling.
00Z - Dev (Lina): Created GitHub PR #4389 to address socket close race in proxy; added unit + integration tests reproducing microburst pattern.

title: Intermittent high latency and packet loss for staff connected to corporate VPN over office Wi-Fi — suspected MTU/fragmentation
body: Issue summary: Over the last 48 hours several engineers on the us-west office Wi‑Fi (Corp-STA) reported intermittent high latency (200–800ms) and packet loss when accessing internal services hosted in our VPC while connected to the corporate IPSec VPN. Impact: Developer workflow degraded (git pushes, large artifact downloads time out), remote debugging sessions dropped. No impact reported for staff on wired desks or staff using the Dedicated VLAN SSID. Environment: Office Wi‑Fi (Corp-STA SSID) on AireOS AP controller (firmware 8.10.140.0), IPSec gateway (vendor: FortiGate FG-300E) with UDP encapsulation (NAT-T), client VPN is our standard AnyConnect replacement (custom), client OSes affected: Windows 10/11 and some macOS 12 clients. comments.body: Team noticed repeated failures with 'Destination Unreachable - fragmentation needed' while trying to push code to internal git over VPN. Wired desks are fine. Filing ticket for network team.

comments.author: Diego Martinez ()
comments.body: Acknowledged. Captures requested; please attach pcap/trace. I'll spin up a test client in lab and reproduce.

comments.author: Priya Nair
comments.body: Able to reproduce from a test laptop. PMTU ICMPs are emitted by gateway but not always reaching clients. Workaround: reduce MTU to 1400 on client or connect to Dedicated-VLAN SSID.

comments.author: Marcus Hale
comments.body: AP controller shows non-standard MTU on radio profile for aggregation. We will roll back that profile to default 1500 on a subset of APs for test.

comments.author: Diego Martinez
comments.body: Rolled back radio profile on AP floor 3/4. Users report improvements; coordinating with FortiGate team for MSS clamp rule as permanent fix.

comments.author: Marcus Hale
comments.body: Opened change request to add MSS clamping rule on firewall for UDP-encapsulated VPN (CR-2026-0312). Scheduled for 2026-03-16 maintenance window.

comments.author: Diego Martinez
comments.body: Recommend communication to office staff with temporary workaround (use wired where possible or lower MTU to 1400).

title: session-kv-lease-sticky-causing-resumed-conversation-p99-latency-spike
body:
Issue summary: ClearHealth reported a series of resumed conversational requests (user resumes session after short disconnect) that observe elevated tail latency (p99 ~4.2s) and occasional timeouts, compared to normal p99 ~250ms. This occurs after sessions have been inactive for ~30-90s. Impact: user-facing latency spikes and a small number of 504 timeouts causing degraded UX in production. Behavior is reproducible for certain session IDs and high-frequency resume patterns.

Context: This ticket was opened by Support after customer escalation. Initial triage indicates the serving runtime enters a blocking cold-path when a session key is considered "leased" by another backend shard due to a lease state that does not expire promptly. The behavior started after the kvcache lease change in release v2.18.

comments.body: Customer reports resumed chat p99 ~4s after 30-60s idle. They see 504s at ~1% of resumed attempts. Attached sample request_ids and session ids.

comments.author: Diego Morales (SRE)
comments.body: Initial hypothesis: lease TTL not expiring for sessions under error path introduced last week. Scheduling quick investigation on kvcache lease state metrics.

comments.author: Priya Nair ()
comments.body: Customer confirmed that restarting the client (which creates a new session_id) avoids the issue temporarily.

title: Ephemeral port aliasing across multi-NAT egress causes TLS SNI mismatch and intermittent presigned artifact 403s
body: DatumStream private deployment (VPCE-enabled) started seeing intermittent 403s from presigned S3 URLs used by the serving runtime to stream model shards. The errors are not consistent per-shard; same shard will sometimes succeed and sometimes return 403s for the same task. Client-side retries succeed most of the time. Customer observed increased 5xxs and request retries cascading into throttling-related symptoms.

Customer observed increased 5xxs and request retries cascading into throttling-related symptoms.

comments.author: Ava Chen ()
comments.body: Customer first reported at 2026-03-10 14:58 UTC. Attached logs show pattern during peak shard traffic.

comments.author: Diego Morales (SRE)
comments.body: Confirmed correlation between NAT autoscale events and spike in ephemeral port reuse. Recommending pinning NAT for immediate mitigation.

comments.author: DatumStream (Customer - ops)
comments.body: We can pin NATs temporarily; will need instructions for our cloud team. Impact window is business-critical mornings (14:00–18:00 UTC).

comments.author: Diego Morales (SRE)
comments.body: Applied NAT pinning and conntrack timeout increase at 2026-03-11 02:10 UTC. Customer verified reduced 403s in subsequent load test.

comments.author: Ava Chen ()
comments.body: Opening engineering ticket to implement SNAT affinity logic: related PR linked above. Will keep customer in the loop and schedule RCA.

title: SSE: lingered TCP socket results in interleaved token fragments and missing final event on client reconnect
body:
Issue summary: Customer reports that after a short mobile network switch the SSE stream resumes but the final 'end' event is missing and some token fragments are interleaved/duplicated. The behaviour is repeatable from the customer's environment when they trigger a cellular->wifi handoff. Impact: Produces incomplete outputs to end users and causes client-side reassembly failures; NoteFlow reported user-visible truncation in 3 of 10 resumed sessions and data loss for the last paragraph in many cases.

Observed behaviour: When client reconnects following a transient socket drop, the resumed stream sometimes contains duplicated prefix tokens, interleaved heartbeat frames, and the terminal 'event: done' or final JSON marker is not received. Client SDK shows partial last-choice and then the connection closes. Server traces show the backend had emitted the final marker.

Expected behaviour:...

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Acknowledged with customer, asked for pktcap and last-resume-token. Customer supplied pcap and session logs.

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: Verified edge envoy showed FIN linger ~35s and then RST during path migration. Confirmed backend logged 'final_event' before RST. Beginning deeper packet-level analysis.

comments.author: Priya Patel
comments.body: Ran reproduce in staging with SO_LINGER toggled. Observed interleaved fragments when upstream closed with linger and envoy connection pooling enabled. Suspect mux layer not atomically swapping stream buffers on resume mapping.

comments.author: NoteFlow
comments.body: Provided repeatable repro plan and consented to enabling additional logging on their dedicated pool for a short window. Asked for ETA on workaround.

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: Applied temporary mitigation: disabled keepalive-reuse on edge envoy for NoteFlow's customer pool; requested customer to validate. Customer confirmed 8/8 handoffs succeeded without truncation during 2-hour window.

comments.author: Priya Patel
comments.body: Opening a follow-up internal task to harden resume atomicity in mux layer and add defensive checks for duplicate-segment detection. See INT-34012 for internal rollout.

title: Autoscaler backoff due to false GPU thermal flags causing batched throughput loss
body:
Issue summary:
ByteStream Analytics (dedicated tenant) reported a sudden drop in sustained batched throughput on their reserved pool during midday traffic on 2026-03-10. P50/P95 token throughput dropped ~30% and p99 saw large variance, with a sharp increase in queued/batched requests and autoscaler entering repeated backoff cycles.

Impact:
- Customer-facing inference latency and throughput degradation for production routes pinned to dedicated pool.
- Visible dropped throughput for long-lived batched streams (LLM chat workloads) and increased request queuing.
- No token-level errors returned to customer (most requests eventually succeed but with higher latency and throttling).

Environment:
- Prod (us-east) dedicated pool; customer reserved capacity; models: redwood/llama-13b-quant and open/opt-6.7b; traffic pattern: sustained batched chat with periodic long-lived checkpointing sideca...

comments.body: Acknowledged customer report and collected initial metrics; created ticket and escalated to on-call SRE.

comments.author: SRE (Marco)
comments.body: Pulled node/exporter logs and autoscaler traces; observed repeated thermal-alert events without corroborating hardware throttling — leaning toward sensor-read failures tied to IO bursts.

comments.author: ByteStream Ops
comments.body: Confirmed they run a checkpoint sidecar that writes multi-GB checkpoints every 90s; agreed to temporarily stagger schedule.

comments.author: SRE (Marco)
comments.body: Applied temporary autoscaler override to ignore thermal-alerts for this tenant's pool for 10 minutes to relieve immediate customer impact (change logged in deployment history).

title: Rapid 5xx spike after scheduler backoff + ephemeral proxy thundering during large embedding fanout
body: Issue summary: BrightWrite reports a sudden, sustained increase in 5xx responses (502/503) from their dedicated tenant beginning ~2026-03-13 09:22 UTC. The spike coincides with a large embedding upload + mixed small chat traffic. Impact: Customer-facing upload jobs failing, automated ingestion retries, and visible latency spikes for downstream queries. Environment: Dedicated tenant on prod - us-east with reserved capacity. Steps to reproduce: 1) Upload a large embeddings batch (~120k vectors) via embeddings endpoint 2) Simultaneously send small chat requests to the same tenant 3) Observe API gateway 502/503 errors and runtime restarts/logged backoffs. Recent changes: no customer model pin changes; autoscaler applied a scheduler backoff tweak earlier today. Logs: see attached SRE logs and proxy traces referenced in investigation notes.

Recent changes: no customer model pin changes; autoscaler applied a scheduler backoff tweak earlier today. Logs: see attached SRE logs and proxy traces referenced in investigation notes.

comments.author: Priya Patel
comments.body: Customer reported ~40% of embedding upload requests returned 502/503. Provided request IDs and timestamps. Escalating to infra. (sla P1)

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: Initial triage shows api-gateway accept-queue growth and many ephemeral-proxy worker restarts around 09:22. Opening investigation.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Observed scheduler backoff oscillation when prewarm requests slowed; ephemeral-proxy thread count spiked then crashed with OOM-suspected logs. Collecting heap and nvml stats.

comments.author: Priya Patel
comments.body: Sent workaround to customer: throttle embedding upload concurrency to 8 and retry with exponential backoff. Customer confirmed temporary throughput improvement.

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: Implemented emergency ingress rate cap for the tenant to prevent further proxy thundering while we stabilize scheduler configs.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Pushed hotfix to scheduler that reduces aggressive backoff resets and staggers prewarm probes. Monitoring for 24h.

comments.author: Customer (BrightWrite)
comments.body: Reporting reduced error rate; embedding upload completed after lowering concurrency. Requesting postmortem and permanent mitigation.

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: Assigned follow-up to runtime team to harden ephemeral-proxy auto-scaling and to evaluate accept-queue sizing.

title: Stateful TCP sequence rewriting by egress firewall causes gRPC frame reordering and corrupted model stream in Private VPC
body:
Issue summary:
Customers on a Private deployment report intermittent corruption of streaming model responses (missing tokens, truncated arrays) when using long-lived gRPC streams. The symptoms began after a change to the customer's stateful egress firewall appliance last week.

Impact:
- FinSight Analytics reports production inference errors for chat sessions (several sessions per hour).
- Affects models served from the customer's private cluster (gRPC streaming generation).
- User-visible: dropped or out-of-order token sequences and occasional model shard fetch retries leading to latency spikes.

Environment:
- Private deployment in customer VPC (us-east).
- Egress path: customer stateful NGFW -> transit VPC -> Redwood VPCE.
- Traffic: long-lived HTTP/2/gRPC connections carrying multiplexed frames and range downloads for large model shards.

Steps to reproduce:
1) Create long-lived s...

comments.body: 'Since last Thursday we've seen a small % of sessions where the assistant returns gibberish or cuts mid-sentence. It correlates with heavy batch jobs overnight.'

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Requested tcpdump from customer egress appliance and serving node. Provided capture template and pcap upload link.

comments.author: Liam Rodriguez
comments.body: Replayed capture in lab with a commercial NGFW in 'optimize' mode. Reproduced frame reordering and shard checksum failures. Suspect sequence-number normalization.

comments.author: Customer (FinSight)
comments.body: 'Disabled the sequence normalization flags on the appliance as advised. Errors stopped within 5 minutes. Thank you.'

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Marking as In Progress for follow-up runbook updates and detection patch. Customer will remain on monitoring for 72 hours.

title: ALPN downgrade + half-open socket accumulation causing elevated 5xx and streaming failures
body:
Issue summary:
A sustained elevation in 5xx responses (502/503/504) and streaming disconnects was observed for the ClaraDocs dedicated tenant beginning 2026-03-13 18:12 UTC. The spike affected a narrow set of routes handled by a subset of edge hosts and coincided with a high rate of short-lived streaming sessions from a third-party SDK that attempted TLS session reuse and sometimes fell back to HTTP/1.1.

Impact:
- ClaraDocs saw ~7-12% of requests failing with 5xx over a ~40 minute window.
- Streaming p95 latency increased to ~3.2s and several sessions experienced abrupt resets.
- A few serving-runtime pods reported worker thread starvation warnings and transient allocation failures followed by graceful restarts.

Environment:
- Production, us-east-1, ClaraDocs dedicated GPU pool.
- Edge: envoy v1.24 (custom build) accepting both HTTP/2 and HTTP/1.1 via ALPN.
- Serving runtime: redwoo...

comments.body: 20 UTC | Aisha Rahman (Support): Monitoring alerted for spike in 5xx for ClaraDocs. Ticket opened and infra on-call paged.
28 UTC | Jonah Patel (SRE): Identified host group with accept_queue_depth spike. Captured tcpdump/envoy logs. Temporarily scaled workers and sysctls adjusted.
35 UTC | ClaraDocs (Customer): We observed errors in our CI/integration runs between 18:15-18:40 UTC. No client-side deploys during that window.
42 UTC | Jonah Patel (SRE): Deployed temporary close-after-idle policy for streaming routes. Accept queue depth declining; continuing monitoring.
05 UTC | Aisha Rahman (Support): Customer reports recovery for majority of requests. Follow-up scheduled for post-incident review.

title: Access grant marked approved but onboarding sync never created runbooks-v2 role bindings
body: Issue summary: A recent internal access request for the 'runbooks-v2' tool is showing as APPROVED in the approval queue, but the provisioning pipeline did not create the corresponding RBAC role bindings or service tokens. Impact: impacted users cannot access runbooks-v2 dashboards or runbook actions; several oncall requests are blocked. This appears to affect only the new onboarding flow that uses the lifecycle-sync lambda rather than the legacy backfill.

title: Restricted forensic sample extract for privacy-incident reproduction
body:
Issue summary:
A customer reported a potential PII leak in generated outputs from an application using redwood/longform-v1. We need a narrowly scoped forensic sample extract from production inference logs to reproduce the issue and validate whether PII appeared in model outputs.

Justification:
- Reproduction required to determine root cause (model prompt leakage vs. prompt content).
- Small, time-boxed sample minimizes exposure.

Scope of data requested:
- Time window: 2026-03-11 00:00:00 to 2026-03-11 23:59:59 (UTC)
- Up to 200 inference records matching the customer tenant and the reported conversation hashes.
- Include fields: request_id, customer_tenant_id (hashed), request_prompt (redacted), response_text (original and redacted copy), model_version, routing_info, timestamp, and system metadata (instance id, region).
- Explicit exclusion: do NOT include raw user identifiers or cu...

comments.author: Maya Chen
comments.body: Responding with approver list (Maya Chen, Ethan Park, Priya Natarajan, Legal: Jonah Ruiz), proposed KMS key arn: arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/forensic-int-32177, and rationale for unmasked copy: required for final triage and legal review; will be deleted after 7 days. Attached staging dry-run results (link to internal artifact).

comments.author: Jonah Ruiz (Legal)
comments.body: Running a quick contract check; if the customer agreement has a data-processing clause that allows debugging telemetry, Legal can approve with a customer notification choice. Legal requests a draft of the outbound notification and confirmation that no contractual BAA or higher restriction exists for this tenant.

comments.author: Ethan Park
comments.body: Performed staging dry-run and included sampling SQL in investigation_notes. Prepared extraction job but holding until Security & Legal approve KMS access and notification approach.

comments.author: Priya Natarajan ()

comments.author: Jonah Ruiz (Legal)
comments.body: Confirmed customer contract permits telemetry-based debugging; Legal approves with condition that customer is notified with opt-out option within 48 hours after extraction completes. Added requirement to retain audit trail of notification.

comments.author: Ethan Park
comments.body: Starting production extraction now. Will post manifest and S3 path once upload completes.

title: SSE stream truncates and resumes with interleaved metadata after edge cache chunk insertion
body:
Issue summary:
Customers observe SSE streams terminating early (HTTP 200 closed) with a partial JSON payload, then receiving a resumed stream that injects an edge-cache metadata chunk in the middle of the response. The resumed payload has metadata frames interleaved with tokens, which client SDKs mis-interpret and assemble into malformed JSON.

Impact:
- Multiple API consumers at Foodly see truncated recommendations or garbled JSON in ~2-3% of long streaming responses (conversations > 4k tokens).
- Causing client-side JSON.parse failures and repeated retries. Some retries lead to duplicated prefixes.

Environment:
- Dedicated customer tenant on prod-us-east using dedicated routing through our edge CDN (FastEdge).
- Streaming via SSE, client uses our JS SDK v2.3.1 behind a corporate proxy.

Steps to reproduce (observed):
1) Client issues long-stream generation request (max_tokens ~ 409...

comments.author: Ava Chen ()
comments.body: Customer Foodly reported repeated JSON.parse errors for long SSE streams. Attached client dump and edge traces. Marking as P1 due to production impact.

comments.author: Diego Martinez (SRE)
comments.body: Confirmed reproducible in our staging with FastEdge. Edge inserted raw metadata on cache-insert. Opening infra PR to change insertion path. Linked PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/infra/pull/4523

comments.author: Lina Park (Runtime)
comments.body: We see merged buffers contain the edge lines during resume. Will add sanitizer to runtime merge logic; need a test matrix to avoid stripping legitimate user output that begins with 'EDGE-'.

comments.author: Customer (Foodly, James R)
comments.body: Thanks for quick triage. We'll toggle cache_insert off while you prepare a runtime hotfix. Please confirm when safe to re-enable.

comments.author: Diego Martinez (SRE)
comments.body: Rolled edge config change to staging that writes comment-prefixed metadata (':EDGE-CACHE-INSERT: ...'). Confirmed SDK ignores those lines. Preparing rollout to prod with feature-flagged kill-switch.

comments.author: Ava Chen ()
comments.body: Notified customer to re-enable in 24h once runtime sanitizer is deployed. Monitoring alert rule (streaming.sse.partial_rate) set for Foodly to 0.5% threshold.

title: Ephemeral cross-account access for CostOps: AWS AssumeRole + GCP Workload Identity to read billing & audit logs
body:
Issue summary: CostOps needs temporary, auditable, least-privilege read access to billing exports and Cloud Audit Logs across a subset of prod and staging projects in both AWS and GCP. Request is for an ephemeral access flow (max 8 hours) using AWS AssumeRole with short-lived credentials and GCP Workload Identity Federation for human-triggered sessions via the existing access portal.

Background: The Finance/CostOps team needs to investigate a billing anomaly impacting several teams in us-east1 and eu-west1. Historically we provisioned long-lived IAM roles which create blast radius and auditing gaps. Security policy favors ephemeral, just-in-time access, scoped to read-only resources and logged with session-level metadata.

Request: 1) Create an AWS IAM role (arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/CostOpsBillingReadOnly-Ephemeral) with a trust policy allowing sts:AssumeRole only from our SSO/...

comments.body: Opening request — attaching a short justification doc and the billing export identifiers. Need access by 2026-03-16 to complete anomaly analysis.

comments.author: Alex Martinez () 2026-03-12
comments.body: Acknowledged. I'll validate existing portal mappings and trust relationships. Tentative plan: repro in staging by 2026-03-13 and propose a minimal role/policy for review.

comments.author: Jordan Liu
comments.body:
Note — AWS account 123456789012 already has a role 'CostAuditRole' with broad read permissions in us-east-1. We should avoid duplicating; recommend creating an ephemeral wrapper role that assumes into CostAuditRole with additional session tags for requestor_id.
Link: https://internal.redwood.ai/runbooks/access-management

comments.author: Elena Park
comments.body: Can we ensure MFA requirement and session tagging are enforced by the broker? I also want explicit deny statements removed only after review. Please send the exact trust policy and the portal PR before we approve.

comments.author: Alex Martinez 2026-03-14
comments.body: Uploaded a draft PR to infra: https://github.com/redwood-inference/infra/pull/6324. PR contains IAM role creation, AWS trust policy, and GCP service account binding. Test plan in PR description. Awaiting Security review.

comments.author: Elena Park 2026-03-14
comments.body: Reviewed PR comments — please add a condition to the AWS trust policy that requires aws:MultiFactorAuthPresent = true and tags: {"requested_by": "${session:requestor}"}. After that change, I will sign off for staging-only rollout.

comments.author: Alex Martinez 2026-03-14
comments.body: Will add the requested condition and re-run unit tests. Scheduling staging test at 2026-03-15 10:00 PDT and will loop in CostOps for verification.

title: Usage Insights: missing route breakdown for OpenAI-compatible endpoints (all traffic grouped under "unknown")
body:
Issue summary
Customer reports that Usage Insights (Console > Usage/Cost > Route/Model breakdown) does not show route-level rows for their OpenAI-compatible usage. Instead, traffic appears either:
- not attributed to any route (missing rows for /v1/chat/completions, /v1/embeddings), or
- grouped under a generic bucket like "unknown" / "unattributed" with correct totals but no breakdown.

Impact
- Customer cannot identify top cost drivers by endpoint for internal chargeback.
- Finance workflow blocked: export is not usable because route dimension is missing for their primary traffic.
- Customer is in active enterprise evaluation; this is a success criterion for beta.

Customer context
- AcmeAI is using Redwood OpenAI-compatible endpoints (OpenAI SDK pointed at Redwood base URL).
- They are comparing totals in Usage Insights vs billing export and invoice totals; totals match within tole...

normalized.description:
Issue summary
Customer reports that Usage Insights (Console > Usage/Cost > Route/Model breakdown) does not show route-level rows for their OpenAI-compatible usage. Instead, traffic appears either:
- not attributed to any route (missing rows for /v1/chat/completions, /v1/embeddings), or
- grouped under a generic bucket like "unknown" / "unattributed" with correct totals but no breakdown.

Impact
- Customer cannot identify top cost drivers by endpoint for internal chargeback.
- Finance workflow blocked: export is not usable because route dimension is missing for their primary traffic.
- Customer is in active enterprise evaluation; this is a success criterion for beta.

Customer context
- AcmeAI is using Redwood OpenAI-compatible endpoints (OpenAI SDK pointed at Redwood base URL).
- They are comparing totals in Usage Insights vs billing export and invoice totals; totals match within tole...

comments.body: 14 PT — Dev Patel (Support) — Ticket created from customer email + SE notes. Customer provided screenshot showing route column = "unknown" for ~92% of cost. Asked for exact base URL + whether they are using OpenAI SDK or direct Redwood API.

02 PT — Markus Klein (Solutions Eng) — Confirmed AcmeAI uses OpenAI SDK with base_url=https://api.redwoodinference.com/openai and model=llama-3.1-70b (via alias). Their requirement: cost by endpoint and model, exported weekly to finance.

25 PT — Chloe Martin (Console Backend) — Reproduced on acmeai-prod workspace. Query API returns route="unknown" rows. This looks upstream of console; pulling a sample of metering events for one hour window.

11 PT — Logan Wright (Telemetry) — Found missing route_template in metering events for compat traffic. For non-compat Redwood routes, route_template is present. Hypothesis: compat router bypasses route-normalization middleware.

06 PT — Thomas Baird (Telemetry Eng) — Confirmed: compat gateway handler emits metering with raw_path but does not call normalize_route(). Our aggregator uses route_template first; if empty, we bucket to unknown. Suggest patching compat emitter to always set route_template and route_canonical.

30 PT — Dev Patel (Support) — Sent customer update: root cause identified; ETA pending fix validation. Asked whether backfill is required or acceptable to start from fix-forward date. Customer replied: backfill “nice to have”, but they need this working for evaluation in the next few days.

18 PT — Chloe Martin (Console Backend) — Looping in Dylan Brooks for prioritization; customer evaluation blocker. Proposed to ship fix as part of route-normalization utility update + quick verification on us-east. Also need to ensure OpenAI-compat paths map to canonical /v1/* routes for reporting.

42 PT — Dylan Brooks (Console & Tooling) — Approved P1. Requested we align output naming with DevEx terminology: show routes as /v1/chat/completions etc (not /openai/v1/*). Add to release notes known issue list if we can’t backfill.

05 PT — Thomas Baird (Telemetry Eng) — Patch prepared to set route_template for compat endpoints. Mappings added: /v1/chat/completions, /v1/completions, /v1/embeddings, /v1/rerank (plus legacy /v1/responses if present). Testing with synthetic traffic shows route_template populated.

27 PT — Chloe Martin (Console Backend) — Validated aggregation picks up routes for new events; existing data still shows unknown. Data freshness: appears within normal lag (~2–3h). Asked Nadia Rahman about whether we can backfill 7d for this workspace only (likely no for beta).

10 PT — Nadia Rahman (Cost Attribution) — Backfill not planned for beta; requires recompute and careful reconciliation vs billing. Recommendation: communicate fix-forward and offer a short window recompute post-GA if needed. Ensure billing totals remain unchanged; this is purely dimension attribution.

48 PT — Dev Patel (Support) — Customer update: fix deployed, data will populate going forward; last ~24h should start showing within a few hours depending on aggregation lag. Offered to hop on a 15-min call to confirm in console and help with export.

05 PT — Markus Klein (Solutions Eng) — Confirmed with AcmeAI on call: route rows now appear for /v1/chat/completions and /v1/embeddings for the last several hours. They accept fix-forward; requested we document that historic buckets may show unknown during beta.
22 PT — Dev Patel (Support) — Marking Resolved. Follow-up item: add a short help-center note about unknown route bucket causes + how to verify OpenAI-compat traffic is mapped.

title: ICMP and UDP filtering at customer edge causes DoT/DoH fallback loop and intermittent gRPC streaming stalls on private deploy
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports intermittent model artifact download failures and GRPC streaming resets from their private deployment. Failures cluster around bulk inference jobs and long-lived streams.

Impact:
- Multiple inference requests returning 502/504 or streaming disconnects for a subset of serving nodes for ~10-30s every 5-20 minutes.
- High error rates during model pulls (~10-15% of downloads) when scale-up events occur.
- Customer workload (batch scoring) experiencing timeouts and downstream job failures.

Environment:
- Private deployment (customer-managed VPC) peered with Redwood control plane.
- Edge: Palo Alto SD-WAN appliance + corporate firewall policy.
- Observed region: prod/us-east-1.

Steps to reproduce (observed pattern):
1) Trigger parallel model pulls by scaling serving nodes (e.g., deploy new model or scale replica count).
2) Start long-lived streaming sessio...

comments.author: Aisha Njoroge
comments.body: Acknowledged. I've collected initial tcpdump and resolver logs from the customer. Escalating to SRE/networking. (sla: P1)

comments.author: Mateo Alvarez
comments.body: Observed PMTU ICMP frag-needed followed by resolver jumping through DoH/DoT fallback. Hypothesis: blocking UDP 53 combined with ICMP rate-limiting causes PMTU discovery to fail and pushes resolver into TLS-based flows which are mangled by the TLS inspection box.

comments.author: NetOps, Sara Li
comments.body: Confirmed we recently tightened egress rules and limit ICMP due to previous amplification attacks. We can temporarily allow UDP 53 and increase ICMP allowance for testing. Requesting guidance on safe config to unblock production.

comments.author: Aisha Njoroge
comments.body: Requested customer temporarily allow outbound UDP/53 to Redwoods control plane and allow ICMP frag-needed messages between serving subnets and their edge for 1 hour. Also suggested bypassing TLS inspection for traffic to redwood-inference endpoints (whitelist).

comments.author: Mateo Alvarez
comments.body: Customer performed the temporary allow. Errors dropped to baseline and artifact pulls completed. Need coordinated long-term mitigation plan.

comments.author: Sara Li
comments.body: We'll implement whitelist for redwood domains and relax ICMP rate-limit. Please provide recommended firewall rules and a runbook entry so SOC can approve change.

comments.author: Aisha Njoroge
comments.body: Preparing runbook & recommended firewall policy. Will include steps for whitelist, ICMP allowances, and verification commands.

title: Connection pool exhaustion causing sustained 504 waves from API gateway
body:
Issue summary: Starting ~2026-03-10 03:15 UTC we observed sustained waves of 504 Gateway Timeouts returned to customers in the dedicated environment. Impact: ~8% of requests to the customer's pinned model returned 504 over a 90-minute window, causing request retries and user-visible failures. Environment: Dedicated tenant (NimbusAnalytics) running in prod us-east-1, pinned to gpt-open-13b-v2. Initial symptom: API gateway returned 504 with upstream connection errors.

High level hypothesis: API gateway reported upstream connection pool exhaustion — idle connections were not being returned to the pool promptly because long-lived streaming sessions (some aborted by clients) did not fully release sockets on the serving-runtime side. This caused connection queues at the gateway to fill and new requests to be dropped with 504s until connections closed.

Customer reported 504s starting ~03:20 UTC. Confirmed scope and escalated to SRE.

Jenna Park (Runtime Eng) 2026-03-10: Identified missing release call in streaming proxy when client disconnects during initial handshake. Preparing hotfix.
Alex Li (SRE) 2026-03-10: Temporarily increased upstream pool and reduced backend keepalive TTL to force earlier socket cleanup as mitigation.
NimbusAnalytics CS contact (Evelyn Torres) 2026-03-11: Confirmed err...

title: Grafana permission denied for Route Experiments metrics dashboards (internal users)
body:
Issue summary:
Several internal Redwood users (Console/Tooling + Telemetry teams) receive "Permission denied" when opening the new Route Experiments Grafana dashboards (guardrails + attribution drop rate). This is blocking on-call prep and beta readiness reviews.

Impact:
- Engineers cannot verify guardrail alerts/dashboards prior to beta expansion.
- Slows down triage of experiment issues because links from alert payloads point to dashboards that are not accessible.

Who is affected:
- Reported by Console/Tooling and Telemetry team members who are not in SRE groups.
- Example users: Dylan Brooks, Chloe Martin (reported via DM to Observability).

What users see:
- Grafana banner: "Permission denied" / "You do not have permission to view this dashboard"
- Direct URL works for SRE users, fails for non-SRE.

Steps to reproduce:
1) Log into Grafana with an internal account not in SRE/Obse...

comments.body: 18 PT — Allison Grant (Reporter): Noticed multiple pings this morning that the Route Experiments dashboards are inaccessible to Console & Tooling. This will block the propagation lag game day and beta readiness checks. Can we fix folder ACLs and document the expected access path?

42 PT — Aisha Bello (Assignee): Confirmed. Repro’d with my non-SRE test account: permission denied at folder level. The dashboards are under the "SRE / Guardrails" folder, which is restricted to SRE + Observability Admin groups.

05 PT — Caleb Johnson: +1. I can’t open the attribution drop rate dashboard linked from the alert rule. Suggest moving these to a shared "Platform / Experiments" folder or adding Viewer access for eng-platform + eng-telemetry groups.

31 PT — Aisha Bello: Proposed change: keep dashboards in current folder, but add Viewer permissions for groups: eng-platform, eng-console-tooling, eng-telemetry-analytics. Will also add a short Confluence note for "How to request access" and update the runbook link section so on-call knows where to go.

20 PT — Aisha Bello: Implemented folder ACL update in Grafana: added Viewer access for eng-platform + eng-console-tooling + eng-telemetry-analytics. Verified with 2 users (Chloe Martin, Logan Wright) that the dashboards now load. Remaining issue: some users still can’t see folder in search unless they have org-level "Viewer" in Grafana (separate setting).
02 PT — Allison Grant: Confirmed dashboards load for me now. Thanks. Can we capture the org-level Grafana role requirement in docs so we don’t keep re-triaging this?

11 PT — Aisha Bello: Doc fix shipped: added a short section to the Experiment Health Dashboards spec explaining required Grafana roles + group mapping, and added "Access" step to the experiment assignment debugging runbook. Also posted a note in #eng-sre to route future requests through internal-support with the user’s Grafana org + groups.
28 PT — Allison Grant: Great. Marking resolved.

title: VPCE path: TLS session-resumption ticket mismatch causes control-plane auth loop and repeated gRPC reconnects
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports repeated control-plane authentication failures and gRPC reconnect storms after they routed control-plane traffic through a corporate VPCE + NGFW that performs TLS inspection using session-ticket rewriting. Symptoms began 2026-03-09 after a firewall firmware update at the customer.

Impact:
- Control-plane agents in the customer's private deployment repeatedly fail TLS handshakes during session resumption attempts and then fall back to full handshakes. This creates a high CPU and connection churn on serving nodes and intermittent 5xx for customer workloads.
- Observed client-facing errors: streaming connections dropping with gRPC INTERNAL or UNAVAILABLE error codes, short-lived auth loops in control-plane logs.
- Affects: production workloads for CardinalHealthTech (private deployment). Estimated ~30% request failure for long-lived streams during peak.

...

title: Intermittent 502/503 errors during large embedding uploads due to chunked body aborts at API gateway
body:
Issue summary:
Customer LexiconSoft reports elevated 502/503 errors and failed embedding upload jobs when sending large batched embedding payloads (~50–200MB) using our REST API over HTTP/2 with chunked transfer encoding. Errors began 2026-03-12 09:10 UTC after a gradual traffic increase.

Impact:
- Several customer embedding jobs failed (25% failure rate on a 40-job run).
- Increased API error-rate across their tenant (~P50->P95 error increase).
- Affects production P95 latency and caused retry storms from client side.

Environment:
- prod cluster in us-east-1; customer on enterprise plan with Dedicated routing to high-priority pool.
- Requests use HTTP/2 with chunked transfer-encoding and per-request streaming.
- Model: text-embedding-3-large pinned for this customer.

Observed behavior and tentative root cause hypothesis:
- API gateway logs show upstream proxy closing the request s...

title: API throttler feedback loop causing surge in 5xx errors and streaming session drops for PaperTrail Labs
body: Issue summary: Starting 2026-03-10 03:40 UTC we observed a sustained elevation in 5xx responses from the API gateway for traffic belonging to PaperTrail Labs' dedicated tenant. Errors manifested as HTTP 502/503 from the gateway and abrupt streaming session terminations. Impact: Customer-facing failures for PaperTrail's realtime chat product (multiple streams dropped, reconnect storms). Scale: Affected roughly 6% of the tenant's requests during the spike window (~10:30-11:15 PDT), with short bursts peaking at ~18% error rate. Business impact: user-facing errors and automated client retries resulting in support escalations. Timeline: - 2026-03-10 03:37 UTC: Monitoring alert triggers (gateway 5xx rate increased). - 2026-03-10 03:40 UTC: Support opened ticket; customer reports streaming disconnects. - 2026-03-10 04:05 UTC: On-call SRE and ingress engineer pulled into Slack (#support and #...

title: Embedding padding/trim mismatch causing rerank order oscillation and autoscaling surge
body: Issue summary: AtlasDocs reports intermittent rerank quality regressions (top-k order flips) coincident with brief 500/timeout spikes and a burst of autoscaling activity. Impact: customer search relevance fluctuates for ~5-30s windows, end-users see wrong top results and increased latency. Observed primarily on dedicated pool in us-east. Hypothesis: mismatched embedding vector length from ingest vs reranker (padding vs trim) interacts with prefix caching and leads to CPU fallback and reordering.

normalized.description: Issue summary: AtlasDocs reports intermittent rerank quality regressions (top-k order flips) coincident with brief 500/timeout spikes and a burst of autoscaling activity. Impact: customer search relevance fluctuates for ~5-30s windows, end-users see wrong top results and increased latency. Observed primarily on dedicated pool in us-east. Hypothesis: mismatched embedding vector length from ingest vs reranker (padding vs trim) interacts with prefix caching and leads to CPU fallback and reordering.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Customer reported intermittent relevance regressions across sessions. Attached examples and trace IDs. Customer currently on dedicated plan; requesting priority response.

comments.author: Rafael Ortega
comments.body: Acknowledged. Pulling logs, will validate cache key schema for prefix cache and check embedding headers.

comments.author: Maya Patel
comments.body: Found periods where cache hit returns had no 'embed-dim' tag; metrics show spike aligned with reported window. Captured heap and CPU profile during event.

comments.author: Rafael Ortega
comments.body: Reproduced locally in load test when mix of trimmed/padded vectors used. Creating PR to include embed-dim in cache key and enforce validation at ingest.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Shared temporary mitigation with AtlasDocs: set client to always send 'embed-dim' header or enable server-side strict validation. Customer enabled header and reported stability for 24h.

comments.author: Rafael Ortega
comments.body: Deployed hotfix (PR #4123) to staging that appends embed-dim to cache key and rejects incompatible cache hits. Running canary on dedicated pool with AtlasDocs; monitoring indicates reduced 5xx and reorder anomalies.

comments.author: AtlasDocs (Customer)
comments.body: Confirmed improvement, no further rerank flips observed during morning traffic spike. Asked for permanent fix timeline and test coverage.

comments.author: Notes
comments.body: linked PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/serving/pull/4123; runbook updated at linked confluence page.

title: IdP-initiated SLO probe truncates session cookie domain causing re-auth loops and 401s in private VPC
body: Issue summary: After configuring IdP-initiated SSO for Nimbus Health's Private deployment, periodic IdP single-logout (SLO) probes from their vendor (AuthWave) are observed to cause the Redwood ingress to rewrite the session cookie domain by truncating tenant namespace suffixes. Impact: End-users and service accounts intermittently hit 401/reauth loops across the Redwood admin and ingest APIs. Expected: SLO probes should not alter or truncate session cookie domain/path; logout events should be processed without breaking other sessions. Environment details: Private control plane deployed into customer's VPC using edge-proxy v2.1.7, ingress-proxy v3.4.0, auth-bridge v1.9. Deployed certs: internal mTLS + customer-managed IdP metadata via SAML. Observed window: recurring since 2026-03-09 02:00 UTC, roughly every 6–8 hours when IdP health probes run.

normalized.description: Issue summary: After configuring IdP-initiated SSO for Nimbus Health's Private deployment, periodic IdP single-logout (SLO) probes from their vendor (AuthWave) are observed to cause the Redwood ingress to rewrite the session cookie domain by truncating tenant namespace suffixes. Impact: End-users and service accounts intermittently hit 401/reauth loops across the Redwood admin and ingest APIs. Expected: SLO probes should not alter or truncate session cookie domain/path; logout events should be processed without breaking other sessions. Environment details: Private control plane deployed into customer's VPC using edge-proxy v2.1.7, ingress-proxy v3.4.0, auth-bridge v1.9. Deployed certs: internal mTLS + customer-managed IdP metadata via SAML. Observed window: recurring since 2026-03-09 02:00 UTC, roughly every 6–8 hours when IdP health probes run.

comments.author: Ava Chen ()
comments.body: Customer reports large number of users are hitting login loops after IdP maintenance windows. Collected ingress and auth logs; provided preliminary correlation to SLO probe timestamps.

comments.author: Ravi Patel (SRE)
comments.body: Reproduced probe in staging; ingress-proxy treats Host header without tenant suffix as 'global' and applies fallback cookie-domain logic that strips dot segments. Hypothesis: domain canonicalization code doesn't preserve multi-label internal domains when Host lacks tenant prefix.

comments.author: Auth team
comments.body: Found a change in ingress-proxy v3.4.0 that tightens effective TLD parsing; edge case triggers when Host header contains fewer labels than expected. Preparing patch to skip truncate behavior for session cookies.

comments.author: Nimbus Health
comments.body: Confirmed that when our IdP probe uses tenant-less host header the admin console fails for all web sessions. We can temporarily disable the IdP probe but need a durable fix.

comments.author: Ravi Patel (SRE)
comments.body: Deployed a hotfix to staging that adds a guard: only perform cookie-domain truncation if host contains tenant namespace pattern. Requesting customer validation and will schedule private control-plane patching if validated.

title: Erratic 5xx surge correlating with prefetch stall + connection-throttling on API gateway
body:
Issue summary:
Customer (CloudNote Labs, dedicated) started seeing a sudden increase in 5xx responses for text-generation API calls beginning ~2026-03-10 01:18 UTC. Errors are intermittent but sustained in bursts over 10-30 minute windows.

Impact:
- ~18% of requests from the customer's endpoint returned 5xx during bursts, affecting production feature serving (user-facing).
- Visible increase in tail latency for streaming sessions.

Environment:
- Hosted: Dedicated pool for CloudNote (us-east region).
- Models: redwood/open-llama-7b pinned -> gptq-6b fallback configured.
- Traffic pattern: periodic prefetch-heavy workload (long prompts + multi-call prefetch).

Steps to reproduce (observed):
1) Run a sustained throughput test with prefetch_enabled=true and keep-alive connections from ~100 concurrent clients.
2) Observe API gateway connection throttling counters spike and upstream runti...

title: Intermittent model artifact fetch failures from private deploy due to split-horizon DNS + TGW route propagation race causing OCSP lookup mismatch
body:
Issue summary: Customer reports intermittent failures fetching model artifacts from S3/HTTP origin during private deployment. Failures correlate with NAT/TGW route churn and control-plane DNS conditional forwarding. Impact: model pulls fail sporadically causing cold-start timeouts and 5xx for in-flight requests. Environment: private deployment in Acme AI VPC connected to Redwood control plane via Transit Gateway and a pair of NAT gateways in the egress subnet.

Observed behaviour: when upstream route propagation completes, DNS results from split-horizon forwarder flip between an internal resolver (that rewrites OCSP responders to private IPs) and public resolution. At the same time ephemeral egress IPs are reassigned during NAT replacement, producing transient TLS stapling verification failures and OCSP timeouts, which our artifact fetch code treats as fatal rather than retryable.

comments.author: Jake Alvarez
comments.body: Provided tcpdump and VPC flow logs. Noted that maintenance window correlates with AWS-managed NAT replacement. Willing to test quick fix (persistent EIP) in dev account.

comments.author: Mei Lin (SRE)
comments.body: Confirmed TGW route propagation logs show delay ~15-25s on propagation to transit attachments. Split-horizon forwarder TTL is 30s which can present mixed answers during that window.

comments.author: Carlos Mendes ()
comments.body: Reproduced locally in staging using TGW route change simulation and patched artifact-fetcher to retry OCSP lookup and fall back to control-plane proxy for up to 6s; initial results show retries reduce failure rate.

title: Long-lived SSE connections receiving 429s after enabling per-route shaping - request for SSE exemption/burst pool guidance
body:
Issue summary: Customer BrightSensor reports increased 429 throttles on long-lived server-sent events (SSE) connections after enabling per-route shaping to isolate webhook traffic from background bulk jobs. Impact: customer-facing stream disruptions and elevated error counts in their UI; some customers see reconnection storms. Environment: Dedicated pool, prod us-east, tenant uses subaccount tokens and separates traffic via route regex patterns (/api/v1/streams/* and /api/v1/bulk/*).

Expected behavior: Long-lived SSE connections should be accounted under a long-lived connection bucket and only incur occasional keepalive token accounting, not the same burst window as short-lived request/response flows.

Actual behavior: After per-route shaping flag toggle, SSE connections begin receiving 429s roughly 5-10 minutes after start; logs show token-window depletion tied to per-route refill t...

comments.author: BrightSensor - Jordan (Customer) 2026-03-10
comments.body: Provided logs and confirmed pattern - SSE 429s start after ~6m and sometimes trigger client reconnect backoff. We're on a Dedicated lease (monthly).

comments.author: Mateo Ruiz (SRE) 2026-03-11
comments.body: Hypothesis - per-route refill windows are optimized for short RPCs; recommend creating separate rate class for long-lived connections with larger burst and longer refill window. Will validate on staging.

comments.author: Mateo Ruiz (SRE) 2026-03-11
comments.body: Ran controlled test - increased burst_capacity on streams route and introduced keepalive exemption that charges 1 token per 60s rather than per packet; 0 429s observed for 30m test. Need product sign-off for persistent config.

comments.body: Needs documented guidance + console UI affordance to configure 'connection class' for routes. Flagging as follow-up product request.

title: Device attestation key rotation breaks CLI session binding for internal devtools
body:
Issue summary:
A recent rotation of the device attestation signing key (identity-service) appears to have invalidated existing CLI session bindings. Developer CLI clients receive repeated MFA prompts and then a hard 'session mismatch' error that prevents access to multiple internal tools (devtools-cli, runbook-runner, internal-grafana-proxy).

Impact:
- ~45 engineers unable to run infra CLI commands or access runbook tooling from corporate devices.
- Automated runbooks triggered from CI intermittently fail due to inability to exchange ephemeral session tokens.
- No broad user-facing customer impact, but critical for oncall and incident response workflows.

Environment:
- Prod control plane (identity-service) in us-west-2.
- Affected clients primarily macOS and Linux corporate laptops with device-attestation enabled.

Steps to reproduce:
1) Attempt to login with devtools-cli from a cor...

title: Amplified health-check + ephemeral key rotation fanout causing sustained 429s for bulk ingest
body: Issue summary: Starting 2026-03-05 03:10 UTC Acme Analytics observed a rapid increase in 429 responses across their bulk-ingest jobs. The errors correlate with scheduled ephemeral API key rotation and concurrent health-check sweeps from their orchestrator. Impact: ~40% of chunked uploads were rejected with 429s over a 45-minute window; ingestion lag increased and downstream retry waves amplified tenant token exhaustion. Environment: Dedicated pool (reserved), prod-us-west, tenant id T-ACME-77. Customer reports they did not change upload concurrency during the window. They are blocked from completing nightly backfills.

normalized.description: Issue summary: Starting 2026-03-05 03:10 UTC Acme Analytics observed a rapid increase in 429 responses across their bulk-ingest jobs. The errors correlate with scheduled ephemeral API key rotation and concurrent health-check sweeps from their orchestrator. Impact: ~40% of chunked uploads were rejected with 429s over a 45-minute window; ingestion lag increased and downstream retry waves amplified tenant token exhaustion. Environment: Dedicated pool (reserved), prod-us-west, tenant id T-ACME-77. Customer reports they did not change upload concurrency during the window. They are blocked from completing nightly backfills.

comments.author: Ava Chen (2026-03-05)
comments.body: Customer reached out via support form at 03:15 UTC reporting elevated 429s during their nightly ingest. Provided initial logs and sample request IDs. Asked for immediate mitigation advice.

comments.author: Diego Martinez (, 2026-03-05)
comments.body: Acknowledged; opened ticket and requested tenant-level rate-limiter dump and exact key-rotation schedule. Added priority P1 due to ingestion blocking. Suggested temporary reduce of concurrency if possible.

comments.author: Priya Nair (SRE, 2026-03-05)
comments.body: Pulled rate-limiter traces. Observed synchronized drain of tenant token bucket coincident with ephemeral key rebinds and a wave of health-check retries. Hypothesis: key rotation forced clients to re-auth and re-open connections; combined with aggressive health probes this produced a short-lived fanout that consumed burst credits across many routes.

comments.author: Acme , 2026-03-05
comments.body: Confirmed orchestrator rotates keys every 5 minutes and health checks are across all workers. Asked for configuration recommendations to avoid losing burst credits.

comments.author: Diego Martinez (, 2026-03-06)
comments.body: Applied a temporary tenant-level burst increase on dedicated pool (x2 for 30 minutes) to allow backlog flush. Customer confirmed immediate drop in 429s but noted concern about recurrence during next rotation window.

comments.author: Priya Nair (SRE, 2026-03-07)
comments.body: Requested follow-up with engineering to consider non-blocking rebind behavior for ephemeral keys and to add exemption for internal health probes from tenant token accounting (or route health-checks to internal admin route).

comments.author: Diego Martinez (, 2026-03-09)
comments.body: Sent recommended long-term configuration and steps to customer; waiting for them to apply staggered rotation and probe changes. Marked ticket In Progress while monitoring next scheduled rotation.

title: Per-tenant delivery receipts, watermarking, and compliance evidence crosswalk request
body:
Issue summary: Customer BrightHealth Analytics requests a bundled compliance artifact that demonstrates per-tenant ordered delivery, per-event delivery receipts, persistent watermarking (monotonic event sequence), and a crosswalk mapping between the audit fields we provide and the customer SIEM schema.

Impact: Customer needs this artifact to complete a data residency + SIEM compliance package for their SOC review. Without the bundle they cannot finalize onboarding of a regulated workload, delaying an enterprise contract.

Environment: Production (us-east-1). Customer is on enterprise plan with private ingestion endpoints and uses our tenant-scoped downstream webhooks into their Splunk HEC cluster.

Request details: BrightHealth expects: (1) a per-tenant manifest showing monotonic watermarks and last-applied offsets for the period 2026-02-01 to 2026-02-28, (2) delivery-receipt logs fo...

title: Scheduled dashboard snapshot suppressed when query includes tracehook + aliased route identifiers
body:
Issue summary:
Scheduled snapshot jobs for dashboards that include a query-level tracehook (tracehook param in the query) are being suppressed and not persisted when the query references aliased route identifiers (route aliases used by the customer).

Impact:
- OrbitPay scheduled daily snapshots for 20 dashboards show 'Last run: succeeded' but no snapshot file appears in the scheduled_reports listing and email reports are empty for affected dashboards.
- This affects reporting and SLO visibility for two top-level product teams during their monthly review.
- Customer-visible data loss for scheduled exports (not ad-hoc snapshots).

Environment:
- Prod (us-east-1)
- Tenant: orbitpay-prod (enterprise)
- Dashboards affected are high-cardinality, use route aliasing for multi-tenant routes and include tracehook parameters in WHERE clauses.

Steps to reproduce (minimal):
1) Create dashboard w...

title: Okta MFA enrollment loop prevents SSO access to internal Console for new hires
body:
Issue summary:
Several new hires cannot complete Okta SSO to access the Redwood Console because they enter an MFA enrollment loop. The Okta flow repeatedly prompts for MFA enrollment and then immediately returns to the login screen without granting a session. This affects onboarding engineers and blocks critical access to runbooks and incident tooling.

Impact:
- 8 new employees (onboarding cohort) unable to access console since 2026-03-12 08:20 UTC.
- Prevents completion of onboarding tasks and emergency incident response for affected users.

Environment:
- Redwood Console (https://console.redwood.ai) behind Okta SSO.
- Affected in prod/us-east for new users created through SCIM this morning.

Steps to reproduce:
1) Create test user via SCIM with same group set as onboarding cohort.
2) Attempt first-time SSO login at https://console.redwood.ai via Okta.
3) Okta prompts for MFA enroll...

title: Timeboxed handoff of sanitized customer telemetry to Finance for SOX evidence
body:
Issue summary: Finance compliance has requested a timeboxed delivery of minimal, de-identified telemetry slices tied to three invoices for Q4 audits. The request is for token-level aggregate metrics and a tiny sample of sanitized request/response metadata (no original prompt text or PII).

Impact: Regulatory audit required by Finance. No customer-facing outage, but we must produce verifiable artifacts within 5 business days to satisfy auditors.

Requested scope: - Aggregate token counts per invoice ID (provided by Finance) for two billing periods. - Sampled request metadata: timestamp, model-id, route-id, response-latency, anonymized session-hash. - Zero original user content; all textual payloads must be removed or replaced with deterministic hashes per our redaction SOP.

Acceptable outputs: CSVs with columns as above, plus a short README describing redaction method and hash salt (K...

comments.author: Aisha Rahman
comments.body: Acknowledged. Need to confirm redaction recipe and KMS escrow plan. Will triage today. Tentative list of fields NOT allowed to appear in any output: raw_prompt, raw_response, user_email, api_key, ip_address.

comments.author: Carl Ng
comments.body: Will perform deterministic hashing review and run synthetic tests against sample payloads. Recommend sample size <= 250 sessions per invoice, sampled uniformly by session start time.

comments.body: Planning extraction steps: 1) narrow telemetry query by invoice tag, 2) run redaction pipeline in staging mirror, 3) produce CSVs and README, 4) request Security to validate. ETA: 2026-03-17 EOD if approvals are fast.

comments.author: Lena Ortiz
comments.body: Confirmed we can create a timeboxed S3 bucket with expiring credentials and object-level access logs enabled. Will add a short CloudTrail snippet to demonstrate chain-of-custody.

comments.author: Priya Singh
comments.body: Confirmed SOX relevance. We need a signed attestation from Finance that they will not attempt to re-identify any customers from provided artifacts. Attestation template linked in Confluence.

comments.author: Aisha Rahman
comments.body: Reviewed redaction test results from staging run. Found two telemetry keys that could leak region-specific hostnames; patched redaction config and re-ran. Clean output confirmed. Approving timeboxed extract contingent on attestation and presence of KMS escrow log.

comments.author: Jordan Patel ()
comments.body: Uploaded final CSVs to s3://redwood-secure-handoffs/INT-999888/ and attached README. Temps creds issued to ben.alvarez@finance.redwood with 72-hour access window. Notified Finance and Legal. Marking for resolution after attestation is returned.

comments.author: Ben Alvarez (Finance)
comments.body: Received files and KMS logs. Signed attestation sent to legal@redwood.ai. Thanks to the team for quick turnaround.

title: Tenant burst traffic caused cascading 502s at API proxy and unhealthy upstreams leading to runtime restarts
body: AtlasRetail reported a sudden increase in 502 errors for their production routes after a traffic surge. Errors are observed at the API proxy layer with upstreams marked unhealthy and several serving runtime worker processes restarting under load. Customer reports higher latency and partial request failures across their pinned model routes.

comments.body: Customer reported customer-visible failures at 12:15 UTC. Collected request IDs and sample payloads.

comments.body: Looking at proxy metrics and connection limits; temporarily increasing connection pool and enabling short-term burst window for atlas-account to mitigate.

title: Evening spike of 5xx on streaming route during autoscaler scale-down (GPU preempt + TLS affinity break)
body:
Issue summary: Between ~19:22 and 19:45 UTC on 2026-03-10 we observed a sharp elevation in 5xx errors for the customer-facing streaming chat route for Nimbus Health (enterprise). The failures were primarily 502/503 on the API gateway and upstream runtime returning abrupt stream termination. Impact: multiple customers experienced dropped streaming responses and retries resulting in degraded UX for embedded chat in production. Intermittent but repeatable during autoscaler scale-down events.

Observed behavior:
- API gateway logs show many upstream 502/503s and TLS handshake reset entries coincident with autoscaler drain events.
- Serving runtime logs show SIGTERM followed by NVML driver resets and abrupt process reaper activity for some worker threads.
- Customers reported partial transcripts and immediate connection resets rather than graceful stream closure.

Why this is likely critic...

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Customer reported sudden wave of streaming disconnects across multiple users. Collected initial logs and escalated to SRE.

comments.author: Diego Martinez
comments.body: Observed GPU resets in kernel logs immediately after SIGTERM on several runtime pods. Correlating with kuedev autoscaler events. Hypothesis: pods are being terminated while heavy KV flush is happening; preStop hook timing is insufficient.

comments.author: Priya Kaur
comments.body: Pushed a canary bump for terminationGracePeriodSeconds and added a preStop sleep+drain. Monitoring now. Reduced hedged retry concurrency at apigw.

comments.author: Nimbus Health - Ops
comments.body: Confirmed reduced errors overnight after the fix. They saw a single reconnect but no transcript loss. Requested a postmortem and permanent config change.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Marked resolved after 48h of stable telemetry and customer confirmation. Added follow-ups into SRE backlog.

title: Branch office HTTP-CONNECT tunnel proxy injects Alt-Svc/HTTP3 causing QUIC fallback and intermittent presigned range fetch failures on private deploy
body:
Issue summary: Customer reports intermittent failures when fetching large model shard ranges from S3-backed presigned URLs using a private deployment behind a branch-office HTTP-CONNECT tunneling proxy. The customer's proxy rewrites HTTP responses and is observed injecting Alt-Svc/HTTP3/QUIC hints into 200 responses. Impact: Several tens of range GETs for large shards are returning 403/416/connection reset or large delays, leading to degraded warmup and increased token latency for hosted inference. Impact window appears correlated with branch-office work hours.

Environment: Private deployment (VPCE-connected nodes) in us-east-1, customers use a branch-office HTTP-CONNECT tunneling proxy for egress that performs response header rewriting and an ALPN/alt-svc cache. Customer reports the proxy is managed by their corporate networking team and is not under Redwood control.

Observed behav...

title: Tenant routed to secondary variant despite explicit model pin during orchestrator retry surge
body: Issue summary: One enterprise customer observed tenant traffic being routed to a secondary (fallback) variant even though their project had an explicit model version pin. Impact: customer-facing production traffic experienced degraded model behavior and increased cost due to falling back to a larger, higher-cost variant. This started during a rolling canary of a separate release when orchestrator retry activity spiked.

normalized.description: Issue summary: One enterprise customer observed tenant traffic being routed to a secondary (fallback) variant even though their project had an explicit model version pin. Impact: customer-facing production traffic experienced degraded model behavior and increased cost due to falling back to a larger, higher-cost variant. This started during a rolling canary of a separate release when orchestrator retry activity spiked.

comments.body: 35Z - Support (Ava Chen): Acknowledged issue with customer, requested environment details and time window. Informed customer of P1 escalation.
00Z - SRE (Luis Martinez): Confirmed pin exists at control plane and edge metadata; capturing control-plane logs and initiating deeper trace collection. Added temporary strict-pin policy for tenant.

12Z - Eng (J. Patel): Reproduced in staging by simulating orchestrator retry storm. Found commit_manager race; drafting patch to preserve grace_window on duplicate commit_id. See PR draft in channel #sre-orchestrator.
00Z - Eng (J. Patel): Hotfix deployed to eu-west. Monitoring shows routing decisions honour pins post-deploy. Will schedule canary of fix to other regions.
30Z - Customer Success (Maria Gomez): Notified Acme AI that patch is live and asked for confirmation of observed behavior. Customer reported model outputs normalized and no further fallback traffic.
45Z - Support (Ava Chen): Closing loop with customer; will follow up on postmortem and next steps for multi-region hardening.

This started during a rolling canary of a separate release when orchestrator retry activity spiked.
