title: SDK sidecar keepalive trickle results in suppressed final chunk for long-running SSE sessions
body:
Issue summary: Customer reports that long-running SSE streams from our hosted endpoint sometimes arrive to their client with all content except the final concluding chunk. Impact: Final sentence or closing JSON metadata is missing in 1-3% of sessions for a specific mobile app version, causing truncated completions and downstream UI to show a perpetual 'typing' indicator. Environment: Dedicated customer (Acme Retail) using Redwood hosted API via the mobile SDK v1.8.3 that runs a local sidecar process to multiplex network activity on flaky mobile connections.

Observed behavior differs from previous SSE truncation tickets because the server completes generation and emits the end event (visible in backend logs), but the client never receives the terminal chunk in a subset of reconnect/resume scenarios.

Observed behavior differs from previous SSE truncation tickets because the server completes generation and emits the end event (visible in backend logs), but the client never receives the terminal chunk in a subset of reconnect/resume scenarios.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Customer reports truncated final chunk in ~1.7% of long SSEs. Provided initial request id and stream ids.

comments.author: Liam Patel
comments.body: Collected edge/rt logs and packet captures; initial hypothesis: proxy coalescing with client-side micro-writes. Asked customer to enable SDK debug logging and to try a short test with keepalive disabled.

comments.author: Acme Mobile
comments.body: We toggled sidecar keepalive batching off and ran 1000 sessions; observed 0 truncations in that run. Re-enabled for subsequent tests and saw truncations return. Shared logs and a capture of client probes.

comments.author: Nina Gomez
comments.body: Noted an ALB change last month that altered small-write coalescing behavior; opened INT-1122 to investigate config. Will test with stricter Nagle settings and TCP_NODELAY variants.

comments.author: Liam Patel
comments.body: Proposed two short-term mitigations and a long-term fix. Posted steps below. Customer accepted mitigation A and is running validation.

comments.author: Acme Mobile
comments.body: Applying mitigation A now, will report back after 24h of traffic.

title: On-demand delta CSV export with UTC normalization, rotating MD5 manifest, and proof-of-exclusion for deleted records
body:
Issue summary: Customer requests a one-off delta export of their tenant activity as a CSV with strict UTC timestamp normalization, a rotating MD5 manifest for each batch file, and a machine-verifiable 'proof-of-exclusion' artifact listing records deleted since the last snapshot. They need the package for a regulatory evidence submission and require deterministic ordering and replayability.

Requirements: (1) CSV delta with canonical UTC timestamps and ISO8601 formatting; (2) each batch file accompanied by an MD5 manifest entry and a signed manifest file (HMAC-SHA256 with their provided per-tenant key); (3) a separate "proof-of-exclusion" file: a compact list of deleted record IDs with per-ID deletion-tombstone hashes and a top-level Merkle-root signed by Redwood to prove non-inclusion in prior snapshots; (4) resume tokens and offsets for replay; (5) timezone normalization mapping note...

comments.body: Hi Support - We need a delta export for tenant fincloud-prod with proofs for deletions. Audit window closes 2026-03-20. Please confirm format and delivery options (SFTP preferred).

comments.author: Diego Morales ()
comments.body: Acknowledged. Can you confirm the last export offset (timestamp) you received? Also we support HMAC-signed manifests; will you provide a per-tenant HMAC key or do you want Redwood to sign and provide a verifiable certificate?

comments.author: FinCloud (Customer)
comments.body: We have last offset token: off_2026_02_25_17_03. We will provide tenant HMAC key via secure upload. Proof-of-exclusion should be verifiable by auditors (Merkle root acceptable).

comments.author: Diego Morales ()
comments.body: Internal note -> pinging infra: need export job to include deletion-tombstones, compute per-id SHA256 hash, and assemble Merkle tree. Also need short-lived signed manifest generation using export-signing key (sig-v1). Assign to Eng export run team.

comments.author: Export Run
comments.body: Investigating feasibility. Our current delta exporter can include tombstones but does not produce Merkle roots. Estimate 2 workdays to add Merkle compute and signed-manifest step as a one-off pipeline change. Will propose a service account to sign the manifest and provide a verification public key.

comments.author: Diego Morales ()
comments.body: Customer updated: delivery window acceptable for 2026-03-16 if we can produce signed artifacts. Asked Eng for timeline and temporary workarounds (e.g., provide per-id hashed tombstone list and sign the file with our PGP key).

normalized.cleanBody:
Issue summary: Customer requests a one-off delta export of their tenant activity as a CSV with strict UTC timestamp normalization, a rotating MD5 manifest for each batch file, and a machine-verifiable 'proof-of-exclusion' artifact listing records deleted since the last snapshot. They need the package for a regulatory evidence submission and require deterministic ordering and replayability.

Requirements: (1) CSV delta with canonical UTC timestamps and ISO8601 formatting; (2) each batch file accompanied by an MD5 manifest entry and a signed manifest file (HMAC-SHA256 with their provided per-tenant key); (3) a separate "proof-of-exclusion" file: a compact list of deleted record IDs with per-ID deletion-tombstone hashes and a top-level Merkle-root signed by Redwood to prove non-inclusion in prior snapshots; (4) resume tokens and offsets for replay; (5) timezone normalization mapping note...

This ticket coordinates procurement, legal review, SCIM/SSO provisioning, and cost-center allocation.

title: Release branch CI: permissions + secrets setup for runtime LTS artifact publishing (signing, provenance, SBOM)
body:
Requesting internal support to configure CI permissions + secrets needed to build and publish Runtime LTS artifacts from release branches (e.g., runtime-1.18-lts) with signing and provenance.

Background
- We are standing up an enterprise-friendly Runtime LTS channel. Release branches must be able to produce auditable artifacts (images + checksums/provenance + optional SBOM) and publish them to the appropriate registries/buckets.
- Current CI is configured for mainline only; release branches are failing due to missing secrets and insufficient permissions.

What we need
1) GitHub Actions permissions for release branches
- Allow workflows to run on branches matching: runtime-*-lts, release/* (final naming TBD but we already have runtime-1.18-lts).
- Ensure branch protection + required checks still apply.
- Enable required OIDC permissions where we are using short-lived cloud creds (pref...

comments.author: Connor O'Brien
comments.body: Creating this as INT because we need infra/CI config changes in GitHub + secret provisioning. Release branch is blocked for pilot timeline.

comments.author: Sahana Iyer
comments.body: Acknowledged. Can you confirm target repos + workflows? Assuming redwood (runtime), redwood-private-installer, perf-canary. Also confirm if we’re publishing to prod registry from GitHub Actions or via an internal release runner.

comments.author: Connor O'Brien
comments.body: Primary is github.com/redwood/redwood (runtime). Private installer + helm can come next; for now just runtime images + checksums/provenance. Publishing is from GHA with environment approvals (release managers).

comments.author: Sahana Iyer
comments.body: Investigated failing runs on runtime-1.18-lts. Root cause: release branches don’t have access to the "release" environment secrets because environment is restricted to main branch only. Also OIDC provider trust policy only allows refs/heads/main. Will update trust to include refs/heads/runtime-*-lts and refs/heads/release/*.

comments.author: Hanae Suzuki
comments.body: For signing keys: Security prefers keyless signing via OIDC + Fulcio/Rekor where possible. If not feasible short-term, we can permit a short-lived transition using KMS-backed key (no raw private key in GitHub). Please confirm current cosign setup.

comments.author: Sahana Iyer
comments.body: Current pipeline expects COSIGN_KEY/COSIGN_PASSWORD secrets. I propose we switch to keyless (cosign sign --identity-token) using OIDC; that requires updating workflow + adding "id-token: write" permissions. Connor, do you want to take workflow change, or should I patch it here?

comments.author: Connor O'Brien
comments.body: I’ll open a small PR to switch to keyless signing for LTS publish jobs. For now we still need permissions + environment protection fixed so we can proceed.

comments.author: Sahana Iyer
comments.body:
Implemented:
- Updated GitHub Actions environment "release" to allow deployments from branches matching runtime-*-lts and release/*.
- Added required reviewers for "release" env: Connor O'Brien + Sahana Iyer + Dr. Aisha Rahman (security) (2 required).
- Updated OIDC trust policy in cloud to permit refs/heads/runtime-*-lts and release/*.
- Split secrets into "release-staging" and "release-prod" envs; prod requires reviewer approval.
Next: validate by rerunning on runtime-1.18-lts with a dry-run publish to staging bucket/registry.

comments.author: Connor O'Brien
comments.body: Ran workflow with publish-to-staging=true. Registry push succeeded. Artifact upload succeeded. Signing step still failing because workflow uses key-based signing; will be fixed by my PR switching to keyless.

comments.author: Connor O'Brien
comments.body: Merged workflow update for keyless signing and provenance attestation upload. Reran on runtime-1.18-lts. All steps green; produced signed image + checksums + provenance JSON and uploaded to staging location.

comments.author: Sahana Iyer
comments.body: Confirmed secrets are not exposed on PRs/forks. Publish jobs require environment approval and only run on push tags/branch dispatch. Added audit note: approvals visible in GitHub environment deployment history; also logging workflow run ID into provenance metadata.

comments.author: Connor O'Brien
comments.body: Verified we can publish an RC tag (1.18.1-lts-rc1) to staging and a GA tag to prod with env approvals. This unblocks first patch runbook. Marking resolved; follow-up ticket needed for private-installer/helm env secrets but not required for this INT.

title: Tenant webhook audit stream: mutual-TLS client cert rotation, backfill requests, and SOC2 evidence bundle
body:
Issue summary:
GreenSpring Health uses our tenant-scoped webhook endpoint for real-time audit delivery. During a scheduled client certificate rotation on 2026-03-09 they reported a gap: ~18k audit events (approx. 3 hours) did not arrive at their collector. They need (1) a validated backfill of the missing events for tenant T-GREEN-12 with chronological ordering preserved, (2) cryptographic proof of the server-side signing/checkpoint used during the backfill window, and (3) a signed artifact demonstrating the client cert rotation timeline (previous cert thumbprint, rotation timestamp, and validation that no events were re-delivered outside the window).

Impact:
- Missing audit records for regulated ePHI ingestion pipeline (GreenSpring Health, HIPAA scope).
- Customer cannot complete urgent SOC2 evidence packet for their auditor until audit continuity is proven.

Environment:
- Tenant: ...

Environment:
- Tenant: ...

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Customer opened with missing audit events and has auditor deadline next week. Marked P1 due to regulatory impact. Assigned to Diego for investigation.

comments.author: Diego Martinez
comments.body: Confirmed receipt of collector logs. Observed a series of TLS handshake failures originating at 02:09:46 UTC (see request-ids batch 87f3-...). Opening infra trace to map delivery attempts to queue state. Placing hold on automated replay until we validate ordering semantics.

comments.author: SRE - Priya Nair
comments.body: Infra trace shows our webhook worker made retries at exponential backoff starting 02:11:02 UTC. Client returned 421 for 18,320 deliveries; our retry policy expired after 6 attempts. Retention projection: events are present in tenant ring buffer and object archive. No evidence of intentional suppression on server side. Need approval to perform controlled backfill to customer endpoint and to generate signed manifest.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Noted customer compliance requirements; requested legal/ps to pre-approve signed artifact format. Escalated to security for signing key use case.

comments.author: Omar Patel
comments.body: Approved use of ephemeral signing key from audit-keyring with documented nonce. Provided PRNG seed and KMS attestation link. Proceed with requested signed manifest once SRE provides backfill plan.

comments.author: Diego Martinez
comments.body: Plan drafted: (1) Generate chronological export from object archive for 2026-03-09 02:00--06:00 UTC for tenant T-GREEN-12; (2) produce gzipped newline-delimited JSON with event_ids and timestamps; (3) compute SHA256 manifest and sign via audit-keyring; (4) push backfill via our delivery pipeline to customer collector in a single PUT to their secure S3 endpoint (customer requested non-live push to avoid duplicate delivery); (5) provide per-request delivery attempt CSV mapping and TLS handshake log snippets. Waiting on customer confirmation for S3 endpoint and acceptance of non-live delivery method.

comments.author: GreenSpring Health
comments.body: Confirmed S3 bucket endpoint is ready (s3://greenspring-uploads/redwood-backfill/) with bucket policy limiting to Redwood IPs. Requests signed manifest and per-request trace. Auditor wants chain-of-custody (who exported, who signed, timestamps).

comments.author: Diego Martinez
comments.body: Noted. Will include operator id and GitHub PR reference for export job. Scheduling export run for 2026-03-12 16:00 UTC. Will attach KMS attestation and operator approval notes to the artifact.

title: Prorated invoice line for async toolcall retry traffic not matching postback records
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports an unexpected prorated invoice line that appears to bill for ~18,000 toolcall invocations during a two-hour window on 2026-03-06. The customer asserts those invocations were retry traffic from a previous deploy and should have been tagged as test/heartbeat and therefore not billed.

Impact:
- Customer sees a +$1,250 prorated line on their 2026-03-06 provisional invoice.
- Customer is an enterprise Private deployment customer with reserved hours; unexpected charge impacts finance reconciliation and internal chargeback.

Environment:
- Peregrine Health private deployment (VPC) with hosted fallback enabled.
- Region: us-west-2.
- Affected model: redwood-gpt-large-v1 (toolcall-enabled variant).

Observed behavior:
- Invoice shows a prorated hosted-toolcall line; billing key points to hosted rate ID for toolcall invocations.
- Postback replays and reconciler...

Observed behavior:

- Postback replays and reconciler...

comments.author: Ava Chen ()
comments.body: Opening ticket on behalf of Peregrine Health. Customer provided postback export and async logs. They request provisional credit while investigation occurs.

comments.author: Marcus Patel ()
comments.body: Acknowledged. Raised INT-1102 for postback-reconciler deeper trace and requested transaction-level reconciler logs from engineering. ETA for first pass: 2026-03-10 EOD.

comments.author: Billing
comments.body: Initial reconciler query shows billed events originate from a 'fallback-pipeline' path that lacked the X-Test-Traffic header normalization step during a short config mismatch between hosted and private fallback connectors. Adding artifacts: reconciler-query-id=rb-20260310-77. Hypothesis: header lost during fallback caused retries to be classified as prod and emitted as postbacks.

comments.author: Marcus Patel
comments.body: Requested a deeper audit of edge-proxy release 2026-03-06T08:45 which touched header forwarding logic. Added related PR to investigation: https://github.com/redwood-inference/edge-proxy/pull/412.

comments.author: Elena Ruiz
comments.body: Customer asked for provisional credit while we confirm RCA. Recommended provisional credit of $1,250 until reconciler replay completed. Paused autopay for this invoice pending resolution.

comments.author: Marcus Patel
comments.body: Reconciler replay completed. Confirmed 18,214 emitted postbacks matched billed line items; root cause: edge-proxy release 2026-03-06 inadvertently dropped test header when routing to hosted-fallback connector for a 9-minute window. Patch merged to restore header normalization. Next steps: issue credit, monitor for recurrence, postmortem due.

comments.author: Marcus Patel
comments.body: Provisional credit of $1,250 applied to customer's provisional invoice. Customer notified and autopay remains paused until final invoice reconciliation. Postmortem scheduled with Edge/Private teams.

title: Regional STS audience mismatch causing WIF->GCP service account mapping failures in dedicated-preview
body:
Issue summary: Over the last 24 hours, provisioning jobs that perform AWS->GCP workload identity federation (WIF) token exchange in dedicated-preview regions are failing to map the federated token to the intended GCP service account. Impact: A subset of dedicated-preview tenants experience failures during onboarding and CI tasks that need cross-cloud access; affected jobs see repeated 403/unauthenticated errors from GCP IAM when exchanging STS tokens. This blocks some Dedicated onboarding flows and debugging of private deployments.

Observed behaviour differs by region: us-east provisioning usually succeeds; eu-west and ap-south attempts fail consistently.

Observed behaviour differs by region: us-east provisioning usually succeeds; eu-west and ap-south attempts fail consistently.

comments.author: Alyssa Chen (2026-03-10)
comments.body: Noticed spike in dedicated-preview failures after deploy of provisioning refactor earlier today. Opening ticket and rolling back candidate is not ideal due to unrelated fixes in that deploy.

comments.author: Priya Nair (2026-03-10)
comments.body: Assigned. Adding SRE to triage and will add short-term override for affected customers. Linking to #support-private channel for customer comms.

comments.author: Mateo Alvarez (SRE) (2026-03-11)
comments.body: Confirmed region suffix present in generated audience for eu-west. Verified provider with -eu suffix does not exist in project 123456. Proposed patch to audience formatter to strip region suffix for WIF provider ids.

comments.author: Dev (2026-03-12)
comments.body: Created PR to fix audience generation: github.com/redwood-inference/provisioner/pull/1423. Adding automated unit test for provider id generation across regions.
Reviewed proposed change; no new privileges introduced. Confirmed that manual override should be logged and audited.

comments.author: CS (2026-03-12)
comments.body: Notified affected tenant admins that onboarding would be retried; provided workaround to create manual provider alias if they prefer faster unblocking.

title: Unexpected invoice charge for March 2026 - needs clarification
body:
Customer reports an unexpectedly large invoice for March 2026 (Invoice INV-2026-03-1187). Expected AR charge for committed Dedicated capacity was $1,245.00; invoice shows $12,450.00. Impact: finance/AR has placed a payment hold and accounting needs a corrected invoice/credit note.

Steps to reproduce / observed details:
1) Customer (Fjord Analytics) viewed Console > Billing > Invoices and downloaded INV-2026-03-1187 PDF.
2) Line items show two identical 'dedicated-reservation' line items (each $6,225.00) which looks like a duplicate.
3) Console usage dashboard for March shows committed capacity usage consistent with expectation (approx 100k tokens/day), not 10x.
4) API request logs for customer account_id fjord-9987 show no anomalous traffic spikes on 2026-03-02..2026-03-31.

What customer asks: Please validate whether this was a billing system duplication, confirm if a credit will be...

Steps to reproduce / observed details:

4) API request logs for customer account_id fjord-9987 show no anomalous traffic spikes on 2026-03-02..2026-03-31.

What customer asks: Please validate whether this was a billing system duplication, confirm if a credit will be...

comments.author: Maya Patel, Finance Lead
comments.body: "We were stunned to see the $12,450 invoice. Our committed monthly reservation is $1,245. Please advise urgently; we're on a payment hold. Attached invoice and CSV."

comments.author: Ava Chen ()
comments.body: "Thanks Maya — filing this with billing. Can you confirm the console account id and provide the invoice PDF (if different from attachment)? We will triage and come back within SLA."

comments.author: Liam Patel (Billing)
comments.body: "Acknowledged — assigning to finance ops and ENG-billing. Initial look: invoice generation job completed at 2026-03-01 02:14 UTC. I'll pull the raw run file and compare."

comments.author: Nora Diaz (-billing)
comments.body: "I pulled the invoice run artifacts. There is an unexpected duplicate reservation_id present in the March aggregation for account fjord-9987. Looks like two reservation rows were merged without dedupe after a retry. Investigating upstream job that produces reservation aggregates."

comments.author: Finance Ops (TBD)
comments.body: "We can issue a provisional credit while ENG confirms root cause. Need approval from accounting manager."

comments.author: Liam Patel (Billing)
comments.body: "Root cause narrowed to invoice-run retry with non-idempotent aggregation; engineering has a patch in review to dedupe by reservation_id. Finance prepared a credit memo draft. ETA for patch to prod and re-run: 2026-03-12."

comments.author: Maya Patel
comments.body: "Thank you. Please send the credit memo and corrected invoice as soon as available. We also need a short explanation we can share internally."

4) API request logs for customer account_id fjord-9987 show no anomalous traffic spikes on 2026-03-02..2026-03-31.

title: Clarify accounting for sustained vs short-burst tokens with HTTP/2 connection reuse and signed-url multipath
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports repeated 429s during a sustained ingest job that uses multiplexed HTTP/2 clients and short-lived signed URLs. They see higher-than-expected throttle counts when reusing the same underlying connection across several ephemeral API keys and signed callbacks.

Impact:
- Intermittent 429s on 3 of 5 ingest workers, causing job retries and delayed backfill.
- Customer on a Dedicated pool; affects SLA and daily throughput.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool (prod-us-west) pinned to redwood/open-llama-13b; autoscaling disabled.
- Ingress: client uses HTTP/2 connection reuse with multiple multiplexed streams, each stream attaching a freshly requested signed URL token per upload chunk.

Steps to reproduce (provided by customer):
1) Open a single persistent HTTP/2 connection from an orchestrator to our edge.
2) Spawn 20 concurrent streams over that connection.
3) For e...

comments.body: Drafting product request and will link to infra PR and staging canary plan. Targeting RFC by 2026-03-17.

comments.author: Customer (Maya R.)
comments.body: Appreciate the timeline. Please include guidance about TTL and recommended stream-per-connection limits until a config is available.

title: dashboard-filter-and-trace-context-lost-on-org-switch-during-session
body: Issue summary: Customer reports that while viewing a dashboard with tenant-scoped filters applied, switching organizations via the org selector clears filter state and causes tracing hook links to open in the newly-selected org context. Impact: Customer-facing dashboards show metrics for the wrong organization after org-switch during the same browser session; trace links in alert emails and dashboard panels resolve to traces in the alternate org resulting in confusion and potential data exposure. Environment: Production, us-east cluster, console frontend v2.11.0, auth service v1.9.2. Observed start: 2026-03-10 10:24 UTC (first customer report).

Environment: Production, us-east cluster, console frontend v2.11.0, auth service v1.9.2. Observed start: 2026-03-10 10:24 UTC (first customer report).

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Customer reported that dashboards displayed metrics from quantahr-prod but trace links opened quantahr-staging traces after switching orgs mid-session. Requested root cause and ETA for fix.

comments.author: Anna Martinez, QuantaHR
comments.body: Confirmed steps and provided a short screencast. Says this caused an on-call pager noise due to mismatched links. Asking for a short-term mitigation aside from hard refresh.

comments.author: Luca Bianchi
comments.body: Captured gateway logs showing token re-issuance on org switch. Suggest ensuring any client-side cached org-scoped request headers are invalidated immediately after org claim change.

comments.author: Elena Park
comments.body: Drafted PR to force reload-on-org-switch and to include 'originatingOrg' metadata on panel results. Assigning validation to QA.

title: NordBank AB: request data residency exception for DR failover (EU -> US)
body:
Customer asks for a temporary exception to EU-only residency to allow US failover during disaster recovery tests and if EU region is degraded. They want a written confirmation of what data may cross regions and confirmation that payload logging is disabled.

Impact: None currently; this is a compliance/architecture request tied to procurement.

Environment: Hosted API, tenant=nordbank-prod, primary region=eu-west.

title: Edge failover + auth rotation causes missing metadata envelope and trailing paragraph silence on streaming resume
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports intermittent incomplete final metadata and a silent trailing paragraph when their websocket or SSE stream resumes after an edge failover/auth cookie rotation. The response body contains the content tokens but the final metadata envelope (terminal JSON object containing stream-level fields like 'id'/'usage'/'choices' metadata) is sometimes missing or cut and the last paragraph is not emitted, causing the client SDK to throw a parse error or appear to hang.

Impact:
- Several Inkwell Media users see a parse error or a UI spinner after a reconnect; last paragraph (summary) often never appears.
- Occurs ~0.5-1% of long-running streaming sessions for this customer during peak traffic.
- Affects critical editor flows where the final paragraph contains a license blurb.

Environment:
- Customer: Inkwell Media (dedicated account)
- Region: us-east
- Model: redwo...

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Created ticket after customer provided captures. Customer reports ~0.8% failure rate during peak; reproduces with auth rotation + edge swap.

comments.author: Diego Martinez
comments.body: Acknowledged. Assigned to investigating SRE and runtime teams. Asking for full PCAP and edge-proxy debug logs. Requested customer to reproduce with verbose logging enabled.

comments.author: Maya Patel
comments.body: Initial look — edge-proxy showed conn close with FIN prior to origin sending terminal metadata in ~2 of 6 captures. Also see truncated chunk with non-UTF8 suffix in one trace. Suspect proxy buffer flush on failover.

comments.author: Runtime (Liam O'Connor)
comments.body: We instrumented streaming runtime around terminal frame emission; in our internal test we saw terminal envelope emitted but client-side reassembly failed if a 0-byte keepalive was coalesced with the final chunk by the proxy. Added hypothesis that header modification (cookie rewrite) triggers a small mid-stream rewrite, causing chunk boundary shift.

comments.author: Diego Martinez
comments.body: Reached out to customer CS with a curated test build (edge-proxy config toggles) and a client SDK recommended retry. Customer responses pending; asked CS to provide next repro window.

comments.author: Inkwell
comments.body: Confirmed they could reproduce on demand in staging by toggling SSO session rotation and that applying the temporary proxy setting stops the truncation.

title: Unexpected spike on March invoice attributed to embeddings usage — meter/billing clarification
body:
Issue summary: Customer reports a large unexpected charge on their March invoice that they believe is due to embeddings usage out of pattern. Impact: Customer-facing billing dispute; finance needs clarification before sending payment. Environment: Production (us-east-1) under enterprise account Novara Health; Dedicated plan with reserved capacity token bucket. Customer says their app did not change during the billing window.

Initial claim: Novara Health observed a 7x month-over-month increase in embeddings token usage between 2026-02-25 and 2026-03-01 triggering overage charges. They attached a CSV of per-route metric counts and a screenshot of the invoice line items.

Requested outcome: Provide a breakdown of what drove the usage spike, confirm whether any metering policy or rate-limit misattribution occurred, and issue credits if overcounting is confirmed.

Steps to reproduce (from...

comments.author: Sofia Martinez (Customer)
comments.body: We received a March invoice that is ~8x our typical charge. Nothing in our deploys should have triggered this. Attaching CSV and invoice screenshot. Please advise.

comments.author: Daniel Park ()
comments.body: Thanks Sofia — we're opening an investigation. Can you confirm whether any background jobs or bulk re-index tasks were run around Feb 27-28? Also sending this to metering/billing. (sla P1)

comments.author: Priya Desai (Billing)
comments.body: I pulled invoice lines; totals match metering's aggregate at a high level. I'll run row-level attribution and share queries.

comments.author: Mateo Alvarez (Runtime)
comments.body: Found concentrated traffic from API key ending -9f2 at 03:12 UTC on 2026-02-27. No 5xx responses in gateway; response latencies were normal but we saw a collector commit lag around that time.

comments.author: Sofia Martinez (Customer)
comments.body: Confirmed — we deployed a search indexer and kicked off a catch-up that night. We believed it to be within our reserved capacity. If part of this was a platform duplication, we appreciate the correction.

comments.author: Daniel Park ()
comments.body: Summary: Mixed cause. Engineering estimates ~3.8M tokens attributable to duplicated ingestion during a collector commit replay. Billing to calculate credit amount. Pending internal task INT-412 for collector dedupe.

comments.author: Priya Desai (Billing)
comments.body: Applied $5,200.00 credit to account. New invoice PDF attached to customer and uploaded to ticket. Remaining balance updated.

comments.author: Sofia Martinez (Customer)
comments.body: Thank you — we accept the credit and will proceed with the adjusted payment. Please keep us posted on the dedupe fix timeline.

comments.author: Daniel Park ()
comments.body: Closing the customer-facing dispute. Leaving linked internal fix INT-412 to Engineering. Recommend postmortem doc on Confluence and adding a billing-alert for bulk-commit re-emissions.

title: SSE session stitching gap when small append payloads follow sidecar auth refresh
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reported intermittent truncation and duplicate partial sentences when resuming SSE streams immediately after a sidecar-driven auth refresh. The pattern only appears when the model emits very small 'append' chunks (< 200 bytes) directly after the auth rotation handshake.

Impact:
- Customer ClearDocs (dedicated) sees ~1-2% of streams where the last sentence is missing and a duplicate prefix appears after a client retry.
- Affects streaming UX for long-form generation (multi-paragraph outputs).

Environment:
- Dedicated pool in us-east (prod) behind sidecar-proxy version 2.12.3.
- Client: ClearDocs web-app using native EventSource SSE; reconnect handler expects a terminal 'event: done' marker.
- Model: redwood-verified-7b; typical generation lengths 400-1200 tokens.

Steps to reproduce (internal repro):
1) Issue a streaming SSE generation while toggling auth rota...

Steps to reproduce (internal repro):
1) Issue a streaming SSE generation while toggling auth rota...

comments.author: agent
comments.body: 'We see the final sentence missing ~1.3% of the time. Reloading the page duplicates the first half of the last paragraph. Happening across multiple users.'

comments.author: Aisha Rahman ()
comments.body: Acknowledged. Collecting traces and asking for an HAR and sidecar logs. Escalating to SRE/serving runtime triage.

comments.author: Marco Liu (SRE)
comments.body: Reproduced locally with auth-rotation simulator. Observed sidecar performing a tiny buffer rewrite that drops the trailing event marker in ~10% of runs. Captured pcap and attached to ticket.

comments.author: Marco Liu (SRE)
comments.body: Hypothesis: sidecar's fast-path that injects a rotated Authorization header can cause an extra chunk flush without preserving the final CRLF-delimited event boundary when payloads are below the chunk-size threshold. Working on a minimal patch to preserve delimiter frames during auth swaps.

comments.author: (serving-runtime)
comments.body: Confirmed server always wrote terminal delimiter to local socket; loss occurs between sidecar read and outgoing socket write. Also saw increased syscall EAGAIN during concurrent auth-refresh window.

comments.author: Marco Liu (SRE)
comments.body: Deployed hotfix to sidecar v2.12.4 to preserve SSE boundaries during header rewrite and to force an atomic flush of final event frames. Rolling to us-east dedicated hostgroups first.

comments.author: agent
comments.body: After patch, customer reports no further duplicated prefixes in a 12-hour sampling window. Closing check requested.

comments.author: Marco Liu (SRE)
comments.body: Verified metric 'stream_termination_malformed' dropped from 1.1% -> 0.02% in us-east dedicated fleet. Marking resolved. Follow-up: add unit test and e2e test in proxy for tiny-chunk auth-rotation case.

title: Half-closed stream: trailing clause dropped when client send window shrinks during auth rotation
body:
Issue summary:
Customer Edify.ai reports that when their browser-based client (web SDK) encounters a rapid shrink of the TCP send window (simulated on flaky mobile networks) concurrent with an opaque auth cookie rotation, the final clause of the generated stream is not returned. The server emits a half-close (FIN) path but the client-side backpressure and connection rebind cause the terminal chunk (closing punctuation + metadata) to be lost.

Impact:
- Intermittent loss of last sentence or trailing clause in streamed responses for a subset of long outputs (~>1k tokens).
- Affects a small percentage of dedicated-customer sessions in us-east under bursty mobile-proxied traffic.
- Customer-visible: generated text ends mid-sentence and no error/partial completion metadata is delivered.

Environment and context:
- Customer using dedicated capacity with reserved GPUs in us-east-1.
- SDK: re...

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Customer reported missing trailing clause on multiple sessions. Provided request ids and HAR. Asked for immediate mitigation steps and SLA acknowledgement.

comments.author: Diego Martínez
comments.body: Confirmed we can reproduce in lab with network shaping. Probable race between socket teardown and flush. Escalating to runtime team. Assigned to Diego.

comments.author: Priya Kapoor
comments.body: Reviewed worker code-paths; identified that final-chunk flush is best-effort and not guaranteed if socket receives RST. Suggested short-term edge grace window and longer-term durability for terminal envelope.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Notified customer of ongoing work and suggested client-side workaround: delay cookie rotation until stream completion where possible or use non-rotating ephemeral token for long streams. Customer testing these workarounds.

comments.author: Diego Martínez
comments.body: Lab hotfix branch created: runtime persists minimal final-envelope to resume-store on EOS (PR linked). Edge team evaluating grace-window behavior.

Impact:
- Intermittent loss of last sentence or trailing clause in streamed responses for a subset of long outputs (~>1k tokens).
- Affects a small percentage of dedicated-customer sessions in us-east under bursty mobile-proxied traffic.
- Customer-visible: generated text ends mid-sentence and no error/partial completion metadata is delivered.

Environment and context:
- Customer using dedicated capacity with reserved GPUs in us-east-1.

title: TTPO deprovision: staggered SSO retirement, MDM re-enroll, and courier-hold for cross-border departure
body:
Issue summary:
HR has requested a two-week temporary passover (TTPO) deprovision for an employee relocating overseas mid-contract. Request is to stagger account deactivation so service accounts and SSO remain available for knowledge-transfer tasks until the courier confirms laptop collection. HR flagged export/compliance concerns for the laptop transit and asked Asset Management to place a hold with the courier.

Impact:
- Potential risk of accidental early SSO/account revocation interrupting handover work (engineering + documentation updates).
- Hardware custody must remain in chain-of-custody due to encryption keys tied to device KMS tags.
- Cross-border courier requires explicit KYC and export paperwork before release.

Environment:
- Internal accounts: SSO (Okta), GSuite, GitHub org, AWS console role.
- Device managed by MDM (Jamf).
- Employee tier: full-time engineering (access t...

title: Edge proxy half-close results in silent stream tail and missing final tokens for SSE sessions
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports that many SSE streams from the hosted API appear to terminate 'silently': the client receives the penultimate chunks and then the connection closes or reuses the underlying TCP connection without the expected final 'end' event or final JSON metadata. The visible symptom is truncated final tokens and missing choice/finish_reason metadata.

Impact:
- Multiple calls from NimbusAI show incomplete responses for medium-length completions (typically 800-2k tokens).
- Affects production traffic routed through our edge proxy in us-west-2.
- Customer experienced degraded UX in customer-facing chat flows (some messages appear cut off).

Environment:
- Redwood hosted API (production) behind edge proxy (Nginx-based layer) -> api-gateway -> serving-runtime (GPUs).
- Customer traffic class: dedicated pool (reserved capacity).
- Observed primarily on HTTP/1.1 keepalive...

title: Kestrel Labs: hosted API timeouts when using structured output (JSON schema)
body:
Issue summary:
Kestrel Labs is seeing intermittent request timeouts (client-side 60s) when enabling structured output with a JSON Schema. Same prompts complete successfully when structured output is disabled.

Impact:
- Kestrel’s production workflow for contract analysis is failing intermittently.
- Reported failure rate ~8–15% depending on schema size.

Environment:
- Hosted API (OpenAI-compatible endpoint)
- Region: us-east
- Models: atlas-7b-instruct and atlas-13b-instruct

Steps to reproduce (customer-provided):
1) Send chat completion request with structured output enabled and a JSON Schema with ~40 properties and nested arrays.
2) Stream disabled.
3) Observe timeout on ~1/10 requests; retries often succeed.

Notes:
Customer says this started after they switched from tool-calling output to JSON Schema structured output (mid-July).

comments.author: Aisha Rahman ()
comments.body: Customer provided request IDs: req_7c2f..., req_9b11..., req_2a0d... All show long tail latency then gateway timeout.

comments.author: Diego Fernandez (Runtime)
comments.body: Pulled traces. The slow requests correlate with schema validation + constrained decoding path. The model is spending a long time in token sampling with low temperature + many constraints.

comments.author: Ethan Park (SRE)
comments.body: Confirmed p99 latency spike for route /v1/chat/completions when structured_output=true. Only visible on a subset of pods after autoscale. Suspect cache fragmentation.

comments.author: Diego Fernandez (Runtime)
comments.body: Workaround: set max_output_tokens lower and simplify schema (avoid large anyOf/oneOf), or switch to tool-calling for now. We also rolled a config change: increased internal structured-output step timeout from 45s -> 75s for enterprise tier in us-east.

comments.author: Aisha Rahman ()
comments.body: Customer confirmed improvement after config change; still sees occasional long responses on very large schemas but no longer hitting gateway timeout.
Marking Resolved. Follow-up action: add a known-issue entry and instrument a separate metric for schema size vs constrained decoding wall time.

body: Set up RBAC role mapping for customer-provided EU-based rollout contractor. Exception scope: access to deployment logs/metadata only; no prompt/response access. Ensure auditing/export is enabled for the exception window.

QuantaLedger asked that their EU subcontractor participate in rollout troubleshooting. Security says ok if least privilege + audited. Creating ticket to track implementation details.

title: Sustained 429s during sliced ingest with priority class mismatch after dedicated pool migration
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports sustained 429 responses across a set of sliced ingest workers after migrating ingestion to a Dedicated pool. The traffic mix is: low-latency chat calls (priority=high) and large multi-part embedding uploads (priority=background). After the migration, background uploads are intermittently exhausting burst credits at the per-route layer and causing opaque 429s for high-priority short calls during peak windows.

Impact:
- ~30% of short chat requests returned 429 between 09:00-11:30 PDT on 2026-03-10 for Streamly AI.
- Customer reports user-facing errors and retry amplification in their orchestrator.
- Business impact: partial degradation of their real-time features during peak ingest windows.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: dp-132-usw
- Region: us-west
- Ingest coordinator version: 2026.02.3
- Rate-limiter service version: 1.14.0-canary

Observed behaviour...

comments.body: Checked rate-limiter logs and saw route-level token depletion aligning with the cron flush. Noted some refill events lagged due to burst-pool-weight recalculation on pool attach.

comments.body: Observed a potential priority-class weight inversion in canary release 1.14.0-canary where background ingestion borrow logic could erroneously draw from interactive burst credits under certain attach sequences.

comments.body: Shared recommended config changes and rollout plan. Scheduling a brief call with customer for 2026-03-12 14:00 PDT to apply non-breaking adjustments and observe behavior.

comments.author: Streamly AI
comments.body: Agreed to the windowed-stagger plan and will apply client-side jitter for the March 12 trial.

title: Buffer credit escrowed after org consolidation refunded to wrong billing profile, provisional negative balance
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports that after a planned org consolidation on 2026-03-10, a held hourly buffer credit (warmup/escrow) was released but applied to an unrelated billing profile within the consolidated tenant. This produced a provisional negative balance on the customer's primary billing profile and blocked autopay on the next cycle.

Impact:
- StellarVoice reported a provisional negative balance of $-1,234.56 on their primary billing profile; autopay blocked.
- Their production inference traffic in us-east experienced quota throttling for ~3 hours while finance blocked charges.
- Customer requests a full reconciliation audit and corrective credit move rather than a blanket refund.

Environment:
- Redwood prod (us-east region)
- Consolidation event executed via org-service migration job on 2026-03-10 02:10 UTC

Expected behavior:
- Held buffer/warmup credits associated with a...

title: Confirmation-token pattern and at-most-once tool invocation guidance across Python/TS/Go SDKs
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports repeated side-effects when using the SDKs' function/tool-calling features with automatic SDK retries and server-side timeouts. They are trying to implement an at-most-once pattern across Python, TypeScript, and Go clients and want concrete, language-idiomatic guidance (confirmation token, callback acknowledgement, idempotency keys, and recommended SDK usage).

Impact:
- Customer (Finrite Inc., dedicated tenant) sees duplicated downstream webhook events and duplicate database writes when SDK retries occur after network blips or streaming reconnects.
- This impacts production workflows for payment reconciliation and is causing financial reconciliation mismatches.

Environment:
- Dedicated deployment in prod-us-east backed by reserved capacity.
- SDKs: python@1.8.2, typescript@2.3.0, go@0.9.1.
- Model: rwd-llama-13b-quant serving on gpu-pool-3.

Steps to r...

title: Possible SSO session replay after employee clicked external form link
body:
Issue summary: An employee clicked a link in an externally hosted vendor form received via email. Within one hour, anomalous session activity for that account was observed: short-lived logins from unfamiliar IPs and requests that matched session-reuse patterns. Impact: potential session token exposure and attempted session replay. Initial scope: single user account (product-ops@redwood.ai), possibly more if the external form captured session info via query params or third-party trackers.

Timeline:

title: Streaming tool/function output tokens billed on final invoice despite console estimate showing zero
body:
Issue summary: Customer reports that streaming requests which include function/tool calls produced console estimates showing 0 tool-output tokens (console estimate column), but the finalized invoice contains token charges attributed to the tool outputs. Impact: LexiHealth reports a $9,200 unexpected charge on their monthly invoice tied to streamed sessions that used function calls for structured data retrieval. Environment: Production, us-east-1, enterprise account with reserved capacity and hosted fallback enabled. This affects streaming chat endpoints where the model returns tool invocations (function calls) and the tool outputs are streamed back as separate chunks.

Expected behavior: Console and estimate API should include a consistent token estimate for tool outputs (either zero if non-billable or the same token estimate used for invoice attribution). Final invoice should not int...

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Thanks for the info. We've pulled the gateway traces and escalated to metering and billing. We'll update within 48 hours.

comments.author: Rajat Singh
comments.body: Reproducer created. The estimate pipeline currently subscribes to model tokenizer events; tool-output events are emitted on a different topic 'tool-stream' and are not included in estimate aggregation. Working on a patch to include tool-stream token counts in estimates.

comments.author: Maya Lopez
comments.body: Billing pipeline is correct: it charges against the canonical event stream. We can provide a provisional credit while estimator fix is developed. Suggest temporary credit equal to billed tool-output tokens for the cycle pending root cause fix and audit.

comments.author: LexiHealth - Jordan Park
comments.body: Appreciate rapid triage. We need an estimated timeline for credit and a fix to prevent recurrence. Renewal decision is on hold until this is resolved.

comments.author: (Liam O'Keefe)
comments.body: Ack. Metering has a PR queued (see related_github_prs). Billing team proposed provisional credit; preparing credit memo draft for $9,200 pending sign-off. Next update EOD 2026-03-12.

title: Elevated 5xx rate for ClearPath during priority-queue fairness imbalance causing runtime failovers
body:
Issue summary:
ClearPath reported a sustained increase in 5xx responses for a subset of API requests tied to their tenant during a period of steady medium concurrency. Symptoms included 502/503 responses from the API Gateway and rapid cycling of several serving-runtime instances for the tenant group.

Impact:
- Customer-facing: intermittent failed requests for embedding and chat endpoints (~3-8% error rate over a 45-minute window).
- Affected customers: ClearPath Analytics (enterprise) — business reports degraded UX and automated ingestion failures.

Environment:
- Prod, us-east-1 region.
- Tier: enterprise tenant pinned to redwood-rlhf-legacy model with fallback to gpt-medium-8k.

Steps to reproduce (reported by CS):
1) Replay customer production traffic at ~120-150 RPS with same request distribution (short chat + embedding bursts).
2) Observe 5xx increase after ~10-20 minutes concen...

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Ticket created after ClearPath CS report. Added initial logs + reproduction notes. Marking P1 due to enterprise SLA impact.

comments.author: oncall
comments.body: Spun up repro harness against us-east cluster, observed same queue_wait increase and runtime drain pattern. Suspect queue fairness bug.

comments.author: Priya Rao
comments.body: Increased per-tenant concurrency cap to 180 for ClearPath and staged a graceful restart of runtime pool (stagger 10s). Error rate dropped from 7% -> 1.2% over 12 minutes.

comments.author: Sarah Lopez, ClearPath
comments.body: Confirming partial recovery; ingestion jobs completed but some backfill failed. Asking for timeline on permanent fix.

comments.author: Marcus Patel
comments.body: Hotfix PR opened to scheduler to adjust fairness weights and decouple high-priority token accounting from head-of-line (https://github.com/redwood-inference/runtime/pull/2481). Rolling to canary now.

comments.author: Priya Rao
comments.body: Canary results look good — queue-age metric reduced, no further runtime drains for 6+ hours. Marking issue Resolved pending postmortem.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Notified ClearPath via email with summary, mitigation steps, and ETA for runbook and permanent scheduler fix. Customer accepted the interim mitigation.

comments.body: 14 PT — Stephanie Nguyen (reporter): Adding context: this is tied to the Aurora Financial security review. They specifically asked for the SOC 2 Type II report under NDA plus our latest subprocessor list and security overview. We want Sales/SE to pull only the latest approved versions (no screenshots / no older PDFs).

22 PT — Dev Patel (assignee): Acknowledged. Before granting access, please confirm (1) NDA status for Aurora Financial and (2) whether the request is logged in the evidence triage queue. Policy is least privilege + time-bound; Sales/SE should not have standing access to the full restricted drive.

31 PT — Avery Johnson: NDA is in flight; procurement sent redlines but we expect signature today. Evidence request has been added to the GRC triage sheet (row updated with due date). We only need the SOC 2 report + customer-facing security program overview for now.

05 PT — Olga Petrov (GRC): Approved for time-bound read-only access for Avery Johnson + Colin O'Donnell to the customer-facing evidence set (SOC 2 report + approved supporting artifacts). Do NOT grant access to internal audit workpapers. Sharing to customer is only after NDA is fully executed. Please add a reminder in the ticket and set auto-expiration.

18 PT — Daniel Carter (GRC): +1. Please ensure they use the canonical artifacts from the vault. If the customer asks for additional evidence outside the standard list, route back through the playbook / triage queue for approval and tracking.

36 PT — Dev Patel (assignee): Implementing: granting read-only access via group "compliance-evidence-readers-temporary" with an expiration date of 2026-02-21. Scope limited to SOC 2 + customer-facing security artifacts folder. Confirmed: no access to internal workpapers folders.

40 PT — Dev Patel (assignee): Policy reminders (please confirm you read): (1) Do not forward/share the SOC 2 report internally outside need-to-know; (2) do not upload to customer portals unless GRC + Legal approve; (3) share externally only after executed NDA; (4) cite artifact version/date in any RFP responses; (5) if a questionnaire asks for something we do not support, use the Exceptions Policy and escalate rather than improvising language.

02 PT — Colin O'Donnell: Confirmed. We will only use the vault copies and will route any non-standard requests through GRC. Once NDA is executed, we’ll share via the approved secure method and reference the artifact version/date.

15 PT — Dev Patel (assignee): Follow-up: access verified by Avery + Colin. Auto-expiration is set for 2026-02-21. Closing ticket. If additional teammates need access, file a new INT ticket with GRC approval and business justification (deal + due date).

title: Sustained elevated 5xx floor for customer during mixed embeddings+chat burst: connection oscillation -> runtime GC/eviction thrash
body:
Issue summary: Starting 2026-03-13 18:45 UTC BrightDocs reported persistent ~5-8% 5xx rate on mixed-embedding and chat routes for their tenant. Errors manifest as 502/503 from api-gateway and upstream runtime connection errors. Impact: production traffic for BrightDocs degraded, intermittent user-facing failures for document ingestion and chat features. Environment: prod-us-east, enterprise tenant (BrightDocs), traffic mix: 60% small chat requests, 40% batched embedding uploads (small/medium).

Observed behavior: When BrightDocs client increases concurrent connections and aggressively reconnects after failed requests, api-gateway 5xx floor rises and remains elevated for ~10-30 minutes before gradually recovering. During elevated period: runtime worker logs show repeated small process OOMs, kvcache eviction storms, and frequent shard reloads (cold-shard events). Apigw logs show 'upstre...

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Acknowledged. Collected tenant and trace IDs and escalated to SRE. Asked customer to pause aggressive retries if possible while we investigate.

comments.author: Marco Ruiz
comments.body: Observed accept queue growth at APIGW and connection churn to runtime. Several runtime workers reported small OOMs; kvcache evictions very high. Adding backpressure at edge could help immediate mitigation.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Informed customer to temporarily throttle client retries and increase retry backoff to 1s. Customer applied change and reported partial reduction in errors after 6 minutes.

comments.author: Marco Ruiz
comments.body: Deployed temporary ingress rate-limiter rule for tenant brightdocs to cap concurrent connections (cap=120) and enabled soft-priority-shedding on their route. Monitoring for stabilization.

comments.author: BrightDocs SRE - Lina Park
comments.body: Confirmed error rate dropped to ~0.8% after throttling. Concerned about future bursts due to our ingestion pattern — requesting long-term fix.

comments.author: Samir Patel
comments.body: Noted related PR implementing kvcache eviction smoothing and improved shard reload backoff: PR #4123. Planning canary to reduce hot reloads induced by transient connection storms.

title: Resume subprotocol mismatch causes session rebind and drops terminal attachment on reconnect
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports that after a brief network blip their websocket stream reconnects but the final embedded attachment (base64 PNG) is missing and the assistant sends duplicated system messages. The application sees a successful 101 upgrade on reconnect yet the streamed session does not resume semantics and the last 'attachment' chunk is never emitted.

Impact:
- ParcelFlow's invoice-generation flow fails to attach generated receipt images to the final response for ~2% of streamed requests during the reconnect window.
- End users see duplicated system messages ("System: Session resumed") and a truncated output (missing base64 attachment).

Environment:
- Customer on Dedicated cluster (prod, us-east) behind corporate edge proxy (vendor: Korunner WAF) and an internal ALB.
- SDK: redwood-js v1.4.2 using websocket with sec-websocket-protocol: "redwood-stream-v1" set by client...

comments.author: (Maya R.) - 2026-03-01
comments.body: Acknowledged report and requested full websocket upgrade traces and a copied server-side request-id. Asked customer to reproduce with SDK debug logging on and to collect ALB logs.

comments.author: ParcelFlow - Samir Patel
comments.body: Provided ALB access logs and a HAR. Confirms internal proxy (Korunner) normalizes sec-websocket-protocol to 'redwood-stream' on reconnect paths. Able to reproduce reliably by toggling mobile interface.

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: Reproduced in staging by simulating header normalization at the edge. Observed session lookup keyed on (resume_token + subprotocol). When subprotocol differs, server treated as new session. Filed hypothesis in investigation notes.

comments.author: Ruth Lin
comments.body: Noted that past design used (resume_token, subprotocol) tuple to avoid cross-protocol resume attacks. Concern: relaxing tuple may open replay surface; propose short-term workaround and long-term change with cryptographic bind.

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: Implemented and tested a patch in staging that prefers resume_token for session rebind and logs a subprotocol-mismatch metric. Patch referenced in PR #412. Asking for rollout to canary Dedicated cluster with feature flag.

title: SSE -> WebSocket upgrade race leads to truncated final chunks and missing attachment URL
body:
Issue summary:
Customers see a truncated final output and missing attachment URL when the client performs an SSE stream that upgrades to WebSocket mid-response. The last few tokens (often the attachment link or final punctuation) are dropped and the stream terminates without the terminal metadata envelope.

Impact:
- KiteNote (enterprise) reports 12% of long-running generation requests lost last-line attachments during heavy traffic windows.
- Affects production traffic in us-west; some artifacts seen in us-east during cross-region failover.

Environment:
prod - us-west, api gateway behind ALB -> edge proxy cluster -> serving-runtime pool. Customer using SDK-js v1.2.4 with automatic SSE-to-WS upgrade enabled.

Expected behavior:
When a client upgrades an open SSE stream to a WebSocket (preferred for bi-directional control), the server should deliver remaining buffered tokens and the f...

comments.body: 05Z]: Customer provided sample request_id=req-7f2 and a short screen capture showing the final attachment URL missing. Customer reports ~12% failure during 09:00-10:30 PST window.
40Z]: Captured tcpdump and proxied logs. Noted fast FIN after 101; putting service in debug mode for next repro window. Temporarily disabled upgrade for KiteNote to mitigate.
12Z]: Reproduced in staging with simulated ALB rebind. Filing kernel-level order maintenance issue and drafting a fix to make final-metadata flush transactional across upgrade boundary. PR TBD.
20Z]: Thanks for the quick mitigation (forcing SSE-only). Can you estimate ETA for fix? This impacts our attachments reliability and downstream indexing.

00Z]: Workaround validated. Patch skeleton created: ensure final-metadata flush is persisted to connection buffer before switching upgrade state. POC PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/serving-runtime/pull/4278

Issue summary:
Customers see a truncated final output and missing attachment URL when the client performs an SSE stream that upgrades to WebSocket mid-response. The last few tokens (often the attachment link or final punctuation) are dropped and the stream terminates without the terminal metadata envelope.

Impact:
- KiteNote (enterprise) reports 12% of long-running generation requests lost last-line attachments during heavy traffic windows.
- Affects production traffic in us-west; some artifacts seen in us-east during cross-region failover.

Environment:
prod - us-west, api gateway behind ALB -> edge proxy cluster -> serving-runtime pool. Customer using SDK-js v1.2.4 with automatic SSE-to-WS upgrade enabled.

Expected behavior:
When a client upgrades an open SSE stream to a WebSocket (preferred for bi-directional control), the server should deliver remaining buffered tokens and the f...

title: Console onboarding nudge intermittently blocks API key creation (Create API key button unclickable)
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports they cannot create an API key in the Console. The "Create API key" button appears, but is not clickable / click does nothing.

Impact:
- Blocks activation milestone (API key creation → first request).
- Customer unable to complete onboarding and send first API call.
- Support volume risk during lifecycle growth experiments rollout (console nudges).

Customer context:
- Company: DevFlow AI
- Plan: self-serve Hosted API
- User report: "The onboarding checklist is telling me to create a key, but I can't click the button. It looks like something is on top of it."

Environment:
- Console: app.redwoodinference.com
- Observed in: prod (us-east)
- Browser: Chrome 121 / macOS (also reproduced on Arc)
- Feature flags: lifecycle growth console nudges enabled for cohort (holdout = false)

Steps to reproduce (internal repro):
1) Use a user in cohort with console onb...

comments.body: 18 PT — Ben Carter (Reporter): Flagging from #support escalation. DevFlow AI cannot create API key; seems like the new onboarding nudge is sitting on top of the CTA. This is directly impacting activation funnel for the sprint cohorts.
42 PT — Support (triage) (Ben Carter): Acknowledged to customer. Requested screenshot + browser/OS. Shared CLI workaround to unblock them for today. Set priority P1 due to activation blocker.

05 PT — Kenji Nakamura (Design): Looked at current banner layout; on smaller viewports the sticky nudge bar can overlap the page header. Suggest we make the banner non-sticky on Settings routes or enforce a safe top offset. Also ensure pointer-events is only on buttons inside the nudge, not on the container.

31 PT — Dylan Brooks (Assignee): Reproduced in prod with a test cohort user. Root cause: NudgeContainer wrapper has pointer-events:auto and spans full width with transparent background; z-index > header. Clicking CTA is intercepted. Implementing CSS fix + route-specific layout guard.

Action: patch NudgeContainer to pointer-events:none, enable pointer-events:auto only for interactive children; reduce z-index; add e2e check for API Keys route.

12 PT — Chloe Martin: Confirmed route-level placement logic is using a generic header anchor; Settings pages have different stacking context. Suggest use dedicated mount point for Settings pages or disable this nudge placement on /settings/* until mount point ships.

03 PT — Dylan Brooks: Hotfix proposal:
1) CSS pointer-events change as above (safe and low risk).
2) Add placement rule: do not render sticky nudge on /settings/api-keys; render inline below page title instead.
Shipping behind existing lifecycle growth flag; will ramp from 10% to 100% of flagged cohort after QA.

26 PT — Leila Farouk (QA): Validated fix on Chrome/Safari, 100%/110% zoom, laptop viewport. Confirmed "Create API key" clickable in all tested variants. Also verified nudge dismiss persists and does not re-overlay on navigation back to API Keys.
10 PT — Ben Carter: Customer updated: shared ETA (today) + workaround until confirmed. Requested they retest after we notify.

02 PT — Dylan Brooks: Patch merged and deployed to prod behind flag. Ramp plan: 10% cohort for 1 hour monitoring console errors → 50% → 100% if clean. Monitoring: console route errors + click-through event to API key modal open.

Notes: no backend change; purely console layout / interaction fix.
07 PT — Allison Grant (Observability): No spike in console JS errors post-deploy. Funnel event "api_key_modal_opened" recovered for the flagged cohort segment compared to prior hour. Looks stable.
18 PT — Ben Carter: Confirmed with DevFlow AI: they can now create an API key and completed first request. Closing the loop; keeping ticket open until full ramp completes.
40 PT — Dylan Brooks: Full ramp complete for lifecycle-growth cohorts. Added regression test coverage for Settings → API Keys route. Marking Resolved.

title: Commit-window race during scheduled model upgrade causes tenant to be routed to fallback variant
body:
Issue summary: During a scheduled model minor-version upgrade in the customer's Dedicated pool, a narrow race in the rollout commit window caused the tenant's session tokens to be routed to a fallback (smaller-quant) variant rather than the pinned primary model. Impact: Intermittent mixed-model responses (some requests returned quantized outputs with subtle tokenization differences) for a subset of BrightForms tenant traffic for ~8 minutes. No data leak; correctness impact limited to degraded generation quality and increased tokenization mismatches.

Background: BrightForms has a pinned model version for their route. We initiated a scheduled minor upgrade on 2026-03-10 02:05 UTC that touched the rollout-manager commit path and the router's pin cache TTL. During commit, some session leases were truncated by a retry path which cleared pin metadata before the router could persist the new...

comments.author: Maya Patel
comments.body: Initial report from BrightForms: 'We saw weird shorter outputs and tokenization edge cases starting at 02:05 UTC for about 8 minutes. Many requests appeared to come from the same session ids.'

comments.author: Diego Ramirez
comments.body: Acknowledged and started trace collection. Observed rollout-manager commit retries and router session-lease truncates. Marked as P1 given tenant production impact.

comments.author: Maya Patel
comments.body: Notified customer of investigation and provided temporary mitigation: avoid scheduling upgrades during BrightForms heavy usage windows and to use shorter lease-extension TTLs as a stop-gap.

comments.author: Diego Ramirez
comments.body: Reproducer run in staging with injected retry confirmed race. Created PR to add idempotent commit semantics to prevent lease truncation on commit retry. See PR #4212.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Requesting a follow-up to add an observability alert for 'pin-loss-rate' during rollouts and to reduce edge negative-cache TTL to 10s for dedicated pools as a protective default.

title: SSE post-churn fragmentation: final sentence missing when client socket rotates quickly on mobile
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports that for streamed SSE chat responses, the final sentence or punctuation is frequently missing when a user on mobile toggles network quickly (cellular <-> wifi) or app background/foreground happens within ~500ms of stream end. This appears as an incomplete final clause rather than a silent tail; choice metadata (completion end event) may be present but trailing tokens are absent.

Impact:
- Skylark Analytics reports ~8% of streaming completions for a specific mobile-heavy route are truncated, affecting end-user readability and downstream parsing.
- Customer uses Dedicated capacity; multiple customers may be exposed but reported threshold is currently for Skylark's production route.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: dp-nyc-3 (prod us-east), node pool running v2.18.1 of serving runtime.
- Edge: internal edge-proxy for region us-east, HTTP/2 between proxy and...

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Acknowledged, collected traces and initial logs from Skylark. Requested sample client runbook and SDK version. Asked for production request IDs. SLA P1 acknowledged.

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: Reproduced locally with an emulated mobile churn script. Observed that edge-proxy reported a FIN during tail flush, upstream returned 200 but last 8-16 bytes were missing from stream body. Attaching local capture to the ticket. Hypothesis: proxy rebind triggers graceful shutdown before kernel buffer flush completes.

comments.author: Priya Nair
comments.body: Looking into serving-runtime flush logic (v2.18.1). Noted a possible race when final chunk is queued and connection state transitions to half-closed. Adding instrumentation to confirm whether write completion callback occurs before connection state change.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Provided customer workaround to increase reconnect delay; customer confirmed reduced frequency of truncation in canary test. Customer requests ETA for permanent fix.

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: Preparing a small edge-proxy config change to delay connection teardown by 250ms after detecting in-flight SSE terminal frames. Will roll to staging for 24h canary. If stable, will plan prod rollout next maintenance window.

title: Tool acknowledgement lost after client-side backpressure causing ambiguous replay and side-effect uncertainty
body:
Issue summary: Customer reports that when their client applies aggressive client-side backpressure (pause/resume on the streaming socket) while the SDK/edge is in the middle of a function/tool call, the tool acknowledgement gets lost. Impact: downstream systems observe either duplicate side-effects after replay or never-observed confirmations, leading to billing ambiguity and customer data inconsistencies.

Environment: Prod, dedicated deployment for Nimbus Health in us-east. Python SDK 1.8.2 used from a serverless worker behind an in-house proxy.

Steps to reproduce:
1) Start long-lived streaming session via Python SDK to call external tool via function-calling feature.
2) Trigger a tool execution that emits a confirmation webhook synchronously from the tool handler.
3) Simulate client backpressure by pausing the TCP read loop for ~500-1500ms intermittently (server in CPU-constrained...

comments.body: Applied workaround; initial verification shows no duplicate charges in two-hour test window.

title: Over-capacity routing toggle caused extended queue wait and retries (Hosted us-east)
body:
Issue summary:
Hosted us-east returning 503 "no capacity" with long queue waits for accepted requests. Evidence of retry amplification.

Impact:
Customer-facing degradation on hosted tier; secondary spillover in eu-west.

Environment:
prod us-east; routing policies recently changed for maintenance.

Suspected cause:
Stale routing rule preventing early spillover.

comments.body: 31 ET (Naomi Feldman): 503s climbing; gpu free slots at 0. Not spilling to us-west.
38 ET (Grace O'Connor): Found maintenance routing toggle still enabled; disabling now.
52 ET (Grace O'Connor): Spillover active; 503 rate dropping.

comments.author: Grace O'Connor
comments.body: Postmortem published; closing ticket.

title: Microburst metering lag causing invoice smoothing discrepancy
body: Issue summary: Customer reports a $3,820 negative adjustment on invoice #INV-2026-03-08 after a scheduled smoothing job; LexiTech observed credits applied then revoked the next hour. Impact: Billing inaccuracy and customer concern; affecting monthly close for LexiTech (enterprise) and blocking finance reconciliation. Environment: prod (us-west primary, with replica in eu-west). Timeline: bursts of high embedding traffic (microbursts) on 2026-03-07 23:40-23:47 UTC; billing smoothing job ran at 2026-03-08 00:00 UTC. Observed symptom: provisional credits created then dropped when smoothing completed, resulting in final invoice delta. Customer-facing impact: incorrect invoice line and confusion; customer requests reversal and justification for adjustments.

Customer-facing impact: incorrect invoice line and confusion; customer requests reversal and justification for adjustments.

comments.author: Ava Chen ()
comments.body: Logged ticket after LexiTech billing contacted CS. Customer wants a clear explanation and an immediate invoice credit for finance close.

comments.author: Marta Lopez (SRE)
comments.body: Confirmed smoothing job executed twice; second run marked final commit and reversed earlier provisional credit. Captured smoother logs and queuetail; suspect delayed postbacks. Marking for billing-engine owner review.

comments.author: Samir Khan (Billing)
comments.body: Reviewed invoice lines; no double-billing observed. Recommend issuing one-time credit and patching smoother logic. Preparing credit memo draft for $3,820.

comments.author: LexiTech Billing
comments.body: Thanks — can we get the credit applied before their month-end close on 2026-03-12? Also need a short explanation to attach to their internal GL.

comments.author: Ravi Patel (, )
comments.body: Issued credit memo CM-LEXI-2026-03-12 for $3,820 and emailed LexiTech with explanation + link to runbook. Patch PR opened and assigned to eng-inference: @marta @samir for review.

comments.author: Marta Lopez (SRE)
comments.body: Added unit tests and a fix to prevent reversal on late postbacks if provisional credit already committed. PR linked. Planning staging canary 2026-03-14.

comments.author: LexiTech Billing
comments.body: Confirmed receipt of credit and explanation. Thanks for the quick turnaround. Asked to be notified when permanent fix is live.

title: Intermittent websocket fragment interleaving after TLS session-ticket resume behind customer edge proxy
body:
Issue summary: Customer reports intermittent truncated or duplicated final JSON arrays on websocket streaming sessions. Impact: Several production flows (web chat + background mobile sync) are returning partial outputs (missing closing array bracket or duplicated terminal 'end' event) causing downstream JSON parsing errors in their backend and mobile apps. Environment: MercuryForms runs on a Private deployment (VPC) with an HA edge proxy (customer-managed) in front of Redwood's VPC egress; they use our iOS SDK in background URLSession mode for mobile sync and a Node websocket client on server-side. Problem first observed after their edge proxy upgrade (transparent TCP pooler) on 2026-03-04.

Observed behavior: - Websocket sessions are established and begin streaming. - On ~0.6% of sessions the final frames arrive fragmented or interleaved: server emits close/terminal event but trailin...

comments.author: Tomás Ortega ()
comments.body: Reproduced locally with harness. Assigned to streaming-runtime team for patching. Opening follow-up INT ticket for internal change (INT-4121).

comments.author: MercuryForms SRE - Elena K
comments.body: Confirmed disabling 'pool-keepalive' on proxy reduced errors in staging. Asking for ETA on permanent fix for production.

comments.author: Tomás Ortega ()
comments.body: Implemented candidate fix: buffer terminal fragments up to 200ms and reorder by sequence id before parse. Running canary on serving-runtime in our staging cluster.

comments.author: Aisha Rahman ()
comments.body: Notified customer of canary testing and recommended temporary mitigations while patch is validated.

comments.author: Customer (Elena K)
comments.body: Canary results look good in staging; will coordinate production rollout window with their infra team.

Impact: Several production flows (web chat + background mobile sync) are returning partial outputs (missing closing array bracket or duplicated terminal 'end' event) causing downstream JSON parsing errors in their backend and mobile apps. Environment: MercuryForms runs on a Private deployment (VPC) with an HA edge proxy (customer-managed) in front of Redwood's VPC egress; they use our iOS SDK in background URLSession mode for mobile sync and a Node websocket client on server-side. Problem first observed after their edge proxy upgrade (transparent TCP pooler) on 2026-03-04.

Observed behavior: - Websocket sessions are established and begin streaming. - On ~0.6% of sessions the final frames arrive fragmented or interleaved: server emits close/terminal event but trailin...

title: Retroactive hourly credit adjustments applied after regional failover causing invoice shortfall
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports retroactive hourly credit adjustments were applied to several running hours after an automated regional failover. The adjustments reduced billed usage lines for 2026-03-08 17:00-20:00 in us-west-2, producing a net negative line item that caused the customer's March invoice to under-report expected charges and created a negative pre-pay balance for their subaccount.

Impact:
- Greenline Health (enterprise) noticed a $9,120 shortfall on the March invoice relating to credits applied retroactively.
- Customer finance cannot reconcile month-end; accounting hold requested.
- No service outage; inference traffic unaffected.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: redwood-dedicated-usw2-gpu-a
- Region: us-west-2 (primary)
- Time window: 2026-03-08 17:00–20:00 UTC

Steps to reproduce (observed):
1) Scheduled leader election in us-west-2 triggered automated failover for ...

Steps to reproduce (observed):
1) Scheduled leader election in us-west-2 triggered automated failover for ...

comments.author: Sofia Ramirez ()
comments.body: Customer submitted billing ticket after noticing negative invoice line. Attached invoice PDF. Asked for urgent review — finance hold requested.

comments.author: Billing Ops
comments.body: Acknowledged. Kicked off ledger lookup for Greenline Health. Asked SRE for postback queue logs for the 17:00–18:00 window.

comments.author: Marcus Li (SRE)
comments.body: Found duplicate runs of hourly-credit-aggregator around 17:00 due to leader handoff. Consumer logs show postback-processor restarted and reprocessed messages with no idempotency key present in credit adjustment path. Log excerpt: "replay_event: true, original_postback_id=null, adjustments_created=42". Pushed initial hypothesis to engineering.

comments.author: Priya Shah (Billing)
comments.body: Correlated ledger lines: 12 retroactive allocations created without invoice_id metadata. These created negative adjustments that were not picked up by invoice renderer. Preparing manual correction batch.

comments.author: Greenline Health - Finance Contact, Marissa Cole
comments.body: Confirmed hold on vendor payment until correction. Asked for ETA on corrected invoice and asked whether penalties/interchange will be covered.

comments.author: Ethan Cole ()
comments.body: Escalated to Product and Engineering. Notified customer we'll issue a credit memo for the shortfall and rerun invoice generation within 24 hours. Opened INT-4392 for ops remediation.

comments.author: Marcus Li
comments.body: Root cause identified — hourly-credit-aggregator relied on ephemeral host-timestamp as idempotency key; during failover postback replay the system created duplicate retroactive allocations. A patch to use stable postback ids and add idempotency checks for credit ledger entries is ready (PR linked).

comments.author: Priya Shah
comments.body: Manual correction applied: reversed 12 retroactive allocation entries and created an invoice correction/credit memo CM-2026-03-1423 for $9,120. Customer notified and corrected invoice reissued. Awaiting customer confirmation of receipt.

comments.author: Marissa Cole
comments.body: Acknowledged receipt of corrected invoice and credit memo. Requested postmortem and assurance on backlog prevention.
