title: RRB-17: Private installer rollback loop / stuck rollback during upgrade
body:
Issue summary:
Customers upgrading Redwood Private 2.8.0 -> 2.9.0 can enter an infinite rollback retry loop. UI shows "Rollback in progress" and never completes.

Impact:
- Upgrade/rollback does not converge; customer cluster remains in degraded state.
- Support load + customer trust risk.

Environment:
- Private installer 2.9.0
- Kubernetes (various), observed in us-east + eu-west

Suspected root cause:
Rollback cleanup hook exits before clearing rollback lock (ConfigMap `installer-rollback-lock`), causing controller to retry indefinitely.

Workaround (customer-safe):
1) Confirm namespace `redwood-private`
2) `kubectl -n redwood-private delete cm installer-rollback-lock`
3) `kubectl -n redwood-private rollout restart deploy/installer-controller`

Acceptance criteria / fix:
- Always best-effort clear lock
- Add TTL + loop detection to stop retries and surface actionable status
- Add m...

title: Persistent silent tail on extended max_tokens causing client heartbeat failures and 504s
body:
Issue summary:
Customers using large max_tokens (>= 120k) combined with multi-part system prompts report long periods of silence near the end of generation followed by client-side 504s or aborted websocket connections. The silence is observed even though the model is still producing tokens on the server side (server metrics show GPU utilization and token emission spikes), suggesting an intermittent stall in the streaming path.

Impact:
- Affected NimbusAI production workloads (dedicated cluster) experiencing ~20-40% of long-run requests terminating with 504 or websocket reconnects.
- Customer reports timeouts on batches used for document synthesis; user-facing features degrade.

Environment:
- prod (us-east-1), dedicated capacity (cluster: nimbus-prod-3)
- Models: redwood-13b-v2 and quantized mistral-7b used in different routes
- Request pattern: large system prompt (~80k tokens) + ma...

title: Blended invoice missing per-workload rate memo for mixed streaming+batch usage
body: Issue summary: Customer BrightScale.ai received March invoice with a single blended line item labeled 'multi-workload blended rate' but there is no memo breakdown showing how streaming vs batch embeddings usage were apportioned or which rate tiers applied. Impact: customer cannot reconcile costs against internal chargebacks and is requesting detailed memo lines or provisional credit. Environment: production billing run for billing period 2026-02-01 to 2026-02-28 covering hosted and dedicated fallbacks across us-east and eu-west. Customer says invoice total matches expectation but line-item explanation is insufficient for their finance team.

normalized.description: Issue summary: Customer BrightScale.ai received March invoice with a single blended line item labeled 'multi-workload blended rate' but there is no memo breakdown showing how streaming vs batch embeddings usage were apportioned or which rate tiers applied. Impact: customer cannot reconcile costs against internal chargebacks and is requesting detailed memo lines or provisional credit. Environment: production billing run for billing period 2026-02-01 to 2026-02-28 covering hosted and dedicated fallbacks across us-east and eu-west. Customer says invoice total matches expectation but line-item explanation is insufficient for their finance team.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Opened ticket after customer support call. Customer requests either a) full memo lines showing per-workload rates and quantities, or b) provisional credit until we provide an audit. Provided them with exported usage CSV as interim artifact.

comments.author: Noah Kim
comments.body: Investigating the invoice-generator memo-fragment suppression flag — looks like a recent threshold change collapsed small-line deltas into the blended bucket. I will fetch the decision logs.

comments.author: Marcus Patel
comments.body: Found the blend-decider change in PR github.com/redwood-inference/billing/PR#2899; a threshold adjustment was deployed to reduce line noise. It suppressed memo fragments when per-workload delta < 0.5% of invoice total. For BrightScale the batch embed delta was 0.32% and got suppressed.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Customer unhappy with suppression rule; they want per-workload detail regardless of delta for enterprise tier. Requested escalation to apply exception and reissue invoice memo lines.

comments.author: Noah Kim
comments.body: Prepared a manual audit CSV with per-workload attribution and suggested provisional credit equal to 0.32% of blended line (rounded) while we re-run memo generator with override.

title: VPCE HTTP/2 connection coalescing reuses host header causing presigned S3 URL 403s from private deploy
body:
Issue summary:
Customer FinVault reported intermittent 403 errors when downloading model artifacts from presigned S3 URLs during model initialization in their Private deployment. Errors only occur in production behind the customer's VPC Endpoint (Interface VPCE) and are traced to HTTP/2 connection reuse/coalescing at the VPCE layer.

Impact:
- Model pulls for a subset of hosts intermittently fail with HTTP 403 (AccessDenied) despite presigned URL being valid for the request timestamp.
- Failures are non-deterministic: repeated curl downloads may succeed or fail depending on connection reuse.
- Causes failed model pulls during cold starts, resulting in occasional inference errors and longer retries for the customer.

Environment:
- Customer: FinVault Inc. (private deployment)
- Region: us-west-2
- Private control plane with interface VPC Endpoint configured to egress to S3 via a shared...

comments.author: Leah Park ()
comments.body: Opened ticket after customer reported model pulls failing with 403s behind VPCE. Collected initial logs and reproduction steps. Recommended disabling HTTP/2 as first triage step.

comments.author: FinVault (Customer)
comments.body: Confirmed that forcing HTTP/1.1 on their workers makes downloads consistently succeed. They prefer a configuration change on their edge appliance if possible rather than changing our SDKs.

comments.author: Mateo Alvarez (SRE)
comments.body: Reproduced locally with test VPCE and edge proxy. Captured HPACK frames showing Host header reuse. Hypothesis: upstream TLS termination + HTTP/2 coalescing. Adding to investigation.

comments.author: Networking (Customer)
comments.body: We scheduled a maintenance window to disable connection coalescing on the appliance and will report back. If that fixes it, we'll consider the ticket resolved after one full day of successful model pulls.

comments.author: Leah Park ()
comments.body: Drafted runbook entry and guidance for private deployments explaining the requirement: either TLS passthrough or no HTTP/2 coalescing when presigned host-based S3 URLs are used. Action: follow up to add to docs and include example SDK flags.

comments.author: Mateo Alvarez (SRE)
comments.body: Pending verification from customer post-maint window. If confirmed, will update ticket to Resolved and open a low-priority docs task to expand VPCE guidance (linked PR created: github.com/redwood-inference/platform/pull/4821).

title: Proxy chunk boundary splits multibyte UTF-8 grapheme, causing truncated final character on SSE resumes
body: Issue summary: Customer reports that after an SSE connection resume (client reconnect + resume token) the final character of the streamed completion is sometimes replaced with a Unicode replacement character or missing entirely. Impact: end-user documents produced by LuminaDocs have truncated final punctuation or a corrupted emoji at the end of sentences in ~0.4% of resumed streams, causing downstream validation to fail. Environment: production, dedicated cluster in us-east, requests proxied through customer's on-prem nginx reverse proxy before reaching Redwood edge.

normalized.description: Issue summary: Customer reports that after an SSE connection resume (client reconnect + resume token) the final character of the streamed completion is sometimes replaced with a Unicode replacement character or missing entirely. Impact: end-user documents produced by LuminaDocs have truncated final punctuation or a corrupted emoji at the end of sentences in ~0.4% of resumed streams, causing downstream validation to fail. Environment: production, dedicated cluster in us-east, requests proxied through customer's on-prem nginx reverse proxy before reaching Redwood edge.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Customer reported via support form; attached example transcript and failing document. They said issue only after connection resume, not on initial stream.

comments.author: Ravi Patel
comments.body: Confirmed similar artifact in edge logs. Captured upstream chunk hex that splits 4-byte emoji across two writes. Filing as P1 given document validation failures for customer pipelines.

comments.author: SRE Team
comments.body: Reproduced locally by adding small proxy buffers; observed mismatch when nginx rewrites chunk boundaries. Created a minimal repro using curl + nginx conf and a test SSE server.

comments.author: (streaming-runtime)
comments.body: Ran a quick patch to ensure our SSE encoder flushes at UTF-8 boundary when emitting final event; created PR #2891 for review. Also advised customer to temporarily disable proxy_buffering or increase buffer size as workaround.

comments.author: Customer (LuminaDocs)
comments.body: Applied temporary nginx change (proxy_buffering off) and reported a disappearance of the corrupted-character symptom in their quick smoke tests.

comments.author: Ravi Patel
comments.body: Scheduled staged rollout of runtime encoder patch to canary and will monitor resumed-stream error rate metric (stream.resume.utf8_errors).
Patch merged and release candidate built (v1.3.2-rc). Canaries show reduction from 0.4% -> 0.01% resumed-stream UTF8 errors.

title: Candidate portal resume upload contained active API key and short-lived service token
body:
Issue summary: A resume upload submitted through a third-party candidate portal contained a plaintext Redwood API key and an ephemeral service token in the resume text. Initial detection occurred via automated DLP patterns that flag high-entropy strings in uploads. Impact: potential exposure of a scoped API key and short-lived token associated with a non-privileged service account used by a CI job. Immediate concern is unauthorized access to internal test harness endpoints and the possibility of lateral token exchange.

Initial detection: 2026-03-09 08:14 UTC by DLP pipeline (upload-scan). The file was quarantined by the vendor but a copy was forwarded to our S3 for analysis (retention policy triggered).

Environment: Candidate portal operated by vendor TalentFlow (third-party SaaS). Upload landed in vendor-managed bucket and vendor forwarded a quarantine copy into our intake bucket i...

comments.author: TalentFlow (vendor)
comments.body: Confirmed quarantine occurred; provided temporary signed URL for quarantine copy. Sending full upload and access logs per request. — 2026-03-10

title: Burst pool scale failures: initially triaged as runtime memory pressure; confirmed quota exhaustion
body:
Issue summary:
Hosted API burst pool failed to scale during traffic spike. Runtime reported allocation failures that looked like OOM, but node provisioning did not happen (quota ceiling).

Impact:
Intermittent 429s and latency spikes for burst routes for ~25m.

Triage notes:
- Hypothesis A: runtime memory fragmentation/OOM
- Hypothesis B: scheduler placement bug
- Confirmed: provisioning blocked by GPU quota ceiling; runtime errors were a downstream symptom

Resolution:
- Quota increased and burst pool scale policy adjusted
- Added dashboard panel for quota headroom vs pending nodes

title: Sawtooth throttling pattern during customer daily batch sweep causing intermittent 429s
body:
Issue summary:
Customer ScoutMetrics runs a nightly daily-sweep job that uploads ~10k small embedding requests in bursts every 7 minutes between 02:00-04:00 UTC. Over the last three nights they saw a repeating sawtooth pattern of 429 responses — throughput spikes then a periodic waveform of throttles for ~30s, recovers briefly, then spikes again. This is affecting their pipelines (15-25% of batches failed), causing downstream retries and significant time-to-completion regression.

Impact:
- Approx 15-25% of batch embedding requests during sweep windows return 429s.
- Customer reports downstream job run time increase from ~12m to ~45m.
- Visible errors in their job logs; customer unable to complete daily indexing reliably.

Environment:
- Tenant: ScoutMetrics (dedicated pool, committed capacity).
- Region: prod-us-east (pool id: pool-ussc-07 provided by customer).
- API path: POST /v1/...

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Ticket opened after email from ScoutMetrics CS rep (case id SM-20260310-11). Attached customer graph and sample logs. Requesting initial triage.

comments.author: Marco Silva
comments.body: Assigned. Running initial checks: pulled tenant quotas and pool config for pool-ussc-07. No manual limit changes in last 30 days. Reproducing workload on staging to validate behavior.

comments.author: Marco Silva
comments.body: Asked customer to provide exact timestamps, sample request IDs, and to confirm if workers use HTTP/2 multiplexing or short-lived API keys. Customer responded: multiplexing=enabled, keys=rotating every 10 minutes.

comments.author: Leila Gomez
comments.body: Observed periodic token-bucket depletion correlated with periodic warmup/scale events on the dedicated pool. Opening INT-5512 for investigation of autoscaler interactions. Linked thread: https://redwood.slack.com/archives/C045POOL/p1678452000

comments.author: Marco Silva
comments.body: Performed controlled staging replay (200 workers * 50 docs) with multiplexing disabled: waveform suppressed, fewer 429s (suggests multiplexing interaction). Sharing logs and hypothesis to SRE.

comments.author: ScoutMetrics rep - Jenna Patel
comments.body: Verified that disabling per-worker HTTP/2 multiplexing reduced failures in their next sweep by ~70%. Requests guidance for configuration that preserves throughput but avoids throttles.

comments.author: Marco Silva
comments.body: Consolidating guidance and next steps; drafting recommended config changes (short term: staggered start, disable per-process multiplexing; medium term: increase pool burst allowance and adjust token refill smoothing).

title: Guidance requested: shard-aware idempotency strategy for multiplexed tool chains across Go/TS SDKs
body:
Issue summary:
NimbusPayments is implementing a high-throughput payment-validation workflow that orchestrates three external tools in sequence (risk-check, account-lookup, settlement-prep). They use multiplexed client connections and the TS client on the web front-end plus a Go worker pool for server-side orchestration. They are asking for best-practice guidance for designing an idempotency strategy that avoids collisions and duplicate tool-side effects when requests are sharded across multiple worker processes and both SDKs are used.

Impact:
- Potential duplicate downstream charges or duplicate webhook posts observed in a narrow subset of high-concurrency runs (approx 0.01% of transactions).
- Customer is hesitant to roll to 100% for a dedicated flow because of regulatory payout concerns.

What they want:
- Concrete, shard-aware idempotency key design patterns that work when request...

comments.body: 05 Diego Martinez: Thanks for the clear reproduction notes, Ava. Initial thought: per-process in-memory cache is the weak link here. If a worker restarts the cache is lost and the server will see a replay as new unless the request-level idempotency header maps to a shared store or is checked against a persistent dedup table.

22 Ava Chen: Customer asked whether increasing TTL to 5 minutes will be safe. They worry about poisoning dedup state for legitimate replays across retries. Also asked about namespace/shard-prefixing best practices so multiple workers can co-exist without collisions.

10 SRE (Priya Rao): Adding notes from infra — dedicated pools have an optional Redis-backed dedup store (internal flag dedup_store=on). We should confirm whether the SDKs support attaching a shard id or a monotonic sequence to the idempotency key and whether we have library helpers documented.

title: SSE stream drops intermediate sentence during mobile network handoff (Wi‑Fi→Cellular)
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports that when a mobile client switches networks mid-stream (Wi‑Fi → Cellular) the SSE session reconnect completes, but the stream restarts from a later token and an entire intermediate sentence is dropped. The resumed stream includes tokens that make the final output syntactically correct but missing a whole clause from the middle of the response.

Impact:
- Affects a small subset of enterprise customers using long streaming responses on mobile apps.
- User-visible missing sentences in generated content (integrated note-taking app) leading to content correctness issues.
- Occurs intermittently during network handoffs; not blocking but causes unacceptable data loss for the customer (enterprise tier SLA).

Environment:
- Prod, us-east region behind our CDN + cloud LB (customer uses iOS app 16.x and Android 13).
- Client uses EventSource (SSE) via our JS shim ...

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Acknowledged receipt; asked customer for HAR and SDK logs and whether they were using our official client. Also requested a short repro prompt that reliably produces the missing sentence.

comments.author: Emily Park, Noteable Labs
comments.body: Uploaded HAR and EventSource logs, confirmed official redwood-client-js v1.4.2. Provided repro prompt and timeline (Wi‑Fi→Cellular). Reports ~30% reproducibility on short handoff (<2s).

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: Found LB GOAWAY during handoff and session_token rotation in edge logs. Noted KV cache validation failures at serving runtime correlated to those timestamps. Tentatively suspect token-to-session mapping race.

comments.author: Rina Patel
comments.body: Reproduced locally with simulated CDN handoff. Confirmed scenario: when session token is rotated during socket migration the resume_offset calculation can pick a conservative continuation index leading to skipping an intermediate sentence. Submitted preliminary PR to improve offset alignment.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Informed customer of ongoing investigation and provided a temporary client-side mitigation (buffer last 2 SSE events and apply simple dedupe by eventId on reconnect).

title: Intermittent presigned artifact truncation + 502s over ExpressRoute: HTTP/2 connection pooling + MTU clamp
body:
Issue summary:
FinTechWorks reports intermittent failures when Redwood private agents fetch large presigned model artifacts (50–200MB) from S3-backed storage. Failures manifest as: (a) partial file writes (artifact truncated by a few KB to several MB) followed by checksum mismatch on agent side, (b) transient 502/504 errors surfaced to the application during model pulls, and (c) sporadic long-lived gRPC stream resets after artifact fetch attempts succeed but streaming later fails.

Impact:
- Several worker nodes repeatedly fail model startup leading to reduced throughput and degraded SLAs for production features.
- Affected traffic localized to FinTechWorks EU private deployment and coincides with scheduled business-hours traffic spikes.

Environment:
- Private deployment (VPC) peered to Redwood control plane via ExpressRoute/partner link.
- Agents run on customer-managed subnet with ...

comments.author: (Aisha)
comments.body: Acknowledged ticket to customer, requested packet captures and edge proxy config dump. Asked for timestamped agent log and expected file size.

comments.author: FinTechWorks - network ops
comments.body: Uploaded tcpdump (pcap) and edge-proxy config snippet. Noted MSS clamp applied by SD-WAN controller to 1200 bytes and HTTP/2 pooled tunnels enabled. Reported issue frequency ~4% of downloads during peak window.

comments.author: SRE (Luca)
comments.body: Reproduced locally by configuring a lab proxy to clamp MSS and enable HTTP/2 connection coalescing; observed truncated body on large downloads when server-side emitted large TLS record crossing MSS. Filed hypothesis in ticket and linked reproducer. Asked for temporary mitigation to disable pooling for Redwood endpoints.

comments.author: Customer
comments.body: Disabled HTTP/2 pooling for a 1-hour window (as requested) and reported immediate drop in failure rate to 0.2% during the test window. Confirmed MSS clamp still in place. Asked for permanent mitigation suggestions.

comments.author: (Luca)
comments.body: Proposed two remediation paths: 1) request customer change to avoid pooling or lift MSS clamp; 2) on Redwood side, serve presigned downloads with smaller TLS record sizes (split writes) as a compatibility workaround. Opened INT-20488 to evaluate artifact-serving server-side chunking and checkout of HTTP/2 behaviour.

comments.author: Customer
comments.body: Prefers server-side mitigation while they schedule change control for SD-WAN. Asked for ETA on Redwood-side fix and possible performance impacts.

title: Intermittent 429 bursts for multiplexed websocket chat causing degraded UX
body: Issue summary: NimbusHealth is seeing intermittent bursts of HTTP 429 responses returned to multiplexed websocket clients on the hosted API during peak business hours. Impact: Users in their clinical triage chat are experiencing message failures and UI retries, increasing perceived latency and leading to dropped conversations. Scope: Affects ~10 client connection pools from NimbusHealth (enterprise account) in us-east region; seen across redwood-chat-small and medium models. Expected: Multiplexed websocket connections should see smoothing by token-level and connection-level rate limits with transparent backpressure, not sudden 429 bursts. Observed: Spikes of 429s lasting 20-40s every ~15 minutes, correlating with bursty fan-out from a single internal service. Steps to reproduce: 1) Establish multiplexed websocket session using shared API key for multiple browser clients (as customer i...

title: Midflight proxy upgrade causes websocket handoff to drop terminal JSON object and leave truncated choice
body:
Issue summary: During a high-throughput window on 2026-03-10, AtlasApps reported that long-running WebSocket streams occasionally end with a partial JSON 'choice' object and no terminal metadata (end_event). Client logs show the final JSON object missing closing braces and the 'metadata' field absent. Impact: Several production chat flows returned truncated final sentences, sometimes causing downstream parsers to throw and the customer to re-request completions, doubling token usage and affecting SLAs.

Environment: Production (eu-west), requests routed through our edge proxy pool behind a CDN with active health checks and transparent connection rebinds. A small subset of requests go through an older proxy flavor that performs an in-flight socket upgrade to another edge on backend health probe.

Observed behavior: Client receives partial JSON chunk (last text tokens present, no "end" ...

comments.author: Lina Patel

comments.body: Reproduced locally using forced connection migration; filing runtime change to ensure terminal marker emission before socket closure during handoff (PR linked).

title: Edge NAT handshake interruption results in incomplete numbered checklist + missing embedded image on SSE stream
body:
Issue summary: Customer reports that long SSE streaming responses intermittently terminate with the last numbered checklist item missing and a final embedded image URL omitted. This happens after their users experience a network path change (ISP NAT port refresh / IP rebind).

Impact: Several LuminaDocs customers see corrupted docs: a numbered checklist in the final paragraphs is cut at item 3 of 4 and the final image embed (markdown ![diagram](...)) never arrives. Problem first reported 2026-03-10; frequency ~2-3% of long (>= 2k token) streams for their users.

Environment: production (us-east), customer on dedicated plan with VPC egress through their ISP (residential & corporate). Affected model: gpt-open-xt-4b.

Steps to reproduce (customer):
1) Start SSE request that streams a long markdown document containing a 4-item numbered checklist and an inline image URL near the end.
2) Wh...

comments.author: from lumina-@luminadocs.com
comments.body: 'We see users losing the last checklist item and final image on posts when their phone swaps from wifi->cellular. Attached a packet capture and a screenshot.'

comments.author: (Ava)
comments.body: 'Thanks — we've reproduced in-house with a NAT rebind test. We're escalating to runtime/edge teams to confirm resume semantics.'

comments.author: (Ravi)
comments.body: 'Preliminary root cause: worker seals session on half-close during keepalive jitter. The resume path rejects epoch mismatch and the fallback finalization doesn't stream the buffered tail to the resume connection. I opened PR to add graceful tail-forwarding and extend resume grace window.'

comments.author: Customer (LuminaDocs)
comments.body: 'Can you suggest a temporary workaround while you patch? Our product UX relies on complete checklists.'

comments.author: (Ava)
comments.body: 'Workaround: ask clients to include a client-side explicit resume_offset in reconnect requests (we can accept earlier offsets) and increase client reconnection backoff to allow server tail-flush (~3s). We'll notify when patch is deployed.'

title: Mid-cycle per-model rounding caused multi-line invoice delta and provisional credit hold
body:
Issue summary: Customer reporting a $4,312.57 discrepancy across three invoice line items after they changed reserved capacity and model variant pins mid-billing-window. Impact: Customer billing shows duplicate-looking microbatch line items with fractional token rounding that sum to an unexpected delta. Provisional credit that normally applies during postback reconciliation was withheld pending investigation.

Context: Novus Retail migrated a portion of traffic from mixlm-13b-v1 to redwood/quantized-medium-7b on 2026-03-08 at ~18:40 UTC to reduce cost. They are on a Dedicated contract with hourly reserved capacity. The pricing tier change happened roughly in the middle of the hourly accumulation window which led to blended per-token rates for the same API key. The customer expects the platform to attribute usage to a single effective tier and apply smoothing/rounding consistently; ins...

comments.author: Ava Chen (2026-03-10)
comments.body: Logged by customer support after Novus escalation. Customer shared invoice PDF and CSV. They've paused autoscaling changes but need provisional credit applied for immediate cashflow reasons.

comments.author: Liam Patel (2026-03-10)
comments.body: Investigating. I've pulled relevant postbacks and correlated edge-proxy traces. Noted mix of tier tags in the window. Added billing/owner (finance) to the thread.

comments.author: Priya Rao - Billing (2026-03-10)
comments.body: Looking into aggregator logic. We currently round at per-model microbatch stage. Finance prefers final-sum rounding. Preparing repro in staging.

comments.author: Jamal Ortiz
comments.body: Edge proxy resize event at 2026-03-08T18:40:12Z shows some in-flight postbacks retried without metadata; likely root cause for lost tag. We need to add idempotent tag propagation on retry path.

comments.author: Emma Li
comments.body: We appreciate the fast triage. Can you provisionally credit the invoice while you investigate? The amount is blocking their payroll approval.

comments.author: Liam Patel (2026-03-12)
comments.body: Temporary workaround proposed: apply a one-time manual credit equal to the delta ($4,312.57) and mark for reversal if reconciliation shows otherwise. Awaiting approval from Finance ops.

title: Access request: Grafana Provision-to-Ready dashboards + Fleet Readiness tooling permissions (least-privilege)
body:
Requester: Harish Reddy (Infra/Fleet ops rollout coordinator)

Request:
Need access for Fleet Ops to the new Provision-to-Ready Grafana dashboards and readiness tooling ahead of staged rollout (us-east / A100). Specifically:
1) Grafana folder: “Provision-to-Ready” (dashboards + alert views; read-only is fine)
2) Fleet Readiness Service internal UI endpoint (read-only; ability to view per-node state + validation failure reason + artifact pointers)

Business justification:
- On-call readiness during staged rollout; need to quickly diagnose nodes stuck in Provisioning/Validating and see step breakdown/failure heatmaps.
- Coordinate with SRE on alert tuning without needing ad-hoc screen shares.

Access constraints / least-privilege request:
- Read-only access only. No permission to mutate readiness state, quarantine nodes, or change image channel.
- Group-based access preferred (no indivi...

comments.body: 18 (Fiona McCarthy): Confirming scope. For least-privilege: we can grant Grafana folder Viewer + Fleet Readiness Service read-only role. Mutating endpoints require separate approval and are restricted to Platform on-call + SRE per current policy. Please confirm the two names and whether access should extend to the broader #eng-infra on-call rotation.
27 (Harish Reddy): Confirmed names are Harish Reddy + Jamal Pierce for now. Not requesting full rotation yet; we’ll expand once we stabilize dashboards/alerts and finalize runbook.

05 (Fiona McCarthy): Approvals required:
- Infra/Fleet manager approval (Vanessa Ortiz)
- SRE approval for dashboard/alert visibility (Sean Gallagher or Rafael Mendes)
Please reply in-thread with explicit “approved” so we can attach to the ticket.
32 (Vanessa Ortiz): Approved for Harish Reddy + Jamal Pierce. Viewer-only access is appropriate for rollout ops. Please ensure access is via group and logged.
10 (Sean Gallagher): Approved. Viewer access only. Any alert rule edits or paging policy changes must continue to go through SRE (Rafael/Miles/Aisha) during rollout week.

44 (Fiona McCarthy): Implementing via Okta/SSO group-based RBAC:
- Group: grafana-provision-to-ready-viewers (Grafana folder viewer)
- Group: fleet-readiness-ro (Read-only access to readiness state + validation artifacts; no POST/PUT)
We are explicitly NOT adding to:
- fleet-readiness-ops (quarantine/retry privileges)
- grafana-admin
Audit note: group membership changes will be logged and reviewable.
ETA: ~30 minutes for propagation.

31 (Harish Reddy): Confirmed I can see the “Provision-to-Ready” dashboards and drill into step timings/failure heatmap. Fleet Readiness UI also loads and shows failure reason + artifact link pointers. Looks good.

02 (Fiona McCarthy): Closing as resolved. If you later need operator actions (retry validation, quarantine override, or image channel changes), file a separate INT ticket with:
- exact endpoints/actions needed
- list of users/on-call rotation
- time-bounded access if applicable
- additional approval from Platform sponsor (Priya Natarajan) because it’s state-mutating.

title: Chunked request + oversized tracing headers cause API Gateway proxy buffering and idle timeouts leading to 504s
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports intermittent 504 gateway timeouts and increased tail latency for long-running chat completions. Issue appears correlated with clients sending chunked transfer requests and very large tracing/correlation headers (X-Trace-Baggage). Impact:
- ~12% of requests from NimbleDocs in us-west-2 are failing with 504 or returning after >30s delay.
- Affects production inference for redwood/gpt-7b-v2-quant used in assistant completion flows.
Environment:
- Prod, us-west-2, Hosted API (edge -> API Gateway -> NLB -> serving pool)
- API Gateway: AWS ALB in front of Nginx proxy instances (managed by infra team)
- Customer client uses chunked transfer with streaming and attaches large tracing baggage headers (~9–12 KB)
Observed behavior:
- Requests with headers >8KB frequently trigger proxy buffering in Nginx and cause the ALB to hit idle timeout waiting for backend byte...

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Opened with customer logs attached. Mentioned they saw spike in 504s starting 2026-03-10 after SDK update that added extra trace baggage.

comments.author: Marcus Patel
comments.body: I reproduced in staging with large headers + delayed backend flush. Nginx proxy_buffering sent to disk after 8KB which delayed backend handshake to ALB. Suggest temporary patch: disable proxy_buffering on stream endpoint.

comments.author: NimbleDocs, Elena Rossi
comments.body: We can trim the header size, but SDK change is org-wide and not easy to revert. Requesting a server-side fix.

comments.author: Priya Kumar
comments.body: Ran a config experiment on canary Nginx to increase proxy_buffer_size to 16k for streaming route. Preliminary tests show reduced proxy temp file writes and no 504s in replicated scenario.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Notified customer of workaround (trim trace header to <4KB) and that infra is testing canary config. Customer accepted workaround temporarily. Keeping ticket open while canary runs.

title: GCP Workload Identity federation -> AWS assume-role: session-tag truncation causing KMS key policy denies during Dedicated provisioning
body:
Issue summary:
During automated Dedicated capacity provisioning for customer-tenant 'acme-ml', the cross-cloud credential broker (GCP Workload Identity Federation -> AWS STS) succeeded but downstream KMS operations failed with AccessDenied. Impact: Dedicated provisioner could not finish creating reserved clusters for two customers, blocking onboarding and test traffic.

Impact:
- 2 Dedicated provisioning runs failed in us-east region (customer-facing).
- Provisioner left partial resources (EC2 instances created without required KMS-wrapped secrets).
- Oncall had to rollback an attempted retry causing extra cost.

Environment:
- Provisioner service: prod-us-east namespace
- Broker: gcp-wif-broker (uses service account svc-provisioner@redwood-prod.iam.gserviceaccount.com)
- AWS role: arn:aws:iam::222222222222:role/redwood-dedicated-provisioner
- KMS key: arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:2222222222...

comments.body: First observed during dedicated provisioning for acme-ml; escalation requested.

comments.author: Miguel Alvarez () 2026-03-10
comments.body: Acknowledged - assigning to SRE + Auth to investigate token size and tag propagation.

comments.author: SRE-Lead (comment) 2026-03-11
comments.body: Confirmed CloudTrail shows missing PrincipalTag keys on assumed role. Captured full STS request/response for sample trace id pr-9857.

comments.author: Auth-Engineer (comment) 2026-03-12
comments.body: We reproduced in staging. Suspect session tag ordering in caller code matters; recommend moving tenant/tag keys up earlier and dropping non-essential tags.

comments.author: Miguel Alvarez 2026-03-13
comments.body: Coordinated with Provisioner owners to open PR to remove extra_tag and always emit tenant as first tag. Built hotfix branch for immediate rollout.

comments.author: Provisioner-Owner 2026-03-14
comments.body: Hotfix merged and deployed to prod canary; provisioning for acme-ml retried and completed successfully. Monitoring for two hours.

title: Timezone-normalized audit stream with deterministic event UUIDs and per-event HMAC for Sumo Logic ingest
body:
Issue summary:
Customer requests a guaranteed-ordered, timezone-normalized NDJSON audit stream suitable for Sumo Logic HTTP source ingestion. Requirements: deterministic event UUIDs (so identical logical events produce identical ids across retries), per-event HMAC signature header with key-rotation guidance, and UTC canonicalization of timestamps. This is intended as evidence for an upcoming security audit and to support forensic replayability across their SIEM.

Impact:
- Greenline cannot complete their SIEM onboarding without a reproducible schema and an example ingest configuration.
- They require per-event HMAC verification for tamper-evidence and a signed sample dataset for audit submission.

Environment:
- Customer production account ingest (Greenline-prod) -> Redwood audit webhook -> Sumo Logic HTTP collector (us1.sumologic.com). Region: us-east-1.

This is intended as evidence for an upcoming security audit and to support forensic replayability across their SIEM.

Impact:
- Greenline cannot complete their SIEM onboarding without a reproducible schema and an example ingest configuration.
- They require per-event HMAC verification for tamper-evidence and a signed sample dataset for audit submission.

Environment:
- Customer production account ingest (Greenline-prod) -> Redwood audit webhook -> Sumo Logic HTTP collector (us1.sumologic.com). Region: us-east-1.

title: Phased access ramp for contractor->employee conversion with device entitlement hold
body:
Issue summary:
We have an external contractor converting to full-time employee (start date 2026-03-22). HR requests a phased access ramp: maintain contractor service accounts for a 2-week overlap but disable privileged groups until completion of background check and I-9. Device entitlement hold: current loaner MacBook must be retained by IT until device transfer checklist completed.

Impact:
Potential gap in access for cross-team handover; risk of orphaned service credentials if not rotated timely.

Context and constraints:
- Contractor uses several service accounts used in CI and internal datasets.
- Payroll/vendor sync must run prior to SSO provisioning for benefits.
- Need to ensure disk encryption keys and KMS access are transferred or rekeyed.

Requested changes:
1) Create bridge account (ephemeral) for two-week overlap with read-only access to production logs.
2) Schedule KMS re...

title: Edge probe split causes half-formed streamed messages after reconnect
body: Issue summary: Customer reports that after an intermittent network disconnect, SSE streams resume but the first resumed payloads are half-formed JSON fragments and the client parser fails. Impact: Several NexaHealth production workflows ingesting streamed clinical summaries are producing invalid partial objects; customer reports ~15% of resumed sessions encounter parse errors and drops. Environment: prod, us-east, edge-proxy path through third-party CDN probe that occasionally inserts synthetic TCP probe packets. Observed behavior: On reconnect the server sends a resumed chunk that starts mid-UTF8 sequence and omits the JSON array opener; client logs "JSON parse error: unexpected token". Expected behavior: Stream resume should deliver complete, well-delimited framing and finalization events so clients can stitch without parse errors. Business impact: High — downstream ingestion pipeli...

comments.author: SRE (2026-03-13 22
comments.body: 10) - Priya Singh: Confirmed CDN probe pattern in edge logs. Captured server-side resume offset and observed chunk starting with 0xE2 (mid UTF-8). Hypothesis: resume offset pointing mid-codepoint. Requesting runtime team to review resume semantics.

comments.author: Runtime (2026-03-14 09
comments.body: 35) - Diego Marquez: Added local patch that scans backward from resume_offset up to 16 bytes to align to a valid UTF-8 boundary and to nearest JSON delimiter when feasible. Lab validation shows significant improvement. Preparing PR for serving-runtime.

comments.body: 00) - Priya Singh: Reached out to CDN support rep to ask about probe window settings; CDN acknowledged probes can be configured but requires change window and impact assessment. Escalating to account team.
20) - Ava Chen: Noted customer's appetite for a coordinated rollout: runtime hotfix + CDN probe config change. Scheduling maintenance window proposal.

comments.body: 45) - Diego Marquez: PR opened: github.com/redwood-inference/serving-runtime/pull/2892 includes defensive resync logic and unit tests covering UTF-8 boundary alignment and JSON delimiter resync. Marked for expedited review.

title: New-hire onboarding: data-visualization console access stuck due to identity crosswalk mapping mismatch
body: Issue summary: A recently hired PM cannot access the internal DataViz console after completing SSO and SCIM provisioning. The user record appears in Okta and SCIM logs, but the console returns 403 with a message 'no roles assigned'. Impact: New hire onboarding blocked (cannot run dashboards or join pairing session). This is delaying a planned sync meeting tomorrow with the product team and is time-sensitive. Environment: Production (us-west), internal console behind identity-proxy, SCIM ingest pipeline v2.2.2. Steps to reproduce: 1) Invite new user via HR-provisioner -> Okta created profile. 2) Auto-provisioner runs SCIM PATCH to identity service. 3) User signs in via Okta SSO and is authenticated, but the console shows 403 and the support-runbook 'check-user-roles' returns empty role list. Observed logs: SCIM PATCH succeeded (200). Okta event shows user assigned to group "prod-datavi...

title: Differential audit-merge manifest for PSP reconciliation and compliance evidence
body:
Issue summary:
Customer (Orchid Bank, enterprise) requires a differential (delta) audit-merge manifest that maps Redwood activity records to their internal PSP transaction IDs for reconciliation and regulator evidence. They want cryptographically verifiable manifests (per-batch HMAC) plus a small sample payload demonstrating mapping logic for two days of high-volume traffic.

Impact:
- Orchid Bank cannot complete month-end PSP reconciliation without the mapping manifest and cryptographic proof. Potential regulatory reporting delay.
- High priority due to accounting/legal timelines for transaction dispute windows.

Environment:
- Production (us-east primary, eu-west failover).
- Customer uses Redwood hosted API and writes audit exports to their S3 bucket (s3://orchid-bank-redwood/exports/).

Requested deliverables from Redwood Support/Eng: 
1) A differential audit-merge manifest format...

title: Negative invoice line for cache-reconciliation credit after mid-cycle rate profile conversion
body:
Issue summary:
OpenFleet reports a negative line item on their March 2026 invoice described as a 'cache-reconciliation credit' that exceeds expected adjustments. This appeared after we applied a mid-cycle model-rate profile change (hosted -> reserved-tier mapping) during a large multi-region batch run.

Impact:
- Customer invoice shows a -$12,842.50 line item labeled 'cache reconciliation credit' for 2026-03-05 to 2026-03-06.
- Customer is concerned this is an erroneous credit that will be clawed back or affect their committed reserved hours. Finance flagged it as a potential over-credit and paused auto-payment.
- Customer-facing feature: billing visibility in Console shows mixed rates for the same request IDs.

Environment:
- OpenFleet org id: org-0943 (enterprise, reserved capacity + hosted fallback)
- Regions: primary us-east for serving, eu-west fallback during failover window
- A...

comments.body: Thank you for the updates. Please advise timeline for reversal and whether our reserved commitment will be affected. We prefer no retroactive clawbacks; we just want invoice corrected for this cycle.

title: Reserved burst credits not allocated to subaccount after plan downgrade during hourly billing window
body: Issue summary: Customer reports that reserved burst credits (hourly burst capacity) were not applied to their subaccount after they downgraded a seat-level Dedicated plan in the middle of an hourly billing window. Impact: BrightCart observed a sudden unexpected invoice adjustment and a missing credit line for the subaccount, resulting in an overage alert and a triggered automated payment hold. Timeline: 2026-03-01 22:05 UTC - Customer changed plan (downgrade) on parent org. 2026-03-01 22:12 UTC - Scheduler ran hourly credit allocation job for burst capacity. 2026-03-01 22:20 UTC - Customer subaccount shows no burst credits applied; parent account shows partial allocation. 2026-03-02 08:00 UTC - Customer opened support ticket. Key question: why weren't burst credits allocated to the subaccount when the allocation job ran within the same hour as the downgrade?

Key question: why weren't burst credits allocated to the subaccount when the allocation job ran within the same hour as the downgrade?

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Hi BrightCart — thanks for the report. We see the missing burst credit and I've escalated to billing eng. We'll update within SLA.

comments.author: Marcus Lee
comments.body: Initial triage suggests allocator snapshot staleness. I've queued a manual correction and will open INT ticket for indexer/allocator coupling.

comments.author: Elena Rodriguez, BrightCart
comments.body: Thanks — the provisional credit arrived and dashboard now reflects correct burst capacity. Can you confirm the root cause so we can avoid this before our next scheduled heavy batch on Monday?

comments.author: Priyanka Nair
comments.body: Reproducer test added to CI to simulate mid-window downgrade combined with allocation run. Indexer refresh-before-allocate implemented in branch fix/alloc-stale-guard. PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/infra/pull/1934

comments.author: Marcus Lee
comments.body: Patch merged to staging, scheduled for canary on 2026-03-06. Follow-up audit scheduled to run across last 72 hourly windows for any similar mismatch and backfill credits if found.

title: Dedicated: request timeouts after autoscaler scale-up (us-east)
body:
Issue summary:
- Northstar dedicated cluster: timeouts increase immediately after scaling from 8 -> 12 GPUs.

Impact:
- ~10% requests failing with 504s at customer ingress.
- Customers see spike during business hours; off-peak is normal.

Primary symptom: Request timeouts
Deployment offering: Dedicated

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: northstar-prod-us-east

Notes:
- Customer uses their own NLB with 60s timeout.
- We suspect new pods are marked Ready before the runtime is actually warm (model load / KV cache init).

Related:

title: PulseRx — mid-stream token interleaving from multiplexer misrouting causing garbled streaming completions and spike in billable tokens
body: Issue summary: During PulseRx's morning ingest window (09:30-10:15 UTC, 2026-03-11) a subset of streaming completions returned interleaved token sequences and occasionally duplicated segments from concurrent requests. This produced garbled patient-facing summaries and inflated token counts on billing. Impact: high — customer-facing clinical summarization pipeline produced invalid notes for ~0.9% of requests in the window, and PulseRx reports a ~22% increase in billable tokens for the same period.

title: Bulk procurement + vendor onboarding: security tooling SaaS seats (SAML + SSO enablement)
body:
Issue summary:
Security team needs to purchase and onboard multiple seats for a set of SaaS tools required for a new detection engineering pod (hired start of Q2). This ticket covers procurement, vendor onboarding (SAML/SSO setup with Okta), license assignment workflow, and completion of vendor security questionnaire where required.

Impact:
- 6 engineers (Detection & Threat Infra) cannot be granted full access to the new SSO-protected tooling until licenses are procured and vendor SSO is configured.
- Onboarding delay will push planned runbook work and SIEM ingestion tasks by ~2–3 weeks.

Requested items (detailed):
1) Rapid7 InsightVM: 6 full analyst seats + 1 admin seat (12-month term).
2) Fugue (cloud security posture): 6 seats (annual) — vendor requires company IA questionnaire and SOC2 evidence.
3) Extra Okta app connections: add FugaCorp (Fugue) and Rapid7 to Okta allowlist and...

title: Go SDK: idempotency key prefixing by orchestrator leads to duplicate function/tool invocations
body: Customer reports repeated duplicate downstream tool/webhook invocations originating from a long-running, multiplexed orchestration flow that uses per-action idempotency semantics. The Go SDK + their orchestrator appear to be prefixing the idempotency key with a shard/orch token before each sub-request; in some cases the prefixed key is truncated by an edge proxy, resulting in collisions and duplicate side effects.

normalized.description: Customer reports repeated duplicate downstream tool/webhook invocations originating from a long-running, multiplexed orchestration flow that uses per-action idempotency semantics. The Go SDK + their orchestrator appear to be prefixing the idempotency key with a shard/orch token before each sub-request; in some cases the prefixed key is truncated by an edge proxy, resulting in collisions and duplicate side effects.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Received initial report from BrightRoute Labs. They provided correlation ids and webhooks showing duplicates. Assigned to Liam for deeper triage.

comments.author: (Liam O'Connor)
comments.body: Confirmed customer's Go SDK version and got reproduction steps. Spun up their sidecar locally to reproduce truncation.

comments.author: BrightRoute Ops
comments.body: We increased sidecar header limit to 128 bytes and duplicates disappeared in staging. We're testing deterministic-hash approach as well.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Customer reports canary running with hash-based idempotency and no duplicates for 8 hours. Preparing resolution notes and KB draft.

title: SSE TLS 0-RTT resumption during edge handoff produces partial markdown code fence and drops trailing list items
body:
Issue summary:
Customer experiences truncated SSE streams when TLS 1.3 0-RTT (early data) is used and the client path migrates across edge nodes. The truncation consistently removes the closing markdown code fence (```), and drops the final 1-3 list items from enumerations. Impact:
- Customer's live documentation generator (NoteWave) shows malformed Markdown and missing bullet points in ~4% of streaming sessions during peak traffic.
- Affects user-facing web previews; breaks downstream parsers that expect closed code blocks.
Environment:
- Customer: NoteWave Labs (dedicated tenant)
- API: Hosted Redwood SSE endpoint via api.redwood.ai (prod-us-east)
- Model: gpt-redwood-3b-v1 pinned version 2026-02-25
- Network: client mobile app on LTE -> public mobile NAT -> CDN edge -> Redwood edge proxy -> serving pool
Steps to reproduce (customer-provided):
1) Use mobile app with TLS 1.3 0-RTT en...

title: Runner host clock drift causing OIDC token validation failures in release pipeline
body:
Issue summary: Multiple release jobs started failing mid-run with OIDC validation errors. Impact: Blocked nightly release pipeline and several internal CI workflows that use self-hosted runners for release artifacts. Brief: During release orchestration we observed repeated failures validating OIDC tokens from runner-hosted jobs. Token validation failed at the 'iat' and 'exp' checks on the control plane; logs show 10-90s skew between runner host and control-plane clocks. This appears to be an intermittent but repeatable problem on a subset of self-hosted runners provisioned from the us-west fleet after last week's image push.

Expected behavior: Runners should have synchronized clocks (NTP/chrony) and OIDC tokens signed by the cloud provider should validate successfully during token exchange.

Observed behavior: Jobs fail with token validation errors; release-orchestrator marks jobs as...

comments.body: Confirmed AMI bake change removed packaged chrony config. Rolling back the image is low-risk; prepping rollback image now.

comments.body: Applied emergency remediation to current runners (started chronyd, pointed to regional NTP pool). Monitoring for recurrence. Also creating playbook steps to detect skew > 5s and restart chronyd automatically.

comments.body: Recommend adding a healthcheck in runner bootstrap that asserts (<5s) drift; add post-deploy validation for future AMI bakes.

comments.body: Confirmed that after remediation failing rate dropped from 18% -> 0.8% over 6 hours. Still phasing rollback to stable AMI to avoid churn.

title: High 429 rates observed after migrating to short-lived API keys with parallel multipart uploader
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reported a sudden spike in HTTP 429 responses after switching to short-lived API keys (30m TTL) and enabling a parallel multipart uploader for large embedding jobs. This started approximately 2026-02-20 17:10 UTC immediately after the key rotation rollout.

Impact:
- CipherDocs ingestion jobs are failing at ~60% throughput due to intermittent 429s.
- Affects critical nightly syncs and causes downstream analytics delays.
- Errors are concentrated on the embedding endpoint and on a dedicated pool in us-east.

Timeline / Observed behavior:
- 2026-02-20 17:05 UTC: CipherDocs rotated to short-lived keys and launched a new uploader service configured for N=16 parallel upload streams per worker.
- 2026-02-20 17:10 UTC: Support alerted by customer about elevated 429 rates.
- Error patterns: bursts of 429s lasting 10–40s with recovery, correlated with large batches (300...

20 UTC — SRE (Liam): Engineering action item created to evaluate rate-limiter change; tracking PR referenced in related_github_prs.

title: Intermittent 429s during mixed keepalive + signed-callback workflow; apparent token "echo" across proxied callbacks
body:
Issue summary: HeliosSearch is seeing transient 429s in their dedicated pool during large-scale document enrichment jobs. The traffic pattern mixes long-lived streaming connections (SDK keepalive) with asynchronous signed-url callbacks to their ingest orchestrator. The customer reports 429s observed only after the callback chain completes for many documents and does not correlate directly to their token usage dashboard.

Impact: Several enrichment batches (~5-10% of requests) are rejected with 429s for 10-30s intervals, causing retries and falling throughput. This is affecting nightly enrichment SLA and causing backfills to miss windows.

Environment: Dedicated pool, prod-us-west, tenant: helios-prod, worker fleet behind a corporate edge proxy that forwards signed-callback POSTs to Redwood signed-url endpoints.

Steps to reproduce (as provided by customer):
1) Start a multiprocess bat...

Steps to reproduce (as provided by customer):
1) Start a multiprocess bat...

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Customer opened. Collected request IDs and sample rejected X-Request-Id headers. Asked for ingress raw tcpdump and proxy logs; customer provided sanitized proxy logs and a few packet captures.

comments.author: Marco Alvarez
comments.body: Noted tenant uses a single API key. Initial hypothesis: edge NAT rotation + proxy replays may create duplicate accounting when callbacks are retried by the proxy. Requesting ingestion of the tenant into a debug token-accounting trace for the suspected window (us-west-2, 2026-03-09 02:00-04:00 UTC).

comments.author: Sara Patel, HeliosSearch
comments.body: We observed the spike correlates to our nightly backfill process hitting ~12k callbacks/min. We see the proxy requeue on transient 5xx from our side, but Redwood rejects are the ones causing retries upstream. We attached sanitized proxy configs showing an aggressive retry policy (3 retries, no jitter).

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Confirmed we will run a rate-accounting trace and check for duplicate preflight/POST counting and token-bucket shard attribution. Asked product if a temporary per-route isolation for callbacks can be applied for this tenant while investigating.

comments.author: Daniel Kim
comments.body: Can enable route isolation on the dedicated pool for signed-callback path (/v1/callbacks/uploaded) as a short-term mitigation (route will use separate burst bucket). Recommending customer reduce proxy retries and add request-id dedupe in edge. Preparing rollout steps.

comments.author: Marco Alvarez
comments.body: Found accounting trace: ~24% of callback POSTs show duplicate ingress accounting events within a 2s window with different edge IPs; many align with proxy retry timestamps. Also saw keepalive streams maintaining open windows causing refill alignment anomalies during the spike.

Impact: Several enrichment batches (~5-10% of requests) are rejected with 429s for 10-30s intervals, causing retries and falling throughput. This is affecting nightly enrichment SLA and causing backfills to miss windows.

Environment: Dedicated pool, prod-us-west, tenant: helios-prod, worker fleet behind a corporate edge proxy that forwards signed-callback POSTs to Redwood signed-url endpoints.

Steps to reproduce (as provided by customer):
1) Start a multiprocess bat...

title: Bastion proxy rewrite causes split terminal fragments and missing final sentences on SSE resume
body:
Issue summary:
Customer (FinLedger) reports intermittent loss of the final sentence for long SSE streams when an upstream bastion performs a short connection reset and header rewrite. The terminal JSON chunk often appears split across the reset boundary and the client receives an incomplete closing fragment (missing end_event / closing bracket).

Impact:
- ~0.6% of long (>20s) streaming responses truncated during high load windows, causing client-side retries and duplicate prefixes.
- Affected flows: interactive chat completions and long-form document generation.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool in Redwood hosted prod (us-east-1). Model pinned: redwood-3b-chat v2026-02-28.
- Customer uses a managed bastion that rewrites headers and proxies HTTP/1.1 <-> HTTP/2. Bastion performs idle probes and occasionally injects a content-length header when replaying buffered data.

Steps attempted to ...

title: Cascading API-key handovers causing tenant concurrency exhaustion and intermittent 429 bursts
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports a burst of 429 responses across their tenant after an automated API key rotation during a high-throughput streaming upload window. The pattern is reproducible when their client rotates keys and simultaneously spawns short-lived prefetch workers that fan out requests to multiple routes.

Impact:
- LexaHealth observed ~30% request failures (429) for 7–12 minutes after an automated key rotation during a nightly data push.
- Business impact: ingestion pipeline stalled; downstream throughput SLA missed.

Steps to reproduce (provided by customer):
1) Configure two API keys (K-A primary, K-B rotated-in) with equal privileges under same tenant.
2) Client spawns 40 short-lived prefetch workers that request streaming completions and embeddings concurrently (burst over ~10s).
3) Client triggers automated rotation: start sending requests with both K-A and K-B for a...

comments.author: LexaHealth (N. Patel)
comments.body: We're seeing a lot of 429s after our scheduled rotation. Rotation is automated via our CI; overlap window is ~30s. We need an emergency workaround.

comments.author: Ava Chen ()
comments.body: Acknowledged. Can you attach request traces and the rotation script? Also please confirm whether you use short-lived worker processes for prefetching.

comments.author: LexaHealth (N. Patel)
comments.body: Traces uploaded to provided S3 path. Yes, we spawn ~40 prefetch workers for each batch job and ramp down after completion.

comments.author: Marcus Lee (SRE)
comments.body: Preliminary review shows tenant bucket drained during rotation spike. Workarounds: (1) pause rotation during heavy ingest, (2) add exponential backoff and jitter on client, (3) request temporary quota bump for rotation window. Can you test pausing rotation in next run?

comments.author: LexaHealth (N. Patel)
comments.body: We can schedule a window to pause rotation — will test tonight and report results.

comments.author: Marcus Lee (SRE)
comments.body: Engineering is evaluating a server-side smoothing feature (INT-4882). We'll update once we have a plan; in the meantime please test the client mitigations above.

body:
Issue summary: Customer reports intermittent streaming disconnects and timeouts starting ~16:05 UTC.

- Hiro Tanaka (Runtime): Prepared hotfix PR 28417 to auto-disable experimental scheduler at high stream concurrency; queued for rollout after stability window.

Resolution: Config mitigation reduced dis...

title: Payload-size throttle triggers upstream restart cycle and elevated 5xx for large embedding uploads
body:
Issue summary: Customer BrightChat reports a sustained elevation in 5xx responses beginning 2026-03-12 02:10 UTC during large embedding multipart uploads. Impact: multiple clients seeing ~15-30% failed requests (5xx) for embedding endpoints; customer observed timeouts and client-side retry storms. Environment: prod us-east; self-serve tenant; burst of concurrent multipart uploads (~100 parallel multipart sessions from a single customer API key). Steps to reproduce:
1) Use BrightChat embedding bulk uploader with 100 parallel workers, each submitting multipart upload with ~12MB parts (total ~120MB per session).
2) Observe API gateway accept-queue growth and some requests truncated by body parser.
3) After sustained churn (~3–5 minutes), upstream serving-runtime processes restart (watchdog), causing 502/503/504 depending on stage.
Observed behavior: apigw emits 502->503 waves; logs show ...

1) Use BrightChat embedding bulk uploader with 100 parallel workers, each submitting multipart upload with ~12MB parts (total ~120MB per session).
2) Observe API gateway accept-queue growth and some requests truncated by body parser.
3) After sustained churn (~3–5 minutes), upstream serving-runtime processes restart (watchdog), causing 502/503/504 depending on stage.
Observed behavior: apigw emits 502->503 waves; logs show ...

comments.author: Ava Chen ()
comments.body: Initial triage completed; escalated to SRE team and opened this incident. Informed customer we are investigating and suggested temporary client-side throttling.

comments.author: Lucas Rivera (SRE)
comments.body: Reproduced locally with simulated uploader traffic. Observed per-request buffer allocation in upload-service grows to full part size (12MB) before streaming to runtime; combined with 100 parallel sessions this trips process vmem threshold. Filed runtime concern to engineering.

comments.author: SRE (on-call)
comments.body: Short-term mitigation applied: lowered apigw per-connection max buffer to 4MB and enabled early 413 response for >8MB parts for affected route. This reduced immediate OOM restarts and lowered 5xx rate to ~3%.

comments.author: BrightChat (customer)
comments.body: Confirmed reduction in failures after advised rate-limiting and suggested uploader changes; however they still see intermittent 5xx during spikes. Asked for permanent fix timeline.

comments.author: Runtime
comments.body: Found that runtime's multipart handler eagerly materializes parts into memory for checksum/validation; added an investigation note and a draft PR that changes checksum to streaming mode (runtime PR linked).

comments.author: Lucas Rivera (SRE)
comments.body: Deployed hotfix pipeline: patched upload-service to stream parts directly to temp file and apply backpressure to upstream; rolling deployment across us-east completed at 07:12 UTC. Monitoring shows 5xx back down to baseline (~0.2%).

comments.author: Ava Chen ()
comments.body: Notified customer of fix and asked them to resume normal upload cadence and report any anomalies.

title: Progress-callbacks + client-side retry causing unacknowledged tool executions and billing confusion
body:
Issue summary:
Customer uses the TypeScript SDK in a serverless worker to issue long-running orchestrations that include multiple tool/function calls per user request. They've enabled progress callbacks (per-action progress events) so their frontend can display live status. Recently they observed duplicate billing for tool executions: the backend logs show the tool run completed, but the client timed out waiting for the final progress acknowledgement and retried the whole orchestration, which triggered the tooling to re-execute. The customer is requesting guidance on how to avoid unverified double-executions and how to reconcile charges.

Impact:
- Customer reports 2-8% increase in tooling cost for a specific heavy workflow.
- UI shows transient "stuck" state when progress callbacks are lost; sometimes user retries from browser which results in duplicate downstream effects (e.g., dupl...

normalized.description:
Issue summary:
Customer uses the TypeScript SDK in a serverless worker to issue long-running orchestrations that include multiple tool/function calls per user request. They've enabled progress callbacks (per-action progress events) so their frontend can display live status. Recently they observed duplicate billing for tool executions: the backend logs show the tool run completed, but the client timed out waiting for the final progress acknowledgement and retried the whole orchestration, which triggered the tooling to re-execute. The customer is requesting guidance on how to avoid unverified double-executions and how to reconcile charges.

Impact:
- Customer reports 2-8% increase in tooling cost for a specific heavy workflow.
- UI shows transient "stuck" state when progress callbacks are lost; sometimes user retries from browser which results in duplicate downstream effects (e.g., dupl...

comments.author: Customer (J. Patel)
comments.body: We see duplicate downstream emails and extra billing. We cannot disable retries in user browsers. Need a reliable pattern to avoid double side effects.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Requesting full request+response traces and the SDK telemetry for that run. Can you include the orchestration payload (sanitized) and confirm whether you set any idempotency headers per-tool?

comments.author: Customer (J. Patel)
comments.body: We attached traces. We did not set per-tool idempotency metadata; we only set a top-level operationId on our side but not passed to the SDK. The SDK call shows no idempotency_key in nested tool RPCs.

comments.author: Diego Martins
comments.body: Reproduced locally with given trace and proxy timeout. Observed: SDK v1.8.2 does not automatically inject top-level idempotency keys into nested tool invocations when using progress callbacks and streaming resume. This leads to the second orchestration retry being treated as a fresh run by the serving runtime.

comments.author: (SRE) note
comments.body: The serving runtime records tool executions by an internal execution id; idempotency detection relies on client-provided idempotency metadata propagated to tool calls. Without it, side effects are not deduplicated. Also noted connection close on ack path causes SDK to treat whole request as failed and will trigger client-side retry semantics.

comments.author: Diego Martins
comments.body: Provided short-term mitigation steps and logged an SDK bug (see related PR). Advised customer on per-tool idempotency headers and suggested increasing proxy timeouts while we release a fix.

comments.author: Customer (J. Patel)
comments.body: Confirmed workaround reduced duplicates in staging. Asked about recommended long-term patterns and whether Redwood will provide server-side de-dup.

title: Self-hosted runners unable to upload job artifacts after storage service-account key rotation
body: - Vault rotation job executed 2026-03-12T03:10Z and wrote new key:ss: gcs-release-sa. - We observed two behaviors: (A) runners that restarted after rotation pick up new key and succeed; (B) runners that continued running show vault fetch success but gcloud auth operations intermittently fail with 'invalid_grant' or 403 when exchanging keys. - No global IAM role changes detected in cloud audit logs at rotation time. - Artifact uploader config unchanged. - Affected runner pods share the same node pools as healthy ones; not a node-level network issue. - Related Slack thread: #ci-ops (link). - Linked issue INT-48917 (ephemeral-runner-vault-rewrap-decrypt-failure) investigated vault rewrap before; not identical but useful for pattern comparison.

title: Rate limit headers inconsistent across endpoints (Limit/Remaining/Reset mismatch; confusing 429 behavior)
body:
Customer reports that rate limit headers are inconsistent across endpoints and sometimes across responses within the same minute. This is causing engineering confusion and retry storms: their client relies on headers to backoff, but the values appear to jump/reset unpredictably.

Observed in Hosted API while using both chat completions and embeddings. Customer also reports intermittent 429s where headers imply remaining > 0.

This ticket tracks Support triage + evidence collection and links to engineering fix workstream (ENG-4825).

Observed in Hosted API while using both chat completions and embeddings. Customer also reports intermittent 429s where headers imply remaining > 0.

This ticket tracks Support triage + evidence collection and links to engineering fix workstream (ENG-4825).

comments.body: 18Z — Priya Shah (Reporter): Customer (Aurora Search) sharing examples where embeddings returns x-ratelimit-* but chat returns ratelimit-*; reset is seconds in one and RFC3339 timestamp in the other. Attaching three request IDs in description. Marking as P2/Sev2 due to repeat pattern and customer retry logic impact.

41Z — Owen Phillips (Assignee): Confirmed this matches other recent 429 confusion tickets. I can reproduce with curl in us-east using my internal test key. Creating escalation thread in #support and looping Platform/Quota owners. Linking to ENG-4825 once created/confirmed.

05Z — Mina Haddad (Routing): Acknowledged. We currently have multiple header emitters (edge gateway vs service) and some endpoints bypass normalization. Also possible that embeddings and chat hit different limiter dimensions. Will sync with quota enforcement owners and update ENG ticket.

22Z — Ethan Park (Platform): Root cause looks like mixed header standards + 429 classification bug for one path. This should be addressed by ENG-4825; please funnel any additional request IDs that show 429 with remaining>0. Also note: fallback routing can change which edge path serves the request; could explain different header families.

10Z — Owen Phillips (Support): Updated customer: we have an identified server-side inconsistency; workaround is to backoff on any 429 regardless of remaining and rely on Retry-After where present. Requested they confirm if issue impacts both us-east and eu-west (they report both).

37Z — Dylan Brooks (Internal Tools): FYI for Support macros—once ENG-4825 lands we should update the canned response to stop suggesting strict reliance on remaining/reset until we confirm consistency across all endpoints. Suggest linking KB draft about 429/quota mismatch.
05Z — Mina Haddad (Routing): Fix rolled out (see PR redwood/pr-19322). Headers now normalized and include consistent reset semantics. Please retest with prior request patterns.

12Z — Owen Phillips (Support): Retested in prod us-east + eu-west: chat and embeddings now return consistent ratelimit-* headers and 429 aligns with remaining==0 in spot checks. Customer notified and asked to validate in their integration. Marking Resolved; will close after customer confirmation or 3 business days.

title: Procure GitHub Copilot for Business seats, migrate contractors off personal subscriptions, and enable SCIM provisioning
body:
Issue summary:
Request to purchase GitHub Copilot for Business for the Engineering org (approx 85 seats) and to migrate ~20 contractors currently using personal Copilot subscriptions to the company tenant. The migration must include SCIM provisioning (via Okta) so seats are provisioned automatically and deprovisioned when contractors end contracts.

Context/Background:
We have increasing usage of Copilot across platform and product teams. Multiple contractors are on personal paid plans which causes billing leakage and security/asset management gaps. Centralizing on a Copilot for Business license will simplify cost tracking, enable org-level policy enforcement, and allow SSO + SCIM lifecycle management.

Requested deliverables:
- Purchase 85 seats of GitHub Copilot for Business (initial), with an option to add 20 contractor seats on the same billing agreement.
- Enable SCIM provisionin...

title: Docs site: add redirects/vanity URLs for AWS/GCP Marketplace submission + partner validation
body:
Requester: Elliot Price (Docs)
Requested by: Partnerships + PMM for AWS/GCP marketplace submission forms

Context
- AWS Marketplace and GCP Marketplace submission forms require stable, publicly reachable URLs for: product overview, onboarding guide, support, and security documentation.
- Current draft URLs have changed due to IA updates and docs PR review feedback; older links in partner portals are returning 404 or redirecting multiple hops.
- We need a small set of vanity URLs + explicit redirects to the final canonical pages, to avoid delays during partner validation.

Impact
- Partner review blocked on validation of URLs (AWS/GCP reviewers click through).
- GTM risk: listing submission iteration delayed if URLs change again.

Request
1) Add/confirm vanity URLs (short paths) that 301 to the canonical pages.
2) Ensure redirects are single-hop (no chaining), and preserve query string...

- /marketplace/security -> 301 -> /docs/security/marketplace-overview
All requested sources returning 301; no loops observed. Trailing slash normalization confirmed.
Handing final vanity URL list to Partnerships + PMM.
08 (Elliot Price): Confirmed AWS portal validator now passes. GCP reviewer also accepted the security overview URL. Closing.

title: Scheduler queue buildup delays streaming start times (Hosted us-west)
body:
Issue summary:
First token times for streaming are extremely high; queue depth climbing. Some clients retry aggressively, compounding load.

Impact:
Customer-visible latency; timeouts at gateway.

Environment:
prod us-west; hosted tier.

Notes:
Suspected regression in scheduler admission control under high concurrency.

title: EDI-imported supplier invoice lost cost-center attributes on multi-line invoices; posted to default project
body: Issue summary: A supplier invoice submitted via EDI (X12 810) with three invoice lines was posted in the finance ledger with no cost-center/project attributes on any lines; the invoice header defaulted to Project 0000. Impact: misallocation of expenses for two engineering projects (ENG-Alpha, ENG-Beta) and potential incorrect vendor payment amounts due to downstream automated chargebacks. This occurred for one vendor (Sable Components) today after an automated batch run. The invoice was accepted by the EDI ingestion service but the AP posting in the ERP dropped per-line cost-center tags when the invoice contained 3+ lines that reference PO line splits.

comments.body: Workaround prepared: create manual journal to move lines to ENG-Alpha and ENG-Beta after payment, but prefer to correct source to avoid repeat.

title: Duplicate gateway route metric on multi-homed laptops causes asymmetric paths and intermittent failures to internal k8s APIs
body: Issue summary: Multiple employees on macOS laptops that are docked (USB-C dock providing wired Ethernet) are observing intermittent failures when calling internal Kubernetes-backed APIs (gRPC timeouts and 502s from the auth-proxy). Impact: Broad internal impact for office-based employees using the docked wired interface; affects engineers and support staff. Suspected root cause: laptops are receiving a duplicate default gateway via DHCP on both the VPN interface and the dock Ethernet with conflicting route metrics, producing asymmetric paths to our k8s cluster nodes which drop TCP connections from unexpected return paths. Timing: Started 2026-03-10 09:20 PST after a DHCP lease policy change rolled to the branch site DHCP server.

Timing: Started 2026-03-10 09:20 PST after a DHCP lease policy change rolled to the branch site DHCP server.

comments.body: 30: Logged initial report after two engineers raised tickets in #infra. Provided pcap and netstat output.

comments.author: Liam O'Connor () 2026-03-10 10
comments.body: 05: Starting triage. Reproduced on my docked MBP. Marking P1 due to broad office impact.

comments.author: Priya Desai
comments.body: 10: Confirmed DHCP policy roll applied at 09:14; we accidentally set interface metric to 0 for ethernet profiles when we intended to set only route priority for guest vlan. Rolling back patch to branch-dhcp-02 now.

comments.author: Daniel Kim
comments.body: 00: Observed conntrack resets on k8s nodes when source IPs mismatch. As a workaround we can add an iptables rule to ACCEPT related packets from the office subnet for a short window, but that weakens path validation. Prefer fixing DHCP/metrics.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: 20: Documented user workaround: instruct users to unplug dock (fall back to wifi+vpn) or manually set network service order; shared KB draft: 'Docked laptop connection failures to internal APIs'.

comments.author: Liam O'Connor 2026-03-13 09
comments.body: 45: Network rollback to DHCP patch completed on branch-dhcp-02 at 09:30. Asking QA to validate. Need to close after 48h of monitoring if no regressions.

body:

- Temporarily reroute long-context workloads to us-west

Notes:
- This is the internal tracking ticket for the quota escalation and follow-ups.

title: Cross-proxy normalization of idempotency header causing duplicate tool/function executions
body: Customer reports repeated duplicate side-effecting tool/function invocations when using function-calling with retries. The customer is on a dedicated plan behind a corporate reverse proxy that normalizes headers (lowercasing, truncation of long header values, and replacing spaces with hyphens). NimbusAI sees duplicate webhook/tool calls on retry even though they supply stable idempotency keys in the SDK. Hypothesis: the proxy is rewriting/persistently normalizing the Idempotency header in-flight, so Redwood's dedupe layer receives a different header value on the retry path and treats it as a new request.

comments.body: Reproduced in staging with header-normalizing proxy; filing INT-4820 to track gateway change.

comments.body: Confirmed they can switch to shorter keys for now; proxy team prioritized a whitelist check but requested a workaround asap.

title: Tool/function calling intermittently returns invalid JSON in tool_calls.arguments (streaming)
body:
Issue summary:
HelioPay reports their tool/function calling integration is failing because the model sometimes emits non-parseable JSON in `tool_calls[].arguments` when using streaming. Their JSON parser rejects trailing commas and unescaped newlines.

Impact:
Production incidents in HelioPay; tool router throws 500s and requests are retried.

Steps to reproduce (customer):
1) Use /v1/chat.completions with tools enabled and streaming=true
2) Ask for a transaction lookup tool call with a long prompt
3) Observe tool_calls.arguments occasionally contain `"amount": 12,}` (trailing comma) or multiline strings without escapes

Customer evidence (snip):
`tool_calls[0].arguments = {"account_id":"A-1092","start_date":"2025-02-01",}`

Notes:
Customer expects strict JSON per our docs; they are not able to switch to permissive parsing.
