comments.author: Aisha Rahman ()
comments.body: Escalating. Customer says this started after 'Feb 10' runtime update. Looping in @Dev Patel + @Elliot Price. Slack thread: #support (ts 1739293321).

comments.author: Dev Patel ()
comments.body: Confirmed we can reproduce with streaming on and high temperature. Looks like partial chunks are being concatenated incorrectly when `tool_call` spans multiple deltas; sometimes we end up with an extra comma before closing brace.

comments.author: Elliot Price (SRE)
comments.body: Checking deploy timeline. There was a canary of tool-calling serializer changes in us-east 02/10 23:00 UTC.

comments.author: Dev Patel ()
comments.body: Mitigation: disable 'aggressive delta coalescing' flag for tool-call arguments in us-east. Rolling back config now.

comments.author: Aisha Rahman ()
comments.body: Customer confirms improvement after rollback; still saw one malformed payload in the last hour. Asking for ETA on full fix.

comments.author: Dev Patel ()
comments.body: Fix in progress: ensure arguments buffer is validated as JSON at end-of-tool-call and retry generation if invalid. Tracking follow-up in SUP-1860.

comments.author: Aisha Rahman ()
comments.body: Closing as Resolved (workaround applied). Documenting known issue + workaround (strict JSON parsing) in Confluence.

title: Authorization drift: tag-based IAM condition prevents dedicated-provisioner from completing cross-account role exchange in prod us-east
body:
Issue summary:
During an automated Dedicated capacity scale-up (provisioner job) in prod/us-east, the dedicated-provisioner is failing to complete the AWS->GCP cross-account role exchange step. New worker nodes are left in a provisioning loop and cannot register with the scheduling service.

Impact:
- Provisioner failures are blocking dedicated pool scaling for two customer-facing pools (internal monitoring shows ~22% reduced capacity).
- Intermittent customer API throttling for dedicated customers relying on autoscale; no data loss but degraded throughput.

Environment:
- Redwood Dedicated provisioning pipeline (prod:us-east)
- Affected components: dedicated-provisioner (k8s Job), STS assume-role workflow, GCP service account impersonation to read KMS and secret manager artifacts.

Steps to reproduce (observed in prod):
1) Trigger scale-up of dedicated pool via the autoscaler.
2) Pro...

title: Workstation experiencing thermal shutdowns and 0 RPM fan telemetry after BIOS firmware rollout
body:
Issue summary:
Several corporate developer laptops started reporting fan RPM as 0 in telemetry and have experienced thermal throttling or unexpected shutdowns after the March 8th BIOS firmware push. Affected devices are primarily Lenovo ThinkPad fleet that received the v1.2.3 BIOS rollback/upgrade during nightly autopilot/intune maintenance window.

Impact:
- Multiple engineers reported sudden shutdowns while compiling or running local model-serving containers.
- Telemetry shows CPU package temps > 95C and fan RPM 0 prior to shutdown.
- A subset of devices are recoverable by power-cycling; others require manual reseating of fan cable or BIOS re-flash.

Environment:
- corp-laptop | us-west region endpoints registered in Intune
- OS: Windows 11 Enterprise 22H2 with latest cumulative updates
- Endpoint management: Intune + Autopilot + custom vendor BIOS automation job

comments.author: Priya Natarajan
comments.body: Filed after two devs reported sudden shutdowns mid-compile. Captured telemetry and device GUIDs. Escalating to hardware bench.

comments.author: Miguel Alvarez
comments.body: Reproduced on bench. Fan reported 0 RPM after update; reseating fan cable temporarily restores. Suspect EC initialization regression. Collecting vendor logs and device serials for vendor case.

comments.author: Vendor (VEND-7782)
comments.body: Acknowledged regression in v1.2.3 EC init routine. Working on hotfix. Requested sample dumps from affected devices.

comments.author: Telemetry Team
comments.body: Dashboards updated to include 'fan_rpm==0' alert and quarantine rule. Canary set to 20 devices for v1.2.4 rollout.

comments.author: Miguel Alvarez
comments.body: Applied v1.2.4 to 5 bench units; CPU stress test passed; scheduling canary rollout. Will update this ticket after 48h canary window.

comments.author: Priya Natarajan
comments.body: Notified engineering teams via #internal-support and asked impacted engineers to hold off heavy builds until canary clears.

title: Sustained short-window 429s after switching to pooled-proxy signed-url strategy during large multipart embedding import
body:
Issue summary:
Customer LexiData reports steady stream of 429 responses for an otherwise healthy multipart embedding import that started after they switched to a pooled-proxy + short-lived signed URL callback flow. The errors are concentrated during concurrent callback fanout and appear as sustained short-window throttles (soft 429s) rather than a single burst.

Impact:
- Production embedding backfill stalled for multiple jobs, causing downstream search freshness degradation for LexiData customers.
- Errors began ~2026-03-09 18:10 UTC and persist intermittently across the affected import windows.

User-reported context:
- They moved from direct single-host uploads to a pooled proxy (customer-side) that multiplexes callbacks to our signed-url endpoint.
- Each signed URL rotation results in ~50 short-lived callbacks at once.
- Happens only in us-east region and only for multipart embedd...

comments.author: Ava Chen ()
comments.body: Logged issue after customer provided request traces and timing samples. Noticed correlation with NAT pool reuse based on src_ip clusters.

comments.author: Diego Ramos (SRE)
comments.body: Confirmed X-RateLimit-Window values show short (250ms) windows hitting zero. Tentative hypothesis: per-route token accounting plus proxy IP fanout causing double-counting across many callbacks that share src IPs.

comments.author: LexiData (Customer)
comments.body: We can provision a separate IP pool on our side but would prefer guidance on server-side config changes first. Adding metrics and a link to our upload proxy logs: https://logs.lexidata.com/ingest/2026-03-09

comments.author: Diego Ramos (SRE)
comments.body: Deployed temporary route-isolation flag for lexidata signed-url path to reduce per-route coupling. Saw immediate reduction in 429 rate by ~70% in next window. Will keep in place while engineering works on fix.

title: Runner init fails: kubeconfig file mode changed by secrets-sync job, preventing runner startup for on-prem release fleet
body:
Issue summary:
During the nightly secrets reconciliation (secrets-sync) we observed that the kubeconfig used by our self-hosted release runners in the on-prem release cluster had its file mode changed from 0640 to 0600. The runner systemd unit runs as user 'runner' and group 'ci', and relies on group-read to access /etc/runner/kubeconfig. After the mode change runners fail to start, fail health checks, and cannot pick up deployment jobs.

Impact:
- Nightly release jobs for several teams (release/* pipelines) failed to start between 2026-03-10 02:10 and 2026-03-10 03:40 UTC.
- ~22 CI jobs were queued and timed out, delaying a cross-functional canary promotion.
- No user-facing production outages, but release cadence blocked.

Environment:
- prod on-prem release cluster in our us-west colo.
- Runner fleet: self-hosted systemd runners (image: redwood/runner:2026.02.1).
- Secrets pipeline...

comments.author: Ava Chen

comments.author: Priya Nair

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: Added acceptance test to orchestration repo that verifies kubeconfig ownership/mode for runner hosts during CI. PR #3762 references this ticket.

title: Handover: escrowed service account and split device custody for transitioning EM
body:
Issue summary:
HR is asking for a controlled handover for an engineering manager (EM) who is transitioning to a new organization within the company. The EM will retain some advisory responsibilities for six weeks while access to certain production tooling must be escrowed. They will also temporarily split custody of their primary laptop with a peer for knowledge transfer. We need to: 1) move selected service accounts into escrow with restricted unlock process; 2) rotate credentials where necessary; 3) create a documented custody schedule for the physical device; and 4) confirm policy approvals from Security and HR.

Impact:
- Risk of lingering privileged access if not properly escrowed and rotated.
- Potential gap in on-call/monitoring ownership during the transition window.
- Hardware custody ambiguity could lead to missed compliance checks for loaner devices.

Background:
- EM (inte...

title: Resume duplication and partial-final-choice when SSE streams are proxied over HTTP/2 multiplexed connections
body:
Issue summary: Customer reports intermittent duplication of the last streamed segment and a truncated final choice after client-triggered reconnects when SSE is proxied through an HTTP/2 multiplexing path. The client receives a duplicate mid-sentence fragment followed immediately by an end event that omits the last few words.

Impact: Affects real-time UX for Acme's live summarization feature. Customers see duplicated words and cut-off sentences ~1-3% of long (>10k token) streams. Reported in production us-east region and causing apparent hallucination-like artifacts in final outputs (customer counts these as correctness failures).

Environment: prod / us-east behind CDN/edge + ALB -> Envoy ingress -> serving runtime. Observed with SSE (text/event-stream) over HTTP/2 multiplexed connections from edge to internal proxies. Affected models: redwood-14b-v1 (rt-compat) and redwood-7b-v1 in...

normalized.description:
Issue summary: Customer reports intermittent duplication of the last streamed segment and a truncated final choice after client-triggered reconnects when SSE is proxied through an HTTP/2 multiplexing path. The client receives a duplicate mid-sentence fragment followed immediately by an end event that omits the last few words.

Impact: Affects real-time UX for Acme's live summarization feature. Customers see duplicated words and cut-off sentences ~1-3% of long (>10k token) streams. Reported in production us-east region and causing apparent hallucination-like artifacts in final outputs (customer counts these as correctness failures).

Environment: prod / us-east behind CDN/edge + ALB -> Envoy ingress -> serving runtime. Observed with SSE (text/event-stream) over HTTP/2 multiplexed connections from edge to internal proxies. Affected models: redwood-14b-v1 (rt-compat) and redwood-7b-v1 in...

comments.author: Maya Patel ()
comments.body: Acknowledged ticket. Asked customer for HAR, last-event-id, and CDN edge request IDs. Escalated to SRE if traces show stream multiplexing anomalies.

comments.author: Acme Analytics
comments.body: Uploaded HAR and edge IDs. Noted issue frequency ~2 in 100 reconnects on long sessions. Provided reproduction steps and timeline.

comments.author: Jonas Müller (SRE)
comments.body: Found pattern in traces: connection pool reused the same HTTP/2 connection and stream IDs were remapped by edge -> origin translation, there are 503 precondition resets logged immediately prior to reconnects for the affected traces. Collecting more stream-level debug logs from envoy.

comments.author: Riley Ortiz ()
comments.body: Reproduced locally using synthetic HTTP/2 multiplexing test harness. Observed race where buffered SSE event chunk is in-flight in the ALB->envoy stream and server resume handler emits the same event again when the client re-subscribes. Hypothesis: a race between stream buffer flush and resume ACK path leads to duplicate delivery.

comments.author: Maya Patel ()
comments.body: Notified customer of investigation. Recommended temporary mitigation: set client-side dedupe on last 2 SSE events and increase heartbeat to 5s to reduce edge idle preconditions. Customer accepted temporary workaround.

comments.author: Jonas Müller (SRE)
comments.body: Proposed long-term fix: add idempotent resume handshake (server-to-client resume-ack with last-seen-sequence) and tighten stream buffer flush ordering in ingress. Opening infra ticket INT-3399 to roll envoy hotfix.

title: Cross-cloud credential broker policy evaluation fails for ML CI ephemeral creds
body:
Issue summary: When ML CI attempts to obtain ephemeral AWS creds via the cross-cloud credential broker (GCP WIF -> AWS AssumeRoleWithWebIdentity), the broker logs show policy evaluation 'DENY' and AWS STS returns AccessDenied. Impact: staging ML CI jobs that rely on ephemeral AWS credentials fail to start, causing CI flakiness and blocking merges for model infra changes. Environment: gcp-staging (project: redwood-ml-ci-staging), aws-staging account (redwood-ml-staging).

Impact details: ~30% of recent CI runs in the past 6 hours have failed at the pre-bootstrap step (credential provisioning). No customer-facing production impact observed at this time, but developer velocity is impacted and a release candidate is blocked.

Steps to reproduce:
1) From a GKE pod using service account 'ci-runner@redwood-ml-ci-staging.iam.gserviceaccount.com' perform metadata token request to get a GCP wor...

07 Priya Natarajan (security): Confirmed there is an OU-level SCP requiring session tag 'project:ml' for AssumeRole. Security prefers broker to supply tags rather than creating SCP exception.

22 Marcus Chen: Working on PR to add session tag mapping. Linking PR after CI completes.
05 Avery Patel: Noted customer/internal impact summary for oncall handoff; escalating priority to P1 given blocked release candidate.

title: On-demand ordered entitlement-change ledger slice with encrypted manifest and HMAC batch proofs for legal review
body:
Issue summary: Finverity Bank (enterprise) requests a forensically-ordered slice of entitlement-change events for a two-week window (2026-02-22 to 2026-03-07) for legal review. They require per-batch HMAC proofs, an encrypted manifest that lists sequence ranges and checksums, and delivery to their SFTP endpoint with PGP encryption.

Impact: Customer legal team cannot complete subpoena review until they receive verifiable, ordered audit artifacts. This is blocking a regulatory deadline.

Request details: Provide a chronological (event-time ordered) ledger export that includes: subject identifier (pseudonymized per prior agreement), change type (grant/revoke/modify), actor principal, timestamp (UTC), request_id/trace_id, and pre-/post- entitlement state where available. Deliverables must include: 1) NDJSON export slices (max 100k events per file), 2) per-file SHA256 checksum, 3) manifes...

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Logged request from Finverity legal. They've attached redaction mapping and their public PGP key (pgp-0x9A3F...).

comments.author: Marcus Ruiz
comments.body: Investigating exporter behavior; parallel writers causing ordering issues. Will implement single-writer path and estimate runtime. Assigned to engineering support pool.

comments.author: Priya Nair (CS)

comments.author: Ops
comments.body: Delivered encrypted manifest and NDJSON slices to sftp.finverity.example:2222; customer ack'd receipt at 22:47 UTC pending verification. Attached delivery transcript and sftp session log (redacted).

title: Rivermark: streamed completions stop delivering chunks (chunked transfer stalls)
body:
Customer reports that streaming responses intermittently stop sending chunks mid-generation. They see partial output and then no more bytes; client times out at ~75s.

They describe it as: "chunked transfer stalls" / "stream stops sending".

Notes:
- Dedicated cluster in eu-west
- Happens more often during peak hours

Repro (customer):
- /v1/chat/completions stream=true
- Long prompts (>12k tokens) + high concurrency

Cross-refs:
- Slack thread in #support about 'streaming freeze' (timestamp 1770582660)

comments.body: Customer call follow-up. They have evidence at the load balancer showing the upstream connection remains open but no more body bytes.

comments.author: Ethan Park (2026-03-01)
comments.body: Looking for correlation with gateway backpressure and flush intervals. Might be hitting per-conn write buffer limits when token rate drops.

comments.author: Connor O'Brien (2026-03-02)
comments.body: We should confirm whether keepalive comments are enabled on this dedicated environment; fix from SUP-1842 may not be deployed there.

comments.author: Ethan Park (2026-03-04)
comments.body: Found dedicated gateway is one minor release behind. Planning patch rollout + add metric for 'seconds since last stream chunk'.

title: Runner secret-cache livelock during bulk key rewrap blocks release cutover
body:
Issue summary:
During a scheduled bulk KMS rewrap for ephemeral runner credentials, a subset of runners entered a secret-cache livelock where vault-sync repeatedly refreshed secrets but the runner-provisioner refused to mark nodes healthy. This prevented the release cutover job from acquiring the required pool of labeled runners and blocked the production deploy window.

Impact:
- Release cutover delayed for two release pipelines (nightly + hotfix).
- ~18% of ephemeral runner fleet in us-west-2 and 12% in eu-central-1 continuously flapped between REGISTERED and UNHEALTHY, failing job assignment.
- Some long-running jobs were evicted and re-queued, creating billing churn.

Environment:
- prod account (redwood-prod) running 2026-03-10 KMS rewrap job initiated from operations-rotate-key pipeline.
- Runner image v2026.02.1, runner-provisioner v1.14.0, vault-sync v0.9.3.
- Autoscaler polic...

title: After-hours staging room lockout during scheduled desk relocation - batch asset scan incomplete, temporary badge reissue and escorted retrieval required
body: Issue summary: On 2026-03-10 during an after-hours desk relocation, the staging room (Floor 5, West wing) auto-locked while IT was mid-inventory. Impact: 28 devices (laptops, docking stations, and monitors) had incomplete barcode scans; several devices became inaccessible behind the locked door, preventing finish of asset transfer to storage. Location: Building 3 - Floor 5 West - Staging Room B. Steps to reproduce: 1) Schedule desk move outside of core hours. 2) Move assets to Staging Room B. 3) Attempt to finish batch scan when door controller triggers auto-lock due to scheduled lock at 19:00. Expected: Batch scan completes and assets are moved to storage with badges reactivated as needed. Actual: Door locked at 19:01 due to controller clock drift; scanner session terminated and 9 devices remained unscanned and behind locked door. Operational impact: Delayed onboarding for 3 hires, p...

title: Roaming between guest & corp VLANs breaks SCEP certificate enrollment -> VPN/SSO onboarding failures at SF office
body: Issue summary: Multiple employees report that corporate VPN (WireGuard SSO onboarding) fails during initial device provisioning when devices roam between the guest SSID (office-guest) and the corporate SSID (redwood-corp) while the captive portal is active. Impact: ~12 users unable to finish device enrollment and obtain machine/user certs between 08:30 and 11:45 PDT on 2026-03-12; blocks VPN SSO onboarding and access to internal services. Initial triage indicates SCEP enrollment requests are timing out or returning incomplete cert chains. Suspected root cause: HTTP AIA/OCSP retrieval from CA is being blocked by an inline ACL on perimeter firewall when traffic hits the captive-portal redirect path, causing the client to fail to build the certificate chain and abort the EAP/SCEP flow. This appears to be triggered by SSID roam events that change VLAN and transiently route enrollment traf...

title: SCIM PATCH Content-Type discrepancy causes role cache divergence and intermittent 403s for recently deprovisioned admins
body: Customer reports intermittent 403s for users whose admin role was removed in the IdP. The deprovisioning event is visible in the customer's SCIM logs, but Redwood's role cache retains the admin binding for ~30-90s causing a short window of authorization flip (allowed -> denied) depending on which token/session path is used. This is causing automated job failures and confusion during immediate post-deprovision operations.

The deprovisioning event is visible in the customer's SCIM logs, but Redwood's role cache retains the admin binding for ~30-90s causing a short window of authorization flip (allowed -> denied) depending on which token/session path is used. This is causing automated job failures and confusion during immediate post-deprovision operations.

comments.body: Ava Chen: Thanks for the report and logs. We're starting an investigation focused on SCIM intake Content-Type handling and role-cache invalidation. We've escalated to infra.

comments.author: Marco Ruiz
comments.body: Reproduced locally with curl sending Content-Type: application/scim+json; charset=UTF-8. We see the delta parser not selected due to strict header match. Working on a patch to normalize Content-Type parsing and trigger immediate cache invalidation for affected user entries.
Communicated workaround to customer: request IdP team to send full-replace (PUT) for role removals, or temporarily add 2-minute drain window to critical jobs. Customer accepted workaround.
Created PR to make Content-Type matching tolerant and add idempotent cacheInvalidator.notifyUserChange() on delta path. Running internal tests and will roll to canary tomorrow.

title: Provisioning stall after depot repair: kernel module signature enforcement blocks corporate NIC driver and prevents management check-in
body: Issue summary: Several Dell Latitude 7440 units returned from depot repair fail to fully provision after the post-repair image. The corporate NIC kernel module (corp-nic.ko) is present on disk but is not loaded due to kernel module signature enforcement. Because the signed driver is rejected, the system falls back to a generic NIC, which receives a DHCP address on an isolated vlan and cannot reach SCCM/Intune, causing provisioning/check-in to stall. Impact: devices remain in 'staged' state, cannot retrieve device policies or complete post-image configuration, delaying reprovisioning and increasing depot turnover time.

comments.author: Samir Patel
comments.body: Confirmed kernel rejects corp-nic due to signature. Reproduced locally on staging image. Adding imaging and security owners.

comments.author: Depot Lead
comments.body: Noted vendor board swap flow includes a BIOS flash that reinitializes UEFI keys. We will capture flash logs for affected units.

comments.author: Maya Gupta
comments.body: Enrolled new signing key in internal MOK test harness; need to align signing process and update runbook — advisories incoming.

comments.author: Samir Patel
comments.body: Temporary instructions created for depot techs to MOK-enroll after repair; preparing permanent fix to re-sign modules with vendor-trusted key and update image signing step.

title: JIT env bootstrap fails when AWS STS session policy exceeds account enforced length; ephemeral SA not provisioned
body:
Issue summary: Automatic just-in-time (JIT) environment bootstrap jobs that create ephemeral AWS credentials for ML debugging fail intermittently in prod-us-east. The provisioner attempts to attach a generated STS session policy (inline session policy) that enumerates allowed S3 prefixes and KMS key ARNs. When the generated policy exceeds ~2048 characters, AWS STS call returns AccessDenied or MalformedPolicy depending on account policy enforcement, and the ephemeral service account creation aborts.

Impact: Developer oncall and data-platform jobs cannot spin up ephemeral sandboxes; several debugging and oncall plays blocked since 2026-03-09 22:00 UTC. Affects ~12 recent bootstrap attempts across two tenant projects. No user data exfiltration observed; primarily availability impact.

Environment: prod account (redwood-prod-ml), region us-east-1, provisioner v1.12.4, sts-provisioner lam...

comments.author: Olivia Chen () 2026-03-10T09
comments.body: 12: Created ticket after multiple oncall reports. Captured batch of failed bootstrap IDs and initial logs.

comments.author: Marcus Patel (SRE) 2026-03-10T10
comments.body: 20: Investigating. Running repro in staging with same provisioner binary. Initial findings point to policy length. Will try a fallback that collapses resource lists into prefix patterns.

comments.author: Aisha Rahman () 2026-03-10T11
comments.body: 02: Noted that a recent org hardening change introduced a Service Control Policy that rejects certain inline policy characteristics in sessions. Coordinating with cloud infra to confirm.

comments.author: Marcus Patel (SRE) 2026-03-11T14
comments.body: 45: Temporary mitigation deployed to prod: provisioner now detects >=1200 char sessionPolicy and instead issues AssumeRole without sessionPolicy plus a short-lived managed role mapping with narrowly scoped permissions. This reduces blast radius while restoring bootstrap success. Monitoring metrics show successful bootstrap rate increased from 63% -> 98% over the last 6 hours.

title: Microburst smoothing credit omission after tag-normalization change caused missing invoice credit
body: Issue summary: Customer reports that a $3,120 smoothing credit (hourly buffer credit) that usually offsets microburst reprocessing was not applied on the finalized invoice for 2026-02-28. Impact: VocalIQ sees an unexpected invoice line of $3,120; customer is on Dedicated capacity and expects smoothing credits for transient microburst replays. Environment: prod-us-east, dedicated cluster vocaliq-ded-1. They executed a scheduled reprocess batch (microbatch job) between 02:10-02:26 UTC that retried ~18k requests. Expected: hourly smoothing buffer credit appears as a negative line item and offsets reprocess charges. Observed: no smoothing credit; charges appear at full rate and no provisional credit was applied. The customer is requesting an explanation and provisional credit while we investigate.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Received initial report from VocalIQ billing contact (maria@vocaliq.ai). Customer provided invoice id INV-2026-02-28-5421 and sample request IDs. Escalating to billing engineering. Assigned to Diego.

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: I pulled reconciler logs and saw a batch of postbacks that were consumed but normalized tag values changed; normalization produced 'vocaliq_replay' (underscore) while smoothing rule looks for 'vocaliq-replay' (dash). Hypothesis: recent tag-normalization change introduced a character substitution for '-' -> '_'.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Notified customer we are investigating and requested the full postback payloads for 02:10-02:26 UTC. Customer provided gzipped postback archive and sample HMAC headers.

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: Confirmed normalization PR #482 (merged 2026-02-27) introduced a unicode normalization step that collapsed non-ASCII dash characters into underscore for legacy clients. This affected rule matching. Opening fix PR to add alias matching and backfill path.

comments.author: Liam Patel
comments.body: Requested estimated provisional credit amount for customer while backfill runs. Support asked Eng for estimate.

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: Backfill job queued; estimated credit to grant: $3,120. Patch to normalization-service deployed to canary (postback-service v1.14.2) and passed smoke tests. Planning to run backfill during low traffic window 2026-03-06 03:00 UTC.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Informed VocalIQ that provisional credit $3,120 will be applied to account as a temporary invoice adjustment; final reconciliation after backfill will confirm. Customer acknowledged and requested follow-up post-backfill.

title: Hosted API: streaming sends a few chunks then goes silent until client timeout
body: Customer says streaming begins normally (first 2-5 data: lines) then no more events. Connection appears open; their client eventually times out. Happens only on one model in eu-west. They do not see 5xx.

comments.author: (Lauren)
comments.body: Asked if they are behind Cloudflare / enterprise proxy; customer says yes (CF).

comments.author: (Alex)
comments.body: Checked runtime logs: generation completed; gateway did not flush final bytes to client for 3 affected request_ids.
Hypothesis: buffering at intermediate proxy due to small chunks + delayed flush; suggested setting 'stream_options: {include_usage:true}' to increase chunk frequency and adding keep-alive pings.
Waiting for customer to confirm whether keep-alives resolve.

title: incident-bot: Slack incident create modal missing required incident_type/subtype fields for some workspaces/users
body:
Issue summary:
- incident-bot is expected to require incident_type + subtype selection in the Slack “Create Incident” modal (taxonomy v1 rollout).
- Multiple on-call users report the incident create modal is missing the incident_type/subtype dropdown blocks entirely.
- Impact: users cannot correctly classify incidents at creation time; in some cases the bot later blocks closure due to missing classification/owner, creating friction during live incidents.

Impact:
- Affects incident creation workflow for a subset of workspaces and/or users (reports indicate it’s not universal).
- Risk: classification compliance metric drops + delayed response due to manual follow-ups.

Observed behavior:
- Modal shows: Title, Severity, Customer tier, Region, Description; but no incident_type/subtype blocks.
- Bot still posts follow-up message referencing “incident_type required” on /incident close in s...

comments.body:
14 PT — Dylan Brooks (reporter):
Getting multiple pings that the incident create modal is missing incident_type/subtype. Can we confirm if the taxonomy modal blocks are behind a workspace flag? This is causing on-call to create incidents unclassified and then chase fields later.

Examples:
- #incidents: user reports no dropdown
- #eng-sre: second confirmation

Priority request: hotfix or mitigation by end of day; taxonomy is supposed to go “required” next week.

28 PT — Rafael Mendes:
Confirming this is happening in at least one active incident channel. We should treat as P1 since it undermines the new classification policy + closure gating.

Mitigation option: if bot can still accept /incident set-type after creation, we can direct on-call to do that, but we need the modal fixed ASAP.

03 PT — Devon Price (assignee):
Investigating. Modal blocks come from the “modal schema v3” endpoint. We do have a phased rollout by workspace_id (added during PR-812).

Early findings:
- For workspaces not in the allowlist, bot serves the old schema (no taxonomy blocks).
- However, closure gating was enabled globally in config yesterday (intended to be rolled out later), so workspaces can get blocked at close without having the fields at create.

Next steps:
1) Identify which workspace_ids are not in allowlist but have gating enabled.
2) Either expand allowlist or disable gating for non-allowlisted workspaces.
3) Add guardrail: don’t enfo...

21 PT — Allison Grant:
+1 on guardrail. Also ensure we don’t increase label cardinality by having “unknown” explode due to this bug. We should push affected incidents into a single “unclassified” bucket until fixed (and backfill later).

07 PT — Devon Price:
Root cause:
- Config drift between two flags:
 - modal_schema_version (workspace allowlist)
 - enforce_required_taxonomy_fields (global)
- A config change yesterday flipped enforce_required_taxonomy_fields globally, but modal_schema_version remained allowlisted.

Mitigation applied:
- Scoped enforce_required_taxonomy_fields back to the same allowlist as modal_schema_version.
- Verified with two users: taxonomy fields now present in modal for allowlisted workspace; non-allowlisted workspace no longer blocked at close.

Hotfix plan:
- PR to add server-side invariant: “required taxonomy enforcement implies taxonomy blocks...

42 PT — Dylan Brooks:
Thanks. Can we get an ETA on the invariant PR? We should avoid a recurrence as we expand rollout to additional workspaces next week.

26 PT — Devon Price:
ETA today. I’ll also add an admin command to print the resolved config for a workspace (modal schema version + enforcement flags) to reduce future ambiguity for on-call.

18 PT — Devon Price:
Fix deployed.
- Added guardrail: if taxonomy fields are required, incident-bot will refuse to serve an old modal schema and will fall back to the v3 schema (or disable enforcement with an explicit warning + audit log entry).
- Added metric + log: incident_bot.modal_taxonomy_fields_present (boolean) tagged by workspace_id (bounded) for rollout monitoring.

Verified:
- Reproduced in affected workspace before change.
- Post-deploy, modal shows incident_type/subtype and owner resolution consistently.

Follow-up:
- Recommend SRE keep enforcement scoped during phased rollout; do not flip global enforcement until modal schema...

27 PT — Rafael Mendes:
Closing. We’ll include this as a rollout lesson learned in taxonomy tooling integration checklist (ensure config invariants; staged enforcement).

title: Binary attachment base64 normalization causes idempotency mismatch for function calls between Python and Go SDKs
body: Customer reports that function/tool calls which include small binary attachments (images encoded as base64) are producing duplicate side-effects when requests are retried. The idempotency key validation on the server side appears to treat payloads encoded by the Python SDK differently than the Go SDK, causing retries from one SDK to be considered distinct requests and leading to repeated downstream webhook invocations.

normalized.description: Customer reports that function/tool calls which include small binary attachments (images encoded as base64) are producing duplicate side-effects when requests are retried. The idempotency key validation on the server side appears to treat payloads encoded by the Python SDK differently than the Go SDK, causing retries from one SDK to be considered distinct requests and leading to repeated downstream webhook invocations.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Logged issue from PineLoop AI support case. They provided request/response samples and webhook traces. Marking as P1 due to production duplicates.

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: Reproduced locally with customer sample. Confirm cross-sdk encoding difference. Filed short repro script and added to linked PR. Requesting SRE input on memory safety for server-side decode-based normalization.

comments.author: Priya Singh (SRE)
comments.body: Server-side decode-for-signature increases peak mem for large attachments; recommend limiting to attachments < 1MB for auto-normalization and fail-safe fallback to hashing raw body for larger payloads. Will add observability for heap on canary.

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: Opened PR for a server-side canonicalization feature flag and unit tests: https://github.com/redwood/server/pull/2134. Added SDK guidance to canonicalize base64 on the client as short-term mitigation.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Shared workaround to customer and confirmed they implemented client-side strip-CRLF patch; customer reported no duplicates in a 24h validation window. Keeping ticket open until canary completes and server-side fix is deployed.

title: Office AP power-save / DHCP rapid-renew causes WireGuard handshake to stall and internal service blackholes
body: Multiple employees reported intermittent loss of internal services and failed WireGuard handshakes when switching between personal hotspot and corporate Wi‑Fi. Symptoms include long pauses during ssh/kshell connect, stalled LLM inference calls from internal tooling, and zero traffic observed through the WireGuard peer despite tunnel remaining logically up.

title: Machine cert CRL/OCSP fetch failure causes VPN tunnel collapse and loss of internal DNS suffixes
body:
Issue summary: After a scheduled corporate machine certificate rotation, a subset of macOS and Windows hosts fail to establish the zero-trust VPN agent. Impact: Affected hosts lose corporate DNS suffix resolution (internal.*), causing service access failures (k8s API, internal dashboards, git over https). Scope: ~120 hosts in us-east over the last 24h. Pattern: failures cluster around clients that were asleep or on flaky Wi-Fi during cert push.

Observed behavior: Zero-trust agent shows cert validation errors; system logs show CRL/OCSP fetch timeouts; VPN client repeatedly tears down and rebinds network interfaces which clears DNS suffixes. Expected behavior: Agent should accept new machine certs or gracefully retry CRL/OCSP without dropping tunnel/DNS on transient fetch failures.

Customer/Business impact: Internal developer productivity affected; CI jobs hitting internal artifact re...

comments.author: Maya Patel
comments.body: 12]: Reproduced with OCSP block. Marking P1. Adding Liam to investigate agent behavior and DHCP interaction.

40]: Captured packet dump from affected host and verified repeated HTTP GETs to OCSP responder 10.11.5.3, then vpn-client issues SIGHUP to network stack. Dump uploaded to S3: s3://redwood-support/INT-221403/ocsp-pcap.pcap

comments.author: Ravi Singh
comments.body: 22]: Confirmed agent 2.7.1 will mark cert as invalid after 2 timeouts and triggers 'rekey-fail' path. 2.8.0-rc defers teardown for up to 5 retries. Need rollback plan or hotfix to increase retry/backoff for 2.7.x.

comments.author: Jocelyn Park
comments.body: 03]: Observed DHCP client on Windows machines resets 'DNS Suffix Search List' when vpn interface is removed; behaviour differs across OS versions. Suggest gating suffix persistence to VPN service if possible.

45]: Working on mitigations: (1) push 2.8.0-rc to Canary pool (50 hosts), (2) add whitelist to firewall to allow OCSP responder from Wi-Fi captive networks, (3) patch DHCP hook to avoid wiping search domains during brief vpn interface down.

title: Add usage UI copy + KB link re: retries billed as separate attempts; streaming cancellation token accounting
body:
Background:
- From SUP-1842 (Northwind AI escalation). Customer confusion around attempt-level accounting.

Requested changes:
1) Usage page: add small helper text near request_id + export: "request_id represents a single attempt. Retries appear as separate attempts and are billed separately."
2) Add link to KB article: usage-accounting-retries-and-stream-cancellation
3) Optional follow-up: add docs snippet under API retry guidance (out of scope for this ticket).

Acceptance criteria:
- Copy visible in Hosted API usage dashboard
- Link to Confluence page included
- Approved by CS + Finance

References:
- SUP-1842
- Slack #eng-platform thread_ts=1739386202

title: Wi-Fi EAP-TLS certificate rotation fails after imaging, blocking SCCM/Intune policy application
body: Issue summary: After new-image provisioning via SCCM/Autopilot + Intune, fleet devices intermittently fail to connect to corporate Wi-Fi. Impact: users cannot reach internal services until manual remediation; affects small percentage of newly imaged devices in US-east. Root cause suspected during certificate rotation flow: machine certificate is not present in the local machine store at time Wi‑Fi profile with EAP-TLS is applied, causing profile application to fail and network location services to stall.

title: QUIC path migration during mobile handoff truncates streaming SSE final fragment via CDN
body: Customer NoteWave Mobile reports intermittent truncated final sentences and missing terminal metadata on long-lived SSE streams when a mobile device transitions between cellular and Wi-Fi. Issue only observed when requests route through our CDN with HTTP/3 enabled. The stream appears to resume but the final 'choice' object and terminal metadata are not delivered to the client, leaving responses incomplete.

title: Go SDK: pointer-omitempty JSON divergence causes idempotency key mismatch for function calls
body:
Issue summary:
Customers upgrading of a Go microservice to the latest go-sdk are seeing duplicate external tool executions after retries. The duplicates occur only when the initial request is routed from a Go client and the retry originates from a Python/TS orchestrator (or vice-versa).

Impact:
- Inkline AI reported 3 duplicate downstream webhook executions over a 24h window resulting in duplicate invoices being created in their billing system.
- Occurs intermittently on high-concurrency request paths where retries/backoff trigger cross-language replay.

Environment:
- Affected customer: Inkline AI (dedicated)
- Region: us-east (prod)
- SDKs observed: go-sdk v0.9.3 -> v0.9.5 (regression), python-sdk 1.12.0, ts-sdk 2.5.1
- Endpoint: /v1/chat/function_call with JSON-encoded arguments that include optional fields (e.g., metadata, callback_url)

Issue summary:
Customers upgrading of a Go microservice to the latest go-sdk are seeing duplicate external tool executions after retries. The duplicates occur only when the initial request is routed from a Go client and the retry originates from a Python/TS orchestrator (or vice-versa).

Impact:
- Inkline AI reported 3 duplicate downstream webhook executions over a 24h window resulting in duplicate invoices being created in their billing system.
- Occurs intermittently on high-concurrency request paths where retries/backoff trigger cross-language replay.

Environment:
- Affected customer: Inkline AI (dedicated)
- Region: us-east (prod)
- SDKs observed: go-sdk v0.9.3 -> v0.9.5 (regression), python-sdk 1.12.0, ts-sdk 2.5.1
- Endpoint: /v1/chat/function_call with JSON-encoded arguments that include optional fields (e.g., metadata, callback_url)

title: Northwind AI reports usage token totals higher than expected (streaming + retries)
body:
Issue summary:
- Customer reports Redwood Usage dashboard + invoice preview shows ~18-25% more tokens than their internal estimates for same traffic.

Impact:
- Finance reconciliation blocked; escalation risk.

Context:
- Customer uses streaming. Client timeout 15s and retries once on timeout.
- Customer assumption: timed-out attempt should not be billed if they did not receive full response.

Initial hypothesis:
- Usage is counted per admitted attempt (retries are separate).
- Prompt tokens counted once admitted even if client cancels stream.
- Need to confirm there is no double-counting of completion tokens on partial streams.

Requested from customer:
- Sample request IDs + timeframe
- Retry/cancel rate metrics

Links:
- Slack: #support thread_ts=1739310605
- Slack: #eng-platform thread_ts=1739386202
- Gmail thread: 20250212-northwind-usage-token-count-mismatch-escalation (Vanessa ...

comments.author: Ben Carter
comments.body: Created after escalation from CS. Assigning to Dev (eng-platform) to validate token accounting for streaming cancels + retry behavior.

comments.author: Dev Patel
comments.body: Pulled 3 sample request_ids. completion tokens match streamed chunks; prompt tokens counted once per admitted attempt. No evidence of double counting. Main gap is comms/UI.

comments.author: Priya Natarajan
comments.body: Please provide customer-facing blurb for (a) attempt-level accounting and (b) prompt tokens counted even if stream cancelled.

comments.author: Dev Patel
comments.body: Plan: (1) publish KB entry, (2) add UI hint on Usage page clarifying request_id = attempt id; retries billed separately, (3) optional: expose retry grouping in export later. Tracking UI work in INT-330.

title: Intermittent path MTU blackhole with 464XLAT/CLAT causing VPN tunnel TLS stalls to internal k8s API
body:
Issue summary: Several on-prem and remote employees intermittently lose connectivity to internal Kubernetes control-plane endpoints (k8s API) over the corporate VPN. Symptoms include HTTPS stalls (TLS connection hung after ClientHello/ServerHello), kubectl timeouts, and SSO failures when Okta redirects attempt to hit cluster endpoints.

Impact: Affects ~8% of remote users on IPv6-only mobile hotspots and some office users routed through a branch edge running CLAT/464XLAT for IPv6-only carrier links. Breaks developer workflows (kubectl, code pushes) and automated jobs that depend on k8s API. No evidence of a serving platform outage; problem correlates with path MTU size and IPv6-to-IPv4 translation path.

Environment: Production, corporate VPN (WireGuard-based branch/host tunnels), edge NAT64/464XLAT devices at two branch sites (boston-edge, seattle-edge). Affected clients: macOS 14 an...

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Created ticket after first customer-facing report from devs in #support. Initial triage indicates MTU-related failure on IPv6-only clients.

comments.author: Diego Martinez
comments.body: Assigned for deeper network trace. Will capture pcap at edge and client. Asked for list of affected users and common carriers.

comments.author: NetOps
comments.body: Captured pcap on boston-edge; saw PTB generated by internal router but not delivered upstream to carrier. Suspect CLAT box rewriting ICMP or carrier filtering.

comments.author: Diego Martinez
comments.body: Lab reproduction succeeded with vendor CLAT appliance. Created GitHub issue for edge NAT64 behavior and PR to add ICMP passthrough rules for WireGuard interface.

comments.author: SRE
comments.body: Noted that enabling 'df' clear on client allowed fragmentation by WireGuard and restored traffic; that's a temporary mitigation if client-side change possible.

comments.author: Diego Martinez
comments.body: Engaging carrier support for IPv6 CLAT behavior; filed support ticket with vendor for CLAT appliance to confirm ICMPv6 handling and PTB translation semantics.
Reviewed workaround and confirmed no policy violation to temporarily enable fragmentation for affected support users.

comments.author: Diego Martinez
comments.body: Proposed multi-pronged mitigation: 1) Add WireGuard postrouting rules to translate/forward ICMPv6 PTB into ICMPv4 PTB, 2) Short-term client MTU profile of 1280 pushed to mobile dev group via MDM, 3) Carrier vendor escalation.

title: UK BACS payment rejected: beneficiary address missing on vendor bank record causing payment hold
body:
Issue summary:
A scheduled UK BACS payment to vendor 'Northbridge Components Ltd' was rejected by the bank with return code RM124 (beneficiary address missing). Payment was submitted on 2026-03-09 as part of the weekly AP run and entered 'Rejected by bank' status in the payments ledger.

Impact:
- Vendor invoice for PO PO-99877 (net £40,000) plus VAT remains unpaid; vendor has flagged potential shipment hold if not cleared within 3 business days.
- Affects one high-value supplier on monthly cadence.
- Treasury team unable to reprocess until beneficiary details are updated in vendor master and bank resubmission performed.

Environment:
- Payments system: Payments Portal v3.4 (production)
- Bank channel: UK BACS via Banking Partner (Clearline Payments)
- Region: eu-west-2 (London)

Steps to reproduce / observed sequence:
1) AP run triggered 2026-03-09 02:00 UTC for UK suppliers.
2) Paym...

title: OpenAI-compat streaming ends early (missing [DONE]) - multiple customers incl. OrchidCRM + BluePeak
body:
Issue summary
OrchidCRM reports streaming responses ending early on OpenAI-compatible API (`/v1/chat/completions`, `stream=true`). They receive HTTP 200 and partial tokens, then the connection closes without `[DONE]`. Similar pattern reported by AsterixPay (SUP-2481).

Impact
Customer is on Dedicated but calling OpenAI-compat route via hosted gateway. Incidents appear concentrated in eu-west.

Customer details
- OrchidCRM request ids: req_7c2bXXXX, req_aa19XXXX (from Slack #support thread 1739316123)
- Client: OpenAI Python SDK

Additional reports
- BluePeak Analytics emailed Support with request ids req_f3a8XXXX and req_c11dXXXX (see Aisha mailbox thread 20250213-bluepeak-streaming-ending-early).

Notes
Please coordinate comms so customers get a consistent workaround: retry only when `[DONE]` missing; consider idempotency key where available.

Next steps
- Confirm if OpenAI-compat st...

title: Streaming stops after first chunk on /v1/chat/completions (Globex)
body:
Issue summary: Customer reports that when stream=true, they receive the first SSE event and then nothing else. Their code keeps waiting; they never see [DONE].

Impact: Their chat UI shows 19 characters then stops; retry produces a second assistant message.

Environment: Dedicated cluster in eu-west; customer uses redwood-sdk-python 0.9.1 with OpenAI-compatible API.

Notes: Non-streaming works. Streaming works from curl but not from their Python process behind corporate proxy.

Impact: Their chat UI shows 19 characters then stops; retry produces a second assistant message.

Notes: Non-streaming works. Streaming works from curl but not from their Python process behind corporate proxy.

comments.author: Avery Johnson ()
comments.body: Customer says only first chunk then hang. Asking for minimal repro.

comments.author: Dev Patel (SDK)
comments.body: Looking at python SDK: we buffer until newline boundaries; if proxy coalesces chunks weirdly, it may appear 'stuck'. But they say curl works so might be their proxy + keepalive.

comments.author: Alex Martinez (Platform)
comments.body: Our SSE events sometimes go quiet while backend is generating. Heartbeat should help. Is eu-west already on the heartbeat build?

comments.author: Dev Patel (SDK)
comments.body: Suggested workaround: set `REDWOOD_STREAM_READ_TIMEOUT=120` and ensure requests uses `stream=True` w/ iter_lines(decode_unicode=True).

comments.author: Avery Johnson ()
comments.body: After enabling heartbeat build in eu-west and customer proxy exception, issue not reproducible.

title: TS SDK: AbortController cancel + immediate retry loses tool-callback confirmations when using delegated token rotation
body:
Issue summary:
Customer using the TypeScript SDK observes missing tool/callback confirmations after cancelling a streaming generation with AbortController and then immediately retrying. Behaviour appears correlated with delegated API key rotation in their auth stack: the retried request uses a refreshed delegated token and the previously-sent confirmation callback for the first attempt is never reconciled, leading to partial downstream side-effects (duplicate back-office jobs or missing confirmation receipts).

Impact:
- Intermittent data-loss/duplicate-side-effect symptoms in customer workflow (invoice submissions are sometimes missing confirmations, occasionally duplicated).
- Affects a subset of streams where client cancels then retries within ~2s window; estimated ~0.8% of request flow for this customer but hits critical payment paths.

Environment:
- Customer tier: Dedicated pool...

comments.body: 21 UTC - Support (Ava Chen): Customer reports intermittent missing confirmation callbacks when rapid cancel+retry. Provided timing samples and minimal repro harness. Asked for SDK/edge network logs.

02 UTC - Eng (Diego Martinez): Reproduced locally with a mock sidecar that rotates Authorization header between retries. Observed server-side that callback confirmation arrives but is rejected by orchestrator when incoming request metadata (delegated-key-id header) differs from stored call context. Hypothesis: idempotency scope uses delegated-key as part of dedupe key; rotation prevents dedupe reconciliation.

30 UTC - Customer (NimbleDocs - S. Patel): Confirmed edge rotates tokens; shared minimal trace IDs: trace-2026-03-13-1149. Provided webhook logs showing missing ack for attempt A. Asked if SDK should include an invariant per-request idempotency key that is independent of delegated delegation headers.

10 UTC - Eng (Diego Martinez): Added test coverage to sdk-ts to preserve user-provided Idempotency-Key header across retries and to not include delegated token info in dedupe key. Minimal repro demonstrates confirmations are matched when idempotency key is preserved. Raised PR in sdk-ts: https://github.com/redwood-inference/sdk-ts/pull/412 (WIP).

05 UTC - Support (Ava Chen): Shared suggested workaround with customer: set a stable per-request idempotency header in client, and configure edge to forward that header unchanged across token rotations. Customer testing scheduled for 2026-03-14 14:00 UTC.

title: Interoffice locker exchange and hotdesk provisioning for hybrid engineer relocating between LON and DUB
body:
Issue summary: New hire (Niamh O'Sullivan) on Product org is transitioning from the London office to a hybrid schedule with regular days in Dublin. Employee has company laptop, docking station, and monitor locked in a concierge locker in LON. She requests a secure locker pickup in London, sanitization of peripherals, and courier delivery to Dublin office followed by hotdesk allocation and temporary badge provisioning for first-week access.

Impact: Employee will be remote-first next week and needs hardware in DUB by 2026-03-18 to attend onboarding and cross-team syncs. Delay will impact onboarding schedule and productivity.

Location: London HQ (King's Cross site) locker #C17; destination Dublin office (3rd floor bench area).

Items to move: 1x MacBook Pro (company asset tag RWI-4762), 1x Thunderbolt dock, 1x 27in monitor (VESA mount), 1x cable pouch. Employee confirms no external dri...

title: token accounting discrepancy when using cached prefix + tool calls
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports token accounting discrepancy: billed prompt_tokens appear to include cached prefix tokens even when cache hit rate is high. This is specifically for chat with tool calls enabled.

Impact:
Customer procurement needs explanation for overage charge vs expected savings from prefix caching.

Details:
- They use prompt caching (prefix cache) and send large system prompts
- They see cache hits in headers (x-rw-cache: hit)
- Yet billable prompt_tokens remains similar to cold requests

Requested outcome:
Clarify billing rules for cached tokens and tool-call message serialization.

Requested outcome:
Clarify billing rules for cached tokens and tool-call message serialization.

comments.author: Chloe Martin ()
comments.body: Customer keeps repeating 'token accounting discrepancy' and is escalating internally. They have screenshots of cache hit headers + unchanged billed tokens.

comments.author: Nikhil Sharma ()
comments.body: We should not bill cached tokens for prompt side, but we DO bill tool-call serialization tokens if present. Need to reproduce with their schema.

comments.author: Applied ML
comments.body: Tool call JSON adds tokens (function name, arguments). If they compare against 'original prompt' they might ignore tool messages appended server-side.

comments.author: Nikhil Sharma ()
comments.body: Found a bug: our billing pipeline counts the pre-cache prompt length, not the post-cache effective tokens, for requests with tool calls + cache hit. Fix in progress behind feature flag billing_cache_fix_v1.

comments.author: Chloe Martin ()
comments.body: Asked customer to switch off tool calling for a subset to validate. Also shared temporary credit while fix rolls out.

title: Billing/Usage spike — customer suspects double-count after increased retries on streaming
body:
Customer reports a sudden increase in billed usage (tokens + request count) starting ~2026-02-10 21:00 UTC. Customer suspects double-counting because they did not intentionally increase traffic.

Impact
- Customer reports ~2.1x increase in daily token usage compared to prior 7-day average.
- Concern about invoice / budget thresholds; asking if Redwood is double-counting streaming requests.

Customer context
- Company: Aurora Search (Hosted API / self_serve)
- Workload: chat completions with streaming enabled; TS SDK
- Recent changes (per customer): upgraded SDK + added more aggressive retry logic due to intermittent timeouts.

Initial intake (Support)
Requested:
- Workspace/org ID
- Example request IDs from before/after spike (minimum 10 each)
- Endpoint(s): /v1/chat/completions vs /v1/completions; streaming on/off
- SDK version + any custom retry settings (max_retries, timeout, backo...

Customer reports a sudden increase in billed usage (tokens + request count) starting ~2026-02-10 21:00 UTC. Customer suspects double-counting because they did not intentionally increase traffic.

Impact
- Customer reports ~2.1x increase in daily token usage compared to prior 7-day average.
- Concern about invoice / budget thresholds; asking if Redwood is double-counting streaming requests.

Customer context
- Company: Aurora Search (Hosted API / self_serve)
- Workload: chat completions with streaming enabled; TS SDK
- Recent changes (per customer): upgraded SDK + added more aggressive retry logic due to intermittent timeouts.

Initial intake (Support)
Requested:
- Workspace/org ID
- Example request IDs from before/after spike (minimum 10 each)
- Endpoint(s): /v1/chat/completions vs /v1/completions; streaming on/off
- SDK version + any custom retry settings (max_retries, timeout, backo...

comments.body: 22Z (Dev Patel): Opened per Aurora Search report of billing usage spike. Asked for org ID + sample request IDs + SDK version + retry settings + whether streaming is enabled. Set priority P2 pending confirmation of impact magnitude.

05Z (Customer - Aurora Search): Confirmed streaming enabled for most traffic. Recently bumped retries from 2 -> 6 and lowered client timeout to 20s due to occasional slow responses. Provided 15 request IDs from 2026-02-10/11 and notes that many requests appear to “restart” after a timeout.

40Z (Priya Shah): Internal usage dashboard review: request count increased ~1.9x; average prompt size stable; completion tokens up proportional to requests. No sign of metering backfill/lag. Error rate modestly elevated around 22:00–23:00 UTC with higher stream disconnects (matches pattern from other streaming tickets).
10Z (Priya Shah): Spot-checked 10 provided request IDs in metering logs: exactly 1 billable event per request ID; token totals within expected ranges. No evidence of double-count per request_id.

05Z (Priya Shah -> Internal note): Hypothesis: client retries on streaming disconnect/timeout are generating *new* requests and re-consuming tokens. Customer likely perceives double-count because the user sees “one” interaction but the client made multiple generation attempts.

20Z (Priya Shah): Shared customer-safe guidance: treat stream disconnects as retriable but ensure backoff + cap retries; increase client timeout; avoid immediate replay loops; log request IDs to correlate retries. Linked streaming troubleshooting/workaround doc and asked whether they can reduce retries temporarily to validate impact on usage.
12Z (Customer - Aurora Search): Reduced retries to 2 and increased timeout to 60s. Usage spike started to normalize within a few hours. Still asking if Redwood can add a way to dedupe retries.

15Z (Priya Shah): Handoff note to Platform/Billing hooks owner: while no duplication found, request for product improvement—idempotency/dedup guidance for streaming retries + better request-id logging in SDK. Looping in Dylan Brooks for internal tracking and to route to the right owner.

31Z (Dylan Brooks): Acknowledged. No current metering duplication issue identified. Suggested adding a Support macro + docs link emphasizing idempotency/retry guidance; also noted TS SDK PR for request ID logging helper is in review (pr-488).

08Z (Priya Shah): Customer update: confirmed billing reflects actual successful generations; spikes aligned with increased retry attempts. Recommended: keep retries low (<=2–3), exponential backoff + jitter, prefer longer timeouts for streaming, log request IDs, and avoid replaying the same user action without an idempotency strategy.

40Z (Priya Shah): Closing as resolved—no evidence of double-counting; usage increase attributed to client retry behavior during streaming timeouts/disconnects. Added internal follow-up: ensure macros link to streaming troubleshooting and request-id logging helper once merged.

title: Python SDK returns 401 after API key rotation (works via curl)
body: Issue summary: Customer rotated their Redwood API key and started receiving 401 Unauthorized when using redwood-sdk-python v0.9.3. Same key works via curl and via the Hosted API playground. Impact: production traffic blocked for one service. Environment: Python 3.11, requests transport, running on AWS ECS. Steps: (1) set REDWOOD_API_KEY env var to the new key (2) run their existing integration (chat.completions) (3) observe 401. Notes: customer suspects the SDK is trimming or transforming the key. Request ID provided in attachment.

normalized.description: Issue summary: Customer rotated their Redwood API key and started receiving 401 Unauthorized when using redwood-sdk-python v0.9.3. Same key works via curl and via the Hosted API playground. Impact: production traffic blocked for one service. Environment: Python 3.11, requests transport, running on AWS ECS. Steps: (1) set REDWOOD_API_KEY env var to the new key (2) run their existing integration (chat.completions) (3) observe 401. Notes: customer suspects the SDK is trimming or transforming the key. Request ID provided in attachment.

comments.author: Aisha Rahman ()
comments.body: Confirmed 401s in logs. Ingestion shows Authorization header present but value differs from expected length; looks like trailing whitespace/newline?

comments.author: Ethan Park (DevEx)
comments.body:
Could be env var parsing; some CI systems inject a newline. We should strip only "
" and surrounding whitespace carefully, but not touch internal chars.

comments.author: Customer (Kiteframe)
comments.body: We store the key in AWS Secrets Manager and inject as env var. We noticed the secret value ends with a newline in their console export.

comments.author: Ethan Park
comments.body:
Repro with key ending in "
"; current SDK uses .strip() which removes more than newline in some cases (incl. leading spaces). Proposed fix: rstrip of "
" only and add validation warning.

comments.author: Aisha Rahman
comments.body: Workaround provided: export key without newline and restart tasks.

comments.author: Ethan Park
comments.body: Fix merged in PR in redwood-sdk-python; released as v0.9.4. Customer confirmed resolved.

title: Python SDK sends 'Bearer Bearer' in Authorization header for some configs
body: Issue summary: Customer upgraded to redwood-sdk-python v0.9.4 and began receiving 401 Unauthorized. Their proxy logs show Authorization header is 'Bearer Bearer <key>' (double prefix). They previously passed api_key='Bearer <key>' (historical pattern from other vendors). Impact: auth failures in one service until rollback. Steps: (1) Initialize client with api_key that already includes 'Bearer ' (2) SDK adds Bearer prefix again (3) API rejects. Expected: SDK should accept raw key only OR detect and avoid double prefix and warn.

normalized.description: Issue summary: Customer upgraded to redwood-sdk-python v0.9.4 and began receiving 401 Unauthorized. Their proxy logs show Authorization header is 'Bearer Bearer <key>' (double prefix). They previously passed api_key='Bearer <key>' (historical pattern from other vendors). Impact: auth failures in one service until rollback. Steps: (1) Initialize client with api_key that already includes 'Bearer ' (2) SDK adds Bearer prefix again (3) API rejects. Expected: SDK should accept raw key only OR detect and avoid double prefix and warn.

comments.author: Ava Chen ()
comments.body: Customer insists they have always stored the value with the prefix; they missed this requirement in docs.

comments.author: Ethan Park (DevEx)
comments.body: This is technically misconfiguration, but we should add a guardrail: if api_key starts with 'Bearer ' then strip the prefix and log a warning.

comments.author: Ava Chen
comments.body: Provided workaround: store raw key only (no prefix).

comments.author: Ethan Park
comments.body: Marking as closed with docs + validation improvement tracked separately (INT-771).

title: Go SDK: streaming chat reader blocks indefinitely when server closes connection
body: Customer reports Go SDK streaming chat occasionally never returns from Next() after the server has closed the TCP connection. They suspect the SDK does not notice EOF promptly. Observed on go1.22, redwood-sdk-go v0.9.1. Impact: goroutines leak and request cancellation fails.

normalized.description: Customer reports Go SDK streaming chat occasionally never returns from Next() after the server has closed the TCP connection. They suspect the SDK does not notice EOF promptly. Observed on go1.22, redwood-sdk-go v0.9.1. Impact: goroutines leak and request cancellation fails.

comments.author: (Ben)
comments.body:
Gathered goroutine dump showing blocked read on bufio.Reader.ReadString('
').

comments.author: (Hiro)
comments.body: Root cause: we wrap the response body in a scanner with too-large token and ignore scanner.Err(); also not wiring context cancellation to body close.

comments.author: (Hiro)
comments.body: Fix: use bufio.Reader with explicit context-aware Close, check errors, and return terminal error on EOF. Released v0.9.2.

comments.author: (Ben)
comments.body: Customer confirms no more goroutine leaks after upgrade.

title: Ambiguous legal-entity on consolidated vendor invoice causing tax allocation hold
body:
Issue summary: A consolidated invoice (Vendor: GlobalCloud Services) was uploaded to the AP pipeline on 2026-03-02. The PDF contains charge lines attributable to two Redwood legal entities (Redwood Inference, Inc. - US and Redwood Inference Ltd. - UK) but the supplier did not provide entity-level purchase order (PO) mapping or VAT breakdown. The invoice was flagged by tax-validation as 'ambiguous legal entity' and moved to a manual hold queue.

Impact: Payment for the invoice (approx. $142,300 USD / £84,600 GBP equivalent) is blocked for both legal entities. Vendor has emailed requesting confirmation of payment date. This affects month-end close for the UK entity and may induce late-payment fees. Finance needs a deterministic allocation before releases to avoid misposting to tax and GL accounts.

Background: Vendor regularly issues consolidated monthlies that include UK professional s...

comments.author: Priya Rao (Tax)
comments.body: Confirmed hold. Cannot accept heuristic allocation for VAT reporting. Need supplier-signed split or individual invoices per legal entity. Escalated to Vendor Ops to request corrected docs.

comments.body: Vendor responded; attached 'statement' file but still no legal-entity mapping. Vendor claims contract references 'Redwood' without legal entity differentiation. Asked procurement to confirm PO routing rules. (see linked_conversation #vendor-ops)

comments.author: AP Lead
comments.body: Ran manual allocation worksheet and proposed interim GL treatment: post to suspense tax clearing account and schedule payment authorization pending supplier evidence to avoid further late fees. Awaiting sign-off from Accounting Manager.

comments.author: Elena Martinez
comments.body: Approved interim suspense posting for partial payment up to 60% of USD portion to reduce late penalties, provided vendor signs a payment agreement. Payment team to prepare a one-time JV to move amounts when final allocation confirmed.

title: Office printer island - intermittent print queue failures and network timeouts
body:
Issue summary: Several staff on floor 3 (printer island A/B/C) report intermittent failures when submitting print jobs. Jobs appear in the client queue but never reach the printer; sometimes jobs error with a network timeout or 'printer not found'.

Impact: Low-medium operational impact; employees are using floor printers for contracts and physical sign-offs. Reported across 6 users over the last 48 hours; not a full outage but recurring and disruptive.

Environment: On-prem office network, wired VLAN 12 (office-devices), printers are HP LaserJet managed via local print server (windows-printsvc-03). Office DHCP and printer IPs appear stable. Printers are assigned static reservations.

Steps to reproduce:
1. From a macOS or Windows client on VLAN 12, submit a 20-page print job to Printer-Island-A.
2. Observe that the job appears in the local client queue.
3. Wait 30-120s; some jobs rem...

comments.body: Creating ticket after multiple reports from floor 3. Attached a couple of user screenshots. Requesting IT triage.

comments.author: Miguel Santos
comments.body: Picked up the ticket. I was able to reproduce with a 40-page PDF; spooler shows the job but drops connection to the device. Collecting spooler and SNMP logs. Planning a brief spooler restart tonight at 02:00 PT if acceptable.

comments.author: NetOps (Liam O'Connor)
comments.body: Observed CRC errors on switch-shelf-3B port 12 at 10:24 on 2025-11-11. Port flapped twice, correlated with one reported failure. Will enable granular port logging and monitor for 72h.

comments.author: Miguel Santos
comments.body: After spooler restart and switch logging, error rate reduced. Still seeing a couple of delayed jobs for large PDFs. Suspect spooler memory limits and large-job timeouts. Considering patching print driver and increasing spooler timeout config.

comments.author: Miguel Santos
comments.body: Follow-up — upgraded printer firmware on Island B and adjusted spooler timeout setting. Monitoring window shows no new user reports in last 2 weeks. Marking as RFC and keeping ticket open for final closure after end of quarter review.

title: NVMe disk signature reorder after depot repair causes Intune/assetdb mismatch and Autopilot hostname collision
body: Issue summary: After depot repair (NVMe replacement or reflow) some devices boot with the OS disk signature and partition GUIDs reordered. This triggers our assetdb/device fingerprinting logic to report a different Device ID back to Intune/Autopilot. Impact: devices appear as duplicate assets, Autopilot hostnames collide (hostname-1, hostname-1-1), and deployment policies targeted by device ID do not apply. Scope observed: ~7 devices from the same repair batch over the last two weeks. Not observed in fresh builds from imaging lab.

This triggers our assetdb/device fingerprinting logic to report a different Device ID back to Intune/Autopilot. Impact: devices appear as duplicate assets, Autopilot hostnames collide (hostname-1, hostname-1-1), and deployment policies targeted by device ID do not apply. Scope observed: ~7 devices from the same repair batch over the last two weeks. Not observed in fresh builds from imaging lab.

comments.author: Priya Shah
comments.body: Filed after three reports from depot last week; batch ID DPT-2026-03-10. Noticed asset tag matched an existing record but DiskSignature differed.

comments.author: Mateo Alvarez
comments.body: I pulled two affected devices into bench, captured disk signatures and partition GUIDs. See attached export in Bitbucket: repo/repairs/INT-892345/ (private). Initial hypothesis: NVMe vendor firmware write during reflow resets/reset UUID fields.

comments.author: Noah Kim
comments.body: Checked assetdb ingestion pipeline. No recent changes; ingestion keys use TPM UUID + disk signature fallback. In affected samples TPM reports are present but disk signature mismatch triggers fallback path.

comments.author: Aisha Patel
comments.body: Re-imaged an affected unit with the same image, device normalized and registered correctly. Suggests issue occurs only post-repair before depot image re-flash. Potential hardware/firmware ordering artifact.

comments.author: Mateo Alvarez
comments.body: Requested depot to run vendor NVMe secure-erase workflow rather than reflow; holding further repairs until we confirm.

comments.body: Creating INT-332 to track the Slack workflow updates discussed in the playbook refresh. comments.author: Dylan Brooks
comments.body: For INT-333 I’m adding Jira required-field enforcement and SLA reminders. If you can ensure the workflow collects SUP key + region + priority, I can mirror those as Jira required fields at triage for consistency.

comments.author: Benji Okafor
comments.body: Prototype built in staging workspace: shortcut posts template + pins template. Still need SUP key prompt → Jira link + pin. Also added links to severity matrix + intake checklist. Need approval from Support Ops on final wording before enabling in prod.

comments.author: Elliot Price
comments.body: Please keep the template customer-safe and avoid default prompts that encourage copying logs into Slack. Suggest adding: “Share only request IDs and timestamps; attach logs in Jira if needed.” I’ll review the final message block.

comments.body: Implemented SUP key prompt and Jira link pinning. Next step is permissions + restricting the shortcut to Support/CS user groups. Pending: final copy review from Dev + Rafael, then enable in prod and post announcement.

title: Streaming chat response stalls (SSE) after ~10–20 seconds for AcmeCo
body:
Issue summary: Customer reports chat streaming responses sometimes stop mid-generation; client continues waiting and the HTTP connection stays open.

Impact: User-facing chat feature appears frozen; retries cause duplicate requests.

Environment: Hosted API; OpenAI-compatible endpoint; SSE streaming enabled.

Steps to reproduce (customer): 1) POST /v1/chat/completions with stream=true 2) Receive a few data: chunks 3) Stream stops; no [DONE]; request does not complete.

Initial notes: Seems correlated with longer generations (>400 output tokens) and mobile networks.

Logs excerpt (customer paraphrase): "got first chunks then nothing for ~60s".

comments.body: Looking at api-gateway logs. I see periodic idle > 30s on the upstream stream when backend is still generating. Might be proxy buffering + heartbeat missing.

comments.author: Ethan Park (Platform)
comments.body:

title: Ephemeral CI runner initContainer crashes due to Vault token refresh race during secret rewrap
body:
Issue summary: Several ephemeral CI runners failed to finish job startup during the secrets rewrap event performed by Secrets Team (2026-03-10 01:13 UTC). The runner initContainer that fetches repo credentials from Vault exits with a 401/permission denied error and the pod moves to CrashLoopBackOff.
Impact: A subset (~8%) of ephemeral runners in prod-us-west failed to complete job bootstrapping for ~45 minutes, blocking CI jobs across multiple repos for internal teams. No user-facing product outages, but CI capacity degraded and release pipelines were delayed.
Environment: prod cluster (us-west), runner autoscaling group: ephemeral-runner-*-pool, Vault path: secret/ci/repos/*, Vault cluster: vault-prod-02.
Observed pattern: Failures correlated with a scheduled Vault rewrap (transit rekey) that rotated wrapping keys and reissued some service tokens. Affected pods show initContainer fai...

comments.author: Priya Desai (SRE)
comments.body: Confirmed >50 runner restarts in 30m window on prod-us-west. Pushed alert to #sre and #ci-ops.

comments.author: Secrets Team
comments.body: Vault rewrap completed at 2026-03-10T01:13:30Z. We saw a short period where token accessors were being reissued; investigating whether our rewrap clients invalidated accessors too eagerly.

comments.author: Marco Ruiz ()
comments.body: Reproduced locally by running a small script that revokes token accessor while initContainer is running; confirmed initContainer exits on 401 without retry. Opened short-lived PR to add exponential backoff retry in init container (see related_github_prs).

comments.author: Ava Chen ()
comments.body: Prod mitigation applied: added a 2m grace window to the rewrap orchestration and scheduled a rolling restart of ephemeral-runner nodes in the affected pool; failure rate dropped to <0.2% in next run.

title: Streaming responses hang mid-completion for LatticeFlow (SSE stalls)
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports streamed chat completions intermittently stop delivering events mid-response. Connection stays open (no error), but no more tokens arrive.

Impact:
Production traffic impacted for LatticeFlow. They have a 60s client-side watchdog and end-users see partial answers.

Environment:
Hosted API, SSE streaming, region us-east.

Steps to reproduce (customer):
1) POST /v1/chat/completions with stream=true
2) Works for ~10-25s then stops emitting data
3) Client waits until watchdog fires

Notes:
Customer wording varies: "hangs", "freezes", "stalls".

Customer examples (redacted):
- request_id: req_9e2c...
- approx time: 2026-02-14 18:12 UTC

Links:
- Gmail thread in Ava Chen mailbox: 20260214-latticeflow-streaming-hangs.json

comments.author: Ava Chen (2026-02-14)
comments.body: Escalation from LatticeFlow. They say the stream freezes mid-response, no error, socket appears open. Added request_id/time in description.

comments.author: Connor O'Brien (2026-02-14)
comments.body: Looking at edge proxy + gateway timeouts. Seeing a pattern: long gaps between chunks when backend stalls, proxy may buffer and then stop flushing.

comments.author: Ethan Park (2026-02-15)
comments.body: Found several requests where upstream (serving) finished, but gateway never flushed final event. Hypothesis: SSE writer stuck when client uses HTTP/2 via certain intermediaries.

comments.author: Connor O'Brien (2026-02-15)
comments.body: Mitigation: reduced gateway write-buffering for SSE route + added periodic keepalive comment event every 10s. Rolling to us-east first.

comments.author: Ava Chen (2026-02-16)
comments.body: Customer confirmed improvement after keepalive change. Closing as Resolved; monitoring for 48h.

title: Add SDK guardrail: detect 'Bearer ' prefix in api_key and warn across SDKs
body: Background: Support saw multiple customer-reported auth issues due to key/header formatting across SDKs (SUP-4821, SUP-4884, SUP-4902, SUP-4933). Proposal: add a shared validation behavior across SDKs: (1) if api_key value begins with 'Bearer ' strip prefix and log warning (2) if trailing CR/LF exists, trim and warn (3) if other whitespace exists, error with actionable message. Also update docs and support playbook.

Also update docs and support playbook.

title: ScribeHQ: streaming responses sometimes end early (partial output, no error)
body:
Customer reports streaming outputs occasionally terminate early with no explicit error. They receive an apparently clean end-of-stream but the assistant text is truncated relative to expected completion length.

Initial signal came via email thread in Ben Carter mailbox (20260303-scribehq-stream-ends-early.json).

Customer details:
- Region: us-east
- Client uses HTTP/2
- Provided HAR capture (har-capture-sse.json) and request_id req_c41a...

Hypotheses:
- upstream connection reset or GOAWAY causing client to treat stream as complete
- gateway incorrectly emitting [DONE] on backend cancellation

Asks to customer:
- raw response headers
- confirm whether they ever see incomplete JSON/event framing
- confirm whether they are using any proxy (e.g., Cloudflare)
