1) Send 300 concurr...

comments.body: 22 UTC - Aisha Khan (Support): Escalated to SRE after initial triage. Customer reports repeatable window; providing console links and sample traffic script.

03 UTC - Diego Morales (SRE): Confirmed burst reproduction in internal testbed using customer's traffic script. Observed allocation lead times ~95-120s and per-GPU kv_cache skew. Opening deeper investigation with serving-runtime and autoscaler owners.

40 UTC - Priya Patel (Serving Runtime): Found that short-streams keep per-stream KV cache pinned until stream termination and our slot packing logic delays eviction. This leads to many smallholders that block efficient batch packing and cause slot fragmentation.

15 UTC - Diego Morales (SRE): Temporarily applied runtime config tweak for BrightNote route to aggressively cap per-stream KV cache retention to 2s after stream end. Immediately observed improved slot reuse and throughput recovery in customer pool.

05 UTC - Evelyn Zhang (Autoscaler): Cloud provider reported transient vGPU placement latency due to rack maintenance; autoscaler retry backoff contributed to >60s additional lead time. Working on parallelizing placement and reducing backoff for dedicated pools.
30 UTC - Aisha Khan (Support): Customer confirmed latency and timeouts stopped after the temporary runtime change; happy with workaround. Requested timeline for permanent fix.

45 UTC - Diego Morales (SRE): Marking as resolved after merged fixes: hotfix to cap KV retention per-stream and autoscaler PR to parallelize placement requests. Follow-ups scheduled: regression test and runbook update assigned to INT-3382.

title: Misaligned queue depth thresholds cause autoscaler slow-to-react and transient GPU shortages for dedicated customer
body:
Issue summary:
Customer BrightLedger reported recurring mid-morning throughput degradation in their dedicated pool that manifests as sudden p50/p95 increases and intermittent 504s for short-lived embedding calls while a subset of long-lived search streams remain healthy. Impact first seen 2026-03-10 08:45 PDT.

Impact:
- Customer experiencing ~2-3x p95 latency spikes for embedding endpoint and occasional 504s for short requests across several nodes in their reserved pool.
- Business impact: data pipeline backpressure causing delayed analytics ingestion for BrightLedger’s ETL window.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool (customer-managed capacity) in us-west-2
- Pool configured with autoscaler thresholds: queue_depth_avg > 12 triggers scale-up, queue_depth_p95 > 8 is logged but not used to trigger.
- Workloads: mix of long-lived streaming search sessions (low CPU, sustained GPU KV cache) and...

Ask: Provide concrete code snippets and a drop-in example showing Vercel AI SDK + Redwood Hosted API to power (1) site-search (embeddings + reranking) and (2) a conversational help widget.

Requirements (customer language / paraphrase):
- "Want a drop-in example: Vercel AI SDK + Redwood Hosted API to power search/chat."
- Workloads: primary = site-search (embeddings + reranking), secondary = chat assistance for checkout flow.
- Latency targets: search p95 100–300ms for vector lookup; chat first-token <300ms acceptable (streaming).
- Throughput: pilot ~10–20 QPS peak, updated expectation discussed as ~15–30 QPS peak with growth to ~50 QPS in 6 months.

title: Provision restricted internal admin access for admission-control debug endpoint (/internal/debug/admission)
body:
Request from SRE/On-call to provision restricted access to the new admission decision debug endpoint introduced in PR #27477.

Background:
- During overload events and/or unexpected 429 spikes, SRE needs a deterministic way to answer: “why was this request admitted/throttled/shed?” without granting broad admin access to routing/policy systems.
- Endpoint is intended for internal use only and is protected behind gateway authn/authz.

Requested capability:
- Allow a small set of on-call engineers (SRE + Routing on-call) to access:
 - GET /internal/debug/admission (and any subpaths documented in PR)
 - In prod regions (us-east, eu-west) from approved corporate networks / on-call tooling.

Security + audit requirements (must-haves):
1) Least-privilege RBAC
 - Create a dedicated role: "admission_debug_reader" (read-only; no mutation endpoints).
 - Scope only to the specific debug endpoint ...

1) Least-privilege RBAC
 - Create a dedicated role: "admission_debug_reader" (read-only; no mutation endpoints).
 - Scope only to the specific debug endpoint ...

comments.body: 14 PT — Peter Holtz (Reporter): Creating this as internal helpdesk since we need formal audit + access controls before we ask on-call to use the new endpoint in prod. Requesting least-privilege group-based access, no customer-key access. See eng-platform Slack thread 1763004555 for context.

02 PT — Kaitlyn Nguyen (Assignee): Acknowledged. I’ll coordinate with Security/Compliance expectations for audit fields + retention. Need confirmation from routing team on exact endpoint paths + whether it sits behind gateway or direct service ingress in prod.

18 PT — Elena Popov: Endpoint is routed via API gateway, path prefix is /internal/debug/admission and /internal/debug/admission/decision. Intended read-only. PR includes a config flag to disable entirely; prod will be on allowlist only.

40 PT — Kaitlyn Nguyen: Proposed controls:
- Okta group: "okta:redwood:sre-admission-debug"
- Internal RBAC role: admission_debug_reader
- Gateway policy: require SSO token w/ group claim AND mTLS from corp network.
- Add dedicated rate limit (e.g., 20 rpm per user) to avoid noisy loops during incident.
Confirming with SRE that this is sufficient.
05 PT — Rafael Mendes: Looks good. Please ensure audit logs include the evaluated policy decision id + request_id so we can stitch with routing logs. Also need a documented Splunk query for on-call.

22 PT — Kaitlyn Nguyen: Implemented in staging:
- Okta group created (pending approvals for initial members)
- RBAC role created and mapped
- Gateway authz policy updated
- Audit logging fields added: actor_email, okta_user_id, src_ip, region, path, http_status, request_id, decision_log_id, tenant_id (if present), and redaction applied for prompt fields.
Sharing Splunk query once prod logs land.

50 PT — Owen Phillips: Confirming Support escalation needs access as part of incident coordination. We will not use this for routine customer questions; only when 429 reason code explanations aren’t sufficient.

10 PT — Kaitlyn Nguyen: Prod rollout complete (us-east, eu-west). Verified:
- Authorized request succeeds (200)
- Unauthorized user receives 403
- Both attempts emit audit events to Splunk
Splunk query (runbook snippet): index=redwood_audit sourcetype=admission_debug actor_email=<email> earliest=-24h
Retention set to 90 days per standard.
Rate limit enforced at gateway.
26 PT — Peter Holtz: Confirmed on-call was able to use endpoint during a best-effort shedding spike to validate why requests were denied. Audit records present. Closing.

title: Cascade fallback to quantized variants caused high tail latency for NimbleDocs dedicated pool — customer escalation
body:
Issue summary:
On 2026-03-10 between 11:42-12:05 UTC NimbleDocs observed high 95/99th percentile generation latency (spikes from ~200ms to up to 3.8s) and intermittent streaming disconnects for chat endpoints. Customer reported increased user-facing errors and slow responses during a high-traffic campaign window. Customer traffic is on a Dedicated pool in us-west and eu-central with routing policy that prefers full-precision variants and falls back to quantized variants when capacity-constrained.

Impact:
- Customer-facing chat UX degraded; several sessions timed out and streaming sessions disconnected (estimated 8% of active sessions during window).
- Billing and SLA risk for a paying Dedicated customer.
- Customer opened an escalation to Support and threatened reduced traffic to Redwood if not remediated.

Timeline (condensed):
- 11:40 UTC: NimbleDocs spike begins (campaign). Autosc...

title: Safely disenroll tenant from aggressive optimizer profile and verify baseline restore
body:
Issue summary:
Customer BrightPost Health reports a rapid increase in tail latency (95p/99p) and intermittent 5xxs after enrolling a subset of their tenants into an aggressive optimizer profile (prefetch + async batching + mixed-precision quantization). They request a safe, tenant-scoped unenrollment from the profile and validation that traffic returns to the previous baseline without adverse side-effects to other tenants.

They request a safe, tenant-scoped unenrollment from the profile and validation that traffic returns to the previous baseline without adverse side-effects to other tenants.

comments.author: Maya Chen ()
comments.body: Customer is nervous about any global rollback; insisted on tenant-only approach.

comments.author: Liam O'Rourke (SRE)
comments.body: Will run staged unenroll now; will update logs and proceed to 50% only if 10% step looks stable.

comments.author: Runtime
comments.body: Noted that mixed-precision kernels were changed in PR 8721; keep an eye on kernel-level prefetch misses.

comments.author: BrightPost Ops (Customer)
comments.body: Please proceed with staged unenroll. We will stand by to verify user-facing performance.

comments.author: Liam O'Rourke
comments.body: 2026-03-10 10:40 - 10% split completed. p95 reduced by 22%, 5xx dropped to 1.1%. Continuing to 50%.

comments.author: Liam O'Rourke
comments.body: 2026-03-10 10:55 - 50% split completed. p95 within 8% of baseline, 5xx 0.6%. Proceeding to 100% and close monitoring.

title: Conservative safety-liner for optimizer toggles causing intermittent 5xxs and truncated responses
body:
Issue summary:
Customer enabled a new optimizer profile (aggressive batching + prefix prefetch + q4 quant) for their Dedicated pool. Within 30 minutes they reported intermittent 500/502 responses and truncated generations (token loss) for a subset of routes. Impact: user-facing failures and increased tail latency for chat features.

Impact:
- ~2.5% request error rate spike for client routes 'messages.create' and 'chat.stream' (customer reporting production impact).
- Some generations stop mid-stream (token loss) requiring client retry.
- Customer is on Dedicated capacity with reserved GPU pool (LumenHealth prod pool).

Environment:
- Dedicated GPUs (4x A10g) in us-east-1
- Runtime: r-inference-3.8.1
- Optimizer profile: "aggressive-multi-batch-prefetch-q4" (created 2026-03-10 09:04 UTC)
- Affected model variants: redwood/gptx-13b-q4_0 (primary), fallback redwood/gptx-13b-fp16

Steps t...

Steps t...

comments.body: 42 UTC - Asha Patel (Support): Thanks for the report, we see the spike on your tenant. Collecting runtime logs and creating an optimizer snapshot now. Will propose low-risk options.
05 UTC - Diego Ramos (SRE): I see KV evictions correlated with increases in batch coalescing. Memory per-worker hit 86% on one node. Standing by to coordinate a partial traffic shift.
12 UTC - LumenHealth (Customer): We need to restore production by EOD. Prefer minimal changes that avoid full revert if safe.

30 UTC - Asha Patel (Support): Proposed plan: 1) snapshot profile, 2) switch 10% traffic to fp16 fallback model, 3) reduce batching target to baseline for impacted routes, 4) disable prefix prefetch per-route if errors persist. Requesting approval to run steps 1-3 now.
36 UTC - LumenHealth (Customer): Approved. Please proceed with stepwise approach and notify before each step.
02 UTC - Diego Ramos (SRE): Implemented snapshot and opened traffic-split at 10% to fp16 fallback. Monitoring metrics. Initial error rate decreased to 0.8% (from 2.5%).

22 UTC - Asha Patel (Support): Reduced batching target from 128 to 48 for tenant routes; kv-prefetch remains enabled. Error rate dropped further to 0.2% and truncated responses stopped for 95% of flows.
15 UTC - LumenHealth (Customer): Thanks, system is usable. Request a formal recovery checklist and permanent mitigation recommendations.

10 UTC - Diego Ramos (SRE): Follow-up: we plan a controlled rollout of a safer optimizer profile that keeps q4 but reduces prefetch window and enforces lower batch caps. Scheduling change window with customer on 2026-03-17.
40 UTC - Asha Patel (Support): Documenting final guidance and post-mitigation validation steps in ticket.

title: Throughput collapse during scheduled QA spike — asymmetric routing caused GPU stripe contention
body:
Issue summary: BrightDesk reports a sustained drop in end-to-end throughput for their reserved pool during a scheduled QA job window on 2026-03-13. The pool experienced a p50 throughput collapse (~35% drop) and p99 latency spikes. Impact: customer-facing inference pipelines falling behind SLA-backed throughput commitments; retries and client timeouts observed.

Observed window: 2026-03-13 09:40—10:25 UTC (customer local: 05:40—06:25).

Initial hypothesis: a routing imbalance after a lightweight router release caused uneven distribution of short-burst requests to a subset of GPUs, creating persistent stripe contention and kernel queue serialization on those devices. This resulted in effective GPU starvation for other tenants on the pool and triggered tight autoscaler oscillation attempts, which failed to rebalance quickly enough due to min-capacity constraints.

normalized.description:
Issue summary: BrightDesk reports a sustained drop in end-to-end throughput for their reserved pool during a scheduled QA job window on 2026-03-13. The pool experienced a p50 throughput collapse (~35% drop) and p99 latency spikes. Impact: customer-facing inference pipelines falling behind SLA-backed throughput commitments; retries and client timeouts observed.

Observed window: 2026-03-13 09:40—10:25 UTC (customer local: 05:40—06:25).

Initial hypothesis: a routing imbalance after a lightweight router release caused uneven distribution of short-burst requests to a subset of GPUs, creating persistent stripe contention and kernel queue serialization on those devices. This resulted in effective GPU starvation for other tenants on the pool and triggered tight autoscaler oscillation attempts, which failed to rebalance quickly enough due to min-capacity constraints.

comments.author: BrightDesk (customer)
comments.body: We're seeing a backlog in our QA runs; several requests are timing out. Please advise. -- Julia K.

comments.author: Asha Mehta
comments.body: Thanks Julia. We're investigating. Initial telemetry shows routing skew. We've opened an engineering investigation and will update in 30 mins.

comments.author: Diego Alvarez ()
comments.body: Rolled back router hash_fn to v1 for us-east pool. Watching metrics now. If throughput recovers we should consider a targeted patch for the hash function and add unit tests that exercise short-stream key distributions.

comments.author: Asha Mehta
comments.body: After rollback we observed p50 throughput recover ~28% within 3 minutes; p99 still elevated on the hot GPUs. Working with Eng to drain and repro cleanup.

comments.author: Router
comments.body: Root cause confirmed: subtle bias in new hash_fn for keys with low entropy. PR created to add per-pool salt and better distribution tests (github.com/redwood/router/pull/1234).

comments.author: Diego Alvarez ()
comments.body: Merged router PR and deployed canary to us-east; added autoscaler guard to temporarily bump min_capacity during high short-stream ratios. Need to run postmortem and update runbook.

comments.author: Asha Mehta ()
comments.body: Customer notified of rollback and ongoing mitigation. Suggested follow-ups: add skew alerting to Console and include routing-hash version in pool metadata.

title: PayStream: inference shard stall cascade causing streaming timeouts and routing loops (major customer escalation)
body: Issue summary: Starting 2026-03-10 19:02 UTC, PayStream experienced degraded streaming responses and intermittent 5xx errors. Customers reported long stalls (10-30s) during streaming completions followed by client disconnects. The user's SDK observed repeated reconnects and routed requests repeatedly to the same serving shard, triggering a routing loop that amplified the outage for several minutes. Impact: Broad streaming timeouts for a high-throughput dedicated customer; measurable SLO breach for streaming latency and error rate. Environment: Dedicated GPU pool (paystream-dc-us-east-1), autoscaled scheduler, routing policy pinned to low-latency shards. Timeline: 19:02 UTC first alerts on increased streaming tail latency; 19:07 escalation to SEV0 when customer reported production failures; mitigation and rollback actions executed by 20:05 UTC; service returned to steady-state by 20:40...

comments.author: Aisha Patel ()
comments.body: Customer reported production streaming failures. Raising to SEV0. Initial ask: immediate mitigation and ETA for fix.

comments.author: Priya Rao
comments.body: Observability shows shard-17 inflight tokens spike and repeated stalls. Moving traffic off shard-17 now.

comments.author: Ops - Martin Kline
comments.body: Confirmed that traffic is routing to a different shard; partial recovery observed but some requests still timeout intermittently.

comments.author: Marco Chen (Runtime)
comments.body: Found CUDA soft ECC corrections in NVML logs for node-gpu-05. Rebooting node. Applying temporary scheduler ballast to reduce reassign churn.

comments.author: Elena Morales (Solutions)
comments.body: Sent SDK retry guidance to PayStream; recommended toggling client retry to exponential backoff while we complete infra remediation.

comments.author: Ben Thompson (CS)
comments.body: Follow-up call scheduled with PayStream execs; preparing timeline and impact numbers for credit discussion.

comments.author: Marco Chen (Runtime)
comments.body: Deployed scheduler rebalancer hotfix in canary then prod; monitoring for regressions. No recurrence observed in 48h.

comments.author: Aisha Patel ()
comments.body: Ticket moved to Resolved after post-incident follow-ups completed and customer sign-off received.

title: Guided stepwise deactivation and safety-net for optimizer changes (batching / cache / quant profiles)
body:
Issue summary:
Nimbus Health reported a 3x tail latency increase and elevated 5xx rate after we applied an optimizer bundle (quant profile + aggressive batching + prefix caching changes) to their Dedicated pool in us-east. They requested guided rollback steps that preserve traffic while avoiding cold-start spikes and token-cost regressions.

Impact:
- ~15% of their live user requests saw end-to-end latency >2s (baseline ~600ms).
- Observed application-level 5xx errors (upstream timeouts) for streaming endpoints.
- Customer cannot pause traffic due to SLA requirements and needs a safe, staged deactivation.

Environment:
- Dedicated capacity: d-pool-nimbus-us-east
- Serving runtime: rwd-serve v3.7.2 (custom build)
- Optimizer bundle: quant-profile: qprofile-alpha-2026-03, batching-window: 50ms -> 10ms, kv-cache-priority: enabled (prefetch on cold start)
- Affected model: gptx-13b-quant-...

- Observed application-level 5xx errors (upstream timeouts) for streaming endpoints.
- Customer cannot pause traffic due to SLA requirements and needs a safe, staged deactivation.

Environment:
- Dedicated capacity: d-pool-nimbus-us-east
- Serving runtime: rwd-serve v3.7.2 (custom build)
- Optimizer bundle: quant-profile: qprofile-alpha-2026-03, batching-window: 50ms -> 10ms, kv-cache-priority: enabled (prefetch on cold start)
- Affected model: gptx-13b-quant-...

comments.author: Aisha Patel
comments.body: Customer raised via support form. Provided initial triage questions and requested runbook for safe rollback. Asked for allocation of on-call infra engineer.

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: Assigned. Noted that dedicated pool autoscaler logs show short-lived GPU reboots around the time of revert attempts. Requested full console rollout history and the exact optimizer flags used.

comments.author: Elena Martinez
comments.body: Provided rollout ID, optimizer bundle name, and confirmation that they pinned gptx-13b-quant-v2. Stated they cannot fully pause traffic and requested gradual steps.

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: Performed quick analysis of logs; observed increased KV cache miss rates and a spike in micro-batching. Hypothesis: aggressive batching-window reduction to 10ms increased queuing and caused downstream timeouts in streaming connector. Proposed staged deactivation plan and asked for permission to execute with customer on call.

comments.author: Liam Chen
comments.body: Noted that rollback from Console will currently apply change cluster-wide which caused cold-start previously. Suggested using 'route by tag' to steer small percentage to previous optimizer variant first.

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: Created safe sequence (see investigation notes) and scheduled a live session with customer at 12:30 UTC. Tagged SRE (Asha Iyer) for autoscaler oversight.

comments.author: Asha Iyer (SRE)
comments.body: Observed that GPU warm pool size dropped during revert. Recommended pre-warming using a warmup flag and temporary traffic shadowing technique to refill KV caches before shifting user traffic.

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: Executed staged deactivation with customer: 1) lowered aggressive batching to intermediate window 25ms, 2) disabled kv-cache prefetch, 3) kept quant-profile but set deopt-guard-mode=true. Result: p95 latency reduced ~30% and 5xx decreased to baseline within 18 minutes.

comments.author: Nimbus Health
comments.body: Confirmed production metrics stabilized. Asked for written playbook and a permanent guard to prevent automatic profile swaps without canary monitoring.

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: Uploaded final resolution notes and recommended long-term fixes. Marked for follow-up postmortem.

Customer DocuScale reports sharp 95th/99th percentile tail latency spikes after a staged optimizer profile rollout that combined quantization profile Q4, aggressive batching, and KV prefix caching. The spikes began ~2026-02-28 22:15 UTC and affect a subset of tenant traffic that sends long-prefixed prompts (avg seq_len 1k+).

- Customer is on Dedicated capacity and requests a targeted, low-risk rollback for only affected tenant routes without full-service degradation.

- Tenant: docusc-tenant-42 (DocuScale production)
- Affected routes pinned to model: gpt-13b-q4_0 (default), fallback to sft-13b-q8_0 observed in traces.

title: SSO session cookie path collision causing tenant session mismatches in private deployment
body:
Issue summary:

FinCloud Apps reports intermittent 403s and wrong-tenant sessions after recent IdP metadata rotation. Root cause appears to be SSO session cookie path attributes being set to '/' by the identity-proxy when tenant routing is path-based (example.com/t/<tenant>), which allows cookies from one tenant to be sent for another tenant's request and results in incorrect tenant binding for the session.

Impact:
- Several FinCloud end-users see 403s when performing console actions as their session is resolved to the wrong tenant.
- Leads to confusing UX where users appear logged in but lack expected entitlements.
- Affects production traffic for a single private deployment customer (FinCloud, enterprise financial SaaS).

Environment:
- Private deployment, single hostname behind customer-managed ingress, path-based tenant routing (/t/<tenant_id>/...).
- Redwood Private v2025.12 wit...

comments.author: (2026-03-02) - Liam O'Connor
comments.body: Replicated locally using path-based routing. Identity-proxy default behavior issues cookie with Path='/' when tenant path prefixing present. Creating investigation ticket INT-4120 for infra change.

comments.author: (2026-03-02) - Priya Rao
comments.body: Noted this is a regression after default cookie refactor in identity-proxy v1.7.0; older versions set tenant-scoped cookie name. Suggests a quick fix: tenant-scoped cookie names + explicit Path setting.

title: Safety-sentinel: preflight and hotpatch plan for tenant optimizer profile causing sporadic tail latency
body: Issue summary: Customer BrightHealth AI reported a sudden increase in 95th/99th percentile tail latency for inference requests after applying a tenant-scoped optimizer profile that enabled aggressive batching, prefix KV prefetch, and a mixed 4-bit quantization profile. Impact: Intermittent API timeouts (up to 30s) affecting live features for 10% of requests, SLO breaches for the customer's dedicated pool. Environment: Dedicated tenant cluster in us-west-2; production traffic; tenant ID bhai-03. Steps to reproduce: 1) Use tenant API key bhai-03 in prod us-west-2. 2) Send typical 1-2k concurrent short-chat requests (avg seq len 120 tokens) over 2–5 minutes. 3) Observe increasing 95p/99p latency and occasional 504s. Observed behavior: Gradual buildup of host-level GPU memory pressure during steady traffic, periodic long-running server-side stalls that correlate with aggressive batch form...

comments.author: Aisha Khan ()
comments.body: Initial ticket created after BrightHealth SRE reported p99 spikes. Attached sample traces and prompt set. Marked P1 due to SLO risk.

comments.author: Engineer
comments.body: Collected serving runtime traces and console charts. Observed burst batch sizes and KV prefetch thrash. Proposed conservative rollback steps and safety-sentinel preflight. Assigned to me for coordination with SRE.

comments.author: BrightHealth SRE
comments.body: Confirmed that toggling to old profile reduced errors but some customer sessions timed out. Requesting hotpatch that minimizes disruption to in-flight sessions. Provided traffic volume and session timeout patterns.

comments.author: Linh Tran (SRE)
comments.body: Ran quick memory and GPU scheduling check on a replica prod host; no OOMs but noted CUDA kernel queues occasionally throttle when batch sizes spike > configured max. Suggest adding transient backpressure on batch-assembly.

comments.author: Engineer
comments.body: Executed tenant-scoped toggle on staging clone (command logged). Results: when preflight shadowing was enabled, we reproduce p99 spike within 6 minutes. Uploaded diagnostic snapshot to GH PR https://github.com/redwood-inference/serving-runtime/pull/1427 for engineering correlation.

comments.author: Engineer
comments.body: Provided one-line hotpatch CLI for customer: redwoodctl tenant toggle-profile --tenant bhai-03 --from profile:aggressive-2026-03 --to profile:conservative-2026-02 --staged --split 80:20 --monitor-metrics p95,p99,batch_size,kv_hit_ratio --escalation-on-regress. Also included console playbook steps in Confluence link.

comments.author: BrightHealth SRE
comments.body: Ran hotpatch during maintenance window following our staged approach. Report: initial 80/20 cut restored p99 to baseline but observed two transient 504s during split. Full rollback completed and currently monitoring. Customer requests a postmortem and preventative guardrails.

comments.author: Engineer
comments.body: Follow-up: Created follow-up engineering ticket INT-339 (internal) to investigate kernel scheduling and implement transient batching backpressure. Scheduling a knowledge transfer with Product on guardrail UI changes.

comments.author: Aisha Khan ()
comments.body: Marking as In Progress while engineering work is tracked in INT-339 and PR 1427. Awaiting confirmed 1h stable window from customer before resolving.

title: Live tenant sees higher tail latency after enabling mixed-quant profile + aggressive batching; request assisted snapshot restore and tuning advisory
body:
Issue summary:
AcuityDocs deployed an experiment enabling a mixed-precision quantization profile (q4_k_m-like) combined with an aggressive per-route batching policy (max_batch=256, max_latency_ms=120). Within ~30 minutes, they observed a persistent increase in 95/99p generation tail latency and a spike in 5xx errors for long-session chat routes.

Impact:
- Customer-facing chat sessions degrade: 99p latency rose from ~800ms to ~2.6s on some tenants.
- Error rate increased from ~0.2% to ~1.8% on affected routes.
- Affects a small set of enterprise tenants on Dedicated pool (AcuityDocs production).

Environment:
- Dedicated GPU pool (dgx-3p-rt-1) in us-west-2
- Serving runtime: v2026.02.11
- Optimizer config snapshot: opt-snap-2026-03-10-09:12 (contains q-profile: mixed-q-fast-2026 and batching policy route: /chat/long-session)

Steps to reproduce / observed timeline:
1) At 08:52 UTC cus...

Need formal approval from customer to proceed.

title: TCP window scaling disabled by corporate firewall causes long TLS handshake and streaming stalls on private deployment
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports repeated long stalls (20-90s) during model initialization and intermittent streaming resets for sessions served from their private deployment in us-east-1. TLS handshakes for model pull and streaming channels complete but token streaming often stalls; eventually client receives a final gRPC status=UNAVAILABLE. Customer noticed the problem roughly after a corporate firewall firmware change that same morning.

Impact:
- Affects AtlasHealth production traffic: ~15% of sessions see >20s stalls, ~3% see full disconnects.
- Impacts both model artifact fetch and live token streaming for redwood-3b-v1 and gpt-medic-2.1.
- Customer is on a privacy-sensitive private deployment (private control plane + VPC peering).

Hypothesis: a middlebox (corporate NGFW) is stripping or disabling TCP Window Scaling option (RFC 7323) and/or clamping TCP options, degrading throug...

comments.author: Maya Chen ()

comments.author: Customer Success (CS)
comments.body: Customer accepted workaround guidance and will schedule firewall policy change across corp during maintenance window next week.

title: Request: preflight health-check and staged soft-rollback runbook for tenant-specific batching/prefix-cache/quant profiles
body:
Issue summary:
BlueHaven reports intermittent 95p+ latency spikes and rare 5xx during peak runs after enabling a tenant-scoped optimizer profile that combined aggressive async batching, KV-prefetch enabled, and per-tenant 4-bit quantization. They request a concise preflight health-check checklist plus a staged soft-rollback runbook they can run with Support/SRE to avoid abrupt service disruption.

Impact:
- Customer-facing inference latency increase (95th percentile to ~1.2s from ~450ms) for long-session chat flows during 18:00–20:00 UTC window.
- Occasional 502 from serving-runtime traced to sudden KV cache misses and transient GPU memory pressure (not consistently reproducible).
- Business impact: critical customer workflows (document summarization pipelines) backlogged; BlueHaven flagged as high-risk for retention if not remediated.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool (customer-dedicate...

comments.author: SRE (Investigation)
comments.body: Observed pattern: async batch size growth + prefix-KV churn when sequence lengths vary widely, causing transient allocation spikes on quant-backed kernels. Expect safe mitigation by reducing async-batch cap and disabling KV-prefetch for this tenant as intermediate step.

comments.author: Rafi Patel ()
comments.body: Executed soft mitigation steps with customer during maintenance window: reduced async_batch_max from 32->12 for tenant profile and toggled kv_prefetch:false for the tenant (Console change). Monitoring telemetry now shows latency dropping toward baseline. Customer to observe for 2 hours.

comments.author: BlueHaven Engineer (Customer)
comments.body: Reports 95p returned to ~520ms and no new 5xx occurrences since mitigation. Requested full runbook to formalize steps for future incidents.

comments.author: Rafi Patel ()
comments.body: Willing to produce runbook; marking ticket Triage -> In Progress to author soft rollback runbook and preflight checklist for customer to reuse.

title: Multi-hop egress observed when pinning to EU — increased P99 latency; clarification on residency policy and routing heuristics
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports a consistent P99 latency increase (~350-450ms) for requests pinned to eu-west when their traffic appears to traverse a US egress/edge before returning to EU serving instances. They are using Dedicated capacity with a strict region pin (project-level pin to eu-west). Customer wants: (1) explanation of why traffic might take a multi-hop path across regions despite pinning, (2) confirmation about whether any data leaves the EU region (egress for processing vs egress for routing), and (3) guidance or configuration to ensure single-region egress for residency guarantees.

Impact:
- Customer-facing inference latency increase on P99 for EU users (from typical 140ms -> 500-600ms for some requests).
- User experience degradation for real-time inference endpoints used in production for trading insights.
- No reported data leak; main concern is latency and confirm...

comments.author: Asha Mehta
comments.body: Thanks for the detailed repro and trace. We're triaging with infra. Can you confirm whether the requests with multi-hop show any different model-version or host tags?

comments.author: Customer (S. Petrov, FinCloud)
comments.body: Confirmed model_version: gptx-3.8-16k@v12 for all traces. Host tags for all 'served' segments are eu-west. The odd leg is always an api-gateway located in us-east.

comments.author: Asha Mehta
comments.body: Noted. Looping in Diego (networking) and runtime to check routing heuristics and sticky-affinity TTL handling.

comments.author: Diego Alvarez
comments.body: Initial finding: smart-routing fallback policy can select an edge in any region when sticky-affinity TTL is expired/violated and the local edge doesn't have a fresh cached route to the serving pod. The selected edge is used to orchestrate the request and may add a cross-region hop before the serving runtime is reached. Investigating whether this path causes egress of request payload beyond EU for processing, or only for control-plane routing.

comments.author: (Mira O'Connor)
comments.body: From a compliance perspective, control-plane routing metadata does not contain customer prompt content. We need to confirm whether any KV cache or prefix tokens are being forwarded during control ops.

title: Latency regression (p95) after upgrading Dedicated cluster from serving-runtime 1.17.4 -> 1.18.0
body:
Customer (Quantgrid) reports a significant latency regression after upgrading their Dedicated runtime image from 1.17.4 to 1.18.0.

Issue summary
- After upgrade, p95 end-to-end latency increased 35–60% on steady workload.
- p50 stayed roughly flat (or slightly worse), but tail latency increased materially.
- The regression is most visible during mixed prompt-length traffic and when concurrency > ~0.8x reserved throughput.

Impact
- Customer SLO is driven by p95. Their upstream service sees request timeouts and queue buildup.
- Customer is asking for immediate rollback guidance and a way to pin runtime versions to avoid future forced upgrades.
- This ticket is being referenced internally as a key motivator for establishing an LTS channel (stability + predictable patch cadence).

Environment
- Dedicated cluster: qg-dedicated-prod (us-east)
- Runtime versions:
 - Baseline: serving-runti...

title: Safe step-down plan for tenant optimizer experiment causing increased P95 latency
body:
Issue summary: AurumHealth enabled an optimizer experiment (tenant profile 'exp/aggressive-mix-v2') that combined continuous batching heuristics, aggressive KV prefetch, and an 8-bit quantization profile. After the change we saw a sharp P95 latency regression and intermittent streaming disconnects for long chat workloads.

Impact: ~30% of tenant requests now have P95 ≈ 900ms (previously ~200ms). Several customer agents reported streaming truncation and occasional 5xx errors. This is customer-impacting during peak hours for AurumHealth's workflow.

Environment: Dedicated pool ded-us-east-3, prod-us-east region, tenant = AurumHealth (dedicated). Traffic shape: many mid-long sequences (avg seq_len 320, 95th 600). Model pinned to llama-3-70b, runtime variant redwood-mix-quant-8bit for the experiment.

Steps to reproduce (approx):
1) Apply tenant optimizer profile 'exp/aggressive-mix-v2' v...

comments.author: Maya Chen ()
comments.body: Opened ticket after customer reported P95 spike during their morning window. Collected initial logs and profile name. Asked SRE for immediate triage.

comments.author: Zoe Patel
comments.body: Observed elevated GPU memory pressure and high KV eviction on ded-us-east-3. Suggest disabling KV prefetch first as it appears to increase memory churn and forces fallbacks to slow-path kernels.

comments.author: Ravi Singh
comments.body: Confirmed related kernel selection regression in PR #2846: new heuristic falls back to safe kernel when quantized mixed types present with large batch groupings. Recommending stepwise rollback: (1) disable kv_prefetch, (2) cap batch group size to 32, (3) revert quant profile to 16-bit on the tenant. We can provide CLI commands for each step.

comments.author: Maya Chen ()
comments.body: Customer is sensitive to downtime. Requested a traffic-split plan and a hotpatch script that can be applied without restarting all workers.

comments.author: Customer (AurumHealth - ops@aurumhealth.com)
comments.body: Confirmed they can accept a 10-minute maintenance window tonight. Asked for order of operations and rollback verification steps.

comments.author: Luis Ortega ()
comments.body: Drafted step-down playbook and scheduled a runbook review with SRE and Runtime Eng at 2026-03-12 11:00. Will produce a hotfix script for immediate mitigation.

comments.author: Luis Ortega
comments.body: Post-meeting note: Agreed plan attached in investigation_notes. Next action: provide commands and a tested runbook (will post by EOD).

title: Cross-workspace Slack role propagation and Confluence space seed automation request
body:
Issue summary:
We need an automated mechanism to propagate role/group membership changes from the central directory to multiple Slack workspaces and simultaneously bootstrap a Confluence space with a small set of templated pages and initial permissions.

Impact:
Currently, when we update pod reviewer groups or oncall role mappings, engineers must manually update membership across three Slack workspaces and then request a Confluence space creation with manual permission granting. This causes delays in onboarding, inconsistent permissions, and occasional missing runbook pages during incident responses.

Request details / Acceptance criteria:
- Implement an automation that listens for updates to the central group registry (LDAP/SCIM source) and propagates role changes to Slack workspaces: #runtime-ops, #model-evals, and #platform-pods.
- Create a Confluence space provisioning task that, ...

comments.body: Filing this to reduce onboarding friction and make incident runbooks reliably available. Attached sample mapping CSV and list of existing spaces.

comments.author: Keiran Patel () - 2026-02-09
comments.body:
Acknowledged. I'll scope the automation into two pieces: a Slack role propagator and a Confluence seeder. Planning to prototype Slack propagation via SCIM push and a parallel Confluence REST-based creation task.
Questions: 1) Who owns the authoritative mapping file? 2) Is there an internal admin console API we can hook into for the one-click trigger?

comments.author: Reviewer
comments.body:
Security notes: automation must use short-lived tokens and write audit events to the central audit log. Confluence permissions should not grant 'admin' to any service account. Also request a review of the Confluence template for any PII before seeding.
Blocking: need confirmation on data classification for seeded pages.

comments.author: Platform
comments.body:
PM input: prioritize onboarding pods involved in runtime-ops first. Add a UI checkbox to opt into 'incident-ready' template vs 'sandbox' template.
Also request a small analytics event emitted after successful provisioning to the usage-metrics topic.

comments.author: Keiran Patel () - 2026-03-05
comments.body: Prototype complete for Slack propagation (test workspace). Confirmed idempotent behavior and rollback by reverting group tag. Need Confluence API quota increase for bulk creates - filing a separate quota request to Atlassian team. Planning a demo 2026-03-12.

comments.body: Thanks — please include documentation for SRE-run rollback steps and an appendix listing events emitted.

title: HydraNet sustained throughput collapse and streaming churn during EOD peak — customer escalation and restoration plan
body:
Issue summary:
At ~2026-03-10 13:20 UTC HydraNet (enterprise customer, heavy EOD ingest) observed a sudden throughput collapse on our hosted endpoint with a concurrent surge in 5xx responses and streaming connection churn. Customer-facing symptom: stalled streaming responses and partial transcripts, causing downstream ingestion failures for HydraNet's reconciliation pipeline.

Impact:
- Duration: ~13:20 UTC to 15:05 UTC (services gradually recovered after mitigation).
- Affected keys: HydraNet production account only (single-customer outage).
- Observable metrics: request success rate dropped to 64% at the peak; median token latency increased from 60ms to 450ms; streaming disconnects increased by 8x.
- Business impact: HydraNet reported delayed reconciliation jobs and customer SLAs missed for two internal teams.

Detection timeline (high level):
- 2026-03-10 13:18 UTC: Automatic SLO a...

- Observable metrics: request success rate dropped to 64% at the peak; median token latency increased from 60ms to 450ms; streaming disconnects increased by 8x.
- Business impact: HydraNet reported delayed reconciliation jobs and customer SLAs missed for two internal teams.

- 2026-03-10 13:18 UTC: Automatic SLO a...

00 UTC — Incident Review (All): Postmortem draft created and action items assigned. Customer satisfied with remediation plan and credits applied. Ticket moving to Resolved.

title: Hotpath tuner escape hatch and validation checks for tenant-induced latency surge
body:
Issue summary: LuminaHealth reported a sharp increase in 95th/99th percentile token latency after enabling a tenant-scoped optimizer profile change (aggressive batching + prefix KV prefetch + 4-bit quant profile). Impact began around 2026-03-09 22:40 UTC for us-west production.

We suspect an interaction between the mixed-precision quant profile and continuous-batching heuristics that causes increased KV eviction churn and tail latency spikes for long-context requests. Customer is on a Dedicated pool with reserved GPUs and needs guidance for a fast, safe rollback without traffic disruption or model-version pin conflict.

comments.body: Thanks Anika — we are triaging. Can you grant SRE temporary access to LuminaHealth tenant metrics via the console (we requested read-only link)?

comments.author: Alice Chen, CS
comments.body: Access granted for 24h. We see user complaints for slow document summarization endpoints.

comments.author: Marco Hernandez ()
comments.body: Performed staging repro and confirmed eviction spike. Drafting safe escape-hatch steps: 1) toggle prefix-kv prefetch off for tenant, 2) reduce async batch target to 32, 3) revert quant profile to 8-bit. Holding for customer approval.

comments.author: Alice Chen (LuminaHealth)
comments.body: Please proceed with non-disruptive changes first (toggle prefetch), prefer console-guided change we can monitor.

comments.author: Jordan Liu
comments.body: Ran preflight checks, snapshot snap-20260310-1120 created. Will apply prefetch=false now in canary (10% traffic) and monitor for 30 minutes.

comments.author: Jordan Liu
comments.body: Canary looks good: 95p latency dropped to ~220ms and evictions reduced to ~30/s. Preparing staged rollback plan to revert 'opt-hotpath-v3' to 'opt-safe-v2' per runbook.

comments.body: Moving ticket to In Progress for full rollback scheduling with LuminaHealth. Next steps: coordinate maintenance window and follow rollback checklist in Confluence.

title: CobaltGrocer: prolonged tail latency and streaming RESETs during peak-hour checkout flows
body: Issue summary: During CobaltGrocer's weekday afternoon peak (13:00–15:00 PST) customers reported elevated completion tail latency and increased streaming connection resets for checkout conversational flows. Impact: checkout chat completions intermittently truncated or failed to stream, increasing abandoned carts and urgent escalation from customer engineering. Environment: prod us-west-2, dedicated routing policy 'enterprise-usw2', traffic routed to dedicated GPU pool 'dg-pool-31'.

title: Safe degradation policy request: guidance for quant/profile + batching + KV cache fallbacks
body: Issue summary: LexiChat reports increased 95th-percentile tail latency and sporadic streaming disconnects after enabling a new quantization profile and aggressive batching + KV prefetch on their Dedicated pool. They want a tested, low-risk policy to degrade features (quant profile, batching, KV prefetch) in production without full rollback to prior model version. Impact: Customer-facing chat experiences intermittent timeouts and elevated token costs due to retries. Request: concrete step-by-step safe-degradation policy and one-line CLI/API commands for toggles, plus verification checklist and recommended monitoring thresholds.

title: Seastar Logistics: inflight batch request reordering causing out-of-order completions and SLA breach
body:
Issue summary:
Between 14:00-16:30 UTC on 2026-02-18, Seastar Logistics experienced out-of-order completions for multi-turn sessions affecting ~18% of requests from their high-priority production pool. The behavior caused application-level state mismatch, several automated retries, and an SLA breach for their enterprise contract.

Impact:
- Customer-visible: Incorrect next-step actions in order routing workflows (18% of calls) causing order duplication and manual reconciliation.
- Scope: Traffic for Seastar's priority project across two frontends in us-west-2 (approx 1.2k req/min peak).
- Severity: P0 / Sev1 - customer production outage with SLA exposure.

Environment:
- Redwood hosted (prod) serving with dedicated priority routing policy for Seastar (enterprise).
- Model: redwood/gptx-13b-v2 pinned.
- Batcher config: dynamic batching with prefix-aware session affinity enabled.

Steps...

title: Circuit-breaker snapshot & rollback guidance for oscillating tail-latency after adaptive prefetch + quant profile change
body:
Issue summary: After applying an adaptive prefetch + quantization profile to BrightHealth AI's tenant in Dedicated, a subset of requests began showing oscillating tail-latency spikes (~95-99p) and occasional client disconnects. Customer wants prescriptive steps to safely engage a circuit-breaker, restore a known-good snapshot for their tenant, and confirm post-rollback validation criteria.

Context: The tenant was part of a controlled optimizer experiment targeting lower token cost using aggressive KV-prefetch and mixed-precision quant profiles. The change was deployed via the console's optimizer rollout on 2026-03-09 21:00 UTC. Suspected interaction between prefetch eviction behavior and quantized kernels causing intermittent token-processing stalls on some GPUs.

comments.author: Diego Alvarez (2026-03-10)
comments.body: Investigating. Requested traces, a sample pcap of requests, and Prometheus exporters for the affected hosts. Kicked off a canary replay job (replay id: replay-9237).

comments.author: SRE - Priya Nair (2026-03-11)
comments.body: Observed kernel fallback logs on host-gpu-20 & host-gpu-14. Suggested temporary circuit-breaker to redirect 50% of traffic to pinned non-quant model variant while restoring tenant snapshot.

comments.author: Customer - BrightHealth (2026-03-11)
comments.body: Agrees to traffic split for immediate relief. Asks for step-by-step rollback that doesn't require a full offboard.

comments.body: Drafted stepwise plan below and requested Approval to execute during low-traffic window (US morning).

title: Guidance request: staged unwind plan for aggressive batching/caching/quant changes causing intermittent 5xxs
body:
Issue summary: Customer applied an aggressive optimization bundle (max batching, prefix KV caching enabled across routes, and a newly promoted quantization profile) last night via Console rollout. Since the change, they report intermittent 5xx errors and increased tail latency for select conversational routes. Impact: Several production customers (internal clients of Sentra Health) experienced failed requests and degraded response times during morning traffic spike. No billing discrepancy reported. Environment: Dedicated pool in us-east, multi-AZ, pinned model versions. This is a production-impacting issue during business hours.

Observed behavior: Over a 90-minute window we saw bursts of 5xxs (502/503) and transient connection resets. Error rates correlate with high concurrent request windows; CPU and GPU utilization spiked then dropped for affected pods. KV cache hit-rate increased ...

title: Intermittent console 504 / aborted uploads: gRPC-web upload stream resets behind customer ADC that coalesces HTTP/2 SETTINGS frames
body: Issue summary: Intermittent upload failures and 504s observed in Redwood Console for a Private deployment. Failures reproduce only when the user's traffic flows through their on-prem ADC which performs HTTP/2/TCP optimizations. Impact: uploads fail to complete, leading to customer inability to persist prompt datasets and occasional console timeouts; affects a subset of users behind the ADC, not all tenants.

title: Priority-shift causing pool reservation tunneling and mid-day GPU starvation for Nova Finance (dedicated)
body:
Issue summary:
Midday on 2025-08-19 Nova Finance (dedicated pool) experienced a sudden p50/p99 throughput collapse for short-burst chat routes. Requests queued; autoscaler did not attach additional GPU capacity in time. Customers saw elevated 429s and 504s for ~15 minutes before partial recovery via manual mitigation.

Impact:
- Customer-facing: 20% of requests returned errors or timeouts during the event window; bulk of traffic served by pinned slot pool.
- Business: Nova Finance is a production dedicated customer with SLA commitments.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: pool-id fc-9a2b (Nova Finance reserved)
- Region: us-west (primary)

Issue summary:
Midday on 2025-08-19 Nova Finance (dedicated pool) experienced a sudden p50/p99 throughput collapse for short-burst chat routes. Requests queued; autoscaler did not attach additional GPU capacity in time. Customers saw elevated 429s and 504s for ~15 minutes before partial recovery via manual mitigation.

Impact:
- Customer-facing: 20% of requests returned errors or timeouts during the event window; bulk of traffic served by pinned slot pool.
- Business: Nova Finance is a production dedicated customer with SLA commitments.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: pool-id fc-9a2b (Nova Finance reserved)
- Region: us-west (primary)

title: Reserved pool warmup causing transient p95/p99 latency spikes and priority inversion with on-demand tenants
body:
Issue summary:
Customer BrightDocs is observing intermittent p95/p99 latency spikes and token throttling across their dedicated reserved pool. Spikes occur during short bursts (1–5 minutes) after a period of low activity and correlate with a high rate of prefetch/eviction churn on the GPU nodes. Symptoms point to priority inversion where on-demand/burst allocations trigger kernel preemptions and eviction storms that delay warmup of pinned reserved workers, causing downstream queueing and token-level throttling at the API layer.

Impact:
- BrightDocs reports elevated p95/p99 for generate and streaming requests; customer-facing features degrade for end users.
- Observed increased 429s/timeouts for some high-concurrency routes routed to the dedicated pool.
- Customer is on a committed reserved pool; they expect predictable latency.

Environment:
- Dedicated reserved pool in us-east, mixe...

comments.body: 10: We reproduced a similar pattern in staging with a mixed A10/A100 pool; attaching kernel logs to the ticket. Preemption correlates with eviction churn.

comments.author: BrightDocs contact - Lina Gomez
comments.body: 00: Thank you — we saw improvement after you disabled burst-to-shared. Can you quantify the expected latency SLO restoration and any cost implications?

title: Autoscaler convergence failure caused transient GPU disappearance, capacity shortfall, and cascading fallback routing
body:
Issue summary:
Between 2026-03-10 18:05 and 18:22 UTC we observed a dedicated customer pool (acme-analytics) experience a sharp throughput collapse and elevated p99 latency. Autoscaler logs show a failed convergence cycle that did not reclaim expected GPU slots; simultaneously the routing layer began falling back requests to smaller model variants, causing both higher token counts and routing churn.

Impact:
- Customer observed ~60% drop in throughput for bursty short streams and p99 latency increased from ~120ms to ~1.2s for their standard route.
- Requests were transparently routed to a pinned fallback model in a different capacity tier, increasing per-token cost and causing billing/usage chatter for the customer.
- No customer data loss; some requests retried by client, amplifying load.

Timeline (local times in UTC):
- 18:04:58: scheduling heartbeats for the pool show a ~9s gap be...

title: Latency regression (p95 decode) on Dedicated after enabling quantization profile on one route
body:
Issue summary
- Northstar Search reports higher p95 latency on a single Dedicated chat route after enabling a Quantization Profile Library profile.
- Regression is most visible on decode latency (streaming token cadence slower); p50 mostly unchanged.

Customer impact
- Customer reports p95 latency increased from ~1.3s to ~2.1s end-to-end on their main chat route.
- They are running near their throughput target; tail latency is causing user-visible delays.

Environment
- Dedicated cluster: nsr-dedicated-prod-01
- Region: us-east
- Route: chat-prod (route_id: rte_9b1d3a)
- Model: rw-llama3-70b-instruct
- Quantization setting change: quantization_profile_ref set from "default" to "qpl://rw-llama3-70b-instruct/int8w-fp16a/v1.2.0" at 2025-02-22 18:42 UTC

Steps to reproduce (internal)
1) Send streaming chat requests (typical prompt length 6–10k tokens; max_new_tokens 300–700) to rte_9b1d3a...

comments.body: 08 UTC — Miguel Santos (Enterprise Success): Customer reports p95 jumped from ~1.3s to ~2.1s after enabling quant profile on Dedicated. They can reproduce consistently on long prompts. Requesting urgent assist; user-facing impact.

15 UTC — Dev Patel (Support): Acknowledged. Pulling dashboards + requesting request IDs and approximate timestamps from customer. Asked whether any other config changed (rate limits, max_tokens, etc.).
33 UTC — Miguel Santos: Provided sample request IDs: req_2f3c1a7, req_2f3c1c2, req_2f3c1d9 (18:50–19:05 UTC). Confirms only change was route toggle to quantization profile v1.2.0.
41 UTC — Aisha Bello (SRE): Seeing clear step in p95 decode at 18:42 UTC (config change). 5xx stable. Queue time up. Suggest rollback route setting first to stabilize, then debug.
55 UTC — Dev Patel: Coordinating rollback with customer approval. Per runbook, route-level disable is safe + reversible. Waiting for explicit OK.
02 UTC — Miguel Santos: Customer approved rollback of quant profile on prod route. They prefer fastest mitigation; okay to keep enabled on staging route for analysis.
10 UTC — Benji Okafor (Config API): Verified current config: quantization_profile_ref=qpl://rw-llama3-70b-instruct/int8w-fp16a/v1.2.0 on rte_9b1d3a. Prepared patch to revert to default.
17 UTC — Benji Okafor: Applied config change: rte_9b1d3a quantization_profile_ref set to "default". Audit event emitted (actor=bokafor).
35 UTC — Aisha Bello: p95 trending down; within 10–15 min should be back to baseline once in-flight drains. kv_cache_evictions dropping as well.
52 UTC — Dev Patel: Confirmed on dashboards: p95 end-to-end back to ~1.3–1.4s; TTFT stable; decode p95 normalized. Updating customer.

11 UTC — Noah Patel (Runtime): Looked at traces for req_2f3c1a7 etc. quant_profile_id present and applied. Under long context we see higher device mem pressure and a slow decode path. Not a correctness issue; profile not a good fit for this workload/SKU combination.

26 UTC — Arjun Desai (Applied ML): We can republish with tighter constraints (max_context=8192) + mark current profile as "not recommended" for this GPU class if runtime confirms. Will coordinate with Quentin (registry) on deprecation metadata.

40 UTC — Dev Patel: Customer confirmed latency stable after rollback; will run staging A/B next week with smaller prompts. Marking ticket Resolved; follow-up tasks tracked internally via Applied ML + Runtime.

title: Surgical deopt & config coalescence due to partial quant+prefetch mismatch causing 95p latency and tokenization drift
body:
Issue summary:
BrightForms observed an abrupt increase in 95th-percentile token latency and sporadic generation truncation after enabling a tenant-scoped optimizer profile that combined asynchronous aggressive batching, KV prefetch, and INT8 quantization on 2026-03-11 03:40 UTC. Customer reports also indicate slight semantic drift in embeddings used for reranking (small but measurable).

Impact:
- Increased 95p latency from ~120ms to ~680ms for their primary chat route (route: /api/v1/generate/brightforms-chat).
- Occasional generation truncation (stream disconnects) on long responses.
- Embedding cosine shift ~0.03 on their eval set causing rerank order flips (affects downstream UX).

Environment:
- Dedicated pool (BrightForms-dedicated-1), prod, us-east-1.
- Tenant pinned routes on mixed model variants: some routes pinned to redwood-open-7b-q8, others to vicuna-13b-int8.
- Optimizer...

title: Safeguarded unwind for optimizer feature-flag that caused increased tail latency and 5xxs
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reported a sudden increase in tail latency and intermittent 5xx responses after enabling a tenant-scoped optimizer feature-flag that changed batching and KV-prefetch behavior for quantized models. The flag was rolled out to a small percentage of traffic and then increased to 30% before the anomalies were noticed.

Impact:
- ~15% of requests from the customer's production traffic saw P95 latency jump from ~220ms to ~1.2s and P99 to ~3s.
- A subset of requests returned 502/504 when the serving process returned an internal error while loading quantized kernels.
- Customer reports increased error rate affecting business-critical inference pipelines.

Environment:
- Customer: Acme Analytics (dedicated, us-east cluster)
- Model: redwood/gpt-small-quant8 pinned at v2026-02-26
- Platform: Dedicated pool ID dp-acme-02, runtime version 3.4.1

Steps to reproduce (observed...

comments.author: (Aisha) 2026-03-12T10
comments.body: 15Z: Logged at customer request. Escalating to SRE and runtime engineering. Customer supplied request-id samples and console audit of flag changes.

comments.author: SRE (Miguel) 2026-03-12T10
comments.body: 30Z: Looking into serving logs and node-level failures; tagging gpux-03/gpux-07. Will validate which processes still have the old kernel cache loaded.

comments.author: Lena Cho
comments.body: 05Z: Confirmed kernel-selection exception stack. Recommending an ordered unwind: 1) disable tenant flag, 2) drain affected workers, 3) clear per-worker kernel cache (kv-store), 4) restart workers to ensure fresh kernel registry loads. Need SRE to coordinate drain and restarts for Dedicated pool.

comments.author: (Aisha) 2026-03-12T11
comments.body: 30Z: Shared proposed unwind steps with customer and requested maintenance window for restarts. Customer gave permission for a rolling drain impacting up to 5% of their capacity at a time.

comments.author: SRE (Miguel) 2026-03-12T12
comments.body: 10Z: Executed staged disable of feature-flag for tenant at 2026-03-12T12:12Z. Beginning rolling drain of dp-acme-02 workers. Monitoring metrics for improvement.

comments.author: Tom Rivera - Acme
comments.body: 25Z: Confirmed they can tolerate the planned rolling drain. Asked for checklist to re-enable flag once fixed.

title: Config surge: aggressive optimizer profile triggered tenant KPIs — mitigation & controlled rollback walkthrough
body:
Issue summary:
An aggressive optimizer profile (experimental profile 'aggr-prefetch-v3') was enabled for BrightHealth AI tenant in Dedicated capacity. Within minutes we observed P95/P99 tail latency spikes and intermittent 5xx responses for long-sequence streaming requests.

Impact:
- Customer-facing latency increased: 95th latency rose from ~120ms to ~2.7s for token streaming paths.
- Spike in 5xx errors (503/504) and a few OOMs on one GPU node in their Dedicated pool.
- Production traffic was degraded for ~18 minutes before mitigations.

Environment:
- Dedicated GPU pool: dhp-dedicated-us-east-4 (A100s), tenant BrightHealth AI.
- Model: redwood-v2-7b pinned at version 2026-02-18.
- Optimizer profile applied: aggr-prefetch-v3 (changes: prefix KV prefetch enabled + target_batch_size from auto->256 + delayed flush window).

Steps to reproduce (approx):
1) Apply optimizer profile 'aggr-...

comments.body: 12 UTC) - Asha Patel: Acknowledged. Opening incident, prioritizing P1. I requested immediate snapshot of optimizer config and tenant traffic split. Asked SRE for node health and autoscaler logs.

comments.author: SRE (2026-03-10 14
comments.body: 18 UTC) - Jamie Liu: Observed increased PCIe and host memory churn on node dhp-node-17; several pods showing high iowait. Autoscaler didn't scale because average CPU across pool remained below threshold; queue growth happened on per-shard basis.

comments.author: Customer (2026-03-10 14
comments.body: 22 UTC) - BrightHealth Oncall: We rolled the change back from our side but see residual 5xx on some requests. Need confirmation the profile is fully disabled per-tenant and whether traffic was routed to fallback model variants.

28 UTC) - Marco Ruiz: Confirmed profile unset at tenant override layer. Initiating controlled per-tenant snapshot restore and staged traffic isolation to remove prefetch hooks. Also applied temporary rate-limit to tenant token throughput to prevent immediate re-thrashing.

comments.author: SRE (2026-03-10 14
comments.body: 40 UTC) - Jamie Liu: Performed a hot-snapshot restore of the last stable optimizer snapshot (2026-03-09T02:12Z) for BrightHealth tenant on 3 affected shards. Evictions fell and queue length returned to normal within 9 minutes.

comments.author: Customer (2026-03-10 15
comments.body: 05 UTC) - BrightHealth Oncall: Confirmed latency recovered and 5xx reduced to baseline. Thanks. Requesting a short post-incident summary and recommended safe profile rollout checklist.
10 UTC) - Marco Ruiz: Created this ticket to capture the mitigation steps, findings, and recommended preflight checks. Added follow-up tasks for engineering to harden circuit-breakers.

Issue summary:
An aggressive optimizer profile (experimental profile 'aggr-prefetch-v3') was enabled for BrightHealth AI tenant in Dedicated capacity. Within minutes we observed P95/P99 tail latency spikes and intermittent 5xx responses for long-sequence streaming requests.

Impact:
- Customer-facing latency increased: 95th latency rose from ~120ms to ~2.7s for token streaming paths.
- Spike in 5xx errors (503/504) and a few OOMs on one GPU node in their Dedicated pool.
- Production traffic was degraded for ~18 minutes before mitigations.

Environment:
- Dedicated GPU pool: dhp-dedicated-us-east-4 (A100s), tenant BrightHealth AI.
- Model: redwood-v2-7b pinned at version 2026-02-18.
- Optimizer profile applied: aggr-prefetch-v3 (changes: prefix KV prefetch enabled + target_batch_size from auto->256 + delayed flush window).

Steps to reproduce (approx):
1) Apply optimizer profile 'aggr-...

title: Assistance requested: enable aggressive batching + int8 quantization caused latency spikes and model regressions; need safe rollback plan
body: Issue summary: After applying Optimize suggestions (batch_size=64, aggregate=auto, quantize_profile=int8_aggressive, prefix_cache=true) customer saw request-level 500/502 errors and elevated end-to-end latency for text-generation flows. Impact: production service for PromptlyAI's assistant showing degraded quality (increased hallucinations) and tail latency spikes ~2.5x. Environment: Dedicated pool in us-east, model pinned to redwood-3b-8k-2026-02-quant; autoscaling enabled. Logs: provided below.

comments.author: Customer
comments.body: We enabled the recommendations from Optimize and saw hallucinations & 502s; can you advise rollback steps? (submitted request with sample payloads).

comments.author: Aisha Patel
comments.body: Acknowledged. Asking for exact config and model variant. Requested logs and traffic window (received).

comments.author: Marco Chen
comments.body: Hypothesis: int8_aggressive causing kernel fallbacks; recommend immediate traffic shift to previous variant and reduce batching. Preparing safe rollback steps.

comments.author: Customer
comments.body: Performed traffic switch and lowered batch_size; observed p95 drop. Requesting written rollback checklist.
Documented checklist and filed INT-402 for engineering investigation. Customer requested follow-up call on 2026-03-12.

comments.author: Customer
comments.body: Call completed; engineering promised patch in next runtime release if reproduction confirmed.

title: Assistance requested: safe deactivation of optimizer stack after perf regression following quant-profile update
body: Issue summary: Customer deployed a new quantization profile + aggressive batching and prefix-cache settings yesterday evening. Within ~2 hours they observed intermittent 5xx/503 responses and a sustained tail-latency increase for long-chat sessions. Impact: Several paying users experienced timeouts; customer reports spike in errors ~1.6% of requests resulting in degraded UX. Environment: Dedicated cluster in us-west, reserved GPUs (A10G), serving-runtime v2.14.2, optimizer-agent v1.7. Steps to reproduce: 1) Send multi-turn chat with 8+ message history and variable prompt lengths (~2-4k tokens). 2) Target model redwood-3.2 with quant_profile=aggressive-8bit and batching.max_batch_size=128. 3) Observe at steady state under 50 rps. Observed behavior: increased 503 and occasional OOM warnings in node logs, 95th/99th latency moved from 240ms/420ms to 1.2s/2.8s respectively. Expected behavi...

comments.author: Elena Morales ()
comments.body: Opening ticket after on-call escalation from HorizonHealth. Attached sample logs and token traces. Asking for prioritized guidance; customer is P1 and on Dedicated plan.

comments.author: Kai Nguyen (SRE)
comments.body: Acknowledged. Requesting exact config snapshot and admin CLI access window. Suggested we schedule a 30-min troubleshooting window with HorizonHealth tomorrow morning PST.

comments.author: HorizonHealth (Customer)
comments.body: Provided config bundle (anonymized) and link to internal perf grafana: https://grafana.horizon.example/d/redwood/latency. Confirmed canary was skipped due to time pressure.

comments.author: Kai Nguyen (SRE)
comments.body: Initial hypotheses posted in ticket: (1) quant profile induced slower kernel fallbacks for long sequences; (2) aggressive batching increased KV cache pressure causing cache thrash. Requested heap and GPU mem metrics for affected pods.

comments.author: (Elena)
comments.body: Noted that reducing batch size to 32 decreased 5xx but tail-latency still elevated. Asked Eng to provide rollback playbook.

title: Adaptive prefetch threshold caused latency spikes with per-route quant profile mismatch; need safe traffic-exit and rollback guidance
body:
Issue summary:
HelixHealth observed an abrupt 95th and 99th percentile latency increase following our runtime's adaptive prefetch threshold update combined with a per-route 8-bit quant profile. The customer is on Dedicated capacity in us-east and reported degraded production SLAs for critical inference routes (medical-summarization and triage-chat).

Impact:
- 95p latency increased from ~420ms to ~2.4s on two routes.
- 99p tail rose to ~4.1s.
- Small but steady 5xx spike from baseline 0.02% to 1.8% within a 30m window.
- Customer-facing errors and timeouts causing partial outage for automated triage flows.

Why this ticket: The customer suspects the adaptive prefetch threshold mechanic increased aggressive KV cache eviction when mixed with a new per-route quant profile, producing repeated cold-prefetches and amplification of tail latency. They need a safe, low-risk exit plan to return...

comments.author: Ethan Cole ()
comments.body: Customer submitted heap and perf traces. They cannot tolerate >1s 95p latency on triage-chat; asking for a hotpath rollback option.

comments.author: Maya Patel (SRE)
comments.body: I'm seeing eviction stamps in the runtime logs matching customer timestamps. Preparing an emergency config toggle that targets only this tenant route.

comments.author: on-call (R. Alvarez)
comments.body: Confirmed quant profile causes ~12% increase in token decode memory for 8-bit path under our kernel selection. Proposing traffic-split 90/10 to fallback model as an experiment before global revert.

comments.author: Ethan Cole ()
comments.body: Customer agreed to a staged traffic-split and prefers we avoid full capacity redistribution during business hours. Approved 90/10 traffic-split to fallback model for 20 minutes as test.

comments.author: Maya Patel (SRE)
comments.body: Executed tenant-scoped prefetch retention increase (rollback to baseline) at 2026-03-13T08:12 UTC. Initial telemetry shows 95p decreased to ~480ms within 6 minutes. Monitoring continues.

title: Request: advice and safe revert playbook for combined batching/caching/quantization tuning causing intermittent 5xx and output regressions
body: Customer ScaleOps enabled an aggressive Optimize profile (aggressive_batching=true, kv_prefix_cache=enabled, quant_profile=custom-low-bit) across several Dedicated pools to cut per-token cost. After rollout to production traffic at 2026-03-09T22:30Z they report intermittent 5xx spikes, elevated tail latency for streaming responses, and subtle semantic output degradation on long-context prompts. They want guidance on tuning knobs (batch size, cache TTL, quant profile) and a conservative rollback/playbook that won't cause a second outage.

normalized.description: Customer ScaleOps enabled an aggressive Optimize profile (aggressive_batching=true, kv_prefix_cache=enabled, quant_profile=custom-low-bit) across several Dedicated pools to cut per-token cost. After rollout to production traffic at 2026-03-09T22:30Z they report intermittent 5xx spikes, elevated tail latency for streaming responses, and subtle semantic output degradation on long-context prompts. They want guidance on tuning knobs (batch size, cache TTL, quant profile) and a conservative rollback/playbook that won't cause a second outage.

comments.author: Aisha Patel
comments.body: Customer reports 5xx spikes started ~30 minutes after enabling the Optimize profile across 3 pools. They have production traffic and asked for an immediate, low-risk rollback.

comments.author: Marco Alvarez
comments.body: Assigned; asking for pool IDs, route IDs, and the exact Optimize profile JSON. Requesting traces and a sample failing req_id.

comments.author: via CS
comments.body: Provided pool IDs: dedicated-pool-43, dedicated-pool-44; route IDs: route-redwood-7bv2-prod; optimize profile attached as JSON in their secure upload.

comments.author: Marco Alvarez
comments.body: Performed non-disruptive canary: reduced batch target to 32 on 10% traffic; observed 5xx rate drop from 3.8% -> 0.9% and 99p latency improvement from 1.9s -> 730ms. Next recommended step: disable kv_prefix_cache on 10% and monitor.

comments.author: Priya Singh
comments.body: Noted memory fragmentation on node gpu-43b; recommended single-node restart after canary validation.

comments.author: Marco Alvarez
comments.body: Preparing formal rollback playbook in ticket and coordinating maintenance window with customer for disabled quant profile revert if needed.

title: KaiaWorks: preserve throughput while unwinding latency-first quant + adaptive-batching profile
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports a sustained increase in 95p tail latency and a small but consistent accuracy drop after enabling a latency-first quantization profile plus adaptive batching and KV-prefix caching on their Dedicated pool. They want guidance for a tactical unwind that minimizes throughput loss and cost regression while restoring latency/accuracy to baseline.

Impact:
- KaiaWorks production traffic (dedicated) experiencing 95p token latency spikes from ~120ms -> ~340ms for long-chat sessions.
- Up to 8% increase in failed streaming sessions (client disconnects observed).
- Measured perplexity/response-quality delta ~+2.6% on our internal prompt set.

Environment:
- Dedicated GPU pool in us-east serving tenant-kaiaworks.
- Model variants: redwood-rlm-7b (primary), fallback llama2-13b-chat.
- Recent change: applied profile "latency-first-quant-v2" + enabled adaptive-batching...

comments.body: 08 UTC - Aisha Patel: Created ticket after initial triage with KaiaWorks. Included telemetry slice and requested immediate guidance for safe unwind.
32 UTC - Marco Lin: Noted hypothesis around KV-eviction churn. Will prepare staged plan and request permission to run shadow tests on a small route.
05 UTC - KaiaWorks (Elena Torres): Confirmed they prefer staged approach and to avoid full revert unless necessary. Approved 10% shadow for step 2 and immediate safety gate for step 1.
20 UTC - Marco Lin: Implemented per-tenant circuit breaker cap on max_batch. Monitoring dashboard link: https://console.redwood.com/dash/kaiaworks/changes/746209
45 UTC - SRE Team (Jordan Mills): Observed initial improvement in batch-size tail but KV hitrate unchanged. Recommending proceeding to soft-demote quant profile in shadow.
10 UTC - Marco Lin: Scheduled soft-demote for 2026-03-13 12:00 UTC on 10% traffic. Customer notified.

title: Slot eviction cascade triggered by ephemeral logging flood in customer's dedicated pool causing sustained p99 throughput regression
body:
Issue summary:
During midday traffic on 2026-03-09 Tellara Health observed a sustained p99 throughput regression and increased error tail for batched text-generation routes on their dedicated pool. Customers reported timeouts and elevated latency for short-stream and batched routes. Impact was observed across their pinned model variants.

Impact:
- Customer-facing throughput p99 increased from ~220ms to >2.1s for batched routes for ~45 minutes.
- Several requests timed out and triggered client-side retries, increasing downstream load.
- Autoscaler triggered multiple partial shrink/grow cycles and several slot evictions.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool id: dp-tellara-02
- Region: us-east
- Models pinned: gpt-open-8b v1, redwood-1.2 v3
- Serving runtime: 2026.02.11-canary build

Steps to reproduce (observed pattern):
1. During the incident window a customer-side background job emitted a ...

comments.author: Aisha Patel ()
comments.body: Initial triage - customer reports p99 latency spike and timeouts. Gathering logs and pool id. Escalated to SRE oncall.

comments.author: Lynn Chen (SRE)
comments.body: Confirmed sustained allocator eviction logs for dp-tellara-02; observed NVMe write latency spikes on 6 hosts. Marking Sev1 and notifying infra team.

comments.author: Tellara (customer)
comments.body: Noted we recently rolled out telemetry sidecar v2.3 with debug=true; investigating config. Asking for temporary mitigation steps.

comments.author: Lynn Chen (SRE)
comments.body: Mitigation applied: isolated noisy hosts from the dedicated pool via host-drain (prevent new attaches) and increased autoscaler cool-down to 5m to avoid oscillation. Notifying customer to rollback sidecar debug flag.

comments.author: Tellara (customer)
comments.body: Rolled back sidecar debug flag; monitoring shows reduced disk write QPS. Thank you.

comments.author: Platform
comments.body: Submitted a follow-up patch to allocator to make eviction threshold more robust to transient heartbeat jitter: PR #1428 opened. Will coordinate rollout to canary first.

comments.author: Lynn Chen (SRE)
comments.body: Observed steady state restored, prefix/kv hit ratio returning to normal and p99 latency back to baseline. Closing immediate incident but leaving follow-up tasks.
