title: Safety review for recent optimizer change + guided rollback and explainability request
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reported throughput degradation and increased 5xx error rate after applying a tenant-scoped optimizer profile that enabled async-batching + kv-prefetch + a quantization profile (mixed-precision) on 2026-03-11 22:40 UTC. Request: assist with safety review, provide an explainability summary of why the optimizer decision increased tail latency for this tenant, and walk through a validated rollback that preserves session continuity and KV cache correctness.

Impact:
- VentureWell (dedicated) is seeing 15-35% increase in p95 latency and intermittent 502/504 from serving nodes for long-session chat flows.
- Approx 40% of their requests hit the model variants that were reconfigured; affects production traffic for their core chat endpoint.

Environment:
- Redwood hosted dedicated capacity (prod-us-west cluster gw-23)
- Affected models: redwood-v2-large (primary), llama...

normalized.description:
Issue summary:
Customer reported throughput degradation and increased 5xx error rate after applying a tenant-scoped optimizer profile that enabled async-batching + kv-prefetch + a quantization profile (mixed-precision) on 2026-03-11 22:40 UTC. Request: assist with safety review, provide an explainability summary of why the optimizer decision increased tail latency for this tenant, and walk through a validated rollback that preserves session continuity and KV cache correctness.

Impact:
- VentureWell (dedicated) is seeing 15-35% increase in p95 latency and intermittent 502/504 from serving nodes for long-session chat flows.
- Approx 40% of their requests hit the model variants that were reconfigured; affects production traffic for their core chat endpoint.

Environment:
- Redwood hosted dedicated capacity (prod-us-west cluster gw-23)
- Affected models: redwood-v2-large (primary), llama...

comments.author: Maya Chen ()
comments.body: Initial triage completed. Collected Grafana and Sentry links. Customer reports the change was applied as part of a cost-optimization push.

comments.author: Rajesh Patel (SRE)
comments.body: Confirmed p95 spike on gw-23 at 22:45 UTC. Observed GPU memory churn and elevated kv-prefetch attempt rates. Tentatively agree mixed-precision + prefetch interaction is likely cause. Preparing rollback playbook.

comments.author: Customer Rep - Alicia Park
comments.body: We need a rollback that doesn't break user sessions; can you advise which endpoints will be affected and an ETA for baseline restoration?

comments.author: Maya Chen ()
comments.body: Proposed staged rollback above. If we follow the gate and only redirect new sessions initially, in-flight sessions will remain on current path until reconnect; we'll share SDK guidance to rehydrate after toggle. ETA for baseline restoration expected 30-60 minutes assuming no OOM spirals during restart.

comments.author: Rajesh Patel (SRE)
comments.body: Executing Step 1 now: routing new traffic to fallback variant. Monitoring panels: https://grafana.redwood/internal/d/optim/tenant-ventruewell-p95 (live).

comments.author: Rajesh Patel (SRE)
comments.body: After routing new traffic, observed immediate drop in new-session p95; however, older in-flight sessions still show stalls. Proceeding with Step 2 to toggle optimizer flag for tenant.

comments.author: Maya Chen ()
comments.body: Noted a handful of 502s during pod restarts; we are restarting per-process as planned and tracking metrics. Customer notified via #support and given a short status update.

normalized.cleanBody:
Issue summary:
Customer reported throughput degradation and increased 5xx error rate after applying a tenant-scoped optimizer profile that enabled async-batching + kv-prefetch + a quantization profile (mixed-precision) on 2026-03-11 22:40 UTC. Request: assist with safety review, provide an explainability summary of why the optimizer decision increased tail latency for this tenant, and walk through a validated rollback that preserves session continuity and KV cache correctness.

Impact:
- VentureWell (dedicated) is seeing 15-35% increase in p95 latency and intermittent 502/504 from serving nodes for long-session chat flows.
- Approx 40% of their requests hit the model variants that were reconfigured; affects production traffic for their core chat endpoint.

Environment:
- Redwood hosted dedicated capacity (prod-us-west cluster gw-23)
- Affected models: redwood-v2-large (primary), llama...

title: Throughput regression after optimizer config change — request stepwise rollback & validation checklist
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports a sudden ~30% throughput drop and increased queue latency after applying an optimizer profile "opt-v2-conservative" (auto-batching + conservative KV cache TTL + new quantization profile) during a scheduled maintenance. Impact: production traffic for VendrAI (dedicated pool) sees elevated tail latency and throttled throughput; some jobs timing out.

Impact:
- Elevated p95/p99 token latency across chat and batch generation routes.
- Throughput down ~25-35% versus baseline for the last 12 hours.
- No 5xx errors observed, but requests are queuing and some client timeouts reported.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: vendrai-dedicated-pool-us-east
- Region: us-east-1
- Models: gpt-4o-mini (chat), redwood/llama-2-13b-quant (batch embeddings)
- Changes applied: optimizer profile "opt-v2-conservative" pushed at 2026-03-09 02:15 UTC via Redwood Console rollout (cana...

comments.body: 03 UTC: Thanks for reporting, VendrAI. We are triaging and will validate a safe rollback path. Can you confirm whether you have a pinned model variant or rely on automatic fallback?

comments.author: VendrAI (Customer) 2026-03-10 10
comments.body: 12 UTC: We do not pin variants; we accepted the global rollout. We can route 10% of traffic to a pinned earlier variant if you provide instructions.

comments.author: Samantha Li (SRE) 2026-03-10 11
comments.body: 00 UTC: Initial findings: KV cache hit-rate collapse is the primary suspect. Suggest rolling back TTL to previous value (300s) on canary subset and monitor p95/p99. Preparing rollback checklist.

comments.author: Marco Reynolds ( - Optimize) 2026-03-10 12
comments.body: 02 UTC: Quant profile q4_0 is relatively new; observed higher memory working set in internal perf tests. Recommend fallback to q4_1 (safer) for affected model while investigating q4_0 behavior.

comments.author: Aisha Patel () 2026-03-10 12
comments.body: 30 UTC: VendrAI approved a staged rollback (canary first). Scheduling rollback at 2026-03-10 14:00 UTC with monitoring window. Customer requested documented validation steps and rollback checklist.

comments.author: VendrAI (Customer) 2026-03-10 13
comments.body: 05 UTC: Confirmed. Also asking for guidance on how to safely test optimizer changes in future without global impact.

title: Fast failover guidance and snapshot-assisted rollback for mixed quantization + tenant-batching profile
body: Issue summary: After applying a tenant-scoped optimizer profile (quantization v2 + aggressive per-request batching overrides) for FinSight Analytics, they observed a 2–5x increase in tail latency for streaming generation requests. Impact: production customers experienced timeouts and degraded UX on chat endpoints during a morning trading window. Environment: dedicated pool (tenanted) in us-west with GPU type A100-80GB, serving model redwood/llama3-8b-quant-v2. Observed: increased p95/p99 token latency to >2s per token, intermittent 502/504s for streamed responses. Expected: modest latency reduction from quantization plus batching. This ticket requests step-by-step safe rollback guidance that minimizes request loss and preserves KV cache state where possible.

comments.body: 05 Priya Shah (Support): Created snapshot and started canary. Notifying SRE and customer. Slack: #support-finsight.
18 Ramon Ortega (SRE): Canary p95 looks promising. Suggest disabling batching override first - less disruptive to token cache contents.
38 FinSight Ops (customer): We see UI improvements on the canary traces. Please proceed with disable batching override.
45 Liam O'Reilly (Support Engineer): Disabled batching override and pinned quant profile to v1.3. Monitoring continues; no new 504s for 20m.
12 Anika Mehta (Opt-Team): Logged INT-4522 to add a compatibility preflight check in optimizer service. PR referenced: https://github.com/redwood-inference/optimizer/pull/4187

04 Priya Shah (Support): Customer confirmed baseline behavior restored and asked for a short runbook we can provide to their ops team. Assigned follow-up to Solutions Engineer for a tenant-facing checklist.

title: Private deployment requires strict runtime version pinning + verifiable artifacts (checksums/signatures/SBOM) for change control
body:
Customer request (Helio Health, Private):

They require:
1) Ability to pin Redwood Serving Runtime to an exact version (no floating tags / no implicit upgrades) across both Private installer and Helm-based installs.
2) Verifiable release artifacts for audit/change-control: SHA256 checksums for images/binaries, signed provenance (preferred), and an SBOM or equivalent bill-of-materials artifact.
3) Documentation that maps an artifact to the originating commit/tag + release notes, and a procedure to verify integrity offline.

Business impact:
- Blocker for production approval. Their security/compliance team will not sign off on a deployment where runtime images can drift or where artifact integrity cannot be independently verified.

Current state observed by customer:
- Installer defaults to channel-based latest (or documentation implies latest) without a hard pin.
- Helm values allow sp...

3) Documentation that maps an artifact to the originating commit/tag + release notes, and a procedure to verify integrity offline.

Business impact:
- Blocker for production approval. Their security/compliance team will not sign off on a deployment where runtime images can drift or where artifact integrity cannot be independently verified.

Current state observed by customer:
- Installer defaults to channel-based latest (or documentation implies latest) without a hard pin.
- Helm values allow sp...

comments.body: 18 (Dev Patel, Support): Acknowledged the request and confirmed we can support explicit version pinning for Private. Looping in Release Eng (Connor O’Brien) and Private packaging (Bruno Silva) to confirm exact supported flags/values and artifact verification path. Linked to SUP-18421 (LTS availability request) for context.

42 (Connor O’Brien, Release Eng): For LTS line we’re standardizing on tags like runtime-1.18.1-lts (exact naming per ADR-027). We can provide per-release checksums and provenance metadata as part of the LTS artifact publish step. We’re landing repo changes under runtime artifact provenance + checksums (PR-23305) and SBOM generation (PR-23402).

07 (Owen Phillips, Support): Clarifying questions back to customer: Are they using Private installer, Helm, or both? Do they need offline verification (air-gapped) or can they fetch checksums from an HTTPS endpoint at install time? Do they require signatures (cosign) or is SHA256 + provenance sufficient?

26 (Customer, Helio Health): They use both (installer for base platform, Helm for runtime upgrades). Primary environment is not fully air-gapped, but verification must be possible without calling out to public registries at runtime. They prefer signatures (cosign/sigstore) but will accept SHA256 + attestation if Redwood documents the process. They need a stable URL pattern to retrieve checksums for a given patch and a retention statement (how long artifacts remain available).

03 (Sahana Iyer, CI/Build): Build pipeline can publish: (1) artifact manifest, (2) SHA256SUMS, (3) provenance JSON referencing git SHA + build workflow run, and (4) optional SBOM artifact. Signing keys access is being set up for release branches (see INT-3302). For now, SHA256 + provenance is immediately available; signature rollout is in progress but not guaranteed for the first patch.

55 (Bruno Silva, Private installer/Helm): Private installer changes are in redwood-private-installer PR-663 to support pinning runtime image to an LTS patch and validating checksums. Helm chart work is in PR-388 to expose an explicit LTS channel/tag value and surface release notes URL annotation. Once merged, we can provide customer with examples:
- Installer: set runtime.image.tag=1.18.1-lts and enable verifyChecksums=true with provided SHA256 file.
- Helm: values.yaml sets runtime.image.tag=1.18.1-lts plus releaseNotesUrl annotation.

20 (Hanae Suzuki, Vuln response coordination): From security/compliance perspective, the recommended customer-facing guidance should include: artifact-to-commit mapping, how CVEs are communicated in LTS notes, and the security patch SLA for LTS. For now, direct them to the reproducible builds + artifact signing doc and LTS release notes format; we’ll follow up with signature details once finalized.

37 (Owen Phillips, Support): Sent customer interim guidance: pin exact runtime tag (1.18.1-lts) and verify SHA256 checksums published alongside the release artifacts. Provided a draft snippet for verification (sha256sum -c SHA256SUMS) and advised to store SHA256SUMS internally for audit. Noted signatures are in-flight, SBOM support pending merge of PR-23402.

04 (Connor O’Brien, Release Eng): Confirmed first LTS patch publish includes checksums/provenance; release notes will include a dedicated 'Artifact verification' block referencing SHA256SUMS and the provenance artifact. Asking Support to ensure customer sees that the pin is per-patch and not automatically updated.

11 (Owen Phillips, Support): Customer confirmed the documented pinning + checksum workflow meets their immediate change-control requirement, pending SBOM availability for final compliance review. Marking ticket resolved; keeping an internal follow-up to notify once SBOM is GA for LTS artifacts.

title: Plus-sign ('+') in SSO attribute gets decoded to space by ingress, breaking RBAC role bindings in Private deployment
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports that a subset of users lose admin/console access after SSO login to their Private deployment. The Identity Provider is sending a multi-value attribute named 'memberRoles' where values are joined by a '+' character (e.g. 'org:123+role:owner+team:infra'). After login the platform's RBAC engine receives spaces instead of '+' characters, which prevents proper role binding lookup and causes 403 on admin console routes while other non-privileged API calls succeed.

Impact:
- Several customer admins (approx 6 users) cannot access the admin console and get 403 on /console/* and /api/admin/* endpoints after SSO login.
- No data loss.
- Customer reports this is blocking an upcoming compliance audit (high business impact).

Environment:
- Redwood Private deployment on customer-managed VPC (on-prem gateway).
- Ingress: NGINX 1.19 reverse proxy in front of the priva...

comments.body: 22 Priya Nair (Support): Ticket opened after customer reported 403s for admin users; collected saml assertion and NGINX config. Escalating to SRE and Private Deploy engineering.
05 Lina Chen (Platform Eng): Initial triage - confirmed '+'->' ' observed in auth logs. Suspect ingress body decoding. Asked customer for raw packet capture and ingress plugin list.

30 Customer (DataNimbus SSO team - Aaron Patel): Provided NGINX config and noted custom lua body filter introduced last month for legacy telemetry. Can rollback if necessary but prefer a product-side mitigation.

15 Lina Chen (Platform Eng): Customer performed rollback of lua filter; after rollback, control-plane received '+' correctly. Provided temporary config diff and instructions. Marked as workaround implemented.

40 Priya Nair (Support): Notified customer that engineering will schedule a follow-up to implement a defensive fix. Created INT-4321 for engineering work to add normalization tolerance in auth parser.
30 Aaron Patel (Customer): Confirms admin access restored and appreciates guidance. Requests ETA on product-level fix and asks for a short runbook for their infra team.
00 Lina Chen (Platform Eng): ETA for product patch proposed: target private-control-plane v2.8.3 (tentative) including a safe-mode flag. Linked PR and draft runbook.

title: Unexpected routing to cold/slow model variant causing elevated latency and request timeouts for customer NovaHealth
body:
Issue summary:
Customer NovaHealth reported a sudden increase in inference latency (p90 jumping from ~120ms to 2.5s) and intermittent request timeouts for chat completions between 2026-03-09T18:00Z and 2026-03-10T03:00Z. They observed a correlation with our fallback routing events where requests were routed to what appears to be a slower model variant (apparently cold or not warmed on Dedicated pool).

Impact:
- Multiple customer API requests returned 504/timeouts and streaming sessions dropped after ~10s.
- Overall p95/p99 latency elevated causing partial degradation of NovaHealth's production feature.
- Customer on Dedicated plan; expects reserved performance SLAs.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: dp-novahealth-us-east-1 (GPU A100 clusters)
- API: hosted endpoint (redwood.api.com) invoking model redwood/gpt-3.8-optimized with fallback variants available
- Observed regions: us-east-1
...

comments.body: Confirmed routing logs show fallback to -vcold variants. Autoscaler did not pre-warm due to conservative policy. Attaching trace IDs in related_github_prs.

comments.author: Elena Park (Platform)
comments.body: We rolled the routing change in INT-1182 to add additional fallback variants. Narrowing scope looks like the right short-term fix for Dedicated pools.

comments.author: Aisha Khan ()
comments.body: Customer confirmed errors returned to baseline after allow_fallback=false. Preparing communication and refund calculation for affected window.

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: Proposal: auto-disable cost-based fallback for endpoints tagged dedicated/enterprise. Will draft RFC.

title: EU-West latency spikes impacting Dedicated tier (p95/p99 regressions on chat + embeddings)
body:
Customer report (via Enterprise Support): Lattice Health’s Dedicated deployment in EU-West is seeing intermittent latency spikes starting ~2026-02-03 08:40 UTC.

Impacted endpoints:
- /v1/chat/completions (streaming + non-streaming)
- /v1/embeddings

Customer-reported symptoms:
- p95 chat latency jumping from ~900–1200ms to 4–7s for 10–20 minute intervals
- p99 > 12s during worst windows
- No sustained 5xx increase, but elevated client-side timeouts due to long tail latency

Business impact:
- Production clinician note summarization flow timing out (customer says they have a 10s overall timeout in their app)
- They are concerned this violates Dedicated latency commitments and asked for an SLO view + explanation of how it’s computed.

Initial scope/notes:
- Region: eu-west only (customer confirms us-east Dedicated looks normal)
- Models: rw-llama-70b-instruct and rw-embed-v3 (both used...

Initial scope/notes:
- Region: eu-west only (customer confirms us-east Dedicated looks normal)
- Models: rw-llama-70b-instruct and rw-embed-v3 (both used...

comments.body: 12 UTC — Rachel Kim (Reporter): Customer reports started 08:40 UTC. Requested 10 example request IDs + timestamps and whether streaming disconnects occurred. Customer says mostly timeouts; no obvious disconnects.

20 UTC — Markus Klein (Solutions Engineering): Confirmed customer’s org is on Dedicated pool eu-west-ded-07. They are pinned to rw-llama-70b-instruct@2026-01-30 and rw-embed-v3@2026-01-15. No customer-side deploy reported this morning.

34 UTC — Aisha Bello (Assignee): Pulled tiered SLO dashboard (Dedicated > EU-West > Chat/Completions). p95 and p99 elevated starting ~08:45 UTC; success rate stable. This looks like saturation / queueing rather than error spikes. Adding annotation for investigation start. Linking runbook: error-budget-breach-mitigation-runbook.

41 UTC — Neha Kapoor (cc via on-call): Please confirm whether this is isolated to one Dedicated pool or region-wide EU-West Dedicated. If budget burn crosses fast window thresholds, treat as incident per new process (even if customer only).

49 UTC — Peter Holtz (Overload/failover SRE): GPU queue depth on eu-west-ded-07 spiked. Headroom dipped below target during burst. Not seeing kernel errors. Suspect autoscaling lag + batcher config too aggressive for this traffic mix (more long prompts than baseline).

02 UTC — Aisha Bello: Checked routing/control-plane changes: no policy change in last 24h for this account. However, EU-West Hosted traffic also elevated due to a marketing event; possible shared infra contention at gateway layer or network egress. Need to confirm Dedicated isolation boundaries. Looping Mina Haddad (Routing) + Neha’s infra on-call.

18 UTC — Mina Haddad (Routing): Verified tier tags are correct at ingress for this account. No cross-tier fallback to Hosted for Dedicated. Dedicated requests remained within dedicated pool, but shared API gateway in eu-west showed elevated CPU. Suggest temporarily shifting this account to dedicated gateway slice if available.
31 UTC — Ethan Park (Control Plane): Confirmed no control-plane policy evaluation latency issues (p99 < 25ms). The spikes are downstream (serving queue + gateway).

44 UTC — Neha Kapoor: Approving mitigation: (1) add capacity to eu-west-ded-07, (2) reduce max batch for chat route for this account until headroom stabilizes, (3) if needed, move to eu-west-ded-09 pool with more free GPUs. Capture changes as annotations on SLO dashboard.

05 UTC — Sergio Costa (Dashboards/alerts): Added customer-specific filter to Dedicated tier SLO dashboard and shared internal link. Also confirmed burn-rate alert fired (fast window) for Dedicated EU-West Chat latency SLO; incident bot posted summary in #eng-sre.

22 UTC — Aisha Bello: Mitigation applied: reduced max_batch_tokens for rw-llama-70b-instruct in this pool, and adjusted continuous batching aggressiveness for the chat endpoint. Latency improving within 5–10 minutes; watching tail.

40 UTC — Infra on-call (via Aisha): Added 8x GPUs to eu-west-ded-07 (capacity add completed). Queue depth down; p95 normalized. Remaining p99 spikes likely from warm KV cache misses after the burst subsided.

06 UTC — Rachel Kim: Updated customer: we identified regional saturation in the Dedicated pool during burst; added capacity and tuned batching. Asked them to re-test and share any remaining request IDs. Also informed them we can provide an SLO view in Console (Dedicated Reliability) once access is provisioned.

18 UTC — Customer (via Rachel): Confirmed improvements; p95 back to ~1.1s. They still see occasional p99 ~9–10s but less frequent. Requesting ongoing visibility and whether these events count against their SLA.

00 UTC — Aisha Bello: p99 still elevated during short bursts; suggests autoscaling reaction time + burst buffering. Proposed follow-up: reserve additional headroom in EU-West Dedicated pools and set tighter alerting for pool headroom before SLO impact. Linking capacity mitigation guidelines page.

10 UTC — Logan Wright (Telemetry): Confirmed the new tiered SLO dashboards are correctly attributing to Dedicated and not mixing Hosted. No cardinality issues observed after enabling account-level view for this customer (uses hashed account id label).

27 UTC — Rachel Kim: Customer asked how retries/fallback affect SLO. Replied with summary: latency SLI is end-to-end from gateway receive to final token (or response end), and Dedicated does not fallback to Hosted; retries are accounted per request attempt; client aborts excluded only when we can attribute a client-side cancel (per SLI standard).

42 UTC — Aisha Bello: Implemented preventative change: pre-scale threshold lowered for eu-west-ded pools, plus a temporary headroom target increase for business hours. No further fast-window burn since 2026-02-03. Marking incident mitigated; leaving ticket open until CS confirms SLO reporting access delivered.

55 UTC — Rachel Kim: Access to Dedicated SLO dashboard granted to CS/SE via INT-9331; we can share screenshots/exports in customer QBR. Customer confirms stable latency past 48h. Closing as resolved with follow-ups tracked separately.

title: Model promote triggers cascading fallbacks during prefix-cache warmup, causing elevated latency and 5xxs
body:
Issue summary:
During an automated promote from canary -> stable for rwd-base-7b-v2 at 03/13/2026 02:40 UTC, the serving runtime returned a series of cascading fallbacks to older model variants for a portion of traffic. Root cause appears tied to prefix/KV cache warming behavior: newly promoted replicas were marked healthy and only accepted traffic before their KV cache prefixes were sufficiently populated, leading to high initial KV cache miss rates, elevated generation latencies, and a spike in internal 5xx errors from the serving runtime.

Impact:
- FoldHealth (dedicated customer) experienced ~30m of elevated median latency (token generation 2x normal) and a 6% increase in 5xx rates on their /v1/generate route.
- Some requests were routed to fallback model variants as the router enforced latency SLO-based failover, resulting in reduced output consistency for ~12% of sessions during...

title: Validate and temporarily pause aggressive per-tenant batching/cache/quant profiles; provide traffic-split mitigation and safe restore
body:
Issue summary: Customer BrightHealth AI reports recurring 95p latency spikes after we applied an aggressive per-tenant optimization bundle (large batch size + KV prefetch + int8-like quant profile). Impact: High - production traffic sees repeated 95th/99th percentile tail latency > 1.2s causing downstream request timeouts and a customer SLA alert. They want a low-risk way to pause the tenant's optimization set, shift a small percentage of traffic to a stable profile, and then validate behavior before a full restore.

We need support-run commands and an ordered playbook that avoids mass restart or global config changes. Customer prefers zero downtime for most users; willing to accept a small controlled traffic split.

comments.author: Asha Patel (2026-03-10)
comments.body: Customer filed initial request after two incidents where 95p latency exceeded SLA. They requested a non-disruptive mitigation path and checklist.

comments.author: Marcus Lee (2026-03-11)
comments.body: Confirmed tenant's optimizer snapshot exists (s3://redwood-backups/brighthealth/optimizers/2026-03-09.json). Drafted stepwise soft-pause commands and test traffic-split rule. Assigned to on-call SRE for execution window.

comments.author: Elena Gomez (SRE) (2026-03-12)
comments.body: Executed 10% traffic split at 02:20 UTC. 10% bucket (stable profile) 95p dropped from 1.25s -> 360ms. 90% bucket still saw spikes. Recommended progressive ramp after 30m steady-state.

comments.author: Customer Rep - Tom Rivera (BrightHealth) (2026-03-12)
comments.body: Approved 10% test; requests that we avoid full quant profile rollback due to latency-sensitive models for some endpoints.

comments.author: Marcus Lee (2026-03-13)
comments.body: Increased split to 50% after stable metrics. No errors observed. Preparing final rollback and restore checklist.

comments.author: Finance Notice (2026-03-13)
comments.body: Potential compute cost increase during stable-profile period flagged to finance; customer notified.

title: Intermittent truncation & 95p latency spikes after enabling aggressive kv_prefetch + mixed-quant profile on Dedicated pool
body: Customer ClearedSky enabled an aggressive optimizer profile (kv_prefetch=high, dynamic_batching=aggressive, quant_profile=mixed-int8-fp16) on their Dedicated capacity pool to reduce per-token cost. After rollout (traffic split 20%->60% over 6 hours) they report intermittent streaming truncations (response cuts before EOS) and a 95th-percentile token-generation latency spike from ~120ms to 420ms on long-sequence requests. Issue affects both chat and streaming generation endpoints. Customer is requesting prioritized assistance for a safe, stepwise rollback plan and an immediate mitigation to restore SLOs while preserving tenant state snapshots.

title: Long-running model-validation sweep processes crowding dedicated GPUs leading to autoscaler starvation and throughput degradation
body:
Issue summary:
Customer GlacierAI reports recurring midday throughput drops in their dedicated pool starting 2026-03-10. Symptoms include prolonged queue growth, increased p50/p99 latency for batched generation routes, and autoscaler failing to add capacity despite sustained queue depth.

Impact:
- GlacierAI observed ~40% drop in throughput for critical routes during business hours, impacting production SLAs.
- Requests were either queued for >2s or returned 503 when autoscaler failed to attach GPUs quickly.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: glc-dedicated-us-east-7 (reserved capacity)
- Region: us-east-1
- Time window: recurring 11:30-14:30 UTC on 2026-03-10 through 2026-03-13
- Models: open-llama-13b (primary), redwood-qa-model-v1 (fallback)

Steps to reproduce (customer):
1) Send sustained mix of long-lived validation streams (keep-alive with intermittent tokens, ~2–4 requests/s per s...

comments.author: Olivia Chen ()
comments.body: Customer reports throughput drop and provided sample request IDs and sidecar logs. Asked for pool and node IDs.

comments.author: Marcus Patel (SRE)
comments.body: Pulled autoscaler attach logs and pool metrics; noticed attach_success_rate drop and many slots marked in-use with low GPU utilization. Created INT-3321 to track internal root-cause analysis.

comments.author: GlacierAI (Customer)
comments.body: Confirmed that pausing their nightly validation sweep temporarily restored throughput; sweep resumes then degradation returns. Asked for recommended configuration to avoid disruption.

comments.author: Serving Runtime
comments.body: Investigating sidecar checkpointing behavior. Found sidecar thread dumping at checkpoint intervals correlating with short NVMe spikes. Hypothesis: checkpoint writer holds GPU-affiliate locks causing attach latency.

comments.author: Marcus Patel (SRE)
comments.body: Deployed temporary routing deprioritization for user-agent 'glacier-validate' to best-effort pool; customer routes to critical capacity restored. Monitoring for recurrence.

comments.author: Aaron Weiss
comments.body: Agreed on two-prong fix: (1) autoscaler heuristic patch to ignore long-held attachments below a CPU/GPU-util threshold, (2) sidecar patch to make checkpoint writer non-blocking with backoff. Assigned follow-up PRs.

title: Unexpected routing to cold/warmup variant increases P95/P99 latency and causes request timeouts for Sparrows Analytics
body:
Issue summary:
Sparrow Analytics reported a sudden spike in P95/P99 inference latency culminating in request timeouts (HTTP 504) for production routes using the redwood-llama-70b-quant-16k profile. Impact was intermittent but reproducible and correlated with traffic spikes and autoscaler events.

Impact:
- Multiple customer API requests returned 504 within a 30-minute window on 2026-03-10 03:20-03:50 UTC.
- Customer-facing latency SLOs breached (P95 > 2s, P99 > 8s; typical baseline P95 ~ 300ms).
- Affected critical job pipelines at Sparrow causing user-facing errors.

Environment:
- Prod cluster: us-east-1, serving fleet for dedicated customers.
- Routes were configured with a fallback policy: prefer lowest-cost quantized variant, fallback to compatible warm variant on capacity constraints.

Steps to reproduce:
1) Replay Sparrow traffic sample (request ids provided below) against prod...

title: Accuracy drift and billing spike after mixed optimizer toggle — stepwise reversion guidance requested
body: Issue summary: BrightHealth reported an increase in per-request cost and a small but measurable NLU accuracy drift after an on-prem tenant-specific optimizer toggle deployed on 2026-03-10. The toggle enabled mixed-precision + aggressive KV prefetch for tenants on Dedicated pools. Impact: ~12% increase in token billing for the tenant's top route, 3–5% drop in exact-match on a customer regression prompt set, and increased 95th-percentile tail latency (from 320ms to 580ms) for sequences >512 tokens. Environment: Dedicated pool in us-east serving tenant brighthealth-prod; deployment applied via tenant optimizer profile v1.3. Steps to reproduce: 1) Send canonical regression prompt set (attached) to model redwood-neo-7b-v2 pinned to profile v1.3. 2) Observe and record per-token billing and EM metrics from console. 3) Compare latency distribution using span traces from the console. Observed ...

comments.author: Marco Delgado (SRE)
comments.body: Ran quick checks on dedicated pool; no GPU errors, but high KV eviction rates observed. Suggested immediate traffic split to stable profile.

comments.author: BrightHealth (Customer)
comments.body: Confirmed rollback of their internal toggles; requesting Redwood assistance for safe profile reversion. Provided regression prompt set.

comments.author: Priya Rao
comments.body: Noted mixed-precision fallback behavior in current runtime; prioritizing patch to reduce fallback latency. Recommended staged revert plan in ticket.

comments.author: Marco Delgado (SRE)
comments.body: Performed tenant snapshot (bh-rollback-0326) and initiated temporary per-route token limits. Monitoring metrics; will start stepwise revert tomorrow morning as planned.

comments.author: Aisha Patel ()
comments.body: Customer scheduled validation call for 2026-03-14 16:00 UTC. Asked CS to share playbook for staged revert and end-to-end checklist.

comments.author: BrightHealth (Customer)
comments.body: Provided acceptance criteria for accuracy: EM loss must be <=1.5% vs baseline and 95p latency <= 400ms for production traffic.

comments.author: Marco Delgado (SRE)
comments.body: Starting Step 3a (disable KV prefetch) at 14:45 UTC; will report telemetry at +30m and +2h.

title: Intermittent burst latency and uneven GPU slot distribution in dedicated pool (bank imbalance)
body:
Issue summary: Multiple StratusDocs tenants in a single dedicated reservation are experiencing intermittent 3–7x request latency spikes during short (30s–2m) burst windows. The spikes correlate to reduced per-GPU throughput despite aggregate GPU utilization being below reservation limits.

Impact: Customer-facing inference endpoints return high tail latencies, causing degraded UX for chat workloads and several user timeouts; revenue impact for StratusDocs is material during peak traffic.

Environment: Dedicated reservation ID dr-78f9 in us-east with 8xA100 nodes, RBAC-policed, autoscaling disabled for the reservation. Affected routes are pinned to redwood-open-vicuna-13b and llama-2-13b-chat.

Observed behavior: During bursts of concurrent short requests (avg tokens per request ~60, 95th concurrency bursts to 120), certain GPUs in the pool show sustained KV cache thrashing and per-slo...

title: Post-optimization latency spike after enabling mixed-quant + aggressive KV prefetch — need safe rollback plan
body: Issue summary: After HealthNow applied the mixed-quant profile (mix-v2) with an aggressive KV prefetch policy and higher batching window, production request P50/P95 latency increased by ~3-6x. Impact: user-facing slowdowns for real-time document summarization flows; elevated error budgets for dedicated capacity pool. Environment: dedicated cluster in us-east, serving model gpt-like-13b-quant-mix-v2. Steps to reproduce: 1) Deploy optimizer profile 'mix-v2' + kv_prefetch: aggressive on tenant HealthNow; 2) Send typical 0.9s median prompt workload with streaming enabled; 3) Observe endpoint latency metrics and per-token time. Observed behavior: sudden increase in tail latency and increased variance on streaming chunk emission. CPU on CPU-side orchestrator spiked briefly; GPU utilization appears lower than baseline. Expected behavior: batching and prefetch should reduce per-token latency/...

comments.author: Aisha Patel
comments.body: Created ticket after HealthNow CS escalated. Customer applied optimizer change yesterday evening and saw degradations at 23:30 UTC. They provided sample trace IDs and a short flamegraph from their observability snapshot.

comments.author: Miguel Torres
comments.body: Confirmed P95 spike on Redwood Console for tenant healthnow-prod (dashboard: /dashboards/tenant/healthnow-prod/optimizer-metrics). Noted repeated host-side serialize spikes and a small number of 5xx retries correlating with the latency spikes.

comments.author: Priya Menon
comments.body: Looked at node metrics in us-east cluster. GPU memory fragmentation visible on a subset of pods running mixed-quant kernels. Observed OOM-kernel fallbacks logged in runtime: 'kernel_fallback=attention_full_precision' in /var/log/serving/runtime.log. Suspect mismatch between quant kernel selector and current attention length distribution.

comments.author: Luca Bianchi
comments.body: Reproduced locally with a traffic replay. When kv_prefetch is enabled aggressively plus batch_window >= 80ms, the runtime groups longer-context requests into mixed batches causing kernel selector to choose a safer (but slower) path. Proposed staged rollback: 1) disable kv_prefetch globally for tenant, 2) force batch_window to baseline, 3) pin model to pre-change variant for 48h while gathering perf traces.

comments.author: Customer (HealthNow - infra@healthnow.com)
comments.body: Confirmed that disabling kv_prefetch and reducing batch window restored acceptable P95 within 10 minutes. Asked for a documented rollback checklist because they run hourly config syncs and want an automated safe-off ramp.

comments.author: Miguel Torres
comments.body: Drafted rollback checklist below and requested SRE safety review. Also flagged potential follow-up: kernel selector telemetry missing for a small number of hosts; we need a bug ticket to add more granular kernel decision logs (INT-XXXX).

title: Guidance requested: safe off-ramp and preflight checks for combined batching + prefix-cache + custom quantization profile
body: Issue summary: BrightDocs enabled a tenant-scoped optimization bundle (aggressive async batching, prefix/KV caching enabled, and a custom quantization profile built from our quant-tool). After a gradual rollout to 20% of traffic, they observed intermittent 5xx errors and request-level tail-latency spikes. Customer requests a prescriptive safe-offramp and preflight checklist to minimize customer traffic disruption while reverting the optimization. Impact: ~10% of requests for the tenant returning 502/503 over a 30-minute window; several high-priority workflows affected (document summarization). Environment: Dedicated pool in us-east with reserved GPUs (G4-like). Steps to reproduce: 1) Tenant runs route with optimizer-profile: {async_batching: aggressive, kv_prefetch: enabled, quant_profile: custom-v3} 2) Synthetic load at 50 RPS with session lengths ~800 tokens 3) Observe intermittent ...

title: Dedicated pool occupancy leak from analytics side-process causing thermal fallback and transient GPU thinness
body: Issue summary: Customer reports periodic sharp throughput drops in their dedicated pool (us-west) between 09:30-10:15 UTC. Symptoms include rising p50/p95/p99 latencies, increased 5xx errors, and transient GPU attach failures. Impact: production inference jobs observe a 40-60% p50 throughput collapse for 10-20 minute windows, affecting user-facing latency-sensitive endpoints. Environment: prod, dedicated pool us-west, affected cluster: redcap-prod-dc-05.

normalized.description: Issue summary: Customer reports periodic sharp throughput drops in their dedicated pool (us-west) between 09:30-10:15 UTC. Symptoms include rising p50/p95/p99 latencies, increased 5xx errors, and transient GPU attach failures. Impact: production inference jobs observe a 40-60% p50 throughput collapse for 10-20 minute windows, affecting user-facing latency-sensitive endpoints. Environment: prod, dedicated pool us-west, affected cluster: redcap-prod-dc-05.

comments.body: 02Z - Miguel Santos (SRE): Confirmed persistent CUDA allocations from a non-inference pid on node redcap-prod-05-gpu0. Captured process stack and sampled memory map.

18Z - Nimbus AI (Customer): We've just rolled a new analytics agent this morning. We'll roll it back to the previous image and report back.
Customer provided stacktrace showing CUDA calls from their agent.
43Z - Aisha Patel (Support): Applied emergency evict rule to remove processes with tag 'sidecar-analytics' from serving nodes for Nimbus tenant. Observed throughput return within 4-6 minutes.
30Z - Erica Zhang (Eng): Scheduled implementation plan for allocator detection in ENG-34521. Will prototype detection algorithm that differentiates inference sessions from background CUDA allocators.
12Z - Miguel Santos (SRE): Lowered autoscaler scale-up cooldown on redcap-prod-dc-05 as temporary safety net. Monitoring continues; no recurrences in 36h.
05Z - Nimbus AI (Customer): Confirmed analytics connector update removed persistent allocations. Performance stable during morning peak. Thank you.

title: Dedicated pool throughput collapse during mid-window: background snapshotter IO suppresses heartbeats leading to GPU allocation gap
body:
Issue summary:
During the mid-day window on 2026-03-10 our customer VetaHealth observed a sharp throughput collapse in their dedicated GPU pool. The pool reported available capacity but the autoscaler did not attach additional nodes for ~7 minutes, causing application p99 latencies to exceed SLO and a backlog of queued requests.

Impact:
- VetaHealth reported 30% drop in served tokens and sustained p99 latency > 3s for 7 minutes.
- Affected: customer dedicated pool in us-east-1 serving model gptx-13b-quant.
- Business impact: user-facing timeouts and failed inference requests for paid workloads.

Environment:
- Redwood dedicated cluster: prod-us-east, pool id pool-ded-0032.
- Autoscaler: predictive + reactive autoscale policy, min 4 GPUs, max 32 GPUs.
- Sidecar snapshotter enabled for fast warm starts.

Steps to reproduce (observed sequence):

Steps to reproduce (observed sequence):
1) Mid-window burst of small short-lived s...

comments.author: Maya Chen ()
comments.body: Customer reported traffic spike and sustained p99. Collected request traces, queue length graphs, and sidecar logs. Escalating to SRE.

comments.author: Arjun Patel (SRE)
comments.body: Observed NVMe write latency spike on pool nodes. Temporarily disabled snapshotter retention job for pool to relieve IO. Forced attach +4 GPUs to drain backlog. Monitoring for recovery.

comments.author: VetaHealth (Customer)
comments.body: Confirmed latency improvement after forced attach. Asked for permanent fix to avoid future production impact.

comments.author: Platform
comments.body: Reproduced heartbeat suppression in staging by running synthetic snapshotter IO during short-stream floods. Heartbeat thread blocked when NVMe latency exceeded threshold. Created issue in runtime to detect blocked heartbeats earlier.

comments.author: Arjun Patel (SRE)
comments.body: Proposed mitigations: (1) move snapshotter IO to lower-priority cgroups to avoid starving heartbeat thread, (2) autoscaler policy change to consider queue growth independent of sidecar heartbeat transient failures, (3) add circuit-breaker to temporarily force attachment if queue growth exceeds X for Y seconds.

comments.author: Maya Chen ()
comments.body: Customer notified of temporary mitigations and timeline for runtime patch. Customer requested postmortem and confirmation of no billed retries due to timeouts.

comments.author: Platform
comments.body: Runtime PR created: github.com/redwood-inference/runtime/pull/6821 - adds watchdog to sidecar heartbeat and records IO-wait context. Leaving this ticket open until deploy and verification in canary.

comments.author: Arjun Patel (SRE)
comments.body: Canary deploy scheduled for 2026-03-14; will coordinate with VetaHealth for warm window.

title: Restore preset and circuit-fence request for optimizer experiment causing latency asymmetry
body:
Issue summary:
Customer applied a tenant-scoped optimizer profile (aggressive batching + KV-prefetch + mixed quant) to a dedicated pool yesterday evening. Since the change, DataWeave Labs reports uneven tail latency and ~15% error increase on multi-turn chat routes. They request: 1) an immediate safe recovery plan that restores known-good defaults for the tenant, and 2) a short 'circuit-fence' routing policy to keep the tenant on conservative serving paths while we validate optimizer flags.

Impact:
- Customer-facing chat agents experiencing sporadic 5xx and increased 95p/99p latency during business hours.
- Traffic volume: ~120 reqs/s sustained, peak 240 reqs/s.

Desired outcome:
- Return tenant to stable performance with minimal disruption to traffic.
- Provide stepwise commands to apply rollback preset and a routing fence (non-destructive, reversible).

comments.author: Maya Chen () 2026-03-10T09
comments.body: 15Z: Customer reports 95p latency up ~60ms and ~8% error rate increase after profile change. Requested safe rollback and routing fence. Attaching telemetry dashboard link: https://console.redwood/dashboards/tenants/dataweave/opt-experiment

comments.author: Derek Patel (SRE) 2026-03-10T10
comments.body: 02Z: I see the same tail spikes on ingestion credit. Starting runbook: recommend applying tenant preset 'stable-serving-v2' and enabling routing.policy=circuit_fence:conservative. Will hand-hold steps.

comments.author: DataWeave Lead (Customer) 2026-03-10T10
comments.body: 18Z: Please minimize downtime — prefer live-config swap without full restart. Happy to shift small % of traffic for canary before full swap.

comments.author: Derek Patel (SRE) 2026-03-10T10
comments.body: 45Z: Agreed. Plan: 1) create snapshot of current tenant optimizer flags 2) apply 'stable-serving-v2' to 10% traffic for 10 minutes 3) ramp to 100% if metrics stable. Will also set routing fence that pins to conservative batching/disable-prefetch.

title: Anycast health-check failure caused regional egress fallback and burst p99 latency
body:
Issue summary:
At ~2026-03-10T02:05:00Z, multiple dedicated customers reported sudden increases in p50/p95/p99 request latency and streaming cancelations. The spike coincided with a change in egress paths where traffic for eu-west POPs was observed to be routing to a distant POP in us-east, then to apac for some flows, increasing TTFB and causing a subset of requests to timeout (504/522).

Impact:
- Quantora Payments (dedicated) experienced ~20% of generation requests timing out for ~18 minutes and p99 latency increased from ~450ms to ~2700ms.
- Several other dedicated customers in eu-west and apac-southeast saw transient tail latency spikes and a small number of 5xx errors.

Environment:
- prod - eu-west primary POP; serving runtime: redwood/llama-2-13b-instruct; API gateway fronted by anycast IPs managed by third-party CDN/anycast provider.

Steps to reproduce (observed pattern):
1)...

comments.author: Customer Success
comments.body: Notified Quantora Payments about mitigation steps and advised temporary region-affinity pinning. Customer confirmed reduced timeouts after pin.

comments.author: via channel
comments.body: Confirmed short-lived probe failures from a subset of their monitoring VMs; automatic withdrawal policy caused route preference change. They are widening their probe origins to include more vantage points.

comments.author: Marco Ruiz (SRE)
comments.body: Rolled back emergency pin for Quantora after provider confirmed stable advertisements and local route reinstatement. Monitoring shows p99 returning to normal by 03:15.

comments.author: Aisha Khan
comments.body: Scheduled post-incident review (PIR) for 2026-03-12; assigned action items for hardening health-check timeout and console emergency controls.

- Quantora Payments (dedicated) experienced ~20% of generation requests timing out for ~18 minutes and p99 latency increased from ~450ms to ~2700ms.
- Several other dedicated customers in eu-west and apac-southeast saw transient tail latency spikes and a small number of 5xx errors.

Environment:
- prod - eu-west primary POP; serving runtime: redwood/llama-2-13b-instruct; API gateway fronted by anycast IPs managed by third-party CDN/anycast provider.

Steps to reproduce (observed pattern):

title: Dedicated pool throughput suppression caused by silent-tenant heartbeat damping
body:
Issue summary: BrightScribe reported a sustained P99 throughput degradation for their dedicated pool during midday on 2026-03-12. Impact: degraded request throughput and elevated queuing leading to increased tail latency for inference requests. Root behaviour appears to be the autoscaler suppressing scale-ups due to misleading heartbeat-derived utilization signals from one or more silent tenants.

Impact details:
- Customer-facing: increased request backpressure, timeouts for a subset of long-lived streams, and ~25% drop in p50 throughput for routed model 'redwood/longform-16k'.
- Internal: autoscaler did not trigger expected burst additions for ~18 minutes despite GPU queue depth > 90% for several allocation groups.

Environment: Dedicated capacity, reserved pool id rp-47, us-east, auto-scale policy 'midday-burst' with predictive window enabled.

Observed by: BrightScribe Support and...

comments.author: Aisha Khan ()
comments.body: Customer reports p99 latencies hitting 18s and a subset of calls timing out. They've pinned rollouts to 'redwood/longform-16k' and are seeing degraded user experience. Asking for priority review.

comments.author: Ben Carter (SRE)
comments.body: Investigating reserved pool rp-47. Autoscaler logs show suppressed scale-ups with 'heartbeat_pulse_low_io' reason. Escalating to serving-runtime and autoscaler owners.

comments.author: Serving
comments.body: Found tenant T-132 active flag set but zero IO. Might be a tenant-side shim/SDK bug keeping TCP connections but not streaming. Requesting customer to check their SDK or sidecar behavior.

comments.author: BrightScribe, Sarah Lee
comments.body:
We deployed a new sidecar last night; will rollback and test. Please advise if we should restart connections.
Customer note: This impacts high-priority flows for our transcription pipeline.

comments.author: Ben Carter (SRE)
comments.body: Manual override applied to rp-47 (+2 slots). Monitoring impact. Initial telemetry shows throughput recovery within 90s.

comments.author: Priya Patel (Autoscaler)
comments.body: Adding follow-up fix to treat 'heartbeat with low outbound packets' as lower confidence for active-weight calculations. We'll propose a configurable threshold to avoid suppression by silent tenants.

comments.author: Aisha Khan ()
comments.body: Customer validated rollback of their sidecar; their traffic returns to normal. Asking for permanent fix and postmortem timeline.

comments.author: Ben Carter (SRE)
comments.body: Creating rollout patch for autoscaler and scheduling canary in staging tomorrow. Preparing postmortem and recommended mitigation steps for customers with sidecars.

title: Tenant preflight: automated fallback script + validation checklist for live optimizer toggles
body:
Issue summary:
BrightPost Health reported sudden 95p latency spikes after enabling a tenant-scoped optimizer bundle (dynamic batching + kv-prefix cache + mixed-precision quant profile) during a gradual rollout. They want an automated, preflightable fallback that can be executed with minimal operator steps and a checklist to validate safe reversion, because their SLA forbids extended degraded latency windows.

Impact:
- Multiple lanes experienced 95p latency >3s for text-generation routes used in user-facing search.
- Customer experiencing urgent business impact (timeout-based retries in downstream services).

Environment:
- Customer: BrightPost Health (dedicated)
- Region: prod us-east
- Models in use: redwood-open-7b-v1 (default), fallback candidate: redwood-open-7b-quant-int8
- Serving runtime version: v5.12.1

Steps to reproduce (observed by customer):
1) Enable tenant optimizer pr...

title: SLO overview card dropping percentile dimension after 30d retention causing flapping alerts
body:
Issue summary:
When viewing BrightBank's SLO Overview card in the Console, the configured percentile dimension (p95) intermittently disappears from the metric series after the 30-day retention boundary is crossed. The chart falls back to showing only p50 and p99. This causes the SLO status indicator and alerting rule (which depends on p95) to flip from OK to No Data and trigger automated alert noise for the customer.

Impact:
- BrightBank is receiving repeated paging for SLO breaches due to missing p95 timeseries data.
- Affects production region us-east for several routes serving the banking app.
- Customer is enterprise tier with SLA; business impact is high (customer-facing latency SLOs).

Environment:
- Console (app.redwood.ai) -> prod/us-east
- Customer account: brightbank-prod (enterprise)
- Relevant dashboards: SLO Overview > Payment API latency (tenant-scoped views)

Steps to ...

comments.author: Asha Patel ()
comments.body: Initial report from BrightBank. Customer reports repeated p95 missing on SLO Overview and resulting alert noise. Attached customer screenshot and alert webhook payload.

comments.author: Liam Chen (SRE)
comments.body: Triage assigned. Reproduced in prod/us-east when time window slides beyond 30d. Hypothesis: retention pruning job deletes raw events and the downstream aggregator drops percentile series if data points fall below heuristics threshold. Escalating to analytics infra.

comments.author: Analytics
comments.body: Investigation in progress. Found that the rollup job that generates 1h aggregates has a misaligned tag for 'percentile=95' which causes it to be excluded when the raw-window is shorter than the aggregation window. Opened PR to fix rollup selection logic. See PR: https://github.com/redwood/inference/pull/1893

comments.author: Liam Chen (SRE)
comments.body: Applied temporary workaround by capping BrightBank's dashboard time range to 29d and advised customer to mute the alert via suppression rule. Customer acknowledged but asked for permanent fix.

comments.author: Analytics
comments.body: PR #1893 ready for QA. Change corrects percentile-tag propagation into rollups and adds a guard to surface a 'partial window' indicator instead of dropping series. Running integration tests against synthetic retention boundary dataset.

comments.author: BrightBank contact
comments.body: Confirmed that with the 29d cap alert noise stopped but they prefer a code fix and rollback path. Asked for ETA for patch to staging and production.

comments.author: Liam Chen (SRE)
comments.body: Noted deployment plan: Canary to staging on 2026-03-14, production rollout 2026-03-17 pending QA. Will update ticket with PR link and rollback steps.

title: Retention policy TTL not being applied to request/response logs in prod us-east (purge lag > configured TTL)
body:
Issue summary:
Northbridge Bank reports that their configured retention policy for request/response logs (TTL = 7 days) does not appear to be enforced in us-east. They are seeing request log entries older than 10 days still present in the Dedicated tenant.

Impact:
- Customer is in an active enterprise security review and needs evidence that retention controls are enforced.
- Operational impact: risk of non-compliance with customer policy until backlog is cleared.
- No serving/runtime impact reported; this is a data lifecycle / purge enforcement issue.

Customer statement (paraphrased):
"We set request log retention to 7 days last week. In us-east we can still query request logs from 10–12 days ago. Is TTL actually running?"

Environment:
- Product: Dedicated
- Region: prod us-east
- Data class: request/response logs (NOT audit logs)
- Customer policy: request_logs.ttl_days=7, prompts...

normalized.description:
Issue summary:
Northbridge Bank reports that their configured retention policy for request/response logs (TTL = 7 days) does not appear to be enforced in us-east. They are seeing request log entries older than 10 days still present in the Dedicated tenant.

Impact:
- Customer is in an active enterprise security review and needs evidence that retention controls are enforced.
- Operational impact: risk of non-compliance with customer policy until backlog is cleared.
- No serving/runtime impact reported; this is a data lifecycle / purge enforcement issue.

Customer statement (paraphrased):
"We set request log retention to 7 days last week. In us-east we can still query request logs from 10–12 days ago. Is TTL actually running?"

Environment:
- Product: Dedicated
- Region: prod us-east
- Data class: request/response logs (NOT audit logs)
- Customer policy: request_logs.ttl_days=7, prompts...

comments.body: 18 PT (Rachel Kim, CS): Opened on behalf of Northbridge Bank. They are blocked in security review; request is to confirm TTL is applied in us-east and provide a short evidence statement + remediation ETA if delayed.
42 PT (Sean Gallagher, SRE): Acknowledged. Pulling retention verification dashboards for us-east. Will also check purge workers health and queue depth.

05 PT (Sean Gallagher, SRE): Found elevated ttl_lag_seconds for request_logs in us-east only. eu-west is normal. purge_rows_deleted dropped sharply 2025-03-10 01:00–05:00 UTC (matches telemetry DB maintenance). purge_failures spiked with error: "context deadline exceeded" from deletion batches.

22 PT (Logan Wright, Telemetry): Confirmed: maintenance window introduced slower deletes due to index rebuild + throttling; purge job framework saw repeated retries and automatically reduced batch size. Backlog accumulated; should recover but may take ~24–48h at current throttle.

31 PT (Ethan Park, Control Plane): Checked retention policy service for tenant. Policy is set correctly (request_logs TTL=7d). No legal hold flags present. Policy evaluator cache updated 2025-03-04; no anomalies.

02 PT (Sean Gallagher, SRE): Proposed mitigation: temporarily increase purge worker concurrency for request_logs in us-east with tighter per-query time limits + off-peak scheduling; also run an on-demand backfill purge up to 'now-7d' for the tenant partitions.

20 PT (Allison Grant, SRE/Obs): +1 as long as we keep DB pressure guardrails. Please set max_rows_per_minute cap and alert on p95 delete latency. Use runbook: runbook-retention-purge-jobs -> 'Handling purge backlog after maintenance'.

10 PT (Sean Gallagher, SRE): Executed controlled backfill purge (job id: purge_request_logs_us-east_2025-03-11T19:58Z). Set concurrency from 2 -> 6 for request_logs only, capped by QPS limiter. Monitoring: ttl_lag_seconds started trending down (from ~410k -> ~260k in first hour).

44 PT (Rachel Kim, CS): Customer asking for: (a) why us-east only, (b) when their view will reflect TTL, (c) whether they can get an evidence snippet for auditors. Please provide a crisp explanation we can forward.

05 PT (Sean Gallagher, SRE): Draft customer-facing summary: 'Retention policy is configured correctly; a temporary purge backlog occurred in us-east following a telemetry database maintenance window. Purge jobs are running and we increased capacity to catch up; expected to return to normal within ~24h. No data access exposure; this is lifecycle deletion timing.'

12 PT (Sean Gallagher, SRE): Backlog reduced further overnight. ttl_lag_seconds now ~55k–80k seconds (~15–22h). purge_rows_deleted steady; purge_failures back to baseline. Expect to be within <6h lag by end of day.

26 PT (Olga Petrov, GRC): For evidence: include (1) tenant retention config export timestamp, (2) screenshot/metric showing ttl_lag decreasing, (3) statement that legal hold not enabled. No need to share internal error logs.

40 PT (Sean Gallagher, SRE): ttl_lag_seconds now ~12k seconds (~3.3h). Considered recovered. Leaving concurrency elevated until 2025-03-13 12:00 PT, then revert to standard. Added note to on-call handoff.
05 PT (Rachel Kim, CS): Customer confirmed: they no longer see >7d request logs in us-east queries as of this morning. They requested a short written confirmation + time window of remediation.

22 PT (Sean Gallagher, SRE): Reverted concurrency to standard; system stable. Provided final wording for customer: 'As of 2025-03-13, request log retention for TTL=7 days is actively enforced in us-east; backlog from 2025-03-10 maintenance has been cleared. Ongoing monitoring in place.'
11 PT (Rachel Kim, CS): Closing the loop. Sent customer confirmation + retention config export and a redacted dashboard screenshot showing ttl_lag returning to normal. Marking resolved.

title: Tenant preflight simulator and surgical rollback plan for mixed optimizer profile changes
body:
Issue summary:
Customer (BlueLeaf Analytics, dedicated) requested guidance and an in-platform dry-run for a tenant-scoped optimizer change that toggles KV prefix caching and applies a more aggressive mixed quantization profile for low-memory GPUs. They want a safe preflight simulation, targeted A/B rollout steps, and a conservative surgical rollback sequence if any downstream latency or quality regressions appear.

Impact:
- Customer reports intermittent 95p tail latency spikes in their production traffic after a recent internal experiment. They have paused global rollout but want a guided re-test with our simulator before reapplying changes.
- Business impact: data-processing pipelines (internal analytics) are sensitive to 95p latency; sustained regressions could force job retries and revenue-impacting SLA breaches.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: dlx-dedicated-us-east-01 (tenant: bl...

title: Private deployment TLS rotation triggered control-plane failover and temporary routing to hosted runtime — potential data exposure for VeritySchools
body:
Issue summary:
On 2026-02-18 at ~08:12 UTC VeritySchools (private deployment, eu-west) reported repeated model responses containing content from other customer sessions. Initial triage showed a control-plane health check spike after the customer's scheduled TLS certificate rotation. The Redwood control plane marked several private nodes as unreachable and policy routing unexpectedly routed a subset of traffic to the hosted inference fleet for failover. That hosted path accepted requests for a short window before route restrictions were applied, resulting in transient telemetry and logs containing request/response fragments that belonged to VeritySchools sessions.

Impact:
- Customer-facing: Multiple VeritySchools sessions observed interleaved transcripts (4 distinct customers reported).
- Security: Potential exposure of session text to hosted runtime telemetry for up to 11 minutes. No...

Issue summary:
On 2026-02-18 at ~08:12 UTC VeritySchools (private deployment, eu-west) reported repeated model responses containing content from other customer sessions. Initial triage showed a control-plane health check spike after the customer's scheduled TLS certificate rotation. The Redwood control plane marked several private nodes as unreachable and policy routing unexpectedly routed a subset of traffic to the hosted inference fleet for failover. That hosted path accepted requests for a short window before route restrictions were applied, resulting in transient telemetry and logs containing request/response fragments that belonged to VeritySchools sessions.

Impact:
- Customer-facing: Multiple VeritySchools sessions observed interleaved transcripts (4 distinct customers reported).
- Security: Potential exposure of session text to hosted runtime telemetry for up to 11 minutes. No...

title: Request: surgical 'quiet-window' pause and safe rollback guidance after tenant enabled aggressive quant + KV-prefetch - tail latency spike
body:
Issue summary:
Customer (OmniScribe, Dedicated) turned on an aggressive optimizer profile (quantized model + KV-prefetch + increased async batching) for low-cost inference during business hours. Shortly after rollout they reported increasing 95/99th tail latency and intermittent streaming disconnects for a subset of keys.

Impact:
Persistent elevated tail latency (95p ~600ms -> 1300ms; 99p spikes >2s) and 0.2% increase in streaming disconnect rates affecting a production tenant with critical SLA. Traffic is steady; packet loss or infra-wide events not observed.

Environment and context:
- Dedicated pool (tier: reserved) in us-east-1.
- Model variants: redwood-7b-v2 (baseline) and a quantized 8-bit LLM build.
- Optimizer profile enabled: kv-prefetch=true, async_batching=aggressive, quant_profile=8-bit-mixed, prefetch_window=64.
- Rollout was tenant-scoped via config snapshot applied 20...

comments.author: Customer (OmniScribe) - 2026-03-09
comments.body: We need a mitigation path - rolling back immediately may affect cost goals but latency is customer-visible and impacting users.

comments.author: Carlos Vega (SRE) - 2026-03-10
comments.body: Hypothesis: KV-prefetch is increasing memory pressure on GPUs under this tenant's prefix distribution, causing cache thrash and increased kernel stalls. Suggest quiet-window + disable kv-prefetch for tenant, then verify latency before touching quantization.

comments.body: Sent step-by-step 'quiesce' playbook draft to customer for approval before runbook execution. Waiting for customer to confirm a 10-minute maintenance window.

comments.author: Customer (OmniScribe) - 2026-03-11
comments.body: Approved maintenance window 2026-03-12 02:00-02:20 UTC. Also requested guidance for partial traffic hedge rather than full cutover.

title: Asymmetric routing via Transit Gateway causing intermittent packet loss and model pull failures for private deployment
body:
Issue summary:
Customer CodaHealth reported intermittent model pull failures and high token latency for private deployment in eu-west-1. Initial symptoms suggested packet loss between the customer's VPC and the Redwood private worker subnets. Impact: several 10-minute windows of degraded inference, model cold-starts failing to fetch weights (S3/registry), and elevated 5xx rates for streaming responses.

Impact:
- Multiple production instances experienced partial outages (model load failures) between 2026-03-10T09:10 and 2026-03-10T11:05 UTC.
- Observed spikes in TCP retransmits and connection resets; requests to model registry timed out or failed with TLS errors.
- Customer-facing latency increased by ~400-600ms for affected requests; some requests hit 5xx.

Environment:
- Redwood Private deployment (customer-managed VPC attached to Redwood Transit Gateway)
- Region: eu-west-1
- Custo...

comments.body: 25Z - Aisha Khan (Support): We see repeated SYN retransmits from workers to registry IPs. Opening bridge with SRE. Customer: @CodaHealth, are you seeing any maintenance on your TGW attachments?
45Z - CodaHealth (Elena, NetOps): No planned maintenance. We'll provide route tables and TGW attachment IDs via secure share.

10Z - Miguel Ortega (SRE): Preliminary root cause is asymmetric routing due to missing propagation entries into central TGW route table. Recommending temporary explicit route entries and enabling propagation on customer's attachment.
40Z - Elena (CodaHealth): We've enabled propagation and added route entries per SRE guidance. Running tests now.
05Z - Miguel Ortega (SRE): Tests show symmetric routing restored, retransmits drop to baseline. Monitoring for 30 minutes - marking as resolved unless further regression.
30Z - Aisha Khan (Support): Follow-up with customer to confirm stability over 48h. Customer confirmed no further incidents and closed the case.

title: warmup-lag-gpu-starvation-on-bursty-short-streams-causing-gradual-p99-drift
body:
Issue summary:
Customers using Dedicated pool for FathomAI observed a gradual increase in p99 latency and a drop in sustained throughput during midday bursts. Symptoms correlate with autoscaler scale-up events where new GPU attachments were delayed and short-lived streams queued behind long-lived prefetch operations.

Impact:
- Customer FathomAI reported elevated p99 from ~450ms to 1.6s across batching routes during burst windows.
- Requests were queued for up to 20s before allocation in worst cases; observed error rate increased to 0.6% (mostly 502/504).
- Affects dedicated pool customers in prod us-east; FathomAI revenue-impacting SLA window.

Environment:
- Dedicated capacity (reserved pool) in us-east-1
- Model: llama-2-13b and redwood-tuned/13b-v1
- Routes: /v1/chat/stream and /v1/generate/batched (low-latency, many short streams)

Steps to reproduce (observed pattern):
1) Ramp 5...

title: Customer reports output regression/drift after silent model change; needs exact model/version change history for investigation
body:
Issue summary
- Customer reports model outputs changed noticeably starting 2025-10-10 ~18:30 UTC without any intentional prompt/code changes on their side.
- They are calling the model alias `rw.chat/llama-3.1-70b-instruct:stable` (not pinned to a specific model version).
- They need a definitive answer for: exactly what model/version/config changed, when it changed, and whether/why the change could affect response style and medical summarization formatting.

Customer impact
- Production: patient-message summarization and triage routing. Customer notes increased verbosity + different section headings and missing a previously reliable "Assessment" subsection.
- Reported downstream impact: higher human review load and failures in a strict JSON extraction step due to extra prefatory text.

Environment
- Dedicated cluster: lattice-dedicated-us-east-02
- Region: us-east
- API: /v1/chat.com...

Environment

- API: /v1/chat.com...

comments.body:
18 UTC — Dev Patel (Support) — Acknowledged to customer.
Asked for: (1) exact timestamps when drift started, (2) whether they pin exact model versions vs aliases, (3) 2–3 request IDs. Also recommended short-term mitigation: pin a known-good version if they have one recorded, otherwise we can help identify previous version from logs.

05 UTC — Miguel Santos (CS) — Jumped in due to dedicated account impact.
Confirmed this is impacting a production workflow and they need a clear change log for their internal compliance review. Customer asked explicitly: "Did Redwood change the model? If yes, what changed and how do we roll back?"

22 UTC — Nora Ahmed (Console / transparency views IC) — Internal.
Noted gap: customer roles cannot currently see the underlying resolved model version for an alias at the time of the request in Console. We can see it internally via admin tools/logs, but this is not exposed in a self-serve way.

10 UTC — Michael Nguyen (Control Plane) — Internal investigation.
Looked up the two request IDs. Both resolved to alias `rw.chat/llama-3.1-70b-instruct:stable` in us-east.
At 2025-10-10 18:31 UTC, the alias target was promoted from model_version `2025.09.18-rw.3` to `2025.10.10-rw.1` for us-east dedicated pools.
Promotion was part of routine rollout train; no customer-visible release note artifact was generated because the model registry record lacked complete public changelog fields.
Potential behavior change driver: updated chat template + stricter "no tool call" guardrails for medical summary route (customer uses structured output mode,...

25 UTC — Dev Patel (Support) — Customer update.
Shared: We did promote the underlying version for the `:stable` alias in us-east around 18:31 UTC. Offered rollback options:
1) We can pin their org to the previous version immediately.
2) They can pin requests to an explicit model version (we can provide the version string) while we investigate.
Customer requested immediate rollback for org_lattice_2041 while we prepare a written summary they can share internally.

40 UTC — Ethan Park (EM Control Plane) — Approved rollback.
Given dedicated customer impact, approved org-level override routing org_lattice_2041 back to `2025.09.18-rw.3` in us-east while we validate if `2025.10.10-rw.1` has a breaking change for strict-JSON workflows.
Asked Support to capture: whether customer is using "structured output" mode, JSON schema enforcement, and whether they rely on specific key ordering (not guaranteed).

05 UTC — Michael Nguyen (Control Plane) — Action taken.
Applied org override routing for org_lattice_2041 to `2025.09.18-rw.3` (us-east) effective immediately.
Verified via a test call: resolved_model_version now returns previous version in internal tracing.
Note: customer does not currently have a way to see resolved_model_version; Support will confirm by comparing output behavior.

12 UTC — Miguel Santos (CS) — Customer confirmation.
Customer confirmed outputs returned to expected format after rollback (~15 minutes after change, accounting for connection pooling/retries).
Customer request: They still need (1) a written explanation of what changed in `2025.10.10-rw.1`, (2) forward-looking policy: will aliases change without notice, and (3) how they can programmatically retrieve the resolved model version and change history.

30 UTC — Dev Patel (Support) — Internal follow-up.
Captured requirement for customer-visible change history:
- Ability to query "what model version did my request use" (resolved version) and "what changed between versions".
- Must include timestamps, deployment mode/region, and rollback guidance.
Shared link internally in #support thread (see Slack: sources/slack/support/1743524300.json).

05 UTC — Quentin Harris (Model Catalog) — Internal.
Confirmed the delta between `2025.09.18-rw.3` and `2025.10.10-rw.1` includes:
- chat template revision aligning with upstream instruct template (can change preambles)
- minor decoding defaults tuned for latency (should not affect determinism given customer settings, but can alter output boundary conditions)
- updated safety phrase list for medical content (could introduce prefatory text)
Noted: this should have been flagged as "potentially breaking for strict JSON" in metadata; current metadata did not encode this risk.

20 UTC — Dev Patel (Support) — Resolution communicated.
Sent customer:
- Confirmation of alias promotion time (2025-10-10 18:31 UTC) and impacted alias/region.
- Workaround applied: pinned org to prior version `2025.09.18-rw.3` (us-east) until they approve re-test.
- Guidance: pin explicit model version for strict-output production workflows; avoid relying on alias `:stable` if schema compliance is critical.
- Next steps: Redwood will provide customer-visible release notes/change history (in progress) and will categorize future changes with breaking/quality/perf tags.
Customer accepted workaround and asked to be included in beta for change...

title: Long pulse shadowing: sustained long-lived streams causing KV cache growth and transient GPU credit drain in dedicated pool
body:
Issue summary:
Customers in a subset of dedicated pools observed sustained P99 and P50 throughput regression beginning 2026-03-10 09:20 UTC. Root trigger: a tenant running many long-lived low-CPU, high-GPU attention streams (chat sessions) produced persistent KV cache growth which pushed allocator heartbeats and evicted small-stream slots. The allocator and autoscaler reacted by delaying new GPU attaches, which amplified queueing for other tenants in the same reserved cluster.

Impact:
- NebulaApps (dedicated) experienced 30-45% reduction in effective throughput for batched routes and a p99 latency spike from ~120ms to ~1.2s for ~40 minutes.
- Automatic fallback routed some requests to lower-capacity variants causing increased cost and degraded outputs for a brief window.
- No data loss; requests were delayed but eventually completed.

Environment:
- prod us-west-2 dedicated cluster p...

comments.body: 35 UTC - Aisha Khan (Support): Customer NebulaApps reports elevated latencies on dedicated pool. Initial signal from console: rising queue depth and attach failures. Escalating to SRE.

41 UTC - Marco Rivera (SRE): Observed KV cache growth on nodes n-az3-0x{45,46,47}. Hypothesis: long-lived low-throughput streams pin KV state and block prefetch eviction paths. Will coordinate runtime team to force a targeted eviction and apply per-tenant throttle as temporary mitigation.
48 UTC - Elena Novak (Runtime): Forced KV prune on nodes and adjusted the KV eviction aggressiveness parameter on the pool. Noting that one tenant produced ~1.6TB of KV in 38m across 6 pods.
05 UTC - NebulaApps (Customer): Confirmed observed latency improvements post-throttle; asked whether this will recur and requested a follow-up explaining root cause and permanent mitigations.

20 UTC - Marco Rivera (SRE): Follow-up: made an autoscaler patch to reduce cooldown on this pool and added a guard to treat prolonged attach delays as a scale signal. Deployed to staging; schedule to roll to prod during low-traffic window 2026-03-13 02:00 UTC.
12 UTC - Aisha Khan (Support): Customer notified of resolution plan and permanent mitigations. Ticket marked Resolved after confirmed stabilization across affected routes.

title: Precision-mode safeguard and transient-load unwind playbook for tenant
body: Issue summary: Customer Brightpost Health reported a surge of 95th/99th percentile latency spikes and an uptick in 5xx errors after enabling a combined optimization package (dynamic batching aggressive thresholds + KV prefetch + 4-bit quant profile) across a set of high-concurrency routes. Impact: Customer-facing API latency spikes caused degraded user experience and some automated retry loops increasing downstream load. Request: Customer requested an orchestrated, low-risk unwind (per-tenant) that preserves warmed KV state where safe, avoids immediate accuracy regressions, and returns traffic to stable latency SLOs without a full service restart. Additional context: the tenant is on Dedicated capacity with pinned model versions and uses long-session chat workflows (median seq len ~820). The optimization change was rolled at 02:15 UTC on 2026-03-10 via the console experimentation flow.
