Request: Customer requested an orchestrated, low-risk unwind (per-tenant) that preserves warmed KV state where safe, avoids immediate accuracy regressions, and returns traffic to stable latency SLOs without a full service restart. Additional context: the tenant is on Dedicated capacity with pinned model versions and uses long-session chat workflows (median seq len ~820). The optimization change was rolled at 02:15 UTC on 2026-03-10 via the console experimentation flow.

comments.author: (Ava)
comments.body: Acknowledged. Opening per-tenant rollback plan and tagging SRE for live mitigation. First response ETA 30m.

comments.author: SRE (Liam)
comments.body: Observed GPU queue backlog on gpool-us-east-3; recommending immediate circuit-breaker on dynamic_batch_size > 32 for this tenant and temporary throttle to 70% of previous concurrency.

comments.author: Customer (Brightpost)
comments.body: Confirmed impact on interactive clinical note generation flows. Requesting minimal functional impact and prefers not to re-run long session histories.

comments.author: (Ava)
comments.body: Drafting a staged unwind playbook: (A) pin quant_profile to bfloat16 for high-session routes, (B) reduce batch threshold to conservative, (C) enable small canary (5%) to validate latency, (D) rehydrate KV from snapshot for routes that accept rehydrate, else allow graceful eviction.

comments.author: on-call (Priya)
comments.body: Prepared a small hotpatch to circuit-break dynamic_batching via flag optpkg-2026-0301-holdpoint and added telemetry guard to alert if eviction_rate > 15% post-change.

comments.author: Customer (Brightpost)
comments.body: Approved staged unwind and requested run during their low-traffic window (03:00–06:00 local).

title: Route pin TTL: occasional cross-region egress observed for pinned routes — residency/latency clarification requested
body:
Issue summary:
Atlas Health (enterprise customer) reports intermittent cross-region egress for requests that should be pinned to us-east. Customer pins a model to us-east with a 6h pin TTL via the Console. They observe spikes in p99 latency and trace logs showing some requests routed to eu-west during sustained traffic bursts.

Impact:
- Occasional requests (roughly 0.2-0.6% of traffic over 24h windows) routed outside the pinned region.
- Increased p99 latency from ~110ms to ~420ms for those requests.
- Customer concerned about data residency / PHI leaving region for those requests.

Environment:
- Redwood hosted prod (us-east primary, eu-west fallback configured).
- Customer model: redwood/gpt-large-v1 pinned via Console to us-east.
- Pin TTL configured: 6 hours.
- Observed over last 48 hours (2026-03-10 to 2026-03-12).

Steps to reproduce (as reported by customer):
1) Create a dedic...

comments.author: Aisha Banerjee
comments.body: Ticket opened after initial CS call with Atlas Health. Customer attached sample request IDs and a screenshot of the Console route overview.

comments.author: Liam O'Connor
comments.body: Acknowledged. Escalating to routing/SRE to look for any race between pin assignment and capacity burst policies. Assigned to me for initial triage.

comments.author: Routing (K. Morales)
comments.body: Initial hypothesis: there is a short window during autoscaler-driven warm-up where traffic may be routed to fallback region if local pool cannot immediately accept new connections. Investigating routing logs and autoscaler events for the reported timestamps.

comments.author: SRE (L. O'Connor)
comments.body: Collected routing decision logs for sample request IDs. Found 3 matching request timestamps where health checks on new us-east nodes were still reporting initializing and the router elected eu-west due to strict latency SLO. Continuing to validate against pin TTL logic. Pending confirmation from runtime team about precedence of pin vs. capacity fallback.

comments.author: Aisha Banerjee
comments.body: Shared customer's compliance concern re: PHI. Customer requests a documented assurance or a config to eliminate any cross-region egress during pin TTL.

title: Sticky pin cache TTL exceeded after regional canary rollback causing session fallback loop
body: Issue summary: After a regional canary rollback in us-east, some PhotonPay sessions remained pinned to the canary variant and continued to route to an evicted serving group. The edge route cache honored a stale sticky-pin token with an extended TTL, which caused the routing layer to repeatedly attempt the pinned variant and trigger fallback logic. Impact: PhotonPay reported elevated 5xxs and long tail latency on customer-facing calls (~3–5% error rate for affected sessions) and inability for affected users to reach their pinned model until sessions were cleared. Environment: Dedicated pool for PhotonPay in prod/us-east; canary weight toggled then rolled back during 2026-03-09 22:00–22:15 UTC.

normalized.description: Issue summary: After a regional canary rollback in us-east, some PhotonPay sessions remained pinned to the canary variant and continued to route to an evicted serving group. The edge route cache honored a stale sticky-pin token with an extended TTL, which caused the routing layer to repeatedly attempt the pinned variant and trigger fallback logic. Impact: PhotonPay reported elevated 5xxs and long tail latency on customer-facing calls (~3–5% error rate for affected sessions) and inability for affected users to reach their pinned model until sessions were cleared. Environment: Dedicated pool for PhotonPay in prod/us-east; canary weight toggled then rolled back during 2026-03-09 22:00–22:15 UTC.

comments.author: Priya Desai ()
comments.body: Logged initial report from PhotonPay CS. Customer reports that users in certain sessions see repeated 502s after canary revert. Asked SRE to collect traces and advise workaround.

comments.author: Evan Black (SRE)
comments.body: Collected edge traces and confirmed route-cache TTL/pin TTL mismatch. Placed a short-term mitigation: added tenant-level cache invalidation signal (manual) to edge-proxy for PhotonPay to flush stale route entries.

comments.author: Marco Rivera ( - Edge)
comments.body: Identified codepath introduced in PR #1259 that copies pin metadata on rehydration. Preparing hotfix PR to stop copying X-Model-Pin when cached target is unhealthy or when rollback event flag is present.

comments.author: Customer (PhotonPay - CS)
comments.body: After Support requested session reset, customer cleared sessions via Console and reported error rate dropped to <0.1%. Asked for permanent fix and verification plan.

comments.author: Evan Black (SRE)
comments.body: Hotfix deployed to canary edge-proxies in us-east (edge-proxy PR #1287). Running targeted migration to invalidate pinned tokens for PhotonPay; monitoring shows errors back to baseline.

comments.author: Priya Desai ()
comments.body: Notified customer of fix and asked for confirmation of restored behavior and permission to close incident after 24h observation.

comments.author: PhotonPay (CS)
comments.body: Confirmed normal behavior; requested root-cause writeup and runbook update for future canary rollbacks.

title: Tenant-aware graceful-degradation and rollback orchestration request for aggressive optimizer profile
body:
Issue summary:
BrightMed reports that after enabling an aggressive combined optimizer profile (async batching + kv-prefetch + mixed quant profile) for a subset of tenants on Dedicated capacity, a subset of their production traffic experienced elevated 95/99p latency and intermittent 5xxs. The customer wants guidance and an orchestrated safe rollback for the tenant to avoid global impact while reverting optimization flags.

Impact:
- Production request 95p latency increased from ~120ms to ~480ms for user-facing inference routes for ~10% of tokens.
- Error rate rose to 0.8% 5xx for that tenant's routes (previously ~0.02%).
- Customer-facing ML product degraded for BrightMed paying customers (dedicated pool).

Environment:
- Dedicated pool (prod-us-east) serving pinned model rw-open-7b-v2 with tenant-specific optimizer overrides.
- Customer traffic routed through regional gateway -> dedi...

comments.body: 12 UTC - BrightMed (Customer): We enabled the optimizer bundle from our CSE yesterday morning. After a brief improvement, tail latency jumped and some user requests timed out. We rolled back some flags but saw inconsistent routing — asking Redwood for a safe tenant-only rollback and steps we can run ourselves.

45 UTC - Maya Singh (Support): Acknowledged. Opening ticket and looping in SRE + Serving runtime so we can validate telemetry and provide an orchestrated rollback plan. First response target set per SLA.

03 UTC - Luis Ortega (SRE): Collected pod-level metrics and stack traces. Observed evictions and increased GC cycles on workers with kv-prefetch enabled. Adding serving team to investigate kernel selection path and memory accounting.

20 UTC - Claire Huang (Serving Eng): Hypothesis - combined async batching + prefetch with high quant compression increases transient memory fragmentation under mixed sequence length loads. Preparing a conservative rollback checklist that demotes features one-by-one with traffic guards.

56 UTC - Luis Ortega (SRE): Customer accepted temporary traffic split. Requesting permission to run tenant-scoped config revert during low-traffic window (03:00-04:30 UTC) and to collect before/after traces for postmortem.
05 UTC - BrightMed (Customer): Approved the proposed low-traffic window and asked for a short runbook they can follow if automatic revert fails.

title: Rollback strategy for interleaved quantization profile + prefix/KV cache updates causing tail latency spikes
body:
Issue summary:
Customer BrightLens reported a production tail-latency regression after a combined rollout that changed the quantization profile and enabled aggressive prefix/KV cache prefetching for several dedicated pools. The change was deployed as a single staged rollout targeting the dedicated us-east pools; after the rollout completed, a subset of requests began to show 2--5x tail latency increases and intermittent 502s for long streaming responses.

Impact:
- User-facing: slow responses and occasional failed streams for ~9% of requests in the affected pools.
- Business: impacts BrightLens' inference SLAs for their internal quota-bearing tenant.

Environment:
- Dedicated GPU pool: brightlens-prod-us-east (NVIDIA H100, 8 gpus per node)
- Serving runtime: 2026.02.2-build
- Config service snapshot: snap-2026-03-10-21:18

Steps to reproduce (observed on prod metrics):
1) Send a 2k to...

comments.author: Ava Kim
comments.body: Created ticket after first customer support call; assigned to Marcus for owned rollback plan.

comments.author: Marcus Chen
comments.body: Acknowledged. Initial hypothesis: quantization change introduced slower kernel for long sequences on some shards; combined with aggressive KV cache prefetch load caused sporadic routing backpressure and timeouts. Will assemble playbook and coordinate with SRE for controlled rollback.

comments.author: Priya Singh
comments.body: Confirmed transient pod restarts in brightlens-prod-us-east starting 2026-03-11T13:40Z. Noticed elevated kernel fusion stall counters on nodes hosting model shard group G-3. Pinning network I/O looked normal.

comments.author: Marcus Chen
comments.body: Drafted stepwise rollback (see investigation notes). Requested customer permission to perform staged rollback during off-peak window 2026-03-12 23:00-01:00 UTC. Customer approved.

comments.author: Marcus Chen
comments.body: Performed initial rollback of quant profile on 20% of the pool and adjusted traffic split. Observed immediate drop in P99 for those hosts. Continuing phased rollback.

title: Dedicated: min bound not met during us-east region GPU capacity shortage (deployment enters degraded state)
body:
Issue Summary:
Northstar Fintech reported that their Dedicated deployment in us-east failed to maintain the configured minimum replica bound during a regional GPU capacity shortage. Their expectation (based on contract + recent enablement of autoscaling bounds) is that min bound is a hard safety invariant.

What happened:
- Customer has autoscaling bounds enabled with min replicas configured for baseline throughput.
- During a us-east GPU capacity shortage event, the fleet was unable to provision enough nodes to satisfy the min bound. The autoscaler repeatedly requested scale-up to min, but capacity could not be allocated.
- As a result, capacity stayed below min for ~47 minutes, queue depth increased, and p95 latency exceeded their SLO during a predictable traffic ramp.

Impact:
- Elevated queueing and latency; no full outage but customer-visible performance degradation.
- Customer c...

- During a us-east GPU capacity shortage event, the fleet was unable to provision enough nodes to satisfy the min bound. The autoscaler repeatedly requested scale-up to min, but capacity could not be allocated.

Impact:
- Elevated queueing and latency; no full outage but customer-visible performance degradation.
- Customer c...

comments.body: 18 UTC — Dev Patel (Support): Customer (Northstar) reports baseline min bound not being maintained; asks if bounds feature is "best-effort". Requesting SRE validation + customer-safe explanation. Added Liam (TAM) for comms.

26 UTC — Rafael Mendes (SRE): Confirmed us-east allocator shortage for requested GPU shape. Autoscaler is clamping desired to min, but allocator cannot fulfill. This is consistent with shortage behavior; we currently lack a first-class "min unmet" surfaced state in Console. Suggest messaging: "min is enforced in decisioning; provisioning is constrained by physical capacity; we're adding degraded-state indicators + alerting."

33 UTC — Liam O'Rourke (TAM): Drafted customer update: root cause is regional GPU shortage; bounds are not ignored; min could not be provisioned. Offered mitigations: temporary max/min adjustment, shifting overflow traffic to Hosted as fallback, or adding burst capacity in another region if acceptable. Waiting for customer preference (they prefer staying in-region due to data locality).

47 UTC — Vanessa Ortiz (Fleet Autoscaling): Additional detail: allocator retries were happening every ~60s; scale-up requests queued but not fulfilled. Recommend enabling internal alert for "min unmet" so on-call can proactively engage before queueing is visible to customer. Pointed to ENG-22151 / PR work in progress for shortage surfacing + backoff behavior.

10 UTC — Miles Thornton (Reliability): From a semantics standpoint, we should document: min/max are hard bounds on desired capacity, not a guarantee of physical availability. However, we should treat unmet min as degraded and page internally for enterprise Dedicated. Also recommend routing integration to expose bounded/degraded signals to Smart Routing to avoid compounding queueing (if customer has multi-deployment fallback).

02 UTC — Liam O'Rourke (TAM): Sent customer email update with clear timeline + explanation + proposed mitigations. Customer requested (a) written RCA summary, (b) commitment that Console will show "min unmet" state, and (c) notification process for future shortages.

21 UTC — Sergio Costa (Observability): We can add dashboard panel + alert rule: current_replicas < min_replicas for >10m AND allocator_capacity_errors > 0. Will keep labels low-cardinality (deployment_id, region). Pending final metric names from standards doc.

08 UTC — Dev Patel (Support): Customer acknowledged root cause; asked us to avoid recommending lowering min in the future (contractual). They prefer us to proactively notify and provide a temporary alternative capacity plan. Marking incident impact ended at 14:52 UTC when us-east capacity recovered and deployment returned to min.

40 UTC — Liam O'Rourke (TAM): Closed loop with customer. Agreed on temporary workaround for future events: if min is unmet for >10m, Redwood to page on-call + notify customer via email and offer manual provisioning in another zone/shape where possible. Customer wants this reflected in support playbook.

title: fastlane-retail-heartbeat-storm-session-tears-and-mitigation-followup
body:
Issue summary:
Fastlane Retail experienced a production incident starting 2026-03-10 03:42 UTC where a sudden spike in control-plane retries caused a heartbeat amplification across their reserved GPU pool. This manifested as repeated session tears, truncated streaming responses, elevated 5xx rates, and unexpectedly high token billing for affected time windows.

Impact:
- ~75% of Fastlane's inference sessions in us-east-1 saw session tear or reconnect within a 22-minute window.
- Customers reported truncated completions and duplicate/replayed chunks in streaming responses.
- Billing exposure estimated at +12% token usage during the window.

Environment:
- Redwood Dedicated (reserved capacity) in prod-us-east
- Pool: fastlane-prod-gpu-pool-3 (A100s)
- Model variants: redwood-7b-quant-v2 (primary) with open-8k-compat fallback

Timeline (high level):
- 03:42 UTC: Spike in control-plane re...

Timeline (high level):
- 03:42 UTC: Spike in control-plane re...

comments.author: Aisha Patel
comments.body: Notified customer Slack channel and opened this ticket. Customer reporting intermittent truncated streams and reconnects. Escalating to SRE.

comments.author: SRE - Liam O'Connor
comments.body: Observed ack backlog on broker for pool fastlane-prod-gpu-pool-3. Increasing broker ack timeout and applying rate limit. Working on routing 40% traffic to pool-2.

comments.author: SRE - Sofia Ruiz
comments.body: After routing change error rates dropped ~78%. Initiated controlled rollback of the kernel profile that was deployed 2026-03-09. Repro consistent in staging with kernel profile active.

comments.author: Miguel Santos
comments.body: Patch landed to add jittered exponential backoff to control-plane handshake retries in SDK (PR 2114). Will schedule hotfix deploy to Dedicated control plane tomorrow if postmortem action approved.

comments.author: Aisha Patel
comments.body: Customer acceptance: Fastlane confirms restore of normal streaming behavior and accepts temporary routing mitigation. Requesting billing credit for token overage window.

comments.author: SRE - Liam O'Connor
comments.body: Post-incident metrics show stable ack latencies and no recurrence in 48h. Closing incident with recommended action items assigned.

comments.author: Fastlane SRE
comments.body: Thanks team — accept credit and request delivery of full post-incident report and code changes. Will need an on-call review session next week.

comments.author: Aisha Patel
comments.body: Noted action: schedule 30m runbook walkthrough with customer week of 2026-03-16. Billing credit process started (Finance ticket FIN-2026-0443).

Impact:
- ~75% of Fastlane's inference sessions in us-east-1 saw session tear or reconnect within a 22-minute window.
- Customers reported truncated completions and duplicate/replayed chunks in streaming responses.
- Billing exposure estimated at +12% token usage during the window.

- Redwood Dedicated (reserved capacity) in prod-us-east
- Pool: fastlane-prod-gpu-pool-3 (A100s)
- Model variants: redwood-7b-quant-v2 (primary) with open-8k-compat fallback

title: Enable additional NCCL/runtime debug logging for TP stall canary window (safe + scoped)
body:
Requester: SRE (Aisha Bello) on behalf of Runtime canary for TP stability fixes.

Context:
We are running a Dedicated-first canary for runtime v1.19 TP stability fixes. We need higher-fidelity NCCL/runtime diagnostics to differentiate:
- NCCL collective ordering mismatch vs
- stream/event synchronization stall vs
- scheduler starvation leading to ranks waiting indefinitely.

Ask:
Enable additional NCCL + runtime collective debug logging for a short, pre-approved canary window, scoped to canary replicas only.

Scope / Constraints (must-have):
1) Scope: Only Dedicated canary clusters (no Hosted global).
 - Cluster(s): ded-canary-a100-ue1, ded-canary-h100-ue1 (exact names in rollout thread #eng-releases)
 - Workload: 2 enterprise accounts participating in canary (names shared in private rollout doc; do not include in logs)
2) Time window: 24–48 hours max.
3) Volume control:
 - Sampling: ...

normalized.description:
Requester: SRE (Aisha Bello) on behalf of Runtime canary for TP stability fixes.

Context:
We are running a Dedicated-first canary for runtime v1.19 TP stability fixes. We need higher-fidelity NCCL/runtime diagnostics to differentiate:
- NCCL collective ordering mismatch vs
- stream/event synchronization stall vs
- scheduler starvation leading to ranks waiting indefinitely.

Ask:
Enable additional NCCL + runtime collective debug logging for a short, pre-approved canary window, scoped to canary replicas only.

Scope / Constraints (must-have):
1) Scope: Only Dedicated canary clusters (no Hosted global).
 - Cluster(s): ded-canary-a100-ue1, ded-canary-h100-ue1 (exact names in rollout thread #eng-releases)
 - Workload: 2 enterprise accounts participating in canary (names shared in private rollout doc; do not include in logs)
2) Time window: 24–48 hours max.
3) Volume control:
 - Sampling: ...

comments.body: 12 (Aisha Bello): Creating internal support task to scope/enable NCCL debug logs for the canary. We need this before the next canary step-up. Requirements: canary-only, rate-limited, restricted retention/index. Tagging Caleb (telemetry) + Logan (telemetry platform) for feasibility.

01 (Caleb Johnson): Acknowledged. Two options:
1) Set NCCL_DEBUG/NCCL_DEBUG_SUBSYS env vars on the canary deployment + route stderr to restricted log index.
2) Use runtime structured logging only (preferred), but NCCL side is still useful for mismatch/missing launches.
Need exact cluster/deployment identifiers + desired sampling approach (per-replica pinning vs %).

18 (Rafael Mendes): For sampling: 1 replica per cluster is fine. We’ll pin by replica label "tp_nccl_debug=true" so scheduling keeps it stable. Window target: start 2025-02-21 16:00 UTC, end no later than 2025-02-23 16:00 UTC.

44 (Noah Patel): Please avoid NCCL_DEBUG=TRACE. INFO should be enough. SUBSYS: COLL,GRAPH,INIT. Only add NET if we see transport-level errors. Also ensure we don’t dump communicator addresses beyond what NCCL prints by default; no extra runtime dumps of GPU memory.

20 (Caleb Johnson): Security/retention plan:
- Create restricted index "prod-runtime-nccl-debug" with 7-day retention.
- Access: on-call SRE, runtime-kernels, runtime-scheduling (group-based), audited.
- Redaction: confirm pipeline does not ingest request payloads; only stderr + runtime structured events. We will block any fields named prompt/input/messages if present.
Performance plan:
- Apply env vars only to the debug-labeled replica.
- Add log line rate limit at collector level (burst 200 lines/sec, sustained 20 lines/sec).

52 (Caleb Johnson): Change prepared for ded-canary-a100-ue1 and ded-canary-h100-ue1. Implementation details:
- Deployment patch adds env: NCCL_DEBUG=INFO, NCCL_DEBUG_SUBSYS=COLL,GRAPH,INIT
- Adds annotation: log_index=prod-runtime-nccl-debug
- Adds label selector to apply only to replicas with tp_nccl_debug=true
Pending approval from SRE on-call to apply.
07 (Aisha Bello): Approved to apply now. Please confirm after rollout and share a saved query example that correlates with request_id / trace_id.

26 (Caleb Johnson): Applied to both canary clusters. Verified:
- Debug logs present only for the labeled replicas.
- Index receiving lines with replica_id, rank, and timestamp.
Saved query: "index=prod-runtime-nccl-debug service=serving-runtime (rank:* OR NCCL*)". Correlation: use trace_id from request logs and filter by replica_id + time window. (Runtime structured tp.collective events are in standard tracing index; NCCL is in debug index.)

03 (Grace Kim): Confirmed we can see collective sequence IDs and a suspected gap before an all_reduce on stream 7. This is enough for now; please keep window until end of day UTC to capture peak traffic.
41 (Rafael Mendes): We have sufficient data. Please proceed with rollback at the agreed end time. Reminder: verify log volume returns to baseline and the debug index retention stays at 7 days.

12 (Caleb Johnson): Rolled back env vars + removed tp_nccl_debug label from canary deployments; restarted impacted replicas. Verification:
- NCCL debug lines stopped within ~3 minutes.
- Collector CPU and log volume returned to baseline.
- Restricted index still set to 7-day retention.
09 (Aisha Bello): Closing out. Data was used to confirm ordering/sync issue; referenced in runtime PR review and canary readout. Thank you.

title: Optimization config safety decision-tree: staged rollback and tenant impact mitigation
body: Customer BrightHealth reports latency volatility and a modest accuracy drift after enabling a composite optimizer profile (async-batching + prefix-cache + 8-bit quant). They enabled the profile against their dedicated pool as a staged experiment. They need a low-risk, tenant-safe decision tree to roll back specific features without impacting other orgs on the same Dedicated cluster.

They enabled the profile against their dedicated pool as a staged experiment. They need a low-risk, tenant-safe decision tree to roll back specific features without impacting other orgs on the same Dedicated cluster.

comments.body: 10 AM] Marcus Liu (assignee): Acknowledged. Starting with a tenant-local rollback plan to avoid cluster-wide impact. Will coordinate with SRE to schedule a controlled canary at 10:30 UTC.

45 AM] SRE on-call (Jordan Reyes): Canary revert of async-batching for brighthealth-prod on rb-07-gpu-3 executed. Immediate 95p improvement observed. We captured kernel-level traces and KV eviction histograms; uploading to the incident bucket.

20 PM] Support (Aisha): Shared step-by-step rollback decision tree and asked customer to hold traffic at 10% while we run the phased plan. Customer agreed to schedule for 2026-03-14 02:00 UTC for full tenant rollout.
05 PM] Customer (BrightHealth - Jenna Ortiz): Concerned about accuracy impact if we fully disable quantization. Prefers minimal changes that restore latency first. Open to staged approach.

title: Assistance requested: Audit and safe escape for combined batching + kv-prefetch + int8 quant profile (BrightPost)
body:
Issue summary:
BrightPost enabled a combined optimization profile that turned on aggressive asynchronous batching, KV-prefetch for prefix caching, and an int8 quantization profile across their Dedicated pool. After the change they report increased 95th and 99th percentile latency and intermittent stream disconnects for chat endpoints. They request a guided audit of the current tenant config and a safe, low-risk escape hatch to restore stable latency without complete model reverts or data loss.

Impact:
- Customer-facing chat experiences show p95 latency rising from ~120ms to ~520ms for short prompts
- Some streaming sessions disconnect after ~12-18s when the request includes long context prefixes
- Revenue-impacting for their PLG usage during business hours in us-east region

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: brightpost-ded-01 (prod/us-east)
- Model pinned: redwood-v2-13b@2026-03-10
- Ap...

comments.author: Samir Kapoor
comments.body: We flipped the optimizer profile yesterday evening and noticed latency surges this morning. Can you help us revert in a way that doesn't drop model pinning? We rely heavily on the cached prefixes for our personalization flows.

comments.author: Ava Patel
comments.body: Thanks Samir — assigning engineering and SRE. Can you confirm the exact profile name and whether this was applied via Console or API? Also sending initial telemetry links to the team. (linked dashboard: https://console.redwood.ai/d/ops/brightpost-ded-01)

comments.author: Samir Kapoor
comments.body: Profile: brightpost-experiment-3 applied from Console under Optimizer > Tenant Overrides. We did not change model pin. All traffic was live during the change.

comments.author: Diego Morales
comments.body: Observed increased batch sizes and higher memory pressure on worker hosts. Some workers entered retry loops when quant scratch allocation failed. Proposing per-tenant kv-prefetch disable as first pass and switch batching from async-aggressive -> async-moderate. Marking as P1 given customer revenue impact.

comments.author: Lin Zhao
comments.body: Suggested safe operation: (1) apply tenant-scoped optimizer snapshot rollback for kv_prefetch only -> monitor p95 for 15 minutes; (2) if stable, scale batching down; (3) if still unstable, transiently redirect 30% traffic to a non-optimized variant using traffic-split route. Will provide exact API calls.

comments.author: Ava Patel
comments.body: Sent step-by-step escape instructions to customer and scheduled a 9:30 UTC window to apply changes with them on call.

title: Guided rollback script and shadow-traffic rehydrate for tenant quant-prefetch eviction causing streaming disconnects
body:
Issue summary: After applying a tenant-scoped custom int8 quant profile and enabling high KV prefetch for context prefix caching, NovatoHealth observed intermittent streaming disconnects (clients receive partial payloads) and a spike in 5xx errors for chat streaming endpoints. Impact: customer production chat features degraded, multiple customer SLAs threatened; some long-running sessions terminated mid-response. They want a tested rollback procedure that can be executed without re-deploying model artifacts, plus a shadow-traffic path to compare behavior before fully switching back.

Why unique: tenant used a combination of aggressive prefetch (prefetch_window=2048) with a custom quant schedule that increases activations-per-layer; at scale this appears to cause memory-eviction pressure in the serving runtime which then triggers transient stream termination while trying to recover. Cu...

title: Norlane: sudden conversation-lag spike across dedicated pool and restore playbook
body:
Issue summary:
On 2026-03-10 02:12 UTC Norlane reported a sudden spike in conversational response latency for their dedicated GPU pool. Multiple user sessions experienced delays of 5–30s per token and several streaming responses stalled for >10s before resuming. Customer-facing latency and timeouts began at ~02:05 UTC and peaked by 02:25 UTC.

Impact:
- Affects Norlane production tenant on dedicated pool (no other customers reported issues from initial triage).
- Increased p50/p95 token latency; several 5xx errors from serving-runtime for long-running streams.
- Customer saw degraded UX and began routing traffic to fallback endpoints; potential billing exposure for replayed requests.

Timeline (abridged):
- 02:05 UTC: First Norlane alert via support channel.
- 02:12 UTC: Ticket created; initial triage by Support (Maya).
- 02:18 UTC: SRE alerted; Autoscaler and serving runtime teams pa...

00 UTC - Arjun Patel (Runtime): Follow-up investigation scheduled; collecting staging reproduction artifacts and planning autoscaler cooldown patch.
27 UTC - Account (Priya Malhotra): Coordinated refund/credit conversation; customer asked for rollback window documentation if regression recurs.

title: Guidance requested to safely retract batching/caching/quantization changes after production latency spike
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports a sudden 2-3x tail latency increase for text-generation requests after enabling a combined optimizer profile (dynamic batching + KV prefetch + q4 quant profile) at 2026-03-10T21:00:00Z. The spike began ~10 minutes after the change and persisted until a temporary binary toggle was applied by the customer to disable dynamic batching.

Impact:
- A subset (~7%) of production requests for NeuroDocs experienced >5s tail latency, causing intermittent client timeouts and degraded UX for end users.
- No 5xx errors observed; error rate unchanged.
- Billing unaffected.

Environment:
- Redwood Dedicated pool: dedicated-gpu-pool-7
- Region: us-west (primary), us-east fallback
- Models: redwood-v2-small (serving runtime with quantized variant open-7B-quantized)
- Traffic pattern: mix of short (<=64 tokens) and long (<=1024 tokens) requests, 70% short.

Steps to repro...

comments.body: 05 Priya Shah (Support): Logged on behalf of NeuroDocs. Customer provided runtime logs and percentile graphs. Asked for an engineer to assist with safe retraction steps and recommended verification queries.

40 Ethan Morgan (SRE): Acknowledged. Will coordinate with runtime team. Initial ask: can you confirm if the KV prefetch rule was enabled for long-requests or short-only? Also requested sample client requests (redacted).
12 Alex Wu (Customer, NeuroDocs): KV prefetch was enabled globally via the profile. Uploaded sample payloads to the secure share.

22 Ethan Morgan (SRE): Preliminary analysis: traces indicate batching actor queuing increases when batcher switches between sequence-length groups; quantized kernel selection shows occasional fallback to a slower path when sequence >256 tokens. Hypothesis: combined optimizer profile created cross-cutting state which increases cold-path transitions and causes increased miss activity in KV prefetch. Proposing controlled retraction steps.
10 Priya Shah (Support): Shared proposed stepwise plan with customer for approval. Scheduled low-traffic window 2026-03-13 02:00-03:00 UTC.
05 Alex Wu (Customer): Approved. Ready for guided retraction.

Issue summary:
Customer reports a sudden 2-3x tail latency increase for text-generation requests after enabling a combined optimizer profile (dynamic batching + KV prefetch + q4 quant profile) at 2026-03-10T21:00:00Z. The spike began ~10 minutes after the change and persisted until a temporary binary toggle was applied by the customer to disable dynamic batching.

Impact:
- A subset (~7%) of production requests for NeuroDocs experienced >5s tail latency, causing intermittent client timeouts and degraded UX for end users.
- No 5xx errors observed; error rate unchanged.
- Billing unaffected.

Environment:
- Redwood Dedicated pool: dedicated-gpu-pool-7
- Region: us-west (primary), us-east fallback
- Models: redwood-v2-small (serving runtime with quantized variant open-7B-quantized)
- Traffic pattern: mix of short (<=64 tokens) and long (<=1024 tokens) requests, 70% short.

Steps to repro...

title: APAC region pin set but some requests served from US during capacity spikes — confirm data residency and mitigation options
body:
Issue summary:
Customer (KumoHealth) has configured region pinning to ap-southeast-1 for all hosted API calls. During a capacity spike on 2026-03-10 they observed increased tail latency and traces that show compute nodes in us-east-1 serving several requests. Customer is asking: did their request payloads leave APAC? What parts of request/metadata cross regions? What are supported mitigations (strict residency, fail-if-cross-region, dedicated capacity)?

Impact:
- Enterprise customer in regulated healthcare use case concerned about data sovereignty and audit controls.
- Observed latency spike impacting UI flows (patients and clinicians).

Environment:
- Hosted API (redwood public), prod - ap-southeast-1 region pin set via customer config.
- Customer account ID: KUMO-001 (internal ref).

Steps to reproduce (customer):
1) Send generation request with region=ap-southeast-1 pinned via SDK...

1) Send generation request with region=ap-southeast-1 pinned via SDK...

comments.author: Sara Lim
comments.body: We saw ~8% of requests served from us-east-1; need confirmation that customer data did not leave APAC. This is blocking compliance signoff.

comments.author: Customer
comments.body: Thanks Sara — escalating to SRE and network. Sharing sample request IDs. Will follow up with timeline and mitigation options.

comments.author: Jordan Patel (SRE)
comments.body: Started triage, pulling routing and trace logs for provided request IDs. Noted some routing decisions for those trace IDs show fallback. Investigating which policy triggered fallback.

comments.author: Jordan Patel (SRE)
comments.body: Preliminary finding: control-plane routing selected a compatible model pool in us-east-1 for a short window when ap-southeast-1 free capacity < threshold. Collecting packet-level and audit-log evidence for data flow path.

comments.author: Lina Gomez
comments.body: Flagging this to product for visibility — we should clarify residency semantics in docs and consider a customer-visible strict_residency option.

comments.author: Customer
comments.body: KumoHealth requested an immediate mitigation: prefer failing requests over cross-region fallback. Asked for timeline to implement mitigation or apply a routing override.

comments.author: Jordan Patel (SRE)
comments.body: Applied temporary routing override for KUMO-001 to enforce fail-on-cross-region for next 48 hours. Monitoring for errors and customer impact.

title: Assist customer with safe revert and guardrails after applying quantization + batching + cache tuning
body:
Issue summary: Customer LexaForms applied a combined optimization profile (q4f16 quantization, increased dynamic batch_size to 256, and aggressive KV prefix caching TTL=30s) to their Dedicated pool last night. After the change they report increased hallucination rate on rw-chat-lg-1 and a spike in tail latency for long-response (>512 tokens) requests. They want step-by-step assistance to safely revert or adjust config without downtime and to introduce guardrails for future tune-ups.

Impact: User-visible quality regressions and tail latency spikes impacting 2 services: chatbot and document summarization. Traffic is routed through their dedicated pool in us-east with occasional failover to eu-west. Estimated affected traffic: ~12% of requests (higher for long-form endpoints).

Environment: dedicated prod pool (lexaforms-dedicated-us-east). Redwood Console traces and latency SLOs attach...

comments.author: Ava Patel
comments.body: Logged per-customer request; gathered Console traces and slack thread; requested read access to LexaForms traffic replay bucket.

comments.author: Liam Chen
comments.body: Confirmed pool is healthy at infra level; no node restarts. Will coordinate safe canary steps with customer at 12:00 UTC.

comments.author: Maria Gomez, LexaForms
comments.body: Agreed to the canary window 12:30-13:30 UTC; provided a 1k-sample prompt set and consent to route 5% traffic for canary.

comments.author: Ava Patel
comments.body: Executed canary route targeting rw-chat-lg-1@v1.13 (no quant) at 5% and set cache TTL to 300s for canary; monitoring p99.

comments.author: Maria Gomez
comments.body: Reports initial signs of improvement in hallucination rate; p99 latency improved from 2.3s to 1.1s for long responses.

comments.author: Ravi Singh
comments.body: Started quick eval of q4f16 on the customer's prompt set; preliminary note: specific multi-turn concat prompts show token-level divergence at attention index ~6.

comments.author: Liam Chen
comments.body: Recommended staged promotion to 30% for 30m; customer approved. Added follow-up ticket for hardening tactics.

title: Aggressive quantization profile applied -> elevated tail latency and decoding errors; request safe staged rollback and verification
body: Customer applied an experimental 'AggressiveQuant' profile to their Dedicated pool to reduce token cost. Within 30 minutes they reported a 2-3x increase in p95/p99 latency, sporadic 5xx decoder errors, and higher output divergence on a set of regression prompts. They want guidance for a safe, staged rollback that minimizes user-facing errors and avoids cache/kv corruption.

normalized.description: Customer applied an experimental 'AggressiveQuant' profile to their Dedicated pool to reduce token cost. Within 30 minutes they reported a 2-3x increase in p95/p99 latency, sporadic 5xx decoder errors, and higher output divergence on a set of regression prompts. They want guidance for a safe, staged rollback that minimizes user-facing errors and avoids cache/kv corruption.

comments.author: Avery Lopez ()
comments.body: Received customer's report and collected logs; escalated to SRE/ML infra. Creating rollback plan draft.

comments.author: Diego Rivera (SRE)
comments.body: Confirmed kernel-fallback traces. Recommend staged weight shift + warmup; avoid full cache flush unless decoder errors continue.

comments.author: ML Infra
comments.body: Quantization-induced logits drift likely; add regression bundle to Optimize eval set after recovery.

comments.author: Customer (Annotate.ai - ops@annotate.ai)
comments.body: Agreed to staged rollback plan. Requested timeframe to minimize peak traffic impact (they have heavy morning traffic 14:00-18:00 UTC).

comments.author: Santiago Kim ( - )
comments.body: Executed initial pin and reduced batching; warmup run completed. Early metrics attached to ticket; p95 decreased from 520ms -> 260ms.

comments.author: Annotate.ai
comments.body: Noted a small change in some outputs but within acceptable range for now. Monitoring closely.

title: Failsafe and step-back plan for recent optimizer changes causing elevated tail latency and intermittent 5xx
body: Customer reports a rise in 95p+ tail latency and intermittent 5xx responses after rolling a combined optimizer profile (aggressive batching + prefix-KV prefetch + mixed quantization) to their enterprise tenant. Behavior started within 30 minutes of the rollout and correlated with traffic spikes from background sync jobs. Customer requests an immediate, low-risk step-back strategy and guidance to restore baseline latency while preserving throughput for critical flows.

Behavior started within 30 minutes of the rollout and correlated with traffic spikes from background sync jobs. Customer requests an immediate, low-risk step-back strategy and guidance to restore baseline latency while preserving throughput for critical flows.

comments.author: Aisha Patel ()
comments.body: Acknowledged receipt, escalated to SRE and requested immediate rollback options. SLA first response met.

comments.author: Marco Li (SRE)
comments.body: Reproduced locally with tenant traffic replay. Confirmed head-of-line blocking hypothesis. Recommend route-level snapshot rollback as fastest safe path.

comments.author: MediLedger SRE
comments.body: Approved snapshot rollback; requested minimal disruption and a short maintenance window. Provided traffic spike timeline for Monday mornings.

comments.author: Sofia Hernandez ()
comments.body: Per-route rollback started for 'reconcile-webhook'. Observed p95 improvement after first 10% stage. Monitoring closely.

comments.author: Daniel Moore (Engineer)
comments.body: Added note—preliminary fix branch pushed: https://github.com/redwood-inference/serving-runtime/pull/2891. Will instrument per-batch variance metrics in follow-up commit.

comments.author: Aisha Patel ()
comments.body: Customer confirmed that core payment flows are back to acceptable latency. Remaining work: engineer patch and canary schedule.

title: Sampling entropy increase and entity formatting drift after optimizer rollout; request assisted flipback
body:
Issue summary:
MediLedger Labs reported generation quality regressions immediately after we enabled an optimizer bundle for their tenant. The change combined adaptive-batching v5, prefix-KV aggressive prefetch, and a newly promoted quant profile q8_perchannel_v3. The customer sees more variable casing on medical entities, occasional unanchored hallucinations in lists, and a ~9% increase in token length for a canonical set of prompts. The rollout to 100% completed at 2026-03-08T05:04:12Z.

Impact:
- Customer automated regression checks show a 9% token inflation and a 7% drop in entity-exact-match on a 200-prompt canonical set.
- Application-level complaints from production users about formatted IDs and extra annotations appearing in generated notes (business impact: potential manual triage workload increase).
- Tail latency 95p increased by ~40ms in the affected shard.

Environment det...

comments.author: Diego Ramirez (2026-03-08)
comments.body: Customer raised after noticing formatted patient IDs had unexpected suffix tokens. They asked for an expedited rollback and an explanation.

comments.author: Maya Chen (2026-03-08)
comments.body: Acknowledged. Taking snapshot of current optimizer config and preparing safe flipback plan with SRE. Will not kill in-flight requests; new routing will apply to new requests only.

comments.author: SRE on-call (2026-03-08)
comments.body: Recommended staged rollback with session-affinity preserved: 40% -> 70% -> 100% with 3-minute observation windows to monitor entropy and latency signals.

comments.author: MediLedger Labs
comments.body: Confirmed they will pause downstream processing for 20 minutes during the rollback window to reduce user confusion if any partial differences appear.

comments.author: Maya Chen (2026-03-09)
comments.body: Executed staged rollback and monitoring; collecting pre/post artifacts to include in a short customer-facing summary.

title: Help requested: staged reversion simulation and runbook for mixed quantization + prefix-caching changes
body:
Issue summary:
CodexHealth applied an optimization bundle (quant_profile=int4-aggressive, kv_prefetch=enabled, batch_policy=auto_coalesce with max_batch_tokens=4096) to their Dedicated pool last night. After the change they observed intermittent latency spikes (95th percentile token time up ~2.8x) and increased tail error rates for streaming requests. They want guidance to: (1) safely simulate a staged reversion in a non-production snapshot; (2) a concise runbook to perform a staged reversion in prod with minimal user-impact; (3) verification checks to confirm the reversion success.

Impact:
- Customer-facing features degrade: chat latency and streaming disconnects affecting ~7% of requests during peak hours.
- Business impact: degraded SLAs for certain enterprise customers; CodexHealth flagged incident to their SREs.

Environment:
- Redwood Dedicated pool (customer-dedicated) running...

normalized.description:

After the change they observed intermittent latency spikes (95th percentile token time up ~2.8x) and increased tail error rates for streaming requests. They want guidance to: (1) safely simulate a staged reversion in a non-production snapshot; (2) a concise runbook to perform a staged reversion in prod with minimal user-impact; (3) verification checks to confirm the reversion success.

Impact:
- Customer-facing features degrade: chat latency and streaming disconnects affecting ~7% of requests during peak hours.
- Business impact: degraded SLAs for certain enterprise customers; CodexHealth flagged incident to their SREs.

Environment:
- Redwood Dedicated pool (customer-dedicated) running...

comments.author: (Asha)
comments.body: Ticket created after receiving escalation from CodexHealth SRE. Initial hypothesis: mixed aggressive quant + kv-prefetch interacts poorly with continuous batching in their long-context workload.

comments.author: (Miguel)
comments.body: Assigned for investigation. Will reproduce using customer's load profile in staging snapshot. Requested the customer's sim corpus and the exact feature-flag dump.

comments.author: (Asha)
comments.body: CodexHealth provided sim corpus (s3://codexhealth-sim/20260311) and flag dump. Noted they previously tested int8 and saw no regressions.

comments.author: (Miguel)
comments.body: Ran isolated sims in internal staging: toggled quant_profile only => marginal latency increase (~1.1x). Toggled kv_prefetch only => 95p latency increase (~2.2x) under bursty long-context traffic. Toggled batch_policy to conservative => reduced latency close to baseline but cost increased. Conclusion: kv_prefetch + auto_coalesce introduces reordering/lock contention under this workload.

comments.author: Priya Kapoor
comments.body: Noted autoscaler kept GPU utilization high; suggested adding a staged traffic-splitting plan (route 20% to baseline variant) to validate in prod before full revert.

comments.author: (Asha)
comments.body: Drafted runbook v0.1 for Miguel to review. Includes stepwise toggles: 1) enable conservative batching, 2) disable kv_prefetch, 3) switch quant_profile to int8-safety, 4) monitor 95p and streaming error. Each step holds for 10 minutes with rollback trigger thresholds.

comments.author: (Miguel)
comments.body: Reviewed runbook and added simulation commands and safe-guard checks (heatmap of kv-cache hit ratios, rpc queue length, gpu kernel stalls). Also added Canary split configuration to route 10/30/60/100 percent over 15 minute ramps.

comments.author: CodexHealth - SRE lead
comments.body: Approved staged reversion plan; requested we run the initial canary ramp tomorrow during low-traffic window and provide a communication template for the feature flags timeline.

title: Incorrect cache setting applied to unintended region (EU) during optimization config change intended for US only
body:
Issue summary
- Northbridge Analytics reported increased p95 latency and intermittent 5xx in eu-west immediately after a cache-related optimization config change that was intended to apply to us-east only.
- Initial review indicates the apply operation was mis-scoped: cache toggle was applied to the EU region for the same tenant/model scope.

Impact
- Customer impact: elevated p95/p99 latency (EU traffic) and sporadic 5xx during peak load.
- Duration: ~46 minutes from first alert to rollback completion.
- Blast radius: single tenant (Northbridge Analytics) but affected an unintended region (eu-west).

Environment
- Product: Redwood Dedicated
- Tenant: northbridge-prod
- Model: llama-3.1-70b-instruct
- Regions configured: us-east (primary), eu-west (secondary)

Observed behavior
- EU region started returning higher latencies and occasional 5xx shortly after config apply.
- Request trac...

normalized.description:
Issue summary
- Northbridge Analytics reported increased p95 latency and intermittent 5xx in eu-west immediately after a cache-related optimization config change that was intended to apply to us-east only.
- Initial review indicates the apply operation was mis-scoped: cache toggle was applied to the EU region for the same tenant/model scope.

Impact
- Customer impact: elevated p95/p99 latency (EU traffic) and sporadic 5xx during peak load.
- Duration: ~46 minutes from first alert to rollback completion.
- Blast radius: single tenant (Northbridge Analytics) but affected an unintended region (eu-west).

Environment
- Product: Redwood Dedicated
- Tenant: northbridge-prod
- Model: llama-3.1-70b-instruct
- Regions configured: us-east (primary), eu-west (secondary)

Observed behavior
- EU region started returning higher latencies and occasional 5xx shortly after config apply.
- Request trac...

comments.body: 12 UTC — Colin O'Donnell (Reporter): Customer (Northbridge) seeing EU (eu-west) p95 spike + intermittent 5xx. They claim only a US optimization config change was requested. Please triage urgently; dedicated prod traffic impacted.
18 UTC — Aisha Bello (SRE): Confirmed. Dashboard shows eu-west p95 doubled starting ~16:10. Not seeing broader platform impact. Checking recent config events and worker logs now.

22 UTC — Aisha Bello (SRE): Found Optimization Config apply event around 16:06 for tenant=northbridge-prod model=llama-3.1-70b-instruct. Audit payload indicates intended_regions=[us-east]. Why did eu-west update?

27 UTC — Benji Okafor (Eng Platform): Looking at apply pipeline logs. Control plane stored scope appears correct (tenant/model/region=us-east). Suspect runtime resolver fetch path might be selecting “latest for tenant+model” without region filter under certain caching paths.

31 UTC — Sean Gallagher (SRE Lead): Recommend rollback now; we can investigate after we stabilize. Use the rollback runbook and confirm active config version per-region. Please post before/after metrics screenshots in incident channel too.

36 UTC — Aisha Bello (SRE): Executed rollback to previous known-good config version (v2026.01.07-14) and forced runtime config refresh in eu-west. Monitoring now. (Ref: rollback-optimization-config runbook)

44 UTC — Colin O'Donnell (Customer-facing): Updated customer: rollback in progress, expect EU to recover shortly. Asked for a few impacted request IDs and a timestamp window for their internal incident report.
58 UTC — Aisha Bello (SRE): eu-west p95 back within 5–10% of baseline; 5xx back to <0.3%. Marking incident mitigated. Remaining work: root cause + prevention.

11 UTC — Benji Okafor (Eng Platform): Repro’d in staging with multi-region tenant: eu-west workers sometimes fetch “latest” config version when region is absent from cache key. Filing fix under ENG-4824 follow-up + runtime resolver change. Will also add guardrail to apply controller: verify region-scoped propagation before marking step complete.

40 UTC — Felix Schneider (Console): Confirmed Console displayed “us-east only” in blast radius and payload included region=us-east. No evidence of UI selecting EU. Agree this is backend/runtime resolution mismatch.

05 UTC — Benji Okafor (Assignee): Patch deployed to production control plane: enforce region in resolution queries for Optimization Config “active version” lookups; added logging when regionless lookup occurs. Runtime fix queued for next train (idempotent applier + resolver cache key). Customer confirmed EU stable since rollback and no further regressions observed.

- Initial review indicates the apply operation was mis-scoped: cache toggle was applied to the EU region for the same tenant/model scope.

Impact
- Customer impact: elevated p95/p99 latency (EU traffic) and sporadic 5xx during peak load.
- Duration: ~46 minutes from first alert to rollback completion.
- Blast radius: single tenant (Northbridge Analytics) but affected an unintended region (eu-west).

Environment
- Product: Redwood Dedicated
- Tenant: northbridge-prod
- Model: llama-3.1-70b-instruct
- Regions configured: us-east (primary), eu-west (secondary)

Observed behavior
- EU region started returning higher latencies and occasional 5xx shortly after config apply.
- Request trac...

title: Tiered off-ramp & validation plan for aggressive batching + mixed-precision quant enabled by tenant
body:
Issue summary:
Customer enabled a high-aggression optimizer profile for tenant mediscale-dedicated that turns on async aggregation batching, KV prefix caching and mixed-precision quantization (q4/q8 hybrid) during a traffic increase window. Within 45 minutes they reported increased 5xx rate for long-stream sessions and a subtle accuracy drift on their evaluation prompts. Customer asks for a conservative, validated off-ramp and verification steps to de-escalate without losing cached benefits for other tenants.

Impact:
- 8% request 5xx increase for long streaming sessions (>= 120s) for mediscale-dedicated.
- Slight accuracy drift observed on scheduled prompt-eval set (A/B drift ~1.8% BLEU-like proxy).
- Potential customer-visible failures in production chat flows.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: mediscale-dedicated (reserved capacity, us-east)
- Runtime version: 2.14.3
- Optimizer prof...

comments.author: Marco Sanchez (SRE)
comments.body: Uploaded scripted steps and verification commands to ticket attachments and Confluence runbook link.

comments.author: Aisha Patel
comments.body: Marking status In Progress. Waiting on scheduled run and post-run results to close.

title: High request latency and fallback-to-legacy-model for Braintree integration during peak—customer escalated
body: Issue summary: During a Braintree Labs payment reconciliation job window (scheduled spikes between 03:30–04:15 UTC), a subset of API calls to the Redwood hosted inference endpoint experienced median request latency increases from ~60ms to 1.8s and a ~12% rate of automatic fallback to a legacy quantized model variant. Impact: Customer-facing batch jobs delayed, some reconciliation runs timed out and retried leading to duplicate downstream transactions and a billing reconciliation discrepancy reported by the customer. Environment: prod cluster in us-east-1, traffic routed through api-gateway -> smart-routing -> dedicated serving pool for enterprise customers. Steps to reproduce: 1) Run the Braintree reconciliation workload (described in customer-provided runbook) with concurrent workers >= 80. 2) Observe latency spikes after warmup period (~5–7 minutes) and increased fallback logs. 3) C...

title: Aurora Payments - streaming disconnect bursts and fallback amplification during overnight payroll window
body: Issue summary: During Aurora Payments' scheduled overnight payroll batch (approx 00:45-01:20 UTC) customers observed repeated streaming disconnects and a rapid sequence of fallback completions that caused duplicate responses and an unexpected billing surge. Impact: ~18% of requests for Aurora's tenant returned either truncated streams or duplicate full completions for a ~35 minute window; downstream reconciliation systems reported duplicate payment authorizations which required manual dedupe. Environment: prod (eu-west-1), dedicated routing policy for enterprise tenants, mixed model variants (redwood-llm-v2 primary, rwd-embed-compact-v1 for embeddings). Timeline (high level): 00:43 - Traffic spike begins coincident with payroll job; 00:47 - First streaming reset alerts and elevated 5xx rate; 00:53 - Router began routing to fallback variant repeatedly; 01:05 - Billing spike detected by...

comments.body: Customer reported large number of truncated streams and duplicate completions. Asked for immediate traffic sample and confirmations about dedicated capacity. Aurora confirmed payroll run starting 00:45 UTC.

comments.author: Rafael Gomez (SRE)
comments.body: Observed sudden increase in stream-init latency on eu-west routers and elevated 502/504 rates from serving-runtime. Noted repeated fallback routing to quantized variant under heavy tail latency. Opening incident INC-2026-03-10-AUR-01.

comments.author: Aurora Ops
comments.body: Confirmed duplicates in downstream ledger; requested urgent mitigation and billing mitigation. Asked for a rollback to pre-canary routing policy if that helps.

comments.author: Rafael Gomez (SRE)
comments.body: Applied temporary fallback-dampening policy and increased concurrency cap for Aurora tenant on dedicated pool. Stream success rate recovered to baseline by 01:23. Asked customer to monitor for reconciliation drift.

comments.author: Elena Cho
comments.body: Provided preliminary impact summary and initiated follow-up with Billing and Account Management for credits. Scheduled postmortem for 2026-03-12 15:00 UTC.

comments.author: Postmortem added by Rafael Gomez
comments.body: Root cause analysis attached; action items assigned (see postmortem_actions).

title: Assist tenant with adaptive session-throttle, mixed batching and quant profile causing intermittent 5xxs; provide staged snapshot rollback guidance
body: Issue summary: ClearLex Health observed intermittent 5xx errors and elevated tail latency after we applied a tenant-specific optimizer bundle (aggressive async batching + KV prefetch + mixed-precision quant profile). They run on a Dedicated pool in us-east-1. Customer requested step-by-step mitigation and a safe staged rollback path that minimizes failed requests and avoids cross-tenant disruption.

comments.body: Initial triage complete, mitigations applied. Awaiting SRE validation before staged rollback.

comments.author: Miguel Alvarez (SRE)
comments.body: Verified eviction churn reduced after disabling prefetch. Recommend traffic-split test with 10% revert first.

comments.author: ClearLex Health (customer)
comments.body: We see reduced errors in last 24h, but some users still report timeouts. We want rollback scheduled during low-usage window.

comments.author: Miguel Alvarez (SRE)
comments.body: Scheduled 2026-03-14 09:00 UTC. Eng (runtime) standby: @Priya Nair. Runbook and rollback scripts attached in Confluence.

comments.author: Runtime
comments.body: Will monitor GPU memory pressure and tiered-eviction metrics. If revert shows improvement, will open a followup PR to tweak batch_coalesce heuristics.

title: Self-service 'Experiment Pod' automation for temporary research projects (Slack + Confluence + Jira)
body:
Issue summary:
We need an automated self-service workflow to provision temporary cross-product 'Experiment Pod' environments for short-lived research projects (hackathons, model eval sprints, partner pilots). Each pod should create a Jira project, a Confluence space, and a Slack channel set using centralized templates and enforced guardrails (retention, membership controls, audit hooks).

Motivation / Impact:
- Current manual provisioning is slow (0.5–2 business days) and error-prone; teams create inconsistent spaces and slack conventions.
- Research teams frequently spin up many short-lived projects; lacking lifecycle automation leads to stale spaces and audit headaches.
- Need to reduce ops overhead while ensuring compliance (retention, DLP hooks, and access approvals).

Background / Context:
- Similar ephemeral automation exists for some pods but is fragmented.
- Security wants app...

title: Cascading 5xx from routing-matrix mismatch and token-prefetch leak during tenant concurrency ramp
body:
Issue summary:
During a tenant concurrency ramp for ClearSight Analytics (dedicated tenant) we observed a sustained elevation of 5xx responses originating at the API gateway with upstream runtime crashes and connection exhaustion. Symptoms began ~2026-03-13 09:42 UTC and persisted intermittently through 2026-03-13 15:00 UTC.

Impact:
- ClearSight reported ~18% of requests returning 502/503/504 during a high-concurrency ramp (affects production traffic in us-east-1).
- Latency for successful requests increased 2-3x for affected routes.
- Customer experienced disrupted embeddings and chat features for ~5 hours.

Timeline (high level):
- 09:38 UTC: ClearSight starts a concurrency-based load test for new feature (reported by customer).
- 09:42 UTC: First spike of 502s seen on api-gateway; Support page shows elevated error rate.
- 09:55 UTC: SRE triage noted upstream runtime OOMs and high ...

comments.author: Aisha Patel
comments.body: Customer reports 18% of requests failing with 502s during a load test. Asked for request IDs and timeframe; customer provided request sample and timestamps.

comments.author: Marcus Lee
comments.body: Observed runtime OOMs and connection_pool exhausted on runtime-proxy_3. Correlates with token-prefetch_active spikes. Starting mitigation planning.

comments.author: Priya Desai
comments.body: Noted recent routing policy commit c3a8b2 that modified variant compatibility flags. Proposing rollback as immediate mitigation.

comments.author: Marcus Lee
comments.body: Rollback of c3a8b2 completed; monitoring for error rate drop.

comments.author: Evan Kim
comments.body: Prefetch parallelism reduced to 1 for tenant route. Observed connection_pool pressure easing within 10 minutes.

comments.author: ClearSight Admin
comments.body: Confirmed partial recovery but noticed longer tail latencies on some heavy requests. Asked about root cause and ETA for full recovery.

comments.author: Priya Desai
comments.body: Captured flame graphs and memory traces showing increased resident set size for heavy variants. Preparing repro in staging with recorded traffic.

comments.author: Marcus Lee
comments.body: Added temporary alert for token-prefetch counter drift and opened follow-up task INT-471112 to harden decrement paths.

comments.author: Aisha Patel
comments.body: Notified ClearSight that mitigations reduced error rate; pending RCA and long-term fixes. Scheduled postmortem meeting 2026-03-16.

title: Prefetch/health-probe interleaving causes widened pin latency distribution and temporary routing fallback
body: Issue summary: Customer reports spikes in tail latency and intermittent fallback to a degraded variant while a tenant was pinned to a promoted model during a canary window. Impact: A subset of requests for NimbusDocs (~0.6% of traffic) observe token-level latency spikes from ~40ms median to 400-700ms 99th, and ~1 in 2000 requests were routed to the fallback variant for ~2 minutes. Environment: Dedicated pool (d-5g), prod us-west-2, pinned model redwood/open-instruct-v1 pinned by tenant override. Steps to reproduce: 1) Pin tenant to promoted model variant. 2) Trigger prewarm / prefetch on the variant from console (simulate prewarm load). 3) Run an artificial traffic spike with 95/5 short/long sequences. 4) Observe health-probe preflight and edge-side prefetch requests interleaving with live tenant requests. Observed behavior: Health-probe and prefetch jobs intermittently hold edge-conn...

Observed behavior: Health-probe and prefetch jobs intermittently hold edge-conn...

comments.author: Sophie Patel
comments.body: Customer (NimbusDocs) reported spike in tail latency coinciding with an aggressive prewarm run initiated from their CI. They've pinned to a promoted variant for a feature rollout.

comments.author: Marco Ruiz
comments.body: Assigned for triage. Initial hypothesis: prefetch / health probes are counted against the same connection and scheduling quotas used by pinned tenant traffic causing transient slow backend flags.

comments.author: Priya Desai (SRE)
comments.body: Reproduced locally with synthetic prewarm; saw probe traffic occupying worker slots and increasing rendezvous scheduling latency. Attached traces to PR #4521.

comments.author: Marco Ruiz
comments.body: Confirmed that sticky session tokens remained bound but routing layer evicted a fraction of requests due to short-lived slow_backend marks. Working on a mitigation: isolate prewarm traffic as low-priority and exempt from slow_backend probes while a tenant-level pin is active.

comments.author: Customer (NimbusDocs) via CS
comments.body: Applied temporary pause of prewarm jobs and saw immediate reduction in fallbacks. Asking for permanent fix timeline.

title: Guidance requested: prioritize low-latency streaming routes while allowing bulk embeddings to use residual burst capacity
body:
Issue summary:
Customer runs mixed traffic on a Dedicated pool: latency-sensitive streaming requests (short chat streams) and high-throughput embedding backfills. During embedding peaks the customer observes 429s on streaming routes despite having reserved Dedicated capacity. They want guidance on configuration patterns (token-weighting, per-route hard caps, burst windows, and routing) to ensure streams remain low-latency while embeddings use leftover spare capacity instead of starving streams.

Impact:
- Intermittent 429s for streaming clients (customer-visible errors, user-facing latency spikes).
- Embedding backfills are important for nightly jobs but can be throttled.
- Customer concerned about reliability of low-latency features and potential billing impact if configurations are misapplied.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool (prod-us-west-2) backing AcroSearch tenant.
- Default quota...

normalized.description:
Issue summary:
Customer runs mixed traffic on a Dedicated pool: latency-sensitive streaming requests (short chat streams) and high-throughput embedding backfills. During embedding peaks the customer observes 429s on streaming routes despite having reserved Dedicated capacity. They want guidance on configuration patterns (token-weighting, per-route hard caps, burst windows, and routing) to ensure streams remain low-latency while embeddings use leftover spare capacity instead of starving streams.

Impact:
- Intermittent 429s for streaming clients (customer-visible errors, user-facing latency spikes).
- Embedding backfills are important for nightly jobs but can be throttled.
- Customer concerned about reliability of low-latency features and potential billing impact if configurations are misapplied.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool (prod-us-west-2) backing AcroSearch tenant.
- Default quota...

comments.author: Aisha Malik ()
comments.body: Attaching sample request IDs and a short dashboard clip showing token consumption spike. Streaming errors started ~14:12 UTC when embedding batch ramped up.

comments.author: Daniel Kim ()
comments.body: Acknowledged. Initial hypothesis: embeddings are consuming burst window tokens because they are high token-per-request; streams consume many concurrent small requests but rely on burst to keep latency. Need to confirm whether per-route token-weights are configured and whether the pool has per-route hard caps.

comments.author: SRE (J. Ortiz)
comments.body: Checked pool metrics. No autoscaler activity coinciding with spike. Quota-service logs show embedding requests increased token debit rapidly and exhausted the tenant burst bucket. Route-level weights were not set on embedding route — default weight=1 leads to large token consumption being attributed equally to streams vs embeddings.

comments.author: (D. Kim)
comments.body: Proposed approach in investigation notes and asked if customer can test a non-production policy that reserves 30% of sustained tokens for streaming routes and sets a lower per-request token_weight for streams to make their usage more favorable in token accounting.

comments.author: AcroSearch Ops
comments.body: Willing to test policy in staging. Concerned about how reserved tokens affect billing and whether embeddings will be indefinitely backlogged.

comments.author: Daniel Kim
comments.body: Suggested fallback routing for embeddings to a lower-cost model during peak windows and a soft-shed policy to queue/smooth embedding workers rather than immediate 429s for streams. Requested permission to run a brief canary policy change during low-traffic window tonight.

title: Tenant sessions routed to cost-fallback variant despite active version pin after prewarm reconciliation
body: Issue summary: Starting ~2026-03-10T08:20 UTC a single enterprise tenant (FinOrbit) observed intermittent requests returning responses from the cost-optimized fallback variant while their route is explicitly pinned to finorbit/gpt-fin-4.0-hrq. Impact: customer saw incorrect model behavior and a 2x spike in token billing (fallback variant has higher per-token cost due to routing mis-classification). Environment: production cluster in us-east-1, Dedicated pool assigned to FinOrbit with explicit version pin configured in Console. Observable window: ~08:20–09:40 UTC on 2026-03-10.

comments.author: Aisha Patel ()
comments.body: Customer reports mixed outputs and higher token costs. Collected request ids and sample responses; attaching to ticket. Asked Eng/SRE to triage.

comments.author: Mateo Rodriguez (SRE)
comments.body: Observed metrics-aggregator GC spike at ~08:15. Orchestrator uses strict TTL gate for prewarm; appears to set conservative fallback mode when view not fresh. Reproduced in staging with delayed heartbeats.

comments.author: Priya Kapoor ()
comments.body: Applied Console override for finorbit-772 -> 'honor-pin' and flushed the orchestrator shard cache. Verified sample requests now hit pinned model. Preparing PR to whitelist dedicated tenants in ORCH-RouteGuard.

comments.author: Liam Chen ()
comments.body: PR #14286 merged to main; added unit and integration tests. Rollout scheduled and monitoring added. Marking this ticket as awaiting customer confirmation for closure after prod wave.

comments.author: via CS
comments.body: Confirmed no further mis-routes after 2026-03-12 rollout. Asked for a brief postmortem and assurance on billing credits for the spike.

title: Interactive channel onboarding wizard with permission matrix and RBAC templates
body:
Issue summary:
We need a guided, interactive onboarding wizard for provisioning Slack channels (public/private), associated Jira project space stubs, and a templated Confluence page. The wizard should surface a permission matrix (who can join, who can post, archival schedule), pre-populated RBAC templates for common pod types, and automate service-account creation for channel apps/bots.

Impact:
Right now onboarding is manual and error-prone: channel creators often pick incorrect privacy settings, forget to add required compliance reviewers, or request overly broad permissions. This leads to follow-up tickets for access changes, intermittent security exposures, and a backlog in the internal-support queue. A self-service wizard reduces support load and shortens time-to-onboard for new pods.

Request / Acceptance criteria:
- Build a lightweight web-based wizard (hosted internally) reach...

comments.body: Filing this to formalize the recurring onboarding requests and reduce manual changes we get in #support. Attached sample incidents from Feb where teams accidentally created public channels with external partners.

comments.author: Leo Alvarez
comments.body: Thanks Maya — can you attach the mockups or a rough wireframe? Also tagging @security to confirm vault/provisioning constraints.

comments.author: Priya Rao ()
comments.body: Quick note — for service accounts we need ephemeral tokens and an approval path for external partner templates. I'll schedule a quick sync to confirm minimal scopes.

comments.author: Automation (bot)
comments.body: FYI, existing /provision-channel Slack command currently requires manual approver mention. This wizard should replace that UX. Posting the current implementation link: https://github.com/redwood-inference/slack-provisioning/blob/main/README.md

comments.author: Leo Alvarez
comments.body: Created prototype backlog ticket INT-1188002 for the mockup work and INT-1188003 for API creds gating. Will update here after initial scoping meeting on 2026-03-05.

comments.author: Maya Chen
comments.body: Uploaded example permission matrix CSV and three draft RBAC templates to the Confluence page linked above.

comments.author: Priya Rao ()
comments.body: Reviewed templates; flagged data-platform template for additional SSO constraints. Leaving a note on the Confluence page for required changes.

title: Embedding dimension and similarity inconsistencies causing slow and low-quality rerank responses
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports sudden degradation in reranking quality and a 2-4x increase in end-to-end latency for the /v1/rerank endpoint after switching embedding model variants. Calls from Acme Financial's Dedicated cluster to the reranker pipeline are returning lower cosine similarity scores than expected for what should be near-duplicate items, and the rerank stage is taking 600-900ms extra per request compared to baseline.

Impact:
- Business-critical search ranking for Acme Financial customers is producing poor results (low precision at top-5).
- Average p50 latency for rerank endpoint increased from ~120ms to ~700ms for affected requests.
- Issue affects production in us-east dedicated pool and impacts ~15% of Acme's traffic (requests pinned to new embedding variant).

Environment:
- Redwood Dedicated (reserved capacity) cluster in us-east-1
- Customer pinned routes to open...

Environment:
- Redwood Dedicated (reserved capacity) cluster in us-east-1
- Customer pinned routes to open...

comments.author: Acme Fin
comments.body: We started seeing worse search results after a model bump. Top results are unrelated and latency doubled. Sent sample payloads and request IDs.

comments.author: Lina Park ()
comments.body: Thanks Emily — received samples and request IDs. Routing looks like some queries are using open-embed-3-large while the index remains on open-embed-3-small. We're switching your query route back to open-embed-3-small as a quick mitigation.

comments.author: Oncall
comments.body: Rolled back routing for Acme to uniform embedding model; initial metrics show latency returned to ~140ms and top-k precision improved on sample set. Captured traces trace-ff12ac and trace-ff12ad for kernel selection analysis.

comments.author: Raj Patel (SRE)
comments.body: Investigation notes: logs show 'DimensionMismatch: src_dim=1024 dst_dim=512 -> pad/truncate path' at rerank preproc. Kernel selector chose cuBLAS CPU fallback on ~23% of batches when dims differed. Looking into selector heuristics and where conversion runs (CPU vs GPU).

comments.author: Aisha Qureshi ()
comments.body: Customer confirms rollback resolved immediate customer-facing issues but requests a formal fix and a flag to reject cross-dimension requests. Acme would prefer an explicit API error over silent conversion.

comments.author: Matt Hargrave ()
comments.body: Proposed fix: model-catalog to add 'embedding_dim' metadata and API-level validation to reject rerank jobs where query_dim != index_dim unless 'allow_compat=explicit' is set. Also proposing a deterministic float16 projection path (PR opened).

comments.author: Matt Hargrave ()
comments.body: Linked PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/model-catalog/pull/88 (adds embedding_dim check). Performance PR for deterministic projection: https://github.com/redwood-inference/reranker/pull/324. Will schedule canary deployment in staging on 2026-03-14.

comments.author: Acme Fin
comments.body: Appreciate the quick mitigation. Please keep us posted on rollout timeline; Acme would like a configuration knob to opt-in to automatic conversion in future with documentation on semantics and cost/latency tradeoffs.
