title: FinSight Labs: sudden tail-latency & 5xx increase after mixed-quant + aggressive batching; request diagnostic + safe switchover plan
body: Issue summary: After applying a tenant-scoped optimizer profile combining mixed quantization (q4_0 for majority layers, int8 for attention) with aggressive batching (max_batch_size=1024, batch_timeout_ms=12) and enabling prefix KV prefetch, FinSight Labs began seeing 95th/99th percentile token latencies spike and a small but growing number of 5xx errors for chat streaming endpoints. Impact: customer-facing chat feature shows degraded latency and occasional aborted streams; revenue-impacting for peak trading-hours traffic. Environment: dedicated pool (fin-tenant-pool-3), prod, us-east-1. Model: gpt-open-16k-q4_0-mixed pinned at v2026-03-09-21:00.

normalized.description: Issue summary: After applying a tenant-scoped optimizer profile combining mixed quantization (q4_0 for majority layers, int8 for attention) with aggressive batching (max_batch_size=1024, batch_timeout_ms=12) and enabling prefix KV prefetch, FinSight Labs began seeing 95th/99th percentile token latencies spike and a small but growing number of 5xx errors for chat streaming endpoints. Impact: customer-facing chat feature shows degraded latency and occasional aborted streams; revenue-impacting for peak trading-hours traffic. Environment: dedicated pool (fin-tenant-pool-3), prod, us-east-1. Model: gpt-open-16k-q4_0-mixed pinned at v2026-03-09-21:00.

comments.author: Anjali Rao ()
comments.body: Customer reported regression after optimizer profile change during market open window. Requested immediate assistance and a safe rollback plan that avoids full customer outage.

comments.author: Marcus Lee (SRE)
comments.body: Confirmed increased 99p at pool level. Spun up diagnostic nodes and collected perf counters. Noting PCIe memcpy stalls and increased CPU waiting on prefetch mutex.

comments.author: (runtime)
comments.body: Proposed quick mitigations: (A) reduce max_batch_size for FinSight tenant to 256, (B) lower batch_timeout_ms to 6ms, (C) disable kv-prefetch per-tenant via feature toggle. Recommended canary these sequentially during low-traffic window.

comments.author: FinSight SRE - Elliot Chen
comments.body: Prefers a conservative approach; requests a plan that guarantees no dropped requests and minimal latency degradation while rolling back. Asks for specific traffic split and timing guidance.

comments.author: Marcus Lee (SRE)
comments.body: Executed a canary reducing max_batch_size to 256 for 5% of FinSight traffic; observed immediate 40% reduction in 95p latency for canary traffic, no increase in 5xx. Proceeding to stepwise rollforward of mitigations.

title: AegisPharm private deployment: multipath failover amplified requests, ordered-stream reassembly failures, and billing exposure
body:
Issue summary:
During a scheduled nightly batch from AegisPharm (private deployment, eu-west), the Redwood private control plane entered a multipath failover loop causing amplified request fanout, repeated retries, and ordered-stream reassembly failures for streaming completions. Symptoms started at ~2026-03-10T01:12Z and lasted for ~90 minutes.

Impact:
- Customer observed duplicate completions and truncated streaming transcripts across ~18% of requests for the 90-minute window.
- Significant token accounting discrepancies led to a billing exposure (estimated 2.4x token inflation for affected requests).
- Several model variants in the customer's pinned rollout experienced high tail latency and intermittent 5xx errors.
- Business impact: AegisPharm paused nightly ingestion jobs and opened an escalation call.

Environment:
- Deployment: Redwood Private (customer-managed control plane)
...

...

comments.author: Maya Chen
comments.body: 22Z: Customer called in reporting duplicated and truncated streams from nightly ingestion. Opened incident bridge and notified SRE and on-call infra.

comments.author: Sanjay Mehta
comments.body: 35Z: Initial triage indicates routing layer returned multiple successful ACKs while backend KV caches were still warming. Temporarily disabled multipath failover for aegispharm-prod-cluster-3 to reduce amplification. Requesting scheduler and router logs for 00:50-01:30Z.

comments.author: Dr. Lena Ortiz
comments.body: 05Z: Confirmed stop of duplicate completions after disabling multipath. Customer paused ingestion and asked for billing mitigation while investigation continues.

comments.author: Riley Park
comments.body: 40Z: Observed elevated eviction_count on GPU pool and TLS renegotiation spikes aligned with retry bursts. Hypothesis: under high concurrency router retried prematurely due to false negative health checks.

comments.author: Maya Chen
comments.body: 12Z: Provided customer with a temporary credit estimate and proposed next steps. Scheduled postmortem call for 2026-03-12.

comments.author: Priya Singh
comments.body: 00Z: Root cause identified in multipath backoff code; race between ack-path and retry-path under partial stream conditions. Preparing fix and tests.

title: POP affinity migration causing cold KV cache warm-up and persistent tail latency
body: Issue summary: Starting 2026-03-10 08:15 UTC, NimbleChat reported elevated tail latency (p99) and intermittent timeouts for requests routed to their dedicated pool. Pattern correlates with our POP affinity migration which moved a subset of NimbleChat traffic from SFO POP to PHX POP. Impact: increased p95/p99 token latency (p99 rose from ~1.1s to ~3.8s) and ~0.7% 5xx/timeouts on user-facing generate endpoints for NimbleChat traffic. Timeline: 08:05 UTC - automated migration triggered; 08:12 UTC - first customer report; 08:20 UTC - alerts fired on routing and p99; 09:00 UTC - Support engaged SRE and routing team. Environment: prod, NimbleChat dedicated pool, us-west-2 region (primary), backup in eu-west-1 for some accounts. Steps to reproduce: 1) Hit /v1/generate with NimbleChat API key pinned to dedicated pool from an us-west client IP that resolves to the new PHX POP via routing table...

comments.body: 18 UTC - NimbleChat (support@nimblechat.com): 'We're seeing a big jump in response times since early morning, some requests time out.'
35 UTC - Support (Ananya Patel): Acknowledged. Collecting request ids and traces. Escalating to SRE and routing.

50 UTC - SRE (Julian Hart): Observed increased kv miss rate and kernel compile spikes on PHX nodes for account namespace nimblechat-prod. Likely cold-cache effect after migration. Adding hypothesis to ticket.
05 UTC - Routing (Lina Gomez): Confirmed migration job migr-2026-03-10-08 applied. Rolling back affinity weights for nimblechat to SFO by 09:45 UTC to reduce impact.
50 UTC - Support (Ananya Patel): After rollback p99 reduced from ~3.8s to ~1.3s. Customer notified of temporary improvement. We are scheduling pre-warm and longer-term fixes.
10 UTC - Infra (Sofia Ruiz): Started targeted KV pre-warm job for PHX node set; observing gradual decline in miss rate. Monitoring continues.
20 UTC - Customer (NimbleChat Ops): Thanks, we've seen improvements but still occasional spikes; would like postmortem and mitigation plan.

title: Guidance for shadow-traffic migration + snapshot rollback after aggressive batch/quant profile change causing intermittent 95p latency increases
body:
Issue summary:
Customer applied a tenant-scoped optimizer profile that enabled aggressive async-batching, KV prefetch and an 8-bit quantization profile. After rollout, they report intermittent 95th percentile latency spikes (~200-400ms above baseline) and occasional streaming partial frames (client sees chunk gaps) for production traffic.

Impact:
- Affects NeonMed's dedicated cluster (single reserved pool) in us-east-1.
- Approximately 12% of requests observed above customer SLA target at 95p during peak.
- User-facing degradations for key interactive endpoint (chat/stream).

Environment:
- Deployment: Dedicated (reserved) pool, tenant NeonMed, 4x A100-equivalent instances
- Runtime version: serving-runtime v2.9.1
- Optimizer profile: tenant/optimizers/neonmed-aggressive-v2 (enabled 2026-03-09 14:20 UTC)
- Traffic pattern: bursty short-turn conversations (mean tokens 80, tail to 1024...

comments.body: 15 UTC - Priya Nair (Customer): Thanks — we pushed the profile at 14:20 UTC yesterday and started seeing spikes at ~14:40 UTC. We can take a short maintenance window if needed but we prefer a rolling mitigation.

02 UTC - Marcos Silva (Support): Received the telemetry links. I'll run a quick canary in our staging cluster and share a concrete rollback procedure. Please provide any recent deployment snapshots if available.

40 UTC - SRE Oncall (Elena Gomez): Observed increased memory working set on the tenant pool correlated with KV prefetch threads. Suggested candidate mitigation: disable prefetch per endpoint and lower async batch delay to 8ms for streaming routes.

30 UTC - Support (Marcos Silva): We've prepared a safe snapshot and a revert script (attached PR). Proposed immediate actions: a) enable 10% shadowing to gather live samples; b) lower batch-size and async delay for streaming; c) standby to execute tenant snapshot revert if 95p does not improve after 2 hours.
10 UTC - Customer (Priya Nair): Starting 10% shadow now. Will report after 90 minutes. We also approved a maintenance window on 2026-03-14 02:00 UTC if needed.
45 UTC - Marcos Silva (Support): Shadow confirmed collecting traffic. Early metrics show 10% shadow has ~80ms higher mean but 95p in shadow is informative for tuning. Continuing to monitor.

10 UTC - Elena Gomez (SRE): If revert needed, we'll execute a per-tenant snapshot restore which is non-disruptive and completes within ~5 minutes per instance. Will coordinate with customer for maintenance window if a full revert is chosen.
40 UTC - Priya Nair (Customer): After lowering async delay in the canary, tail looks better. We'll keep shadowing for another hour. Thank you for the fast response.

title: Guidance for safe backstop and feature sunsetting of optimizer flags without snapshot restore
body: Issue summary: Customer Lumina Health plans to sunset an experimental optimizer flag (async-prefix-prefetch) across several tenant pools but does not have snapshot backups for the feature-state. They requested a backstop strategy that does not rely on snapshot restore to ensure safe rollback and no customer-visible regression. Impact: potential 95p/99p latency increase and token cost changes during sunsetting; possible transient 5xx for tenants with mixed-optimizer-profiles. Environment: Dedicated GPU pool (prod, us-east) serving gpt-3.5-redwood-v2 and gpt-4-lite. Steps to reproduce/context: 1) Toggle optimizer flag off via console feature toggle for a small subset of tenants. 2) Observe latency and error metrics for the next 15 minutes. 3) Gradually expand toggle to remaining tenants. Observed behavior: Customers report small but sustained 95p latency increase (200-350ms) and occasio...

comments.author: Maya Patel ()
comments.body: Opened ticket after Lumina Health requested a zero-snapshot backstop. Attached metric slices and initial trace IDs: trace-0a1b, trace-0a1c.

comments.author: Diego Alvarez (SRE)
comments.body: Observed 95p latency delta of ~220ms for tenant pool A immediately after toggle. Proposed traffic-split backstop (10/90) and staged worker recycle. Linking internal runbook RFC: https://confluence.redwood/display/INF/Optimizer+Sunset+Runbook.

comments.author: Customer - Priya Reddy
comments.body: Concerned about potential cost surge and user-facing tail latency. Asked for explicit step-by-step playbook we can run during our maintenance window.

comments.author: Diego Alvarez (SRE)
comments.body: Shared a playbook draft: 1) Create target traffic-split 90:10 optimized:vanilla; 2) Disable optimizer flag for 10% canary; 3) After 30m stable, expand by doubling canary; 4) If 95p exceeds threshold, pause expansion and route 100% back to optimized; 5) If errors >1%, revert DNS-level routing for affected pools. Attached helper script: github.com/redwood-inference/tools/optimizer-backstop.sh#L1-L200.

comments.author: Maya Patel ()
comments.body: Noted Lumina's maintenance window scheduled for 2026-03-18; requested final checklist and rollback commands. Asking for confirmation that snapshotless backstop will not require model artifact changes.

comments.author: Diego Alvarez (SRE)
comments.body: Confirmed no model artifacts change required. Added recommendation for staggered worker recycle (5 min between host drains) to allow KV warmup and avoid mass cold starts. Added note to monitor 'kv-cache-hit' and 'streaming-chunk-latency' metrics closely.

comments.author: Priya Reddy
comments.body: Asked whether we can automate hold points at each expansion step in Console. Requested a runbook that can be executed by their on-call without deep infra privileges.

comments.author: Maya Patel ()
comments.body: Responded with link to Console runbook and temporary delegated role creation steps. Next action: prepare final Canaries configuration and pre-signed script for the customer.

title: MarinerCommerce multi-region late-token tail surge and SLO exposure
body:
Issue summary: Between 2026-03-10T11:40Z and 2026-03-10T12:50Z MarinerCommerce experienced elevated tail latency on streaming and non-streaming generation across us-east and eu-west leading to SLO breaches for 95th/99th percentile token latency and several customer-visible timeouts. Impact: customer-facing checkout autocomplete and fraud-scoring features saw degraded response times and several 5xx responses when retries cascaded. Estimated affected requests: ~45k across both regions. Financial sensitivity: enterprise contract with SLO-based credits; potential exposure if we do not provide remediation and timeline.

High-level hypothesis: a combination of a kernel selection change on a quantized 70B variant and a routing decision that funneled high-concurrency short-sequence traffic to a newly provisioned pool with suboptimal batching parameters produced high tail latency. Smart-routin...

title: High initial-token latency on chat completions after runtime 1.5 + smart-router rollout
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports noticeably slower chat responses where there is a visible pause before the assistant emits the first token. This started shortly after our runtime 1.5 rollout combined with a new smart-router policy that prefers model variants with 'cost-optim' labels in low-cost cells.

Impact:
- Several production chat flows at Acme Fintech have median initial-token latency increase from ~220ms to ~1.1s and p95 ~2.4s.
- Affects user-facing chat widgets for ~18% of requests that route through the new policy.
- Higher perceived lag causing customer support escalations and potential user churn risk.

Environment:
- Redwood prod, us-east-1 region, Dedicated pool for Acme Fintech.
- Runtime version: 1.5.0 (rolled 2026-03-09 02:00 UTC).
- Smart-router policy: prefer-cost-variants v2 (rolled 2026-03-09 01:50 UTC).

Steps to reproduce:
1) Make a ChatCompletion request with st...

comments.author: Priya Shah () - 2026-03-09T05
comments.body: 30Z: Received escalation from Acme Fintech. They reported significant pause before assistant starts typing. Collecting traces and sample requests now.

comments.author: Miguel Alvarez (SRE) - 2026-03-09T06
comments.body: 50Z: I see patterns where 'cheap-variant' nodes show kernel_warmup ~0.45s more than baseline. Traces attached. Looks like kernel selection picks a slower execution path for some quant profiles.

comments.author: Backend
comments.body: 30Z: Confirmed that runtime 1.5 changed preferred kernel for qat models when sequence_length < 128. This falls back to a different micro-kernel that has a higher one-time setup cost.

comments.author: Platform
comments.body: 50Z: Applied an account-scoped routing override for Acme Fintech to prefer low-latency variants. Observed immediate improvement in customer metrics. Preparing a short-term config patch to router to reduce selection of cold variants.

comments.author: Priya Shah () - 2026-03-09T10
comments.body: 05Z: Customer confirmed improvement after override; still monitoring. Marking customer as 'Waiting' for patch deployment.

comments.author: Backend
comments.body: 40Z: Deployed hotfix that changes kernel selection ordering to avoid the high-warmup micro-kernel for short sequences and added a warmup cache for infrequently-used variants. Router policy weights adjusted to include a latency penalty term.

comments.author: Platform
comments.body: 00Z: Rolling metrics show median returns to ~250ms, p95 ~600ms for the affected customer. Closing the incident after customer validation.

comments.author: Acme Fintech (Customer) - 2026-03-12T09
comments.body: 22Z: We've validated on our side — initial response times are back to acceptable levels. Thanks for the quick action.

title: Abort running optimization experiment and rehydrate previous per-tenant inference profile (batching/caching/quant)
body: Issue summary: MediCore reported sustained p95 latency increases and 503 spikes for tenant medi_mdt_42 after we enabled a staged optimizer experiment that adjusted batching window, KV prefix caching policy and swapped to a q4_0 quant profile in the dedicated pool. They asked us to immediately stop the experiment and restore their previous per-tenant profile because production traffic is impacted.

comments.author: Ethan Brooks (SRE)
comments.body: Investigating. Observing KV cache eviction spikes and a mismatch between long-session tokenization patterns and the q4_0 quant path. Preparing a controlled abort plan (staged traffic switch).

comments.body: Confirmed they did not change client behavior. Provided request IDs and times. Asked for temporary traffic pin to fallback model if roll back fails.

comments.body: Asked for confirmation to execute restore of snapshot snap-2026-03-07-medi_mdt_42 and to disable optimizer-experiment-v2 for that tenant. Scheduled 3-minute quiet window to perform steps.

title: Orchestrated demotion plan for sustained concurrency surge after optimizer profile change
body:
Issue summary: Customer reports a repeated increase in 95th/99th percentile latency and request timeouts for long-lived conversational sessions after enabling a new optimizer profile (aggressive async batching + KV prefetch + mixed-precision quant profile). Impact: Several users experiencing degraded UX, spawning retries and increased cost. Scope: Dedicated pool in us-east-1, traffic spike correlated with nightly batch job and many concurrent streaming sessions.

We see partial failures (502/504) and occasional 5xx during KV eviction windows. Customer requests a safe, orchestrated rollback/demotion plan that m...

comments.author: Aisha Patel ()
comments.body: Thanks for the report. We're coordinating an SRE-led demotion plan that avoids a hard rollback. Can you confirm whether you have per-tenant snapshots enabled and whether traffic split is possible on your side?

comments.author: TempoHealth (Customer)
comments.body: We can do a 10/90 traffic split for a single route and we have per-tenant snapshots enabled. We prefer minimal session disruptions; how long will staged demotion take?

comments.author: Diego Morales (SRE)
comments.body: Proposed plan: 1) Gate new requests to a fallback model variant with conservative optimizer settings (async batching disabled, kv-prefetch off, quant profile: ptq-conservative). 2) Gradually re-route 10%->30%->60%->100% over 30-minute windows. 3) Run background warmup for KV cache for the fallback variant using shadow replays. 4) If incidents persist, snapshot rollback to pre-change optimizer profile. We'll orchestrate via console and provide toggle scripts.

comments.author: Aisha Patel ()
comments.body: Implemented first 10% gate at 2026-03-13T08:00Z. Early metrics show 20% reduction in 95p latency on the gated cohort. Continuing to 30% at 08:40Z per plan.

comments.author: TempoHealth (Customer)
comments.body: 100% roll to fallback completed; observed stable latencies for 24 hours. Please confirm closure checklist and any follow-ups.

body:
Issue summary:
Customer BrightPost reported a sudden oscillation in throughput and intermittent 5xx spikes after a tenant-scoped optimizer profile was enabled for aggressive batching + KV prefetch + q4_0 quantization. They requested help validating configuration changes and an orchestrated rollback that avoids broad capacity churn.

Impact:
- Intermittent 5xx responses and user-facing latency spikes (P95 increased from ~180ms to ~750ms for the tenant).
- Affected a single dedicated tenant cluster serving production traffic for BrightPost Health; visible error rate increase ~2.8%.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: dp-us-east-1-06
- Service: serving-runtime v2.14.1 + optimizer v0.9.3 flags enabled per-tenant
- Models: redwood-llm-13b (q4_0 experiment for cost savings)

Steps to reproduce (provided by customer):
1) Enable tenant optimizer profile "bp-aggressive-v2" which sets: async_batchi...

comments.author: Aisha Patel ()
comments.body: Initial triage completed with customer. Captured snapshot token snap-20260309-bp-A1 and requested telemetry (p95, p50, 5xx, kv-latency) for the 30m window after toggle.

comments.author: Diego Martinez (SRE)
comments.body: Reproduced delayed KV fetches on dp-us-east-1-06 when kv_prefetch + high batch_target are enabled under bursty tail. Opening hypothesis that aggressive prefetch + q4_0 increases memory churn leading to eviction storms.

comments.author: BrightPost (Customer)
comments.body: Confirmed that we saw the same in our internal load tests. Need stepwise rollback that keeps 99th percentile stable for other tenants.

comments.author: Diego Martinez (SRE)
comments.body: Ran a dry run of the sanity-gate checks in staging: validate-optimizer-tuning script checks (seq-length histogram, token-distribution shift, p95_delta threshold 200ms). Logging results to /var/log/opt-sanity/bp-aggressive-v2-20260311.log.

comments.author: (Aisha)
comments.body: Proposed rollback orchestration: 1) Gated traffic split to fallback model (redwood-llm-13b-fp16) at 10%, 2) Restore tenant config snapshot to snap-20260308-safe, 3) Disable kv_prefetch for tenant for 30 minutes, 4) Monitor for 30m and incrementally ramp back. Customer approved to proceed within maintenance window.

comments.author: Diego Martinez (SRE)
comments.body: Executed controlled rollback during maintenance window: snapshot restore + incremental traffic split. Observed P95 fall back to ~210ms and 5xx rate drop to baseline. Captured timeline in /incidents/SUP-207654/trace-rollback-20260312.json and linked PR to ops-scripts to automate the sequence.

title: Noisy-neighbor tail latency increase for non-burst Dedicated tenant during another tenant’s burst window (us-east / H100)
body:
Issue summary
- Northpeak Search (Dedicated, burst NOT enabled) reported elevated p99 latency and intermittent request timeouts during a 35–45 min window on 2025-05-16.
- Correlation observed between Northpeak’s latency spike and a separate tenant’s burst activity against the shared us-east H100 burst pool.

Impact
- Customer-reported: p99 latency increased from ~2.2s baseline to 6–9s for chat completion requests; a subset of requests hit client timeout (30s) and were retried.
- Redwood telemetry: elevated tail latency (p95/p99) on Northpeak’s dedicated route, plus increased queueing time at router/scheduler boundary.
- Duration: ~14:05–14:47 UTC (approx; see investigation notes).

Customer expectations / concern
- Customer is explicitly not opted into burst and expects neighbor activity not to affect their SLOs.
- Asked whether Dedicated implies strict isolation and whether Redwood i...

- Correlation observed between Northpeak’s latency spike and a separate tenant’s burst activity against the shared us-east H100 burst pool.

Impact
- Customer-reported: p99 latency increased from ~2.2s baseline to 6–9s for chat completion requests; a subset of requests hit client timeout (30s) and were retried.
- Redwood telemetry: elevated tail latency (p95/p99) on Northpeak’s dedicated route, plus increased queueing time at router/scheduler boundary.
- Duration: ~14:05–14:47 UTC (approx; see investigation notes).

Customer expectations / concern
- Customer is explicitly not opted into burst and expects neighbor activity not to affect their SLOs.
- Asked whether Dedicated implies strict isolation and whether Redwood i...

comments.body:
18 UTC — Miguel Santos (Reporter)
Customer (Northpeak) escalation via CS: “Our p99 jumped to ~8s starting around 14:05 UTC. We do not have burst enabled. Did you move us onto shared capacity?” Customer provided request IDs and noted spike coincided with their internal batch job, but request rate is within normal envelope (+10%).

Asks: please confirm isolation, and whether an incident is in progress. Tagging SRE due to potential neighbor impact + beta burst testing today.

23 UTC — Sean Gallagher (Assignee)
Acknowledged. Treating as Sev2 until confirmed. Pulling dedicated tenant latency dashboard + burst pool panels. Also pinging #incidents and #eng-sre.

Immediate checks:
- Any failover/fallback routing toggles?
- Any evidence Northpeak ran on burst pool nodes?
- Burst pool saturation and admission rate in us-east/H100.

31 UTC — Allison Grant (Observability)
Dashboard notes:
- us-east/H100 burst pool utilization hits ~92–96% between 14:06–14:41 UTC.
- burst_admission_denied_total spikes after 14:20 UTC, but only after saturation was already high.
- Router queue depth increased ~3x during that window across multiple tenants (not only burst-enabled).
- Northpeak p99 latency tracks router queue depth more closely than GPU busy time.

Hypothesis: shared router/scheduler bottleneck rather than Northpeak running on burst GPUs.

36 UTC — Mina Haddad (Smart Routing)
Routing check: Northpeak tenant policy still pins to dedicated pool, burst disabled. No policy change events. No evidence of fallback into burst pool either.

However, we do see increased “admission decision latency” in routing logs during that time. If routing calls admission controller synchronously for burst-enabled tenants, and that service is overloaded, it could increase latency for unrelated routing paths depending on how shared the threadpools are.

44 UTC — Rafael Mendes (SRE Manager)
Suggest applying runbook step 1: reduce max burst ratio and/or enable global circuit breaker for us-east/H100 while we confirm blast radius.

Also: check if we are sharing the same ingress/Envoy cluster for both dedicated and burst traffic. If yes, we may be CPU-saturated on ingress which would explain cross-tenant impact.

51 UTC — Sean Gallagher (Assignee)
Mitigation applied at 14:49 UTC:
- Reduced burst pool safety cap (us-east/H100) from 0.30 to 0.15 of regional headroom.
- Temporarily disabled burst allow-list for the one tenant driving sustained burst (per Product-approved beta controls).

Result: router queue depth trending down within ~3–5 minutes; Northpeak p99 started recovering by ~14:55 UTC. Continuing to monitor for 30 minutes before telling customer we’re stable.

05 UTC — Miguel Santos (CS)
Updated customer: We are investigating a suspected interaction with ongoing burst beta activity in the region. Confirmed no intentional changes were made to Northpeak’s Dedicated configuration. Provided status: mitigation in place and metrics recovering. Customer asked for RCA within 48 hours.

12 UTC — Ethan Park (Control Plane)
Checked audit events for Northpeak burst settings: no changes in last 14 days; burst_enabled=false; no admin overrides.

Admission controller metrics:
- P99 admission decision latency rose from ~12ms to ~180–250ms during 14:10–14:35 UTC.
- CPU throttling observed on admission controller pods.

This supports the theory that a shared dependency (admission controller) increased request processing time. Action item: make admission checks async/isolated per routing path, or ensure non-burst traffic doesn’t wait on burst admission control.

02 UTC — Ravi Menon (Routing)
Deep dive:
- Dedicated traffic path SHOULD bypass burst admission entirely, but current routing implementation still performs a “capability fetch” call that hits the same policy evaluation service used by burst admission (shared client + shared circuit breaker).
- Under load, the shared client pool backs up and impacts non-burst tenants.

Fix direction:
1) Split routing clients: non-burst path must not depend on admission controller/policy engine latency.
2) Add strict timeouts + fail-open for non-burst capability fetch (do not block request).
3) SLO gate: if non-burst p95/p99 degrades, automatically hard-disa...

40 UTC — Sean Gallagher (Assignee)
Status update:
- No evidence Northpeak requests ran on burst GPUs (confirmed by capacity attribution logs and per-node tenant tags).
- Root cause is shared routing dependency coupling: burst admission / policy evaluation latency bled into non-burst routing due to shared client pools and insufficient isolation.
- Mitigations still active: lowered us-east/H100 burst cap + tenant-level burst paused.

Requesting approval to re-enable burst only after PR-23988 SLO gating is deployed to prod us-east.

22 UTC — Keiko Yamamoto (Autoscaling/Guardrails)
Added an additional guardrail on the burst pool autoscaler to avoid pushing admission controller and router into CPU contention:
- If router CPU > 75% for 5m OR admission decision p99 > 100ms for 5m, freeze burst scale-up and progressively tighten burst token bucket rate.

This is a short-term safety valve until routing decoupling lands.

11 UTC — Miguel Santos (CS)
Customer-facing summary sent to Northpeak:
- Confirmed: Northpeak traffic remained on reserved dedicated capacity (no spillover onto shared burst pool).
- What happened: during a burst beta test in us-east/H100, the burst admission/policy evaluation path experienced elevated latency. A shared routing dependency caused increased queueing that affected some non-burst tenants’ tail latency.
- What we changed immediately: tightened burst caps and paused sustained burst activity in the region; latency returned to baseline.
- Preventing recurrence: shipping an SLO regression gate + additional isolation in routing; add...

27 UTC — Sean Gallagher (Assignee)
Closing as resolved.

Resolution criteria met:
- Northpeak p99 back to baseline for 72 hours.
- Guardrail tightened in us-east/H100.
- Engineering work tracked and prioritized (SLO gate + routing decoupling).

Follow-ups:
- Ensure SLO gate (PR-23988) is enabled for us-east before resuming beta bursts.
- Add explicit runbook step: verify non-burst routing path does not hit admission controller under load.
- Link this incident in burst beta retro notes.

title: Tenant optimization safe-exit: create tenant snapshot and emergency toggle for aggressive prefetch + int8 profile
body:
Issue summary:
Customer BrightWell Health reports a sudden increase in 95/99p tail latency and sporadic stream disconnects after applying an optimization bundle (kv-prefetch enabled + int8 quantization profile) to their dedicated tenant. They request assistance to create a per-tenant safe snapshot (config + runtime state) and a minimal emergency toggle to disable only the aggressive prefetched KV path while preserving other optimizations.

Impact:
- Elevated 95p/99p latency from ~120ms to 900-1,200ms for chat/streaming flows.
- ~3% of streaming sessions terminating with a handshake error in the last 12 hours.
- Business impact: BrightWell deferred next-week rollout until mitigated. Dedicated tenant — high priority.

Timeline / Context:
- 2026-03-09 14:10 UTC: Customer applied optimizer bundle via Console (profile: bw-health-int8-prefetch-v2).
- 2026-03-09 17:05 UTC: Support observed f...

comments.body: 02 UTC - Asha Mehta (Support): Initial triage done; collected tenant ID and recent console audit. Asked engineering to confirm snapshot feasibility for dedicated tenants.

45 UTC - SRE Channel (#sre-support): Liam O'Connor assigned for tenant snapshot. Noted that runtime v3.8.1 supports per-tenant config snapshots via 'rwctl tenant snapshot' but must coordinate with autoscaler to quiesce nodes for consistent KV map.

20 UTC - Liam O'Connor (SRE): Performed dry-run snapshot on staging cluster; created command sequence and checklist. Shared tentative rollback toggle: 'tenant.config.optimizer.prefetch=false' and 'tenant.config.quant.profile_override=fp16-fallback'. Need windows to apply in prod with very small traffic split.
30 UTC - BrightWell Health (Customer): Requested that snapshot include the exact keyset for KV cache (for auditing) and that rollback be reversible within 15 minutes if negative impact observed.

00 UTC - Asha Mehta (Support): Confirmed reversible plan and proposed a blue/green traffic split at 5% for 10 minutes for verification before full switch. Created SUP-63722 as follow-up for automated gate script (linked).

title: Private deployment: how to safely override hardware tuning profile (H100/L40S/B200) via Helm without breaking supportability
body:
Customer (Northpeak Bank) is preparing an upgrade of their Redwood Private deployment and wants to know how to override the runtime hardware tuning profile safely.

Questions from customer:
1) Can we force the H100 profile even if GPU detection returns a different string / variant?
2) Can we override specific knobs (batching, queue limits, KV cache budgets) without forking the entire profile pack?
3) What is the supported override order for Private deployments (Helm values vs env vars vs config files)?
4) What are the rollback steps if tail latency regresses post-change?

Customer context:
- Private (customer-managed) Kubernetes
- Primary GPU fleet is H100 (mixed SKUs reported differently by node drivers)
- Strict operational requirement: no unsupported changes that make upgrades difficult; needs a documented, supportable approach

Impact:
No active incident. Blocking customer’s plann...

comments.body:
2025-12-06 | Kira Thompson (Support) | Triage
Customer asking for a supportable way to override hardware profile selection in Private. Assigning to Diego for deployment/config guidance; may need confirmation from Runtime (Ethan/Benji) on override precedence and which knobs are safe to pin.

Customer explicitly does NOT want to patch runtime images or maintain a forked profile pack.

2025-12-06 | Diego Santos (Eng - Cross-deployment config compatibility) | Initial guidance
Key points for Private:
- Recommended approach is to use Helm values (chart-managed) to choose the profile pack version and (optionally) force a profile name if GPU SKU detection is unreliable.
- Overrides should be limited to supported top-level fields; avoid pinning low-level kernel toggles unless directed by Redwood (risk of incompatible combinations across runtime upgrades).

Verification:
- Runtime startup logs include profile/version compatibility matrix and selected profile.
- Metrics and debug endpoint can show selected profile + override source.
- There is a CLI/tooling command to dump effe...

2025-12-07 | Diego Santos (Eng) | Concrete configuration pattern (Helm)
Proposed (supportable) override pattern for Private:

A) Pin a profile pack version (recommended)
- Set the chart value to pull/use a specific profile pack version that is known compatible with your runtime release.
- Benefit: you stay on a curated set of defaults; easier to reason about upgrades and regression triage.

B) Force a specific hardware profile by name ONLY when detection is ambiguous
- Use this sparingly; the runtime’s hardware detection normalizes many GPU SKU string variants, but Private environments sometimes differ by driver/runtime.
- If you force H100 while actually running a different device, you c...

2025-12-07 | Kira Thompson (Support) | Customer response / details
Customer confirms single Helm release per environment.
They want:
- Exact keys to set in values.yaml
- How to confirm selected profile at runtime without shell access (they can access Prometheus + logs)
- Whether they can set profile per node pool (answer: they can via separate Helm releases or separate values for different deployments, but not within a single deployment unless they split workloads)

Customer can provide GPU strings seen in logs if needed; they noted they’ve seen both "NVIDIA H100" and "H100 SXM" variants depending on node image.

2025-12-08 | Diego Santos (Eng) | Example + verification steps
Example values.yaml pattern (names shown as they appear in our chart docs; your chart version may vary—use the chart README from your deployed version to confirm exact paths):

1) Pin the profile pack:
- Set a profile pack version compatible with your runtime (runtime will also log compatibility at startup).

2) Optional: Force profile selection:
- Set a forcedProfileName (e.g., "h100-balanced") if GPU detection is ambiguous.
- Leave unset for default behavior (recommended).

3) Optional: Supported overrides:
- Only set overrides for keys explicitly supported by the profile schema; otherwise the runtime validation will reject/...

2025-12-09 | Kira Thompson (Support) | Customer confirmed / closing
Customer confirmed they can pin pack version and will avoid forcing the profile unless detection is ambiguous after the upgrade.
They will:
- Start with default detection
- Enable profile selection metrics panel
- Use a single-node canary

They requested we document this in the Private operator guide; I pointed them to the runbook + ADR above and noted we can follow up if they hit a detection mismatch.

Closing as answered; customer will reopen if needed.

title: Autoscaler stickiness during overlapping cold-starts causing gradual p99 latency rise for dedicated pool
body:
Issue summary: Over the 2026-03-12 morning peak, customers on a reserved dedicated GPU pool experienced a slow but persistent p99 latency increase (from ~700ms to ~2.6s) and sporadic 5xx attach errors. Impact: customer-facing routes (primarily short streaming chat calls) degraded for multiple tenants in the affected pool. Root cause unknown; initial evidence points at autoscaler 'stickiness' when many small cold-starts overlap with a backend sidecar that delays heartbeat emissions, preventing timely GPU attach decisions.

Environment: production reserved pool: dedicated-cluster-07 (prod-us-east), affected routing group: dp-tier-3, time window: 2026-03-12 09:20-10:40 UTC.

High-level symptoms: gradual p99 increase over ~80 minutes, increase in attach backoff events, autoscaler did not add capacity promptly, batcher queues elongated, and short streaming calls blocked longer than expecte...

body:

title: Request: official backup/restore + DR runbook for Redwood Private (VPC) deployment (FinBank)
body:
Issue summary:
FinBank (Private / VPC) is requesting an official, supportable backup/restore runbook for Redwood Private control plane and a DR checklist suitable for internal audit. They are in late-stage security + reliability validation and need a customer-facing guidance doc (or an approved interim runbook) to proceed.

Customer ask / urgency:
- Provide "official" Redwood guidance for: (1) consistent backups, (2) restore into a clean cluster, (3) DR procedure (single-region) with validation steps.
- Confirm what is included/excluded (Kubernetes objects vs Redwood control plane state; secrets handling).
- Provide encryption + key management expectations (customer-managed KMS key, rotation expectations, behavior if KMS unavailable).
- Provide recommended storage backend patterns (S3 + KMS + least privilege IAM).
- Provide evidence artifacts they can keep for audit (backup manifest, ...

Customer ask / urgency:
- Provide "official" Redwood guidance for: (1) consistent backups, (2) restore into a clean cluster, (3) DR procedure (single-region) with validation steps.
- Confirm what is included/excluded (Kubernetes objects vs Redwood control plane state; secrets handling).

- Provide recommended storage backend patterns (S3 + KMS + least privilege IAM).
- Provide evidence artifacts they can keep for audit (backup manifest, ...

comments.body: 12 (Markus Klein): FinBank is blocked on DR/backup guidance for Private. They explicitly asked for a "Redwood-supported runbook" they can attach to their internal controls review. They are OK with an interim PDF/export from Confluence if we mark it as "Draft" and add support boundaries + prereqs. Can Support/SRE align on what we can safely share this week?

01 (Sean Gallagher): Triage: this is effectively a docs/supportability request, but we need to ensure the steps match current installer + helm behavior. Assigning to me for coordination. I’ll pull in Kira (docs) + Marcus (security) for wording and what’s in/out of scope. Target: share a vetted interim runbook by EOW.

26 (Kira Thompson): Docs status: customer-facing guide is in progress (redwood-docs PR-812). For interim sharing, we can export the Confluence runbooks + add a front-matter section: supported versions, prerequisites, and explicit exclusions. Please confirm the minimum supported Private version for `backup create` / `restore apply` so we don’t overpromise.

40 (Ethan Park): Current behavior: `backup create` produces a manifest + encrypted tarball of control-plane state. Restore ordering expects CRDs present and helm releases installed to the target version before applying state (PR-489 covers validation/report). Not ready to claim it’s fully self-serve without operator familiarity. Recommend we provide a "supported with Redwood assistance" note for restores until after the game day hardening.

18 (Marcus Lin): Security guidance for customer-facing language: 1) backups must be encrypted at rest; 2) do not include raw secret material in backup artifacts unless explicitly configured; default is to back up references/metadata and rely on KMS/HSM + secrets manager as the source of truth; 3) include checksum + signature verification steps where applicable; 4) state retention is customer-configurable and should align to their policy. Also include behavior guidance if KMS is unavailable during backup/restore (fail closed).

07 (Sean Gallagher): Proposed interim package to send FinBank:
- Confluence exports: Overview + Control Plane Backup Runbook + Control Plane Restore Runbook + DR Plan (VPC single-region)
- ADR excerpt for backup format/encryption rationale (sanitized)
- Short support boundaries note: we support backup creation as self-serve; restores should be performed in a planned window with Redwood on call for first run.
Please confirm if we should include Velero integration guidance or keep it out to avoid confusion.

44 (Colin O'Donnell): For FinBank specifically, keep Velero as "optional" with clear include/exclude list. They asked whether they can just rely on cluster-level backups; we should emphasize application-level restore for Redwood control plane state, and that etcd snapshot restore is last resort. If we include it, include the coordination point: do not snapshot secrets as plain YAML, and align timing so control-plane snapshot is consistent.

22 (Sean Gallagher): Sent interim runbook package to FinBank via Markus (SE) including Confluence exports + a 1-page cover note with:
- Scope: control-plane state + critical configuration; excludes model weights/caches; dataplane is stateless.
- Storage: S3 + KMS; least privilege IAM; retention suggestions.
- Validation: smoke test checklist + restore report expectations.
- Known caveats: restore requires matching target version + CRDs present; Redwood assistance recommended for first restore.
Awaiting customer feedback.

53 (Markus Klein): Customer feedback: acceptable, but they asked for two clarifications in writing:
1) RPO/RTO targets we recommend for single-region VPC (even if "best effort")
2) Explicit statement that backup artifacts do NOT contain plaintext secrets by default.
Can we add a short appendix + send?

15 (Sean Gallagher): Yes. I’ll add a short appendix referencing our internal target matrix (without overcommitting SLA). We’ll phrase as "reference targets" contingent on storage backend and operator readiness. Security statement: "no plaintext secrets by default; backups include secret references/metadata; customer secret store remains system of record". Will send today.

02 (Sean Gallagher): Appendix sent to Markus for forwarding. Also pointed them to the upcoming customer-facing docs publication path and noted we’ll notify when PR-812 lands. Internally tracking doc publication and version compatibility under ENG-9828/ENG-9830.

title: Dedicated capacity: duplicate completions and unexpected billing spike during routing cache invalidation (Greenfield Health, EU-north)
body: Issue summary: Greenfield Health reported a large unexpected billing spike and multiple duplicated chat completions during a planned cache invalidation window on Dedicated capacity in eu-north. Impact: ~18k duplicate tokens billed to customer over a 35 minute window; several sessions observed duplicate message blocks (same content repeated twice or more) causing downstream dedup and audit failures. Compliance impact: customer runs PHI-bearing prompts; duplicates caused duplicate PHI entries in their logs which they flagged as compliance risk. Timeline: 2026-03-10 02:05 UTC — scheduled cache invalidation started; 02:08–02:43 UTC — elevated duplicated completions and billing; 02:46 UTC — mitigation (routing lockout) applied; 03:10 UTC — full rollback/patch deployed. This ticket tracks the incident, investigation, and post-incident actions.

title: 504s and large latency spikes for generations with very large max_tokens / extended contexts
body:
Issue summary:
Customers using very large max_tokens and long prompt contexts are receiving 504 gateway timeouts and large tail latencies for generation requests. Errors started increasing after the runtime release 2026.03.08 which included a kernel-selection optimization and new KV cache eviction heuristics.

Impact:
- NovaSearch (enterprise) reports ~12% of their long-run generation attempts (max_tokens >= 100k) are returning 504s in us-east.
- A few other self-serve and dedicated customers have sporadic timeouts when using streaming + extended contexts.
- User-facing impact: end-users see truncated responses or failed generations for long-document summarization and ingestion jobs.

Environment:
- prod: us-east cluster serving mixed hosted/dedicated workloads
- Affected models: gptx-32k-quant, gptx-64k-legacy pinned on customer accounts
- API: /v1/generate streaming and non-streamin...

title: Staged attenuation and snapshot rehydrate guidance for tenant suffering cost/throughput spike after combined batching+prefetch+quant change
body: Issue summary: LuminaHealth reported a sudden per-token cost increase (~3x) and elevated 5xx rates for a specific tenant after enabling a combined optimization bundle (dynamic batching bump, aggressive KV prefetch, int8 quant profile). Impact: production traffic for LuminaHealth faced degraded tail latency, increased cost, and user-visible errors for ~15% of sessions. Environment: Dedicated capacity, prod-us-east, tenant id TLN-998. First observed: 2026-03-10 09:12 UTC. Customer timeframe: they enabled the optimizer bundle via Console feature flags during a morning deployment window.

comments.body: Acknowledged and asked for exact feature flag values; requested immediate log exports.

comments.author: Ravi Singh
comments.body: Applied temporary rate cap and pushed fallback routing to fp16 variant; monitoring for improvements.

comments.author: LuminaHealth - Priya Desai
comments.body: Noted latency improved after fallback; asked for recommended safe re-enable steps.

comments.author: Marco Li
comments.body: Confirmed kernel fallback pattern; suggested lowering dynamic_batching.max_batch_size to 512 and testing incremental kv_prefetch with pre-warm windows.

title: High P99 latency spikes after region-pin rotation for enterprise customer in ch-central: multi-hop routing oscillation
body: Issue summary: LumaHealth reports persistent P99 spikes (2–6s) for inference requests from ch-central after they rotated their region-pin TTL and triggered a pin refresh. Impact: customer-facing production endpoints for patient triage are experiencing degraded tail latency and occasional streaming disconnects. Timeline: 2026-03-11 22:10 UTC pin rotation initiated by customer via Console; 2026-03-11 22:12–22:40 UTC intermittent P99 spikes observed and escalated to Support. Environment: prod traffic routed through Hosted API into Dedicated pool reserved for LumaHealth, tenant set to enterprise with strict data residency to ch-central. Steps to reproduce: 1) Create a request from ch-central region with model redwood-vicuna-13b pinned to ch-central. 2) Adjust the pin TTL via Console to force a pin rotation. 3) Send steady load (RPS ~20) and monitor p50/p95/p99 on Console route and on Graf...

Impact: customer-facing production endpoints for patient triage are experiencing degraded tail latency and occasional streaming disconnects. Timeline: 2026-03-11 22:10 UTC pin rotation initiated by customer via Console; 2026-03-11 22:12–22:40 UTC intermittent P99 spikes observed and escalated to Support. comments.author: Customer (2026-03-11 22
comments.body: 45 UTC): We're seeing P99 jump from ~700ms to 3–4s intermittently after we changed the pin TTL — this is impacting our triage flows. Can you explain why requests are leaving CH?

comments.author: (Asha Patel, 2026-03-11 23
comments.body: 02 UTC): Thanks for the report. We're opening an investigation and collecting route-level traces and Grafana panels. Can you confirm if you changed any Dedicated pool scaling policies at the same time?

comments.author: Customer (2026-03-11 23
comments.body: 10 UTC): No scaling changes. Only updated the pin TTL via Console to test faster failover behavior. We need a fix or a recommended rollback plan.

comments.author: (Diego Martinez, 2026-03-12 08
comments.body: 30 UTC): Preliminary routing logs attached. We see route-select flips between ch-central and eu-west; we're engaging SRE to look for autoscaling or health-report flaps.

comments.author: SRE (Marta Ivanov, 2026-03-12 10
comments.body: 15 UTC): Observed brief worker registration delays in ch-central between 22:10–22:16 UTC correlated with the pin rotation window. Worker join events were followed by transient 'throttled' flags from the scheduler for ~40s.

comments.author: (Luis Chen, 2026-03-12 13
comments.body: 40 UTC): Kernel configuration mismatch ruled out; we reproduced oscillation in a staging replay by toggling pin TTL and simulating delayed worker joins. Suggestion: pause smart-routing re-eval on pin-refresh for 60s to allow pool stabilization.

comments.author: (Diego Martinez, 2026-03-14 09
comments.body: 20 UTC): Planned mitigation: apply a temporary routing policy to LumaHealth tenant that pins sticky routing for 90s post-pin-rotation and disable immediate canary fallback. ETA for change: 2026-03-14 11:00 UTC. Will monitor p99 for 1 hour post-change.

comments.author: Customer (LumaHealth SRE, 2026-03-14 09
comments.body: 35 UTC): Please proceed with the mitigation. We'll hold further pin TTL changes until you confirm stabilization.

title: Intermittent GPU starvation causing request fallback chains and aggressive queue thinning in dedicated cluster
body: Issue summary: Starting 2026-03-10 08:10 UTC VectorHealth reported sustained 5-10% request failures and large latency tails from their dedicated tenant in us-west-2. Impact: customer production inference pipelines experienced elevated P99 latency (2-5x baseline) and sporadic request routing to lower-cost fallback model variants, causing reduced output quality. Environment: Redwood Dedicated cluster gdc-usw2-12 serving VectorHealth reserved pool (8xA100 nodes) with autoscaler enabled and burst capacity off. Steps to reproduce: 1) Send steady 10 RPS traffic mix of 1k token prompts to gptx-7b pinned model. 2) Observe queue length and scheduler logs over 30 minutes. 3) Correlate with node GPU utilisation and host-level telemetry. Observed behavior: - Autoscaler did not add nodes despite queue growth, instead scheduler started preemptively re-routing requests to lower-cost quantized model ...

normalized.description: Issue summary: Starting 2026-03-10 08:10 UTC VectorHealth reported sustained 5-10% request failures and large latency tails from their dedicated tenant in us-west-2. Impact: customer production inference pipelines experienced elevated P99 latency (2-5x baseline) and sporadic request routing to lower-cost fallback model variants, causing reduced output quality. Environment: Redwood Dedicated cluster gdc-usw2-12 serving VectorHealth reserved pool (8xA100 nodes) with autoscaler enabled and burst capacity off. Steps to reproduce: 1) Send steady 10 RPS traffic mix of 1k token prompts to gptx-7b pinned model. 2) Observe queue length and scheduler logs over 30 minutes. 3) Correlate with node GPU utilisation and host-level telemetry. Observed behavior: - Autoscaler did not add nodes despite queue growth, instead scheduler started preemptively re-routing requests to lower-cost quantized model ...

comments.body: 01] Support (Aisha): Thanks for the report — we've started triage and pulled the last 6 hours of metrics. Can you confirm if any long-running jobs were added to the pool around 07:50 UTC?

12] Customer (VectorHealth - James Lee): No scheduled jobs were changed. We did run an A/B eval ramp at 07:45 that increased prompt length, but it was targeted to their staging cluster; nothing should have hit production.

05] Eng (Miguel): Found 2 nodes with repeated allocation errors and high kernel eviction counts. Suspect cross-tenant bin-packing causing fragmentation. Recommending temporary tenant isolation and a restart of the offending serving pods.
22] SRE (Li Wen): Implemented temporary soft-isolation: tainted 2 nodes and evicted non-reserved pods; scaled reserved pool by +2 nodes as emergency mitigation.
00] Support (Aisha): After the mitigation, VectorHealth reports P99 latency returned to baseline and fallback routing stopped. We're monitoring for recurrence over 24h.

10] Eng (Miguel): Root cause analysis complete — noisy tenant's long-sequence evals created fragmented GPU memory; autoscaler policy prevented rapid burst. Patch submitted to autoscaler to relax burst gating when allocation errors exceed threshold (PR #210).
20] Customer (James Lee): Thanks — glad to see a plan. Please share any long-term recommendations for pool sizing and isolation.

Impact: customer production inference pipelines experienced elevated P99 latency (2-5x baseline) and sporadic request routing to lower-cost fallback model variants, causing reduced output quality. Environment: Redwood Dedicated cluster gdc-usw2-12 serving VectorHealth reserved pool (8xA100 nodes) with autoscaler enabled and burst capacity off. Observed behavior: - Autoscaler did not add nodes despite queue growth, instead scheduler started preemptively re-routing requests to lower-cost quantized model ...

title: Assistance requested: optimizer misconfiguration caused throughput/latency degradation after quant/profile change; request recovery playbook and operator checklist
body: Issue summary: Customer reported a sharp increase in p95/p99 latency and user-facing timeouts after applying an updated quantization profile plus aggressive batching/caching recommendations. Impact: production inference failures and degraded UX for a subset of endpoints (customer's dedicated pool). Timeline: profile pushed ~2026-03-13 10:17 UTC; first alerts at 10:45 UTC. Customer requests an operator-focused recovery playbook to safely unwind optimizations and restore baseline latency without data loss or model-version drift.

normalized.description: Issue summary: Customer reported a sharp increase in p95/p99 latency and user-facing timeouts after applying an updated quantization profile plus aggressive batching/caching recommendations. Customer requests an operator-focused recovery playbook to safely unwind optimizations and restore baseline latency without data loss or model-version drift.

comments.author: Customer (AtlasHealth) 2026-03-13 11
comments.body: 02 UTC: 'We started seeing 504s shortly after the update. We need a safe plan to get back to baseline while we investigate quality tradeoffs.'

comments.author: (Aisha) 2026-03-13 11
comments.body: 15 UTC: 'Gathering config snapshots and telemetry; requesting permission to apply a staged rollback on a single canary lane.'

comments.author: SRE (Diego) 2026-03-13 11
comments.body: 28 UTC: 'Recommend immediate mitigation: reduce batch_max_tokens to 256 and batch_window_ms to 10 on canary nodes, disable prefix KV cache for the canary, and monitor p95/p99 for 10 minutes before widening.'

comments.author: Customer (AtlasHealth) 2026-03-13 11
comments.body: 35 UTC: 'Approved canary mitigation. Please proceed; we need clear checkpoints before full revert.'

comments.author: runtime team
comments.body: 10 UTC: 'Prepared non-quant variant (fp16) with identical model artifact tag; readiness check passed. Will coordinate a canary swap once mitigation metrics stabilize.'

comments.author: (Aisha) 2026-03-13 12
comments.body: 40 UTC: 'Canary metrics returned to baseline p95 within 8 minutes after mitigation. Preparing operator checklist for full recovery and post-mortem steps.'

title: Lingering client sockets from SDK 1.5 causing allocator attach lag and transient GPU starvation in dedicated pool
body:
Issue summary: Multiple customers on Dedicated pools (notably Nestly AI) reported sudden mid-day throughput collapse in us-west-2. Symptoms: high attach latency, GPUs showing as "unavailable" for short windows, autoscaler overprovisioning then failing to attach more slots, resulting in sustained p99 latency increase and request queueing. Impact: customer-facing inference throughput dropped ~45% for 11:20–12:15 PT window, causing timeouts and degraded SLAs (affects bursty short-stream workloads).

Timeline: 2026-03-12 11:18 PT — first alerts from console (p99 spike). 11:22 — Nestly support ticket opened. 11:27 — SRE paged; initial triage found attach latencies spiking to 2.1s (baseline ~80ms). 11:45 — noticed high ephemeral socket count on sidecar-proxies and many TIME_WAIT/FIN_WAIT connections. 11:55 — rolled mitigations (sidecar restart + temporary increase of autoscaler cooldown) re...

comments.author: Ava Thompson ()
comments.body: Initial triage attached logs and linked Nestly ticket — customer reporting 500s and long queues.

comments.author: Diego Martinez (SRE)
comments.body: Reproduced locally with stress harness and short-conn pattern; sidecar epoll loop saturating. Restarted sidecars on impacted nodes — p99 improved to 300ms then 95ms after rollback.

comments.author: Priya Rao (SDK)
comments.body: Confirmed SDK 1.5 introduced new default connection behavior; patch PR opened to re-enable keepalive by default for dedicated-pool customers (https://github.com/redwood-inference/clients/pull/214).

comments.author: SRE Note
comments.body: We should consider adding a per-sidecar fd soft-limit alarm and prefer connection-reuse in clients. Also tracking related autoscaler PR #991 for fast-path attach.

comments.author: Nestly AI - Emily Chen
comments.body: We rolled back to SDK 1.4.2 as requested and saw latency go back down. Would like to coordinate a grace period before SDK 1.5 is re-promoted.

title: Regional affinity routing behavior during capacity surge and pin-unavailable fallthrough
body:
Issue summary: Customer reports higher p95 latency for requests originating from EU users after they pinned to eu-west. When eu-west pool reported transient capacity pressure the routing behavior appeared to route some traffic to us-east resulting in increased latency and customer concern about data egress.

Impact: Intermittent increase in p95 latency from ~140ms to ~420ms for inference calls; some requests show cross-region egress. Customer is dedicated tier with reserved capacity, expecting strict region affinity unless strictly unavailable.

Observed window: 2026-03-01T09:10Z to 2026-03-01T09:44Z.

Expected: When a customer pins region to eu-west with dedicated capacity, traffic should preferentially remain in eu-west. Only when strict affinity policy is not possible (e.g., no compatible capacity and strict failover disabled) should traffic fall back, and this behavior should be c...

comments.body: Picked up ticket. Reached out to customer for logs and traffic samples. Asked for timeframe and confirmation of pin settings.

comments.author: Maria Duarte, LuminaHealth
comments.body: Provided sample request IDs and said they must avoid cross-region egress for compliance reasons. Asked for immediate mitigation.

comments.author: Mateo Ruiz
comments.body: Confirmed autoscaler saw short capacity exhaustion; routing policy is 'prefer' which allows soft fallback. Recommend strict_region if customer prefers fail-fast. Linked autoscaler panic logs: https://internal.redwood/sre/logs/ausc-20260301-09:11

comments.author: Sophie Chen
comments.body: Logged UX issue to improve wording in console (PR https://github.com/redwood-inference/console/pull/981). Agreed to prioritize inline warning and link to routing runbook.

comments.author: (Liam O'Connor)
comments.body: Implemented temporary routing policy change per customer's request and validated via request headers. Waiting for customer confirmation that behavior is acceptable.

title: Chat sessions show prolonged priming and delayed first tokens after kernel optimizer rollout
body:
Issue summary:
Customer (NovusWallet, dedicated) reports a noticeable increase in time-to-first-token (TTFB) for short-chat completions (priming/initial message) starting immediately after the kernel optimizer hotfix (runtime v2.4.7 -> 2.4.8) and a compiler micro-optimization merged on 2026-03-09. The effect is most visible on small payload chats (<= 80 tokens) and impacts both streaming and non-streaming completion endpoints.

Impact:
- Customer reports degraded UX: single-turn quick replies take 2.5–4s longer to produce the first token vs baseline.
- Error rates are slightly elevated for streaming sessions (intermittent 504s during slow priming).
- Affects dedicated cluster for NovusWallet and appears in us-east and us-west pools.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: nv-rt-dedicated-42 (prod, us-east-1)
- Runtime: serving-runtime v2.4.8 (kernel optimizer + scheduler affinity change)
- Co...

comments.author: Ava Patel
comments.body: Received initial report from NovusWallet via support portal. Customer cites a sudden UX regression after the runtime update on 2026-03-09. Collected request IDs and asked for sample traces and representative prompt payloads.

comments.author: SRE - Marco Ruiz
comments.body: Confirmed spike in TTFB on us-east dedicated pool starting ~2026-03-09 22:00 UTC. Grabbed flame graphs and allocation traces; pointing to pre-inference validation step as hot path.

comments.author: Priya Srinivasan
comments.body: Reproduced locally when enabling compiler optimizer flag. Observed fallback into compatibility kernel for certain quantized tensors — adds 200–350ms on first token. Opening PR to add telemetry: https://github.com/redwood-inference/runtime/pull/482

comments.author: Scheduler - Omar Haddad
comments.body: Noted that the scheduler affinity change in v2.4.8 can increase short-request queue latency under burst. Working on a conservative affinity override for dedicated pools. PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/scheduler/pull/214

comments.author: Ava Patel
comments.body: Shared rollback plan and mitigation with NovusWallet. Customer accepted temporary pinning to older runtime nodes for small payload traffic. They requested ETA for permanent fix and asked for usage credit for the days impacted.

comments.author: Liam Chen
comments.body: Canary rollback executed on 4/8 nodes in NovusWallet pool; initial telemetry shows median TTFB back down to ~150ms for small prompts. Continuing to monitor p95 and 504s.

title: Unexpected routing to batch-optimized fallback causing abrupt p99 spikes and timeout errors
body:
Issue summary:
Customer (ZenoRetail, dedicated) is seeing sudden p99 latency spikes and a wave of 504 timeouts starting 2026-03-10 ~09:15 UTC. Traffic that normally hits the low-latency variant is being routed to a batch-optimized fallback variant which has significantly longer cold-start and per-request latency under typical single-request traffic patterns. Impact: several production storefront features were rate-limited by the customer, affecting order recommendations and search autocomplete.

Impact:
- Observed p50/p95/p99 jump by ~2x/3x/6x respectively during incidents.
- ~1.2% of requests returned 504 over a 30-minute window; customer reports user-facing timeouts.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool (ZenoRetail, reserved GPUs) in us-west-1
- Models involved: gptx-8k-lowlatency-v2 (preferred) and gptx-8k-batch-opt-quantized (fallback)

Notes: This ticket captures the investigation into...

comments.body: Acknowledged. Spun up temp override to pin route to lowlatency variant and notified infra to pre-warm one batch variant replica.

comments.body: Confirmed that user timeouts dropped and that product is back to usable in affected endpoints. Requested root-cause and prevention steps.

comments.body: Infra onboarding template updated in branch 'onboard-fix-minwarm'; will coordinate QA and rollout to dedicated pools. Related PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/infra/pull/98

title: Sustained 5xx spike on AtlasAI tenant after Fluent Bit backlog saturated node I/O causing runtime page-faults and restarts
body:
Issue summary:
Customer AtlasAI reported a sudden, sustained increase in 5xx responses across generation and streaming endpoints beginning ~2026-03-14T10:12Z. Errors were observed for ~20 minutes and correlated with elevated host disk iowait and multiple serving-runtime restarts.

Impact:
- Enterprise customer (AtlasAI) observed 30-40% request failure rate for streaming sessions and short generations.
- Customers reported timeouts and half-closed streaming sessions which required client-side reconnects.
- Business impact: degraded revenue features for AtlasAI; ticket opened by CS for immediate mitigation.

Environment:
- Deployment: Dedicated private cluster for AtlasAI (enterprise).
- Region: prod us-east-1.
- Routing: gateway -> per-tenant edge proxy -> serving-runtime pool pinned to tenant.
- Observability/log pipeline: Fluent Bit forwarder on each node collecting runtime logs and ...

comments.body: 20Z - Aisha Patel (Support): Logged ticket from AtlasAI after they reported 30% failure rate on streaming endpoints. Escalated to SRE on-call.
24Z - Ethan Moore (SRE): Observed iowait spike on tenant nodes; traced to Fluent Bit forwarder backlog. Opening investigation.
30Z - Aisha Patel (Support): Notified AtlasAI that we're throttling log forwarder and applying temporary storage IOPS increase; asked for confirmation if they initiated any large-batch log export.

37Z - AtlasAI (customer): Confirmed they had turned on additional log archival (Console) ~10:00Z for a compliance request. Noted service quality is restored after your changes. Thanks for rapid response.
05Z - Ethan Moore (SRE): Collecting artifacts (iostat, dmesg, fluentbit metrics) and preparing follow-up with runtime team to add protection against forwarder-induced iowait spikes.

title: ZephyrInsurance inter-tenant context bleed during cluster maintenance — urgent remediation and post-incident audit
body:
Issue summary: During a scheduled maintenance window on 2026-03-10 for a control-plane kernel migration, a replication edge-case caused in-memory KV entries belonging to one tenant to be served to a different tenant's inference stream. Impact: Potential PII exposure across 3 sessions for ZephyrInsurance and another enterprise tenant; streaming responses contained short prompt fragments from unrelated accounts. This ticket tracks the incident timeline, mitigation, customer communication, and required follow-up audits.

Environment: Hosted API, production, us-east-1 region, serving-runtime v2.14.1, control-plane migration job #cp-migrate-20260310.

Immediate impact: Customer-facing streaming completions returned mixed-context fragments for a subset of requests between 2026-03-10T03:22Z and 2026-03-10T03:46Z. Affected traffic routed through GPU pool rp-us-east-1-gpu-4.

Preserved artifac...

comments.body: 30Z - Maya Chen (Support): Customer reported streaming included fragments from other prompts. Confirmed request IDs and escalated to SRE on-call (pager duty P0).
42Z - SRE (Ethan Rivera): Isolating promotion events. Pausing migration across us-east-1. Notifying other impacted tenants and pausing all scheduled maintenance.
10Z - SRE (Aisha Patel): Hotfix applied to zero buffers on promotion; canary traffic shows no mixed-context. Preparing rollback plan if anomalies reappear.

00Z - CS (Jordan Lee): Notified ZephyrInsurance legal and security teams with initial IR doc, shared affected request IDs and remediation steps. Offered dedicated audit and credit. Customer requested an executive review.
20Z - Security (Nina Gupta): Performed initial log sweep; confirmed no persisted cross-tenant artifacts. Recommended extended telemetry retention and a scoped forensics engagement.
00Z - Incident Commander (Ethan Rivera): Declared incident resolved after 48 hours of clean streaming traces and SRE sign-off. Follow-up actions assigned.

title: Prod dedicated pool throughput regression caused by ephemeral customer sidecar cohort creating GPU slot fragmentation and autoscaler misclassification
body:
Issue summary:
Customer BrightHome AI observed degraded throughput and elevated p99 latency for their dedicated pool during daytime peak. Symptoms included sudden slot fragmentation (many small GPU attachments that never consolidated), autoscaler repeatedly scaling down after short-lived attach spikes, and brief regional GPU shortages that triggered cross-tenant fallback routing. Impact was limited to the BrightHome reserved cluster but autoscaler oscillation caused transient performance variance for other dedicated tenants in the stripe.

Impact:
- BrightHome AI production routes experienced ~35% throughput reduction and increased failures on batched routes.
- Autoscaler made multiple downscaling decisions that later had to be reversed, causing thrash and occasional 5xx fallbacks for low-priority routes.

Timeline (local times, PDT):
- 07:50 — Support received first alert from Bright...

comments.body: We're seeing a lot of 502s and p99 latency spikes starting ~08:00 PDT. Aisha opened the ticket referencing our reserved pool.

comments.author: (Aisha)
comments.body: Investigating; I've escalated to SRE and asked for allocator dumps and autoscaler logs.

comments.author: SRE (Tom)
comments.body: Noted high connection churn from a small cohort of sidecar hosts; appears to be many short streams (single token-ish requests) interleaving. Starting temporary throttle on their connection concurrency.
Customer accepted throttle test; reported immediate reduction in failures within 15 minutes.

comments.author: SRE (Tom)
comments.body: Adjusted autoscaler downscale hysteresis to 6m for the stripe. Monitoring for oscillation.

title: Guided 'sane-fallback' mode and orchestrated unwind for aggressive optimizer profile causing tail-latency spikes
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports intermittent 95/99p tail-latency spikes after enabling a tenant-scoped aggressive optimizer profile (async batching + kv-prefetch + mixed-quant async path). The customer asks for a guided, low-risk way to move to a temporary 'sane-fallback' operational mode (turn off the aggressive knobs per-tenant) and to orchestrate a safe unwind that preserves session KV state where possible.

Impact:
- Brightroute AI (dedicated) is seeing 95p latency climb from ~220ms -> >1.2s for long sessions (context >2048).
- Customer-facing inference endpoints are experiencing degraded SLA adherence for peak traffic windows.

Goal:
- Provide an operator-tested stepwise plan to toggle a \"sane-fallback\" mode for the tenant, validate behavior, and roll back to the aggressive profile only when safe. Include preflight checks, telemetry assertions, and a snapshot/restore approach f...

title: Pinecone enterprise contract negotiation and seat provisioning for vector search rollout
body:
Issue summary: Request procurement of an enterprise Pinecone contract to support Redwood's vector search + embeddings production rollout. We need seat provisioning, SSO/SAML, VPC peering, and an enterprise SLA. This ticket tracks negotiation, legal review, PO issuance, and seat provisioning coordination with Infra and Product teams.

Background: The Search & Retrieval team plans to move an internal pilot into production for the Redwood Console's semantic search and a few customer-facing features. Pilot used Pinecone managed service; scale and compliance requirements now require an enterprise contract and dedicated project management.

Requested timeline: contract signed + seats provisioned by 2026-03-31 to meet product release milestones.

comments.author: Aisha Patel
comments.body: Opening ticket. Product requests enterprise features and predictable pricing for upcoming Console search rollout. Attaching preliminary cost spreadsheet and pilot usage metrics.

comments.author: Miguel Santos
comments.body: Reached out to Pinecone AE Jordan Lee for formal quote and enterprise terms. Requested SSO and networking requirements. Awaiting quote; will open PR to finance once PO estimate confirmed.

comments.author: Nina Cho
comments.body: We need a dataflow diagram showing what PII (if any) will be stored/processed in Pinecone and retention policy. Please attach to this ticket. Also requesting DPA redline window.

comments.author: Ravi Kapoor
comments.body: Provided preliminary security questionnaire to Pinecone; scheduled joint call 2026-03-09 to review VPC peering and encryption at rest options. Also asked for SOC2 type 2 and pen test summary.

comments.author: Leah Kim
comments.body: Preliminary budget sign-off for up to $130k/year contingent on legal and security approvals. Please add final vendor quote and PO fields.

comments.author: Miguel Santos
comments.body: Received updated quote from Pinecone including discounts for annual commit and 10 dedicated projects. Uploaded quote to vendor portal link. Preparing PO draft.

comments.author: Nina Cho
comments.body: Reviewed initial DPA; proposing three redlines around data deletion timelines and subprocessor notifications. Shared redline doc with vendor; awaiting response.

title: Chat session launch lag after serving-runtime heap tuning and model repin
body:
Issue summary:
Customers reported higher-than-usual time-to-first-token (TTFB) when starting chat completions after the 2025-02-11 deployment that included a serving-runtime heap tuning change and a model repin to redwood/gpt-medium-v2-quant. Impact:
- Multiple enterprise customers (notably Acme Finance Inc.) observed median TTFB increase from ~120ms to ~850ms for the first response in chat sessions.
- Some sessions time out on client after 8s, resulting in 504s and session re-tries.
Environment:
- Prod clusters in us-east (primary) and us-west (fallback).
- Deployment on 2025-02-11 contained: runtime heap tuning (gc thresholds lowered), compiler kernel swap, and model pin from -v2 to -v2-quant for cost testing.
Steps to reproduce:
1. Start a new chat session via /v1/chat/completions with model redwood/gpt-medium-v2-quant and streaming=true.
2. Observe initial token emission latency m...

comments.body: Customer reported multiple 504s and slow initial chat replies; we're triaging.

comments.author: Liam O'Connor (SRE)
comments.body: Noted deploys 2025-02-11 included heap tuning and compiler kernel swap; prioritizing canary rollback.

comments.author: (runtime)
comments.body: Found GC pause events correlated to request timestamps; identified heap tuning flags that reduced heap slack.

comments.author: (compiler)
comments.body: Disabled lazy kernel init on canary; canary metrics show TTFB improvement.

comments.author: Aisha Cortez ()
comments.body: Customer confirms improved latency after mitigation; monitoring continues

title: Transient GPU visibility loss causing stepwise throughput degradation for dedicated tenant
body:
Issue summary: Beginning 2026-03-10 09:12 UTC, AtlasDocs (dedicated pool) observed stepwise throughput degradation across their reserved capacity window. The platform reported transient GPU visibility losses on a subset of nodes which triggered autoscaler shrink and forced funneling of traffic to fewer GPUs, increasing p99 latency and reducing sustained throughput.
Impact: Customer-facing inference pipelines experienced 30–40% reduction in tokens/sec for 2 of their primary routes and elevated p99 latency from ~120ms to ~480ms. No user prompts were lost but tail latency caused downstream timeouts in customer orchestration (retry storms).
Timeline highlights:
- 09:12 UTC: first sign of allocation delay spikes (allocation-failures metric up to 1.2%).
- 09:14 UTC: scheduler logged node-agent disconnects; 3 nodes reported GPU visibility loss for 10–25s.
- 09:20 UTC: autoscaler interpreted ...

- 09:14 UTC: scheduler logged node-agent disconnects; 3 nodes reported GPU visibility loss for 10–25s.
- 09:20 UTC: autoscaler interpreted ...

comments.body: 35 UTC - Diego Morales (SRE): We've applied an autoscaler hold for AtlasDocs to prevent further shrink events while we investigate node instability. Quarantined nodes node-uw2-47 and node-uw2-61 for deeper diagnostics.
48 UTC - Aisha Patel (Support): Customer reports that the downstream orchestrator timed out on webhook calls. They increased retry backoff per our suggestion; they want ETA for full resolution.

05 UTC - Eng (Liu Chen): Early hypothesis: intermittent PCIe reset or driver watchdog on a subset of Ampere GPUs triggered temporary visibility loss. Need node dmesg and nvidia logs. Asked infra to collect /var/log/messages and /var/log/nvidia-installer.log from quarantined nodes.

20 UTC - Infra (Samir Kapoor): Collected node logs and saw repeated pcie link down/up events on node-uw2-61 around 09:13 UTC. Also observed kernel oops relating to nvlink health probe; possible kernel module regression after 2026-02-20 driver bundle.

10 UTC - Diego Morales: Rolling plan: (1) hold autoscaler for 24h while we schedule kernel/driver rollback on the affected node pool; (2) add additional node-agent health check for GPU visibility and classify short blips under a grace window so autoscaler doesn't immediately shrink. Draft PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/serving-runtime/pull/2145

45 UTC - Customer (AtlasDocs - Elena Rojas): Thanks for the quick mitigation. Please advise if you plan to notify us of schedule for longer maintenance; our production window is weekdays 14:00–20:00 UTC.
00 UTC - Aisha Patel: Noted customer maintenance window. Scheduling engineer-led rollback to a previously stable driver for the pool and will send follow-up timeline before change window.
