title: OrchidLogistics: GPU eviction in dedicated pool caused partial transcript corruption and audit log delivery delay - customer escalation & post-incident action plan
body:
Issue summary: On 2026-02-25 12:07 UTC a subset of requests routed to OrchidLogistics' dedicated GPU pool experienced in-flight GPU evictions that correlated with partial transcript corruption (missing tokens/mid-stream truncation) and a delayed audit log delivery to Orchid's S3 bucket. Impact: ~11% of requests for the customer during a 14-minute window had partial transcripts or repaired replays; audit log ingestion lagged by up to 3 hours for the same time window, preventing immediate forensic validation. Immediate customer impact: downstream reconciliation pipelines flagged missing segments and Orchids' billing reconciler reported a discrepancy. Customer escalated to CSM and legal raised an evidence request within 3 hours.

Timeline (high level):
- 2026-02-25 12:06 UTC: Canary autoscaler triggered a rapid pod replacement in dedicated pool (us-west).
- 12:07-12:21 UTC: Multiple GPU ...

comments.author: Samir Patel ()
comments.body: Customer reports increased wait for initial token after our optimizer swap. Requested request IDs and sample traces; escalated to SRE. (see attachments)

comments.author: Maya Rivera (SRE)
comments.body: Confirmed elevated TTFB in our internal traces for dp-0319. p95 jumped from 220ms to ~1.8s. Opening investigation: hypothesis list includes kernel selection cold path (JIT/compile), KV cache warming, and routing to quantized variant causing extra pre-processing.

comments.author: Jerome Liu
comments.body: Noted that PR #4823 introduced a lazy-kernel-initialize path which may add overhead on first batch for certain sequence-length distributions. Also seeing scheduler choose low-fill micro-batches at session startup. Adding telemetry to measure kernel_init_duration and first_batch_fill.

comments.author: to customer
comments.body: Temporary mitigation: pin route to previous runtime tag rtime-2026-02-28 which avoids the optimizer swap (instructions provided). Customer confirms they can apply pin and will test ramp for next business day.

comments.author: BrightHealth AI (Customer)
comments.body: After pinning to rtime-2026-02-28, initial token latency returned to expected baseline. Customer requesting a permanent fix so they can resume optimizer-enabled runtime.

comments.author: Maya Rivera (SRE)
comments.body: Additional finding: kernel_init accounted for ~600-900ms on affected requests and scheduler's early micro-batches had fill factors <25% causing additional queuing. Working with runtime eng to add eager kernel pre-touch for dedicated pools and tweak batching heuristic thresholds for session-start windows.

comments.author: Jerome Liu ()
comments.body: Raised github PR to add a small pre-warm step when a session is allocated on dedicated pools (see PR #5078). Also adding metric kernel_init_duration to the console for per-pool alerting.
Updated ticket with workaround steps and ETA for code rollout. Assigned follow-up task to validate hotfix in canary and then schedule a controlled rollout for dedicated pools.

title: Sustained pipeline lag in dedicated pool triggered by burst of small-tenant cold-starts
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports sustained p99/p95 latency regression in their dedicated GPU pool that coincided with a sudden increase in many very short-lived streaming requests from several of their microservices. The behaviour started ~2026-03-12T09:15 UTC and persisted for multiple hours with intermittent recoveries.

Impact:
- Affected customer: Acme AI (dedicated).
- Visible degradation: p99 latency increased from ~220ms to 900-1,500ms for text generation routes; throughput dropped ~18% on the dedicated pool.
- Error rate: no large spike in 5xx; a small increase in 429s for burst limits on some routes.

Environment:
- prod | us-west-2 dedicated pool (pool id: ded-usw2-37).
- Models: redwood-open-7b (primary), redwood-compat-2.7b-quant (fallback).

Steps to reproduce (best-effort):
1) Send a high fanout of short, streaming requests (avg latency per request < 200ms, request tokens...

comments.author: Marco Ruiz
comments.body: Ran quick trace. Noting high kernel_warmup events and batch sizes often 1-2. Attempting increased batching window rollout to trial config change.

comments.author: Elena Gomez, Acme AI
comments.body: Confirmed we deployed a new worker pool yesterday that fans out many tiny requests. We'll provide request logs and traffic samples.

comments.author: Ava Patel
comments.body: Shared recommended client-side pooling pattern and sample code. Requested telemetry showing which microservice is the top contributor.

comments.author: Marco Ruiz
comments.body: Applied autoscaler debounce and report: allocation churn dropped 72% after change; batch size improved. Need deeper analysis on slot fragmentation across the fleet.

comments.author: Priya Shah
comments.body: Opened infra PR to add small-stream-optimized pool config and autoscaler-clustering heuristics: https://github.com/redwood-inference/infra/pull/4123

comments.author: Ava Patel
comments.body: Customer accepted workaround for now; requested timeline for infra change. Scheduling follow-up call 2026-03-16.

comments.author: Marco Ruiz
comments.body: Next action: run allocation heatmap and per-tenant allocation dwell statistics (ETA 48hrs).

title: Provision contextual Slack thread archiver that auto-creates Jira runbooks and performs on-the-fly translation
body: Request: Build a Slack automation that watches designated channels, snapshots long/important threads to Confluence as lightweight runbooks, optionally spins up a linked internal Jira ticket when the thread contains action items (detect via simple heuristics), and performs on-the-fly translation for non-English threads. Rationale: We have recurring support and post-incident threads that live in Slack and get lost; engineering and SRE teams want a low-friction way to capture actions and runbooks without manual copy/paste. This should reduce knowledge loss, speed incident follow-ups, and satisfy retention/compliance needs. Constraints: minimal latency impact to Slack UX, strictly respect channel-level RBAC and privacy flags, and provide admins an allowlist of channels and a per-channel retention tag. Privacy note: translation and content extraction must obey existing data classification ...

comments.body: Can we reuse the existing thread-pruner lambda and extend it to write to Confluence? Need to check scopes.

comments.author: Maya Patel ()
comments.body: We prefer a new Slack app rather than overloading the pruner; also want translations tested with our EU customer threads.

comments.author: Priya Rao ()
comments.body: Note: any export of threads tagged PII must be blocked. Add a pre-export classifier step and owner approval workflow.

comments.author: Alex Novak (SRE)
comments.body: Quick check: Jira project mapping should support per-team defaults and override per-channel. Also recommend a feature flag rollout to limited pods.

comments.author: Sarah Li (Docs)
comments.body: When runbooks are created, they should include metadata: originating channel, thread ts, author, and translated languages. We can add a template macro to render that.

title: Identity cookie path namespace collision causes intermittent tenant resolution and cross-tenant auth errors in private deployment
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports intermittent 403/401 errors for admin and service accounts after a recent ingress upgrade. Errors are scoped to a private VPC deployment and only occur for a subset of tenant sessions.

Impact:
- Multiple internal tools for VectorHealth cannot perform tenant-scoped admin actions during the window.
- Errors are intermittent which causes user confusion and increases support load.
- Customer is on a Private deployment with sensitive workflows; they flagged this as impacting a scheduled compliance demo.

Environment:
- Deployment: Private (customer-managed ingress + Redwood private control plane)
- Region: us-west-2 (customer edge)
- Affected services: auth proxy (edge), session-store (redis), control-plane authz checks

Steps to reproduce (observed by customer):
1) Log into tenant A via SSO (IdP A). Cookie set for domain example.customer-vpc.internal with ...

1) Log into tenant A via SSO (IdP A). Cookie set for domain example.customer-vpc.internal with ...

comments.author: Aisha Kumar ()
comments.body: Initial triage and requested cookie dump, recent deploy timeline, and sample request IDs. Escalated to SRE due to P1/sev1 impact.

comments.author: Ethan Park (SRE)
comments.body: Confirmed customer saw ingress-proxy v2.4.0 upgrade around window. Asked customer for sample request IDs and the ingress routing logs from 2026-03-08 02:50-03:30 UTC.

comments.author: VectorHealth (Customer)
comments.body: Uploaded cookie samples and two request IDs (req-9a1b, req-9a1c). Noted issue reproduces after tenant switch in the same browser session.

comments.author: Ethan Park (SRE)
comments.body: Found ingress logs showing the same cookie name 'rwd_session' present for both tenantA and tenantB, but with different path attributes (/ and /app). Observed that some ingress instances were configured with cookie-path rewrite enabled in v2.4.0.

comments.author: Marta Li
comments.body: Noted potential regression introduced by cookie-path rewrite feature. Recommended rolling ingress-proxy back to v2.3.8 in a canary first and requesting a workaround to disable cookie-path rewrite for private customers until fix.

comments.author: VectorHealth (Customer)
comments.body: Applied temporary mitigation (ingress config change) per Support's instructions and reported drop in errors to near-zero. Requested permanent fix timeline.

comments.author: Ethan Park (SRE)
comments.body: Creating follow-up engineering ticket and preparing a patch that preserves tenant-scoped cookie naming and disables path-rewrite semantics by default for private deployments.

comments.author: Aisha Kumar ()
comments.body: Marking ticket In Progress; customer working on compliance demo with mitigation in place.

comments.author: (comment)
comments.body: Patch branch created: github.com/redwood-inference/ingress-proxy/pull/678. Patch includes: (1) tenant-scoped cookie prefixing, (2) explicit per-tenant cookie path enforcement, (3) feature-flag to opt-in path rewrite in hosted mode only.

title: Midweek fractional GPU starvation triggers batched-route fallback oscillation for customer NimbleDocs
body: Issue summary: Customer NimbleDocs reports degraded throughput and repeated fallback routing during their midday traffic spike. Impact: sustained p50/p95 throughput drop on their dedicated pool, many short-lived batched requests degraded to fallback model variants; customer SLA degradation risk. Observed behavior: routing alternates between primary reserved-slot instances and fallback burst pool every ~90s; batch queue grows then drains in oscillation. First reported 2026-03-10 09:40 PDT. Customer reported increased 5xx rate when load is steady and many short streams are present.

Observed behavior: routing alternates between primary reserved-slot instances and fallback burst pool every ~90s; batch queue grows then drains in oscillation. First reported 2026-03-10 09:40 PDT. Customer reported increased 5xx rate when load is steady and many short streams are present.

comments.author: (Aisha)
comments.body: Customer reported steady request rate but intermittent throughput drops starting 09:40 PDT. Logged their traffic sampler and asked for traces.

comments.author: SRE (Liam)
comments.body: Investigating autoscaler logs; seeing frequent scale-down decisions triggered by failed attachments. Pulling GPU host S.M.A.R.T and dmesg logs.

comments.author: NimbleDocs ops
comments.body: We saw users hit higher latency and occasional 502s on 10-Mar 09:40-09:55 PDT. We can throttle short-chat traffic for experiment if useful.

comments.author: Priya Rao
comments.body: Noted two GPU hosts with elevated PCIe xfer latency (host-gpu-12 and host-gpu-17). Opening infra-priority alert for hardware triage (PR #918 referenced).

comments.author: (Aisha)
comments.body: Applied recommended temporary routing change (hold fallback for 60s) and extended autoscaler cooldown. Monitoring with customer now.

comments.author: NimbleDocs ops
comments.body: After the change latency improved and p95 stabilized. Will report if recurrence happens during next peak.

comments.author: SRE (Liam)
comments.body: Plan: replace suspected hosts from rotation after maintenance window; longer-term: refine autoscaler signals to consider short-stream fraction to avoid oscillation.

title: Misaligned batcher window triggers eviction wave and sustained p99 jitter for dedicated tenant
body:
Issue summary:
A dedicated-tenant (NimbusAI) is experiencing repeated p99 latency spikes and throughput flicker across their reserved pool beginning 2026-03-10 08:40 UTC. Symptoms correlate with autoscaler scale-down windows and an eviction wave that affects both short-lived inference streams (interactive chat) and long-lived batched streams (bulk embedding).

Impact:
- Customer-facing latency p99 increased from ~450ms to 2.1s intermittently during peak window (08:30–09:30 UTC).
- Sustained reduction in overall throughput (~18% drop) for the tenant over a 45m window.
- No explicit OOMs or kernel panics observed; GPUs briefly detach from serving pool and are reattached after eviction waves.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: nimbus-dedicated-cluster-07 (G5/mixtures), autoscaler policy: predictive with conservative cooldown (300s).
- Region: us-west-2.
- Routes impacted: /v1/chat/stream (s...

comments.body: Customer raised ticket after noticing repeated p99 spikes during their morning window. Collected initial logs and escalated to SRE.

comments.author: SRE (Evan)
comments.body: Reproduced correlation between autoscaler scale-down and eviction logs. Noted GPUs gpux-12/13/14 showing repeated detach cycles. Beginning deeper kernel and NVLink diagnostics.

comments.author: NimbusAI - Alex Chen
comments.body: We saw a similar blip last week but it resolved. After your last change to predictive policy, frequency increased. Happy to provide sample traffic replay and allow limited maintenance window.

comments.author: SRE (Evan)
comments.body: Requesting permission for a 30m maintenance window to disable predictive scale-down and force a graceful drain to validate mitigation. Also requesting access to representative traffic replay (sanitized).

comments.author: Priya Kumar
comments.body: Flagging potential bug in batcher drain logic where eviction is synchronous instead of async during scale-down. Opening a tracking PR to add non-blocking drain and KV cache handoff metrics. See related PR linked.

comments.author: (Tanya)
comments.body: Provided customer the tentative maintenance window 2026-03-11 09:00–09:30 UTC and instructions for safe replay files.

title: Guided demobilization of tenant optimizer stack after sustained latency and spawning 5xx errors
body: Issue summary: ClearWave Labs reported a sustained rise in 95th/99th percentile latency and intermittent 5xx errors after enabling a tenant-scoped optimizer profile that combines async-batching, kv-prefetch, and aggressive per-tenant quantization. Impact: Production traffic for several routes saw elevated tail latency (95p >= 1.4s, baseline 95p ~0.45s) and ~1.2% 5xx rate for ~45 minutes, causing degraded UX for multiple customer endpoints. Environment: Dedicated pool in us-west-2, tenant id cw-prod-17, config snapshot applied 2026-03-10T01:22:00Z. Steps to reproduce: 1) On dedicated pool, enable optimizer profile cw-agg-2 on tenant cw-prod-17. 2) Send mixed-length traffic similar to ClearWave's traffic pattern (avg seq len 380 tokens, bursts of 20 concurrent requests). 3) Observe 95/99 p latency increase and occasional serving-runtime OOM/retry 5xx. Logs / traces: - Observability link...

Impact: Production traffic for several routes saw elevated tail latency (95p >= 1.4s, baseline 95p ~0.45s) and ~1.2% 5xx rate for ~45 minutes, causing degraded UX for multiple customer endpoints. Environment: Dedicated pool in us-west-2, tenant id cw-prod-17, config snapshot applied 2026-03-10T01:22:00Z. Logs / traces: - Observability link...

comments.author: (Maya)
comments.body: Initial triage completed. Reached out to SRE to check GPU restarts and to ClearWave for traffic capture. Assigned to Rafael for config rollback assist.

comments.author: SRE (Rafael)
comments.body: Observed worker churn correlated with kv-prefetch saturation and large async batch windows. GPU memory peaks followed by retries. Patching a quick circuit to reduce async batch max_window to 16 for affected tenant could stabilize.

comments.author: (Maya)
comments.body: Customer agreed to a controlled demobilization sequence. ETA to start: 03:30 UTC. Customer noted this is high-impact for nightly jobs between 03:00-05:00 UTC.

comments.author: Priya Anand
comments.body: Executed soft-demobilize: disabled kv-prefetch for tenant, rehydrated model pin to rw-7b-fp16, and set per-tenant max_async_batch=16. Preliminary telemetry shows 95p reduced to ~0.5s and 5xx dropped to 0.02%.

comments.author: Customer (ClearWave, Alan)
comments.body: Thanks team — telemetry looks stable this morning. Please provide recommended long-term plan and opt-profile adjustments.

comments.author: (Rafael)
comments.body: Documenting follow-up actions and scheduling postmortem. Leaving ticket open for confirmation of follow-ups.

comments.author: (Maya)
comments.body: Added action item to add per-tenant optimizer snapshot-and-rollback tool to backlog (ENG-issue #4578).

Steps to reproduce: 1) On dedicated pool, enable optimizer profile cw-agg-2 on tenant cw-prod-17. 2) Send mixed-length traffic similar to ClearWave's traffic pattern (avg seq len 380 tokens, bursts of 20 concurrent requests). 3) Observe 95/99 p latency increase and occasional serving-runtime OOM/retry 5xx. Logs / traces: - Observability link...

title: Burst cascade causing rapid autoscale thrash and degraded dedicated throughput for enterprise embeddings workload
body:
Issue summary:
Customer LexaSearch reported repeated throughput collapse and elevated p95/p99 for dedicated pool during short embedding bursts between 02:00-03:00 UTC. Behavior appears tied to a bursty stream of ~50k small (<100 token) embedding calls over 2-3 minutes that coincided with the autoscaler initiating multiple quick scale-ups and immediate scale-downs.

Impact:
- LexaSearch reported 30-60% reduction in steady-state throughput for their reserved capacity route during spikes.
- Several 5xx and timeouts observed from their client; downstream pipeline retries amplified burst.
- Business impact: search indexing delayed; premium SLA risk.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: reserv-lexa-prod-usw-1
- Region: us-west
- Models: open-embedding-large-v2 (primary), open-encoder-bf16-2 (fallback)
- Customer plan: enterprise, pinned route to dedicated pool

1) ...

title: Request: Incremental Kafka audit export of tenant-scoped request/response pairs with per-token cost attribution, reversible pseudonymization, signed batch manifests, and legal-hold carve-outs for regulator
body:
Issue summary:
Customer (Delta Bank, enterprise) requests a high-fidelity, near-real-time Kafka export of per-tenant inference request/response pairs (RPC-like call pairs) to integrate with their QRadar SIEM and internal billing reconciliation. Export must include per-call token counts and cost attribution, reversible pseudonymization for PII, a signed batch manifest (HMAC/ED25519) per exported partition with resume tokens, and a legal-hold carve-out mechanism to prevent deletion of specific customer user IDs on demand from their compliance team.

Impact:
- Delta Bank needs this for regulator evidence and billing reconciliation across multiple regions. Without this export they cannot accept Redwood as a vendor for regulated production workloads.

Request details / acceptance criteria:
1) Incremental Kafka topic per-customer (or partitioned with tenant ID header) with ordered events an...

comments.body: Uploaded Delta Bank compliance questionnaire and example CSV of their expected fields. Customer emphasises regulator audit window and ability to request decryption under legal processes.

comments.author: Marco DeLuca ()
comments.body: Triage complete. Looping in @eng-billing and @eng-data-privacy to assess feasibility. Temporary workaround: we can provide nightly S3 snapshot containing full raw events (with stronger redaction) until Kafka stream is ready.

comments.author: Priya Nair ( - Billing)
comments.body: Billing can emit per-call cost in near-real-time but requires a lightweight join; will prototype an enrichment lambda. Will open PR for schema mapping. (ref: https://github.com/redwood-inference/platform/pull/2153)

comments.author: Damien Ruiz ()
comments.body:
Reversible pseudonymization design options: (1) envelope encryption with per-tenant KEK stored in KMS with audit logs; (2) HSM-backed token vault for legal-decryption flow. Need legal to sign off.
Action: schedule security review with Delta compliance.

comments.author: Aisha Khan (CS)
comments.body: Customer accepted nightly S3 snapshot workaround for PoC. Awaiting PoC schedule and security review meeting times.

title: Staggered backout assist for tenant experiencing prefetch + 4-bit quant profile mismatch
body:
Issue summary:
LuminaDocs reported elevated 5xx rates and tail latency for long chat sessions after enabling a new optimization bundle that combined aggressive KV prefetching with a 4-bit quantization profile.

Impact:
- Customer observed 2.8x tail latency (95/99) on conversational sessions > 2k tokens and sporadic 502/503 responses.
- Affects a subset of tenant traffic routed to dedicated capacity pool in us-east.
- Customer is on a production schedule; impact is user-facing for document generation flows.

Environment:
- prod (us-east), Dedicated capacity pool #dcp-lumina-01
- Model: rw-alto-7b-4bit, optimizer profile: pulse-prefetch-v3 + quant-4b-aggr

Steps to reproduce (as provided by customer):
1) Start a long-running session via the Redwood API (streaming enabled) with average request length ~1.8k tokens.
2) Hit the same session for several requests (prefix reuse).
3) Observe el...

title: KV cache warmup stall causing increased latency for rapid repeated prompts
body:
Issue summary:
Customer reports that when they replay short conversational prompts in quick succession (5–10s intervals), observed tail latency for repeat requests increases ~2x after the first two requests. This appears specific to repeat prompts that should hit the prefix/KV cache.

Impact:
- PromptlyAI production customers are experiencing slower-than-expected responses on multi-turn quick-replay flows (chat previews and autosave previews).
- Error rate is not elevated (no 5xx at API layer) but p95/p99 latency degrade from ~180ms to ~380ms on affected replicas.

Environment:
- Redwood hosted (dedicated pool) in us-east-1, tenant PromptlyAI (dedicated).
- Model: gptx-14b-instruct pinned to v2026-02-28.

Steps to reproduce (customer supplied):
1) Send short prompt sequence (seed prompt + 2 repeat prompts) with streaming enabled and short inter-request gap (6s).
2) Observe normal late...

title: Guidance for safely rolling back combined batching/caching/quantization profile changes after latency and error spikes
body:
Issue summary:
Customer EchoHealth reported a spike in tail latency and a rise in 5xx stream disconnects following a combined change that: enabled a new quantization profile, increased batching window from 8ms to 25ms, and enabled aggressive KV prefix caching for session replays.

Impact:
- Intermittent p95 latency regressions from ~120ms to 780-1,200ms for certain conversational workloads.
- Increased streaming disconnects (stream_close_rate +3.4%) and transient token generation 5xxs.
- Customers on dedicated pool are seeing increased request timeouts triggering client-side retries.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: echohealth-prod-gpu-a (us-east region)
- Serving runtime version: 2.9.1+opt (deployed 2026-03-11 03:20 UTC)
- Optimizer profile: hybrid-agg-v2 (batch_window=25ms, kv_prefetch=true, quant=amq8-approx)
- Traffic: 95% chat, 5% embeddings

Steps to reproduce:
1) Route a steady ...

comments.body: Maya Chen): Confirmed canary and full pool had identical symptoms; attached a trace where kv_prefetch shows repeated cache miss thrash.

comments.author: Liam O'Connor
comments.body: Ran quick heat-checks: increased batch window correlates with CPU saturation on worker pods and larger micro-batches causing spike in memory pressure when combined with quantized kernels. Hypothesis added to investigation notes.

title: Inference latency regression after runtime 1.18 rollout (p95 +40–80%) impacting chat completions
body:
Issue summary:
- Customer reports significant latency regression after Redwood serving runtime 1.18 rollout.
- Observed across Chat Completions and Text Generation (non-streaming and streaming), with biggest impact on longer outputs.

Customer impact:
- Aurora Search reports production SLO breach on end-user search-assistant flows.
- Symptoms: p95 latency increased ~40–80% depending on model; elevated tail latency spikes (p99) during peak.
- No material increase in 5xx, but increased client-side timeouts due to slower responses.

Scope / blast radius (initial):
- Regions: us-east (strongest), eu-west (moderate).
- Models most impacted: rw-llama-70b-instruct (highest), rw-llama-8b-instruct, rw-mistral-7b-instruct.
- Endpoints: /v1/chat/completions, /v1/completions.

Timeline (UTC):
- 2026-02-13 17:52: Customer email + ticket opened. Customer notes issue began “shortly after” runtime up...

comments.body: 07 (Priya Shah): Confirmed spike on inference latency dashboard for us-east starting ~17:40 UTC. Creating bridge per playbook and paging SRE if we confirm multi-customer impact. Asking customer for 3 request IDs, region, model, and whether streaming.

16 (Owen Phillips): Pulled logs for req_01HZZ9Z2S5KJ9A3FQ4VQ9K2E7W. No 5xx; end-to-end latency increased with longer generation. Noted runtime_version=1.18 tag on the serving pods handling the request. Looping in #eng-oncall.

28 (Sean Gallagher): Seeing same pattern across a few tenants (not just Aurora). Treating as Sev2 until we confirm if all regions affected. Pausing further rollout of 1.18; evaluating rollback vs config revert.
14 (Rachel Kim): Sent customer update: we identified regression correlating with a runtime rollout; mitigation underway; next update within 60 minutes. No RCA promised yet.

12 (Sean Gallagher): Rollback started in us-east hosted pool. Early signs: p95 improving. Will proceed with eu-west next if stable. Please keep customer comms aligned to mitigation status (rollback in progress).
21 (Owen Phillips): Customer confirms p95 back to normal range in us-east; eu-west improving. Marking ticket as mitigated pending 24h monitoring.
40 (Owen Phillips): 48h check: latency stable and back to baseline. Closing as Resolved. Action item remains for runtime team to root cause 1.18 regression and add perf guardrails before re-roll.

- Endpoints: /v1/chat/completions, /v1/completions.

title: Dedicated pool throughput sag in us-west: background IO surge causing GPU attach latency and tenant throttling
body:
Issue summary:
During the late-afternoon window on 2026-03-10 we observed a sustained drop in throughput for a reserved dedicated pool in us-west-2 serving NimbleDocs (dedicated). Symptoms included elevated attach/slot acquisition latency, increased 429s on batched routes, and an observed decline in concurrent GPU allocations for the affected pool.

Impact:
- Customer-facing: ~12% increase in end-to-end p95 latency for their batched text generation routes between 14:10-15:05 PST; intermittent 429s and throttled requests.
- Platform: allocator reported temporary reduction of available attachable slots; autoscaler did not spawn new nodes due to perceived low utilization.

Environment:
- Prod, us-west-2, dedicated pool id: rp-2831 (NimbleDocs reserved cluster).
- Serving runtime: 1.8.3-release
- Allocator: v0.22.1
- Snapshotter-sidecar: v0.9.6

Steps to reproduce (observed pattern):
1) S...

comments.author: (Evelyn)
comments.body: Customer reported increased errors and p95 latency. Collected request ids and sample traces (trace ids sent to SRE).

comments.author: SRE (Ravi)
comments.body: Observed NVMe write latency spike across 6 hosts in rp-2831. Attach latency correlation looks strong. Captured dmesg and nvme logs to /logs/rp-2831/2026-03-10/.

comments.author: Maria Lopez
comments.body: Noted a recent config PR merged last night that increased flush batch concurrency for faster checkpointing (PR #4389). Rolling throttle to 2 concurrent flushers on affected hosts.

comments.author: (Evelyn)
comments.body: Deployed throttling; customer reports error rate falling but still seeing elevated p95 for short bursts. Continuing to route 10% traffic to eu-west as a mitigation.

comments.author: Allocator (Jon)
comments.body: Investigated autoscaler signals. Found that available_slots metric becomes stale while attach RPCs hang; proposing adding attach RPC timeout and separate "attach_inflight" metric to autoscaler decisions. Created follow-up INT ticket.

comments.author: SRE (Ravi)
comments.body: Collected kernel stack samples and nvme SMART dumps; attached artifacts to SUP-865432. Drafting postmortem outline and runbook changes. Customer notified of ongoing remediation plan.

comments.author: Customer (NimbleDocs)
comments.body: Thank you for the mitigation. We still see occasional spikes in the late-afternoon window; please advise ETA for permanent fix.

comments.author: (Evelyn)
comments.body: Noted in thread - owners assigned. Maria to prepare config revert candidate, Jon to publish autoscaler change proposal. ETA targeted for 2026-03-18 for staged rollout.

comments.body: /logs/rp-2831/2026-03-10/, https://grafana.redwood.ai/d/rp-2831/attach-latency, https://github.com/redwood-inference/snapshotter/pull/4389

title: Hotpath inspection and safe undo for config misalignment affecting tail latency
body:
Issue summary: CortexLabs reported a sudden increase in tail latency and intermittent 5xx errors after an optimizer configuration change was applied to their dedicated pool. The change included a mixed-precision quantization profile, aggressive batching windows, and enabling KV cache prefetch for low-latency paths.

Impact: Customer-facing inference for chat and generate endpoints experienced p95 latency jumps and a small but persistent 5xx rate, impacting a single high-traffic tenant. Traffic volume is production, dedicated pool. Customer has asked for a safe rollback procedure and guidance on how to profile the regression without causing customer-visible downtime.

Environment: prod-us-east dedicated pool (CortexLabs-reserved), affected model variants: llama-2-70b pinned -> mixed-quant profile; some requests hit a fallback redwood-rlhf-13b variant during overflow.

Ask: 1) Confirm r...

title: Initial-response stall for chat streaming after runtime kernel swap and model pin
body: Issue summary: Customer NovaLaw AI observed elevated initial latency (stall before first token) on streaming chat completions after we rolled a runtime kernel swap and they pinned dialog-7b-v2. Impact: customer-facing UX regression with user timeouts and increased support load. Environment: Prod us-east with Dedicated capacity. This ticket captures the triage, investigation, and short-term mitigations.

Impact: customer-facing UX regression with user timeouts and increased support load. Environment: Prod us-east with Dedicated capacity. This ticket captures the triage, investigation, and short-term mitigations.

comments.author: Aisha Patel
comments.body: 35: We've reproduced with NovaLaw test harness. Attaching sample request IDs and perf traces. Requesting SRE assistance to look into kernel-level stalls.

comments.author: Marco Rivera
comments.body:
05: Reviewing traces now. There's a clear 2.5-3s stall prior to first read from GPU in a subset of sessions. Assigning to kernel team.
Slack thread: #inc-ttfb (https://slack.redwood.ai/archives/C023TTFB)

comments.author: Priya Menon
comments.body: 40: We merged an eager-load change in PR #2142; checking if eager load could block due to page faults under certain VM flavors. Starting controlled rollback on one Dedicated pool.

comments.author: NovaLaw - Oliver Chang
comments.body: 25: Appreciate the quick reproduction. Our users hit timeouts after 3s and we saw retries. Please advise workaround ASAP.

comments.author: Marco Rivera
comments.body: 00: Short-term mitigation: we will roll back PR #2142 on affected Dedicated nodes in us-east. ETA 60m. Will monitor p95 first-token latency.

comments.author: Aisha Patel
comments.body: 15: NovaLaw confirmed reduced failed chats after rollback on their Dedicated pool. They request a postmortem and timeline for permanent fix.

title: Privileged access request: sampled streaming metrics & telemetry for customer-facing streaming sessions
body:
Issue summary: Security & Customer Success have requested time-limited privileged access to sampled streaming telemetry for a small set of customer streaming sessions. Purpose is to investigate a perceived quality regression in streamed partial responses and to validate whether KV cache snapshots and chunked frames correlate with dropped stream events.

Impact: Investigation may require access to raw telemetry that contains identifiers (session_id, request_id), partial token outputs (sampled streaming frames), and internal routing metadata. This data may include customer PII in freeform prompts; request must be minimized and redacted where possible.

Requestor: @customer-success (Escalation: Claire Royce) and Security (Escalation: Marco Silva).

Access requested: Read-only, time-bounded (48 hours) access to a curated sample set of streaming telemetry for up to 12 sessions across 3 cus...

title: Guidance requested: traffic-gated demotion plan for hybrid batching + KV-prefetch + quant experiment
body: Customer Novalune ran an experiment that enabled KV-prefetch + aggressive batching and switched a subset of traffic to a q4 quantization profile on their Dedicated pool. After the change they report intermittent 5xx errors (mostly 503/504) and sporadic tail-latency spikes on a small set of high-concurrency routes. Customer is asking for a stepwise, low-risk demotion/rollback plan that preserves throughput for unaffected routes and avoids mass disruption.

title: KV-prefetch + aggressive quant profile causing intermittent token errors and elevated tail latency; guidance for safe unwind
body:
Issue summary:
Customer enabled two optimization changes in their Dedicated cluster: 1) kv_prefetch=true (prefix/KV warm-up across shards) and 2) quant_profile=aggressive_int4. They also increased preferred_batch_size to 256 in the route config. After deploy, NovaDocs observed intermittent token corruption (garbled tokens mid-stream), elevated P95/P99 token latency and a small number of 5xx stream terminations for long-context prompts (>= 8k).

Impact:
- Production inference degraded for ~3% of requests to the affected route; affects user-facing document generation workflows.
- Elevated tail latency (P95 up from 120ms to 470ms) and sporadic 5xx stream drop.
- Business impact: some time-sensitive document renders missing tokens; customer requested guidance and an emergency safe rollback.

Environment:
- Dedicated cluster (customer-managed reservation) in us-west-2
- Model: redwood/llam...

title: Syslog/forwarder schema mapping and retention attestation for regulated audit exports
body:
Issue summary:
MedData Analytics (enterprise) has submitted a security questionnaire and requests a formal artifact bundle and technical details to support their SOC2 and PCI assessments. They need: (1) exact syslog/CEF/JSON event schema we emit for tenant audit events, including field-level descriptions and examples; (2) confirmation of retention policy (how long raw audit events and normalized events are retained) and an attestation statement that deletion is performed when TTL expires; (3) a one-time sample export for 2026-02-01 to 2026-03-01 containing anonymized events, checksums, and a manifest; (4) instructions and an anchor config for configuring a push-based syslog forwarder or pull-based S3 export for their SIEM ingestion.

Impact:
Customer compliance deadline for SOC2 readiness is 2026-03-20; they cannot complete their evidence package without the artifacts. No production o...

title: Latency regression after enabling new optimization config (batching/caching) on Dedicated us-east
body:
Customer reports a latency regression immediately after enabling a new Optimization Config setting (batching heuristic update + KV prefix cache toggle) on their Dedicated deployment in us-east.

Issue summary
- Dedicated deployment: northbridge-analytics / deployment `nb-prod-dedicated-us-east-1`
- Model: llama-3.1-70b-instruct
- Change applied: Optimization Config version `ocfg_2025-12-18_03` (previous: `ocfg_2025-12-10_02`)
- Symptoms: p95 latency increased ~3x, queueing/TTFT increase, customer impact on synchronous endpoints
- Immediate mitigation: rollback to prior Optimization Config version (confirmed recovery)

Customer impact
- Impact window: 2025-12-18 16:22–17:05 UTC (approx)
- p95 latency: ~0.9–1.1s baseline -> 2.6–3.2s after change
- p99 latency: ~2.8–3.5s baseline -> 6.5–7.8s after change
- Error rate: no material change (customer reports <0.3% 5xx)

- Immediate mitigation: rollback to prior Optimization Config version (confirmed recovery)

Customer impact
- Impact window: 2025-12-18 16:22–17:05 UTC (approx)
- p95 latency: ~0.9–1.1s baseline -> 2.6–3.2s after change
- p99 latency: ~2.8–3.5s baseline -> 6.5–7.8s after change
- Error rate: no material change (customer reports <0.3% 5xx)
- Business impact: deg...

comments.body: 31 UTC — Hannah Rivera (Support) — Ticket opened via email from Northbridge Analytics. They report "latency tripled" after enabling new optimization settings in their Dedicated deployment. Requested request IDs + approximate change time. Set priority P1 due to production customer impact.

38 UTC — Customer (Northbridge Analytics) — Confirmed change time ~16:22 UTC. Observed header `x-redwood-opt-config-version: ocfg_2025-12-18_03`. Their own Grafana shows p95 jumped from ~1.0s to ~3.0s. Error rate steady. Asked if they can revert quickly without waiting for a full support cycle.

43 UTC — Aisha Bello (SRE) — Looking at dedicated-us-east-1-gpu-pool-3. Not seeing elevated 5xx; seeing scheduler queue depth increase and TTFT increase for this tenant. GPU memory utilization trending higher since 16:22 UTC. Correlates with config change annotation on rollout dashboard.

46 UTC — Aisha Bello (SRE) — Proposed immediate rollback to prior optimization config version for tenant+region scope. Coordinating with on-call runtime for any cache invalidation risks; expect brief transient but should reduce tail latency.

49 UTC — Noah Patel (Serving Runtime) — Agree likely batching residency + cache pressure. Rollback should be safe. Watch for brief latency spike from cache invalidation; should settle within minutes. Please capture before/after metrics and annotate.

52 UTC — Aisha Bello (SRE) — Executed rollback for northbridge-analytics (us-east) from `ocfg_2025-12-18_03` -> `ocfg_2025-12-10_02`. Monitoring p95, queue depth, GPU mem, and throughput. Added manual annotation on Optimization Rollout Health dashboard.

03 UTC — Hannah Rivera (Support) — Update to customer: rollback completed; please confirm if your p95/p99 are trending back to baseline. Also asked if they changed request mix (prompt lengths/concurrency) during the window.

10 UTC — Customer (Northbridge Analytics) — Confirmed recovery. p95 back to ~1.1s, p99 ~3.4s within ~8–10 minutes. No changes in traffic mix. Asked for a short RCA and whether this config can be safely re-tested with a canary.

18 UTC — Aisha Bello (SRE) — Post-rollback: queue depth normalized; TTFT down; GPU memory utilization decreased ~8–12% relative. Suggest re-test in off-peak with a smaller canary scope (or a less aggressive batching profile). Logging internal follow-up: add gating checks for latency tails + GPU saturation signals before promotions.

05 UTC — Hannah Rivera (Support) — Sent customer summary: regression correlated with optimization config `ocfg_2025-12-18_03` enabling adaptive batching v3 + KV prefix cache. Rollback restored baseline. Offered to schedule a controlled canary test with Redwood team and share recommended settings for long-prompt workloads. Marking incident resolved; will update if engineering identifies a specific fix/guardrail.

title: Assistance requested: stepwise deactivation and safe offramp for aggressive batching/caching/quant flags after tenant tail-latency increase
body:
Issue summary:
Customer BrightHealth Analytics reports a sharp rise in 95th/99th percentile tail latency for a subset of traffic after enabling an aggressive optimizer profile (KV prefetch + large async batching + quant profile q4f16). The increase began at ~2026-03-09T22:10 UTC and correlates with an internal optimizer rollout for the tenant.

Impact:
- Affected traffic: ~8% of requests from the customer’s production routes (models listed above).
- Symptoms: elevated p95 and p99 token latency, intermittent streaming disconnects for long-context sessions, and a modest increase in 5xx rate (~0.4% -> 1.6%).
- Business impact: BrightHealth reports degraded UX for clinical note summarization flows — user-facing timeouts observed, blocking an in-progress demo.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: dedicated-cluster-bh-prod (us-west)
- Routing: tenant pinned to redwood-13b-q4f16-kv-prefetch; fall...

comments.author: Aisha Patel ()
comments.body: Filed ticket after receiving logs and metrics from BrightHealth. Customer notes that the change was a config flip from their side via Console. Asked for immediate rollback guidance and timeline.

comments.author: Ethan Morales (SRE)
comments.body: Ack'd. Starting an initial hypothesis runbook. Will prioritize per-tenant gating checks and snapshot the optimizer profile state. ETA for first checklist: 90 minutes.

comments.author: Ethan Morales
comments.body: Snapshot of tenant optimizer state captured (snapshot id: snap-20260310-bh). Recommended first-step: disable kv-prefetch for tenant only, keep batching limits unchanged. Asked customer to standby for a controlled toggle window.

comments.author: Emma Liu
comments.body: Confirmed they are on standby for toggles. Provided consent for Support to perform tenant-gated changes during low-traffic window (13:30-14:30 PDT).

comments.author: Ethan Morales
comments.body: Performed tenant-gated kv-prefetch=false toggle at 14:22 PDT. Monitoring shows p95 dropped ~18% and p99 dropped ~30% within two minutes. No increase in 5xx. Proceed to next recommended step: reduce async batch window from 80ms -> 30ms for tenant.

comments.author: Marisol Vega
comments.body: Noted that quant profile q4f16 may still cause kernel path variance; suggest keeping current quant but add a conservative batch size cap (max_tokens_per_batch) as a next safety knob.

comments.author: Ethan Morales
comments.body: Implemented stepwise changes in staging and validated with tenant simulator. Drafting final off-ramp checklist for customer and internal runbook update.

comments.author: Emma Liu
comments.body: Appreciate the stepped approach. Requested a recommended config they can pin while root cause is investigated.

title: Rollback/pin race during canary rollout causes traffic to unexpectedly fallback to legacy model
body: Issue summary: Customer StorifyAI reported that during a canary rollout of model storify/generative-story-2.1:v1.5 the service suddenly started routing a subset of traffic to an older legacy model (redwood/gpt-base-1.0) despite a customer pin to v1.4. Impact: ~18% of requests for the pinned route returned older-model outputs with higher latency and lower quality; customer reports production users seeing degraded results and increased error rate in downstream metrics. Environment: prod/us-east (dedicated cluster for StorifyAI).

Impact: ~18% of requests for the pinned route returned older-model outputs with higher latency and lower quality; customer reports production users seeing degraded results and increased error rate in downstream metrics. Environment: prod/us-east (dedicated cluster for StorifyAI).

comments.author: Leah Kim () 2026-03-10
comments.body: Customer opened ticket after noticing quality regression on production. Provided request examples and timestamps. Asked for immediate mitigation and explanation.

comments.author: Marco Alvarez (SRE) 2026-03-10
comments.body: Acknowledged, escalated to routing team. Asked customer to hold traffic for canary and provided temporary config to force route to pinned version via console override.

comments.author: Routing
comments.body: Initial hypothesis — routing decision tree prioritized 'available model variants' ahead of 'customer pin' during a concurrent state transition (scale-down / rollout). Reproduced locally with scale-down simulation. Filing PR to change routing precedence.

comments.author: Customer Success
comments.body: Applied temporary workaround for StorifyAI: manually re-bound pin to the exact instance hash and disabled auto-burst for the route. Customer confirmed immediate reduction in legacy model hits.

comments.author: Marco Alvarez (SRE) 2026-03-12
comments.body: Patch merged to staging (PR#981). Will schedule canary deploy of routing change to prod on 2026-03-13 after 24h soak in staging. Leaving ticket open until prod rollout and customer validation.

title: Unexpected routing to variant with KV cache disabled leading to cold-KV misses and p99 spikes
body:
Issue summary:
Customer Nimbus Analytics began seeing sudden p99 latency regressions and 504s for production endpoints between 09:12-09:40 PDT on 2026-03-12. Traffic was routed to a slower variant which exhibited repeated cold KV cache misses and long container init times.

Impact:
- Multiple endpoints for rw-instruct-v1 showed p50 ~60ms (normal) and p99 increased from ~220ms to ~3.1s.
- Customer reported degraded UX and rate of 504s rose to ~2.3% of requests in the window.
- Affected traffic: dedicated capacity pool for Nimbus (reserved rack).

Environment:
- prod-us-west (us-west-2)
- Dedicated capacity pool: nimbus-dedicated-01
- Model pinned at rw-instruct-v1-2026-02-28 for the customer's route

Steps to reproduce (as replicated by Support):
1) Send 1000 concurrent short inference requests (seq len 64) to the customer's public route during routing policy that prefers latency-optim...

comments.author: Priya Singh
comments.body: Customer reported p99 spikes and intermittent 504s. Collecting traces and top-of-rack metrics.

comments.author: Tom Bauer
comments.body: Initial investigation shows routing to latency-opt-v2 which has kv_cache_enabled=false in its runtime manifest. Possible rollout automation misconfiguration. Escalating to infra eng (#infra).

comments.author: Mateo Rivera (SRE)
comments.body: Confirmed repeated image pulls for latency-opt-v2; base image size changed in last build causing slow starts. Also saw eviction churn on a subset of GPU nodes in the dedicated pool.

comments.author: Tom Bauer
comments.body: Temporarily pinned routing policy to avoid latency-opt-v2 for Nimbus routes. Reduced 504s to ~0.1%. Preparing hotfix to re-enable KV cache for the variant.

title: Aurenet: dedicated pool mis-registration caused cross-variant routing, streaming truncation, and billing exposure
body:
Issue summary:
Aurenet observed a major outage impacting their dedicated pool (prod-us-west) starting 2026-03-09 22:12 UTC. High rates of 5xx responses and streaming truncations were reported; several long-running requests retried and incurred unexpected token billing.

Impact:
- Customer-facing latency/5xx spike: ~12% of requests for Aurenet returned 5xx over a 24-minute window.
- Streaming truncation: several streaming sessions cut mid-response, clients reopened sockets and retried.
- Billing exposure: retries and duplicate completions caused an estimated ~8% increase in token usage for the affected window.
- Affected endpoints: text-generation streaming and chat endpoints pinned to redwood-4x-13b variants.

Timeline (high level):
- 2026-03-09 22:12 UTC: Aurenet support reported increased 500s and truncated streams via in-app alert and support portal.
- 22:15 UTC: On-call SRE acknow...

comments.body: 17 UTC - Support (Maya Lin): Aurenet reported widespread streaming truncations and elevated 5xx on dedicated pool. Customer is on enterprise plan; escalating to SRE. First response provided and customer notified we are investigating.

22 UTC - SRE (Jordan Patel): Noted unusual pattern in node registration logs: several nodes re-registered within the upgrade window without 'variant_compat' meta. Routing policy assumed compatibility. Triage moving to scheduler owners.

35 UTC - Scheduler owner (R. Alvarez): Hotfix to enforce capability on registration assembled and smoke-tested against staging; preparing prod rollout. Linked PR: https://github.com/redwood/scheduler/pull/312
50 UTC - Support (Maya Lin): Sent customer status update: temporary routing to fp16 variant and token-credit offer. Customer acknowledged and requested ETA for full fix.
15 UTC - Customer (Aurenet - S. Chen): Thank you for quick mitigation. Please include an action that prevents this from recurring during node maintenance.
02 UTC - SRE (Jordan Patel): Post-deploy verification completed; adding runbook updates and console alerts as follow-ups. Will schedule chaos test with SRE team for April.
30 UTC - Support (Maya Lin): Final customer email with post-incident summary, credits applied, and link to postmortem page. Customer accepted credits and requested a technical debrief next week.

title: Autocapacity signal mismatch causing regional GPU thin-spot and elevated p99s for NimbleRetail dedicated pool
body:
Issue summary:
NimbleRetail reports sustained p99 latency elevation (2.8s -> 8.1s) and intermittent request timeouts from their dedicated GPU pool in us-west-2 during mid-day traffic. The symptoms coincide with a regional autoscaler event where capacity did not materialize despite node-level capacity being available elsewhere in the region.

Impact:
- Customer-facing model endpoints experienced p99 latency regressions and ~3% increases in 5xx error rate for ~90 minutes.
- Affected routes include short-chat streaming and embeddings.
- Revenue-impacting SLA risk; customer flagged to Enterprise CS.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: nimble-dedicated-usw2 (customer-reserved cluster)
- Region: us-west-2 (prod)

Impact:
- Customer-facing model endpoints experienced p99 latency regressions and ~3% increases in 5xx error rate for ~90 minutes.

- Revenue-impacting SLA risk; customer flagged to Enterprise CS.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: nimble-dedicated-usw2 (customer-reserved cluster)
- Region: us-west-2 (prod)

comments.author: Maya Chen
comments.body: Initial triage done with customer. Collected request IDs and sample trace links. Customer reports impact started ~14:10 PST and resolved briefly after 15:45 PST with a temporary mitigation by routing to a fallback cluster.

comments.author: SRE (Liam O'Connor)
comments.body: Observed autoscaler decision anomalies — hold decisions during prefetch spikes. Pushing for deeper allocator traces. Added labels and escalated to infra-oncall via #incidents-usw2.

comments.author: NimbleRetail - Alex Diaz
comments.body: Confirmed fallback routing reduced errors but increased cost. Wants root cause and durable fix; asks for timeline and mitigation to prevent recurrence.

comments.author: Priya Nambiar
comments.body: Ran targeted repro in staging and reproduced attach_backoff when kv-cache growth >20MB/s per node. Hypothesis: prefetch-induced I/O pressure increases allocator latency and triggers hold heuristic in autoscaler. Opened GH PR to relax hold threshold temporarily.

comments.author: Maya Chen
comments.body: Notified customer of interim mitigation: adjusted fallback policy to enable cross-region attach for critical routes and queued follow-up maintenance window. Customer acknowledged.

comments.author: SRE (Liam O'Connor)
comments.body: Rolling patch applied to autoscaler config in prod us-west-2: lower sensitivity to transient prefetch spikes and force attach retry after 45s. Monitoring closely.

comments.author: Maya Chen
comments.body: Customer reports p99 returned near baseline after patch. Keeping ticket open for postmortem and permanent fix work.

title: Unexpected routing to edge-cpu-fallback causing long-tail latency and memory thrashing for NovaDocs prod
body:
Issue summary:
Customer (NovaDocs, enterprise/dedicated) reports a sudden tail-latency regression and increased 504s starting 2026-03-12 11:20 UTC. Traffic that should be routed to their pinned dedicated GPU variant is intermittently routed to an edge-cpu-fallback replica in us-west-2. The edge-cpu variant exhibits long cold-start times (~6–12s P99) and memory thrashing (OOM+swap churn) when model shards load, causing end-to-end request timeouts for NovaDocs API calls.

Impact:
- Customer-facing API calls experience P99 jump from ~350ms to 8–12s and increased 504 rate from 0.01% to 1.8% for a subset of requests.
- Affects critical document-embedding and summarization endpoints used in production.
- High-severity for customer (enterprise contractual SLA).

Environment:
- NovaDocs dedicated pool in prod (us-west-2) with regional failover enabled.
- Routing policy: primary -> dedicated-g...

- Customer-facing API calls experience P99 jump from ~350ms to 8–12s and increased 504 rate from 0.01% to 1.8% for a subset of requests.
- Affects critical document-embedding and summarization endpoints used in production.
- High-severity for customer (enterprise contractual SLA).

Environment:
- NovaDocs dedicated pool in prod (us-west-2) with regional failover enabled.
- Routing policy: primary -> dedicated-g...

comments.body: 45 UTC — Aisha Patel (Support): Customer reported spike; attached user traces and Grafana snapshots. Marked as P1 and escalated to SRE.

05 UTC — Marco Hernandez (SRE): Confirmed tail-latency increase on NovaDocs route. Routing events show ~12% of traffic diverted to edge-cpu-fallback between 11:20–11:50 UTC. Kicked off pod-level telemetry capture.

40 UTC — SRE Team (thread #support): Observed autoscaler weight decay event at 11:18 UTC (commit 9f2c3e4). Suspect that fast weight decay + short cooldown caused fallback activation. Collecting kernel vmstat and dmesg from edge-cpu pods.
10 UTC — Aisha Patel (Support): Customer accepted temporary workaround to pin routing to dedicated-gpu-1 for critical routes while we investigate. Asking for ETA on permanent fix.
30 UTC — Marco Hernandez (SRE): Implemented emergency routing override for NovaDocs (pin to dedicated-gpu-1). Observed metrics return to baseline within 3 minutes. Continuing root-cause analysis.

20 UTC — Runtime Engineer, Priya Singh: Reproduced local load with quantized artifact and confirmed higher peak RSS on edge-cpu artifact due to older allocator behavior. Preparing patch to harmonize kernel selection and reduce mapped file churn.

title: Elevated first-token latency on chat completions after compiler + runtime swap
body:
Issue summary:
BrightLoop reported significantly elevated time-to-first-token (TTFB) for chat completion requests starting ~2026-03-11 22:10 UTC after their account was routed to the new runtime (runtime-1.9.0) and an upstream compiler swap (jit-compiler v3.4). Impact: ~10-25x increase in median TTFB for short chat prompts, customer-facing timeouts on interactive product flows and increased error rates in client telemetry.

Impact:
- Customer-facing: end-user chat flows in BrightLoop web app (session flow) saw interactive timeouts (client 10s) in ~12% of sessions for a subset of routes.
- Business: brightloop has dedicated capacity; this affects a paid account on reserved capacity (throughput SLA).

Timeline / Observed events:
- 2026-03-11 21:50 UTC: internal rollout of runtime-1.9.0 + compiler swap began for a subset of dedicated pools.
- 2026-03-11 22:06 UTC: BrightLoop's requests f...

title: Console analytics: sudden loss of per-route usage metrics and tracing events after retention policy update
body:
Issue summary: FinSaaS reports that per-route usage dashboards and tracing hooks in the Console stopped showing recent events and p95/p99 charts between ~03:00-07:00 UTC. Several alert rules (per-route latency and error-rate) did not fire during an observed latency spike.

Impact: Customer-facing dashboards blank for recent window; alerts suppressed leading to delayed mitigation for increased latency. Affects FinSaaS production (enterprise tenant on dedicated capacity).

Environment: prod-us-east (customer dedicated cluster), Console v2.4.1, metrics ingestion pipeline backed by metrics-store (ClickHouse), traces via otel-collector -> trace-store (Bigtable), retention worker service: retention-sweeper v1.7.0.

Steps to reproduce:
1) Login to Console as FinSaaS admin -> open Usage > Per-route metrics for the last 6 hours.
2) Select any high-traffic route (e.g., /api/generate).
3) Observ...

title: Private install: CPU/memory saturation alerts became noisy after upgrade (thresholds too tight for inference pods)
body:
Issue summary:
After upgrading HelioBank’s Redwood Private cluster, Prometheus began firing frequent CPU/memory-related alerts for the serving runtime even though application-level latency/error SLOs remained healthy. Customer reports alert fatigue and requests guidance on tuning + confirmation of whether this is a known issue with the minimum observability pack.

Impact:
- High-volume alert noise (non-actionable) causing on-call fatigue.
- No customer-facing outage reported; inference remained available.

Customer context:
- Deployment: Redwood Private (on-prem, air-gapped)
- Upgrade: Redwood Private 1.9.4 -> 1.10.0 (same hardware footprint)
- Observability: bundled Prometheus + Grafana; alerts routed to OpsGenie

What’s firing (examples provided by customer):
- RedwoodServingHighCPUUtilization (warning) flapping during normal batch spikes
- RedwoodServingMemoryWorkingSetHigh (warnin...

normalized.description:
Issue summary:
After upgrading HelioBank’s Redwood Private cluster, Prometheus began firing frequent CPU/memory-related alerts for the serving runtime even though application-level latency/error SLOs remained healthy. Customer reports alert fatigue and requests guidance on tuning + confirmation of whether this is a known issue with the minimum observability pack.

Impact:
- High-volume alert noise (non-actionable) causing on-call fatigue.
- No customer-facing outage reported; inference remained available.

Customer context:
- Deployment: Redwood Private (on-prem, air-gapped)
- Upgrade: Redwood Private 1.9.4 -> 1.10.0 (same hardware footprint)
- Observability: bundled Prometheus + Grafana; alerts routed to OpsGenie

What’s firing (examples provided by customer):
- RedwoodServingHighCPUUtilization (warning) flapping during normal batch spikes
- RedwoodServingMemoryWorkingSetHigh (warnin...

comments.body: 12 UTC - Dev Patel: Opened on behalf of HelioBank (Maya R., Platform Eng). They upgraded to 1.10.0 last night and are getting ~150 alerts/hour, mostly CPU/memory warnings on serving-runtime. Asking whether they should disable the new rules or if there’s an updated bundle.

03 UTC - Rafael Mendes: Can you ask for: (1) a screenshot/export of firing alerts w/ labels, (2) pod resource requests/limits for serving-runtime, (3) Prometheus rule group name + pack version, (4) whether these are paging or ticket-only in their routing policy?

21 UTC - Dev Patel: Customer provided details: Pack version reported by Helm values is observabilityPackVersion=0.4.0 (bundled with 1.10.0). Serving runtime: requests cpu=4, mem=16Gi; limits cpu=24, mem=32Gi. Alerts are routed as pages due to generic OpsGenie mapping (anything ‘warning’ -> page). They can change routing but prefer we adjust defaults / provide explicit guidance.

05 UTC - Rafael Mendes: Reproduced in staging by applying similar requests/limits; CPU warnings fire during normal batching spikes. Our rule uses utilization vs limit with short window; recommend moving warnings to ticket-only + raise thresholds + add longer ‘for’. Also memory working set threshold should compare to request (or use node pressure signals) to avoid constant noise on large models.

44 UTC - Tomáš Novák: +1. We should ensure annotations explicitly say “ticket-only” for warnings and reserve paging for sustained saturation + node pressure or error budget burn. Customer’s paging policy is mapping all warnings to page; we should document that mapping is not recommended.

30 UTC - Rafael Mendes: Proposed immediate tuning for customer (values override): set CPU warning threshold to 0.90 with for=15m; set memory working set warning to 0.92 with for=20m; keep critical at 0.98 with for=10m; route OOMKilled to ticket unless it repeats (increase to ‘>0 in 30m’ AND ‘restarts > 2 in 30m’).

02 UTC - Dev Patel: Sent customer workaround + routing guidance. They’ll implement in the next maintenance window and confirm. Asked if we can provide a patch so they don’t need a permanent fork of rules in their air-gapped bundle.

18 UTC - Sergio Costa: We have a rules change in-flight to reduce noise for Private: increase thresholds, add ‘for’ durations, and change some warnings to be disabled-by-default. Linking PRs: observability-pack PR-18372 and PR-18356 (includes runbook links + severity mapping updates). Helm wiring in redwood-helm-charts PR-18405 will allow customer overrides without patching manifests.

40 UTC - Dev Patel: Customer confirmed improvement after tuning: CPU warnings reduced from ~150/hour to <5/hour; memory working set warning still firing on one pool during model warm-up but no longer paging after routing change. They requested a recommended default routing policy snippet for warning vs critical.

55 UTC - Rafael Mendes: Closing as resolved with workaround + fix queued. Follow-up: ensure next Private bundle includes updated rules and docs explicitly state recommended alert routing (warning->ticket, critical->page) and that CPU/memory utilization warnings are early indicators, not necessarily customer-impacting.

title: HarborTech production surge: warm-start latency amplification and unordered streaming fragments (major incident)
body: Issue summary: During a customer-driven traffic surge and scheduled canary workload ramp (HarborTech), production requests experienced a sudden multi-minute warm-start latency amplification followed by a period of unordered streaming fragments (stream pieces arriving out of sequence or truncated). Impact: Customers reported increased completion latency (p95 for short-chat flows rose from ~120ms to ~2.3s) and intermittent transcript reordering for streamed responses. Two HarborTech endpoints observed duplicated partial chunks and out-of-order final tokens for ~22 minutes, triggering an enterprise escalation. Environment: production cluster serving us-east-1; affected pool: dedicated-dp-ht-02 (reserved capacity).

normalized.description: Issue summary: During a customer-driven traffic surge and scheduled canary workload ramp (HarborTech), production requests experienced a sudden multi-minute warm-start latency amplification followed by a period of unordered streaming fragments (stream pieces arriving out of sequence or truncated). Impact: Customers reported increased completion latency (p95 for short-chat flows rose from ~120ms to ~2.3s) and intermittent transcript reordering for streamed responses. Two HarborTech endpoints observed duplicated partial chunks and out-of-order final tokens for ~22 minutes, triggering an enterprise escalation. Environment: production cluster serving us-east-1; affected pool: dedicated-dp-ht-02 (reserved capacity).

comments.author: Maya Chen ()
comments.body: Customer reported multiple truncated streams and large latency tail. Opened incident and escalated to SRE. Notified account exec and CS manager.

comments.author: Diego Alvarez (SRE)
comments.body: Observed warm worker evictions and a burst of GC/kv-eviction logs. Suspect aggressive KV eviction policy during warmup combined with kernel-selection fallback. Collecting traces and heap profiles.

comments.author: Platform
comments.body: Initial hypothesis: quantized variant's kernel selector intermittently chooses fallback code-paths for long-context warmups, causing repeated cache thrash and delayed stream assembly. Starting a canary rollback to full-precision variant for affected pool.

comments.author: Maya Chen
comments.body: Informed HarborTech that we are rolling a targeted mitigation (canary rollback + forced prefix cache warm). Customer provided spike schedule — they were ramping a marketing batch, exacerbating warmstart.

comments.author: Diego Alvarez
comments.body: Mitigation applied: pinned dp-ht-02 to redwood-open-xxl-v1 (non-quantized) and increased KV cache soft limit temporarily. Early telemetry: p95 reduced back toward baseline within ~9 minutes after change. Waiting an hour for stability window before final closeout.

comments.author: HarborTech - CS contact
comments.body: Acknowledged restoration but requested post-incident follow-up with concrete action items to prevent recurrence and billing credit for impacted time. CS requested detailed RCA.

comments.author: Diego Alvarez
comments.body: RCA and action plan uploaded to Confluence and shared with HarborTech. Issued a one-day service credit and scheduled follow-up architecture review with Platform Eng, SRE, and HarborTech engineering for 2026-03-18.

title: Post-deploy safety sanity check: combined quant + large batching + KV-prefetch caused tail latency spikes and intermittent 5xx
body:
Issue summary:
Customer applied a combined optimization profile that enabled an aggressive quantization variant (gptj-6b-quant-v2), increased target_batch_size to 256, and turned on KV-prefetch (kv_prefetch=true) across their dedicated pool. Within 20 minutes they observed a 3x increase in 99th-percentile latency, sporadic 5xx responses for generation requests, and a rise in token-level decoding errors (invalid token sequences).

Impact:
- Several production endpoints returned intermittent 500/502 for ~10% of requests during peak windows.
- 99p latency regressed from ~210ms to ~750ms.
- Customer user-facing errors and queueing leading to increased error budget usage.

Environment:
- Dedicated pool: df-prod-dedicated-7 (us-east)
- Model variants: gptj-6b-quant-v2 (rolled out via optimize profile), fallback gptj-6b-vanilla-v1
- Client traffic: steady QPS ~120, 90% short responses (<128 ...

comments.body: Approved temporary pin and asked for post-mortem and configuration guardrails before re-enabling hybrid profile.

title: Staged warmup-induced tail latency and safe off-ramp for tenant optimizer warmup
body:
Issue summary:
Customer NovusDocs reports intermittent 95-99p tail latency spikes (200-800ms above baseline) after we enabled tenant-scoped optimizer warmup and aggressive prefix/KV prefetch profiles. Impact: degraded meeting transcription responsiveness for end users during bursty traffic windows.

Impact:
- Multiple production endpoints for NovusDocs experience elevated tail latency during warmup periods, visible for ~5-20 minutes after a scale-up event.
- No increased 5xx rate; throughput unchanged.
- Customer requests a safe, per-tenant off-ramp and a short-term workaround while we investigate.

Environment:
- Dedicated capacity pool (dedicated-tier) in us-east-1
- Runtime: rwinf-2026-02-optimizer-flagset
- Allocated profile: tenant: novusdocs-prod -> optimizer: mixed-quant + aggressive-kv-prefetch + async-batching

Steps to reproduce (as reported):

Impact:
- Multiple production endpoints for NovusDocs experience elevated tail latency during warmup periods, visible for ~5-20 minutes after a scale-up event.
- No increased 5xx rate; throughput unchanged.
- Customer requests a safe, per-tenant off-ramp and a short-term workaround while we investigate.

Environment:
- Dedicated capacity pool (dedicated-tier) in us-east-1
- Runtime: rwinf-2026-02-optimizer-flagset

Steps to reproduce (as reported):

comments.author: Aisha Kapoor
comments.body: Opened with customer-provided traces and a HAR of the session. Customer asking for immediate per-tenant fallback while we diagnose.

comments.author: Miguel Santos
comments.body: Acknowledged, setting priority P1 and scheduling an initial hypothesis meeting with SRE and Runtime Eng.

comments.author: Lena Zhou
comments.body: Hypothesis: aggressive async-batching + kv-prefetch warms transient KV thrashing under tenant burst patterns. Suggest toggling prefetch to conservative and enabling per-tenant preflight snapshot restore.

comments.author: Omar Reyes
comments.body: Ran controlled repro in staging: reproduces with interleaved sessions. Confirmed elevated tail latency correlates with concurrent KV prefetch churn.

comments.author: Miguel Santos
comments.body: Pushing a safe off-ramp: staged policy to reduce kv_prefetch concurrency and set batching to sync for this tenant. Customer agreed to a 30-minute maintenance window for canary rollout.

comments.author: Elena Martin, NovusDocs
comments.body: Confirmed noticeable improvement after canary. Observed normal 95p latency restored within 7 minutes of change. Asked for guidance to avoid recurrence.

title: Canary rollout latency regression in prod (no auto-mitigation); request SLO-based auto-rollback for model rollouts
body:
Issue summary
Northstar HR reported elevated tail latency on their Dedicated endpoint during a model canary rollout. The customer observed p95 and p99 latency increases and intermittent client timeouts during peak traffic. The rollout did not automatically pause/rollback, requiring manual intervention by on-call.

Customer impact
- Customer-facing latency degradation for chat completions.
- Intermittent client-side timeouts (HTTP 499s observed from the gateway; no increase in 5xx at the platform edge initially).
- The customer explicitly requested an automated safety rail: SLO-based auto-rollback (or auto-pause) for canary/A-B rollouts.

Environment
- Dedicated deployment
- Regions: us-east (primary), eu-west (secondary)
- Route: /v1/chat/completions (tenant: northstar-hr)
- Rollout type: canary traffic shift (10% -> 25% planned)

Timeline (all times PT)
- 2026-02-17 09:42: Rollout st...

- Intermittent client-side timeouts (HTTP 499s observed from the gateway; no increase in 5xx at the platform edge initially).
- The customer explicitly requested an automated safety rail: SLO-based auto-rollback (or auto-pause) for canary/A-B rollouts.

Environment
- Dedicated deployment
- Regions: us-east (primary), eu-west (secondary)
- Route: /v1/chat/completions (tenant: northstar-hr)
- Rollout type: canary traffic shift (10% -> 25% planned)

Timeline (all times PT)
- 2026-02-17 09:42: Rollout st...

comments.body: 02 PT - Liam O'Rourke (TAM): Customer reports 'responses are ~2x slower and some requests time out' starting ~09:45 PT. Confirmed they were on the canary route. Asked for immediate rollback and requested longer-term auto rollback based on latency SLOs.

07 PT - Sean Gallagher (SRE): Acknowledged. We see p99 jump on candidate in us-east after 25% step. Pausing rollout now; will rollback to baseline. Not seeing corresponding edge 5xx, mostly elevated service latency + some 499s.

14 PT - Rafael Mendes (SRE): Rollback executed. Watching for recovery. Note: current SLO monitors are not rollout-scoped; hard to attribute quickly in dashboards without manual filtering. This is a known gap being addressed in the auto-rollback project.

22 PT - Liam O'Rourke (TAM): Customer confirms latency is back to normal. They’re asking for: (a) configurable thresholds, (b) audit log of rollouts/auto actions, (c) ability to suppress during known incidents but otherwise auto rollback by default.

41 PT - Allison Grant (Observability): Adding note for follow-up: need canonical labels for rollout_id/model_version/region to make rollout-scoped SLO evaluation reliable without high-cardinality blowups. Pointing to standards work + dashboard PR in observability-pack.

10 PT - Sean Gallagher (SRE): Support update to share externally: Today, rollouts rely on alerts and manual intervention; we’re implementing SLO-driven auto-pause/rollback with burn-rate windows + minimum sample sizes to avoid flapping. Target: staged enablement after internal canary validation.

05 PT - Liam O'Rourke (TAM): Sent customer summary + path forward. Offered early access once auto-rollback is available for Dedicated (us-east first). Marking ticket resolved; leaving linked engineering follow-ups in postmortem + rollout auto-rollback initiative.

title: Live traffic: request to safely step back prefetch eviction + aggressive quantization after p95 latency surge
body: Customer reported a sudden increase in tail latency (p95 and p99) for inference calls after enabling an experiment that combined session coalescing, aggressive prefix-KV prefetching, and mixed-precision quantization. They request stepwise assistance to revert the optimizer changes for their tenant without traffic loss and with minimal token cost impact.

normalized.description: Customer reported a sudden increase in tail latency (p95 and p99) for inference calls after enabling an experiment that combined session coalescing, aggressive prefix-KV prefetching, and mixed-precision quantization. They request stepwise assistance to revert the optimizer changes for their tenant without traffic loss and with minimal token cost impact.

comments.author: Maya Patel () 2026-03-10T14
comments.body: 28Z: Logged initial ticket after customer called support channel. Created snapshot and started staged rollback on subset of hosts.

comments.author: Carmen Ruiz (SRE) 2026-03-10T14
comments.body: 40Z: Observed eviction thrash on gpu-12-03 and queue delays. Recommended conservative prefetch and staged rollout. Confirmed no HW OOMs.

comments.author: Alex Chen (Customer - MedLab) 2026-03-10T14
comments.body: 50Z: Thanks — the staged hosts show immediate improvement. Please proceed to roll the rest, and confirm we won't lose active sessions.

comments.author: Liam O'Connor () 2026-03-10T15
comments.body: 05Z: We used a maintenance marker to avoid mid-flight reconciles. Active sessions retained; long sessions may experience a brief reconnect if eviction occurred — documented in runbook.

comments.author: Maya Patel () 2026-03-11T09
comments.body: 12Z: Follow-up scheduled with MedLab for 2026-03-13 to validate the conservative profile under peak traffic and share the safe-toggle script.

comments.author: Alex Chen (Customer - MedLab) 2026-03-11T11
comments.body: 00Z: Requesting a snapshot of the per-route latency dashboards and the checklist to run internally before toggling again.

title: Dedicated pool IO wave causing gradual p99 latency regression and transient GPU allocation failures
body:
Issue summary:
Customer (Acme Retail) reports steady degradation of tail latency (p95->p99) across their reserved dedicated pool over the last 18 hours with intermittent failures to allocate GPU slots for batched requests. The impact has been progressively worsening during US-West peak hours, causing production user-facing timeouts and increased error rates on chat endpoints.

Impact:
- Customers experienced p99 increases from ~350ms to 1.2s on rw-chat-13b-v1 flows.
- Intermittent 5xx errors and 'AllocationFailed' errors for short-lived batched streams.
- Affects 3 critical routes used by Acme's checkout assistant.

Observed timeframe:
- First symptoms observed 2026-03-13 09:50 PDT, escalated after sustained drift through the afternoon.

Environment:
- prod - us-west, Dedicated pool ID: dp-64a1
- Customer tier: dedicated (reserved capacity)
- Affected models: rw-decoder-3b-v2 (tokeniz...

comments.author: Alex Kim ()
comments.body: Customer reported progressive p99 regression. Initial telemetry points to pool dp-64a1. Escalating to SRE.

comments.author: Priya Desai (SRE)
comments.body: Noticed NVMe queue depth spikes on n-west-025/026/027 correlating with short-stream flood. Suspect noisy neighbor. Will throttle and stage autoscaler rollback.

comments.author: Elena Martinez
comments.body: Confirmed route slowed, accepted temporary pool migration. Provided request IDs and sample traces attached in email.

comments.author: Infra
comments.body: Rolling partial rollback of packing thresholds to previous stable commit (infra#2278) for dp-64a1. Observing for 30m.

comments.author: Priya Desai (SRE)
comments.body: Throttle applied to noisy tenant id t-8812. NVMe queue depth dropping on n-west-025. Allocation failures reduced. Monitoring continues.

comments.author: Alex Kim ()
comments.body: Notified customer of next steps and mitigation. Customer asked for timeline on permanent fix.

comments.author: Infra
comments.body: Running fio reproducer during maintenance window; preliminary results show attach latency increases with sustained small IO reads at 2-3k IOPS per NVMe queue. Documenting in infra PR.

comments.author: Priya Desai (SRE)
comments.body: Recommended long-term: enforce per-tenant short-stream rate limits on dedicated pools and add IO pressure alerts on pool health dashboards. Added follow-up ticket to infra backlog.

comments.author: Alex Kim ()
comments.body: Updated Acme: temporary pool move holding, autoscaler rollback appears effective. Customer requested postmortem with timeline once root cause confirmed.
